# Thread: steaming in the shower?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steaming-in-the-shower.4/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-03-14T23:35:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steaming-in-the-shower.4/post-7>

So I've been building up my cold tolerance for a couple of weeks. I'm at the point now where I can do a 10-15 minute cold shower. I haven't used hot water in about two weeks and I've been splashing my face as much as possible. I also wear nothing but shorts and a t-shirt even though it's been as low as 45F and rainy during this period.

Last nite while I was cold showering I noticed water vapor rising off my skin. Also no shivering during the cold shower except a little in the legs.

Is this progress?

I've found that the showers are much easier to get into post workout. For example after playing a soccer game or doing some weight lifting.

Let's hear it.

jesse

### Mooringsman — 2012-03-15T00:31:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steaming-in-the-shower.4/post-77>

Well, I know it seems against the laws of physics, but lots of sportsman can be seen steaming in cold wet conditions - UK rugby players to name just one group.

### AKMan — 2012-03-15T00:33:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steaming-in-the-shower.4/post-84>

You should see what I look like when I get out of my 105 degree hot tub on a -20 day! I think the steam you are describing is more a function of air relative humidity, temperature and water evaporation rates. Good job, though!

### hellojtm — 2012-03-15T04:40:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steaming-in-the-shower.4/post-459>

Good answers. The humidity was probably fairly high, but no different than it was during the other showers where the water wasn't vaporizing. I'm not claiming it was steam  That would be absurd. Still curious if others passed through this phase or not, due to curiosity.

### ashryn — 2013-01-19T02:36:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steaming-in-the-shower.4/post-71560>

I know this is an old post.. But i fog up my glass shower walls in the cold shower too...

# Thread: Omega 3/6 ratio
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PattyLA — 2012-03-15T00:25:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-58>

I have followed the GAPS diet for over 2 years. I don't consume "vegetable oils". I eat wild caught oily fish 1-4x a week. I take fermented cold liver oil daily. I eat grassfed beef and pastured chicken. I started the leptin reset in late Nov of '11

I also happen to have chronic lyme disease. My latest cd57 was 29.

I am trying to do CT but just drinking ice water is giving me terrible brain freeze. My face turns white after 2 min in the ice water and I get brain freeze from that too.

Why is my omega 3/6 ratio off with this kind of diet? Does inflammation use up omega 3's rapidly? I'm going to add more fish oil to my regimen but is there something else that may be making it hard for me to absorb those omega 3's? OR am I probably just using them up much faster than I am taking them in.

I have terrible brain fog now that the weather is warming up here. I iced my belly and that helped the brain fog but now I have a bad headache.

Can anyone help?

### kkcarlton — 2012-03-15T00:29:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-70>

Hi Patty,

I have chronic Lyme too (CD57 was 20) and I am waiting on my O6/O3 test kit to come in. Did you actually get yours tested?

~k

### AKMan — 2012-03-15T00:50:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-115>

If mine came back bad, I can't really think of anything I would change. I eat no veg oil or PUFA in general except krill and fish oil. Maybe one day I'll break down and do an on-line test.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-15T00:59:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-131>

You didn't mention seafood? Have you been eating a lot of it? It might help with the CT.

Are you overweight? I think that might make adjusting you ratios more difficult because of all the O6 that's likely trapped in the fat.

Best of luck to you on your journey to optimal!

### Martin — 2012-03-15T03:16:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-361>

PattyLA, what is your ratio? I would think the only way to know for sure is to start testing. I had no clue where I was even after eating mostly Weston Price-inspired foods for nearly five years - grass-fed, local, raw dairy, etc. Not much fish until recently. I was pleasantly surprised to finally test and get my ratio - 4.1 to 1!!!!! Where's that bucket o' cold water?!

I had bad illness episode three years ago and suspected Lyme, but did not present many of the neuro symptoms. My CD57 was 60. All I knew was that it nailed me BAD. I have not been the same since and it drove me to Kruse's site. I found Dr. Ritchie Shoemaker thru Kruse's site and tested my DNA susceptibility to mold, Lyme, etc and discover that I have mold and low alpha MSH genes. Mold, as it turns out, has been my nemesis. And the low aMSH explains my lifelong struggle. Makes me think about all those old houses I worked on with my restoration business in Atlanta!

My house tested off the charts and I'm in the middle of remediation as I write this. As soon as it is cleared of contamination, I'm starting CT and then seeing a great mold doc in Atlanta to start clearing the biotoxins. Finally! After so many years of suffering! A way through!

I don't like to spend money or go to doctors, but all that changed when I saw the pathway that Drs. Shoemaker and Kruse are showing us. I started testing. I see the way out now. I'm spending the kids inheritance to get optimal. No more guessing, wondering and wishing.

Martin

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-18T17:11:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-2387>

So I hope I'm not introducing more confusion on this topic, but I have been a wee bit confused on the PUFA sections of the CT posts. I still need to go back and re-read to see if I can straighten myself out. Currently my thoughts are that more O6 in the membrane is the signal/cumulative result of the "harvest" and with the onset of "winter" the dark and cold triggers the reversal of O6 to O3 in the cell membranes. O3s are needed in the membrane because cold temps require more membrane flexibility. So when we CT we are trying to trigger the winter/hibernation biochemical pathway in the body that decreases O6 and increases O3. Kind of a seasonal garbage collection. Since the benefit of the switch to O3 was escaping me, so I googled and found this interesting article:

[http://jn.nutrition.org/content/133/6/1913.full](http://http://jn.nutrition.org/content/133/6/1913.full)

"....The data indicate that dietary (n-3) PUFA are capable of profoundly altering the composition of phospholipids that constitute lipid rafts in T-cell plasma membrane...reducing T cell signalling.....Dietary fish oil, containing (n-3) PUFA, has been shown to suppress human T-cell function via reductions in the secretion of interleukin-2, the primary autocrine and paracrine T-cell growth factor...."

Reduced immune activation is important for those of us with AI issues.

Any thoughts?

### shilohman — 2012-03-23T18:16:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-5031>

WeAreDevo,

Here is what Dr. K has to say about fish oil supplementation from his fish oil blog. To sum it up,

use your diet to bring your omega 6/3 ratio to where you want, then fine tune with fish oil.

> THE MOST IMPORTANT FACTOR IN THE O6/O3 ratio is CONTEXT!!!!! This blog was really motivated by a conversation I had yesterday on the Santa Monica Pier with two college students who were going to attend the Ancestral Health Symposium at UCLA later today. I felt after speaking with them that this blog needed to be written because I think there is much mischaracterization out there about O6 fatty acids. They should not be vilified indiscriminately. We need an small excess of O6 to O3 normally in a healthy human diet.
>
>
>
> The current SAD “grossly” exceeds that normal excess to a great degree. Therefore, we can not and should not vilify all O6 fats. The real problem we face is that certain chronic disease conditions such as obesity, arthritis and ischemic heart disease, is where the damage has already occurred. This situation results from a chronic stimulation of the inflammatory pathways of eicosanoids in response to this neolithic insults. This is evidenced by an elevated HS-CRP, IL-6 or TNF alpha on blood testing. This is not the fault of AA or DGLA in our diets, but it is the original cause of damage of the disease process.
>
>
>
> The key point is that we should strive for a diet that limits omega six consumption to the appropriate ratios for human optimization based upon an ancestral biochemistry. This ratio is 2 to 1 to 6 to 1 by most good studies I have read. So when you begin your own trek to get healthy and optimized I would strongly suggest you get your own omega 6/3 ratio drawn so you can see how far your past diet led you astray from what should be ideal………if that number is greater than 6 to 1 and you have active chronic inflammatory markers that are elevated, then your first move should be to bring down the omega 6 content of your diet and increase the dietary consumption of omega 3’s before you supplement with fish oil!
>
>
>
> My concern is that fish oil supplements are becoming the “proxy” for good dietary choices in our paleo/primal community. Once you get your diet repaired and back to ancestral balances of O6/O3 fats, then you can titrate the effective dose of supplemental O3 oils to your desired O6/O3 ratio based upon your own blood work. This is the context I use for myself and patients.
>
>
>
> If you overdo your fish oil supplementation and don’t test you could actually hurt yourself and your cause long term. You may even cause stalls of changes in your VAP and HS CRP levels. They may also confound you and your doctor because you forgot the context of why this has happened. Remember fish oil in supplement form is a “PUFA too” and is subject to serious peroxidation and ALE formation when it is taken improperly or when it is adulterated. So it too, can be a double edge sword like the O6 content has been vilified in our community. REMEMBER CONTEXT is critical for Optimal!

### SimonM — 2012-04-07T08:28:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-12406>

It is probably mainly down to supplementation. High DHA fish oil in particular. My wife says we have more supplements in the house than actual food

I'm low, almost no-grain - having evolved that way gradually after first getting rid of wheat, then a period of gluten-free substitutes. I am wary of seeds. I eat nuts and nut butters. I eat meat, fish, eggs and so on. On reflection I am pretty careful about the oils I use, so that must help. At one time my diet seemed to me mainly canned tuna, mayonnaise and rye crispbreads  Once I recovered from that, I started looking at the oils I was using and changed those from canola, sunflower, safflower and all those to coconut oil for cooking, olive oil for cold stuff, butter or ghee if I am having a rare carb-fest with rice cakes.

I went on Ebay and got a cold water circulating machine (think igloo cooler with a water pump) and a 22 x 13 pad for 40 bucks used including shipping. Normally runs 150 -200 dollars new. It runs 40 degree water through the pad. I place it on my torso against my skin, underneath a compression shirt to hold it in place. Twice a day for 30-1 hr. works great!

i dont really use baths and am already finding that with constant AC and nightly ice packs my calories are way dowm

other day I was STUFFED at 870

yesterday 490

today 222 - and absolutely no problem

Im totally floored

### Marsha — 2012-03-15T04:43:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/product-ideas-reviews-sources-for-implementing-cold-thermogenesis.38/post-465>

This might be useful for spot icing - part of protocol after/during face dunks and before total ice baths.

[http://www.bedbathandbeyond.com/product.asp?SKU=18700786&RN=200&](http://http://www.bedbathandbeyond.com/product.asp?SKU=18700786&RN=200&)

This gizmo cost ca. $10 and is said to 'melt the fat through heat that your body generates' - ya' right!...

... I don't care about that - only that it was big enough and strong enough to hold two large flexible ice bags - one for belly and one for back and still let me move around during the day. I think it works well with tights or compression hose/spanks against the skin. I zipped the thing on- to the tightest I could get, (has lots of zippers and fits up to 50" waist) then needed help to slip the ice bags in - more laughter from my husband.

Trisha, I have the same problem with a shallow tub. I have found that by rolling over and lay on my tummy for a while does the trick! I prop myself on my elbows so I can adjust the amount of exposure I have. Got to the point where I do half on my back and the other half on my stomach.

### JC2K — 2012-03-15T19:15:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/product-ideas-reviews-sources-for-implementing-cold-thermogenesis.38/post-902>

I had looked at the ArcticHeat vest. Seemed to have pretty small amount of cooling for the cost. It's got 4 strips on the front and 3 on the back. The intent is really to keep people cool in the heat, not for weight loss though. The other downside is if you're large, and you do lose a lot of weight, the size of the vest doesn't change. So you'll need to use bands or something to keep it close to your body. So, if you've got the money to burn, it's probably ok, but I wouldn't spend the cash on it.

I plan to sew a front/back vest with velcro straps to hold it tight, with pockets to hold ice packs. I will also make a velcro wrap with ice pack pockets to wrap around my belly. Maybe something for my thighs, too. If you know someone who sews, it should be hard to do.

### Adriana — 2012-03-20T17:07:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/product-ideas-reviews-sources-for-implementing-cold-thermogenesis.38/post-3256>

I took a "Paleo Sabbatical" from mid-December to mid-March and come back to the CT rage...There's never a dull moment in Jack Kruse Land, is there?

After getting somewhat caught up on the topic I was at my chiropractor on the spinolator and water massage tables and wondered whether massage+cold would increase the effectiveness. As luck would have it, I went to Walmart to buy reusable ice pillow packs and compression shirts/ shorts and came across an " open box" Motor Massage Cushion for $19.99 which has settings for lumbar, back and sides. I plugged it in to a portable power pack with 12volt power. Topped it with my ice packs and here I am, chillin' in the comfort of my bed. I can lay it flat to do the front. It's not much of a massage, more of a vibration, but I'm hopping it will magnify the benefit.

### nonchalant — 2012-03-20T17:11:43+0200

YouÃ¢â‚¬â„¢ll feel cooler almost immediately. Gelmat absorbs the heat from your body, and maintains a temperature of 5Ã¢â‚¬â€8 degrees cooler than the ambient temperature of the room. Stays cool for about an hour, then refreshes itself as you change positions through the night. Eco-friendlyÃ¢â‚¬â€uses non-toxic gel and is safe for all family members. Soft cotton cover is antimicrobial. Approx. 35"x35".

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-02T18:41:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/product-ideas-reviews-sources-for-implementing-cold-thermogenesis.38/post-29071>

> **Groketteliveson;814 wrote:** Anyone have any better ways to get the water colder in the tub than just dumping bags of ice in? The ice just melts so fast. I've been using ice blocks which help but not much better. I need to buy a whole other freezer just for ice!

I dump a 20lb bag in the tub after it's filled with cold tap water. I found that if I added 2 cups of Epsom salts to the bath water as it is filling, the water stayed much colder and there were ice cubs left when I drained the tub after 55mins. Try the Epsom salts.

Grace

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-02T22:36:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/product-ideas-reviews-sources-for-implementing-cold-thermogenesis.38/post-29100>

I can't seem to get anywhere with anything but the ice cubes. The frozen blocks just don't seem to cool it the same for me.

I don't do icing anymore. I've figured out a computer/office set up for the tub. Got a $8 big shelf to go across tub.

I also don't do compression anymore. I think some of the "little" things Jack says help tend to help get you into it, but the net effect really isn't much different - so if you can do it without, go for it.

Also for plugging tub blue painters tape around the top drain. The plastic thingy in the reccomends section SUCKS.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-03T04:12:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/product-ideas-reviews-sources-for-implementing-cold-thermogenesis.38/post-29163>

+1 for the overflow cover. I've got one to give away....

And the Elasto-Gel brand cooling packs they sell on Amazon for various body parts - useless. They don't really get cold or stay cold.

Thera-Pearl brand cooling packs are a little more useful but still not very cold for much more than 15 min or so.

### David — 2012-06-03T04:31:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/product-ideas-reviews-sources-for-implementing-cold-thermogenesis.38/post-29167>

> **Cavemam;29200 wrote:** The plastic thingy in the recommends section SUCKS.

Yeah, it just won't stick to the tub. I hang a wet hand towel over the thing, which tends to both hold it in place and meter the water flow into the drain. At least that keeps the water at max depth longer (up to an hour...)

### jbond56 — 2012-06-03T15:12:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/product-ideas-reviews-sources-for-implementing-cold-thermogenesis.38/post-29192>

> **David;29267 wrote:** Yeah, it just won't stick to the tub. I hang a wet hand towel over the thing, which tends to both hold it in place and meter the water flow into the drain. At least that keeps the water at max depth longer (up to an hour...)

Wow, I love that thing. I had problems with it staying on at first. Now it stays on for about 10 baths. The trick is to keep it under hot water until it is very flexible then imediately attach it pressing each suction cup as hard as you can.

### Holly — 2012-06-03T15:35:56+0300

# Thread: I'm First!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-first.39/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AKMan — 2012-03-15T00:41:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-first.39/post-102>

Nothing really to say. Just wanted to be first! Ha. I figured I earned it, I have been following 'The Madman' around since early last year. One day, a person named DigitalSurgeon posted on Mark's Daily Apple, then a guy named TheQuilt started posting on PaleoHacks. I would regularly see 'Jack' or 'Dr. K.' on all the blogs I was reading. When I found out they were all the same person, I was flabbergasted. He reminds me of the chess savants you see in Central Park playing (and winning) 20 games at once, going from board to board leaving people perplexed. Well, good job, Dr. Kruse, Jack, Dr. K., Quilt, DigitalSurgeon, or whoever the heck you are today! I hope this forum takes off and you change the world!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T00:50:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-first.39/post-114>

I first came across Jack on Jimmy Moore's Facebook - I said way back then that this guy is going to rock the medical world. Friends and relatives think I'm nuts - do I care - NO, I shall grow old gracefully and healthfully thanks to Jack

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:56:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-first.39/post-124>

I first ran into a version of Jack on Paleohacks, and started the leptin reset after hearing Jimmy Moore's podcast.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T02:34:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-first.39/post-292>

I'm the 4th to post if I hit quick reply fast enough! So is our illustrious Dr. K going to respond to folk in this forum, as well as, in his blog?

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T15:53:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-first.39/post-740>

i dont care about being first but i wanted to post the first thing i encountered from Dr. kruse that impressed me. "If you are hungry during the day then you didnt eat enough protein/fat for breakfast." easy enough to test. i cooked 12 oz of steak for breakfast the next day. couldnt eat it at once. so i cut it into little bits and ate one every few minutes until it was gone. took me a hour and a half to finish. i wasnt hungry for 10 hours. that was a huge liberation for a constant nibbler. this has plagued me my whole life and it was gone in one day. nov 10, 2011. i will never forget. doing the reset since then.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-18T04:42:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-first.39/post-2290>

Ditto, AkMan. I've been lurking here and there, following Dr. Kruse around for almost a year now. I went low-carb with Jimmy Moore in February 2010, went Paleo early last year, and am now having entirely too much fun eating well, face-dunking, spot-freezing and CTing. My husband has lost weight just by tagging along with me, though I haven't coaxed him into the water yet. We only had to lose about 25 pounds each, and we've met our weight goals. Many things have changed for the better since I found JK, and Optimum is around the corner! I really have enjoyed all the blog posts and comments, and feel like I know so many people here, even if they don't know me. Please know, everyone, that I'm urging you on even if I'm not very vocal.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-tests-and-saliva-too.57/post-201>

Okay, I am 57, hypothyroidal, have multiple chemical sensitivities and electrical sensitivities, and am having issues with the smart meters put in my neighborhood. I am trying to make sense with all the info from the blog. I need to know just what type of blood tests are necessary to take. I know about the telomeres, and have a lab right here in my city to use for that. I can do the paleo diet and just trying to get more fat to eat. Supplements are not an option right now. I will have to reread the entire index blog to find out what tests to take. Thanks for your info.

# Thread: If I can inspire someone, I'd happily go first!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-can-inspire-someone-id-happily-go-first.60/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 46
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T01:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-can-inspire-someone-id-happily-go-first.60/post-212>

To make it short and sweet, I was a massive hormonal mess after the birth of my 3rd child in late 2010. When the baby was 7 months old I decided it was time for change, and I went primal. Its been 9.5 months, and I have uncovered and fixed many health-issues since then, but many things are still a work in progress. I know we all appreciate a good visual in order to really see the success I'm talking about!

_ETA: I followed the Leptin-rx from July 2011, was LS (except liver) from Sep 2011, started post-Rx protocol 2 months ago, and CT 4 weeks ago. I focus on protein/fats, heavy lifting with multiple reps, 1-2 sprints weekly. Average calories pr day 1800-2200, 70 percent being from fat like coconut oil.

_

My before photos, at 190 lbs:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

After 5.5 months, about 40 pounds down:

After about 8 months (7 months of Leptin-rx eating, 1 month of post-rx protocol)

This photo was taken a few days ago, after 3 weeks of daily CT-sessions. My weight remains 47 pounds down from starting-weight, but the fat is going, and the muscle is slowly packing on. I expect my next set of photos (a few months from now) to be totally awesome!

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T04:43:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-can-inspire-someone-id-happily-go-first.60/post-463>

> **Lexi;384 wrote:** fantastic! did you take stuff to help fix the hormones or did you do it all thru diet?

I have been using a range of supplements on and off. Didn't know I had hormonal issues until 8.5 months into this WOE... turns out I'm quite high in estradiol/E2. And my liver=function is below average... so I'm taking EstroFactors (metagenics-brand) to promote healthy estragen-metabolism, and have done so for the last 4 weeks. Its really helping, I can tell by mood/sex drive/breast tenderness. Most of my results on the outside were due to Leptin-rx, clean eating, good sleep and HIIT/slow walking.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:09:31+0200

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:10:45+0200

### Llanabanana — 2012-03-15T21:57:16+0200

### Glamorama — 2012-03-16T02:15:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-can-inspire-someone-id-happily-go-first.60/post-1173>

> **Casey;1099 wrote:** Hi Glam! I'm Mamma2Groklets (from Aus) on MDA. How are you doing CT? I know you were doing a lot of frozen gel packs. Do you do baths as well?

I've done spot-CT over my waist/belly daily for a month, and did submerges once a week up until this week. Now I plan on doing full soaks every evening. I think it will help me speed up the process of healing. I'm such a mess atm (insomnia, hormones) that I think I need this CT to work faster... Submerges are more efficient than just spot-CT.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-16T02:24:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-can-inspire-someone-id-happily-go-first.60/post-1180>

> **Llanabanana;1030 wrote:** Wow, first of Congrats!
>
> My question for you is how did you do it? I have been on LR for 6 weeks...I am seeing some signs of LS, not all of them tho. I do believe my cortisol is messed. I will need to test for this thru a natropath, which costs money I don't have a this time.
>
> Did your monitor your calories? When did you start to lose the lbs? I hope you will give us a few more details, as I am struggling with wanting to lose more, its just not happening
>
> Thank you and you are a inspiration to us all!

Hi, I never monitored calories. In 9 months I have typed in my daily meals into FitDay twice (out of curiousity) to get a rough idea of what my macros looked like. So the percentages were the following: fat 70, protein 20, carbs 10. The fat is mostly CO, as I need it to heal both my gut, liver and thyorid.

I lost huge numbers initially, 2.2.-3 lbs pr week in the first month. Then it slowed down to about 1 lb pr week... and it got slower and slower the lower my BMI became. Average loss over 9 months has been a little less than 1 pound weekly (300 grams). So its slow... very slow. But in total I shed 47 pounds of fat, bone- and muscle-mass stayed exactly the same, which is very good news!

After the intital first 5 months, it felt like I've hit stall after stall after stall. I still have about 10-15 pounds to lose, but if I go by scales its not budging. Going by body comp, my guess is that I've packed on muscle... I am a whole size smaller but weighs the same as I did 6 weeks ago.

Healing is a slow process. If you suspect cortisol-issues, the Rx- and CT will be your best friends. Look at the sun first thing upon rising (or light of any source really), eat within 30 min, after sunset, keep light in the house to a minimum/wear yellow-tinted glasses. Doing this for a bit should help restore some balance. CT-sessions after dinner is good for DHEA, I find I'm always sleepy and ready for bed after CT.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-04-18T23:01:44+0300

> **ginastarke;12471 wrote:** How did you manage 7 months of leptin rx?I've only been in a month and the boredom of meat /eggs/non-starchy veg is starting to set in for me. Sheesh. I sound like "Lisa" , Robb Wolf's old nightmare client.
>

> Since you mentioned a leaky gut, what seasonings do you use? The no nightshade rule banned almost all the spices I normally rely on.

Ginastarke -- I have cut out nightshades and eggs, which is limiting, especially because I LOVE spicy foods with chilis, peppers, tomatoes, paprika, etc.

One thing I did was stockpile my seasoning cabinet with new goodies. When I have a hankering for spicy, I choose curries instead. I regularly use a wide variety of spices, changing it up everyday so I don't get bored of the meat and veggies. Just a few I use: turmeric, garlic, onion powder, mustard powder, sage, fennel, S&P, cinnamon, nutmeg, allspice, ginger, cilantro, parsley, basil, oregano, rosemary...the list can go on and on! I make my own sausage -- using ground pork and sometimes turkey. I make sweet meats with pork -- cocoa, cinnamon, salt, nutmeg, allspice. I make spicy stuff -- curry, turmeric, garlic, cumin, etc. The sky is the limit. Get creative! Enjoy!

You look great, Glam! Congrats!

# Thread: Weight loss stall or plateau
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### David — 2012-03-15T01:58:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-227>

I see quite a bit of information on line regarding the very frustrating stalls in weight loss that many people experience.

Could Leptin resistance be the root of the problem? Is there any science, or physiological explanation, that can help determine what is actually happening in such cases?

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T02:03:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-236>

I often read about people who stall at what could still be described as a very heavy weight for their height. So obviosuly its not a healthy or ideal set-point. In those cases I personally believe hormonal issues are to blame. Leptin is the master-hormone, so it does make sense to fix that first before trying anything else. It certainly worked for me.

### AKMan — 2012-03-15T02:13:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-250>

### fabbecky — 2012-03-15T02:20:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-272>

Glam - looking super hot! Phew...

To OP, I will say that the LRx is not a magic bullet to fix stalls or plataeus, BUT it will help you in fixing your overall health and pin pointing problems. Due to the Rx I just figured out this week I have a major sensitivity to dairy that causes super blood sugar crashes and uncontrollable exhaustion.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T02:46:27+0200

### Myree67 — 2012-03-15T05:10:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-524>

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:18:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-533>

A stall can often be triggered by fluid retention that continues for prolonged periods of time, this has totally been my case. The culprits are normally gut-issues (like food allergies) or hormonal imbalances (too much or too little... either way).

### BenG — 2012-03-15T15:01:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-705>

I busted through mine by adding more cold.

### David — 2012-03-15T20:24:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-954>

I found something that seems to answer my question. (But I will continue to search.) For anyone interested, here is what I have learned. It will be no surprise that overcoming leptin resistance with the Leptin Rx appears to be critical to weight loss.

From Robert Lustig, “The Trouble with Fructose”:

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zpxm4kmcDww\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;zpxm4kmcDww\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zpxm4kmcDww\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;zpxm4kmcDww)

LEPTIN AND LEPTIN RESISTANCE

-   Leptin levels are a function of adipocyte energy stores
-   Leptin tells your brain how thin you are, not how fat you are
-   The brain perceives leptin deficiency as a state of starvation
-   Leptin deficiency causes energy expenditure to decrease, and thyroid levels to decline, while leptin repletion corrects them
-   Caloric restriction leads leptin decline before weight loss, and promotes drive to resume caloric intake
    Obese subjects are hyper-leptinemic and "leptin resistant"
    **_If we could fix leptin resistance, there wouldn't be obesity

    _**

    WEIGHT LOSS PLATEAU

    Due to decreased energy expenditure to offset decreased caloric intake

    Decreased non-exercise associated thermogenesis (NEAT\*) – less activity

    \* E.g. Fidgeting, which helps to burn calories due to moving muscles constantly. The number of calories burned could be as high as 120 calories an hour.)

    Decreased resting energy expenditure may manifest as:

    Decreased thermic effect of food - perhaps from 50 calories per kilogram LBM to 42 calories per kilogram LBM
    Mitochondrial adaptation (UCP’s) - reactive oxygen species (ROS) production may be increased, which often precedes cell death and decreased liver function and possible slowing of cellular energy delivery.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-16T16:26:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-1451>

Weight stalls/set points can be the result of hormones not operating optimally. ie if your hypothalamus-pituary-thyroid isnt operating optimally it will basically put your body into a cycle that will keep your body functioning below what it should be. Pregnenolone steal is one such example.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-17T00:08:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-1733>

In my experience, some stalls are to be expected. Your metabolism doesn't just change in a linear fashion, more in fits and starts. If a stall lasts for more than 6 weeks, something needs tweaking or testing. Personally, I think sleep is an under appreciated component to weight loss, as is stress reduction. I had a patient that was checking the scale every day. I told her to quit it, because she was doing great and looking good. She was just obsessed with the number on scale. I told her to focus on restful sleep instead -turn off the lights and go to bed early. She called me 20 days later after she went shopping for a smaller pant size.

### tellmisty — 2012-03-17T16:21:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-1981>

My understand is Jack personally never did the Leptin Reset by itself. He did the reset + CT. I believe the combination of both of those is what leads him to "not believe in stalls." There are plenty of people who have not been able to hit their weight-loss goals with the LR alone. I just had a salivary hormone test done and boy, was it revealing!!! Lots of things out of whack. Jack gave me a list of things I could do or ... I could cold adapt. Needless to say, I've been doing the protocol since (just over a week). With what Jack has shared about CT, I can't imagine anyone who's serious about being optimal and ESPECIALLY if they have ANY hormone imbalances whatsoever (don't we all???) not doing the CT protocol. We know what must be done; now we must do it!

### ginastarke — 2012-03-18T02:29:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-2196>

> **Myree67;525 wrote:** I've dealt with setpoints and stalls both and usually it's because I haven't been eating enough.

That is so me, too- I got talked into counting calories, and every time I do it, I get into a cycle of lowering my calories, stalling out, getting frustrated, lowering them more, until I can't take the frustration. I can pretty easily stay within a certain limit of calories, but the minute the weather gets nice I want to be out on my bicycle all the time and it turns into a constant calories in/calories out math test. It's like balancing my checkbook every two hours

### TheKid — 2012-03-18T14:00:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-2344>

I posted this in another thread, but thought I would put it here as well since it seems related. Here's my experience so far:

You know, I've been wondering the same thing about gaining and stalling out. I myself ahve been on the Leptin Rx for 6 weeks. Dropped a couple pounds max, but generally stayed within the same average weight range. I stopped all workouts exept riding my bike to work. I was really puzzled at the lack of continued weight loss.

I started CT two weeks ago and suddenly got crazy carb cravings. They got so intense couple of days ago that I succumbed and had a 4 donuts and large latte. Doubled back the next day with 2 donuts and latte.

Almost IMMEDIATELY I bloated. Pants fit tighter. Little more noticeable belly fat. It was awful.

What puzzled me was that I had previously lost cravings altogether and in fact was to the point where I frequently felt OK to skip lunch and just eat dinner. Other signs of LS were not really there yet; but I thought I was further along in the process. Can't figure out why the cravings came and why the binge had such a profound impact. Back to the drawing board.

### Destiny — 2012-03-18T16:36:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-2379>

My journey has been frustrating too. I believe this is equivalent to the damage in your body.

I always was a normal weight around 130-135 lbs but my body composition was not ideal and lost a gall bladder in the process. In last few years, I started gaining and was sure of the estrogen dominance. I started supplementing with progesterone cream. That helped with my PMS but the weight was going up. In the meantime, I was treated for Lyme and H pylori. I ended up at 152 lbs when I found dr Davies and dr Kruse. I have been on the reset since December. All in all, I only lost 5 lbs but my body composition is better. Also my FBS went down.

In January, I had salivary test done - almost no estrogen, high nocturnal cortisol (a lot of palpitations going on), high DHEAs, normal progesterone and testosterone. I got BHR estrogen supplementation. Do I have the pregnalone steal going on even with high DHEAs?

### David — 2012-04-06T15:51:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-12093>

I have not actually seen a stall, per se, myself. I began observing the Paleo diet last October and started the Leptin Rx over seven weeks ago. I was experiencing a weekly weight loss of about a pound each week at that time, with about a pound variation each day. Until I began CT.

I then gained five pounds over the next week or so, as I started to cold-adapt with face dunks in ice water. I then began cold showers (following my usual morning ritual of a warm soap â€˜nâ€™ scrub), working up to 30 minutes at max cold setting over a few days. I immediately started to lose weight, about seven pounds in the next week. It appears that weight loss has resumed where it was initially and continues the daily fluctuation steadily downward.

I have been icing my torso for a couple of weeks while watching television (through orange glasses) and had only one bone-headed incident of icing too long: skin temperature on my gut got down to 44F! No apparent harm, but I am much more careful now.

I started with cold baths two days ago and I already see the need for ice to get the temperature lower. I will use a bucket full of ice on my next dunk.

I am starting to see nearly all of the reset factors and benefits, although my FBG is now up to around 100, so I may still have liver LR. I have ordered hormone tests and expect results soon.

Opinions?

Destiny I think you just solved something that plagues me on and off.. Palpitations at night. Sometimes I wake up heart pounding so hard and fast! Always with a nightmare before so I wondered if the pounding caused the nightmare or the other way around. Ack!! So what do we do for high nighttime cortisol?.. I am on the reset with CT (just starting the soak phase but doing daily dunks and spot icing!) time for my own salivary test.

### TerrierMom — 2012-04-06T16:14:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-12108>

> **ginastarke;2202 wrote:** That is so me, too- I got talked into counting calories, and every time I do it, I get into a cycle of lowering my calories, stalling out, getting frustrated, lowering them more, until I can't take the frustration. I can pretty easily stay within a certain limit of calories, but the minute the weather gets nice I want to be out on my bicycle all the time and it turns into a constant calories in/calories out math test. It's like balancing my checkbook every two hours

Gina Jack has never been about calories. In the CT blogs he talks about how a very low calorie diet will be optimal.... But you work your way there and truly when CT )has activated and grown your BAT, you lose excess calories as heat anyway and don't store as fat anymore.

I'd say just go through the process, get your cold on.. I've found it naturally regulates my caloric intake. With a BAB I've mostly stopped eating lunch! I get mildly hungry about 3 ( breakfast at 6) but easily hold off til 5 or 6 (with no jitters shakes or homicidal feeelings whatsoever, unlike when I ate SAD and had to eat every 3 hours or ELSE!)

With the cold too and as I progress on the reset my appetite shifts... I just go with it. It's crazy how my body tells me when it's full now. Haven't felt that for 20 years (unless it was to unbutton my pants due to fullness!!).

Good luck breaking your stall.. Go get cold!! So much easier than chronic cardio!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-06T16:16:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-12110>

> **Krusing\_tptimal\_in\_CA;12174 wrote:** Destiny I think you just solved something that plagues me on and off.. Palpitations at night. Sometimes I wake up heart pounding so hard and fast! Always with a nightmare before so I wondered if the pounding caused the nightmare or the other way around. Ack!! So what do we do for high nighttime cortisol?.. I am on the reset with CT (just starting the soak phase but doing daily dunks and spot icing!) time for my own salivary test.

definitely do the salivary cortisol test that wake up with nightmares and heart pounding can be high OR low cortisol. between 1-3 am is when the blood sugar levels normally drop the proper response is to send cortisol to even things out and prevent you from waking. if the body is not regulated then you will either get too much and wake with palps or you get none and the body compensates with adrenaline and you wake with a nightmare _and_ palps

### TerrierMom — 2012-04-06T16:19:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-12113>

Thanks Colleen. Friend of mine also struggles with this so this is great info. Learning so much about my body! Always assumed it had to do with blood sugar (borderline t2d) ant figured it was my BS dropping too far or something... Sounds like I was maybe half right!, wonder what doc will want me to do for it tho? Off to mine the blog for cortisol issues and comments now!!

### David — 2012-04-06T16:51:00+0300

**

I certainly agree, this is also my experience. I DO feel much less prone to irritation and get FULL on BAB and early dinner.

I suspect that CT is very beneficial for me and I second the idea to work your way into CT, Gina**!

### Destiny — 2012-04-06T19:17:53+0300

### ginastarke — 2012-04-13T17:20:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-14724>

Stall fixed. one little ice pack on my stomach took off 2 pounds in 2 days- back to progress!

### Cron23 — 2012-04-13T18:04:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-14740>

> **ginastarke;14793 wrote:** Stall fixed. one little ice pack on my stomach took off 2 pounds in 2 days- back to progress!

n-Ice! I dropped 13 lbs the first 2 weeks. I started with LR and CT (full bath) and stalled one week. I'm pretty muscular and don't have much to lose but no amount of workouts/strick diet would budge my weight. I doubled up my Ice baths and lost another 3 lbs within a few days. I was weighing daily but will check in this weekend. Still can't believe this is working and it doesn't seem to be related to my diet. I have been strict Paleo for 3+ years (I have logged my food for years). I am eating the exact same food as I have for 3 yrs. Just at different times (BAB and dinner only) and CT.

Anyone else notice get through stalls with added ice time?

I do miss my workouts ;(

### shilohman — 2012-04-13T20:16:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-14776>

Added ice time helps me. When I started CT I was a 36 waist. I did what Dr. K suggested and bought a pair of shorts that were very tight in the waist, these were a size 32-34. Now these shorts are very lose and my next pair will be a size 30-32. Can't say how much my weight has dropped off because I don't weigh, too stressful.

### Destiny — 2012-04-20T02:15:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-16801>

> **Krusing\_tptimal\_in\_CA;12174 wrote:** Destiny I think you just solved something that plagues me on and off.. Palpitations at night. Sometimes I wake up heart pounding so hard and fast! Always with a nightmare before so I wondered if the pounding caused the nightmare or the other way around. Ack!! So what do we do for high nighttime cortisol?.. I am on the reset with CT (just starting the soak phase but doing daily dunks and spot icing!) time for my own salivary test.

I started taking a PS supplement - 200 mg and magnesium malate in the evening. The estrogen supplement is helping for sure as well. In the mid of February, I started with CT. All in all, I sleep better and feel better but it is a process. My palpitations are almost gone.

### 2bls — 2012-05-25T21:21:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-27413>

> **Dan in Utah;1735 wrote:** In my experience, some stalls are to be expected. Your metabolism doesn't just change in a linear fashion, more in fits and starts. If a stall lasts for more than 6 weeks, something needs tweaking or testing. Personally, I think sleep is an under appreciated component to weight loss, as is stress reduction. I had a patient that was checking the scale every day. I told her to quit it, because she was doing great and looking good. She was just obsessed with the number on scale. I told her to focus on restful sleep instead -turn off the lights and go to bed early. She called me 20 days later after she went shopping for a smaller pant size.

thanks for this, I stress over the scale every morning too. I get upset/mad at myself when the scale jumps the wrong way...I have to stop and only occasionally step on the scale. I feel smaller after 10 days and my clothes feel a little looser, but the scale has gone up 2.5 lbs. I was very upset over that this am so I'm sure my cortisol shot through the roof...

### Petra — 2012-06-17T14:47:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-stall-or-plateau.63/post-31730>

Can weight loss stall occur, after leptin reset, because of decrease in leptin? (low leptin,brain thinks: Starvation?)

Looking for some insights,

Petra

# Thread: coconut manna- gritty?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sandy — 2012-03-15T02:11:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-244>

I got a jar of coconut manna today. Taste is great, but the texture is not very pleasant. kind of gritty like paste

I guess I should be glad I really don't like it- its pretty pricey!

anyone else?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-15T02:20:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-269>

> **sandy;245 wrote:** I got a jar of coconut manna today. Taste is great, but the texture is not very pleasant. kind of gritty like paste
>
>
>
> I guess I should be glad I really don't like it- its pretty pricey!
>
>
>
> anyone else?

I heat the bottle up a little bit to make the manna soft and more liquidy and then it's much less gritty. I don't use a microwave so I just put it in a pan of hot water.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T02:59:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-335>

I've got a ton of good quality CO and just purchase coconut flour to mix and make a manna like paste. Much less expensive. I've got some coconut cream from Tropical Traditions that is like manna. When I mix my own, it is identical to what is in the jar. But neither is gritty, just creamy and thick.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T03:02:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-339>

I had the same experience. I didn't find it worth the effort/price. Gritty. . .I tried some in coffee and it just didn't seem as aromatic as just the plain CO. I ended up making little balls with the mana/coco powder and some extra CO. Just meh.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T03:04:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-342>

Try putting some lemon juice and zest in those little balls. WAY good!

### trudyap — 2012-03-15T03:09:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-348>

I was going to order some of the Coconut manna but I still do not know exactly what it is and I don't find that information on Nutiva's website...Is is a sugar free product? Does anyone know exactly what it is? How are you using it?

### Dineen — 2012-03-15T17:12:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-803>

@trudyap -- Yes, it's sugar free. As to exactly what it is -- think of it as coconut "butter" like you would think of any other nut "butter". It's finely ground coconut meat to a paste consistency.

I've used it to eat by the spoonful when I was hungry in the first week or two (or 3) of the reset. Because it's more than just the oil and has the coconut fiber it's a tad more filling. If I add it to my coffee, the grit of the fiber/meat is a bit off-putting but not too awful, but personally I find the oil alone sweeter tasting for some reason, probably the aroma like Caveman mentioned.

You could use it in curries instead of coconut milk with a bit of extra liquid too.

@grammasmitty -- that coconut flour and oil idea sounds great. I think I will give that a try for some things.

### DarleenMB — 2012-03-15T23:36:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-1082>

EWWW don't get the nutiva. I just bought a jar and it's basically coconut OIL with almost no coconut meat. I had had another brand prior the nutiva that was just FAB. Should have bought it again, instead.

Or, as suggested, just mix your CO with some coconut meal and make your own.

### trudyap — 2012-03-16T00:47:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-1115>

Darleen do you remember the name of the other brand you like better? I'd like to get something.

### Hotmail — 2012-03-16T13:16:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-1362>

Hi ladies, which brand do you normally get?

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-16T15:12:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-1416>

> **Hotmail;1364 wrote:** Hi ladies, which brand do you normally get?

I LOVE tropical traditions

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-16T16:29:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-1455>

I was wanting to make smoothies. Does Dr. K say that we can use hemp or chia seeds or are they considered grain?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-16T16:32:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-1458>

> **Dragonlady;1457 wrote:** I was wanting to make smoothies. Does Dr. K say that we can use hemp or chia seeds or are they considered grain?

Seeds & grains are not optimal. Can you do them? Yeah sure, but why would you want to if it will slow your way to optimal

If you want to pursue shakes consider coconut milk, raw eggs ( from free range organic chicken) & HWC. However if you have Leaky gut you need to avoid eggs & dairy

### sandy — 2012-03-16T16:52:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-1474>

>
>
>
> Or, as suggested, just mix your CO with some coconut meal and make your own.

hmm, thats odd. I did get the Nutiva and it seems to have plenty of meat to me. I think that is why its so gritty

### Sandie — 2012-03-16T20:41:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-1644>

Daeleen, would have a "recipe" for how to make the coconut oil and coconut flour manna you make? Thanks.

### Rhoswen — 2012-03-17T08:05:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-1881>

> **DarleenMB;1083 wrote:** EWWW don't get the nutiva. I just bought a jar and it's basically coconut OIL with almost no coconut meat. I had had another brand prior the nutiva that was just FAB. Should have bought it again, instead.
>
>
>
> Or, as suggested, just mix your CO with some coconut meal and make your own.

I noticed that with the Nutiva if you don't put the jar in a pan of hot tap water to get it all melty, all the oil stays at the top of the jar. When I let it sit in my pan of hot tap water while I'm cooking other stuff, the oil integrates with the meat and it's very tasty!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-17T14:48:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-1942>

I had this on my Pinterest and haven't had a chance to make it yet. But it sounds like coconut manna to me. Doubt I would use the other ingredients though.

[Coconut Cream Concentrate](http://http://wellnessmama.com/3112/how-to-make-coconut-cream/)

### Christi Lynn — 2012-03-21T02:10:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-3657>

I just make my own. I've bought a few different brands (nutiva, artisana, etc.) and find them similar whether called coconut manna or coconut butter. It can be great made into truffles or in some baked goods if you do recipes of that sort. Just buy fine coconut flakes (unsweetened) and toss into your food processor and turn it on. You'll have to stop it here and there and push it down off the sides. If you're in a bigger hurry then toss some melted coconut oil in there and it whips up quicker. The longer you process the less grainy it is. Next up I'm going to try my vitamix to see how it compares! Using organic coconut flakes from my local store it turns out to be $4 to make a bath that was a bit too much to fill the 15oz glass jar the nutiva came in! My store charges a lot for that coconut so I know it will be much cheaper once I order my coconut flakes online!

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-23T20:34:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-5093>

Sounds like I need to try adding CO to fresh coconut meat and blend them together . . . and that is just the base. Interesting opportunities. I need to see how cinnamon and other ingredients could be added.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-23T21:27:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-5116>

Thanks! I'm gonna try this recipe as soon as I run out of the one I have. I think it's from Wilderness Naturals.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-24T01:32:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-5228>

> **RBee;1947 wrote:** I had this on my Pinterest and haven't had a chance to make it yet. But it sounds like coconut manna to me. Doubt I would use the other ingredients though.
>
> [Coconut Cream Concentrate](http://http://wellnessmama.com/3112/how-to-make-coconut-cream/)

Oh yum! I think this would be amazing with some pure cocoa powder added in. I've been afraid of starting the coconut manna because I don't want to overdo the carbs, but I might try mixing some of this up this weekend for an occasional treat!

### PrimalPam — 2012-03-24T04:20:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-5301>

I found the CO Manna so odd-tasting and oddly textured until I immersed the jar in hot water and stirred it up. Whole different opinion now. Now, I eat it by the spoon, and it's creamy and yummy.

I make CO milk (easy, in a blender with hot water), so will have to try the CO manna recipes. Thanks!

### Rhoswen — 2012-03-24T09:23:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-manna-gritty.67/post-5344>

> **BJK77;5261 wrote:** Oh yum! I think this would be amazing with some pure cocoa powder added in. I've been afraid of starting the coconut manna because I don't want to overdo the carbs, but I might try mixing some of this up this weekend for an occasional treat!

You'd have to eat A LOT of manna to get a lot of carbs.  Probably a lot more than most people could tolerate! A big tablespoon of manna has a little more than 3 carbs in it.

# Thread: Can I 'Stay Logged-in'
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-stay-logged-in.69/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AKMan — 2012-03-15T02:12:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-stay-logged-in.69/post-247>

Most forums have a check box under the log-in box that allows you to stay logged-in. It's really nice not to have to re-log in everytime you visit.

Thanks

### Melinda — 2012-03-15T03:27:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-stay-logged-in.69/post-377>

I'm having the problem that when I jump around in the forum, I keep having to re-login. Maybe I'm doing it wrong. When I finish reading posted comments I click on "Forum" to get back to all the topics and I'm booted off.

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-15T10:50:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-stay-logged-in.69/post-638>

Same here Melinda. I'm looking around, then realise I'm not logged in anymore.

### beachy — 2012-03-16T16:05:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-stay-logged-in.69/post-1432>

Another request for a stay logged in button. Thanks!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-16T16:10:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-stay-logged-in.69/post-1438>

It's there but only on a certain page and now I can't remember where I found it. I had the same problem all of yesterday until I found this magic page. Try settings or edit profile...there is a log in page that is nothing but 'please log in' underneath the usual site's banner that has a 'remember me' box to check.

### Dextery — 2012-03-16T16:22:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-stay-logged-in.69/post-1447>

For those that can't stay logged in...from the home page attempt to log in with a bad log in and then you are taken to page that allows you to log in again. On that log in page...there is a box to check that says keep me logged in.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-16T16:40:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-stay-logged-in.69/post-1464>

> **Dextery;1449 wrote:** For those that can't stay logged in...from the home page attempt to log in with a bad log in and then you are taken to page that allows you to log in again. On that log in page...there is a box to check that says keep me logged in.

Thanks Dex for the fix!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-16T16:43:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-stay-logged-in.69/post-1469>

Ah ha! Perfect, thanks!

### beachy — 2012-03-16T17:12:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-stay-logged-in.69/post-1487>

Thanks Dextery and Clabbergirl!

### Croak — 2012-03-16T17:34:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-stay-logged-in.69/post-1507>

Or just try to reply to a thread without being logged in, same page will come up.

### Melinda — 2012-03-17T04:52:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-stay-logged-in.69/post-1859>

> **Croak;1509 wrote:** Or just try to reply to a thread without being logged in, same page will come up.

### nonchalant — 2012-03-20T23:46:25+0200

### admin — 2012-03-20T23:53:27+0200

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T02:13:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/font-size.70/post-251>

Hi, Doc

I see there's a way to change the font and size when posting, but is there a way to display all the posts in a larger font and darker color?

Squinting away here, it was sure easier thirty years ago ...

### Lyndra — 2012-03-16T01:44:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/font-size.70/post-1155>

I hear you on the small print. I use the Chrome browser and it lets me "zoom in" to make the print bigger. That doesn't work for all websites but seems fine with this forum.

### admin — 2012-04-02T22:49:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/font-size.70/post-10476>

You can use your browser to zoom in or out to make content larger or smaller. To zoom in in firefox use CTRL+ or CTRL- to zoom out.

# Thread: Severe dizzy spells lying down
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jane — 2012-03-15T02:18:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/post-264>

When I'm lying down, every time I turn over, especially on my left side, I have brief severe dizzy spells, almost as if I've been twirling around and can't stand up any longer. I'm wondering if it's from being Keto for four weeks or if my brain is rewiring,detox maybe. I went through some pretty wild brain/hormonal incidents during menopause (I'm post now) and this reminds me of those episodes. My blood glucose and pressure is fine. I'm tired of feeling like Linda Pearl in the movie The Exorcist!

Has anyone else experienced this?

Thanks!

Jane

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-15T02:51:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/post-320>

Because the dizzyness comes on when you turn over, it sounds like it could be this [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benign\_paroxysmal\_positional\_vertigo](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benign_paroxysmal_positional_vertigo)

You could try some exercises and see if they help. Here's one, but there are many more on youtube

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hhinu\_oU\_hM\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;hhinu\_oU\_hM\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hhinu\_oU\_hM\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;hhinu_oU_hM)

### Jane — 2012-03-15T04:11:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/post-431>

Coriander,

Thanks so much! It's nice to know that the pesky little rocks in my head might have been knocked loose during the reset.

Jane

### exotec — 2012-03-15T05:22:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/post-535>

Jane -

I had (have) exactly the same symptom. I went to an E-N-T specialist, and they confirmed the "loose crystals". They have a procedure, simple and manual, for resetting those crystals. It was great for a while. I need to go back. For some people it's a permanent fix ... for others (like me), the "glue" where the crystals belong isn't viscous enough to hold them indefinitely, and they have to reset occasionally.

Go to a specialist! They can fix you!

### sandy — 2012-03-15T16:46:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/post-789>

Oh boy, I get this once in awhile too. I hate it. Feels like a very bad drunk and like I am falling. Have not had it in a few months- they come and go for me. Hope they don't come back with the reset

### Jane — 2012-03-15T18:20:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/post-866>

Hi Sandy,

I hope it goes away with the reset and CT too! Have you tried the link to the maneuver on you tube? It seemed to help me a bit last night.

### sandy — 2012-03-15T20:21:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/post-950>

hi Jane

I have not had these in awhile- they kind of come and go for me. Last time it was really bad I tried the thing where you lie upside down. Thing is, it was kind of difficult for me at my weight and lack of flexibleness.

Its weird- comes out of nowhere, hangs around for awhile, then just goes away. It is scary when its happening tho. At one point it was so bad I was worried about driving, because just turning my head brought it on

### IceBarbie — 2012-03-15T20:55:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/post-982>

Yep...I have the same thing (BPPV) since a whiplash injury 15 years ago. It comes and goes depending on where those "rocks" settle in the inner ear. I have found lately that sleeping with my head elevated using 2 pillows helps because now my "rocks" aggravate me when I lie flat for too long. The symptoms change along the way. Sea-sickness meds help because they supposedly calm the vestibular system. My ENT was absolutely useless for helping me after my first full-blown episode. We came home from the ENT (after spending $945 for nothing) and my hubby went online and researched the "manuever" to fix it and did it for FREE! My chiro also fixed it so I could lie on my right side again after 3 years of not being able to without getting dizzy. Doctors are just so useless....

### Jane — 2012-03-16T05:29:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/post-1290>

Hi Barbie,

I know, so many doctors are clueless, but hopefully we will be able to turn some of them around so they become partners in our healing. I've never had this problem before, so I'm hoping it will go away when we become Optimal.

### Jane — 2012-03-16T05:33:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/post-1291>

Sandy,

Yes, it is scary. I've been wondering about the safety in driving too.

Have you tried the maneuver that Coriander linked to at YouTube? It involves sitting up, turning your head and lying down with your face still pointed at the ceiling, then sitting up again. It's not too hard and you don't need to be flexible or hang your head over the edge.

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-16T08:02:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/post-1323>

Those exercises were suggested for my dh when he first went to the dr for his weird dizzy episodes. Didn't help at all, and it turns out (neuro's best guess) that he has Vestibular Migraine. He's trying beta blockers, but hoping that sticking to Keto LeptinRx/Leaky Gut Rx and CT will fix it.

### Jane — 2012-03-16T19:00:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-dizzy-spells-lying-down.71/post-1591>

Coriander,

I've had some brief relief from the exercise. It's interesting that you mention your dh's Vestibular Migraine. When I was going through menopause I experienced those too, sometimes with pain and sometimes without, just dizziness and altered vision. In the last few years I've occasionally experienced episodes of Ocular Migraines (altered vision, but no pain). The first occurred immediately after an angiogram.

I found this information on a website

# Thread: Meal timing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing.82/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T02:56:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing.82/post-330>

What's the minimum time between meals recommended? I'll do the BAB and then (as of this point) get hungry about 6 hours later, sometimes 7. So that puts me at around 1:30-2 pm. In order to finish dinner 3 hours before bedtime, I'd need to eat by 6:30 at the latest, which only leaves about 4 hours, sometimes less. Is that okay?

I'm hoping to pare down to BAB and dinner, 11-12 hours apart, but I'm not there yet. And I struggle to have a minimum of 3 hours between dinner and bedtime, with my schedule and the kiddos. \*sigh\*

Any suggestions? Eat a lighter lunch at a set time even if I'm not hungry? Have a light dinner if I know I'll be going to bed within 3 hours?

Thanks.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T03:18:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing.82/post-362>

Hi Jeepifer.... with enough fat in the meal, i find I'm okay with just 2 a day - 6-7 a.m. and sometime between 2-3 usually. I'm fairly hungry now (9:15 p.m.) but it's bedtime now anyway and it helps me plan breakfast  I'm hoping the ice tubbing CT will kick in and haul my tush out of this weightloss stall  Since starting it, my appetite's picked up, but Dr. K suggested more fat, so I've really gotta let go of my weight-loss instincts and trust....

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T03:52:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing.82/post-411>

It depends what other issues you have going on... if you had cortisol issues you need to eat lunch. however if you're able to get through to 2pm - have a light lunch - or move dinner forward if you can...

somedays I find I look up to discover I've completely missed lunch.... and dinner is right around the corner... however that only happened for me w/in the past 3mths... prior to that I had been on the LR since the beginning of june - following the protocol strictly - trying to focus on the meal timing, light cycles, ect.

### Myree67 — 2012-03-15T05:03:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing.82/post-509>

Minimum time is ideally 5 hours but if just starting out can start at 4 and increase to 5-6 or more. 5-6 hours between meals and 11-12 from dinner to breakfast.

### BenG — 2012-03-15T14:58:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing.82/post-704>

You can increase your protein during the BAB to decrease hunger throughout the day. When I was LR alone, I needed 60g to get to dinner without hunger. Since CT, I don't even need 50 to get there. YMMV.

> **Myree67;510 wrote:** Minimum time is ideally 5 hours but if just starting out can start at 4 and increase to 5-6 or more. 5-6 hours between meals and 11-12 from dinner to breakfast.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Door Slammed on the Ancient Pathway?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/door-slammed-on-the-ancient-pathway.83/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T03:03:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/door-slammed-on-the-ancient-pathway.83/post-340>

I had a bowl of strawberries. Has the Ancient Pathway been closed? Will it take a long time to open back up (a certain amount of cold). I know the good doctor has an 100/0 approach, I'm looking to understand this consequences of this or a bowl of home made ice cream. My goal for CT (other than being superhuman) is to have my brain bypass my thyroid.

What do you think. Might there be obvious way to sense the ancient pathway (maybe other than I feel hot)?

Optim

### Kirstin — 2012-03-15T03:38:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/door-slammed-on-the-ancient-pathway.83/post-394>

If you have stayed under the carb limit and are doing the reset, you are probably OK. I think Dr. K has said that these 'cheats' set us back 10-14 days if you are over your carb limit. Other than that, just consider the seasonality of the food - strawberries at this time of year in the northern hemisphere are not optimal.

### BenG — 2012-03-15T14:39:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/door-slammed-on-the-ancient-pathway.83/post-694>

The cheats were specifically in reference to the reset. The Carbs shouldn't break adaptation. Dr. K said keep it under 50g. Armstrong and Phelps and Wim are Cold adapted too and don't eat low carb. They just aren't optimal and are killing their stem cells and longevity eating carbs in winter.

> **Kirstin;395 wrote:** If you have stayed under the carb limit and are doing the reset, you are probably OK. I think Dr. K has said that these 'cheats' set us back 10-14 days if you are over your carb limit. Other than that, just consider the seasonality of the food - strawberries at this time of year in the northern hemisphere are not optimal.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T21:22:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/door-slammed-on-the-ancient-pathway.83/post-1000>

Thanks BenG - I was talking cold adaption and not leptin resetting. Dr K seems to indicate that ANY carbs will trigger NPY response that closes the pathway.

I don't want to be wasting my time if very small "mismatches" ruin the whole thing.

### quelsen — 2012-03-15T21:34:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/door-slammed-on-the-ancient-pathway.83/post-1011>

You must understand why things work on a Starship - James Tiberius Kirk

I am going to be a total nazi here in this forum - This is the optimal forum not the less than optimal lets cheat every chance we get forum.

Nice i do on MDA and Optimal Health Cave

Whyenell did you eat fruit?

Carbohydrates are all sugars but all glucose saccharides are not all the same.

Ingestion of glucose sources during a Ketogenic attempt is not optimal. it is possible with lab work to find the "sweet spot" but then why bother.

Fructose ( fruit sugar ) is the worst of them all as they fructose molecule passively diffuses into cellular membranes. This means that you cant do anything to stop its movement or its activity. At least with glucose the liver soaks it up and replenishes the muscles first before it does anything else. so theoretically you could east some glucose and not kick yourself out of ketogenisis so long as you are really well established.

But Fruit..... you may as well just go to Baskin-Robin's and get a HFCS Blizzard. That WILL set you back. How long depends on so many factors it isnt worth the money to run the labs.

if you want to Keto-CT you walk away from fruit.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T22:21:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/door-slammed-on-the-ancient-pathway.83/post-1041>

Thanks quelsen - I appreciate your thoughtful reply, even if it is a bit ... rigid

It sounds like your saying any fruit will throw a person out of keto. And I assume you believe loss of keto closes the ancient pathway. I'd like to test for this instead of taking yours and doctor K's word for it, but don't know how to test for the ancient pathway like some do with ketosis sticks. I don't think there is an answer out there yet for this (I know doctor K has talked like keto tests are a crutch), but it seems like a whole community of people doing this might figure one out. Especially if it is such a open/closed pathway as doctor K believes it is. That way we can live biologically and psychologically optimal.

I'm certainly not trying to cheat fruit in as a part of my (fall/winter for sure) regular or even semi-regular diet, but I do have a hard time believing my biology doesn't appreciate a half dozen juicy strawberries on occasion. I know these things depend on goals though, and mine are (a mildly pleasant) life extension and thyroid function management.

Ok ok I ate the strawberries because the rest went into making homemade fruit leathers for my kids, and I didn't let remainders go to waste...

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T22:40:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/door-slammed-on-the-ancient-pathway.83/post-1057>

here's something he posted to us back on MDA

"Gretchen and et all.

I want you to concider this one change in the way you talk

Make that change in your vocabulary today and see what happens"

I started figuring out why I cannot_ and I finally got better.
