# Thread: Detox or Food Intolerance??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detox-or-food-intolerance.161/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-03-15T18:36:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detox-or-food-intolerance.161/post-878>

I'm really struggling to figure out if I've developed a new food intolerance or if what I'm experiencing is just typical detox from the Rx and CT. What kinds of detox reactions is everyone having?

I find myself getting very weak - legs and upper body/shoulders/neck - about 2-3 hours after breakfast and lunch. Strangely, it doesn't seem to happen after dinner. It also seems to be worse the 3 days a week I have to go to work. I don't know if that's because I'm sitting so much and just get stiff/sore or what it could be.

The only new food I've really introduced in the last few weeks has been seafood, but I've never had a problem with it in the past. I've been eating sardines, salmon, scallops, crab, etc. I'm careful to get BPA-free canned products and fresh when possible.

I'm also finding myself getting nauseous again several hours after eating - even after the weakness happens. This happened when I first started the Rx too and I thought it was due to the HCL I was taking, so I've since stopped that and it did go away. Now it's back!

The weakness is just terrible. My limbs feel so heavy and it's like I just can't get them to work. Putting one leg in front of another to walk is truly hard. I don't know what to think. I also feel much more tired and find myself just mentally zoning out.

Anyone experience symptoms like this just from detoxing? I've been on GAPS for a year and a half and didn't really expect much detoxing on the Rx or CT.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-15T19:18:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detox-or-food-intolerance.161/post-905>

I started a thread on this topic in the CT forum. Here's the link. Lots of people posting in that one, so hopefully you will find it useful:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?49-quot-Die-Off-quot-Symptoms](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?49-quot-Die-Off-quot-Symptoms)

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-15T20:11:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detox-or-food-intolerance.161/post-943>

> **BJK77;879 wrote:** I'm really struggling to figure out if I've developed a new food intolerance or if what I'm experiencing is just typical detox from the Rx and CT. What kinds of detox reactions is everyone having?
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> The weakness is just terrible. My limbs feel so heavy and it's like I just can't get them to work. Putting one leg in front of another to walk is truly hard. I don't know what to think. I also feel much more tired and find myself just mentally zoning out.
>
>
>
> Anyone experience symptoms like this just from detoxing?

I experienced the same feeling and also ended up with hives..but I think it was from a newly discovered egg sensitivity. I don't eat eggs now and I don't have the problem anymore.

### Llanabanana — 2012-03-15T20:55:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-questions.168/post-983>

I have a feeling that my cortisols are screwed up. I am notiicing that in the a.m. I use to be sluggish, but that is getting better, but in the evening, 9:30 I am ready for bed, my eyes are tired, body too. But....as soon as I lay down, my mind is going, all kinds of random thoughts..nothing serious.

Anyone have any ideas or thoughts on this...(not like I need more thoughts lol), but I will listen to yours

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T21:58:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-questions.168/post-1030>

salivary cortisol testing is the only way to verify....

### Petra — 2012-03-16T12:54:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-questions.168/post-1357>

In the presumption your are female, can it be in the cycle?

I have one week, like that, the second week of my cycle. Not sure what to make of it, but my minds goes all the way at night, when I want to sleep. Just a few days, and off it is.

Some people also tell me they sleep not so well, when the moon is full or almost full.

Wishing you happy thoughts,

Petra

>
>
>
> Wishing you happy thoughts,
>
> Petra

Thank you! I have noticed that there are times when I am not ready for bed and cannot wind down, while most of the time I am bed ready by 9:30. I have never thought to see if it is connected to my cycle. Why oh why do I not think of these things!

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-29T05:39:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-questions.168/post-8114>

If this happens often, though, a salivary test might be a good idea.

### Destiny — 2012-03-29T13:18:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-questions.168/post-8183>

I had heart palpitations and waking up at night with a strong heartbeat. I also felt an unpleasant rush -salivary cortisol testing confirmed a high nocturnal cortisol.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-supplements-w-o-stearic-acid-magnesium-stearate.169/post-984>

I am looking for sources of supplements w/o the additives stearic acid, magnesium stearate.

In particular, 7-Keto DHEA.

### MonteD — 2012-03-16T17:21:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-supplements-w-o-stearic-acid-magnesium-stearate.169/post-1497>

You have a few options.

[http://nutrigold.com/7-Keto-DHEA](http://http://nutrigold.com/7-Keto-DHEA) You can get it off of Amazon.

If that doesn't work then search for "7 keto soft gels". NOW brand makes a good one. There are a few lesser known brands that have soft gels as well. Sometimes they use titanium dioxide but you can find one without that in it as well.

### ASHO — 2012-03-16T17:53:01+0200

>
>
>
> If that doesn't work then search for "7 keto soft gels". NOW brand makes a good one. There are a few lesser known brands that have soft gels as well. Sometimes they use titanium dioxide but you can find one without that in it as well.

Apex Energetics makes a regular DHEA in liquid form and they make all their supplements without excipients (they cater to autoimmune people). Premier Labs is another excipient-free company.

# Thread: Four days in: nasty head cold.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/four-days-in-nasty-head-cold.175/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shah78 — 2012-03-15T22:33:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/four-days-in-nasty-head-cold.175/post-1053>

Four days into CT (n=1) and got a head/sinus cold. (I am still cycling chills and fever.) I stopped the CT (n=1) experiment till I feel better. But should I continue my mild introduction into CT anyway.?I use mild (wimpy)Theraflex gel ice packs , not real ice or the tub yet. I'm open to all experimentation.(as long as its wimpy/slow/steady) EDIT:4/8/12: Three weeks progress report. This is much easier then I would have originally guessed. I alternate cold baths and Gel packs daily. Water 58 degrees for an hour and 14 square feet of theraflex packs at a time. The Packs are actually colder. I live in Florida, so I've had to ramp up my water cool down "technology". Had to go from 40 lbs.of ice(gallon plastic jugs) to eighty lbs for tomorow.Will eighty lbs. fit into the tub? Will there be room for me? We'll see. The gel packs may prove easier for Florida.I can load up over twenty square feet of the stuff. Sort of like being buried in very cold sand. But my original question still stands. Do you ct while sick?

### Dextery — 2012-04-08T22:56:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/four-days-in-nasty-head-cold.175/post-12783>

The CT is strengthening your immune system. It would appear, your immune system found some toxin(s) in your body and it is getting rid of the toxin by attacking the invader and it is being expressed by the head cold by elevating your temperature. Continue with the CT.

Also, everyone should be supplementing with Vit D3 to also assist the immune system. I get Country Life brand from [www.iherb.com](http://http://www.iherb.com) because the carrier in the gel caps is medium chain triglyceride oil rather than soybean oil, sunflower oil or olive oil. If you do buy from them, use DEX002 and you will get $5 off your first order. They are also a good source for Vit K2 MK4 from Thorne Research.

### agatha — 2012-04-08T23:39:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/four-days-in-nasty-head-cold.175/post-12793>

Yes, I got this in the second week. I thought the whole world would drown in the mucous coming from my nose! It's a detox.

### Adria — 2012-04-09T00:32:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/four-days-in-nasty-head-cold.175/post-12805>

Yes also having terrible colds since CT. I normally never have a cold, its been many years.

First one came and went and was mild around the 3rd wk. Now in wk #6 I got a severe head cold.

Not sure about detox as my husband became sick 2days after I did. Now he has pneumonia.

### Dextery — 2012-04-09T00:42:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/four-days-in-nasty-head-cold.175/post-12809>

> **Adria;12874 wrote:** Yes also having terrible colds since CT. I normally never have a cold, its been many years.
>
>
>
> First one came and went and was mild around the 3rd wk. Now in wk #6 I got a severe head cold.
>
>
>
> Not sure about detox as my husband became sick 2days after I did. Now he has pneumonia.

All associated with compromised immune systems. 50,000IU of Vit D3 for a week will strengthen your immune systems...which activates the killer T cells to go out and kill off the invading virus and bacteria that is causing the head cold/pneumonia.

I have been supplementing D3 for over 4 years and have not been sick one day during that time. Used to get 3 a year. And CT also strengthens the immune system.

Do not be discouraged. You are in the right place for optimal health.

# Thread: Educating doctors
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educating-doctors.177/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MJ* — 2012-03-15T22:52:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educating-doctors.177/post-1062>

I would love to see a forum for ideas, suggestions, articles etc., that can be used to help us explain what we are trying to achieve to our own doctors. Even start a directory of docs who are open to this.

### caroline — 2012-03-19T09:42:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educating-doctors.177/post-2667>

What a great idea. I used to have a wonderful Doc -a young woman. She was just great and very open minded but when I asked about HCG - she truly thought I had lost my mind!! However I would have been able to give her stuff to read etc and she would have embraced it. Unfortunately she has moved away - I am very tempted to follow her to the ends of the earth if need be! At the practise I go to now - I can't get any of them to listen at all or do the labs I want. They have no interest if my blood work looks reasonable no matter what I say

# Thread: Melatonin = More Effective CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin-more-effective-ct.179/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AKMan — 2012-03-15T23:33:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin-more-effective-ct.179/post-1076>

From a recent study, notice any other themes from Dr. K's blogs? : [http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1467-789X.2010.00756.x/abstract;jsessionid=2CBACA76EADCB416B16C710DD93437A7.d02t01?systemMessage=Wiley+Online+Library+will+be+disrupted+17+March+from+10-14+GMT+%2806-10+EDT%29+for+essential+maintenance&userIsAuthenticated=false&deniedAccessCustomisedMessage=](http://http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1467-789X.2010.00756.x/abstract;jsessionid=2CBACA76EADCB416B16C710DD93437A7.d02t01?systemMessage=Wiley+Online+Library+will+be+disrupted+17+March+from+10-14+GMT+%2806-10+EDT%29+for+essential+maintenance&userIsAuthenticated=false&deniedAccessCustomisedMessage=)

A worldwide increase in the incidence of obesity indicates the unsuccessful battle against this disorder. Obesity and the associated health problems urgently require effective strategies of treatment. The new discovery that a substantial amount of functional brown adipose tissue (BAT) is retained in adult humans provides a potential target for treatment of human obesity. BAT is active metabolically and disposes of extra energy via generation of heat through uncoupling oxidative phosphorylation in mitochondria. The physiology of BAT is readily regulated by melatonin, which not only increases recruitment of brown adipocytes but also elevates their metabolic activity in mammals. It is speculated that the hypertrophic effect and functional activation of BAT induced by melatonin may likely apply to the human. Thus, melatonin, a naturally occurring substance with no reported toxicity, may serve as a novel approach for treatment of obesity. Conversely, because of the availability of artificial light sources, excessive light exposure after darkness onset in modern societies should be considered a potential contributory factor to human obesity as light at night dramatically reduces endogenous melatonin production. In the current article, the potential associations of melatonin, BAT, obesity and the medical implications are discussed.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-16T00:58:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin-more-effective-ct.179/post-1119>

Good stuff. So much is coming together. Gotta go get some blueblocker glasses...

### Croak — 2012-03-16T02:05:28+0200

### AKMan — 2012-03-16T02:18:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin-more-effective-ct.179/post-1177>

> **Croak;1169 wrote:** I think the Doc is going to come back and say you don't need the melatonin supplementation, that the CT and circadian light management will induce the same effects.

### x_danny_x — 2012-03-18T05:27:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin-more-effective-ct.179/post-2303>

I still take melotonin. I like it when it helps release growth hormones. Good for a workout with heavy weights.

### Kasra — 2012-03-27T20:04:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin-more-effective-ct.179/post-7214>

> **Croak;1169 wrote:** I think the Doc is going to come back and say you don't need the melatonin supplementation, that the CT and circadian light management will induce the same effects.

I agree that cold adaptation and light management are two profoundly useful tools in regulating circadian rhythm and optimizing metabolism. The study linked by AKMan corroborates this.

However, several studies linked in that same source show that endogenous melatonin production _drastically_ decreases with age. I imagine that being cold adapted and wearing blue-blocker glasses could could only mitigate this.

Thus, I think you should not be so quick to discard the idea of melatonin supplementation, especially in light of its exceptional (albeit largely anecdotal) safety record.

### Croak — 2012-03-27T21:58:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin-more-effective-ct.179/post-7313>

And I'm pretty sure the response is still going to be no need to supplement, period. Kruse has clearly stated he's dead set against melatonin supplements. As a tool to reset the circadian clocks after jet lag, he's ok with it, but that's a day's use here and there, not long-term supplementation.

Remember, his specialty isn't weight loss or fitness, it's _longevity_. Taking into that account, I'm guessing he's seeing the pineal gland working just fine in older patients following his protocols, and I suspect that supplements can exacerbate the loss of function.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-28T15:43:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin-more-effective-ct.179/post-7688>

What about SAD light therapy boxes? Would those still be okay to use?

Typically, you expose yourself to the light boxes for 20-40 minutes in the morning.

### kalibex — 2012-03-28T16:13:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin-more-effective-ct.179/post-7697>

> **villjamur\_stevenson;7728 wrote:** What about SAD light therapy boxes? Would those still be okay to use?
>
> Typically, you expose yourself to the light boxes for 20-40 minutes in the morning.

You know what would be interesting? If CT turned out to negate the need for such SAD treatments... What if it turns out that we (if properly cold-adapted) would feel less depressed in the cold season, but would naturally show slightly altered behavior patterns in the winter (which we would have to understand, and adapt to).... If so, that would be another nice CT benefit...

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-28T17:00:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin-more-effective-ct.179/post-7721>

> **Croak;7351 wrote:** And I'm pretty sure the response is still going to be no need to supplement, period. Kruse has clearly stated he's dead set against melatonin supplements. As a tool to reset the circadian clocks after jet lag, he's ok with it, but that's a day's use here and there, not long-term supplementation.

# Thread: Another meal timing question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-meal-timing-question.183/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### blindpig — 2012-03-16T01:10:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-meal-timing-question.183/post-1128>

I read the thread down below about meal timing but I'm still not clear if what I have been doing is the best. I have been doing the reset since November, but I know I am still not LS on all levels, so I continue.

For quite some time, this has been the pattern of my appetite: I get up at 5:00am and eat within 30-45 minutes. I have plenty of protein and plenty of fat (I would be embarassed to say how much coconut oil and butter I put in my BP coffee every morning). Then I am not hungry for about 7 to 8 hours, but I am hungry at that point and it would be difficult to wait any longer. I eat a large lunch then, with lots of protein and fat again. After that I am not hungry again in time for dinner, so I normally don't have any dinner. I do sometimes get hungry before bed, but then it's too late, so I just don't eat. If I do eat anything before my "3 hours before bed window" closes, it will usually just be a cup of bone broth with a shot of hot sauce or something similar.

This has been serving me well, I believe, in fact I have experimented and when I have eaten a larger evening meal I have woken up hungry in the night. But my concern is that by not eating from roughly 1:00pm to 5:00am this is too much like IFing, which I know I should not be doing at this point. Opinions, anyone?

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-16T01:19:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-meal-timing-question.183/post-1131>

> **blindpig;1129 wrote:** I read the thread down below about meal timing but I'm still not clear if what I have been doing is the best. I have been doing the reset since November, but I know I am still not LS on all levels, so I continue.
>
>
>
> For quite some time, this has been the pattern of my appetite: I get up at 5:00am and eat within 30-45 minutes. I have plenty of protein and plenty of fat (I would be embarassed to say how much coconut oil and butter I put in my BP coffee every morning). Then I am not hungry for about 7 to 8 hours, but I am hungry at that point and it would be difficult to wait any longer. I eat a large lunch then, with lots of protein and fat again. After that I am not hungry again in time for dinner, so I normally don't have any dinner. I do sometimes get hungry before bed, but then it's too late, so I just don't eat. If I do eat anything before my "3 hours before bed window" closes, it will usually just be a cup of bone broth with a shot of hot sauce or something similar.
>
>
>
> This has been serving me well, I believe, in fact I have experimented and when I have eaten a larger evening meal I have woken up hungry in the night. But my concern is that by not eating from roughly 1:00pm to 5:00am this is too much like IFing, which I know I should not be doing at this point. Opinions, anyone?

You could probably classify that as IFing but I would go by how you feel and if you don't feel hungry then don't eat. I don't see anything wrong with that.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-16T02:56:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-meal-timing-question.183/post-1207>

Maybe try a smaller lunch earlier in the day, so you can have dinner at the appropriate time.

### blindpig — 2012-03-16T18:35:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-meal-timing-question.183/post-1569>

> **Groketteliveson;1132 wrote: You could probably classify that as IFing but I would go by how you feel and if you don't feel hungry then don't eat. I don't see anything wrong with that.
>
>
> PaleoCowgirl;1208 wrote:** Maybe try a smaller lunch earlier in the day, so you can have dinner at the appropriate time.

Wahhhhh! I agree with both of you, so I still don't know which is optimal!

### Birdy — 2012-03-16T18:47:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-meal-timing-question.183/post-1581>

It IS like IF'ing, but the important thing is to not IF at breakfast, so if you feel fine with it, no problem.

### blindpig — 2012-03-16T18:53:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-meal-timing-question.183/post-1584>

> **Birdy;1583 wrote:** It IS like IF'ing, but the important thing is to not IF at breakfast, so if you feel fine with it, no problem.

Thanks for breaking the tie, Birdy (that used to be a nickname of mine!)... I will quit worrying about the "rules" and stay with what's working for me. And goodness knows this is working for me, it is nothing short of amazing.

Nope cuz Dr. K said estrogen is there too and needs to be detoxed. So, even though I don't have much I am pouring water over my throat and chest while in 50 degree water. I want to be healthy at some point in my life and if that means the little things get littler, than so be it. If I am cold before I get in the tub and no I can't get the upper half that day, I keep pouring the water over my belly too. It turns pink so I'm pretty sure I'm good with getting it cold. I have thought about bringing ice packs in for it but I also realize this should be a limited thing cuz I really need to get more of me in the water, not less. Good luck to you.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-16T02:45:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-in-the-cold-tub.184/post-1194>

I spot-CTed my tummy/waist for the last month, and the boobs shrunk regardless. So did my shoulders, arms and other upper-body areas that weren't ever in contact with the ice blocks. My boos have gone from 14 EE down to 12 D now... not tiny but quite small compared to a few months ago. I've had horrendous issues with excess estrogen in the last 3 months, CT just made it worse. The good thing is that it means the crappy WAT is going!

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-16T03:03:55+0200

Embrace it. There's certain places you gain fat when you're estrogen dominant - chest being one of them. So what you had wasn't healthy. I'd say it's one of the sure signs you're getting closer to optimal.

I'm in the camp of the smaller the better/more practical  I pretty much never wear a bra anymore and once you've ditched it for a while, you won't be able to stand wearing one anymore! Plus there is a high correlation between breast cancer and bras... the more you wear a bra the more likely you are to get breast cancer (that's why I ditched them).

>
> Note: It's a correlation not causal relationship between bra wearing and breast cancer. NEVER EVER wear a bra at night and try to go without one as much as possible. PSA over

We all want to look good no doubt. I've never worn a bikini and would love to have the body confidence to do that. But first and foremost, CT is about health. Detox symptoms abound because we're loaded up with toxins - in the liver, the fat tissue, etc etc. I can speak first hand about breast cancer because 18 months ago I was having a mastectomy. Trust me, small toxin-free breasts are better than ones loaded up with estrogen. If you don't detox them, the surgeon will.

### Adria — 2012-03-18T00:07:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-in-the-cold-tub.184/post-2149>

I'm week 4 CT and have been paleo-keto for 3 years. Since paleo-keto the girls have gotton bigger, fatter. For CT I take my baths up to my waist. My bottom and thighs are already much thinner. I'm fitting in my smallest pants, had to dig them out of my closet. I'm not taking ice baths, just cold about 65 degrees. After my bath I dry up and then use ice packs, I sit on 2 - one for each cheek, and the others I move around on my shoulders, stomach, chest, neck, head. So far the girls seem the same size but if its better, healthier to dunk, then I'll start icing them even tho I"m not very well-endowed!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-18T15:25:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-in-the-cold-tub.184/post-2361>

though I don't need it yet - Lake Ontario water in the pipes is still PLENTY cold!! - I've been spot placing the home-made icepacks in the tub. EASY to make (thank you's to whoever it was on the blog!) and in the long run, likely cheaper than bags and bags and bags of ice.

1 part rubbing alcohol (isopropyl alcohol) to 2 parts water in a ziplock bag, double bagged to help minimize leakage. Made my 'ice vest' with those and duct tape, worked a treat  now that I don't need it anymore, I'll be recycling the contents into more ziplock baggies to spot place and use in the tub if the Lake warms up too much.

# Thread: How do you know your Leptin is reset? And how about relapse?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 71
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Petra — 2012-03-16T02:43:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1191>

In January I did a strict reset. Did not lose weight, but I felt full quite fast and had no cravings for sugar and/or carbs.

Now with CT I get cravings again even for carbs. Did the reset not work out?

Lately I eat some 75-100grams of carbs a day, is that causing relapse and I'm getting Leptin Resistant again? How does that work?

Thanks already for your thoughts and input,

Petra

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-16T02:54:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1206>

With CT, you're also supposed to be eating keto style and limiting carbs. With the reset, if you have weight to lose, you should stay under 50 grams of carbs/day...but with keto, it's much lower than that. If the carbs are making you crave, I would cut them out or limit them much more, adding in more fats instead. Just my two cents...

### Glamorama — 2012-03-16T02:58:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1210>

There's a list on the blog about the signs to look out for ebfore you are fully LS. Doc says it takes 6-8 weeks, but often longer, depending on your health-status as you start. I have a feeling age matters too.

Having been there and done that (the banana-incident in nov 2011...), I'm tipping whats happening is that CT gets you hungry ebcause its burning fat. Our bodies' antural response to that is to ask to be fed by driving hunger. The fact that your each for carbs, to me indicates that you're not leptin sensitive at the liver (this was me for a long long time), OR you have Candida-overgroth in your gut. 75-100 grams carbs is a lot if its not summer, and you want to lose weight. I did 80-100 grams over summer, but now I'm down to 40-60 grams daily (fall here in Oz). By June/July I plan on being keto.

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-16T03:21:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1223>

A nugget Dr. K posted in response to a question I had that's something to consider if things aren't working out with CT:

> @Souldanzer…….you have hit on something I have just mentioned in passing. The Ancient pathway is built for survival and longevity but one thing it does not protect us from is excessive CRH from light……..so just say you become a modern cold adapted hominid…….and you eat correctly…….but you use light from your modern technology at night……Well this is a major biologic mismatch that raises cortisol……….The Ancient pathway has no answer for this so it can destroy the effects that the Ancient pathway provides. You may realize now, why I was in the dark talking, to Sean Croxton on my underground wellness appearance………Many people have not picked this up but this is a major reason I am concerned about our modern paleo’s. Not only do they eat carbs at the wrong time they also use technology all the time including the wrong times.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-16T03:30:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1236>

> **Souldanzer;1224 wrote:** A nugget Dr. K posted in response to a question I had that's something to consider if things aren't working out with CT:

I've naturally eliminated the use of TV and iPad most evenings now, and in bed by 9 pm, lights off by 9.30 pm the latest. A natural urge that started after I begun CT. I noticed CT made me sleepy, but then the TV would wake me up again! Planning on getting yellow lense glasses to wear at home for the times I want to watch a movie or stay up to watch a docu etc.

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-16T04:11:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1257>

I'm honestly wondering if the stress effect doesn't just come from technology... but any other stress as well. Everything was dandy with my eating until I hit a major stressor in my life recently. Then things went downhill. All the CT in the world doesn't appear to fix that.

But then, I have AN brain and that might make things radically different...

Petra

### TheKid — 2012-03-16T12:15:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1352>

Wow. I'm glad I'm not the only one facing cravings. I started CT about 2 weeks ago and seemingly out of nowhere cravings popped up this week. I'm 6 weeks into the Leptin Rx and really thought I had cravings beat. Do you guys really think it is lack of Leptin sensitivity in the liver? Or could it be something else?

And if cravings are happening to people, what do you do to cope?

### Glamorama — 2012-03-16T12:17:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1353>

### Petra — 2012-03-16T12:26:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1355>

> **TheKid;1354 wrote:** Wow. I'm glad I'm not the only one facing cravings. I started CT about 2 weeks ago and seemingly out of nowhere cravings popped up this week. I'm 6 weeks into the Leptin Rx and really thought I had cravings beat. Do you guys really think it is lack of Leptin sensitivity in the liver? Or could it be something else?
>
>
>
> And if cravings are happening to people, what do you do to cope?

For me I write them off as looking for comfort now that I CTing. The cold is making me unconformable. I'm not there yet, that is why I fell back on some carbs again. Which is no good choice, might you consider giving in to the cravings. Brain fog is coming up.

It is definitely no hunger pang. So Jack's right, cold reduces hunger feelings, yet cravings are coming back and twisting my sobriety.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-16T14:50:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1403>

For the cravings try upping your fat content during the day, I find mine are abolished if I eat 70+% fat in the day. it\`s really hard to want carbs with that level of fat in the diet.

### Croak — 2012-03-16T17:18:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1493>

It's not looking for comfort, it's your body saying:

"HEY, I'M EATING YOU ALIVE HERE TO STAY WARM, YOU NEED TO THROW SOME LOGS ON THE FIRE IF YOU WANT TO KEEP WHAT YOU GOT!"

You're not craving carbs per se, you're just getting a strong hunger signal _after_ CT which is normal, and you are translating that to carb desire.

When the doc says CT kills hunger, he's right...no hunger at all when cold, but as you warm up that's a different matter.

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-16T18:00:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1539>

> **Croak;1495 wrote:** It's not looking for comfort, it's your body saying:
>
>
>
> "HEY, I'M EATING YOU ALIVE HERE TO STAY WARM, YOU NEED TO THROW SOME LOGS ON THE FIRE IF YOU WANT TO KEEP WHAT YOU GOT!"
>
>
>
> You're not craving carbs per se, you're just getting a strong hunger signal _after_ CT which is normal, and you are translating that to carb desire.
>
>
>
> When the doc says CT kills hunger, he's right...no hunger at all when cold, but as you warm up that's a different matter.

My hunger comes in phases. For the most part CT has cut the hunger cravings. Guess I need to start paying closer attention as to when the hunger is triggered.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-16T18:19:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1553>

Hey Petra,

Here's my list of signs of leptin sensitivity:

• Less hunger and cravings

• Sleeping like a rock

• Feeling refreshed in the morning

• Less muscle soreness

• Mental calmness

• Steady weight loss

• Increased libido

• Softer skin and stronger nails

• Improved mental ability

• Fewer headaches

• Dreaming, especially about moving your body (dancing, exercising, exploring, etc.)

• More energy

• More sweating with exertion

• Firmer gums, smoother teeth

I guess a relapse would be a reverse of the above.

I crave carbs, too, at times, but much less than a few months ago. If someone's metabolism has been leptin resistance/insulin dominant, they're riding on a repeating 90-120 minute hyperglycemia/insulin surge/reactive hypoglycemia metabolic roller-coaster. Leptin will eventually tame insulin, but it can take awhile. I think adequate sleep, stress management, stopping inflammation, and CT are equally important as carb restriction. Eating seasonally make a difference, too. If your stuck, you need labs to guide your way out.

Good luck!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-16T18:49:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1583>

> **TheKid;1354 wrote:** Wow. I'm glad I'm not the only one facing cravings. I started CT about 2 weeks ago and seemingly out of nowhere cravings popped up this week. I'm 6 weeks into the Leptin Rx and really thought I had cravings beat. Do you guys really think it is lack of Leptin sensitivity in the liver? Or could it be something else?
>
>
>
> And if cravings are happening to people, what do you do to cope?

Same here... I thought I was losing it--to suddenly start having carb cravings after never even thinking about food for nearly 6 weeks. I have been doing the CT for a few weeks, but really lowered the temperature this past week and that's what has brought on the cravings.. nothing serious, but noticeable. I pop a couple macadamia nuts and that seems to fix the problem. Not sure if macadamia nuts are the thing to eat during the winter--but then again I don't know if the 75 deg. temps in the desert in March is winter

### WandaP — 2012-03-16T22:58:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1699>

I'm new, so I have a question. Is "keto" putting your body in ketosis? What do you do to overcome bad breath that goes with ketosis? I did Atkins about 15 years ago for about 4 months, but I got a terrible breath with ketone burn. Also, I know I have had candida overgrowth in the gut. My legs are all itchy and yeasty, but they are already better after just 3 weeks on the leptin reset. (I need to up my protein at breakfast, still. So hard to reach 50 grams of protein!)

### claudia rowe — 2012-03-17T12:48:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1913>

> **WandaP;1701 wrote: I'm new, so I have a question. Is "keto" putting your body in ketosis?
>
>
> It is, yes.
>
>
>
> WandaP;1701 wrote:** What do you do to overcome bad breath that goes with ketosis?

It's awful, isn't it? I blend masses of fresh mint and parsley, then pass them through a sieve, add water and voila, a mouth wash. I swish with half a big glass and spit and swallow the remainder. It's delicious and does seem to work better than commercial mouth washes.

I also brush about 6 times a day with a very soft toothbrush (so as not to wear out the enamel).

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-17T14:08:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-1928>

>
>
>

Yup. I don't have cravings. It's just ...simple... STARVATION! I'm pretty peckish by bedtime (even after eating a fatty supper and 4T worth of "squirrel candy" - mainly coconut oil, manna and some stevia) and especially now that I've dropped the tub temp a bit more, I'm RAVENOUS in the wee hours of the night. I ignore it, wait for breakfast, and wake up with a headache for a few days. Can't wait til this wild blood-sugar swinging settles down, but I'm noticing a trend: hunger goes with blood sugar swings, eventually goes with a bout of nausea and another weightloss and fasting blood sugar plateau busted. It's a good thing  (I hope?)

### tellmisty — 2012-03-17T16:13:54+0200

Just my two cents.

### claudia rowe — 2012-03-26T16:08:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-6320>

I'm wondering how long it takes, also. I'm at the end of week 8, have stuck to the Leptin Rx plan religiously and my serum leptin levels are the worst they've ever been. Actually, all my bloods are out of whack including free T3, rT3, DHEA and cortisol. Actually, these are the worst bloods I've had in 8 years except that my lupus antibodies are slightly lower. Even my cholesterol, normally pretty low, is higher than usual.

The MS related neck tremor is significantly better, almost completely gone.

I'm frankly a little concerned. After 8 weeks strict paleo, supplements, water, CT and only eating BAB and then coconut oil for afternoon tea, I've gained 4kg and my bloods have seemingly gone backwards.

I'm mystified!!

Have you had your sex steroid hormones tested? When you are gaining weight and having cravings it's usually a sign that your hormones are out of whack. I did great the first six weeks. Then started CT and the cravings and hunger started and then the weight came on. I gained 6# back. Then I got tested and sure enough my hormones were WAY out of whack. It sounds like there are a lot of good things going on for you so there are other issues still present in the body. Check hormones!

### Cody — 2012-03-26T19:31:48+0300

### Destiny — 2012-03-27T01:39:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-6661>

> **Cody;6478 wrote:** Dr. Kruse has said many times that at the beginning of CT, you're going to have out of whack hormones. They should settle down with time. Patience, people. How many years did it take you to get all messed up???

I agree, we all need to grow patience. There are so many differences about us! There is no simple solution right away. We need to keep encouraging each other and share our individual experiences since it might help somebody here.

I was super frustrated last week when I ended up at the same weight after starting with all of this 5 months ago. I gained back by doing CT and I was crushed. Then, I started counting my blessings. Due to all of this I found out that my hormones were out of balance. I also was able to lower my fasting blood sugar, etc.

I am probably on a long journey to a better health but at least I am.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-27T01:49:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-6664>

> **Cody;6478 wrote:** Dr. Kruse has said many times that at the beginning of CT, you're going to have out of whack hormones. They should settle down with time. Patience, people. How many years did it take you to get all messed up???

Yes, but he also said you can get your hormones tested and not wait for the CT to fix your hormones. CT will do other great things. If they are seriously out of whack like mine are there are other complications that can take place. He specifically told me that I need to treat my hormone issues aggressively. Waiting only intensifies the imbalance. Patience is a great asset and of the utmost importance but anyone who has waited to get things tested, too long know their patience in this particular case didn't pay off.

### Destiny — 2012-03-27T01:57:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-6676>

Waiting only intensifies the imbalance. Patience is a great asset and of the utmost importance but anyone who has waited to get things tested, too long know their patience in this particular case didn't pay off.

You are right, I am for testing! When I did it, I found out my problems. On the other hand, you still need a lot of patience to work on the problems. It is so hard not to compare your results against the results of others. Sometimes, it seems that almost everybody is doing better than you do.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-27T02:29:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-6697>

> **Destiny;6712 wrote:** You are right, I am for testing! When I did it, I found out my problems. On the other hand, you still need a lot of patience to work on the problems. It is so hard not to compare your results against the results of others. Sometimes, it seems that almost everybody is doing better than you do.

Hey I was doing great in the beginning. Lost weight, more energy than I'd had in years, and just plain felt happy and good. Then I tested. I found out that the CT was causing release of the estrogen and I was hugely estro dominant. My level is higher than the highest point in the range for a pregnant woman. Definitely very bad for a non-pregnant woman. So I am with ya on the patience for sure. At this point I have to be and it will take a few months to get this squared away. But I think the patience comes when you know what you are going to be working on. That is why Dr. K always tests himself every 3 mo or more. If you don't test you may or may not know where you need to start.

Destiny try not to compare as everyone is in a different stage than another. I try to learn and get excited from those who have preceded me in the process. Don't forget some of the folks on this board have been working on this for 6-8 months. I've only been at it for 3 which puts me in the infancy stage compared to others. We will all get there through perseverance, hard work, testing, and patience.

### claudia rowe — 2012-03-28T01:03:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-7406>

I'd take it as a great favour if patty cakes and others did not make categorical statements about things I did not say.

1\. Didn't say I was having cravings of ANY sort.

2\. Have not eaten off Leptin rX, not even a mouthful for 8.5 weeks. The weight gain IS NOT due to cravings or any other such nonsense. I eat exactly to plan.

3\. The weight gain is due to chronic leptin resistance and has been for more than 8 years, seemingly.

4\. The neck tremor is almost gone under the Leptin rX and for that I'm wildly happy.

5\. My estrogen, DHEA, pregnenolone, testosterone and cortisol levels are tested every 3 months and are all within normal range.

What mystifies me is that my serum leptin levels, according to Dr Kruse the reason why I'm gaining weight, are worse than they've ever been under this regime.

Again, I'm not eating even a mouthful of anything not prescribed in the plan and I'm taking the full contingent of supplements.

Any ideas that relate to this exact situation (and not something the I didn't say) I'd be so happy to hear.

Many thanks.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-28T01:19:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-7412>

Claudia - are your sex hormones near the top of the range? My DHEA & pregnenolone, for instance, are within the lab range, but nowhere near th enumbers needed for optimal. I can dig up and post the optimal numbers (from Dr. Dzugan, I believe) if you think it would be helpful. It seems most often that weight gain during the LR is due to hormonal imbalance, and especially from sex hormone imbalance.

The weight gain doesn't worry me at all. I'm so used to eating barely nothing and the weight going up and down by 5-7kg without too much effort.

### claudia rowe — 2012-03-29T01:46:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-8005>

> **MamaGrok;7450 wrote:** Claudia - are your sex hormones near the top of the range? My DHEA & pregnenolone, for instance, are within the lab range, but nowhere near th numbers needed for optimal. I can dig up and post the optimal numbers (from Dr. Dzugan, I believe) if you think it would be helpful. It seems most often that weight gain during the LR is due to hormonal imbalance, and especially from sex hormone imbalance.

That's a good point, mamagrok. I have sort of guinea-pigged myself over the years to get the right levels for each. Free T3 I keep bang on mid-range, for instance. DHEA slightly towards the top end, pregnenolone way too high, TSH really low, testosterone slightly on the low side etc for no scientific reason but more on the basis of how I feel!

I've always been low carb and always had big energy reserves but have been eating tiny amounts of food for many years now because of weight gain problems. My rT3, not unexpectedly, is always way too high and every trick and hack I've tried has made no difference whatsoever.

As I said in the previous post, I was very confident that the leptin serum levels would drop with this regime. I wasn't expecting them to go up and by quite a bit.

Be interesting to see if others here are having the same experience. I wonder if it's a "gets worse beofre it gets better" thing. Not very scientific, I know, but I'm hopeful.

Kills 'em instantly. I may need another one an hour later, but I keep at the coconut oil until the cravings subside. This only happened in the first few weeks of LR. I'm in week 7 now & haven't had any cravings for several weeks.

### janagram — 2012-04-19T22:20:21+0300

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-20T01:44:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-16788>

Yes, it IS a snack. It is NOT allowed on the leptin reset unless you have PCOS & subsequent mood and/or energy crashes when you stop snacking and go low carb.

BUT

I used it in the same way as if I did have PCOS, because I had incessant cravings leading to binges that kept me from being able to follow the LR if I \*didn't\* use CO. So it was a temporary means to an end (by no means intended to be a permanent habit) that kept me from falling off the wagon in the first few weeks.

### Destiny — 2012-04-20T02:04:47+0300

I started taking a PS supplement - 200 mg and magnesium malate in the evening. I also tested my hormones at the same time so I also went to see my OBGYN to get an estrogen supplement since I was very low on that. In the mid of February, I started with CT. All in all, I sleep better and feel better but it is a process.

### ealachan — 2012-04-24T23:18:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-18454>

Hi all - quick question: I have been getting a sensation of heat on my upper back about an hour after eating almost every meal for the past week or so. My skin doesn't actually FEEL hot, but it feels like it's radiating heat, if that makes sense. It's always in the same spot, from the back of my neck down to about halfway between my shoulder blades. It's not at all unpleasant, but it's very noticeable.

Does this have any signifigance with regard to leptin sensitivity, or is something else going on?

For what it's worth, I've been on the reset for almost 8 weeks, have lost some weight (25ish lbs by my home scale but only about 10 by my doctor's scale) and inches (3" off my belly, two or so inches off my hips / lower stomach, two-ish inches around my ribcage, an inch or so off each thigh, inch or so off my upper arms), generally sleep well, have a generally stable mood and no carb cravings (the only cravings I tend to have are for shrimp, oddly). I've had a wicked urge the last week or so to lift heavy things, but I've been talking myself out of it with the rationale that I don't want to set myself back.

edited to add: I was doing regular spot icing 3-4 times a week for an hour at a time up until last week, when I started doing cold-water soaks (legs only...bathtub is not fat-girl-friendly...haha). I've definitely noticed increased cold tolerance in the last couple weeks.

### MartiD — 2012-04-30T17:41:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-your-leptin-is-reset-and-how-about-relapse.189/post-20076>

I am trying to figure out where to go from here. I've been on the reset since end of October after finishing second round of HCG. That fall I dropped another 12 lbs. I stared CT in early February and am down another couple. I do feel like I have stalled a bit and wonder if or when I will become LS. I look over the signs of improvement from the Quick Start guide:

Signs itâ€™s working:

Change in your sweating pattern -_not sure about this cause I not sure what that means...more sweating, less sweating?

Energy levels will rise - _no doubt I have more energy than I did six months ago!

Hunger and cravings will become less and eventually disappear -definately less...cravings are controlled!When you awaken you will feel refreshed and well rested -I've hoped out of bed for 6 months and had BAB done in 30 minutes!_

Men: quicker weight loss

Women: Mood changes (calmer/sleepy), then sleep will improve. Clothes will fit differently and weight loss will occur if you continue the program

Mood is definately better..just ask DH and kids! Sleep like a rock! No additional weight loss but I have gone down a size in jeans since February even though weight isn't much different._

I have started doing some heavy weights at home and adding in a 2 mile walk with sprints ever few day in last two weeks.

Only mild soreness in thighs day after sprints. I have this strange urge to exercise. Thoughts? How do you really know if you are LS without the blood test? I am struggling to find a local Doctor so not sure when I will get next round of tests done.

Other stats that might be useful..I'm 43 yr old female, 5' 6", current weight 172.8. According to BMI I am still overweight although I know that chart doesn't really mean anything. I have had a particial hysterectomy (still have ovaries), no hormones issues, Vit D good, iron good, thyroid levels in normal range ( I know that doesn't mean much!) Besides two back operations in 13 years I am healthy!

# Thread: paleo doc
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-doc.190/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sisterclare — 2012-03-16T02:51:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-doc.190/post-1202>

I am about to get my tests done. I have a normal doctor who hasn't a clue on nutrition but has agreed to help me on anything. I have been seeing him for twenty years. My question is: I am getting on a paleo diet and was wondering if I need an actual paleo doc? There is a chiropractor in town who consults and lives the paleo diet but not sure if I can afford her because the insurance only covers so much if it is a chiropractor. I am hypothyroidal, have multiple chemical sensitivities, and also electrically sensitive, moreso now with smart meters. Thanks.

### MJ* — 2012-03-16T02:56:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-doc.190/post-1208>

Could you stick with your doctor that you trust and have a consult with the chiropractor, explaining your needs and see if you could meet with her to go over the diet and see what she recommends if anything?

### JC2K — 2012-03-16T19:17:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-doc.190/post-1605>

I'd do what MJ says. You've got a relationship with the doctor, and they're at least open to listening to it. I bet the #1 thing that will really get your doc on board is how your tests/health change though.

### nonchalant — 2012-03-16T23:34:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-doc.190/post-1716>

You are very lucky to have your normal doc, who won't fight you on tests.

### sisterclare — 2012-03-17T03:07:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-doc.190/post-1813>

> **MJ\*;1209 wrote:** Could you stick with your doctor that you trust and have a consult with the chiropractor, explaining your needs and see if you could meet with her to go over the diet and see what she recommends if anything?

That is a good idea. I might do these tests over time as needed, and go from there.

### L8F — 2012-03-18T19:23:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-doc.190/post-2423>

> **MJ\*;1209 wrote:** Could you stick with your doctor that you trust and have a consult with the chiropractor, explaining your needs and see if you could meet with her to go over the diet and see what she recommends if anything?

+1 on this

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-03-16T16:21:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapted-mattress-pad.201/post-1445>

Remember that the studies that have been done on CT. 60 degree temps or lower for hours showed activation of BAT. This would do the same thing.

The effect is cumulative.

Follow the protocol to reach cold adaption, then reduce your bath to once a week along with daily ice packs and nightly sleeping on the cold mattress.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-21T00:04:52+0200

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-28T16:15:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapted-mattress-pad.201/post-7698>

I received the cool/heat sleep pad yesterday. The unit is relatively quiet. You hear air pushing but it sounds something similar to a ceiling fan. As far as how cold the mattress gets, not very cold. I turned the cooling unit down to 50 and the unit never reached that. I never did receive the second one I ordered so I could compare the two. Thought maybe there was an issue with the pad itself and then I pulled out the manual and read this:

IMPORTANT Thermoelectric performance is a function of ambient room temperature. For example, if the room temperature is 80 F, the cool-heat sleep pad can cool down to 62 F(18'degrees difference). So at 80 F room temperature, if you set the powere unit to 55 F, the sleep pad will cool to 62 F.

So that being said, my room temp was 64 F. I had the unit set to 50 F and it never even reached past 60 F. So I called the company and they were good about sending a shipping label so I could pack it up and exchange for another unit. If the next one behaves the same way then I will just return it for good. But if you wanted to cool down while sleeping I think it would be good. The goal for me is to get my skin tempeture down and I don't think this pad is designed for that.

I'm guessing the seat pad is the same way.

>
>
>
> So that being said, my room temp was 64 F. I had the unit set to 50 F and it never even reached past 60 F. So I called the company and they were good about sending a shipping label so I could pack it up and exchange for another unit. If the next one behaves the same way then I will just return it for good. But if you wanted to cool down while sleeping I think it would be good. ### Lyndra — 2012-05-29T06:30:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapted-mattress-pad.201/post-28139>

I bought one of these [http://www.solutions.com/1/1/3334-cool-gelmat.html](http://http://www.solutions.com/1/1/3334-cool-gelmat.html) in an effort to sleep through my hot flashes. It definitely helps cool off my body. When I roll over from one side to the other, I can feel heat radiating off the other side. The downside is it has a weird feel to it - you will definitely know you are sleeping on something. I've tried it underneath the top sheet and it still feels strange, but not quite as much as when lying directly on it. It will not make you icy cold but it does a reasonable job of cooling.

### angieonthegogo — 2012-05-30T03:06:38+0300

### David — 2012-06-04T02:32:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapted-mattress-pad.201/post-29272>

> **Anna K.;29316 wrote:** anybody else tried it?

Yes, I have been using mine since the end of March. It does cool me down at night, but is nowhere near being a substitute for an ice vest (which I also use daily, when possible) and I believe the pad cannot approach an ice-cold bath for effective CT. However, I imagine that I am getting chronic cold exposure, at least. A definite plus is that I seem to need less A/C. Also, my GF uses hers to HEAT and reports that it is much more effective than an electric blanket.

# Thread: Lipid Panel Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipid-panel-question.202/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-16T15:10:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipid-panel-question.202/post-1414>

I don't have perfect health... that's what I'm here for  I've been eating paleo for a couple of years and recently started the LR and have been doing CT for a couple of weeks. Little more background, I completed a round of HCG in January and in the middle of Dec I had labs drawn. My Dr. had requested new labs because 6 months prior my cholesterol was too high for her liking. She told me the same 'ol Western Medicine advice: eat low fat and reduce my calories and exercise. So, my diet a couple weeks prior to the draw was zero fat and very low carb. However, my labs were worse then they were 6 months earlier. Here's where I'd like some advice as I'm not too concerned with cholesterol, however, I do have a lot of heart disease in my family and don't want to completely ignore the figures either.

Cholesterol Total: 266

Triglycerides: 76

HDL: 87

VLDL: 15

LDL: 164

C-Reactive: .74

Little more history. 1 1/2 years ago I was diagnosed with adrenal fatigue as my cortisol numbers are very low. They are coming back and I'm hoping with CT I'll be able to heal them completely. However, currently if I don't take my supplements I can barely make it through the day. I do know that your body will make extra cholesterol to try to compensate for the low cortisol.

Any thoughts??? I have to return to the Dr next week and am preparing myself for her to chew me out for not dieting and exercising enough. Oh, BTW... I'm 5'2" weigh 138 and have more muscle than most women.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-16T15:31:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipid-panel-question.202/post-1422>

Your cholesterol isnt' bad at all... your HDL is great, your LDL is fine... I'd worry about your trigs... my first question would be - were the LDL's a direct measure or calculated? that coupled w/your HDL and your trigs - can skew it... your CRP - was it a hs CRP? you really want to get your hsCRP as low as posible

most likely since you've got cortisol issues - you've got pregnenolone steal occurring - your cortisol problems - tell your liver to make more ldl - so it can convert it to pregnenolone and then use it to convert to the sex hormones - but b/c you've got cortisol issues - the pregnenolone is being shunted to support cortisol production. this in turn will turn off your hypothamlus -pituary and thyroid off - basically you're body is just idling and can't get in gear. its functioning just enough thats it.

I'd ask for a full sex hormone panel (Pregnenolone, alderstone, testosterone, progesterone, hs Estridol, total estrogens, prolactin, free T3, Reverse T3, Thyroid antibodies (both TPO and AB), DHEA-S, IGF-1, homocysteine, hs CRP, fasting blood glucose and fasting insulin

then you can begin to see the full picture.

the following link on MDA is Griff's Cholesterol primer - read it and figure out what your ratios are - that's a better indicator of what's going on...[http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread4723.html](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread4723.html)

additionally - you can always ask for a CIMT - which is a scan of the carotid arteries - which will tell you if you have any thickening, plaque etc - and is a better indicator of potential heart issues.

fwiw - a history of FHC (Familial hyper-cholesterol ) while it scares the crap out of CW - in the hands of a doc knowledgeable about lipids - will just go ok - you're mom/dad/brother/sister/aunt/grandfather etc have FHC - and no cardiac events - ok... no biggie, and then they'll just monitor you closely... one things you want to do is avoid statins - they aren't beneficial to women at all... and recent research is showing they can degrade the health of women....

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-18T14:17:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipid-panel-question.202/post-2348>

I really appreciate your insight. I read Griff's information a couple of months ago... great stuff! And, yes, when I use the Iranian formula my overall score is much better.

I do have a couple of questions though. You said you would be more concerned about triglycerides. Are you suggesting that mine are too high, or that is where I should focus my concern. I thought a score under a 100 for triglycerides was good??

Also, I will get a copy of my latest hormone panel. If there is a pregnenolone steal how does one go about fixing that, or will CT eventually right everything? I didn't mention that I currently take bio-identical progesterone on days 14-28.

Thanks so much for all you help!

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-19T15:17:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipid-panel-question.202/post-2711>

If I remember correctly DR K likes to Trigs below 60

Pregnenolone steal - needs to focus on fixing the adrenals, and the use of the hormones... CT will work...but there are other ways... post what labs you've got and we'll get some more ppl to hack them...


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: How do you feel right before getting in the tub?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evo Mama — 2012-03-16T15:20:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1418>

I ask because so far, every time, my heart is beating really hard and I feel a bit of anxiety, I suppose about the initial plunge (which is really not that bad - it's my mind that thinks it will be worse than it actually is). I know Dr. K said that the body can warm up in anticipation of a cold bath, but I don't think that this (high HR) is what he meant.

I'm concerned that this is causing a nighttime spike in my cortisol. Once I'm in the bath, my HR slows down and bit, and by the time I'm done, I'm perfectly calm. Most of the time, I'm so relaxed I don't really even want to get out. I end my sessions because it's late and I need to get to sleep, more so than for any other reason.

I should mention I've only done 3 ice baths so far, so I suspect this response to getting in will diminish in intensity, but I wanted to see what others think re: cortisol and if that could be an issue. I should also mention I have no trouble falling asleep after bath.

Has anyone else been experiencing this? What are your experiences prior to taking the plunge?

What do ya'll think about the possible cortisol surge? Problematic or no biggie since I calm down after I'm in the bath for a few minutes?

Any thoughts would be GREATLY appreciated. Have I said lately how much I love this board?

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-16T15:27:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1420>

I don't have a tub so I've been doing showers for almost 4 weeks, and I still get some anxiety and have to consciously make myself relax instead of clench up. I keep reading of people "loving" it, but that hasn't happened for me so far. I love the feeling afterwards though! In the hours after a cold shower, I feel just awesome. I've been using a wrapped cold pack on my back and tummy too, on the drive to work, and that is no problem whatsoever.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-16T15:41:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1423>

I'm not having the anxiety you're describing, but I do really have to make myself do it. Sort of like mentally prep for it and just do it. Once I'm in, I'm good for 45 min or more because, like you've said, it's relaxing. I would hope that the relaxation would counter any cortisol spike the anxiety might cause, but of course am not sure. Sounds like a good question for Dr. Kruse. I expect this will get easier as your body adjusts and realizes there is benefit to the cold water. I've found that if I read a little of the CT protocol posts or their comments before I get in that it steels my resolve to take a CT bath. Maybe that would calm your anxiety about it somewhat? Or you could always go the desensitization route and go back to face dunks and/or spot-icing, _then_ get in the bath, say, within minutes of each other. The mental system shock would be over by the time you lowered yourself into the water?

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-16T16:13:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1440>

I don't have the anxiety either but I also have to get myself mentally prepared. I'm more of the hot bath kind of gal, never really take showers just hot baths. It's been a real adjustment for me. Sure wish I enjoyed the cold baths like I enjoy the hot ones! I try not to think about it too much and just try to get it done daily. Been trying to get it done twice a day but sometimes I just don't have the mental energy knowing what I have to go through.

### MartiD — 2012-03-16T16:20:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1443>

I actually look forward to them now...sick I know! LOL But I feel really good for several hours afterwards so I think that is the reason. I do a 10-15 minute in the morning and then a longer one at night but I do it early enough that I have at least two hours before I go to bed because I do have lots of energy after. I do sleep good as long as I don't take the bath to late. I have been doing them 3 weeks and my recovery time is much shorter...last night I didn't think my legs and butt were cold when I got out so dh felt them and said I was freezing. It felt like a sauna in our house when I came out. I had no feelings of being cold at all afterwards which is new just in last fews days. Of course it might be due to exceptionally warm weather here and the house is warm.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-16T16:26:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1452>

I have found a big difference with showers vs. soaks. I think showers are harder! Plus, I'm a demon with my infrared thermometer and it never gets my skin to the zone (50-55), even when coming out at 45. Seems like just enough time between drops for skin to heat back up.

On the upside, I've lost weight before using CW and the jillian-michaels exercise crazyness. It's nowhere near as awful as heading into another 2 hr workout.

I am going by the "this is a step to adapted" and assuming it won't be so horrid once I get there!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-16T16:28:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1454>

> **Cavemam;1454 wrote:** I have found a big difference with showers vs. soaks. I think showers are harder! Plus, I'm a demon with my infrared thermometer and it never gets my skin to the zone (50-55), even when coming out at 45. Seems like just enough time between drops for skin to heat back up.
>
>
>
> On the upside, I've lost weight before using CW and the jillian-michaels exercise crazyness. It's nowhere near as awful as heading into another 2 hr workout.
>
>
>
> I am going by the "this is a step to adapted" and assuming it won't be so horrid once I get there!

Good point, Cavemam! I've been through the hours and hours of exercise to get results too, and I'd take a few minutes of 'mental prep' and seconds of sucking in breath over that chronic exercise ordeal any day.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-16T16:59:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1478>

Thanks, all!

I'm going to start reading a bit of the CT blog series before my bath (I printed them out so I can read them by candlelight in my womancave). I think I'll also to try the face dunk to see if that helps calm me down. Hopefully, the relaxation in tub is cancelling the cortisol surge I feel before getting in.

### Croak — 2012-03-16T17:02:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1482>

Don't do the plunge then. Get in the empty tub and start filling with you in it. I think you'll find that's much less of a shock, especially if you're early in the adaptation process.

You can take the edge off even further by having an half-inch or so hot water in the tub when you start adding the cold. The hot water tends to float over the denser cold water, so it acts as a buffer as the water level rises, but eventually reaches equilibrium.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-16T17:03:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1483>

> **Croak;1484 wrote:** Don't do the plunge then. Get in the empty tub and start filling with you in it. I think you'll find that's much less of a shock, especially if you're early in the adaptation process.
>
>
>
> You can take the edge off even further by having an half-inch or so hot water in the tub when you start adding the cold. The hot water tends to float over the denser cold water, so it acts as a buffer as the water level rises, but eventually reaches equilibrium.

Thanks. I'll give this a try today. Smart thinking, Croak.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-16T17:16:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1491>

> **Croak;1484 wrote:** Don't do the plunge then. Get in the empty tub and start filling with you in it. I think you'll find that's much less of a shock, especially if you're early in the adaptation process.
>
>
>
> You can take the edge off even further by having an half-inch or so hot water in the tub when you start adding the cold. The hot water tends to float over the denser cold water, so it acts as a buffer as the water level rises, but eventually reaches equilibrium.

This is the method I used initially and I must say it really, really helped make the start of the whole thing a little easier, now that I look back. I've since abandoned it but notice that stepping into a few inches of cold water as it fills still takes my breath away for a second or two and not so much when I sit down and then begin running water in.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-16T17:28:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1501>

Part of my anxiety involves worrying about doing this long term. Once I get in the tub, it's not so bad but I beforehand, when I think I'll be doing this every day for who knows how long, it's more daunting. I've decided it's like changing any kind of habit - think about doing it just this one time; not tomorrow, next week, etc. One day at a time. That's how I changed my eating habits. I didn't focus on what I could never eat again, I'd just think about what I was doing at this meal.

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-16T17:45:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1521>

Anyone tried adding ice after getting in the tub? Seems like it would be less of a shock to do it that way.

Edit: I didn't see Croak's comment. Ditto what he said!

### MJ* — 2012-03-16T18:03:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1541>

> **Evo Mama;1420 wrote:** I ask because so far, every time, my heart is beating really hard and I feel a bit of anxiety, I suppose about the initial plunge (which is really not that bad - it's my mind that thinks it will be worse than it actually is). I know Dr. K said that the body can warm up in anticipation of a cold bath, but I don't think that this (high HR) is what he meant.

Did you start with the face plunging? There is a reason for the protocol...and for the record I haven't started the cold therapy except for cooling my showers to build up to it, and I'm reading a lot...with the face plunge in ice water, you active the dive reflex and that will lower your heart rate, maybe that could help reduce your anxiety some once you are ready to get in the tub. I like the idea of starting in an inch of warm water in the tub too and adding water while you are in it - it's less of a shock, plus you could gradually get used to it, make it cool for a couple of day, then get colder over time and eventually add ice, and you can wear neoprene socks and a hat while you adjust to doing this...

### Birdy — 2012-03-16T18:06:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1543>

I am already a swimmer and go through this whenever I get into the water. Just dive right in. If I have to think of anything, I think of how hard it was to be on a cw low-fat reducing diet, and then a wave of relief passes over me. I think of it more like shiatsu. It hurts good, and nothing beats good sleep.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-17T00:49:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1753>

> **MJ\*;1543 wrote:** Did you start with the face plunging? There is a reason for the protocol...and for the record I haven't started the cold therapy except for cooling my showers to build up to it, and I'm reading a lot...with the face plunge in ice water, you active the dive reflex and that will lower your heart rate, maybe that could help reduce your anxiety some once you are ready to get in the tub. I like the idea of starting in an inch of warm water in the tub too and adding water while you are in it - it's less of a shock, plus you could gradually get used to it, make it cool for a couple of day, then get colder over time and eventually add ice, and you can wear neoprene socks and a hat while you adjust to doing this...

Yes, I started with face plunges, so I've been basically following the protocol. I agree that's key.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-17T00:52:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1754>

Okay, today I ate sashimi, did face plunges for 10 minutes, filled the tub about 4 inches, got in and filled the rest of the way. My heart rate was up a little, but nothing like before. Thanks!

### Croak — 2012-03-17T01:06:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-right-before-getting-in-the-tub.203/post-1757>
