# Thread: Sleep Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-question.230/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evo Mama — 2012-03-17T11:47:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-question.230/post-1910>

The time change has really screwed my sleep up!

So, I'm going to bed a little later than I used to, mostly because I'm doing CT after I get my son to sleep, and I'm waking up an hour earlier because of the time change. My bio clock hasn't figured out that the external clocks have shifted. So, before CT, I was sleeping 8-9 hours, and now it's closer to 7-7 1/2. I wake up refreshed and my energy throughout the day is good. Do you think the less sleep is okay, or should I be trying to rework things to get more sleep?

### marthabob2u — 2012-03-17T15:24:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-question.230/post-1955>

> **Evo Mama;1915 wrote:** The time change has really screwed my sleep up!
>
>
>
> So, I'm going to bed a little later than I used to, mostly because I'm doing CT after I get my son to sleep, and I'm waking up an hour earlier because of the time change. My bio clock hasn't figured out that the external clocks have shifted. So, before CT, I was sleeping 8-9 hours, and now it's closer to 7-7 1/2. I wake up refreshed and my energy throughout the day is good. Do you think the less sleep is okay, or should I be trying to rework things to get more sleep?

I'd say that f you are feeling good during the day the amount of sleep you are getting is fine. Your body will likely let you know if you should be catching more winks. It usually takes a few days to get the rhythm going again after a time change.

### tellmisty — 2012-03-17T18:01:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-question.230/post-2026>

I find that after CTing (and time change), my nighttime sleep seems to be dropping to 7 hours, too; might be normal to get less if your sleep is better ...

# Thread: Gained weight after signs of LS?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-17T14:44:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1940>

Hi all,

I'm making my debut.  I've been reaping so much info from you all, and finally decided to post.

I started the Leptin Reset the week of Christmas, (immediately after finishing a round \[my first, and last round\] of hcg injections) and began seeing all the signs of being Leptin Sensitive at the beginning of February. I stabilized at my last injection weight of 116.4 throughout this whole time. (and aside from discovering a pork sensitivity, felt really great!)

In the past 4 weeks or so, I've started including HIIT 1 or 2X a week, along with the LHT program from MDA.

Now in the past couple weeks I've started to 'look bigger' in the mirror, and now I realized the scale is up 5 lbs!

The only things I've changed in the past couple weeks are :

\- the continued workouts, as described.

\- eating more butter

\- eating a slight amount of seeds and nuts (like a mini-handful, but most days of the week)

Now I think that I may have a leaky gut, since having 10 years of experience with anorexia/buliemia during my teen years (I'm 29 yrs now), and also a whole host of allergies (most are gone now!)

Does anyone have any clue as to what is going on? Or has anyone experienced anything similar, as far as the weight gain?

I have yet to switch over to the post-leptin eating plan, although I am often times just eating 2 meals a day, and my breakfasts have gotten a bit smaller 'on their own'.

And I have cut my afternoon coffee since a couple days ago, and the seeds and nuts. But still eating vlc (only a few green veggies, fruit only a couple times a week)

Sorry about the novel, and thank you in advance for any feedback you have!!

### tweety — 2012-03-17T14:59:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1944>

Some people are sensitive enough to fruits and nuts that it can definitely stall weight loss, if not lead to bloating or weight gain. On the other hand, are you sure its fat gain? IOW it could be muscle gain from the workouts. How do your clothes fit? Some things to consider here, but I am sure you will get other good advice from the more seasoned resetters.

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-17T15:07:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1945>

Thank you, tweety, for your response!

I haven't taken measurements in the past few days, and my clothes are fitting alright, although I do see my belly sticking out a bit. It almost seems like bloat, since I used to be able to see definition in my arms, but now they just seem bigger (like the muscle is being masked right now!)

I appreciate your feedback, and I will continue to cut the nuts and also really watch the fruit....funny thing is, I don't usually even have a 'taste' for the fruit, which is GREAT in my eyes!

Thanks again

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-17T15:08:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1947>

I also just seem to be bigger all over.....does this seem like a sign of 'bloat', or is it more a sign of fat gain?

### L8F — 2012-03-17T15:30:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1958>

Hmmm, wondering if it is the HIIT--too often? And or nut/seed issue causing bloating? How is your gut? Have you had any testing?

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-17T15:46:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1965>

> **L8F;1963 wrote:** Hmmm, wondering if it is the HIIT--too often? And or nut/seed issue causing bloating? How is your gut? Have you had any testing?

I have not had any testing...

My gut is better than it was, but still not optimal. Have been constipated lately. (I had a long history of IBS along with food allergies, and as soon as I cut the grains this dramatically improved)

I may try to cut the HIIT for now....it just feels so good to move! Would you say that the LHT is okay to keep? I'd like to try to continue doing something, as long as it isn't screwing me up in the long run.

I would have never imagined that nuts/seeds could cause this much drama in my body....but maybe it's possible?

Ideally I would have liked to lose another couple pounds down from my 116.4, but now that I am up the 5 lbs, the 116 sounds so ideal!

Thank you for your feedback, L8H!

### tellmisty — 2012-03-17T16:02:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1971>

Are you doing CT / ice baths, JoyfulJoyful? I'd do the leaky guy prescription stat and add in the ice baths (I think CT is what ultimately resolves most things and melts the fat). Ask Jack if you should stop doing HIIT ... I'm not sure that will serve you in the long run ...

I don't know if Jack has his bone broth recipe on that prescription, but he told me to do it for my leaky gut - a cup day for a week, then three times weekly for the next 2 months. It's organic beef broth (I got mine from Trader Joes) and grass-fed beef bones (got from Whole Foods) with a splash of apple cider vinegar, cooked for 20 hours on low in the crock pot. You can add in other "soup" ingredients if you want (onions, garlic, veggies, beef, etc). Broth is good in the fridge for 4 days; I had to freeze some.

### L8F — 2012-03-17T16:37:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1988>

Agree with bone broth.

You can add in other "soup" ingredients if you want (onions, garlic, veggies, beef, etc). Broth is good in the fridge for 4 days; I had to freeze some.

Thanks for your advice, tellmisty!

I just started slowly doing CT. I haven't done the face dunks, but I started with soaking my feet with cold tap water (about 58 degrees from the tap) for a couple days, and then doing ice packs on my belly for 30+ minutes for a couple days. I just did my first cold bath today, straight cold tap water (no ice), for about 10 minutes. Then I tried a colder than usual shower. I got hives all over my legs! I will persevere though, it is really inspiring to hear all the success stories.....I take it that you have done CT?

Thank you very much for the bone broth recipe....Can you tell me what amount of each ingredient? (how much beef broth and what quantity of bones?)

I will try that right away!

And thanks, L8F for seconding the bone broth!

I really appreciate all the advice!!

### tellmisty — 2012-03-17T18:46:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2046>

I asked Jack for amounts, and he seemed to think it didn't matter. I fill the crock pot with broth (to get as many cups as possible) and just toss in a bone. LOL! A friend told me to add the apple cider vinegar, as it pulls micro-nutrients from the bone.

Jack told me to follow a Paleo diet, no nightshades (peppers, tomato, eggplants ... anything else?), no dairy (except eggs and ghee), and no gluten for Leaky Gut. I'm not sure how universal his recommendations were; I followed his instructions to a T. Seems the last round of labs had indications that my gut was better and thyroid was healing; yay!!!

### Grottenolm — 2012-03-18T03:39:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2235>

This sounds like inflammation from the nuts, possibly. The exact same things happen to me even if I consume a small handful of nuts a few days in a row- all over size gain and water retention, distended belly, sometimes accompanied by acne. However, as soon as I stop the nuts the bloating and size go away in about 3-4 days. Unfortunately I love nuts and sometimes eat them anyway, lol!

### TheKid — 2012-03-18T09:17:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2317>

You know, I've been wondering the same thing about gaining and stalling out. I myself ahve been on the Leptin Rx for 6 weeks. Dropped a couple pounds max, but generally stayed within the same average weight range. I stopped all workouts exept riding my bike to work. I was really puzzled at the lack of continued weight loss.

I started CT two weeks ago and suddenly got crazy carb cravings. They got so intense couple of days ago that I succumbed and had a 4 donuts and large latte. Doubled back the next day with 2 donuts and latte.

Almost IMMEDIATELY I bloated. Pants fit tighter. Little more noticeable belly fat. It was awful.

What puzzled me was that I had previously lost cravings altogether and in fact was to the point where I frequently felt OK to skip lunch and just eat dinner. Other signs of LS were not really there yet; but I thought I was further along in the process. Can't figure out why the cravings came and why the binge had such a profound impact. Back to the drawing board.

I had the same thing happen last week. I had a bunch of fruit \[wonderful peaches at a friend's\] and then couldn't stop eating for 2 days - it was as if a switch had been flipped and I had no control at all. quite disturbing.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-18T17:13:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2388>

> **TheKid;2324 wrote:** Can't figure out why the cravings came and why the binge had such a profound impact. Back to the drawing board.

it\`s what you chose to binge on that had that effect: Wheat, vegetable oil trans fats and sugar and plenty of each. to that you added a side helping of alpha-casein through the latte which is often a cross reactant to those who are gluten intolerant.

the bad news is you were bloated and felt crappy and are starting again from step 1. the good news is you discovered you are gluten intolerant and likely should avoid dairy so that may make the rest of the journey easier for you as now you know you can\`t have these foods without doing your body harm.

my suggestion is grieve the loss of these foods, seriously until you truly accept that you cannot have gluten again you will keep doing this to yourself, and move on.

I find for myself the best way to drop the cravings is to eat 80+% fat for a day or two. also the next time you do crave carbs go with less harmful versions such as sweet potato drenched in butter, frozen berries with coconut milk (makes a hell of a sherbert), 99% chocolate, a latte made with really good, organic, single estate, wet process coffee and coconut milk. any or all of these are far better for you than the donuts and latte

### David — 2012-03-18T17:16:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2389>

I had been experiencing a steady one-to-two pound weight loss each week, until I started CT a couple of weeks ago. Not only has the weight loss stopped, but my scale shows a net gain of four pounds over the past few days. I have not noticed any particular cravings and my meals are unaltered, still < 25 carbs per day.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-18T17:26:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2390>

did you get tested before or have any idea what needs fixing

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2631>

>

> I really appreciate the advice and I am very excited to implement it all!!

If you have leaky gut, Jack advises no dairy except ghee (pasturized butter) and eggs. Since you are sensitive to eggs, leave them off, and that might be confirmation that diary is no-no. I think he'd recommend coconut milk instead of heavy whipping cream.

Once your leaky gut heals, I believe you should be able to try adding these items back in to see how your body handles it ... should be okay!

I haven't given in to them (have upped my fats when I feel them) but I find it tricky that I'm doing a bit of 'white knuckling' again re: diet. (I haven't gained weight, just the cravings.)

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-19T17:14:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2752>

Nope, don't think it's a coincidence at all. I've definitely noticed cravings for things I haven't wanted in months as well. my guess is that the toxin release is putting those substances in our systems which is triggering the cravings.

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-19T18:13:32+0200

However, as soon as I stop the nuts the bloating and size go away in about 3-4 days. Unfortunately I love nuts and sometimes eat them anyway, lol!

Thanks for your input, Grottenolm...I cut out the nuts 2 days ago, and after the first day the scale went \*up\* .2 lbs, but today it is down 1.6 lbs. I know what you mean, I would hate to have to give up nuts! But if its necessary then I will do it, since if they are causing inflammation then that would mean that my body really doesnt like them! I want to deal with the leaky gut so that hopefully someday soon I can eat all the things that I am forbidden right now!

Thanks for sharing your experience....and it's great that you know what causes you the inflammation. It is so hard when you cant pinpoint what is going on!

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-19T18:25:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2790>

Dropped a couple pounds max, but generally stayed within the same average weight range. I stopped all workouts exept riding my bike to work. I was really puzzled at the lack of continued weight loss.
>

> I started CT two weeks ago and suddenly got crazy carb cravings. They got so intense couple of days ago that I succumbed and had a 4 donuts and large latte. Doubled back the next day with 2 donuts and latte.
>

> Almost IMMEDIATELY I bloated. Pants fit tighter. Little more noticeable belly fat. It was awful.
>
>
>
> What puzzled me was that I had previously lost cravings altogether and in fact was to the point where I frequently felt OK to skip lunch and just eat dinner. Other signs of LS were not really there yet; but I thought I was further along in the process. Can't figure out why the cravings came and why the binge had such a profound impact. Back to the drawing board.

Try not to be discouraged.....the way I look at it is this: at least you have confirmation now that gluten is no good for you, along with the other questionable ingredients in the donuts, as ColleenM said. I had some bad reactions to eggs and pork earlier (and have had more than my share of awful experiences with gluten, which I willingly subjected myself to!) and it was hard to feel the discomfort and see the scale go up (and my clothes fit so tight), but I really learned then that I shouldn't be touching that stuff. It strengthened my willpower in the end.

I have also wondered why the CT causes the cravings, and I guess we should be encouraged to know that it IS doing something, in getting all this junk stirred up and out of our bodies!

Keep your chin up! Youre doing a good thing!

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-19T18:33:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2794>

> **tellmisty;2638 wrote:** If you have leaky gut, Jack advises no dairy except ghee (pasturized butter) and eggs. Since you are sensitive to eggs, leave them off, and that might be confirmation that diary is no-no. I think he'd recommend coconut milk instead of heavy whipping cream.

Tellmisty, thanks for your advice!

I did cut out the heavy whipping cream starting yesterday, and WOW I can't believe how tasty the coconut milk is!!! I would love to eat the whole can!  I don't feel deprived with it in my coffee, and I looked forward to my morning coffee this morning, knowing that I could add that in, AND that it wasn't bad for me! (i hope!?)

I also cut out the butter, and bought some ghee yesterday.

I never thought that since I was sensitive to eggs (which was also a shock for me to discover....I ate so many eggs previously! Like 5 a day while pregnant with my second son...And the interesting thing is that I had chronic back pain for years, and once the eggs were gone, I've since been fine!! Thank GOD!!) that it could also indicate a dairy sensitivity. I really appreciate you sharing your tips and input. And it sounds like you are very driven and applied to doing things right to get healthy. it is very encouraging and inspirational, to hear that you did things 'to a T'. And how awesome to hear that you are feeling better and seeing positive results in your labs. That is so cool. I also want to do things right and not waste any more time going 'half-way' and never seeing any results! I'm in this and already seeing SO many benefits. I'm so grateful!!

Thanks for sharing what Jack shared with you also, and I believe that I will benefit from the advice! I'm hoping to make the bone broth tonight...yay!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-19T20:04:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2853>

> **ColleenM;2760 wrote:** Nope, don't think it's a coincidence at all. I've definitely noticed cravings for things I haven't wanted in months as well. my guess is that the toxin release is putting those substances in our systems which is triggering the cravings.

Makes sense.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-19T21:32:25+0200

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-ct-make-living-in-a-hot-humid-place-worse.235/post-1952>

Michele

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-ct-make-living-in-a-hot-humid-place-worse.235/post-1956>

No it won't. Basics law of thermodynamics as you use CT to become cold adapted your body's energy systems will become more efficient, making it easier to cool you in warm weather, and warm you in cold weather...

CT will make your boy more efficient which will = greater temperature tolerances.

Adam

### Phoenix — 2012-03-17T18:17:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-ct-what-is-the-prescription.236/post-2033>

In a nutshell you need to work your way up to 50-55 degree baths. 30 to 40 minutes in the cold water bath is optimal. Dr. Kruse does up to 3 hours. I read somewhere that 15 minutes at 55 degrees is the equivalent of running for 60 minutes.

It sounds a lot harder than it is. I'm a wimp, and I worked my way up to 20 mins at 55 degrees in about 10 days. The link that @JeffieJoe gave you will explain it a lot better than I did.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-17T16:43:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-1992>

So what LHT program do you follow?

PBS fitness? Starting strength?, Body by science? Convict conditioning? Or something completely different?

For the past yr and change I've been doing PB fitness....now I'm transitioning it more toward BbS

What are you doing for your LHT?

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-17T20:22:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-2086>

I have been doing a more advanced version of SS, called Texas Method (TM) for 4 wks. I train 3 x week, basically: 1 moderately heavy, 1 very heavy, 1 moderately light. And pull ups. No conditioning, just some walking here and there. I'm a 40 yo female, not a novice and not young, so SS would not be for me.

### SimonM — 2012-03-17T22:25:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-2117>

OK guys, I \*thought\* I was up on this stuff, but can you explain LHT, PB, SS...?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-18T03:46:04+0200

> **SimonM;2123 wrote:** OK guys, I \*thought\* I was up on this stuff, but can you explain LHT, PB, SS...?
>
>
>

LHT = lift heavy things

SS = starting Strenght

BBS = body by science

CC = convict conditioning

The purpose of the above is 3 things , get strong and fit & LGN (look good naked)

### Glamorama — 2012-03-19T05:21:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-2608>

Gretchen, I started out doing a lot of Jillian Michaels, but now that my focus is on LHT and not HIIT, I'm loosely following the Starting Strengh-schedule/plans, as well as the PBF stuff.

### fabbecky — 2012-03-19T14:13:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-2691>

I have started kettlebell training recently and love it! I am also going to start training for a half marathon. I figure my actual race will be lots of walking and some sprinting.

### Croak — 2012-03-19T17:58:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-2779>

My HT is **me**. You'd have to put a gun to my head to get me lifting weights again, let alone setting foot in a gym. Been there, done that to excess in my youth, don't want any part of that culture these days. This is my journey, and it doesn't require membership fees, lockers, or lycra.

And I know that Grok didn't have kettleballs, barbells, or a row of Nautilus machines.

### hazyjane — 2012-03-21T01:01:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-3609>

I really like kettlebells and I do bodyweight stuff, too, like planks and regular/reverse pushups.

I also love certain isometric moves from Bar Method and certain moves from T-Tapp (not a tap dance workout, lol!). T-Tapp is totally dorky, but Teresa Tapp really knows muscle mechanics and her small moves produce results. She's in fantastic shape.

I like moves that give you the most results in the least amount of time

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-22T19:12:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-4521>

I am looking into kettlebells for a lifting workout. Was a gym rat for years...even had a person trainer for 7 of them. I kinda want to stay away from the gym and maybe just do some things at home. Any suggestions on DVDs or internet programs for how to kettlebell it?

I've heard a lot about T-Tapp too. Since you mentioned it hazyjane which of her DVDs do you recommend?

Thanks!

### MouseBandit — 2019-04-13T23:40:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-268019>

I'd be very interested in what JK has to say about Teresa Tapp and her theories and workout. I'm no fitness expert, but having read a lot of the different fitness books and theories over the years, hers makes, by far, the most sense to me, and it seems to be especially excellent for women. I did HIIT for a long time in my 20s and like Croak, I don't want any part of the gym culture or weight-lifting world (which seems so often to lead to the bodybuilding cult). I think bodyweight exercises and LHT are natural, normal and effective, and I think T-Tapp is both effective and therapeutic in many regards, including electrical flow throughout the body.

### Sun Disciple — 2019-04-14T17:45:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-268043>

> [MouseBandit said:](/goto/post?id=268019)
>
> I'd be very interested in what JK has to say about Teresa Tapp and her theories and workout. I'm no fitness expert, but having read a lot of the different fitness books and theories over the years, hers makes, by far, the most sense to me, and it seems to be especially excellent for women. I did HIIT for a long time in my 20s and like Croak, I don't want any part of the gym culture or weight-lifting world (which seems so often to lead to the bodybuilding cult). I think bodyweight exercises and LHT are natural, normal and effective, and I think T-Tapp is both effective and therapeutic in many regards, including electrical flow throughout the body.

Whatever you do. do it outside. So far I have messed with bodyweight programs (onnit), functional movement like movenat or ido portal, kettlebells (pavel tasouline} Yoga, Barefoot hiking, Hill sprints and now Im messing with the X3 bar outside for heavier resistance training. I can throw it in a duffle bag and take it outside anywhere. Id also like to try gymnastic rings and set them up outside.

### Bee — 2012-03-17T19:23:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2063>

Anyone know what Dr. K thinks of skin brushing to stimulate the lymph system and get rid of toxins? Is it okay? If not, anyone know why?

### Garfield — 2012-03-17T19:34:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2068>

The only thing I have seen is that Dr. K told someone else **not** to brush their skin before CT.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-18T02:16:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2190>

I've been brushing my skin 2-3 times weekly before normal showers since July.

### Grottenolm — 2012-03-18T03:51:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2243>

Yes, I heard dr k say that you should not do skin brushing before a ct session. I have been skin brushing since July, before showers, and it has done amazing things for my skin! Less dry, nice and smooth, no need to rub much coconut oil on it anymore.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-18T03:52:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2244>

I've brushed off and on over the past 6 yrs. but I love it when I get to go to the Korean day spa b/c for $80 I get a full body scrub down, and full body massage which is approximately 3hrs, and use of the facilities, pools/ showers, tatami mat rooms. Pure bliss

### JC2K — 2012-03-18T04:26:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2279>

I thought it was AFTER CT that Dr K was talking about. Which is more understandable than before. Essentially, it's a bad idea to vigorously rub down the skin after cold swims/hypothermic conditions because of "after drop". What happens is the cold blood from your extremities goes to your core and causes your core temp to drop further. A bad idea, because we don't really want the core temperature to drop a lot, just the skin temperature.

### Mom — 2012-03-18T05:46:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2306>

I had started skin brushing a week or two before trying CT...to try and firm up skin after losing 40lbs. on HHCG. But I have red dots all over my skin now...some look like acne, some are just red spots. I assume it's my skin detoxing, but not sure. I did dry brush before CT bath last night and now I have more spots. If its from releasing toxins, that's good news. But I'm not sure if it's that. Has anyone else had breakouts from dry brushing?

JC2K, I hope you are right and it's alright to do it before CT. I stopped brushing when I started CT, but then thought it made sense to combine it, and I would like to continue both.

### JC2K — 2012-03-19T17:12:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2751>

Hrm, did find where Dr K was answering a question on it, and it was in re. prior to CT. Not sure on the effect of it beforehand. Maybe getting extra circulation into the skin and then getting it cold causes problems?

I don't think there's any problem with you doing skin brushing, just don't do it right before or after the CT.

### Mom — 2012-03-19T23:26:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2953>

> **JC2K;2759 wrote:** Hrm, did find where Dr K was answering a question on it, and it was in re. prior to CT. Not sure on the effect of it beforehand. Maybe getting extra circulation into the skin and then getting it cold causes problems?
>
>
>
> I don't think there's any problem with you doing skin brushing, just don't do it right before or after the CT.

OK, good to know. I am curious why though. I will keep brushing seperate from CT from now on.

# Thread: Is this frostbite??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Souldanzer — 2012-03-17T20:01:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/post-2079>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-17T21:35:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/post-2107>

> **Souldanzer;2085 wrote:** My left leg fell asleep twice during CT without me noticing (I was working on my laptop in a funny position, reaching out of the tub  ).
>
>
>
> Now I have an area next to my shin that is slightly red and rather painful.
>
>
>
> I'm thinking if your leg falls asleep there's reduced blood flow and so this could happen even if otherwise you would've been just fine? I've been CT for a few weeks now and this has never happened before.

Hi Souldanzer, I had this happen to the outside of my thigh early on. When I asked Dr K his question was 'was your leg up against the side of the tub?" It sounds like yours was? I think the cold plus the extra pressure could created lower than needed skin temps and therefore frostbite. Mine was pressed against an ice pack simultaneously (I didn't notice either until after I got out.)

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-17T22:05:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/post-2114>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-18T01:00:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/post-2166>

> **Souldanzer;2120 wrote:** can't quite remember... other than that I was leaning over so this could have totally happened.
>
> I kept the area out of the water for now when I CT. I did add ice blocks last night
>
> Gotta be more careful. Thanks, now I know what to watch for.... the falling asleep thing probably didn't help the matter, either...

I also have a strong feeling that the ice packs you put in coolers get colder than normal ice and also warm slower. I have found that while I e next to my skin starts to melt immediately, the ice packs do not. I think they are a danger for frostbite when compressed. But I can't be sure (though I'm playing it safe from now on...)

### MonteD — 2012-03-18T01:41:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/post-2178>

> **Souldanzer;2085 wrote:** My left leg fell asleep twice during CT without me noticing (I was working on my laptop in a funny position, reaching out of the tub  ).
>
>
>
> Now I have an area next to my shin that is slightly red and rather painful.
>
>
>
> I'm thinking if your leg falls asleep there's reduced blood flow and so this could happen even if otherwise you would've been just fine? I've been CT for a few weeks now and this has never happened before.

Personally I think that ice packs directly against bare skin for prolonged periods are a bit dangerous. Frostbite begins to happen at around 32 degrees. There isn't much of a margin for error at this point. However, what your describing sounds less like frostbite and more like frostnip.

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-18T02:58:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/post-2217>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

# Thread: Bad hormones levels without being leptin resistant?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-hormones-levels-without-being-leptin-resistant.250/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndra — 2012-03-17T22:29:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-hormones-levels-without-being-leptin-resistant.250/post-2118>

Long question coming, here's a summary: leptin resistance means your hormones will be messed up, but is the reverse also true? That is, does having screwed up hormones necessarily mean you are leptin resistant and so you should follow the protocol to reset?

Background: I had a hysterectomy 18 years ago and was continuously on synthetic hormones until Feb 2011. All last spring, summer & fall, I was a mess in terms of hot flashes, lack of sleep & energy, and poor recovery from exercise. I briefly went on synthetic hormones starting in October, had labs done in December and started bioidentical hormones & supplements in January 2012.

The results from the labs were mixed - some good, some bad. The good: HDL of 78, D3 of 69, K2 of 76, homocysteine 7.3, other vitamins & minerals OK. The bad: cortisol high, many hormones low or undetectable (DHEA, aldosterone, testosterone, progesterone derivatives), estrogens a problem ("good" ones low, "bad" ones high). I also had high hsCRP (4.4) so I underwent an EBCT which came back clean, no coronary calcium detected.

I still am not sleeping well, waking every 1-1/2 to 2 hours. The hot flashes are gone except for the last couple of days, but maybe that is related to the CT I started 2 weeks ago.

I am 5'6", 120 lbs and am not looking to lose weight.

I read this in the Hormones 101 blog: So anytime cellular stress is high (high HS CRP), it also forces all the hormone backbone substrate called pregnenolone to be shunted to cortisol production. This is called pregnenolone steal syndrome. What exactly does this mean, Doc? DHEA, Androstenedione, Vitamin D, testosterone, estrogen, and aldosterone production all fall dramatically. These are all of the hormones that are made from a common precursor. Remember that chronic Leptin resistance leads to huge hypercortisolism all the time! NEVER FORGET THIS BIOLOGIC FACT!

Because I don't fit this pattern (Vit D is good, HDL is high), I suspect my problems are due to years of being on synthetics and then dropping them for 9 months and not because of leptin resistance. I'm thinking the high hsCRP is due to the hormone imbalance and not leptin resistance.

Does that make sense?

Would I benefit from following the leptin reset RX anyway?

I'll be retesting in another couple of months.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-19T23:14:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-hormones-levels-without-being-leptin-resistant.250/post-2948>

Your hsCRP shouldn't have shot up because of not being on hormones. Just getting on bioidentical hormones won't fix everything. It didn't for me. My CRP was still high and so was my homocysteine (doc likes that below 5). But doing the leptin reset sent both of those plunging and my cholesterol is just over 200 now, a normal range instead of extremely low like usual.

I think there is a thread for leptin stuff with those who don't need to lose weight. You might look over there and chat with others in the same situation.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-20T00:07:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-hormones-levels-without-being-leptin-resistant.250/post-2964>

> **colleencoble;2969 wrote:** Your hsCRP shouldn't have shot up because of not being on hormones. Just getting on bioidentical hormones won't fix everything. It didn't for me. My CRP was still high and so was my homocysteine (doc likes that below 5). But doing the leptin reset sent both of those plunging and my cholesterol is just over 200 now, a normal range instead of extremely low like usual.
>
>
>
> I think there is a thread for leptin stuff with those who don't need to lose weight. You might look over there and chat with others in the same situation.

Thanks for the support and pointer to the other thread. It's so frustrating to have conflicting lab data and not know how to interpret it.

# Thread: Leptin reset when possibly Leptin sensitive?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-when-possibly-leptin-sensitive.253/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-17T23:44:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-when-possibly-leptin-sensitive.253/post-2141>

Is there any harm in doing a leptin reset if one is leptin sensitive as I am not sure my level of sensitivity/resistance. I have been on a low carb Paleo Diet for 13 months , and pretty close to my goal weight. I am thinking of following the Leptin Reset protocol for 8 weeks just to make sure. Just wondering if its OK as I may be Leptin sensitive by now. Thanks!

### Lyndra — 2012-03-18T00:19:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-when-possibly-leptin-sensitive.253/post-2151>

Great question, I've been wondering the same thing.

### Kirstin — 2012-03-18T01:37:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-when-possibly-leptin-sensitive.253/post-2177>

Dr. K has said that it won't \*\*hurt\*\* anything, but basically you would be wasting your time (which he has said would be better spent optimizing). You would be trying to bypass a system in you that already works. The reset is designed to bypass the way Leptin is sensed in the brain by stimulating the vagus nerve and resetting that system via this bypass so the brain eventually becomes sensitive to Leptin again.

### tweety — 2012-03-18T12:49:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-when-possibly-leptin-sensitive.253/post-2332>

What signs make you think you need a reset?

I was paleo for almost a year and pretty close to goal weight, but flabby/chubby belly. Add to that screwed up hormones and adrenals and I definitely needed the reset. I had immediate benefits like increased energy, shifts in body composition, improved mood... I definitely was not LS at any point (brain, muscles, or liver), even though I have been slim. I can attest to that because 10 months later and I am still working out the issues. Hormones and screwed up cortisol and yeast/parasite overgrowth will stop you dead in your tracks on the way to progress. I even went backwards!

If you don't need the reset, do the post RX and add in CT and you will benefit from that!

# Thread: CT Beginning
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-beginning.254/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kpcst — 2012-03-18T00:45:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-beginning.254/post-2162>

I started CT last week and after attending PaleoFX I am even more committed to it.

I had a DEXA Scan at the UT Fit Lab as well. I also just did a max a few weeks back at my Crossfit box.

I figure this was a great combination of things to help me track the benefits of CT as I go.

My stats are Male.32.6'.200lbs . My Dexa showed I was 23% bodyfat. My Max's were 330 Squat. 340 DL. and 180 Shoulder Press. I plan to proceed with doing as much CT as I can. I also intend to restrict my diet even more and try eat fewer carbs and only ones in season. I will also stop eating as much coconut oil until the end of May. My workouts are currently moderate crossfit 3x a week. I'm going to cut that back to once a week. and do OLY Lifts once a week.

Hoping CT improves the stats listed above and I'll be able to update this periodically with some changes etc.

Working on getting some tests done as well. Especially the Telomere one.

# Thread: How soon did your CT mayhem start?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 34
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### blindpig — 2012-03-18T02:30:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2198>

Glamorama asked how long the CT mayhem lasts. Well, I want to know how soon your mayhem started after you began doing CT?

I know my hormones are really whacked and I am expecting all manner of crazy stuff. I have played around some with face dunks and spot icing, but as I have no thermometers to check skin and water temps I haven't gotten serious about doing them until yesterday. Yesterday I did two separate face dunks in much colder water than I had before, for much longer duration. I also did spot icing about twice as long as I had before, in more areas.

By last evening, I was an emotional wreck. Moods up and down, crying at the drop of a hat, craving sweet stuff, acting like a PMSing teenager. (I am about 13 years post-menopausal and haven't had any food cravings AT ALL since I started the Leptin Reset on November 2.) I have been just as crazy all day today.

Is it possible that one day of serious face dunks and spot icing could cause this reaction? Or has reading this forum planted suggestions in my head?

I was getting ready to start some cold soaks. I will have to have a long heart to heart talk with DH first if this is how it's gonna be. We can't afford a divorce attorney.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-18T04:00:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2255>

I went straight into spot-CT on my tummy, 45 min from day 1. I think the mayhem begun on day 3 or 4.

### blindpig — 2012-03-18T20:39:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2441>

Thanks, Glamorama, I know you have really been having a time of it and it helps to know when and how it started for you. I really don't know how those of you with young kids and jobs are getting by.

I don't think I truly and completely believed I could ever touch "optimal" because of my myriad of health issues. I didn't think it was even realistic to dream that big. Now I do. I am convinced that my hormonal upheaval was from the intensified CT that I did on Friday. I think I needed something to slap me upside the head and show me just how powerful this is, and I have been duly slapped. I am in awe of what that one day did to me (even though it was very unpleasant).

DH and I had a long talk this morning and we are making a plan so that we can make this a priority for me, no matter what challenges the CT might bring. We have set a date and we are clearing our calendars of all our on-going stuff so that nothing is going to interfere with this. I am beyond pumped to get this started. Even if it means weeks of hormone hell!

I would still be interested in hearing others' experiences regarding how quickly hormonal upheaval began if you care to share...

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-18T21:34:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2446>

I am interested in hearing more. Being a guy, I think we respond differently. I'm just not sure how.

### shah78 — 2012-03-18T22:02:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2451>

Negitive side effects happened after60 hours. Started to get a sorethroat and fuzzy head/sinus. Positive side effects started within hours. After initial ice gel seesion, a very "enriched" love session with wife. Was a little skeptical though. But right after shirtless pre dawn b'ike after thirty six hours, had "enriched" gym session. So good I injured myself doing one too many chin. I didn't belive it was true. But now a week later, I am convinced. So the bottom line. I felt the CT effects almost from the get go. Note:I'm in excellent shape, LS,and have no cravings> Just a cold wimp!

### blindpig — 2012-03-18T22:09:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2455>

Hey, Don,

I am certain that you (lucky?) guys are going to respond differently than we girls are just because of the hormonal differences, but I'm not sure how, either. I was going to tell you that if you haven't read the thread titled, "How long before the CT mayhem ends?", you might want to give it a look. But then I went back and re-read it, and now I'm thinking you might want to skip it because it might just scare the bejeezus out of you!

I suggest you start a thread asking for the guys specifically to share their experiences. I know I would like to hear how the guys are reacting, too, since I am trying to drag my DH along on this crazy ride.

Happy CTing to you!

### Glamorama — 2012-03-19T01:08:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2502>

I always want to highlight that the positives of CT became apparent in less than a week of spot-CT. For some reason we all tend to focus of the bad (read: detox), but once you're out of the messy stuff that CT creates, you're stronger, elaner and better for it. I'm just about to see light at the end of the tunnel, and I have done 4 weeks. I make family-life work around my CT... I do baths after the kids are in ebd at night, and spot-CT when the baby naps and the twins are in kinder.

### Honey Crisp — 2012-03-19T13:03:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2678>

> **shah78;2458 wrote:** Negitive side effects happened after60 hours. Started to get a sorethroat and fuzzy head/sinus. Positive side effects started within hours. Note:I'm in excellent shape, LS,and have no cravings> Just a cold wimp!

I've been dabbling with CT. Yesterday, I got a bit more serious about it. I did a cold shower in the morning and a cold bath in the evening. This morning I woke up with a sore throat, congestion and an arthritic feeling in some of my joints. I thought the cause was from sleeping with the window opened. Now, I'm wondering if it's side effects.

Thanks for sharing your experience.

### caroline — 2012-03-19T13:51:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2687>

Is anyone having bloating after CT and spot icing? This started a couple of days ago and I first thought it was something I was eating but nothing has really changed - so I am now sure it is the CT. What should I do? I have very few problems except I have a lot of fat in the middle area and pretty thin every where else. I assume this is detoxing. This fat middle area started a few years ago when I was under a lot of stress with a very sick husband so I would guess this is a hormonal thing?? I am post menopause. I would appreciate any thoughts. Thanks

### blindpig — 2012-03-19T15:48:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2722>

For some reason we all tend to focus of the bad (read: detox), but once you're out of the messy stuff that CT creates, you're stronger, elaner and better for it. I do baths after the kids are in ebd at night, and spot-CT when the baby naps and the twins are in kinder.
>
>
> Very good Point, Glamorama. I guess it is just our human nature that makes us focus on the negative. I am actually kind of looking forward to the detox symptoms because to me they will be proof of the power of the CT, and they will be what keeps me going when the going gets rough. (I just have a couple events coming up that require that I be sane...then it will be balls to the wall!) If the CT can cause powerful negative effects, to me that means it can also cause powerful positive effects. I am happy you are getting close to the light at the end of the tunnel. I have been following your progress and you have been one of my inspirations. You are looking amazing and you deserve to feel amazing too!
>
>
>
> caroline;2695 wrote:** Is anyone having bloating after CT and spot icing? This started a couple of days ago and I first thought it was something I was eating but nothing has really changed - so I am now sure it is the CT. What should I do? This fat middle area started a few years ago when I was under a lot of stress with a very sick husband so I would guess this is a hormonal thing?? I am post menopause. I would appreciate any thoughts. Thanks

I don't have a bit of advice about the bloating, Caroline, but I just wanted to say that it makes sense to me that it is hormonal. That is how I am shaped, too...pretty thin everywhere but a nice layer of belly fat which I am certain is courtesy of my menopausal hormones. And if the CT shakes up the hormones the way I am now convinced that it does, I can certainly believe that it could be messing with your hormones and causing the bloating. Hopefully someone will chime in with some advice for you. I hope you will keep us all posted.

Hope it's the start of things happening! So far I've gained 5lbs but lost 2 inches around the tummy and gained an inch around the waist ... feeling very bloated and sore today.

### Swagger — 2012-03-23T17:35:15+0200

>
>
> Makes ya wonder, doesn't it? But I bet you wouldn't trade for that wonderful feeling!
>
>
>
>
> > **oliviascotland;2878 wrote:** I'm a week in now, and feeling exhausted, itchy and very, very cold and achy everywhere. Yesterday felt very upbeat and energetic, but today have been feeling awful. Hope it's the start of things happening! So far I've gained 5lbs but lost 2 inches around the tummy and gained an inch around the waist ... feeling very bloated and sore today.
>
>
>
> I think it is the unpredictability that must be so hard. You never know how you are going to feel from day to day. Here's hoping things are continuing on a positive path for you.
>
>
>
> Swagger;5029 wrote:** Started the Rx 15 days ago and recently started CT. I've been sub 25 carbs along with doing CT and I have had emotional swings as well. I am a guy, so don't think the ups and downs are exclusive to you ladies. I was a complete bas\*\*\*d last night until my CT. Ive had my ups too, but the down is just as strong. Not like "go kick a puppy" angry, but still a bit hard to manage. Dealing with the itchy skin too. I'm losing inches of my chest but the belly has stopped shrinking since starting CT. I think my brain is like Stu from "The Hangover". It's running in circles screaming "WHAT IS GOING ON!!!" like it just found a jungle cat in the bathroom.

Thanks for the laugh, and thanks for the reminder that you guys can be having a tough time of it, too. This is some crazy ride we are on, huh?

### Adriana — 2012-03-23T18:13:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-5027>

Big zit - day 4

Breakthrough bleeding - day 7

Nonstop headache - day 7... On day 2 of that.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-23T18:16:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-5030>

> **Swagger;5029 wrote:** Started the Rx 15 days ago and recently started CT. I've been sub 25 carbs along with doing CT and I have had emotional swings as well. I am a guy, so don't think the ups and downs are exclusive to you ladies. I was a complete bas\*\*\*d last night until my CT. Ive had my ups too, but the down is just as strong. Not like "go kick a puppy" angry, but still a bit hard to manage. Dealing with the itchy skin too. I'm losing inches of my chest but the belly has stopped shrinking since starting CT. Love it! Stay away from day-care centers and the humane society. The idea is the "confusion" to the system/brain is what's needed for re-wiring. At least that's what I'm going on!

It's really helping to see all the itches on here. Makes me feel like it's working or at perhaps misery just loves company.

I am taking photos of my crazy skin eruptions (overnight skin tags!) so I can tell if they are changing. Perhaps I'll share once they are GONE. As it is, no dresses or revealing clothing for work - my body looks crazy diseased! I'm grateful my face is lookin good. I swear the face dunks have made me look younger.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-24T00:25:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-5193>

Anyway, tried CT a couple times a couple weeks ago, cold bath in 62-63 water. Then one coldish shower last week. This week, I've started a more hardcore Leptin Rx and CT combo. So CT twice so far this week. 20-25 minutes in 55-56 degree water in the tub. (We have a deep tub) Last night, very emotional before my CT. Today, much calmer, but I've had a headache all day, which is HIGHLY unusual for me. I guess I don't have anything else to attribute it to than CT.

### kalibex — 2012-03-28T16:05:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-7694>

### Myree67 — 2012-03-28T19:53:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-7802>

This must be what's going on with me! A few things didn't go my way yesterday and usually I can brush it off and let go and let God..not yesterday... I was forgetful and irrtitable. I went to the dentist and had an eye appt. I couldn't follow simple directions...opthamologist told me to look up...I turned my whole head up making it kinda hard for him to see my eyes...I need new glasses and Ibecause I,m spending so dang much money on good food and supplements we can't afford them...just the lenses for my existing glasses after insurance is $262 I'm still really irritated especially since none of this has helped at all with my chronic pain and of course I can't find a doctor who believes all my symptoms are related and just want to drug me up further... THEN I found out I've over done it with the Vitamin D3 and now iit's at 117 from the 31 it started out at... NOT having a good week... I'm a believer of positive outlook and seeing the lessons I have in trials and tribulation but this week I am really having difficulty not feeling sorry for myself... then I broke down last night and ate gluten free cookies.... I was so good about my eating habits since July but when I got sick in February I resorted to eating bad and now can't seems to eat protein from good meat or fat enough to hold me over for 5 hours so I'm really frustrated because my HMO doesn't seem to want to help me be optimal... sorry just had to vent to people that know what I'm going through

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-28T20:04:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-7810>

I think my biggest negative was being freezing cold afterward. We've also had wild temperature swings where I live, so one week it was 80 degrees in the house and the next it was 65. But I haven't worn a coat or jacket since learning about CT, and I have not suffered, even into the 40s.

@Myree, you can get very cheap eyeglasses from eyebuydirect.com. They may not be the prettiest, but at least they could get you through until you have more $.

### vwaggs — 2012-03-29T04:42:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-8088>

My mayhem didn't start until about a month into doing baths every other day for 30 min. ### [email redacted] — 2012-03-29T06:18:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-8130>

I guess my mayhem started mildly a week or two ago, but it really kicked in last night/today. I started CT about 5 weeks ago now, but I have been taking it slowly. I am 57 and post menopause. I have thought my thermostat has been wonky since the frequent hot flashes ended several years ago, so the few times I had a very warm flash over the last couple of weeks, I put off to simply adapting to the cold. My only other weird symptom was one evening getting a zit on my right face cheek while simultaneously getting a zit on left bum cheek. Odd, and they were gone the next day. I have never been prone to zits though.

However, in the middle of last night I woke up very hot, and threw off the covers. While I never got the hot flash sweat and subsequent freezing phase, it took me a while to cool down enough to sleep again. Maybe 10-15 minutes.

And then this morning my rosacia flared up something fierce. My face is RED. I haven't had a flare up like this in years.

I'm really irritated now because I had a phone consult with their fancy pain doctor and the doctor doesn't remember what terms she used when telling me what was wrong with me... DIDN'T document my visit evidently... urgh I had to have a friend drive me up to Seattle( over 1.5 hours) causing horrible pain for me for this doctor to not "remember" and then denied telling me I have Autonomic Neuropathy in one nerve on my right leg... said neuropathy is a general term used for any kind of nerve damage... now I got poked with electric needles in 2009 and was told then that I had A Neuropathy in my right leg... I'm going to demand that they refer me to a local Neurologist AND refer me to a Homeopath or something! I'm so furious at this point!

# Thread: RSD - does anyone have it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2012-03-18T04:15:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2267>

My daughter has had RSD for about 12 years - from an injury to her hand. She is always in pain pretty much and now it seems to morph into fibromyalgia. The only treatment that has ever worked is to go into the hospital for a week \[twice a year\] and be on a Ketamine drip. She hates it so much but it is the only thing that gives her some relief.

It seems to me that the leptin reset and CT would be very helpful.

Any thoughts anyone???

### caroline — 2012-03-18T04:19:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2272>

I should have said what RSD is - since most Docs don't even know about it. It is Reflex Sympathetic Dystrophy Syndrome. It was her physio that diagnosed her much later on. Apparently if you start treatment right away you have a chance of reversing the nerve damage. Hers was left much too long.

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-18T07:18:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2315>

> **caroline;2273 wrote:** My daughter has had RSD for about 12 years - from an injury to her hand. She is always in pain pretty much and now it seems to morph into fibromyalgia. The only treatment that has ever worked is to go into the hospital for a week \[twice a year\] and be on a Ketamine drip. She hates it so much but it is the only thing that gives her some relief.
>
> It seems to me that the leptin reset and CT would be very helpful.
>
>
>
> Any thoughts anyone???

I am sorry to hear about your daughter's pain....

I am not to familiar with RSD aside from having 2 friend that suffer with it. I have watched both of them struggle with pain, and doctors thinking it was all in their head, and family members misunderstanding their condition....my heart goes out to you and your daughter. I'm sorry that I'm not able to offer much advice here, aside from saying that I would imagine that the Leptin rx would help, since it seems to help with SO much! Really my thought is, it couldn't hurt to do it?!

One of the friends of mine who has RSD also has IBS/gut issues...does your daughter any gut issues?

### caroline — 2012-03-18T11:01:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2322>

Thankyou joyfuljoyful for your kind thoughts. I love your name! Dr K was very critical of one poster and their name. He said your name should be positive and up lifting or something along those lines. yours is wonderful!

You are exactly right - people often think the pain is in your head. I had a friend in Canada who would go to the mayo clinic for treatment. Some people would amputate the offending limb to relieve the pain - sadly - even that doesn't work. My daughter was hurt at work and went thru an agonizing five years and tried to get compensation. Even tho everyone agreed she would never work again \[she was 27 when it happened\] she was generally ridiculed and treated like she was lying - even by doctors. One specialist asked her to remove her watch and her shoes and then in his report said she was fine because she was able to do these things. The thing is - even when you have this - you still have to do things and you still have to use your hand regardless of how much pain you are in. Sorry to be so long winded.

I am not sure about the gut issues - I would suspect yes. She is carrying a lot of weight around her middle. In both pregnancies she got gestational diabetes. She eats a lot of junk but thankfully makes sure her little boys eat very healthy.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-18T17:50:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2395>

My cousin has had it for many years. She had a few surgeries long ago and thinks that the metal implants might have been the trigger. It took numerous visits to various doctor to figure out what was ailing her and like others, was accused of being a hypochondriac. Unfortunately, she follows a SAD diet, smokes heavily, and isn't apt to change. I wish you well for your daughter.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-18T17:53:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2396>

My friend's niece has it and has undergone some type of extensive physical therapy that has allowed her to be a normal 16 yr old. I don't know the name of the therapy but if you would like it let me know as I can always find out.

### caroline — 2012-03-19T09:21:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2660>

It took numerous visits to various doctor to figure out what was ailing her and like others, was accused of being a hypochondriac. Unfortunately, she follows a SAD diet, smokes heavily, and isn't apt to change. I wish you well for your daughter.

Thanks very much Lyndra - I think people with RSD get very depressed from always being in so much pain and they dig themselves into a hole which they can't get out of

### caroline — 2012-03-19T09:28:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2661>

> **Patty Cakes;2403 wrote:** My friend's niece has it and has undergone some type of extensive physical therapy that has allowed her to be a normal 16 yr old. I don't know the name of the therapy but if you would like it let me know as I can always find out.

Thanks Patty Cakes - that would be interesting to know. Apparently - in Mexico - they do a Ketamine treatment where they put them way under in a coma. I spoke to my Daughter's Doc about it and he said it is very, very dangerous because they put them so far under.

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-19T21:09:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2892>

> **caroline;2329 wrote:** Thankyou joyfuljoyful for your kind thoughts. I love your name! Dr K was very critical of one poster and their name. He said your name should be positive and up lifting or something along those lines. yours is wonderful!
>
> You are exactly right - people often think the pain is in your head. I had a friend in Canada who would go to the mayo clinic for treatment. Some people would amputate the offending limb to relieve the pain - sadly - even that doesn't work. My daughter was hurt at work and went thru an agonizing five years and tried to get compensation. Even tho everyone agreed she would never work again \[she was 27 when it happened\] she was generally ridiculed and treated like she was lying - even by doctors. One specialist asked her to remove her watch and her shoes and then in his report said she was fine because she was able to do these things. The thing is - even when you have this - you still have to do things and you still have to use your hand regardless of how much pain you are in. Sorry to be so long winded.
>
> I am not sure about the gut issues - I would suspect yes. She is carrying a lot of weight around her middle. In both pregnancies she got gestational diabetes. She eats a lot of junk but thankfully makes sure her little boys eat very healthy.

Thanks, caroline, I'm glad you like my name  I agree that it is nice to have something uplifting, and I am so grateful that I do have JOY!

One of my friends was injured at work, and since her accident (about 4 years ago), workmans comp has tried so hard to 'prove' that she is not really injured....it is so sad. Thank God they haven't won that battle and she is still under her doctors care. She goes in for pain treatments a couple times a month. Hers progressed from one foot to the other, and now is in her hands. The one thing I would wonder about CT with RSD patients is this: my friend said that some RSD patients cannot feel the cold in the affected body part any longer. The other friend of mine who suffers from RSD is proof of this, I've seen her in the cold of winter with no socks or shoes on (on her affected foot). So I wonder if this would be good or bad for the affected area. Sorry I don't have any real knowledge on this.

### caroline — 2012-03-19T22:06:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2924>

Thanks Joyful. Now that you mention that - she never wears a jacket or sweater always says she is hot. interesting

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-20T00:01:11+0200

### natural_girl — 2012-03-21T01:04:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-3611>

Caroline,

I was dx with RSD in my right arm in 2000. I went through medical hell with it. Many local nerve blocks, stellate ganglion blocks, spinal cord stimulators and almost a med pump. To think of the drugs I used to take. I take nothing now! NOTHING! After years of going to specialists I was tired of nothing really helping so I found natural health & a Dr. to help me. I took my life back. I did regular myofascial massage, tons of Hellerwork, and completely changed my eating & lifestyle. I have never looked back...Keep the faith it is like anything else you have to want it so bad you can taste and NEVER use the words I can't

> **caroline;3768 wrote:** Thanks so much natural\_girl
>
> I will google all those things you talk about. Where are you located? Are you pain free now? Do you do CT? I am so happy you were able to take your life back - you have to be a very strong person to do that

Caroline,

I am definitely a strong person....but desperate times call for desperate measures and it was all a journey I was meant to take. I was not going to let the meds kill me or own me....I had 3 small children at that time. I am in Ohio but the lady who did my Hellerwork actually trained in Australia at the Hellerwork school there! It was expensive & excruiating painful but it was nothing short of a miracle along with a 100% strict organic diet, no grains or dairy & no nightshades. I also spent a fortune in supplements (still do) but it was better than the alternative.

I am pain free most of the time and have a bit of pain now and again but usually don't need anything more than an adjustment or massage. I do CT but that was long after my healing but I think it will only strengthen my system. If you need any further info feel free to PM me. You & your daughter are in my prayers as I know the hell RSD can bring.

### caroline — 2012-03-22T11:05:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-4293>

>
>
>

I was not going to let the meds kill me or own me....I had 3 small children at that time. I am in Ohio but the lady who did my Hellerwork actually trained in Australia at the Hellerwork school there! It was expensive & excruiating painful but it was nothing short of a miracle along with a 100% strict organic diet, no grains or dairy & no nightshades. I also spent a fortune in supplements (still do) but it was better than the alternative.
>

> I am pain free most of the time and have a bit of pain now and again but usually don't need anything more than an adjustment or massage. I do CT but that was long after my healing but I think it will only strengthen my system. If you need any further info feel free to PM me. You & your daughter are in my prayers as I know the hell RSD can bring.

Thankyou so much for your reply. I will look into all that although I don't know how receptive my daughter will be. She totally trusts her pain management Doc and I think she is just resigned to it all. Although she never complains - just gets on with it. She has 2 very active little boys and doesn't let anything get in the way of them experiencing everything possible. Thanks again. Good luck to you and I would love to hear how you are going with CT. Please keep me posted

### devil in angel — 2012-03-29T05:39:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-8113>

> **caroline;2273 wrote:** My daughter has had RSD for about 12 years - from an injury to her hand. She is always in pain pretty much and now it seems to morph into fibromyalgia. The only treatment that has ever worked is to go into the hospital for a week \[twice a year\] and be on a Ketamine drip. She hates it so much but it is the only thing that gives her some relief.

>
>
>

Although I can't help you, but I really hope that your daughter better soon,

# Thread: Anyone tracking body composition on CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BenG — 2012-03-18T10:57:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-2320>

Hi all,

Has anyone been tracking their body composition on CT? I was losing about 3lbs a week just doing Leptin reset, but since starting CT, my weight has nearly stalled (losing about .5-1lb a week), but my clothes keep getting loser. I'm figuring I'm either holding water, or increased lean/muscle weight. Anyone noticed similar findings? I just find it hard to swallow that I'm gaining lean weight on only about 1500kcal a day (and I'm full on that) from only 4 weeks of CT.

Thanks.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-18T11:45:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-2328>

Haven't tracked any body composition, but I have lost inches around my stomach (2 to be precise) and have gained 5lbs :/ Happy about the inches lost, and wondering what the gain is?

### Inger — 2012-03-18T13:12:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-2336>

I have not lost anything.. I have been doing CT 2,5 weeks now. Only showers as I have no tub.

I swear my breast have gone smaller (and they are already quite small eh) and I have lost fat on my torso, even if I have almost no fat there.. I can see my ribs now. I am waiting to loose the fat on my arms and legs though..

But really, I was sure I had lost some weight, but when I stepped on the scale today - all the same.

Strange ideed. I have been eating really much though, but only the good stuff. I guess around 1500-2000 calories/day LOT of fat and seafoods.

I am 5,4 and 119 pounds, 35 yo.

Inger

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-24T01:14:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-5213>

> **BenG;2327 wrote:** Hi all,
>
>
>
> Has anyone been tracking their body composition on CT? I was losing about 3lbs a week just doing Leptin reset, but since starting CT, my weight has nearly stalled (losing about .5-1lb a week), but my clothes keep getting loser. I'm figuring I'm either holding water, or increased lean/muscle weight. Anyone noticed similar findings? I just find it hard to swallow that I'm gaining lean weight on only about 1500kcal a day (and I'm full on that) from only 4 weeks of CT.
>
>
>
> Thanks.

Yes! I have gained 3-4 pounds in about 3 weeks. I do some baths, some soaks in the pool, and spot CT with ice packs almost every night. I don't feel like I've been doing anything extreme at all, but I have lost body fat for sure. Down to about 28% from 31%. This is welcome as I have gotten to the point where I don't really need to lose weight, but I do need to increase muscle and lose fat. I don't do any cardio. I do a few sets of body weight squats, chinups, and pushups after CT every few nights. I'm pretty impressed. I'm actually seeing more definition and I feel I haven't done that much!!! I'll take it. I'll keep you posted.

### Cody — 2012-03-24T01:32:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-5230>

I've gained about 8 pounds, but that was coming off a very calorie restricted diet. My waist has gotten smaller and my muscles have gotten noticeably larger. Good stuff.

### Kpcst — 2012-03-24T03:39:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-5278>

I am. Had a dexa scan last week. Plan on doing CT 60-90 days then having another.... We shall see.

KP

### Glamorama — 2012-03-26T02:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-6154>

Yep I am. I get my BF percentage checked every 4-8 weeks. Scvales much the same, but less fat over the boobs, belly and hips, while muscle and strenght is rapidly increasing.

# Thread: The Sleep Thread FFTA
