**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 65
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evo Mama — 2012-03-18T13:36:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2338>

Here's where we celebrate and comiserate our previous night's sleep.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-18T13:37:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2339>

I'll go first. Last night, I slept like a rock for the first time since starting CT, and I feel great!

### Phoenix — 2012-03-18T16:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2374>

I'm exactly the opposite. Since starting CT I'm up hot flashing between 2 and 4 every night. I try to get 8 hours, but CT is only letting me get 6 or less. Makes for an exhausting day. I'm staying with it though ... CT must be affecting my hormones, and I'm hoping that it will eventually level off. I'm 55 and think it may be something menpausal women are going to go through with CT.

Anybody else have this experience?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-18T17:00:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2385>

> **Phoenix;2381 wrote:** I'm exactly the opposite. Since starting CT I'm up hot flashing between 2 and 4 every night. I try to get 8 hours, but CT is only letting me get 6 or less. Makes for an exhausting day. I'm staying with it though ... CT must be affecting my hormones, and I'm hoping that it will eventually level off. I'm 55 and think it may be something menpausal women are going to go through with CT.
>
>
>
> Anybody else have this experience?

I had that within the first week of starting CT. It lasted another couple of weeks and then stopped. I was so glad because it was interfering with sleep. It's your hormones moderating but they have to go through the wonky stage first. I jsut found out I horribly estrogen dominant with estradiol at over 500 so continuing on with CT and letting the hormones do what they are going to do....hopefully leave!

### Lyndra — 2012-03-18T17:44:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2392>

I'm waking up every 1-1/2 hours feeling warm. It feels like the hot flashes I experienced when I wasn't taking hormones but a bit milder. I feel surprisingly good during the day considering how poorly I'm sleeping at night. I'm really hoping this will pass soon. I've been doing cold baths for 11 days now.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-18T19:05:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2412>

> [Lyndra said:](/goto/post?id=2399)
>
> I'm waking up every 1-1/2 hours feeling warm. It feels like the hot flashes I experienced when I wasn't taking hormones but a bit milder. I feel surprisingly good during the day considering how poorly I'm sleeping at night. I'm really hoping this will pass soon. I've been doing cold baths for 11 days now.\[/QUOTE
>
>
>
> Your experience almost mirrors mine. I am soon to be 59 and have been postmenopausal for about eleven years (not sure exactly how many years). I took bio-identical hormones for 10 yrs, but I have been off of them now for about a year. I have had only seven CT sessions so far in the tub and about seven icing session (burnt my upper arm the first time I tried it - stupid). CT has not yet improved my sleep pattern. I take my magnesium at night along with three melatonin under the tongue. It has always has, and still does, take me 1.5 - 2.0 every night to get to sleep. I think perhaps I am stressing too much about getting to sleep, and that I need to not worry so much about it. I too wake up as if I were having a small hot flash most nights now. I believe that has only begun since I started CT. I notice that in the evenings before, I retire, that my cheeks are a bit warm when I don't remember them warming up in the evenings before CT. I generally take my tub in the mid-afternoon.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-18T19:08:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2414>

Sorry gang, but I struggle with using a computer, so if my replies look a little goofy, well it's just my learning curve.

Susan M. :>)

### Lyndra — 2012-03-19T16:00:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2726>

### fitness@home — 2012-03-20T02:57:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3051>

Another postmenopausal woman here. I am still relatively early on in my CT. Have done face baths for about a week and used ice packs on abdomen & waist. Last night I did a 35 minute soak in the tub (tap water only) followed by ice packs for another 30 minutes.

Slept like a zombie  It was great...and doesn't happen very often for me.

I know there are detox and hormonal symptoms coming my way as I get more serious using colder water/ice in the baths. It helps reading about everyone else's experiences.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-20T15:44:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3221>

> **Phoenix;2381 wrote:** I'm exactly the opposite. Since starting CT I'm up hot flashing between 2 and 4 every night. I try to get 8 hours, but CT is only letting me get 6 or less. Makes for an exhausting day. I'm staying with it though ... CT must be affecting my hormones, and I'm hoping that it will eventually level off. I'm 55 and think it may be something menpausal women are going to go through with CT.
>
>
>
> Anybody else have this experience?

I'm 52 and also had major hot flashing in the middle of the night and trouble sleeping after CT, but the past two nights have been much better and I've actually slept better than I have in years--without ibuprofen (been off that for 2 weeks) and melatonin (actually sleep better without it) I'm hoping my hormones are stabilizing to optimal. I'm finding that about 30 minutes after CT in the evening, I become extremely drowsy and am in bed by 8:30.. Anyone else get really tired after CT?

### Syl — 2012-03-20T17:08:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3257>

> **PaleoNana;3244 wrote:** I'm 52 and also had major hot flashing in the middle of the night and trouble sleeping after CT, but the past two nights have been much better and I've actually slept better than I have in years--without ibuprofen (been off that for 2 weeks) and melatonin (actually sleep better without it) I'm hoping my hormones are stabilizing to optimal. I'm finding that about 30 minutes after CT in the evening, I become extremely drowsy and am in bed by 8:30.. Anyone else get really tired after CT?

sometimes i feel tired cause my muscles seem to constricting harder toward the last minutes of my 30 minute tub soak and it actually feels like a work out (i'm beginning to see six pack  ) other times it clears my head when i can study my butt off (i'm a college student  ). i guess it all depends on where everyone is on the hormone balance scale and omega 3 ratio (and propably other factors too).

### Phoenix — 2012-03-20T21:31:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3452>

This is encouraging, @PaleoNana. I was just thinking about taking a bath tonight and dreading it. Hopefully tonight will be a better night for me.

### Phoenix — 2012-03-20T21:33:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3453>

> **Syl;3280 wrote:** sometimes i feel tired cause my muscles seem to constricting harder toward the last minutes of my 30 minute tub soak and it actually feels like a work out (i'm beginning to see six pack  ) other times it clears my head when i can study my butt off (i'm a college student  ). i guess it all depends on where everyone is on the hormone balance scale and omega 3 ratio (and propably other factors too).

I noticed this too. Yesterday I had sore muscles all day. Not in a big way, but enough to let me know that something happened to them. Kind of like I had worked out.

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T21:49:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3464>

> **PaleoNana;3244 wrote:** Anyone else get really tired after CT?

I've noticed that I get crushed at night. Not so much being right after CT though, but more of a "since I started CT" thing. It's giving me a bit funky sleep cycles. I'm assuming things are working through, but as an example, last Sunday afternoon, I decided to take a short nap because I was feeling a bit tired and woke up 13 hours later (no CT that day)...

### chocolate — 2012-03-20T23:08:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3520>

Does anyone notice if wearing the blue blockers has a cumulative effect, just like CT? It seems to me that it does, but I am in the Mojave Desert and we have the most intense UV in the world. I wonder if anyone else has noticed, or if it is just placebo? It seems to be doing something.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-20T23:37:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3535>

In general my sleep is better with CT. CT has worked best for me when I can do it mid-afternoon. To warm up after, I throw my kettle-bell around for awhile. Then I have the rest of the day to warm up and I sleep well.

When I do it too close to bedtime, or go to bed kind of chilled...no bueno. I toss and turn trying to get warm.

A few times, I've really slept well and pop outa bed before my alarm. Like I said, a few times...most of the time it's sheer force of will to get up.

I know I need longer, higher quality sleep. That's one of my main hacks to do.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-20T23:40:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3538>

No sleep again last night. Zero. It was the fact that I had 85% choc at 7.30 pm. Too much caffeine for my poor brain to shut down. Not even CT helped.

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-20T23:45:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3544>

> **Glamorama;3563 wrote:** No sleep again last night. Zero. It was the fact that I had 85% choc at 7.30 pm. Too much caffeine for my poor brain to shut down. Not even CT helped.

The wind bothered me. I didn't fall asleep until shortly after 11pm. Roaring through the trees, clattering and banging. It was worse than the possums!

### Mikey7 — 2012-03-21T02:08:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3652>

I went on a taping spree today. I taped up all my LED lights... it gonna be so dark in my room now. CT plus complete darkness = good night of sleep.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-21T02:10:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3656>

I'm starting yellow lenses glasses soon. Sick of my unpredictable insomnia. I think it is a sideffect from CT, it's slowly easing.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T04:44:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3767>

CT on a Friday night can knock me out within half an hour of getting out of the tub. somehow the CT allows me to let go of the accumulated stress from the week and I am hard pressed to keep my eyes open

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-22T12:08:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-4294>

So far I have been sleeping much better, fewer waking episodes during the night, and no need to pee during the night either. I have been completely exhausted by about 9 in the evening and, by the time I've done the evening chores and get to bed, I'm asleep by 10 and up again feeling refreshed around 7 in the morning. But, if I do my cold soak after about 5.00 p.m., then it's no sleep for me.

### MartiD — 2012-03-22T14:01:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-4306>

> **ColleenM;3792 wrote:** CT on a Friday night can knock me out within half an hour of getting out of the tub. somehow the CT allows me to let go of the accumulated stress from the week and I am hard pressed to keep my eyes open

I agree..can't do it to late in evening. Last night for some reason I was shivering for awhile after and I haven't done that in week. The dog also woke me up at 2:00 am. I have slept really well with CT but it also improved dramatically with Leptin RX in October. Does anyone take Melatonin?

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-22T14:20:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-4312>

> **Mikey7;3677 wrote:** I went on a taping spree today. I taped up all my LED lights... it gonna be so dark in my room now. CT plus complete darkness = good night of sleep.

I've done this too. I also have the tv and dvd player plugged into a socket that is turned off by a light switch on the wall, so I just flip that switch at night, and turn everything off. I'm still looking for a good "blackout curtain" hack, since there's some artificial light still getting in from my blinds.

My sleep has been unpredictable, like most of you. Last night was good, though, but the night before was rough.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-22T14:28:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-4317>

I had my first bad night of sleep in a long while. I got in later than normal, so I had to fit in a 20 immersion (50F) just before bed. So i got in bed cold, and I had a difficult time sleeping. This was the first time I didn't wait for a period from bath to bed. I woke up hot like usuaul - but getting to sleep in the first place was a struggle. I probably won't do that again. I also had some cramps in my toes.

### sandy — 2012-03-22T18:32:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-4492>

### Glamazon — 2012-03-28T16:22:11+0300

### Glamazon — 2012-03-28T16:28:45+0300

The keto paleo helped bring back the bladder control.

I also have severe muscle cramps across the top of my feet and up my calves that have returned for the night and sometimes during the day when I stretch my legs.The only way to alleviate them now is to sit straight up or go walk around. ### differentstory — 2012-03-28T18:08:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-7747>

I feel for you guys!!

Just wanted to add my two cents and say that I am having a weird CT-sleep thing that I was curious if anyone else is having.

My sleep has not been screwed up. I do think I was getting a week of rough sleeping at first because my dreaming was in overdrive. And, of course, they were all bad, stressful dreams where I forgot to pay bills or failed all of my classes (haven't been in school in years, go figure).

I am hoping that CT/Leptin Rx helps my sleep problem. I am a 9-hour-er. I have to sleep almost exactly 9 hours where others around me feel great after 7 or 8 hours. I am also not much of a morning person. I have a hard time turning off the lights before midnight and don't really get going until noon. What I would give to wake up at 5am after only 7 hours of sleep!!!

Currently, I am having these "blackouts" when I sleep where I almost don't even remember going to sleep and I have no "feeling" of time passing! For instance, last night I noticed I was pretty tired. After CT that ended at 9pm, I felt like I could close my eyes and go to sleep at any second. But, of course, tv got the best of me and I stayed up watching it until 11:30. At 11:30, I finally gave in and I remember turning off the TV, rolling over, and. . .that's it! The next time I open my eyes its 3:30 in the morning and I'm hot.

It might not seem like much but it reminds me of anesthesia (if you've ever been put under) and I am experiencing this everyday now. I'm wondering if its just because I am so exhausted from the CT? I am not in good shape in the least. I've had other instances of this too where I get sleepy, close my eyes, open them again and it's 3 hours later. It's such a weird feeling (apparently hard to describe too!).

I know this is definitely a CT thing as this started a week after the crazy dreams.

I have been CT'ing (full emersion) for two weeks now. Icing one week before that.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-28T20:32:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-7838>

Has anyone noticed they are having more/remembering more dreams since staring the leptin RX and CT?

I am sleeping mostly very well these days. I had suffered insomnia for years, and had mostly fixed it with the occasional blip before starting the leaky gut rx. But I was still tired all the time (likely from all the sugar and junk I was eating). But 2-3 days a week I would wake up at 4 a.m. and not be able to go back to sleep.

Since starting the RX and CT, my sleep has been very sound. I fall asleep quickly, which is a nice change. I am still waking up to use the restroom at night, but I fall right back asleep. I use a Phillips light that dims gradually at night and rises gradually in the morning, and I almost always wake up before the audio alarm.

But I'm having so many vivid dreams now. It's pretty wild. I can't ever remember having this many dreams.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-04-03T17:38:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-10782>

Last night was the first time I had lousy sleep since starting CT, and it was uncharacteristic of my sleep even before CT. I did a 35 min CT soak in the tub and then went to bed (yes I know I should be doing this earlier in the evening to prevent 'toxin dump destroying sleep', but I am having to pick my suboptimal battles). I was nice and relaxed afterward, no shivering or anything as usual, and I even took two 3mg melatonin when I only ever take 1. For some reason I woke at around 3:30 - wide awake. I feel awful this morning and am pounding down the caffeine to get me through the next 16+ hours of responsibility laid out before me. I don't know how people live with insomnia - I'm such a lightweight. I can't handle more than a couple of nights or I'm a virtual shell of a person.

I'm hoping this was a fluke and not a result of my cold bath. I've taken 14 cold baths before, all of them longer than last night's, and had nothing but good, deep sleep afterward. Fingers crossed this doesn't happen again.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-09T17:04:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-12983>

I slept **hard** this weekend. Saturday morning I was complaining to my husband about him having the light on, but he said it was the sun in the window! And he was right! I couldn't believe it. The chickens were upset that they hadn't been fed. And I actually took a long nap Friday and Saturday evening, something I haven't done since my carb-crazed days years ago. The naps didn't keep me from zombie-like sleep at night though.

I usually sleep pretty well. When I went on the Leptin Reset, for the first time I started waking before the radio. That was amazing to me. Then I also started CT, which made me start waking up in the middle of the night, usually for just a few minutes. But I still got fairly good sleep. Now this change. Just thinking about it makes me want to take a nap!

I don't know what is in store for me. I've still got a rash on my right hand, still haven't lost weight to speak of, but my arms muscles are looking good, and the tummy fat is leaving, and even my thighs are getting squishy. I feel spectacular. No way I'm stopping CT, but I'm going to have to start buying more ice! My multitude of ice cube trays just can't keep up with the tap water temp increases.

# Thread: My Unintentional, Unknowing Experience with the Leptin Rx
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-unintentional-unknowing-experience-with-the-leptin-rx.265/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheKid — 2012-03-18T14:26:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-unintentional-unknowing-experience-with-the-leptin-rx.265/post-2351>

I've been on the Leptin Rx for 6 weeks and have been doing CT for 2 weeks. Not much success so far. However, back in November, before I had become aware of the Dr. Jack, I think I had an unintentional run-in with the Leptin Rx and CT. I will note my observations after the narrative:

Back in November of 2011 I was vacationing with the family. During the first week, nightly temps were in the low 40s at best. Durnig the day it would hit the low to mid 60s. Our accommodation was rustic: there was not heating other than a space heater and showering was done essentially outdoors. So despite taking a warm shower, there was constant cold otherwise.

During the second 2 weeks, the temps increased a bit, but nights were still pretty cold. Throughout this time, we would just wear jackets during the day to stay comfortable and would bundle under heavy covers at night.

Interestingly, I was very inactive this entire time. Because of an autoimmune flare and a ligament pull, I was not able to really exercise much. The first week I walked for 3 hours one day. The next week I did some kettlebells and squats once. The last week I did about 8 sprints. That's it.

In terms of eating, I was downing immense amounts of fatty meat and eating salad. I added in a small amount of seasonal fruit in the last week.

So to summarize, it was very cold, with no real indoor heating. I ate ton of protein, fat and leaves. And I slept for as long as I wanted and sat around most of the day with exception of a few bouts of exercise and small amounts of light walking.

The results were amazing. Pounds were just flying off. My pants were fitting so loose it was unbelievable. In the pictures I took during that period, I look like a different person.

Since returning, and discovering Dr. Jack, things have not gone so well. Weight is up, I'm stalled out, and have even experienced cravings. So here are my observations of my unintentional Leptin Rx w/CT:

\- Being naturally exposed to cold all day for extended periods is maybe superior to trying to "dose" yourself with cold each day through CT.

\- Being fully and completely ketogenic may be a game changer. And the carbs some of us may be counting out each day on the Leptin Rx may lead us unwittingly out of ketosis.

\- By exerising once a week, I probably allowed my body to reap the benefit of full adaptation to the exercise stimulus.

\- Sleeping to as much as your body seems to want is probably a very good thing. Even though we try, many of us probably don't sleep as much as we should, and the quality of sleep during a normal night vs. a vacation night is probably not as good.

\- Stress can be a game changer. Sure, I was sick and not feeling well, but I had no responsibilities other than restnig and trying to feel better. I bet that helped me.

Anyway, just my experience. Thought it migth be helpful.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-25T02:54:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-unintentional-unknowing-experience-with-the-leptin-rx.265/post-5652>

Very interesting and so true about your quality of sleep. I know how essential it is yet in this crazy and hectic modern world we all live in it's one of the most challenging aspects of this journey to optimal.

# Thread: Can you eat too much liver?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-03-18T18:56:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-2407>

What do you think? Is it possible to eat too much liver? I seriously love the stuff and have been going through about a pound every 2-3 days, so roughly 5-7 oz a day.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-18T22:14:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-2458>

I think it's possible to each too much anything, no matter how good it is for you. I suppose the questions are why are you eating so much? Do you need it? (If it's a K2 issue, can you do testing?) Do you need something else instead/with? As long as you're feeling you've got good answers to your questions, I'm guessing it's fine. If something doesn't add up, keep digging.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-18T22:19:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-2460>

I'm going to have the osteocalcin test this week so I guess that will tell me if it's a K2 issue. Because it's so high in several vitamins it just made me wonder if I should be concerned about any vitamin toxicity issues with too much. I know too much of a good thing tends to be bad, but I don't know what that limit would be with liver. I'm probably overthinking it . . .

### Glamorama — 2012-03-19T00:57:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-2497>

You should be very careful with liver, as its high in copper and Vit A. You are eating a lot of it. I read somewhere about suggested weekly amount but I can't rememeber now... but yes, be careful!

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-19T03:21:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-2549>

yes, you can eat too much liver. also depends on what animal's liver you're eating. Chicken liver is low in vitamins compared to turkey, which is sky high (vitamin A) or beef (copper issue)

maybe consider spectra cell nutrient analysis to see if there's a deficiency underlying

Our bodies can handle way more vitamin A than most people think... I was taking 50,000 a day for months to combat PMS ala WAPF style... but I was also watching for toxicity symptoms. Never got any. Just got better. Make sure you get equivalent amounts of vitamin D if you get a lot of A.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-19T15:29:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-2715>

It is beef liver that I tend to devour, so I guess I should be concerned about copper toxicity. At my last appt, my doctor suggested getting the NutraEval from Genova. I had it done a year ago too - very comprehensive info on nutrient status. It looks very similar to the Spectracell. Anyone familiar with the NutraEval? The good thing is I would only be responsible for $169 of it and my insurance covers the rest. I don't think they'll cover any of the Spectracell.

### thomasmite996 — 2012-03-28T11:09:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-7643>

I thing you have to show serious in eating very things . liver is very important and sensitive part of our body. It have need care and look after. eat all those items which have essential vitamins and low cholesterol level.

Weird stuff this CT.

# Thread: Hot Tub While CT???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Myree67 — 2012-03-18T21:48:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/post-2448>

Ok so I don't know if anyone has the answer but my hubby got our hot tub hooked up last night and going. I've been sitting in it because it gives me so much relief from the chronic pain. Do you think this will hurt my CT? I don't have a bath tub so can't actually take a cold bath. I've been using home made gel packs that are colder than ice packs on my low back, tummy and tricep area because my upper arms have HUGE bat wings! I'm also doing the cold face dunk in the bathroom sink.

### L8F — 2012-03-18T22:04:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/post-2452>

Most people, me included, have been answered that the hot defeats the cold. Cold should help chronic pain, especially if coupled with diet, but it is not as INITIALLY as relaxing feeling. The goal is as cold as possible as much as possible once acclimated per the instructions and building up period. Safety first....

### Myree67 — 2012-03-19T09:34:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/post-2665>

Thanks... I'm only going to use it when I'm hurting alot more than usual... it's still freezing at night here so I made sure that I stood naked outside afterwards for several minutes and then came into the house that was 65° I get up every hour and go outside with a light weight jacket on and stay out for 10 minutes... it's only 30° out there tonight but the cold actually felt good on my legs... this is pretty difficult for me since I'm hypothyroid and have a really hard time getting warm once I'm chilled so I'm having to start slow unfortunately...

### Myree67 — 2012-03-19T09:39:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/post-2666>

Eek my degree symbols didn't format lol it's 65 degrees F in the house and 30 degrees outside... it was a balmy 50 degrees F today in the heat of the day and I'm running around in thin jeans and a sweatshirt on... tonight I did use a jean jacket lol... I really hate this cold stuff but I'll do it if it gets me out of pain and healthy!!!

### AKMan — 2012-03-19T13:06:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/post-2680>

> **Myree67;2673 wrote:** Eek my degree symbols didn't format lol it's 65 degrees F in the house and 30 degrees outside... it was a balmy 50 degrees F today in the heat of the day and I'm running around in thin jeans and a sweatshirt on... tonight I did use a jean jacket lol... I really hate this cold stuff but I'll do it if it gets me out of pain and healthy!!!

We have a hot tub. We have been keeping it at 102 deg. I was feeling guilty about using it, but it does help warm you up when you are chilled to the core. Last week, I lowered it to 90 degrees and it feels pretty good.

In theory, any temperature less than body temp is considered 'cold'. I'd say that if you have a hot tub, use it on days you don't CT or at least 6-8 hours before of after you CT. The magic of CT seems to come from activating the body's cold receptors in the skin and you don't have to be cold and miserable all the time for it to work.

Unless you die, your body temp will be around 98 degrees, you can't change that no matter how much you CT or HT (hot tub).

### Myree67 — 2012-03-21T04:07:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/post-3752>

Thanks alot! I tried the cold shower today and actually got it down all the way brrrr we are on well water and it's pretty cold this time of year!

So, I'm in Tampa for 2 weeks for some job training. It was -20 when I left Fairbanks yesterday and 82 in Tampa. I had 12 raw oysters for lunch and stopped at the local grocery store for sardines for meals for all week.

I'll be curious to see how this compares with playing in -20 temps without many clothes on.

I saw an ice machine down the hallway I will probably hit later for some ice-pack time!

Later

# Thread: Firefox  font size Issue?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/firefox-font-size-issue.277/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### pjms9 — 2012-03-19T02:55:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/firefox-font-size-issue.277/post-2539>

I am not sure any body used mozilla firefox to use this forum. when I tried to use firefox for the forum, the thread content font size is HUUGE. What can be done to rectify this. All the forum heading and menu items are all fine.

### admin — 2012-04-02T21:47:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/firefox-font-size-issue.277/post-10445>

That may be something specific to your browser settings. Inside of Firefox, you'll see "View" in your toolbar. Under "View" is an option to "Zoom". Your content may be zoomed in - giving the appearance of HUGE text. Try zooming out. It should fix your problem.

# Thread: Walked into the pond
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/walked-into-the-pond.281/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2012-03-19T04:15:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/walked-into-the-pond.281/post-2568>

Ending week five of ct. Following the protocol. Started with face dunking for a week. Went to 20 lb ice on chest with compression shirt 1-2 weeks. Layed down in the snow a few times. Started taking 52 deg showers once a day for a week then stepped it to two times a day for a week. Tonight I walked out into a 50 deg pond under a clear starlit sky. I just walked in slowly until i was up to my chin and stood there for ten minutes. I am going back in the am. This is starting to get really amazing. I test bg before ct and after. Ten minutes of ct will drop my bg 40 points. I try to ct 2 hrs post prandial if possible. Stick with it it gets easier everyday.

### kalibex — 2012-03-19T12:19:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/walked-into-the-pond.281/post-2674>

> **Dali Dula;2575 wrote:** Ten minutes of ct will drop my bg 40 points.

Very cool!!

### AKMan — 2012-03-19T13:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/walked-into-the-pond.281/post-2683>

Thanks for sharing the BG info. The way brown fat was 'rediscovered' recently was by cancer researchers. They would fill people with glucose and give them a PET scan to see where the glucose was going. If they had cancer, the cancer cells would actively start heating up as they metabolized the glucose. [http://www.lipidworld.com/content/10/1/238](http://http://www.lipidworld.com/content/10/1/238)

They noticed something strange. Bands of high activity around the collar bone, chest and back in people when the temperature in the room was cool, and none when it was warm. They found they had to keep it warm to prevent these false alarms. They also found it was BAT (brown fat) causing the activity.

They did lots of studies using PET to see what activated BAT, short exposures to cold activated it and repeated exposure grew it. It doesn't even need to be cold-cold, just like 68 degrees in the room, but ice water on a foot will make it explode.

I wonder if that is the drop in BG you are seeing, caused by activation of BAT and it's metabolism of glucose.

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-19T13:29:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/walked-into-the-pond.281/post-2686>

> **Dali Dula;2575 wrote:** Ending week five of ct. Following the protocol. Started with face dunking for a week. Went to 20 lb ice on chest with compression shirt 1-2 weeks. Layed down in the snow a few times. Started taking 52 deg showers once a day for a week then stepped it to two times a day for a week. Tonight I walked out into a 50 deg pond under a clear starlit sky. I just walked in slowly until i was up to my chin and stood there for ten minutes. I am going back in the am. This is starting to get really amazing. I test bg before ct and after. Ten minutes of ct will drop my bg 40 points. I try to ct 2 hrs post prandial if possible. Stick with it it gets easier everyday.

I've noticed the BG drop also. If I CT at night it drops but it doesn't stay that way by the time morning rolls around even though I haven't eaten anything.

### Dali Dula — 2012-03-19T14:14:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/walked-into-the-pond.281/post-2692>

> **Groketteliveson;2694 wrote:** I've noticed the BG drop also. If I CT at night it drops but it doesn't stay that way by the time morning rolls around even though I haven't eaten anything.

Yes, I have the same thing happening.

# Thread: Overly Zealous
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-19T13:53:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2688>

I've been doing CTing for 2 weeks now. After re-reading yesterday on Dr. K's blog I thought I would start spot icing my stomach as my lower stomach had extra fat and isn't the prettiest after two Caesarians. Yesterday, I thought I'd strap on some ice while I was busy working and it honestly never felt cold. I should have known better as I have nerve damage from the surgeries. However, after 45 minutes I removed the ice to see a VERY red belly. After some time the spot warmed up and got much warmer than the rest of my body. This morning I woke up to find it looking bruised. Has anyone else experienced this? I think maybe it's frostnip???

### vwaggs — 2012-03-19T16:09:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2730>

Is it painful? I think I remember someone posting this same thing on the blog comments and Dr. K said it was "normal" but I am not 100% sure. I would start the ice with a thin t-shirt in between the ice and your skin until you have done it a few times to get used to it. I know a lot of people have done this and it helped.

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-19T16:49:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2741>

No, it's not painful, but I don't have normal feeling in that region of my stomach anymore. However, it is very ugly!
[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/2749=7-2012-03-19_09-14-00_133.jpg)

### BJK77 — 2012-03-19T18:46:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2799>

Yes, I had something similar happen though it wasn't quite as dark of a bruise as you have. It took several days for it to go away, but it eventually did. I'm much more careful with the ice packs now. Did you have the ice directly on your skin? You should have a compression shirt in between skin and the ice.

### ealachan — 2012-03-19T18:52:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2805>

My stomach FEELS a little bruise-y where I've been icing it, but it hasn't actually gotten bruised-looking. I've been doing the ice in a bag against whatever thin shirt I have on at the time, not against the actual skin.

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-19T20:06:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2854>

I had it over my shirt in the beginning. I had my shirt tucked into my waistband and the icepack between my shirt and pants; however, I was grooming the dog and the shirt became untucked at some point and I didn't even notice.

> **BJK77;2808 wrote:** Yes, I had something similar happen though it wasn't quite as dark of a bruise as you have. It took several days for it to go away, but it eventually did. I'm much more careful with the ice packs now. Did you have the ice directly on your skin? You should have a compression shirt in between skin and the ice.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-19T20:16:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2862>

I haven't had any bruises - but when I started the icepacks it felt like a bruise in random patches on my gut.

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-19T20:51:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2880>

Go too far, risk tissue damage. Dr. K has mentioned Body Fat percentage is a variable with CT. I'd treat that bruise like a big "don't do that again" sign. PS you look great!

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-20T14:01:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-3187>

Do you mean to not spot ice again, or be much more careful to ensure that the ice doesn't get in direct contact with the skin? I'm determined to get rid of the extra insulation there  BTW, thanks for the compliment.

> **Chrisgraff;2889 wrote:** Go too far, risk tissue damage. Dr. K has mentioned Body Fat percentage is a variable with CT. I'd treat that bruise like a big "don't do that again" sign. PS you look great!

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-20T15:55:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-3230>

I'd lay off the spot icing until that bruise was completely healed. A frostbite scar isn't the result you're looking for! ;-)

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T16:19:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-3237>

Dr. K replied similarly to someone - lay off that spot until it's totally healed, then be sure the next time to keep cloth between you and the ice.

The first time or two I iced, I started with shirt, then moved to ice directly on skin. I got a bruised feeling area, but no actual bruise. I made sure to keep the shirt there the next few times, and now I don't need to bother with the shirt, no soreness, just a nice cherry red. Fat is leaving the area rapidly!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-20T17:05:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-3254>

> **JeffieJoe;2749 wrote:** No, it's not painful, but I don't have normal feeling in that region of my stomach anymore. However, it is very ugly!
> [](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/2749=7-2012-03-19_09-14-00_133.jpg)

A picture is worth a 1000 words!

Is it painful like a bruise? I have had no problem getting my skin down in temp with clothes between the ice a moi. I would totally let that heal before freezing that area again. It's an "adaptation".

I have not been able to tolerate cold showers more than 2 mins, but icing by area has been a breeze. One of the "benefits" of this is you don't feel pain in the same way with cold therapy, thus overdoing it might be pretty easy to do.

Compression clothes or even a towel between should help.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-20T23:50:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-3549>

Directly icing for too long is not the right approach in my opinion. You just need to the get the skin to 50-55F. Try 10 minutes on, 10 off or something like that. It doesn't have to hurt! As a matter of fact, if you are pushing it too hard, you're probably spiking stress hormones that can be counter-productive.

One little thing I'm trying: sucking ice cubes while I chill out in the tub. Dr. Kruse says,

> "This process is controlled by surface skin cold receptors and wiring from the mouth, gut, Peripheral Nervous System and to the Central Nervous System via the spinal cord and then to the brain. The brain gets inputs of this tract from the vagus nerve, and from a CD 36 receptor in the mouth which relays sensory inputs to the spinal cord and from the surface cold receptors.
>
>
>
> What does CD 36 do for mammals? CD36 is an oral receptor in the lingual papillae of taste buds that mediated perception of long-chain fatty acids. It involves the gustatory neural pathways in cranial nerve 9 and 10 (glossophayrngeal nerve and the vagus nerve). These inputs head to the floor of the fourth ventricle (area postrema) of the hind brain to synapse in the nucleus of the solitary tract. Here they interact with the somatic sensory cold receptor system of the face and of the body."

Sucking on a frozen sardine my just be the ticket!

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-28T00:13:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-7393>

> Compression clothes or even a towel between should help.

# Thread: Share your CT experiences

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-19T17:00:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-2745>

Hi All, I've been a lurker for years, but never felt compelled to join the forum until the Dr Kruse posted the Cold Thermogenesis protocol. I started this about 3 weeks ago, and once I started adding the ice packs last week with a compression shirt, my body started feeling like it was going through this transformation. Since i"m just starting this up, I was wondering if the people who have gone through this would chime in with their experiences. I've searched the thread, but didn't see to much with experiences all in one thread.

I'm being cautiously optimistic at this point, but feel like i might be finally busting through a weight loss plateau I have been stuck on for years.

Cheers!

### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-20T02:27:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3027>

please describe the transformation...

### AKMan — 2012-03-20T02:47:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3040>

My biggest fear with this whole CT craze that Dr. K got rolling here, is that people will get discouraged in a few weeks or months and quit.

One of the wildcards is Dr. K. will say something like, 'when you are cold adapted, you will lose weight, increase health, etc..' But, how do people in the real world know when they are cold adapted? A 30 year old lady, 30 lbs overweight, puts an icepack on her belly 3 nights a week...her husband laughs at her and she put on 5 lbs in a month. Was she cold adapted?

Same lady, does the same thing, but eats a completely keto diet--loses 5 lbs. Was she cold adapted?

All the studies I've read, and I've read a lot of studies! mostly point to BAT (brown fat) as the big winner in CT. If you can activate your BAT, it will definitely boost your metabolism. Trouble is, the only way to know if you've activated your BAT is through a PET scan under controlled conditions--doubt that's cheap!

However, all the studies I've read say that some, most, or all of us have some BAT. I tend to believe the 'all' camp. It is suggested BAT is greatly reduced in obese, elderly, and men. Young, thin, and women have the most. It's also said to be seasonal, changing with light and temp.

It has been well studied that brief exposures to cold activate BAT. One-time footbaths in 40 degree water result in BAT activity. Chronic cold exposure, as they put it, results in new BAT being formed, and old BAT being expanded.

I like to think that daily or 3X weekly icepacks, anywhere on the body, will stimulate growth of BAT. I like to think that daily ice-water immersion of the whole body will turn a person into BATMAN (pardon the pun).

I think ultimately the success of CT will hinge on whether people can do it long enough to activate and recruit new BAT. I doubt we will hear success stories other than, 'I broke a 6 mo stall', for a long time. It would be awesome to hear people saying 'I cured my diabetes' or 'my Alzheimer's went away' in a year or two, but even then there will be naysayers attributing results to something else. If someone doesn't get results after a year of doing CT, we will say, 'you musta cheated on your keto diet' or'you didn't get cold enough'.

Anyhow, CT has to be like exercising--just do it if you believe in it and don't freak if you don't get fast results. Find a way to give yourself some chronic cold exposure and see what happens.

And report back!

If you want to see some good studies, go to Google Scholar, type in 'cold thermogenics obesity BAT' or similar, then sort results for hits 2011 or newer. Awesome stuff out there!

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-20T03:07:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3063>

I've been doing hand and foot cold water baths to prep for full immersion baths and the arthritis in my hands has gone away! Unfortunately it comes back any day that I don't do this, but on the plus side it keeps me motivated to never skip a day  I noticed this effect day 1, so great instant gratification there.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-20T03:23:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3071>

Akman, I think you are spot on re: the wild card of how long it takes/how you know. It helps to know its not linear but I, for one, am not seeing amazing changes 3-4 weeks in. To make things worse, I am cold all the time now. I go numb in the bath so I stop shivering, but as soon as I get out it takes me a few hours to get warm again. Then I keep drinking my iced drinks and try not to wear too much clothing and I'm cold again. I realize the specifics of how long it will take are unique to each of our situations, but it's good to be reminded that it's not an easy or immediate arrival. Cause when you add in the carb cravings that have come back since stating CT with the feeling cold.... Well it takes a lot of faith and tough mindedness to keep going.

### SuzieQ — 2012-03-20T03:53:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3086>

It has help to cure my insomnia. I have been Ct'ing since the beginning of Feb, only skipping 2 days so far. My skin has really gotten dry though in the areas that I CT. No weight loss or inch lose yet.

### SuzieQ — 2012-03-20T04:16:51+0200

ooh almost forgot, I have no idea if this is connected to CT but nothing else has really changed.... No matter how much I brush my teeth and floss I always get bad build of plaque behind my bottom teeth. It is always an issue when I get my teeth cleaned every 6 months. Well, today I was running my tongue along the inside of my bottom teeth and I felt some loose stuff. I ran my finger along the back of my front teeth and out came chunks of harden plaque. I kept wiping my finger back there and each time I did, the more the plaque came off. I was so excited that I had to show my husband. shrugs\*

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-20T14:28:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3196>

So I know this is really early in the game. I have been doing the ice packs for about an hour each night starting a week ago. Last night I did my second immersion bath (I tired a 10 minute one on the weekend) and I did 15 minutes.

Here's the things I've noticed - I wake up in the middle of the night, hot and sweaty. I don't notice any difference in my sleeping patterns - but I have always been a sound sleeper (been doing a paleo type diet for 12+ years so maybe that helps).

When i first started the ice packs, I notice some sore spots on my fat, but that has pretty much gone away after 4-5 days.

I have lost 3 pounds since starting the ice packs last week.

I go through periods of having amazing energy and thought processes, to being utterly tired and I need a nap in the day.

My belly fat feels less dense and more fluid. I wish I had measured my waist when I started, but it just feels like it is shrinking.

I honestly didn't expect anything to happen at this point, so that's why I was wondering about people who have been doing this longer than me - what is down the road from here?

### Destiny — 2012-03-20T14:58:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3207>

What am I doing wrong? I was 152 lbs in October and started on low carb, no wheat at that time because after always being 130-135 lbs I needed to do something.

I lost 2 lbs on it before starting LR in December on which I lost additional 3 lbs so I was down to 147 lbs. During that time my fasting blood sugar was climbing up well over 110.

I had salivary testing done in January. That one showed that my estrogen was almost nonexistent, however, my DHEAs was high for my age (50). My testosterone was in the upper range, my progesterone too was OK (have been supplementing). My cortisol was decent in the morning, a bit lower at noon and spiking at night.

I got estrogen supplementation in February. Once I started, my sugar went down to 80th and I was hoping that my weight will go down. That did not happen. I started TC at the end of February. **This morning my weight was up at 150 lbs and I have increased appetite** but I still follow the LR rules. That really crushed me. What else can I do? If I have enough DHEAs, should I take Pregnenolone or not?

I am not sure what else to do. Where is my problem? All of this has been working for my husband! He started at 229lbs and is 196lbs!! We do the same stuff and I do it more precisely. He gets results and I don't. Why? I am really frustrated this morning.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T15:01:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3208>

Because you're a woman. Yeah, it sucks, but we have much more complicated hormonal stuff going on. Do you think the weight is fat, or could it be water retention? I gained 6lb in one day two weeks ago, and it went away over 9 days. It was obvious it was water, b/c I didn't even eat 6lb worth of food, but a lesser amount can be deceiving. I also measure a lot, and the tape tells me when I'mholding on to water - my wrist and calf swell. My arms and thighs do, too, but I know it's pure water when my calf goes up half an inch in a day.

I have no idea what the answer to water retention is, but it's not at all the same thing as gaining fat, so it's an important question.

### ealachan — 2012-03-20T17:46:24+0200

I started supplementing K2 a couple months ago (and I eat a ton of pastured eggs), and flossing my teeth the other day, a fair amount of plaque that had seemed really hard and weren't moved by the floss before, crumbled and came off my teeth all in one fell swoop. ### JC2K — 2012-03-20T17:55:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3292>

One thing I've notice so far is some pretty funky tiredness at night. It all started after the time change though. Guess it was a bad idea to start CT at the same time.  Essentially what happens is I get totally crushed around 7pm. Hit bed and sleep like the dead till 12-1am. Then I'm up the rest of the night and can't sleep. I used to stay up till 11pm/midnight before and sleep till 8am or so. I've been trying to push that back an hour or so to adjust it by forcing staying awake. Still working on that though.

Note, this is pretty much happening every night so far. Not doing CT every day either.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-20T18:00:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3295>

> **JC2K;3309 wrote:** My fear is that people will not pay attention to the protocol, go way overboard early and hurt themselves, then the protocol get's blamed for it and gets bad press. I can see it in the news now "person gets frostbite following the kahrazy Dr K protocol to lose fat." add in misc non-related doctors saying how dangerous and unproven it is and boom, all without giving it a chance...

This is what I'm thinking too. People get tied up in neolithic thinking and want everything now, now, now, which leads to skipping steps in the protocol and going harder and faster than what is advisable.

Also, I think people outside the Paleo community who get wind of this might try the CT without addressing the diet, which could also be problematic, especially if they are eating SAD and have totally f-ed up O3/O6 ratios.

To share my experience thus far, it's been on a non-linear trend towards awesomeness. I have experienced gains in energy and sex drive (and enjoyment - wink); decreases in cravings/appetite; improvements in body comp/weight; increases in tolerance to cold; improved mental clarity; improved sleep (though my circadian clock is still waking me up with a lovely shot of cortisol an hour before I need to wake up due to the stupid time change). I've also experienced hot flashes (or a sort), loose stools (still experiencing this, but it's getting better), and momentary dips in energy. I've been CTing for 2 weeks. I wouldn't say I'm adapted yet, but I'm on my way!

I seem to sleep really well until 1 AM or so and then I'm "wired, but tired" and just cannot get back to sleep.

It worries me that it's an adrenal issue, but I thought CT was supposed to correct those issues.

maybe be I'm starting to see CT Tolerance... Whats funny its the upper body that's shivering...trunk/legs no shivers???

also sitting down to a lunch of bacon while I work... got to get into keto!

>
>
>
> And report back!
>
>
>
> If you want to see some good studies, go to Google Scholar, type in 'cold thermogenics obesity BAT' or similar, then sort results for hits 2011 or newer. Awesome stuff out there!

Thank you for this post, because it is just what I needed to read today! I have been icing, as well as getting in 9 soaks in 54 degree water, for 40 minutes at a time, over a ten day period. I am doing this to improve my lower spine, and if I lose a couple of pounds in the mean time, that is just gravy. I am having a problem, however, in that the first 6 soaks were very doable, but the last three are getting increasingly more difficult. Even though the water temperature is the same day-to-day my ability to remain in that frigid water is waning. Yesterday, while I was soaking, my thighs never did get to the doable point. The goosebumps and the stinging from the cold never quit the entire time I was in the water. I am afraid it might be even worse when I do my soaking later today. I make sure to keep my feet out of the water, and I only have the water up to my waist. Has anyone else had this happen? I thought that it was going to get more tolerable, not less.

If this continues to get increasingly more painful (yes actually painful), then I guess I will have to resort to the icing. I also notice, that when I use the ice wraps on my upper arms (were the most of my adipose fat likes to reside!) that that process is probably the hardest for me to maintain. I have every intention to get cold adapted, but if spot icing will do the job then I just might move that way if soaking does not improve.

I will not know if the protocol of diet and CT is making a difference until I get a DEXA, sometime this year - seems like a long time away. Thankfully, I have this forum to help keep me going.

Susan M.

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T18:52:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3331>

> **BJK77;3324 wrote:** It worries me that it's an adrenal issue, but I thought CT was supposed to correct those issues.

I'm giving it time to shake out. Doesn't seem to be an adrenal issue to me so far. I'm not too concerned with it. Yet.  I'm fine during the day, it's just a wall I'm hitting at night. I do recall something somewhere about needing less sleep on CT.

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T19:07:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3342>

> **Shijin13;3336 wrote:** Ok...Why is it I no longer shiver while in a cold bath? now that its getting warm - maybe my tap water isn't cold enough? today after my CT session in the bath I got out dried off, and started a CT Spot session... my trunk is cold for the 1st time and I'm shivering again...normally my feet feel cold -but right now I feel like heat is rolling off them...
>
> maybe be I'm starting to see CT Tolerance... Whats funny its the upper body that's shivering...trunk/legs no shivers???

Check the temp of your water. Could be warmer than you think, could be you're adjusting. Hard to tell. The CT spot stuff is definitely colder than the water though.

For me, the shivering is pretty much limited to the areas that are cold (so the trunk shivering and legs not shivering would be normal for me).

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-20T19:14:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3349>

>
> For me, the shivering is pretty much limited to the areas that are cold (so the trunk shivering and legs not shivering would be normal for me).

I'm bright cherry red when I get out... and my body temp according to IR temp gun ranges btwn 56-62degress...wondering if the warmer temps is making it easier... yeah for me its all upper body shakes... non on the lower body at all..

### vkiernan — 2012-03-20T20:08:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3390>

I have had headaches and nausea from the CT baths for a few weeks - 30 minutes in 50 degree water for 3 weeks. That finally went away. I never shiver in the tub, only when I get out. An hour or so later I'm good but now I do small exercises to warm me up afterwards. Just started this this past week and it works fairly well. What I noticed this weekend was after working outside cleaning flower beds and gathering 5 trailer loads of wood with my dh, I was not a bit sore and I have not been working out due to adrenal fatigue. I expected something to hurt and not one thing. I worked for 3 days outside. Crazy. Got to be something to it. I might just start doing weights before dinner, then eat and sit in the tub afterwards like Dr. K would do and see if I get any soreness. In the process of getting firewood, my tendonitis flared but I felt nothing the next day. I'm a believer and not giving up. And yes, I think everyone's mileage may vary on this depending what we are all dealing with.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-20T20:34:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3405>

I love the pics on here. . . so here goes.

I've seen comments from some worried about some of the negatives. Thanks for commenting, it made this a little less scary for me!

Today was my 2nd day of icing torso exactly per the RX. I had been doing two weeks of face dunks, working up to 20 mins. I had tried several cold showers and could only do

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-20T20:40:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3410>

Enjoying reading everyone's experiences so far.

Susan M, for what it's worth - I took 8 baths (30-45 min each) spread out over 2.5 weeks before I had any kind of pain in extremities. Then out of nowhere, the 10th bath caused my toes and feet to ache and just plain hurt. Took longer to warm up that particular night afterward also. There were 3 days between it and the next bath, but no pain on the next bath and I used more ice to get the water a little colder. I believe it really is as Dr. Kruse has said - nonlinear. Our body may take things in stride and then one day have trouble, then back to business as usual, then trouble. Maybe this is part of the exercise in learning to listen to our bodies and trust it instead of overriding it with our neolithic thinking.

Options you can try that others have had success with is keeping toes/feet out of water or wearing socks/neoprene. If your upper arms are the hardest, keep them out of the water or icing and add them later when the full baths are easier to tolerate. I can't tolerate much cold on the backs of my arms and that's one of the places I need it the most. I'm going to get there, just maybe slower than I want. You will too.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-20T20:51:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3421>

Thanks Clabbergirl. I just finished my fatty bf and my cold water and am getting ready to do it again. We will see if there is more or less pain today. I do keep my piggies out of the water as much as I can using neoprene water shoes, and that does help. I don't soak from the waist up, as a matter of fact, I wear a sports bra during soak time otherwise I am just too cold to tolerate the entire 40 mins. I will be icing my arms this evening while I am reading. It's odd, but the icing of my arms is worse than the soaking, but I still can't make myself lay down in the tub to cover my arms. I was always one of those weenie kids that hated to get splashed, and now here I am torturing MYSELF with cold water. Who'd thunk!

Gotta embrace the cold ........ !

Susan M.

### Kathy — 2012-03-20T21:43:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3461>

I'm on the 12th day of face dunking & have also been keeping the house cooler, wearing fewer clothes & lowering the hot water in the shower - not cold showers, just cooler...

This morning I was feeling quite chilly so decided to "treat" myself to a warmer shower. I noticed that the water felt much warmer on my face than it did on the rest of my body. When I turnded down the hot water and it reversed: the cooler water felt colder on other parts of my body than my face! Maybe just my face is cold adapting?!?

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-20T21:45:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3462>

> **Cavemam;3458 wrote:** Oh good lord I hope not! If you have this rash from CTing - don't read the wiki on "cherry angiomas". I'm assuming it will go away after awhile. Didn't mean to alarm you, lol. I asked because I've noticed just this morning that I'm getting little red dots all over my upper thighs - places and in a concentration I'm 98 percent positive I've not had before. I've always had the odd red dot here and there (assumed they were those cherry angiomas as a doc told me once), but these are tinier and prolific - similar to what your photo shows. Noticing these on my legs this morning was enough to snap me out of my regular early morning stupor because I have not noticed these. I thought, _great, CT is going to make me have MORE of these things??_ (I don't have any itchiness, however.)

I have been submerging my legs in the CT baths so I can attribute it to cold water on them at least, unlike your experience. I seem to remember reading something Dr. Kruse wrote about cherry angiomas (if this is in fact what they are for me?) ...off to research this further.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-20T23:53:41+0200

> **Clabbergirl;3434 wrote:** \]
>
> If your upper arms are the hardest, keep them out of the water or icing and add them later when the full baths are easier to tolerate. I can't tolerate much cold on the backs of my arms and that's one of the places I need it the most. I'm going to get there, just maybe slower than I want. You will too.

Good advice. I was putting my upper arms in the bath at the 10 minute mark but that made the remaining 35 minutes just a shiver-fest, plus terrible shakes after the bath for more than an hour. The last 2 nights I've excluded my arms and the bath was far more comfortable and post-bath warm-up was easier. No need to rush through this - just let the body adapt on its own schedule.

### Destiny — 2012-03-20T23:57:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3557>

> **MamaGrok;3231 wrote:** Because you're a woman. Yeah, it sucks, but we have much more complicated hormonal stuff going on. Do you think the weight is fat, or could it be water retention? I gained 6lb in one day two weeks ago, and it went away over 9 days. It was obvious it was water, b/c I didn't even eat 6lb worth of food, but a lesser amount can be deceiving. I also measure a lot, and the tape tells me when I'mholding on to water - my wrist and calf swell. My arms and thighs do, too, but I know it's pure water when my calf goes up half an inch in a day.
>
>
>
> I have no idea what the answer to water retention is, but it's not at all the same thing as gaining fat, so it's an important question.

I understand, however, it is hard to be almost the same weight I was 6 months ago after all the effort I put in.

# Thread: Arctic Heat Vest, quick review
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arctic-heat-vest-quick-review.296/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### L8F — 2012-03-19T21:11:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arctic-heat-vest-quick-review.296/post-2893>

Ok, so I activated (soaked in water) the vest for the first time today. I soaked for about ten minutes and watched it grow nicely. Jelly-like filling in the tubes sewn into the vest. Then I spun it in a salad spinner to remove excess water. Then I froze it. It came out stiff and frozen. At first it was too tight and cold to zip up, but it rapidly softened once out of the freezer, but it is still clearly frozen. I am now able to zip it.

I also have the neck thing which is a really large bib thing that is a bit awkward to tie around the front of the neck, but must say it fits nicely over my lower belly where the vest doesn't cover. I like it so far.

PROS: Easy to wear a bunch of frozen stuff around, and doesn't add much bulk, looks decent, and gym wear-ish.

Easy to freeze and use.

CONs: Vest is really an upper torso icer. Cold strips end above the bottom of my ribs. Must use other methods for abdominal colding. This is not good for me since Belly fat is key area to target. That being said, the neck thing is like a belly apron for me right now.

Haven't yet frozen the tie on cold strips that I also got. This feels really nice to wear, and is nice in this warm sunny weather we are having. I can see why athletes love to wear this in the heat.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/2902=8-vest1.jpg)

>

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-19T21:41:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arctic-heat-vest-quick-review.296/post-2911>

Thanks for the photo, certainly something that might be great to grab out of the f reezer for a car ride or chores without much drama. Does it look like you could put two together to make a long hanging apron to cover more?

### L8F — 2012-03-19T21:50:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arctic-heat-vest-quick-review.296/post-2919>

Hmm, putting two together would be hard, unless you think two zippered vests would be easy to rework (I don't sew and they are $$$).

I am also long in the torso, so I am bummed a bit, and need more contraptions.

The size small fits me as in the pic above, and I am 5'7"+ and chest about 36 B/C with long torso. It is snug, but not stifling.

Oh yeah, the grey bulging stripes are the cold stuff, but it does seem to keep whole torso cool, though I haven't taken skin temps yet cause I don't feel like it right now.

if we could only find thigh trousers like that. LOL I just got out of the very cold pool. I stayed in until I started shivering, about 20 minutes. It's windy and cooler than normal here in Phoenix (about 62) but it sure felt colder than that in that pool! The water is down to 50 degrees today.

# Thread: All seafood or just cold waters seafood.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-seafood-or-just-cold-waters-seafood.298/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-19T21:44:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-seafood-or-just-cold-waters-seafood.298/post-2916>

In the efforts to eat more water food, is everything good - or do we need to focus on cold water varieties? Also, any hints for canned/tinned seafood consumption?

### hazyjane — 2012-03-19T22:20:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-seafood-or-just-cold-waters-seafood.298/post-2932>

I've been eating a lot more cold water fatty fish like canned Alaskan pink salmon (I like to make salmon cakes with it,), trout and smoked sardines in olive oil. I've been eating more shrimp, too, for the iodine.

### AKMan — 2012-03-20T01:21:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-seafood-or-just-cold-waters-seafood.298/post-3001>

I'm going mostly for the salt-water fishy's. They have more iodine and omega 3. Fresh water fish is probably fine, too, but mostly what you get in the grocery store is Talapia and that just seems like a crap fish to me, but I may be wrong. This summer I will be eating lots of fresh caught trout and pike.

### Adriana — 2012-03-24T09:21:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-seafood-or-just-cold-waters-seafood.298/post-5343>

> **Cavemam;2926 wrote:** Also, any hints for canned/tinned seafood consumption?

I made some great salmon patties this week with canned salmon:

1 large can salmon

2 eggs

2 tbsp coconut flour

1 tsp dijon mustard

3 sliced green onions

Brown in a skillet in butter. Nom!

### Adriana — 2012-03-24T09:25:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-seafood-or-just-cold-waters-seafood.298/post-5345>

> **Cavemam;2926 wrote:** In the efforts to eat more water food, is everything good - or do we need to focus on cold water varieties? Also, any hints for canned/tinned seafood consumption?

I made some great salmon patties this week with canned salmon?

1 large can salmon

2 eggs

2 tbsp coconut flour

1 tbsp lemon juice

1 tsp dijon mustard

3 sliced green onions

Brown in a skillet in butter. Nom!

### Birdy — 2012-03-24T19:03:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-seafood-or-just-cold-waters-seafood.298/post-5473>

Thanks for the recipe. I am craving salmon patties this week. Do you fish out the bones or leave them all in?
