# Thread: Question re: LR and IF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### esam5 — 2012-03-19T22:45:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-2941>

I am 3 weeks into the reset. I was reading over at another forum where Dr. K is also active where IF is not reccomended until leptin sensitivity is reset as it can cause harm. here is my situation, then my question is at the end......

my meals are 7am, 1pm and 7pm ( I usually go to bed around 10:30 or so....)

I eat a BAB, about 50gms protein, low carbs, and generous fat for satiety ( want to increase my protein to lose weight)

I do not snack, it has on occassion been a challenge, but I truly have been faithful.

my other meals are generally very low carb as carbs trigger cravings. I eat predominately meat, eggs, more meat!! I do have butter, mayo, CO, and some cream in my morning coffee. I keep protein shakes to a minimum, and I'm working to eliminate cheese. ( as it isn't paleo)

I'm trying to keep my water intake up and I try not to have anything processed ( triggers)

my goal is to lose about 80lbs ...I'm 5'9" and am 248.

Sometimes, after lunch, my stomach is upset and at dinner time I'm not hungry, so I won't eat. last night was a good example, I didn't feel true hunger until around 10pm then it was too late, so I went to bed without eating. Lunch was around 2pm. I'm not really fasting on purpose, but should I have eaten something small around 7pm? or is it ok to skip if I wasn't hungry? I'd say in the last 3 weeks, I've only skipped a meal three times. it is usually dinner, or lunch. Never breakfast.

I'm really committed to this program, I'm very strict with my eating schedule and with 5 kids in the house, I'm doing really well to giving up snacking!!!!!

### Owl — 2012-03-20T07:18:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3161>

Intermittent fasting. Skipping meals.

### Owl — 2012-03-20T07:27:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3163>

> **esam5;2951 wrote:** Sometimes, after lunch, my stomach is upset and at dinner time I'm not hungry, so I won't eat. last night was a good example, I didn't feel true hunger until around 10pm then it was too late, so I went to bed without eating. Lunch was around 2pm. I'm not really fasting on purpose, but should I have eaten something small around 7pm? or is it ok to skip if I wasn't hungry? I'd say in the last 3 weeks, I've only skipped a meal three times. it is usually dinner, or lunch. Never breakfast.

The question is, why is your stomach upset after lunch? Are you eating too large of a meal?

It's ok to eat just breakfast and lunch. Or just breakfast and dinner. The goal is to eventually eat two meals per day, and breakfast and late lunch is fine. Some people go to two meals right away, some take longer.

Dr. K doesn't want you to IF if you're hungry. But if you're not, go ahead and skip a meal.

If you get into the habit of being hungry late at night, go ahead and eat a small snack for dinner. You don't want to go to bed hungry every night.

I usually eat only breakfast and a late lunch. But if I feel like I didn't eat enough for lunch, I'll have just a few bites of meat for dinner to keep from getting hungry later.

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-20T15:34:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3217>

It's OK to skip dinner if you are not hungry. And it's OK to eliminate snacking for the children too. Snacking sets up lifelong behaviors and takes the emphasis off eating a good meal.

### esam5 — 2012-03-20T20:14:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3392>

> **Owl;3185 wrote:** The question is, why is your stomach upset after lunch? Are you eating too large of a meal?

that may be it, but I had my gall bladder removed last year and sometimes I get those acid-y burps and such. I get heartburn still and sometimes get nauseaus. Even right before I get "hungry" I feel a bit nauseus. I don't know what exactly is going on. it goes away, but is uncomfortable.

I will try to cut back on portion size, and I am cutting out dairy as well,. I really want to get to just eating 2 meals a day, it would make me happy.

my hunger will go away, it doesn't keep gnawing at me.... I do notice when I don't have enough protein at breakfast I get more hungry during the day. thats why I like my BAB to set the pace......

### ealachan — 2012-03-20T20:28:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3402>

> **esam5;3416 wrote:** that may be it, but I had my gall bladder removed last year and sometimes I get those acid-y burps and such. I get heartburn still and sometimes get nauseaus. Even right before I get "hungry" I feel a bit nauseus. I don't know what exactly is going on. it goes away, but is uncomfortable.
>
> I will try to cut back on portion size, and I am cutting out dairy as well,. I really want to get to just eating 2 meals a day, it would make me happy.
>
>
>
> my hunger will go away, it doesn't keep gnawing at me.... I do notice when I don't have enough protein at breakfast I get more hungry during the day. thats why I like my BAB to set the pace......

For what it's worth, I had my gallbladder out in November of 2008 and it took the best part of two years for my system to find its "new normal" in terms of digestion. Doctors seem to like to tell people "Oh, you don't need a gallbladder", but that's not _actually_ true. It has a very specific function and your body DOES need it. When you have it taken out, your body has to adapt to NOT having it, which can take quite a while, depending on a lot of factors.

### Owl — 2012-03-20T20:56:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3427>

I'm not an expert on this, but have you tried Betaine HCL? I know a lot of people here find heartburn/GERD much better when using HCL. Reflux is usually caused by too little stomach acid.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T04:25:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3760>

or if you don\`t have a gallbladder I believe purified oxbile is recommended as well

### esam5 — 2012-03-22T18:57:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-4507>

thanks for the suggestions,

I have not tried either of those. I will take a prilosec once in awhile. I have found that I tend to get reflux several hours after I eat. and I may be having a bit too big of a meal, but certainly no larger than I would have eaten prior to this WOE.

yesterdays late lunch was about a 7-8oz steak, grilled, and 3 small romaine leaves chopped, and a 2inch piece of cucumber cut up, some italian dressing and SF jello cup. Considering that I had only 2 meals yesterday, that seemed fine for me, but about 3-4 hours after I ate I was burping up my salad. (yeah, I know, GROSS) sorry.

good to know that it will take awhile to get to my new normal.

I was very surprised that they removed my gall bladder, I had only the one attack, but it was a doosey, was in the ER, then admitted me and my test results even 24hours later were pretty high, it was very inflamed. (ice cream was the last thing I ate before the attack, I have no desire to eat it ever again!!!)

I assume these are both supplements I can get at my local healthfood store???

# Thread: Frozen shrimp
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### L8F — 2012-03-19T23:52:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-2960>

So I eat a lot of frozen wild shrimp. It is easy and tasty. BUT, I cannot seem to get it to dry out before sautÃƒÂ©ing. Then it turns more like a watery cooked shrimp. Still tasty, but not the oily, buttery sautÃƒÂ©ed shrimp that I am looking for. Any suggestions? Baking in oven to dry, will that work? And how long?

### ealachan — 2012-03-20T02:30:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-3029>

It's kind of a pain in the butt, but if you thaw them out in cold running water and then let them sit in the fridge (loosely covered) for a few hours before you want to cook with them, it should help.

Or, take some paper towel or a clean tea towel and pat the shrimp dry once they're thawed, right before you toss them in the pan. This is what I usually do.

### L8F — 2012-03-20T19:20:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-3354>

Thanks! I was thinking about it this AM while still sleepy, and wondered if steaming them cooked, and THEN pan frying would solve the excess water leak issue...Also pain in the butt, but might rid the excess water. I am dreaming of a scampi type thing,

### ealachan — 2012-03-20T19:30:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-3368>

You'd probably overcook them if you did it that way. Overcooking turns them really chewy. Shrimp, like most seafood, is not very forgiving when it comes to overcooking. There's a pretty small margin between "cooked just enough" and "ooh, shrimp-shaped erasers in garlic sauce!"

When I'm cooking seafood, I tend to err on the side of very slight undercooking, because the carry-over heat from the pan and the plate will finish the cooking by the time you go to actually eat the dish.

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-21T01:05:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-3612>

Don't waste that yummy broth that comes out of frozen shrimp!

I use frozen, too. I never really sauté them for exactly that reason but throw the whole bag frozen into a pot, boil them (they will release enough fluid), then drain the broth, put it in the blender with some spices and fat, whizz and use it as a sauce/soup.

You could probably throw them in to a frying pan after this process but I think there really is the danger of overcooking if you're sensitive to that.

### L8F — 2012-03-21T03:16:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-3713>

> **ealachan;3392 wrote:** There's a pretty small margin between "cooked just enough" and "ooh, shrimp-shaped erasers in garlic sauce!"

Hahahaha. Thanks guys, I guess I will just suck it up and go with saucy slightly watery shimp sautés. Will have to find a good wild source restaurant for scampi once I decide to retry butter.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T03:28:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-3726>

Salt! You thaw them, salt them, then leave them on a napkin/towel in the fridge. Alternately, you brine them then just paper towel dry them.

### L8F — 2012-03-22T14:38:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-4323>

Cool!! Thanks. Will try tonight. Have some defrosting in fridge

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-22T15:32:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-4352>

When you can have butter again I've got a really good Cajun shrimp recipe to share. \*It supposed to be shrimp and grits but I don't make the grits portion of it. \*In the past I substituted rice for the grits. \*I was thinking you could probably substitute shredded cauliflour if you wanted instead. \*But since it's cooked in a sauce it wouldnt matter if you shrimp turned out watery or not.

Peel and devain about a pound of shrimp or more

Coat shrimp with about 2 Tblsp. Of Tonys Louisianna Cajun seasoning. \*I use this brand because the other brands can be too salty! \*You will also \*have to play around with the seasoning according to your own tastes.

Also throw in for the coating

1Tbsp. Italian seasoning

1Tbsp of paprika and some pepper to to taste

Saute the shrimp in a pan with some melted butter and minced garlic till cooked. \*Pull out and set aside.

With all the drippings still in the pan you can make a sauce if you wish by adding a little bit of cornstarch( or some other appropriate thickener) and cook for about 5 to 10 minutes till a light color brown. \*Slowly add some chicken stock and some heavy whipping cream, add a little bit of worcestershire \*sauce and maybe some hot sauce to taste. \*You can add bacon or ham to the sauce if you wish.

When sauce is finished mix the shrimp in the sauce or just drizzle the sauce over the shrimp and over the bed of cauliflower.

I have to say it is really spicy so if you like spice it's great! \*

### L8F — 2012-03-22T16:12:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-4382>

yum....thi is the stuff I have been craving. I am making do with shrimp in ghee and chopped garlic and turmeric. Gotta get cooking.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-29T16:56:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-8258>

I stocked up on shrimp last week when Whole Foods had their one-day special in them. I've been steaming them until almost done and then sauteeing. Seems to work well. They're not soggy anyway!

# Thread: CT weight loss results
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoMom — 2012-03-20T00:29:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-2974>

I've been CT'ing for a bit over a week now. I'm just getting into the baths but at a wimpy 68 deg. I haven't had any help in the fat loss department yet. I've been doing face dunks, cool showers and tons of spot icing on stomach, whole back, legs.

How long til others noticed fat loss and was it only after being able to do full on ice baths?

Thanks!

### AKMan — 2012-03-20T01:18:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-2999>

I'm a guy, so we're different (you probably knew that). I started CT'ing in bitter cold Alaskan air and doing all I could to stay cooler than normal. I had been on a stall since November, but after about 3 weeks, I was down 5 lbs. I'm pretty much at my goal weight, but have that spare tire, loose skin we all get--thyat seems to be disappearing very well.

I hope you get serious and give this a good try, there's not many people doing this and everybody we have doing it and reporting back will let us know how it works. It's definitely not a 'get skinny overnight' kinda deal.

Good luck!

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-20T01:34:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3008>

I won't be giving up, that is for sure. I'm in this for way more than weight loss, but I'm certainly looking forward to that. I'm only looking to lose about 7 pounds, so hopefully it will happen fast once it starts.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-20T02:52:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3043>

5 lbs. down since starting CT in mid-Feb.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-20T03:33:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3077>

No weight lost since starting 4 weeks ago, but BF-percentage went down quite fast in the first week, and muscle is really coming out... so i think I've lsot fat but gained muscle (I lift heavy).

### Rhoswen — 2012-03-20T07:25:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3162>

I'm down nearly 10 pounds since starting CT February 22nd.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-20T10:34:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3174>

A week in and I've gained 5lbs but lost 2 inches around the tummy. Baths are daily and getting colder after my first "too cold" one, and my daily shower is now cold as well! Even better in some ways is that our main source of heat for the house is no longer safe to use, so the top temperature inside is now a balmy 53.6F, and I'm resisting adding layers

### David — 2012-03-20T13:59:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3186>

Two weeks of face dunks (57F skin temp), a week of concurrent 30 minute cold showers (63-64F water temp) in the sixth week of Leptin Rx: daily BAB, 6 hours between meals, no snacks, nothing after seven-thirty PM, less than 25 g carbs daily, all Rx supplements recommended by Dr. Kruse and I sleep in a pitch black bedroom. Since beginning CT my weight is up two pounds. I had been following a Paleo diet since last November and lost 35 pounds. I will start the next step, icing the torso today.

### MartiD — 2012-03-20T14:02:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3188>

Being doing baths pretty consistent since end of February and added ice spotting belly this week. Haven't done measurements but am down about 3-4 pounds plus reshaping hips and waste. Working on lower belly and thighs still but I also have the funny feeling fat....seems softer, lighter if that makes sense!

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-20T14:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3200>

down 3 lbs in one week since starting the ice packs.

Some background - I have been at this current plateau for many many years after losing 50 llbs on a low carb/paleo type diet.

I haven't changed my eating or exercising pattern - the only thing different is the CT.

# Thread: Is it poseible to go from LR to LS in less than 2 weeks?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evo Mama — 2012-03-20T00:37:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/post-2979>

A little background ... I was LC Primal for nearly two years before just recently simultaneously doing the LRx and CT. Before starting, I was stalled at about 15-20 pounds of fat from ideal body composition. I was in good health, but not optimal at all. Since, I've lost at least 5-6 pounds of fat and have all the signs of LS in his Leptin FAQ, but didn't really have these signs before starting the LRx & CT (which makes me think I was LR before).

But is that possible? Dr. K says 6-8 weeks, but also said CT speeds it up. But that fast?

What do you all think? Possible?

PS - Sorry about the typo in the title. If a mod sees this, please correct. Thanks.

### L8F — 2012-03-20T00:39:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/post-2981>

I would say that it is, esp since you were already LC Primal for so long giving you a nice jumpstart to all the bad stuff--meaning grains and leaky-ness. Also you have new signs, and didn't have them before. I think for me it took just over 4 weeks, and possibly sooner...

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-20T11:08:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/post-3175>

> **L8F;3003 wrote:** I would say that it hhis, esp since you were already LC Primal for so long giving you a nice jumpstart to all the bad stuff--meaning grains and leaky-ness. Also you have new signs, and didn't have them before. I think for me it took just over 4 weeks, and possibly sooner...

Thanks. I'm going to give it a couple more weeks, just in case, then I'll move to the Post Leptin Reset protocol with CT for a bit longer to see if I can get to improved/ideal body comp before the summer. Then, I hope to enjoy a short summer before going keto and doing CT, starting in late summer. I guess the bottom line is I feel much better than before I started, and I'm filled with optimism.

Once I fully fix myself, I'm looking forward to use this info to help others. So freakin exciting!

### mattlovell — 2012-03-20T21:08:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/post-3434>

hi - where do you find the leptin diet please?

thanks!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T21:10:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/post-3435>

Mattlovell - google search "My leptin prescription"; I think that's the title of D.r Kruse's main LR post.

evo mama - what is the CT protocol you've been using (type, frequency, duration...)?

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-21T01:51:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/post-3636>

> **MamaGrok;3460 wrote:** Mattlovell - google search "My leptin prescription"; I think that's the title of D.r Kruse's main LR post.
>
>
>
> evo mama - what is the CT protocol you've been using (type, frequency, duration...)?

Wake up, quick progressively cold shower (10 min), BAB, facial plunges (10 min), all of that daily, then 30-45 min baths every other night. Water out of tap is about 50 degrees. No real icing yet.

I looked at the Life Extension PQQ. There is one without soy or rice. If you live in the US this might work well. Here is the link:

[http://www.lef.org/Vitamins-Supplements/Item01568/Mitochondrial-Energy-Optimizer-with-BioPQQ.html?source=search&key=PQQ](http://http://www.lef.org/Vitamins-Supplements/Item01568/Mitochondrial-Energy-Optimizer-with-BioPQQ.html?source=search&key=PQQ)

Unfortunately, I live in Canada and some of the ingredients are restricted for shipment. I have found a brand in Canada which has 20mg PQQ and 300mg co-enzyme CoQ10 (unfortunately not the ubiquinol type). Here is the link:

[http://www.aor.ca/html/products.php?id=256](http://http://www.aor.ca/html/products.php?id=256)

I just noticed you are from Australia. I hope those leads are useful were you live. Good luck in your search.

# Thread: Hack my labs: LDL and Total Cholesterol have gone psycho on Paleo/Leptin/CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 42
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-20T04:35:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/post-3104>

Hey all,

I'm freaking out a bit about my latest labs.

Quick sketch: I'm 45 y.o. without major medical issues, eating paleodiet for 6 months, VLC x 1 month (mostly meat, fish, butter, eggs, coconut oil, occasional cream, dark chocolate, etc. no grain, no sugar, some veggies and fruit occasionally, but very little carbs overall< 50 grams/day. I've been doing CT daily. I'm up to 45 minute baths in 50 F water daily for the past 10 days, and cold air exercise for a month prior to that.

Here's the good I've seen so far: weight down from 185ish to 165ish, body composition much improved, mental health much better, sleep is better, I work out with a kettle-bell 4 days a week, hike, sprint, garden, etc. I feel good!

I'm cool with the following: TGs 58, HDL 55, DHEA 5.8, HS-CRP 2.2, cortisol, magnesium, CBC, CMP, TSH all normal, Vitamin D 39, HBa1c 5.6.

Here's what I'm a bit freaked about: total LDLp increased from 1587 to 3076, small LDLp increased 534 to 869, total cholesterol shot up from 224 to 343, and LDL went from 153 to 276. This change is from 6 months ago. This is from a Lipid NMR test.

I've been very strict on my diet because I really wanted to see what it could do for me, and for the most part I'm totally happy about my progress, but I'm a bit nervous about my LDL, TC and LDL particles now. They are nutso high. I can't imagine what a cardiologist would be putting me through if I went in with these...well actually I can, but I digress.

I'm a family doc and have been recommending a paleodiet for some of my patients that want to give it a try. I follow everyone's progress closely with the above set of labs. Honestly, everyone has done great, so I don't plan on bailing out on this whole paleo/primal/leptin idea. There is just too much going right for my patients with it, but seriously...a total cholesterol of 343. I don't think I've seen one that high this year...and it's mine! Ahhhh.

I'm thinking I might have a wacky Apo E genotype, low copper, or maybe CT is just killing all my fat cells and just dumping their contents into my blood.

Today I don't no whether to laugh or cry. Other than the wacked cholesterol, I'm totally good with where I'm headed, but I don't want to be a poster child for the paleo-critics, so hack my lipids gang!

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-20T04:52:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/post-3113>

I'm not a doctor, but I've been reading a lot of paleo type forums for a while now. My observation, fwiw, is that the cholesterol goes up when first adopting the lifestyle, but it eventually comes back into range. I can't tell you how many times I've seen posts about increased cholesterol. I sometimes will point a person to Chris Kressers "I have high cholesterol and I don't care vlog" [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0\_7u7aPkUQ0\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;0\_7u7aPkUQ0\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0\_7u7aPkUQ0\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;0_7u7aPkUQ0)

### colleencoble — 2012-03-20T05:15:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/post-3124>

Did you do more than a tsh? that's useless to detect thyroid. And often a low thyroid results in high cholesterol. I think that's maybe the reason for the way cholesterol can increase at first on a VLC diet. It depresses the T3 for a bit. But if it continues, I'd get free T3 and free T4 run. Your vitamin D is still too low and your hs-CRP is too high. Mine dropped from about your level to .1 in six months on the leptin reset.

My husband's cholesterol dropped 100 points in a very short time on Armour, just to give you an example of what that can do. So take a closer look at your thyroid at your next labs. Great weight loss though!

### Owl — 2012-03-20T07:15:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/post-3159>

I agree with Colleen. The "normal" lab ranges are not optimal, far from it. She's an expert here on thyroid problems, and has helped me with mine. My TSH was in the normal range, but FT3 and FT4 were low, ReverseT3 was high, so it would pay to test yours too.

Optimal Vit. D according to Dr. K is 70-100. Dr. Stasha Grominak says people with sleep apnea should aim for 60-80. Below or above that, sleep is worse. Her video: [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h7cbBB1c0IM\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;h7cbBB1c0IM\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h7cbBB1c0IM\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;h7cbBB1c0IM)

Hs-CRP ideally should be under .5.

HA1c is high, it translates to an average BG of 114. Ideal fasting BG should be in low 80's, so to have an overall average of 114, your BG must be high a lot of the time. With your weight loss/VLC diet, it should come down over time, since A1c is an average of about 3 months.

Hopefully someone else will help with your cholesterol. I'm not an expert on that at all.

Dexter or Dr. K, where are you?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-20T15:35:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/post-3219>

Dan

Take a look at my labs... I had an NMR done last august and again this Jan... my TC went from 296 to 340!

Labs (NMR) [http://i303.photobucket.com/albums/nn155/Shijin13/LAB%20Work/labscomparisonpg1-1.jpg](http://http://i303.photobucket.com/albums/nn155/Shijin13/LAB%20Work/labscomparisonpg1-1.jpg)

Labs comparison pg 2: [http://i303.photobucket.com/albums/nn155/Shijin13/LAB%20Work/labscomparisonpg2.jpg](http://http://i303.photobucket.com/albums/nn155/Shijin13/LAB%20Work/labscomparisonpg2.jpg)

Labs comparison pg3: [http://i303.photobucket.com/albums/nn155/Shijin13/LAB%20Work/Labscomparisonpg3.jpg](http://http://i303.photobucket.com/albums/nn155/Shijin13/LAB%20Work/Labscomparisonpg3.jpg)

So now that you've looked at mine - let's look back at yours:

hsCRP - HIGH - you have chronic inflammation!

HbA1C - HIGH - another indicator of chronic inflammation

Vitamin D3 - LOW (too low) last august I got my Vit D3 up to 117, using both mid day sun and 10000IU of D3/day - I backed off the supplementation from august to the time change - then 5K/day - until I got my Jan labs back - now I'm back at 10000IU/day

When I found my new PCP (who's a paleo doc) she sent me in for Carotid Intima-Media Thickness (CIMT); based on the fact I have a history of familial hyper-cholesterol - this might be the case w/you (do you know what your parents lipids look like?) FHC isn't necessarily a bad thing - it just means you body produces more cholesterol than the \*CW NORM\*... and based upon new research - high cholesterol helps protect against diabetes, Alzheimer, dementia, etc - not necessarily a bad thing...

WHAT IS CIMT?

CIMT is a new noninvasive ultrasound test that is being recommended by the American Heart Association and the American College of Cardiology to screen for heart disease in apparently healthy individuals ages 45 or greater. CIMT stands for Carotid Intima-Media Thickness.

This test verified that I have chronic inflammation - even though my hsCRP is LOW! here's the results [http://i303.photobucket.com/albums/nn155/Shijin13/LAB%20Work/20120228CIMTresutls.jpg](http://http://i303.photobucket.com/albums/nn155/Shijin13/LAB%20Work/20120228CIMTresutls.jpg)

I'm 38 yrs old - but this shows that I have the arteries of a 51yro - lots of inflammation; fortunately no plaque

My PCP thinks the inflammation is a result of dairy consumption - all dairy except ghee is out for me now... I'm even considering kicking eggs to the curb as well...

Now...I think you need to run the remaining hormone labs - pregnenolone, DHEA-S, Testosterone (Total, free & %), alderstorone , psa, & the estrogen that should be tested for men (I forget which one), and a full thryoid panel: Free T3, RT3, T4, Thyroid antibodies TPO & AB; plus salivary cortisol w/dhea-s

I'd gather a guess that you've got some pregnenolone steal going w/your LDL's jumping...

As for your trigs - they are ok. How long have you been paleo/grain free?

You HDLs need to come up - so for the time being - you should skip all night shades until you get them above 60!

And you NEED to bring your VIT D3 up!!!!

Keep working on the diet - my PCP told me she expected that my inflammation and LDLs will continue to come down the longer I'm on the Paleo diet. She expects it will take at least another year of paleo for my body to turn back the inflammation - I expect less w/the CT.

One things she's going to do is run another set of labs on me at the end of april - she's going to send me in for a berkley heart lab [http://www.bhlinc.com/pati\_results.php](http://http://www.bhlinc.com/pati_results.php)

Its pretty comprehensive -test that includes all genetic markers.

For however long you've been paleo - you were CW for far longer - it will take the body a while to repair the damage done!

gretchen

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T16:30:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/post-3242>

ITA with colleencoble's analysis of the paleo/thyroid/LDL connection. My LDL, too, has gone up, while HDL & trigs have continued to move toward AWESOME, inflammation down, etc., The longer I've been paleo & LC, the worse my thyroid has gotten, until last month when I started taking a dessicated thyroid that is nothing but gland & herb (thyrogold). Since then, energy is finally turning around - I expect my LDL will, too, over the next year.

Most concerning to me is your small dense LDL going up. Any suspicions on why that might be? When were your previous labs, compared to when you started paleo? Are you 100%, or do you have a 10% non-paleo allowance?

### Owl — 2012-03-20T21:49:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/post-3465>

Dan, you didn't put what the range was for your DHEA, but you want that at the top of the range. Low DHEA will destroy your sleep.

And good idea to get salivary cortisol. My sleep was crappy too. I was a night owl, staying up till 1am, waking up a few times each night, and never feeling rested in the morning. I found out my night and morning cortisol was sky high. I was on daily Asmanex, a corticosteroid asthma inhaler which raises cortisol. With the permission of my doc, I went off of it. I also started taking phosphatidylserine at night, which lowers cortisol. It has helped tremendously - I am in bed by 11 and sleep through the night till 6 or 7am.

I started on the leptin reset last summer, and have done everything I can to reduce inflammation, and therefore cortisol and asthma, since then. I have some lung congestion right now, mostly due to seasonal air pollution here in Phoenix. But I haven't needed albuteral yet, so I'm happy I'm off the Asmanex.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-20T22:39:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/post-3502>

> **Shijin13;3403 wrote:** Dan
>
>
>
> the following link - was when I had my AHHHH HAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA moment! Btwn the pregnenolone stea, and the lack of conversion of T3..... it all made sense (Especially why I was cold in the middle of a DC Summer - wearing cashmere sweaters!)
>
> [http://perfecthealthdiet.com/?p=4457](http://http://perfecthealthdiet.com/?p=4457)
>
> especially after reading Jack's response to Paul in the comments... I went to my PCP at the VA - she listened but sent me to endo - who blew me off... finally have a new PCP who's paleo, and using DzLogic to address the hormones, including T3 in BHRT form.

I spent my lunch hour reading through this blog post. Thank you Shinjin13.

This fellow,Gregory Barton, seems to have had a similar experience with VLC and resultant high lipids. Here's what he had to say:

"One of the claims of low carb dieting is that it will normalize the symptoms of metabolic syndrome. Blood pressure, blood sugar and blood lipids, it is claimed, will all come down on a low carb diet, in addition to weight. For most people this happens. But there is a significant minority of people on Paleo and other low carb diets whose blood lipids defy this claim. (See the list of low-carb celebrities with high LDL in this post.)

Why should this happen? Why should some people’s lipids fall on low carb while other people’s lipids rise? Suboptimal thyroid might be the proximate cause for lipids rising on a low carb or paleo diet. Broda Barnes and Lawrence Galton have this to say about thyroid disorders:

“Of all the problems that can affect physical or mental health, none is more common than thyroid gland disturbance. None is more readily and inexpensively corrected. And none is more often untreated, and even unsuspected.” — Hypothyroidism: The Unsuspected Illness

I went very low carb in April in an effort to address metabolic issues, eating as little as 15grams carbohydrate per day. I had great results with blood pressure, sleeping, blood sugar and weight loss. But lipids bucked the trend.

I had expected triglycerides and cholesterol to drop when I cut the carbs, but they did the opposite: They surged. By July my total cholesterol was 350, LDL 280, and triglycerides bobbed around between 150 and 220.

I did some research and found several competing theories for this kind of surge:

Saturated fat: The increase in saturated fat created a superabundance of cholesterol which the liver cannot handle. Also, Loren Cordain has claimed that saturated fat downregulates LDL receptors.

Temporary hyperlipidemia: The surge in lipids is the temporary consequence of the body purging visceral fat. Jenny Ruhl has argued that within a period of months the situation should settle down and lipids should normalize.

Hibernation: The metabolism has gone into “hibernation” with the result that the thyroid hormone T4 is being converted into rT3, an isomer of the T3 molecule, which prevents the clearance of LDL.

Malnutrition: In March, Paul wrote that malnutrition in general and copper deficiency in particular “… is, I believe, the single most likely cause of elevated LDL on low-carb Paleo diets.”

Genetics: Dr. Davis has argued that some combinations of ApoE alleles may make a person “unable to deal with fats and dietary cholesterol.”

I could accept that saturated fat would raise my cholesterol to some degree. However, I doubted that an increase in saturated fat, or purging of visceral fat, would be responsible for a 75% increase in TC from 200 to 350.

There are two basic factors controlling cholesterol levels: creation and clearance. If the surge was not entirely attributable to saturated fat, perhaps the better explanation was that the cholesterol was not being cleared properly. I was drawn to the hibernation theory.

But what causes the body to go into hibernation? According to Chris Masterjohn, a low carb diet could be the cause. Although he does not mention rT3, he warns,

“One thing to look out for is that extended low-carbing can decrease thyroid function, which will cause a bad increase in LDL-C, and be bad in itself. So be careful not to go to extremes, or if you do, to monitor thyroid function carefully.”

If low carb is the cause, then higher carb should be the cure. Indeed, Val Taylor, the owner of the yahoo rT3 group, commented that “it is possible that the rT3 could just be from a low carb diet.” She says, “I keep carbs at no lower than 60g per day for this reason.”

Cortisol and Getting “Stuck” in Hibernation

So what about temporary hyperlipidemia? Bears hibernate for winter, creating rT3, but manage to awaken in spring. Why should humans on low carb diets not be able to awaken from their hibernation? There are many people who complain of high cholesterol years after starting low carb.

A hormonal factor associated with staying in hibernation is high cortisol. It has been claimed that excessively high or low cortisol, sustained over long periods, may cause one to get “stuck” in hibernation mode. One of the moderators from the yahoo rT3 group said:

High or low cortisol can cause rT3 problems, as can chronic illness. It would be nice if correcting these things was all that was necessary. But it seems that the body gets stuck in high rT3 mode.

James LaValle & Stacy Lundin in Cracking the Metabolic Code: 9 Keys to Optimal Health wrote:

When a person experiences prolonged stress, the adrenals manufacture a large amount of the stress hormone cortisol. Cortisol inhibits the conversion of T4 to T3 and favours the conversion of T4 to rT3. If stress is prolonged a condition called reverse T3 dominance occurs and lasts even after the stress passes and cortisol levels fall. (my emphasis)

What I Did

First, I got my thyroid hormone levels tested. A blood test revealed that I had T4 at the top of the range and T3 below range. Ideally I would have tested rT3, but in Thailand the test is not available. I consulted Val Taylor, the owner of the yahoo rT3 group, who said that low T3 can cause lipids to go as high as mine have and, “as you have plenty of T4 there is no other reason for low T3 other than rT3.”

I decided to make these changes:

Increase net carbs to ~50 grams per day. Having achieved my goals with all other metabolic markers I increased carbs, taking care that one hour postprandial blood sugar did not exceed 130 mg/dl.

Supplement with T3 thyroid hormone.

In case the malnutrition explanation was a factor, I began supplementing copper and eating my wife’s delicious liver pate three times per week.

I decided to supplement T3 for the following reasons:

The surge in TC was acute and very high. It was above the optimal range in O Primitivo’s mortality data.

I increased carbs by 20-30g/day for about a month. TC stabilized, but did not drop.

The rT3 theory is elegant and I was eager to test my claim that the bulk of the cholesterol was due to a problem with clearance rather than ‘superabundance’.

What happened?

I started taking cynomel, a T3 supplement, four weeks ago. After one week triglycerides dropped from 150 to 90. After two weeks TC dropped from 350 to 300 and after another week, to 220. Last week numbers were stable.

Based on Paul’s recent series on blood lipids, especially the post Blood Lipids and Infectious Disease, Part I (Jun 21, 2011), I think TC of 220 mg/dl is optimal. As far as serum cholesterol levels are concerned, the problem has been fixed.

I believe that thyroid hormone levels were the dominant factor in my high LDL. Saturated fat intake has remained constant throughout.

My current goal is to address the root causes of the rT3 dominance and wean myself off the T3 supplement. I hope to achieve this in the next few months. My working hypothesis is that the cause of my high rT3 / low T3 was some combination of very low carb dieting, elevated cortisol (perhaps aggravated by stress over my blood lipids!), or malnutrition.

Another possibility is toxins: Dr Davis claims that such chemicals as perchlorate residues from vegetable fertilizers and polyfluorooctanoic acid, the residue of non-stick cookware, may act as inhibitors of the 5′-deiodinase enzyme that converts T4 to T3. Finally, Val Taylor claims that blood sugar over 140 mg/dl causes rT3 dominance. I couldn’t find any studies confirming this claim, and don’t believe it is relevant to my case. Val recommends low carb for diabetics to prevent cholesterol and rT3 issues but warns not to go under 60g carb per day.

Issues with T3 Supplementation

There are some factors to consider before embarking upon T3 supplementation:

Preparation: In order to tolerate T3 supplement you have to be sure that your iron level and your adrenals are strong enough. This requires quite a bit of testing. I’ve read of people who cut corners with unpleasant results.

Practicalities: T3 supplementation requires daily temperature monitoring in order to assess your progress. People who are on the move throughout the day would find this difficult.

Danger: Once you get on the T3 boat you can’t get off abruptly. Your T4 level will drop below range and you will be dependent on T3 until you wean yourself off. If you stopped abruptly you could develop a nasty reaction and even become comatose.

My advice for anyone doing very low carb

As Chris Masterjohn said, in the quote above, if you are going to do very low carb, check your thyroid levels. I would add: Increase the carbs if you find your free T3 falling to the bottom of the range. It might be a good idea to test also for cortisol. A 24-hour saliva test will give you an idea whether your cortisol levels are likely to contribute to an rT3 issue. It might also be a good idea to avoid very low carb if you are suffering from stress – such as lipid anxiety!"

The whole post is here: [http://perfecthealthdiet.com/?p=4457](http://http://perfecthealthdiet.com/?p=4457)

I'm formulating a plan...

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-22T18:38:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/post-4497>

Dr. Kruse made some comments regarding high cholesterol and LDL over on Dr. Davis' blog:

> With regards to the large LDL content. Again I am not a cardiologist, but a neurosurgeon. The heart and brain work on the same principles of biochemistry. And since these two organ take up much of the CO in the body they share much in common. It is also why when we see neolithic disease affect the heart their is also a similar effect in the brain. The Apoe4 story is one such case. But so is the large LDL story. Cardiology CW today believes that LDL matters. In neurosurgery today its clear in neurodegenerative disorders we are lacking LDL and cholesterol more often than not. This is incongruent to biochemistry. Dr davis has said here in this thread the jury is out on large LDL in his field. I am submitting that LDL matter little in any field. Why? If it did the body would have a specific detailed regulatory control method in place to deal with it and it does not. In nature, with the consumption of organic, unprocessed parent essential fats rather than adulterated oils (PUFA’s) and transfats, LDL cholesterol is supposed to be made up of significant amounts of properly functioning “parent” omega-6, linoleic acid (LA), and is not supposed to be harmful. It is the natural transporter of parent omega-6 and parent omega-3 into the cells. It is thus not critical to lower LDL cholesterol, nor is the absolute LDL number as important, if the diet contains sufficient unadulterated parent essential fats. These are unadultered PUFA’s of both the omega 6 and 3 variety for clarity sake. Also take note that the body has no natural “cholesterol sensor” in the bloodstream—it would if its levels had to be maintained within exact limits; such as, sodium, calcium, and glucose levels are monitored in many systems. For example, glucose levels are maintained to an amazingly tight 0.1% in each of us! So Nature implemented biological mechanisms if required. There is no need for a cholesterol sensor because the absolute number is irrelevant. That was my advice to Jack Kronk three weeks ago when he posted his VAP numbers and when I blogged about his numbers. I understand that Dr Davis may not want to go out on a limb as I have here. But I dont view it as a limb when the biology and biochemistry support my beliefs fully that LDL cholesterol levels are completely irrelevant to heart disease propagation. This is why I advocate copious amounts of coconut oil to patients with heart disease and all neurodegenerative disorders including brain tumors and seizure patients. In fact, my literature is loaded with thousands of papers supporting my belief made here. I think its long over do that cardiologists start reading “other pathways” of data to reach conclusions that their own field muddies.

[http://www.trackyourplaque.com/blog/2011/07/the-exception-to-low-carb.html#comment-23777](http://http://www.trackyourplaque.com/blog/2011/07/the-exception-to-low-carb.html#comment-23777) Interesting...

I can't get my hs-CRP under control and I'm wandering if you or Dr. K knows if this ALWAYS indicates inflammation. I feel great, have eaten 80/20 primal for a couple years, and was 100% on the leptin reset (with some high-fat dairy) for six weeks when the lab was drawn. My CRP is 3.4 and was 3.5 in October. This last lab had a previous reverse T3 to free T3 issue cleared up (switched to T3 only Cytomel) and Vit D at 101. I do have lower end testosterone readings and had been doing HCG three times a week in January to work on this. I'm doing all this to lower "inflamation" (and take care of my thyroid, and shred all belly fat I guess...), so it's sure frustrating to not see the number really move.
>

>
> Waiting on telomere results...

Like I've said in other threads, I'm kind of a "newbie" when it comes to a Dr. Kruse style hack, but HS-CRP is a non-specific test. It goes up for all kinds of reasons. The big 3 in my mind are infections, auto-immunity, and diet. Sleep can probably play a role, too. These all cause inflammation, but different mechanisms. HS-CRP is just the liver's response to badness being thrown its way, regardless of the cause.

My HS-CRP is nowhere near optimal, so I have work to do there, too. I'll let you know what I find out as I kick it to the curb.

Sincerely, Your brother in the HS-CRP kickin' mayhem.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-04-11T23:33:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/post-13911>

> **KiwiLauren;9171 wrote:** I \*think\* I found a correct conversion to get my NZ numbers to speak American. If I am correct, here are the 'translated' lipid findings:
>
>
>
> hs-CRP remains 1.20
>
> cholesterol is 313
>
> Trigs are 62
>
> HDL is 84
>
> LDL is 216 (I do not have a breakdown between small and large)

Since your thryoid and ferritin look okay, I'd bet your either Apo E 4/4 genotype or you've just lost quite a bit of weight. I'd just recheck it when your weight is stable.

I got kinda hung up on my high cholesterol, but it's just one piece of a very big puzzle. I'm not so stressed about it now. I think about it as substrate for my hormone production (which I need because I found out my testosterone is low), and not something that's gonna kill me ASAP.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-12T06:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/post-14091>

> **Dan in Utah;13980 wrote:** Since your thryoid and ferritin look okay, I'd bet your either Apo E 4/4 genotype or you've just lost quite a bit of weight. I'd just recheck it when your weight is stable.
>
>
>
> I got kinda hung up on my high cholesterol, but it's just one piece of a very big puzzle. I'm not so stressed about it now. I think about it as substrate for my hormone production (which I need because I found out my testosterone is low), and not something that's gonna kill me ASAP.

Thanks, Dan, MamaGrok and tigerlilly. Dr K wasn't worried about my cholesterol either (but wants my hs CRP down!). I have lost about 20 pounds in the last 4 months (maybe a bit more - I don't own a scale). I didn't realise one's cholesterol would bounce around during weight loss, this is very good to know. Thanks again all.

### baadamaslitle9257 — 2013-09-05T04:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-ldl-and-total-cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-paleo-leptin-ct.312/post-94848>

# Thread: Can I have dark chocolate while on the reset?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-20T15:31:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-3216>

Hi everyone,

I've been doing the reset for 6 weeks now along with CT, and wondering if it's ok to have 99% dark chocolate (4g of carbs per 50g portion).

Any thoughts or recommandations?

Thank you!!

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T22:45:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-3508>

I wouldn't. But I tend to binge if I'm not strict. Re. the dark chocolate, to be honest, it's kind of disgusting to me at that percentage...

### David — 2012-03-20T23:11:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-3523>

Dr Kruse hasnÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t discussed chocolate much, except in the post regarding adrenal fatigue:

[http://jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/)

Ã¢â‚¬Å“Avoid caffeine, **chocolate**, ephedra, guarana, kola nut, and prescription stimulants. (Ritalin)Ã¢â‚¬Â￾

### Glamorama — 2012-03-20T23:41:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-3540>

I had plenty of 85% and 100% while I was resetting.

### L8F — 2012-03-21T03:18:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-3715>

I used it oo, but had to be careful not to slip into lower percentages...tonight I made the slip right into a whole bar of 55% almond and sea salt, and now feel a rock in my gut, and regret regret. Depends if it gets you going down a craving path or not.

### Owl — 2012-03-21T06:33:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-3796>

Dr. K regularly eats 99 per cent chocolate from World Market - 2 bars a week, he says. It's pretty good - it tastes like it has more sugar in it than that. Really good when you haven't been eating any sugar for months!

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-21T10:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-3809>

I did with red wine. It worked fine for my weight loss. I bought cocoa powder added it to my coffee and other mixes and ate Green and Black's Organic dark chocolate 85%. It tasted very good and organic to boot. It has some milk in it so I am eliminating it right now. No dairy.

Gonna have to make the cocoa powder work. I may go back and try the 99% at World Market. The problem is the ingredients say chocolate liqueur if I'm not mistaken. Don't know what that means so I don't eat it.

### Casey — 2012-03-21T11:31:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-3815>

Definitely depends on the individual. If you want coconut oil in your diet then I'd suggest making something like Dr K's Optimal Bark. That way you're getting extra benefits and the choc will stretch further.

### AKMan — 2012-03-21T13:49:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-3824>

After eating 80 then 90% chocolate, I now find I can eat 100% dark chocolate, they kind they sell for baking. It is wonderful! Espcially if you eat it while drinking hot tea, because it doesn't melt in your mouth as readily as lower percentage chocolates.

I remember about a year ago I tried 100% and nearly gagged. Your taste really changes on paleo. try it.

### Vicster — 2012-03-21T13:51:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-3826>

> **Owl;3821 wrote:** Dr. K regularly eats 99 per cent chocolate from World Market - 2 bars a week, he says. It's pretty good - it tastes like it has more sugar in it than that. Really good when you haven't been eating any sugar for months!

The World Market 99% chocolate is good, really good. The reason is, it has chocolate liquor and sugar in it with 14g of carbs per serving. I stay away from it because I'm trying to keep my carbs under 25, but if you are not too worried about carbs and want a treat give it a try. It's also cheaper than some of the other higher percentage chocolates out there at $1.99 a 3 oz bar.

### MJ* — 2012-03-22T17:55:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-4460>

> **Casey;3840 wrote:** Definitely depends on the individual. If you want coconut oil in your diet then I'd suggest making something like Dr K's Optimal Bark. That way you're getting extra benefits and the choc will stretch further.

Casey how is that made? Thanks...

### ChimpChick — 2012-03-22T18:38:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-4498>

I ordered this and it is awesome! Melt it in a double boiler and from there you can add anything you want. I do a batch of just stevia and vanilla extract. Then a batch with Co and sometimes I add mac nuts.. I got these silicon mini brownie trays at Michaels and they make the perfect size candy square. I also eat the chips plain sometimes.. Much cheaper then buying dark chocolate bars and no sugar!

[http://www.bulknaturaloils.com/Products/15910-bulk-organic-cocoa-liquor.aspx](http://http://www.bulknaturaloils.com/Products/15910-bulk-organic-cocoa-liquor.aspx)

"Jedwarsd is a wholesale supplier of bulk organic cocoa liquor. The bulk cocoa liquor, also known as mass, or paste is produced by finely grinding roasted cocoa beans to a smooth, liquid state and then cooling and forming into blocks or wafers. Our cocoa liquor is in wafer form for ease of use. Cocoa liquor is also known as unsweetened baking chocolate."

### Cody — 2012-03-22T18:45:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-4501>

> **JC2K;3533 wrote:** I wouldn't. But I tend to binge if I'm not strict. Re. the dark chocolate, to be honest, it's kind of disgusting to me at that percentage...

Umm. If it tastes disgusting to you, how are you going to binge??? Engage your brain on that for a second. LOL

### Casey — 2012-03-23T02:54:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-dark-chocolate-while-on-the-reset.316/post-4772>

> **MJ\*;4488 wrote:** Casey how is that made? Thanks...

It's in his recently released ebook that can be bought for $12.95, so I won't post actual recipe here. Basically you melt choc with coconut oil and then add any add-ins that you like - I add crushed vanilla bean, dried coconut, orange zest, stevia and some Celtic salt.

This is pretty strange, but last week 99% dark chocolate from World Market tasted a bit like mud, but yesterday it tasted almost like milk chocolate. But when we checked my husband's BS an hour later, it was already down to 91. I have to be careful though, because any amount of sweet sensation sets me off looking for more of the sweet stuff. Sigh.

# Thread: Can anyone hack me?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-hack-me.317/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-20T16:28:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-hack-me.317/post-3240>

Hormone Test 03/05/2012 Units Range

Estradiol (saliva) 0.6 pg/ml 1.3-3.3 Premenopausal (Luteal)

Progesterone (saliva) 8 pg/ml 75-270 Premenopausal (Luteal)

Ratio: Pg/E2 (saliva) 13 Optimal: 100-500 when E2 1.3-3.3 pg/ml

Testosterone (saliva) 31 pg/ml 16-55 (Age Dependent)

DHEAS (saliva) 6.8 ng/ml 2-23 (Age Dependent)

Cortisol Morning (saliva) 6.1 ng/ml 3.7-9.5

Cortisol Noon (saliva) 1.8 ng/ml 1.2-3.0

Cortisol Evening (saliva) 0.8 ng/ml 0.6-1.9

Cortisol Night (saliva) 2.4 ng/ml 0.4-1.0

Free T4 (blood spot) 1.6 ng/dL 0.7-2.5

Free T3 (blood spot) 2.5 pg/ml 2.5-6.5

TSH (blood spot) 0.6 uU/ml 0.5-3.0

TPO (blood spot) \* 15 IU/ml 0-150 (70-150 borderline)

Vitamin D, 25-OH, D2 < 4.0 ng/ml

### colleencoble — 2012-03-21T00:50:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-hack-me.317/post-3594>

Oh my, your hormones are in the toilet! I'm not familiar with the tsh blood spot. I'll have to look that up. Cortisol too high at night. I'd start some pregnenolone, vitamin D and dhea. And I'd find a good bioidentical hormone doctor to get things rolling better. You could try the CT for a while and retest in 3 mos if you like. I'm not very patient myself. I like fixed NOW. LOL

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-21T14:54:34+0200

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T17:29:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-hack-me.317/post-3908>

I'd start some pregnenolone, vitamin D and dhea. And I'd find a good bioidentical hormone doctor to get things rolling better. You could try the CT for a while and retest in 3 mos if you like. I'm not very patient myself. I like fixed NOW. LOL

Thanks for your replies, ladies. I only know what I've read on the forums about bioidentical hormones and, although I am not opposed to them, shouldn't I be able to fix myself without taking exogenous hormones?

AND I read somewhere that with my pattern of high night cortisol and low dhea that I should eliminate caffeine... Comments?

### L8F — 2012-03-22T01:30:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-hack-me.317/post-4126>

Agree with above, with the addition that it looks like from your Prog number that you had not yet ovulated, which is borne out by the fact that your period is late. So you have to shift the numerical interpretation to follicular phase, which makes the ratio a bit different. LRx should help even some of this out, and CT too....

I forget how long you have been on this LRx??

### DarleenMB — 2012-03-22T02:04:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-hack-me.317/post-4146>

You cannot produce what your body has STOPPED making. I went on BHRT last June via the Wiley Protocol which returns your hormones to optimum levels for your TWENTIES. I'm 65 and had a total hyst about 11-12 years ago. The change has been profound. I would strongly encourage you to read Wiley's book "Sex, lies and menopause."

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-22T02:46:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-hack-me.317/post-4174>

> **cjco;3933 wrote:** Thanks for your replies, ladies. I only know what I've read on the forums about bioidentical hormones and, although I am not opposed to them, shouldn't I be able to fix myself without taking exogenous hormones?
>
>
>
> AND I read somewhere that with my pattern of high night cortisol and low dhea that I should eliminate caffeine... Comments?

High night time cortisol is often one of the first signs of adrenal problems. The cortisol readings will go up, usually the night time one, before they go low. Your body is in a constant state of "flight" at ngiht. Eliminate any stimulants like caffeine. I can't remember if you are doing the circadian protocol but I'd suggest that. I know PS is good to take at night to calm that high cortisol.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-22T20:53:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-hack-me.317/post-4599>

> **L8F;4152 wrote:** Agree with above, with the addition that it looks like from your Prog number that you had not yet ovulated, which is borne out by the fact that your period is late. So you have to shift the numerical interpretation to follicular phase, which makes the ratio a bit different. LRx should help even some of this out, and CT too....
>
> I forget how long you have been on this LRx??

I had been on it for just over a month before I tested....

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-22T21:08:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-hack-me.317/post-4605>

Ok, anybody that's listening....

I'm at my wits end. I'm feeling worse in every possible way. Less than a week into the rese t(back in January) , I had gained weight where I've NEVER gained before. I noticed my bra strap was feeling tight and pinchy. Kept up with it even though my weight kept going up. Now I' still fat, it's spring, when I should be enjoying the lovely weather and when my neighbors ask me if I'm hot wearing jeans I just want to say "HELL YES BUT THERE'S NO WAY ANY OTHER HUMAN BEING IS GOING TO HAVE TO SUFFER THE AGONY OF GAZING UPON MY LEGS". On top of that, I am more tense and uptight than ever. If I do manage to control my temper, I can still feel it raging inside. My daughter spilled a little of her breakfast on the table this morning and I held it in but I was SO ANGRY. Just a couple of minutes ago my other daughter asked me to open the playroom window and I started crying because I felt like I just couldn't do it. I can acknowledge that I feel utterly overwhelmed in a situation that should not overwhelm anyone. Everything overwhelms me. On top of that, my binging has not improved nor has my obsession with food. I am so happy for those people that can post that because of their BAB and CT they are happily getting by just on breakfast but at the same time I resent them. I have struggled and suffered for so long with these issues and I've tried so many different things I can't even list them all. Not one of them worked. I now feel that nothing will work and I am hopeless. The worst is how this impacts my family- especially my kids who just absorb everything they pick up from me. And how I am WASTING MY PRECIOUS LIFE AWAY with these feelings and emotions that I can't get a handle on and being chubby and being blind to any of the great people and experiences in my life.......

### PrimalPam — 2012-03-23T01:37:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-hack-me.317/post-4732>

Can you post a daily food log so we can see if we can pinpoint something?

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-23T02:04:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-hack-me.317/post-4745>

I agree with everyone who has posted so far, and also want to give you a big virtual hug. I know exactly what you're feeling right now. Totally been there Your solution may not be the same as my solution, but there is a solution for you. Time will figure it out. I am still there feeling like I'm screwing up my kids, although now it's mostly b/c of fatigue instead of anger, but I've been in the overwhelmed-feelings place, too, and I also know that my children \*will\* see the healthy me and I will teach them what I've learned and the love we have for each other will, I trust, heal the wounds.

Bless you, and keep on going. Please do post a sample day and help us hack you.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-23T02:19:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-hack-me.317/post-4751>

> **cjco;4633 wrote:** Ok, anybody that's listening....
>
> I'm at my wits end. I'm feeling worse in every possible way. Less than a week into the rese t(back in January) , I had gained weight where I've NEVER gained before. I noticed my bra strap was feeling tight and pinchy. Kept up with it even though my weight kept going up. Now I' still fat, it's spring, when I should be enjoying the lovely weather and when my neighbors ask me if I'm hot wearing jeans I just want to say "HELL YES BUT THERE'S NO WAY ANY OTHER HUMAN BEING IS GOING TO HAVE TO SUFFER THE AGONY OF GAZING UPON MY LEGS". On top of that, I am more tense and uptight than ever. If I do manage to control my temper, I can still feel it raging inside. My daughter spilled a little of her breakfast on the table this morning and I held it in but I was SO ANGRY. Just a couple of minutes ago my other daughter asked me to open the playroom window and I started crying because I felt like I just couldn't do it. I can acknowledge that I feel utterly overwhelmed in a situation that should not overwhelm anyone. Everything overwhelms me. On top of that, my binging has not improved nor has my obsession with food. I am so happy for those people that can post that because of their BAB and CT they are happily getting by just on breakfast but at the same time I resent them. I have struggled and suffered for so long with these issues and I've tried so many different things I can't even list them all. Not one of them worked. I now feel that nothing will work and I am hopeless. The worst is how this impacts my family- especially my kids who just absorb everything they pick up from me. And how I am WASTING MY PRECIOUS LIFE AWAY with these feelings and emotions that I can't get a handle on and being chubby and being blind to any of the great people and experiences in my life.......

I'm so sorry you are feeling this way. I've been there with feeling overwhelmed....overwhelmed with the most simple tasks. I even went to the doc on my 50th birthday to convince her that I really needed those thyroid drugs as my labs proved it and all she did was say that based on the symptoms I'm agoraphobic and she sent me off with t script for Wellbutrin. I'm very social and knew there was a problem when I didn't want to go out with my friends. For me it took getting on thyroid meds to help with that feeling of not being able to handle what was in front of me even though it may not be that much. It also helped with energy, and being able to take on more and do more in my life. I hope you can get to a doc somehow because you need some BHRT at the very least but your thyroid is starting to slow but it may need just a dab of progesterone to help it along.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-23T14:56:50+0200

> I'm at my wits end. I'm feeling worse in every possible way. Less than a week into the rese t(back in January) , I had gained weight where I've NEVER gained before. Now I' still fat, it's spring, when I should be enjoying the lovely weather and when my neighbors ask me if I'm hot wearing jeans I just want to say "HELL YES BUT THERE'S NO WAY ANY OTHER HUMAN BEING IS GOING TO HAVE TO SUFFER THE AGONY OF GAZING UPON MY LEGS". On top of that, I am more tense and uptight than ever. If I do manage to control my temper, I can still feel it raging inside. My daughter spilled a little of her breakfast on the table this morning and I held it in but I was SO ANGRY. Just a couple of minutes ago my other daughter asked me to open the playroom window and I started crying because I felt like I just couldn't do it. I can acknowledge that I feel utterly overwhelmed in a situation that should not overwhelm anyone. Everything overwhelms me. I am so happy for those people that can post that because of their BAB and CT they are happily getting by just on breakfast but at the same time I resent them. I must be getting too much protein or not enough fat or just don't understand how to baby a liver. My ultimate goal is to allow the liver to make as much shbg as possible and find an at home test to monitor with.

E.G. would an insulin monitor be a good tool? I have never monitored hormones or any thing else. ### Baba — 2012-03-22T09:32:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-management-tools-optimal-liver-monitoring-at-home.318/post-4287>

I must confess that I don't understand most of what you write... but I can tell you about my experiences with healing the liver.

It takes time. This is not something that will show results in a few weeks.

With me the LR resistance of my liver is reflected in my blood sugar, so a BS meter is all I need. I am not diabetic. While eating SAD I had normal blood sugar, but on a low-carb diet my liver works overtime and used to give me a fasting value of over 100. It is slowly going down now.

Choline (think liver and egg yolks) helps. I also took lecithin sporadically. NAC every morning. Sylmarin (milk thistle) is also supposed to help, but I did not try this.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-24T12:41:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-management-tools-optimal-liver-monitoring-at-home.318/post-5358>

> **Baba;5379 wrote:** Well, at least according to Jack Kruse, leptin resistance occurs at the hypothalamus, the liver and the muscles.
>
> I cannot really explain to you why my fbg went up when I started low carb, but the only source of sugar when you're not eating carbs is your liver.
>
>
>
> I know other people have this - Peter at Hyperlipid has blogged about it, for example. Still, I measured 105 again yesterday and it is a bit frustrating.

Here is an explanation from Chris Kresser for the high FBG readings found in low carb dieters:

One caveat here is that very low-carb diets will produce elevated fasting blood glucose levels. Why? Because low-carb diets induce insulin resistance. Restricting carbohydrates produces a natural drop in insulin levels, which in turn activates hormone sensitive lipase. Fat tissue is then broken down, and non-esterified fatty acids (a.k.a. Ã¢â‚¬Å“free fatty acidsÃ¢â‚¬Â￾ or NEFA) are released into the bloodstream. These NEFA are taken up by the muscles, which use them as fuel. And since the muscleÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s needs for fuel has been met, it decreases sensitivity to insulin. You can read more about this at Hyperlipid.

So, if you eat a low-carb diet and have borderline high FBG (i.e. 90-105), it may not be cause for concern. Your post-meal blood sugars and A1c levels are more important.​

Restricting carbohydrates produces a natural drop in insulin levels, which in turn activates hormone sensitive lipase. Fat tissue is then broken down, and non-esterified fatty acids (a.k.a. “free fatty acids” or NEFA) are released into the bloodstream. These NEFA are taken up by the muscles, which use them as fuel. And since the muscle’s needs for fuel has been met, it decreases sensitivity to insulin. You can read more about this at Hyperlipid.

### chocolate — 2012-05-15T03:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-management-tools-optimal-liver-monitoring-at-home.318/post-24080>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21157426](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21157426)

Daidzein supplementation prevents non-alcoholic fatty liver disease through alternation of hepatic gene expression profiles and adipocyte metabolism.Abstract

INTRODUCTION:

Globally, non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) continues to rise and isoflavones exert antisteatotic effects by the regulation of hepatic lipogenesis/insulin resistance or adiposity/a variety of adipocytokines are related to hepatic steatosis. However, there is very little information regarding the potential effects of daidzein, the secondary abundant isoflavone, on NAFLD. Here, we have assessed the hepatic global transcription profiles, adipocytokines and adiposity in mice with high fat-induced NAFLD and their alteration by daidzein supplementation.

METHODS:

C57BL/6J mice were fed with normal fat (16% fat of total energy), high fat (HF; 36% fat of total energy) and HF supplemented with daidzein (0.1, 0.5, 1 and 2 g per kg diet) for 12 weeks.

RESULTS:

Daidzein supplementation (≥ 0.5 g per kg diet) reduced hepatic lipid concentrations and alleviated hepatic steatosis. The hepatic microarray showed that daidzein supplementation (1 g per kg diet) downregulated carbohydrate responsive element binding protein, a determinant of de novo lipogenesis, its upstream gene liver X receptor β and its target genes encoding for lipogenic enzymes, thereby preventing hepatic steatosis and insulin resistance. These results were confirmed by lower insulin and blood glucose levels as well as homeostasis model assessment insulin resistance scores. In addition, daidzein supplementation inhibited adiposity by the upregulation of genes involved in fatty acid β-oxidation and the antiadipogeneis, and moreover augmented antisteatohepatitic leptin and adiponectin mRNA levels, whereas it reduced the mRNA or concentration of steatotic tumor necrosis factor α and ghrelin.

CONCLUSIONS:

These findings show that daidzein might alleviate NAFLD through the direct regulation of hepatic de novo lipogenesis and insulin signaling, and the indirect control of adiposity and adipocytokines by the alteration of adipocyte metabolism.

### chocolate — 2012-05-15T03:21:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-management-tools-optimal-liver-monitoring-at-home.318/post-24082>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7695042](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7695042)

Daidzin, an antioxidant isoflavonoid, decreases blood alcohol levels and shortens sleep time induced by ethanol intoxication.

Abstract

The extract from an edible vine, Pueraria lebata, has been reported to be efficacious in lessening alcohol intoxication. In this study, we have tested the efficacy of one of the major components, daidzin, from this plant extract. When ethanol (40% solution, 3 g/kg body weight) was given to fasted rats intragastrically, blood alcohol concentration (BAC) peaked at 30 min after alcohol ingestion and reached 1.77 +/- 0.14 mg/ml (mean values +/- SD, n = 6). If daidzin (30 mg/kg) was mixed with the ethanol solution and given to animals intragastrically, BAC was found to peak at 90 min after alcohol ingestion and reached only 1.20 +/- 0.30 mg/ml (n = 6) (p < 0.05 vs. controls). **The ability of daidzin to delay and decrease peak BAC level after ethanol ingestion was also observed in fed animals. In both fasted and fed rats given alcohol without daidzin, BAC quickly declined after reaching its peak at 30 min. By contrast, BAC levels receded more slowly if daidzin was also fed to the animals. Daidzin showed a chronic effect. Rats fed daidzin for 7 days before ethanol challenge, but not on the day of challenge, also produced lower and later peak BAC levels. Interestingly, daidzin, whether fed to rats only once or chronically for 7 days, did not significantly alter activities of either alcohol dehydrogenase or mitochondrial aldehyde dehydrogenase in the liver. Further experiments demonstrated that daidzin shortened sleep time for rats receiving ethanol intragastrically (7 g/kg) but not intraperitoneally (2 g/kg).** To test whether daidzin delayed stomach-emptying, \[14C\]polyethylene glycol was mixed with ethanol and fed to rats.(ABSTRACT TRUNCATED AT 250 WORDS)

# Thread: Compression shirts for ladies
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compression-shirts-for-ladies.319/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Adriana — 2012-03-20T17:17:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compression-shirts-for-ladies.319/post-3266>

I just got back from Wal-Mart where I picked up compression shirt and shorts. I didn't bother to look for women's as the only ones I could find were in sporting goods next tothe basketballs. They come in Adult and Youth sizes.

I had mixed success on sizing. I'm 5'5 160#, 38DD.

Shorts - Franklin Adult Medium, around $11, fit snugly but not uncomforrtable. Available in white or black. Success.

Shirt - Riddle, Adult Medium, around $16, way too loose although the box says it is for 37-39" chest. Does not come in Adult Small, next size down is Youth Large. Will be exchanging it.

(see my recent post in the Products thread on use of massage cushion with compression shorts and ice packs).

### Adriana — 2012-03-20T22:07:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compression-shirts-for-ladies.319/post-3483>

Scratch the Riddel line for women. The Mens Medium is too big and the next size down, Youth Large is too tight.

Target carries the Champion line, but the Men's Small is too loose.

I have settled on a women's Spanx body shaper tank top from Target. Most of those body shapers are way too thick and I think would block the cold. The Spanx line had the thinnest fabric.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-20T22:40:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compression-shirts-for-ladies.319/post-3503>

### nonchalant — 2012-03-21T03:54:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compression-shirts-for-ladies.319/post-3745>

I got mine at Amazon. Fits well, per the chart.

[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001CU31TE/ref=oh\_o06\_s00\_i00\_details](http://http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001CU31TE/ref=oh_o06_s00_i00_details)

Sizing Information:

\* XS (0-2): 31- to 33-inch bust, 23- to 25-inch waist, 33- to 35-inch hips

\* S (4-6): 33.5- to 35.5-inch bust, 25.5- to 27.5-inch waist, 35.5- to 37.5-inch hips

\* M (8-10): 36- to 38-inch bust, 28- to 30-inch waist, 38- to 40-inch hips

\* L (12-14): 38.5- to 40.5-inch bust, 30.5- to 32.5-inch waist, 40.5- to 42.5-inch hips

\* XL (16): 41- to 43-inch bust, 33- to 35-inch waist, 43- to 45-inch hips

\* XXL (18): 44- to 46-inch bust, 36- to 38-inch waist, 46- to 48-inch hips
