# Thread: Massage+CT!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/massage-ct.320/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Adriana — 2012-03-20T17:34:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/massage-ct.320/post-3277>

I mentioned this in the CT products thread but decided to start a stand-alone thread.

I was at my chiropractor on the spinolator and water massage tables and wondered whether massage+cold would increase the effectiveness. As luck would have it, I went to Walmart to buy reusable ice pillow packs and compression shirts/ shorts and came across an " open box" Motor Massage Cushion for $19.99 which has settings for lumbar, back and sides. I plugged it in to a portable power pack with 12volt power. Topped it with my ice packs and here I am, chillin' in the comfort of my bed. I can place it in a reclining position or lay on it flat to do the front or sides. It's not much of a massage, more of a vibration, but I'm hopping it will magnify the benefit. It is cheaper and more comfortable than buying a second set f compression garments and stuffing them with gel packs.

I think I'll take my ice packs to my next chiro appointments to use in conjuction with their tables as the massage is much more vigorous.

What do you think?

### AKMan — 2012-03-20T23:44:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/massage-ct.320/post-3543>

I think that sounds awesome!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-find-the-leptin-rx-diet.325/post-3507>

Here is a list of notes I made for my own reference:

\[\*\]Calories are important when LR (leptin resistant, if > 30lbs overweight) but mean nothing once LS (leptin sensitive). Macronutirents count when LR and mean nothing when LS.
\[\*\]LR = sugar burner, not a fat burner. Do not do glycolytic exercise until muscles / liver become completely LS.
Eat a ketogenic version of the paleo diet for 6-8 weeks. 3 major causes of LR: Carbs, Fructose and Omega 6. Limit to 25 grams of carbohydrates per day if overweight.
Never snack at all, initially and forever. Snacking completely stresses liver metabolism. The liver must use gluconeogenesis normally both asleep and awake. Snacking destroys timing and circadian clocks that work in unison with Leptin. Nothing after 7:30 PM.
Eat three small meals a day initially; adapt to two a day. (Big breakfast/small dinner

### Lexi — 2012-03-20T20:54:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-rapid-detoxing.326/post-3424>

**I would really like to hear from people (and Dr. K!) about chelation therapy, IV treatments of Vit C, DMSO, etc.

**

Has anyone tried these? How have they worked for you?

Just from my last attempts at weight loss, I know my detoxing is going to be brutal. I am also getting ready to replace my amalgams. From my pyro diagnosis, I am expecting copper toxicity as well (waiting for test results).

Please share your experiences with the above. Thanks!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T21:14:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-rapid-detoxing.326/post-3440>

I might not do the amalgams at the same time, FWIW.

Some strategies I've seen success with during hard-core detox are detox baths (30m or so in a hot bath with 2C epsom salts, sea salt, raw ACV, or baking soda - now I'm dissolving them in hot water and then putting that whole mix into my cold bath) and liver support (I'm using Yogi Liver Detox tea right now). And just plain old backing off a bit if the detox gets to the point of making you want to quit.

I've heard of others using other liver supports (like the Estrosomething Glamorama is using), activated charcoal, chelation therapy, and (I think) clay. The idea is to open the detox pathways to help your body cope with the toxins you're dumping. I haven't heard of using vitamin C megadosing for this, but am interested, as I use it for all acute infections already.

I kind of think that might be the proper route. I was reading that fructose sits near the hypothalmas and stalls for twenty minutes and that is pretty much the target. The fatty fruits are loaded with minerals and malic acid is present as well. Other than fatty fruits, the green fruits (low sugar) seem specified. Sort of no harm no foul. I know that fruits aren't circadian to winter, but the Pacific Ocean is 54 degrees. I am thinking the cold water to burn out the liver fat and the fruits to free up the metals for chelation might be a priority. I don't know much and I am WAGing. I would appreciate anyone else's noodling or opinions. I know cancer needs fructose to reproduce, but surely, we are adapted for a little in the summer?

### Sherry — 2012-03-21T01:00:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-rapid-detoxing.326/post-3607>

I did IV chelation about 8 years ago for mercury and lead. It did not help all that much and was quite expensive. I made a green juice with lots of cilantro and that seemed to work better. I also used a product called Detoxamin chelation suppository. They are calcium disodium EDTA. These seemed to work the best. I had testing done to determine how much was being released every few months so was able to determine where the results were best. I'm not sure if the effects are cumulative - i.e.; did the IV jump start the whole process? All I know is that during the 2 months I did the IV I released very little mercury or lead.

### chocolate — 2012-03-21T01:45:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-rapid-detoxing.326/post-3630>

I think trying the at home stuff first might be good to help with understanding side effects. The edta suppositories sound about as good as an iv. Thanks so much. Manageable as well. I can get the hair and urine without the iv. And I can get going right away.

Also, has anyone had any luck repatriating zinc? It seems very confounding. The copper dump causes vomiting, I am reading.

Things are as extreme as they can possibly get and I would be completely surprised, if they weren't up there. I am learning lots as I go. Finding out what we don't want to do and what we haven't done yet for ourselves is our best starting point. I didn't realize hopping up iodine would help take care of mercury. There has to be a med free way of doing it. I am finding lithium tends to be effective medicine when high levels of mercury are present. Take away the mercury and maybe the lithium too. Lithium murders the thyroid, the thyroid needs iodine.... You are right... we are guessing, but paying insurance companies to tell us no, doctors to shrug and say things just happen (you have to pay for that kind of diagnosis) and being subjected to someone that knows you are desperate (like an undertaker) leaves us picking each other's brain, quite a resource. I doubt if one of those suppositories are any worse than a diet dr pepper. I guarantee that stuff much worse has been injected by licensed, highly trained professionals given utmost discretion. Shoot, I doubt if one of those suppositories are any worse than a bowl of frosted flakes. At least your liver, pancreas, and stomach get left out of the equation.

### PaleoMom — 2012-04-05T17:43:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-rapid-detoxing.326/post-11750>

Chelation can be very difficult to do safely. I tested high in lead, mercury and copper and it has taken me years to find a safe way of chelating. I tried IV, oral, low dose oral (Andy Cutler method), heavy metal detox clay baths (these were actually mildly helpful and at least didn't make me worse) and except as noted they all made me MUCH worse.

You have to find something that will release the stored metal (and at a rate your body can handle), bind with the metal (or they will just redistribute to a new location, most likely your brain) and escort it safely out of the body. This is why a person should not go lightly into chelation without doing their research first. Things like cilantro alone can release the metal, but won't necessarily take it out of the body, especially if your body is already dealing with other toxins and it can't handle it all at once.

After about 15 years of trying I finally came across a paleo dentist who claimed to have a safe way of chelating. After only about 3 weeks on his program, problems I'd had my whole life went away, and I've had NO negative effects from the treatment (all the others made me really sick and my symptoms remained worse even after stopping treatment). If anyone is thinking of treating heavy metals I really recommend this program a lot. Chelation drugs (IV, oral, and even natural ones) can have lasting permanent effects, so please be careful!

[http://idahonaturaldentist.com/mercury-cleanse-program-2](http://http://idahonaturaldentist.com/mercury-cleanse-program-2)

# Thread: Beating sugar addiction, ED, CF,  much more!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 65
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T22:42:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-3506>

The leptin reset has been the nail in the coffin to my long, long battle to destroy sugar addiction for good.

I had a c-section birth, normal vaccine & antibiotics as a child of the 80's, and fairly SAD (but at least somewhat homemade) diet growing up. **In college, I jokingly declared myself a breadaholic, but since bread is low fat, yay for me! I also noticed that caffeine, sugar, sodas, and most meds had no effect on me, and noted addictive tendencies (I'd drink a Coke in 10 seconds flat), and was glad I never indulged in alcohol b/c of that. I was in an intense academic program, but never felt stress. I also slept way too little.

**In my 20s, I was hard-core chronic cardio, low-fat, and while I wasn't intentionally, I was practically a vegetarian, eating maybe 4oz of meat per day. **I was in outstanding physical shape, ~13% body fat, able to do anything I wanted physically, and no major health problems.

At the birth of my first child, I switched to a high-whole grain, lower sugar, no-more-Froot-Loops diet, and made an attempt at eating veggies for the first time. I also noticed an increased tendency to eat large quantities of sugar when I had it, fixate on sweets, and sneak them. I just thought it was a lack of self-control, but it was a total lack. I was also very stressed about working (I wanted to be with my babe) & extremely sleep-deprived (didn't realize I needed 9hrs of sleep per night and was falling asleep while driving and talking).

**I was taking more and more antibiotics for UTIs from about 22 to about 29, a horrible year of near-constant infections and antibiotics (I finally discovered vitamin C megadosing for infection). **The sugar cravings went out of control during this time. From age 25 (birth of my second child) to 31, I battled sugar & carb binges with no success.

At age 31 (birth of fourth child), several things happened. I had been getting more and more tired (but thought I was just lazy and busy). **I switched from skim to whole dairy,** and I added in fermented cod liver oil. I eliminated the many high-whole-grain, low-sugar cereals from our life. Numerous minor health problems started to go away. And for the first time, the incessant sugar/grain cravings in the morning died back** (still had them all afternoon and night).

At 32, I gave up grains and allowed myself unlimited natural fats.** I was now eating a Weston Price style traditional foods diet. Within three months, numerous health problems I didn't even know I had (like chronically inflamed nasal passages - when they cleared, I gained 5 notes on my vocal range!) disappeared. (Many of these are documented on the first page of my MDA journal.) But more importantly, I was finally able to stop eating sugar.** I had only a serving of wheat or sweet here or there for six months, when I went on vacation and things started crashing again. I gained for a year until I discovered Radiant Recovery.

At this point, binges were maybe once a week instead of every day. They were still wreaking havoc on my health. RR says to eat 25g of protein within an hour of waking** (sound familiar?). (Its downfall is preaching multiple servings of whole grains per meal. Notably, I never knew anyone who lost weight on RR, even while ending their sugar addictions.) I had been having just oatmeal with milk prior to RR. After a few months of this, I lost my desire for sugar almost entirely.** I was able to go several weeks at a time without it. I was able to go sugar-free almost 100% from the time I turned 33 till the birth of my fifth child almost a year later. I was eating one serving of oatmeal and half a piece of whole sourdough bread at this time.

Around the time I turned 34, I gave up the small amounts of grains I was having because I had GAINED weight from 1mo postpartum on. I was not about to go up from 189lb!! I had to do something drastic, so I started my primal journal at Marksdailyapple.com, and got the grains out. Immediately I started losing weight - **yes, just .5 serving per day of wheat kept me from losing weight. But I still battled sugar cravings whenever I was around my vvery favorite sweets, and once I had one bite of something sweet, I'd have more and more until anything at all sweet, even nasty commercial candy, looked good and I'd go bananas on it till I felt horrible in every way.

I continued to lose .5lb/week, on average (it was really in fits and starts), until **I was 35. I was really having success on the GAPS diet by this time, keeping the sugar to maybe once per month, binges maybe once every 1-2 months, until **I hosted a party where I just fell completely apart. I went on to have binges every weekend for 6 weeks, eating 100% primally and with no cravings all week only to fall apart on the weekend, and gained almost TEN FREAKING POUNDS, going from 143 to 152. I was also bingeing on anything even remotely carb-y - 24-hr fermented homemade yogurt, coconut milk, any kind of nuts, butternut squash for Heaven's sake. I was totally desperate, and didn't know what to try next. I posted this plea for help on MDA: [http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread32223.html](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread32223.html)

Just two days later, the mega-thread started.

I doubted whether 50g could be better than 25, but gave it a shot. **When I started the BAB, instantly I stopped having any desire to snack.** I have not had a snack since June 2011. Instantly, I lost all desire for sweets.** (I also had a crazy week or two of crazy die-off.) I couldn't even find the desire when I looked for it.

Again, I went on vacation and didn't do much wrong, but it was enough to re-spark the desire. Towards the end of two weeks, I foolishly took advantage of the fact that I \*could\* have one bite, or even one serving, of a sweet and not go crazy. On the last day, I had a near-binge, and I proved Dr. Kruse right - I lost nearly all of the gains I had made on the LR so far (including the soft skin and shrinking waistline).

I continued to battle binges about every 6 weeks, though never as intense as before. Still, it kept re-setting my reset. Dr. Kruse laughed at me and mocked me and I just ignored him and kept going.  In September, I got the idea that I should increase my breakfast. I took my BAB to 65g**. I was still hungry after a 65g protein breakfast every morning, and honestly felt I could eat it all over again, but didn't, and kept eating a full lunch and full dinner. After a month at this level, I noticed I was completely satisfied with my breakfast. Then - one day I didn't want lunch. I stopped eating lunch in November 2011 and haven't needed it in the five months since. I also marked October 30, 2011, as the last binge.

I have not binged in five months.**

This is unprecedented. Again, the cravings are nearly non-existent. . There are things that make them speak every now and then, very rarely, but they only speak - they don't shout incessantly. I tell my body about the reasons I'm not giving in, and I have a glass of tea and they go away. Gone. Gone! No shouting cravings, no incessant cravings.

Even my dinner is getting smaller, so small that I started decreasing my BAB. **I'm now at 50g each morning, and staying full till at least 5:30 almost every night. If I get hungry a little earlier, I have a glass of water and it goes away - something that never worked before. I have a modest dinner and have no problem lasting from dinner till breakfast.

In Dec & Jan, I had some labs done and learned that despite eating only very rare wheat for the last several years, I have antigliadin sIgA over the "positive" amount, and ANA (autoimmune) test shows me just over the positive amount there, too. I also have severe fat malabsorption. It frankly scared the crap out of me, and I went 100% gluten- and dairy-free on January 1, 2012. (I had tried many times to go long periods without wheat, but never could have enough strength of will to do it. The cravings were so nominal now that going without was now possible, easy even.) Since then, I am finally winning the battle against chronic bloating (I look only 3mo pregnant most days instead of 5-6!). Also, mid-January, my skin turned silky soft after a lifetime of sandpaper and bumps. About that time, my sleep finally returned to the heavy sleep I'd always had (I started having mild insomnia about a year earlier).

In February, I started ThyroGold, a 100% organ & herb (no fillers) thyroid supplement. Since then, I've had gradually increases in energy. Even after going primal, my energy levels continued to plummet until I felt every day like I was running on 30 or 40 %. Now, I feel like I'm on 50% most days and 60% some others. This is the longest I've had decent energy in years.

I'm re-gaining interest in activities I long stopped bothering with because the satisfaction to energy ratio was way skewed. Since starting CT in December (morning breakfast outside in the winter air), I went from eating in 40 degrees with a wool coat on to eating in 27 degrees with a t-shirt on, comfortably. Water CT so far is producing lots of die-off - I can't wait to see what's on the other side.

The Leptin Reset has turned out to be the key to winning the war I have long waged against sugar addiction.** All the steps I had success with on the way there were all components - if only I had known them all at once years ago! I now have the crucial key to being able to get healthy - for so long I wanted to eat 100% WAP or 100% GAPS or 100% primal, but \*couldn't.\* Now I can do whatever nourishing food protocol I need. It's only up from here.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-20T22:51:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-3513>

I love your story!!! its so inspiring MamaG!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T22:56:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-3515>

To sum up, the key has been FAT and PROTEIN and MORE FAT and PROTEIN. The 50g protein every morning is non-negotiable if you have cravings or binge eating that you cannot resist. Do whatever it takes to get to that level. And once you see the difference, if you still battle it some, increase it yet again. DO NOT LIMIT fat. Your body needs to get the nutrients it needs to heal, and fat is crucial to that. You can put on some modest limits once the cravings are gone, but if you feel them come back, step it back up again for a while. The excess weight will leave after the healing is well on its way, and your hunger will \*naturally\* decrease to the point that you don't have to limit anything intentionally, or very little.

Some photo proof:

High school graduation: 136lb

College graduation & marriage: 132lb

First conception: 132lb. Had baby, lost a lot of weight with CW healthy mindset.

I was 115.5 lb when I started gaining weight as the antibiotics and sugar cravings increased when I was about 23.

Second conception: 122lb

Third conception: 138lb

Fourth conception: 144lb

Fifth conception: 156-160lb (this was after "the best ten pounds I ever gained," which was what happened when I had started nourishing my body with protein and fat, but hadn't yet had enough healing to turn off the cravings. After this, I've done nothing but lose)

After fifth baby, I lost ten pounds at birth to get to 189. I lost a few over the next few days to get to 185. I lost nothing more. Two months post-partum, I went from 185 to 189 and panicked. I dropped wheat stat. This is what I looked like then:

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-21T02:12:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-3661>

Thanks for the kind thoughts, all! I didn't want to take the time to write it all out, but what kind of starfish is that?! Dr. Kruse and others have thrown me back into the sea; I would be a selfish narcissist to refuse to share what has helped me in my path.

> **Marsha;3576 wrote:
>
> So disappointed that I'm much rounder than when I started the LRX -not even my "big" clothes fit! Must start my journal so I can really hold myself accountable -waiting for my lab results, maybe not such a good reason to wait.
>
>
>
>
> The most important ten pounds I ever gained - the ten pounds that I put on from the time I stopped skipping meals to make up for my binges, and insisted on getting in plenty of nourishing fat & protein, to the time that added nutrition did its work to stop those constant binges.
>
>
>
> Lexi;3596 wrote:**
>
> i can see the sugar monster creeping back in despite doing the reset, just from a few "bites" here and there.... it just doesn't work. you can't fool your biochemistry!

Ain't it true? Two very important lessons I've learned:

-   "Just one bite" is a TOTAL LIE until you have healed from all neolithic disease, and been there a long time.
    It's probably impossible to avoid "just one bite" until you've gotten far enough into the reset (the benefits of the protein & fat increases), but you just keep on trying as hard as you can, dusting yourself off when you fall, and doing it all over again. One day, it's the last time. Then the healing can take off.

To quote Churchill:_
Never give in. Never give in. Never, never, never, never--in nothing, great or small, large or petty--never give in, except to convictions of honor and good sense.
_

Never. Fall down, get up. Fall down, get up. Going back is not an option.

### L8F — 2012-03-21T02:37:03+0200

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-21T02:47:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-3694>

My progress on the leptin reset:

**Positive Signs

Cravings: gone unless I have something sweet due to a brain-fart, then they're still manageable

Hunger: no desire to snack AT ALL. No desire to eat lunch 99% of the time, and easily ignorable the 1%

Sleep: solid again, after total chaos at the beginning of the LR

Skin: SUPER soft (with the occasional weird slightly bumpier day) after a lifetime of dry, dry bumpy sandpapery skin.

Mood: Far more stable, not falling apart late afternoon, not screaming when vexed!!

**Inconclusive Signs**

Heat: definitely warmer at night, but not kicking covers off except for once in a blue moon

Fat loss: still losing about 1-2lb/month (yeah, I'm gonna win a prize for rapid weight loss for sure)

Sweating: Not much opportunity to sweat, but I am sweating profusely every week during Mass (standing in warm balcony singing the whole hour) and did not used to. Not stinking, just sweating. In fact, stinking seems to be pretty much eliminated altogether!

Negative Signs**

Waking: still feel like death, or death warmed over

Energy: low, but slowly moving upward

Muscle recovery: climbed cliffs, hiked, & rode horses last Friday, and could hardly walk Sat & Sun. Not so good.

I've needed to reduce BAB from 65g to 50g lately in order to maintain an appetite for dinner. Still feel like eating the full 50g + broth + ferment + carbs every morning.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-21T02:56:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-3700>

> **L8F;3705 wrote: Holy crap!!! Great job on the story and photos. You have made such progress. Amazing that you stuck it out, strong willpower. Worth it!**

**Thought-provoking comment. So many people think it takes willpower, and that those who have addictions or get fat are just lacking in it. The reality, IMO, is that people get that way because they do what everyone else is doing (including following cw health advice), and they exert their willpower in the ways CW tells them to do to end the problem, but IT DOESN'T WORK that way. Eventually most people give up.

At some point, I realized it wasn't that I lacked willpower (or that they did), but that our willpower wasn't being applied to the things that actually help you overcome these problems. Once I found the right path, yes, willpower played a HUGE role in staying on it, but continued to play little to no role in my ability to avoid sugar. I used my willpower to stuff myself with fat and protein, and that, in turn, took the cravings down to so small a level that I could use simple willpower to overcome them.** It was impossible, though, to use willpower to ignore the cravings prior to this.

Without that fat and protein, I don't believe many people on earth could have victory against addiction and obesity, long-term - because the cravings are relentless. You can ignore them for a few hours, but not 24/7, day after day, every day of your life. Eventually you give in, and if you give in just one time in a week, you still stay addicted and fat. But give people the right information, and they can use their will power to implement that program, and the program itself will get rid of the cravings.

I do think one way in which I was blessed was with an extra portion of stubbornness ... I just simply would not accept that I had to be this way for the rest of my life. I had to find a solution, and would never quit till I found it.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-31T19:16:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-9282>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

This was last week when I weighed two pounds more and before losing another 1/4" on my forearm, and 3/4" on my rear.

I was just looking at my measurements and saw that I lost **4" in the waist** the first week I went from 1/2 svg whole wheat sourdough a day to none. Yet it took me two years to realize I had a gluten problem! (Total of 15" lost on the waist, from 46 to 31, and still 3-5" left to get to my old self, no belly fat!)

And I lost 5" in the thigh. 5"! How do you even get 5" on a thigh and still be able to walk?! Proves it wasn't just "baby fat" I suppose.

Amazing. Totally amazing. Though you've certainly paid your dues, haven't you? You deserve every success you continue to find!! And your example keeps me (and many others, I'm sure) going when I've hit what I only hope is bottom. ### MamaGrok — 2012-03-31T20:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-9292>

I know how many, many times I saw people at MDA who were near their goal weight and it was almost painful to see, because I was just at the beginning of my journey, but ...

that was just two years ago. Two short years. If we knew that a particular major life goal that we could either work hard at for two years, or not work at it and it never happen, yeah, I can wait two years. If I had known it would take this long at the beginning, though ... I'm glad I didn't.

I hope I can be the hope for others that others were for me. I still have my inspiration picture from an MDA poster, and I'm still using it for inspiration, because I'm still not there! Still got some tweaks to make in health and some of those pesky places I never had fat before (like behind my hips, weird)...

We all have our broken, and we all have our healing. I pray all of you have the health that is your birthright!

### agatha — 2012-04-09T21:43:29+0300

### Lyndra — 2012-04-09T22:13:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13116>

I just went back and re-read this whole thread and found inspiration. I'm feeling a bit down since I succumbed to sweets (GF cake for a friend's birthday, then GF cookies). I know to get back on the wagon and focus the willpower where it will do the most good. One bite is all it takes - so true for me. Thanks for sharing.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-09T22:47:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13129>

One bite really does do it, but don't get too down, Lyndra - did you binge, or did you just eat them? Even if you binged, you didn't binge on \*true\* crap. Celebrate "better than before" as you move your way to "best"!

My update: I'm now at the weight I was before conceiving, three babies ago. (I always knew it wasn't "baby weight," since no baby needs me to get fat thighs when they're already fat, lol, but also because I always started gaining again \*before\* the next conception.)

### nonchalant — 2012-04-10T16:30:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13325>

> Fat. Protein. Rinse. Repeat. (All. Day. Long.)

Thanks, MamaGrok. I needed that. You are right, you have to exert your willpower to eat that 50g.

Thank you for the inspiration, I've been slacking on that part. The one week I really upped the protein/fat, that week I lost weight. Now I've been settling down to a more "reasonable" breakfast, and the weight is sticking.

\-Sally

### nonchalant — 2012-04-10T16:43:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13329>

> **MamaGrok;13198 wrote:** One bite really does do it, but don't get too down, Lyndra - did you binge, or did you just eat them? Even if you binged, you didn't binge on \*true\* crap. Celebrate "better than before" as you move your way to "best"!

That's true. I think a lot of us aren't really binging anymore, in the true sense of the word, and when we \*do\* indulge, it's with better crap.

When I'm bad, it's with darkish chocolate and nuts, or sometimes, \*gasp!\* fruit. Heh, much, MUCH better than a few years ago!

But I'll start stuffing in the protein and fat again, and watch the scale relax a bit.

Hey, I've been moving my CT baths to nightime, and I noticed last night that my belly fat had GREATLY diminished! This morning it was back to normal, however. But I'm still excited. Wow that was so inspiring! Guess the muscles or skin was tense or something. This is such an amazing process!

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-10T16:56:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13332>

When you say 'belly fat', do you mean how much your belly sticks out, or how much you can pinch? Your description of it going away, then returning, almost sounds more like bloating.

### Lyndra — 2012-04-10T18:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13379>

> **MamaGrok;13198 wrote:** One bite really does do it, but don't get too down, Lyndra - did you binge, or did you just eat them? Even if you binged, you didn't binge on \*true\* crap. Celebrate "better than before" as you move your way to "best"!
>
>
>
> My update: I'm now at the weight I was before conceiving, three babies ago. (I always knew it wasn't "baby weight," since no baby needs me to get fat thighs when they're already fat, lol, but also because I always started gaining again \*before\* the next conception.)

Good point - not a true binge as I used to do them. I could easily down one sleeve of Girl Scout cookies in a sitting and feel like I could eat the other without trying. This time I ate the cookies over a period of a few days. I should have known not to have a package in the house as they'll get eaten (by me!). If I'm going to indulge, it has to be away from home where my quantity is limited. It's a bit disappointing, though, as I thought I had my sweet tooth more in control than that.

Congrats on achieving your previous weight! When is the 10K you're planning on running?

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-10T19:32:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13408>

No particular one. First, I have to have a decent level of energy on a regular basis - getting there very slowly. When I have a normal human level of energy again, I'll start SimpleFit, regular walks, and occasional sprints, and then just pick a race and go for it, lol.

I posted this in another thread, I think, but DH has already shown me how. He has always emphasized meat & fat & veggies in his diet, with very little grains or sweets, just by his nature. He's lean & strong, even though he rarely gets to exercise anymore. He goes out and plays softball with the work team, or flag football with the homeschool dads & sons, 4-5 times a year, and doesn't get winded and isn't sore a bit the next day.

One year our tailgating group went to a bowl game together and decided to run a 5K together. (I was super pregnant, lucky me, so no go.) They trained and trained, except dh, who just showed up and ran. He kept pace with the fasted guy from our group and at the end, easily could have run it all over again. No puffing, no exhaustion, no soreness - no training. Just overall good health.

Don't worry - we all think we have more control over our sweet tooth than we do. Wise people kept telling me that people like us have to accept we can NEVER have even one morsel of anything sweet again. I do think they're wrong, b/c I believe this is not inherent in us (I know it's not in me; I was never this way before the antibiotics barrage), but I think they're right while we're in the midst of it. It's such a strong urge that it even took me years to get to the point where I could not have one bite. And now I can park here for a while and let it heal, so one day I can have my daughter's wedding cake and move on with no care, no obsession, & no damage, just like I can enjoy a steak today.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-10T20:50:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13430>

MamaGrok, yeah, must be bloating. It seemed like half my (excess) tummy was gone!

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-10T21:46:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13450>

Bloating is my bane. I didn't even know I had it until a bunch of the belly fat went away and I could see the dramatic difference between my gut and the rest of me, and from the beginning to the end of a meal. I spent much of last year looking 5-6 months pregnant, and now that I have completely eliminated wheat, dairy, & FODMAPs, am finally making some headway. Now I gotta get mygut healed so I can add some ofthose back (at least the garlic & butter!) without looking pg again!

### nonchalant — 2012-04-11T00:51:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13508>

Well, I'm not gassy, and wheat isn't in the picture. I have cut way back on dairy, but yes, it's so hard to say no to raw, grass fed cream and butter. I do eat onions & cabbage, but dropping them would be no problem.

I'm going to CT tonight again and see what my tummy does. Maybe measure it.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-11T01:19:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13518>

I have never been gassy, either. It's a total mystery to me - what is all that stuff if it comes and goes without gas?! And was having wheat about one serving a month.

It took about 2 weeks after totally removing dairy to see a reduction in bloating that stuck (I put how many months pg I look in my journal each day so I can see patterns), but it still didn't go all the way down. Finally I gave up FODMAPs (this is a MAJOR burden for garlic-loving me!!!) and saw another big reduction. Coconut products also seem to trigger bloating for me.

I think it'll be something different for everyone, but there are certain food categories that seem to affect the most people.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-11T21:25:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13858>

Thanks for the advice, Mamagrok. I know dairy gets to me, because of my puffy eyes. They \*start\* to go down after 2 weeks of no dairy.

I think I might be able to describe my tummy better. Underneath the fat layer is muscle! My abdomen feels like I have been doing situps like a champ! Amazing, because it has been a year since I've lifted any of my barbell weights, or done any exercise. Hubby and I still occasionally stroll around the block, arm in arm, but that would hardly count as significant exercise.

I was planning on starting back up with the exercise in January, but decided to see what I could do about weight loss without it. Sorta my own N=1. I never expected to put on muscle, though!

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-12T01:59:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-13989>

I haven't done sit-ups in six years, but still have rock-hard abs under the blub & bloat. I think it's not so hard to maintain with everyday activity once it's there?

Muscles rock!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-04-23T02:45:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beating-sugar-addiction-ed-cf-much-more.330/post-17940>

> **MamaGrok;13587 wrote:**
>
> Coconut products also seem to trigger bloating for me.

Sadly, I think this is me, too. I had a big bag of coconut chips that were too addicting and made me feel bloated, so I threw them out. I also had a jar of coconut cream that I opened up. I had 2 T with lunch, and had an instant headache, carb/sugar cravings and bloating. That stuff is delish and super spendy, might I add, so I'm kind of disappointed it's going to be a no-go for me. Bummed....

### Ayla2010 — 2012-05-06T00:06:48+0300

# Thread: Portable Mini Air Conditioners
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/portable-mini-air-conditioners.331/>

**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-03-20T23:30:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/portable-mini-air-conditioners.331/post-3534>

Any suggestions for a little portable a/c that I can use while at work? People in my office are so ridiculous they keep the temperature around 78 degrees and I just about die. It's always bothered me, but it seems to be even more so now that I'm doing CT. I thought maybe a small, portable a/c unit would work and found this on Amazon, but it gets some crappy reviews: [http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B003WC4EYQ/ref=ox\_sc\_act\_title\_1?ie=UTF8&m=A1K5JKSMY09R3W](http://http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B003WC4EYQ/ref=ox_sc_act_title_1?ie=UTF8&m=A1K5JKSMY09R3W)

There are even cheaper ones on ebay, but I want something that's actually going to work . . . although it needs to be fairly quiet too. Anyone ever use something like this?

### Adriana — 2012-03-21T00:02:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/portable-mini-air-conditioners.331/post-3559>

> **BJK77;3559 wrote:** Any suggestions for a little portable a/c that I can use while at work? People in my office are so ridiculous they keep the temperature around 78 degrees and I just about die. It's always bothered me, but it seems to be even more so now that I'm doing CT. I thought maybe a small, portable a/c unit would work and found this on Amazon, but it gets some crappy reviews: [http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B003WC4EYQ/ref=ox\_sc\_act\_title\_1?ie=UTF8&m=A1K5JKSMY09R3W](http://http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B003WC4EYQ/ref=ox_sc_act_title_1?ie=UTF8&m=A1K5JKSMY09R3W)
>
>
>
> There are even cheaper ones on ebay, but I want something that's actually going to work . . . although it needs to be fairly quiet too. Anyone ever use something like this?

The Cool or Heat Seat Cushion goes fown to 50 degrees and will give you contact [http://www.coolorheat.com/](http://http://www.coolorheat.com/). Also google cooling seat pads. Theres one on Amazon for $20. [http://www.amazon.com/Cooling-Cushion-Wheelchair-Office-Return/dp/B004SR95C0](http://http://www.amazon.com/Cooling-Cushion-Wheelchair-Office-Return/dp/B004SR95C0)

### BJK77 — 2012-03-21T03:19:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/portable-mini-air-conditioners.331/post-3717>

Thanks Adriana. I think I might try the one on Amazon for now and see how it goes.

# Thread: Confession....completely off the wagon today
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColdBren — 2012-03-21T00:12:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/post-3568>

Had some friends over today and went a little crazy on the wine, crackers, cheese....BAGELS...I am so mad and have that carby/wine shaky feeling now. Whats the best thing to do for the rest of this day? Its now 3:10 pm. Do I eat, not eat? What do people think the best way to get through the sure to come carb cravings? I hate starting over. Will go sulk in an ice bath

### PrimalPam — 2012-03-21T00:42:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/post-3590>

I've been on the LR since Jan. 1. I, too, have gone off the wagon; the longest was a whole week. It was my birthday...and boy did I celebrate...horrible sleep, upset stomach, GERD returns, weight gain...

My best advice is, the very next time you eat, make it super clean Paleo. If you're absolutely starving, have a slice or two of bacon. Then hold out till your next meal, which might be a BA salad with meat, CO oil, veggies. Don't beat yourself up about it. But DO take note of how you feel, how you sleep, what your skin looks like, when you eat processed carbs, because this knowledge will help you a ton the next time you see that bagel that's so "hard to resist".

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T02:00:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/post-3644>

suffer through tonight! Should give your body the fasting to get through the junk. Start the day with the BAB and move on! It's all about how you try again when failing. If you are not failing at anything, you are not doing anything!!

### Petra — 2012-03-21T02:13:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/post-3663>

Do not stress out is my strongest advice. You are defined by your habits, not the incidents.

Like Caveman said, sit today out and have a great big fat breakfast tomorrow!

sleep well,

Petra

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-21T03:27:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/post-3724>

What I would do:

\- eat the next meal, no matter how full you feel. (skipping it always leads me to cravings later)

\- Absolutely get the protein and fat in next breakfast. Do not skimp.

\- have a little bit of fruit the next day to taper off cravings. At the end of a meal. Just a little bit.

\- no stressing. Dust off, move on.

\- celebrate anything about it that was a success ... you had fewer than you would have had six months ago? You went two weeks between cheats instead of two days? What was better than in the past?

Your resident screw-up expert,

MamaGrok

### caroline — 2012-03-21T03:40:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/post-3733>

great advice from MamaGrok. I so agree - if I try to skip the next meal to compensate I wind up way worse off and craving everything. I think some bacon is a good idea when hungry. I sometimes think it is the fat I am craving - hence the mega amounts of cheese I can eat when in this situation!

> **MamaGrok;3749 wrote:** What I would do:
>
>
>
> \- eat the next meal, no matter how full you feel. (skipping it always leads me to cravings later)
>
> \- Absolutely get the protein and fat in next breakfast. Do not skimp.
>
> \- have a little bit of fruit the next day to taper off cravings. At the end of a meal. Just a little bit.
>
> \- no stressing. Dust off, move on.
>
> \- celebrate anything about it that was a success ... you had fewer than you would have had six months ago? You went two weeks between cheats instead of two days? What was better than in the past?
>
>
>
> Your resident screw-up expert,
>
> MamaGrok

### ColdBren — 2012-03-21T04:18:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/post-3759>

Great points!! Thanks everyone

### AKMan — 2012-03-22T02:37:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/post-4167>

Hey, ColdBren - Looking at your sig line, I sense you don't know what it's like to be really fat and unhealthy. Take it from me, it's no fun! I have been doing the paleo/primal thing for almost 2 years and at this point you couldn't pay me to eat a bagel or a cracker. I hope you can get to 120s? as you seem to desire--and you can, but those cheats need to be dealt with, not afterwards--but before you do them.

I know you wouldn't be here if you weren't serious about getting optimal, so next time the situation arises, stick to the wine and cheese or dark chocolate--but say 'hell no' to the bagels (and cookies, ice cream, bread, rolls, pasta, etc....)

Good luck!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-22T03:46:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/post-4207>

On the lines of what AKMan is saying, when I first gave up grains, but still battled cravings & binges, I drew a line - I would not touch anything that I didn't love. That meant no crackers, pasta, white rice, most rolls, etc. At least I'd stick to stuff that had \*some\* positive!

It worked - even in the midst of enormous, horrifying binges, I never had any of those things. I haven't had Ritz, bagels, spaghetti, buns, etc., in years. I didn't miss them at all. Over time, that line moved - I no longer liked Wonder bread, storebought cookies,, storebought cake, etc. Now there's almost nothing in the category of wheat or sugar that could call me at all. But long ago, before I could get there, I drew my line - no more things (that didn't nourish me) that I didn't love.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-22T03:54:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/post-4213>

I can say that premeditation has worked for me on this front. (Though for me, too, the stakes feel really high and the lines are drawn!). However, when I know I'm faced with a party, meal out, social situation, etc I prepare myself. Not just food-wise, but mentally. Being around others who may be like-minded in many ways but think cold-seeking, paleo-eating folks are nut jobs, I find the 'story' I tell myself about needing to be socially appropriate, relaxed (vs. uptight and weird about what I eat) and how I need to fit in can get really big in my head. Big enough to drown out the mature line-in-the-sand voice that says 'hell no.' So now I prepare and I let that "I feel insecure and need to fit in" voice have her say BEFORE the others arrive. Then I assure her (this is metaphorical, you realise?) that I will find a way to fit in and enjoy myself without needing to cave on the food front. That can be done a variety of ways depending on the setting. But, at least for me, it always begins with me realising that I'm up against more than just dietary choices.

### ColdBren — 2012-03-22T04:30:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/post-4225>

I need the slap in the face, thanks guys! It's not just the weight, I have some major adrenal/hormonal things that I have been battling for about 20 years. Doctors put me on meds thinking it was psychological but I am now realizing it seems to completely calm down with paleo. I have seen the cravings go down a bit, but can't wait to get to a point where I wouldn't want a bagel!  Lauren, I deinitely do notice premeditation/planning always works better, I need to work on that. But, I did perfect today YAY with help from you guys telling me to get my act together!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-22T04:51:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confession-completely-off-the-wagon-today.333/post-4239>

I find it takes a combination of tactics, persevered over time, to make it work. The behavioral/psychological tactics definitely play a role - but they were never enough alone. Once I got the big protein breakfast going, the cravings just died back to a very manageable point where I could then use those behavioral tactics and have them work!

I used to think bagels were manna. I ate them every day in college. Usually many of them. Now I think they're just flavored paste (and I get constipated just thinking of eating one!). Things change, thank goodness!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Symptoms I've not seen on here before.  Post unusual CT symptoms/benefits.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 147
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T01:31:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-3624>

Ok, 2 weeks face dunking, 2 CT ice pack sessions.

One thing I've noticed the last couple of days, that I've not seen on here. Skin tags on my neck and neck/shoulder area!

I wonder if this is too much o6 getting detoxed? Any clues? Anybody else? Jack, is this expected.

I really REALLY want them to go away. Not had those much, and I think they look a little old lady!

Vain in Colorado.

### chocolate — 2012-03-21T01:37:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-3626>

I have a skin tag near my eye. Other stuff is getting much nicer. I had a fatty deposit near my other eye melt. My palms peeled also. Really odd. They weren't dry or scaly, they just peeled or more like shed. My eye wrinkles are still bad. But I'm not dunking, I just swim in a pool an go under water. After I shower, I turn it on cold until I start deep breathing.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T01:49:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-3633>

The somewhat alarming thing is these are NEW. Didn't have them 3 weeks ago. I think I've only ever had one, and that was during pregnancy. My hope is it's CT, and will go away with perserverance. It was warmer today, and I wore a boatneck kind of shirt, and was running my hand along my neck - and voila! I noticed I had like 6 areas where they were already tags, or were brewing.

### tweety — 2012-03-21T02:19:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-3667>

I think I remember reading skin tags are connected to estrogen dominance. So perhaps your body is clearing excess estrogen?

### hazyjane — 2012-03-21T02:31:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-3674>

Huh. I've heard that skin tags are usually connected to insulin resistance, but that wouldn't make sense in this case...

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T05:52:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-3787>

They are common during pregnancy because of high hormone levels. I had a few that are gone now. I'd imagine that's what is being flushed out, and they should hopefully go away! That's just a guess, tho.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-21T20:43:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-3988>

Dr. Kruse thinks skin tags are a sign of high blood sugar, type 2 diabetes, etc. I think there's considerable overlap, too, in my experience:

> What about Omega six content of your diet? Many of you know that I think this is the biggest game changer for our epigenomic programming and is at the seat of most of our metabolic issues as modern humans. Soybean oils are particularly problematic. So what should you look for?
>
>
>
> Look for skin tags in folds of skin or around your neck. It is a pretty common finding these days. When I see them I tell the patient the next step will be diabetes generation.
>
> [http://jackkruse.com/what-can-you-find-about-your-own-health/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-can-you-find-about-your-own-health/)
>
>
>
> Your barber notices that you are developing skin tags in your neck folds. Your doctor sees them in your armpits and chest too now. You skin color is very uneven. If someone orders a HbA1c, they also mention that your HbA1c is over 5.5 now.

[http://jackkruse.com/what-is-biologic-concrete/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-is-biologic-concrete/)

Maybe check sugar, A1c, and omega6/3 profile?

I have no idea why one would pop out suddenly, however.

### dinglebutt — 2012-03-21T20:47:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-3991>

Yeah I have skin tags and other IR symptoms, and I'm as big as a whale.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T22:13:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-4035>

Doc Dan,

Yea - that's the weird thing. Blood Sugar is fine, No diabetes stuff. . I've been low 06, VLC paleo for over 8 months. I suppose I should actually have tests though. Any chance you can treat/order test remotely doc?

It's not just ONE skin tag, it's like several areas with sets of them - in various stages. And it's within the last 4 days. W E I R D.

I was eating keto paleo before starting CT, and that is really the only lifestyle difference I've done. I'm going to keep going and see what happened.

I am itchy rashy, on arms/legs too. I'm powering through, as I seem to be tolerating the CT with no signs of issues where I'm icing. Things are a nice cherry red as expected.

I really dread trying to find a doc that would think this is all kosher. From your perspective - what would be the best way to approach?

I've got great insurance, and I'm willing to pay!

### Lexi — 2012-03-21T22:15:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-4036>

### tweety — 2012-03-22T01:32:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-4127>

I have had them myself since college, as well as my sister and my father. My weight has always been good, but I do have estrogen dominance. I am definitely not insulin resistant, nor my sister. My dad I cannot say, because he is overweight and probably has issues we don't even know about. He is however low in testosterone and had several other symptoms of male estrogen excess.

>
>
> [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2856367/](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2856367/)

This would help explain I think. I think high omega-6 is why people get and have tags as routine. These however just popped up out of the blue (not having them before) in just a few days. If fat is burning, (particularly belly fat where I'm icing), then its surging my system with estrogen. According to the Article you linked, areas of the neck (where I have this) have lots of estrogen binders thus go the tags there.

It makes me feel a little better anyway! I just hope they go away. Jack - have you seen this before?

### Kathy — 2012-03-22T02:15:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-4153>

> **Cavemam;4061 wrote:** Doc Dan,
>
> ...
>
> I really dread trying to find a doc that would think this is all kosher. From your perspective - what would be the best way to approach?
>
>
>
> I've got great insurance, and I'm willing to pay!

Not Dr Dan, but I went through the dread for a long time. Like lots of things, worrying about it was a lot harder than just doing it.

If you've got great insurance AND are willing to pay, I say get going & find yourself a Doc to work with. It probably won't be the first one you see, but keep looking, it's worth it.

What worked for me was realizing I didn't have to share everything the first appt... I could just ask about one thing, maybe bring up diet, see how that went, go home and think about whether or not I wanted to go back.

Turned out it was all about building a relationship and just like any relationship, it took time and not everything was revealed immediately!

> **Cavemam;4061 wrote:** Doc Dan,
>
>
>
> Yea - that's the weird thing. Blood Sugar is fine, No diabetes stuff. . I've been low 06, VLC paleo for over 8 months. I suppose I should actually have tests though. Any chance you can treat/order test remotely doc?
>

>
> It's not just ONE skin tag, it's like several areas with sets of them - in various stages. And it's within the last 4 days. W E I R D.
>

>
>
>
> I am itchy rashy, on arms/legs too. I'm powering through, as I seem to be tolerating the CT with no signs of issues where I'm icing. Things are a nice cherry red as expected.
>

> I really dread trying to find a doc that would think this is all kosher. From your perspective - what would be the best way to approach?

CT has given me a rash on my forearms: it's follicular eczema. (In my case anyway. Sort of like "small, itchy zits.") I'm fairly certain it's from the CT, but I don't know why.

I'd look on PaleoDocs website. Maybe there is someone in your area you could work with.

I'd give your doctor a paper about leptin resistance. That's how I got started into this. A "formerly" obese patient of mine did this for me and I was so intrigued by his weight loss, BP changes, and reversal of Type 2 diabetes, that I had to figure this out. I've been "going back to school" in this regard ever since.

Finding a doc who can "partner up" with you is going to be easier than finding a Dr. Kruse clone. (Obviously.) Might not be optimal, but could work.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-23T15:23:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-4937>

I've been having aches in my arms and legs down to what feels like the bone. It usually happens the morning after a soak for an hour or so. I don't know if it's CT or part of whatever awfulness I've been going through this past week (which is supposedly CT detox), or what. Just this week I started feeling about 15 min of nausea after a bath. It passes quick enough and since I'm taking a bath 5-6 hours my last meal, I assume it's because of the time since food intake. But truth be told, several people had mentioned nausea symptoms and I sort of discounted it as connected (the ever present skeptic that I am). Maybe I was wrong.

Also, right after I get out of a bath, deep veins in my legs are very visible. These aren't little varicose veins on the surface or even broken capillaries, but deeper veins that turn bright green while the skin is cherry red. WTH?

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-23T15:35:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-4942>

I get that really numb lack of ability to use my arms when they get too cold. When I've gone walking outside and it's below freezing I get that. Hard to use my hands for a bit. I wonder if you should keep your arms out. . .perhaps your body isn't quite heating up enough in the extremities yet.

Yay on the symptoms! It's more proof something is working. Heck, perhaps soon you will be rashy and itchy - too.

Jack stresses doing this on a full stomach, perhaps see if you can do that more and if it helps.

Keep going! Good Luck.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-23T15:44:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-4949>

Hard to use my hands for a bit. I wonder if you should keep your arms out. . .perhaps your body isn't quite heating up enough in the extremities yet.
>

> Jack stresses doing this on a full stomach, perhaps see if you can do that more and if it helps.
>
>
>
> Keep going! Good Luck.

This is an ache in my arms and legs that shows up 6-8 hours after the bath - not during and not immediately following when my body is trying to warm up. It's not numbness or heaviness - I wake up with what feels like an onset of the flu or something. Like the bones actually ache inside the muscles. I haven't been submerging my arms at all, and I can't do much about my legs since that's the only part of me that I can really cover with water.

As for meals, I've been eating the 3rd meal around 6-7pm to make sure I allow for 4 hours between last meal and sleep (as underlined in CT7), go to my 2nd job, then am home around 10:45pm for a CT soak. I'm afraid to eat anything before the bath when it's that late at night and I don't have time to take one before going to my 2nd job. I am so frustrated. And THAT's probably increasing cortisol and not helping one damn bit. Ugh.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-23T16:04:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-4957>

> **Clabbergirl;4981 wrote:** This is an ache in my arms and legs that shows up 6-8 hours after the bath - not during and not immediately following when my body is trying to warm up. It's not numbness or heaviness - I wake up with what feels like an onset of the flu or something. Like the bones actually ache inside the muscles. I haven't been submerging my arms at all, and I can't do much about my legs since that's the only part of me that I can really cover with water.
>
>
>
> As for meals, I've been eating the 3rd meal around 6-7pm to make sure I allow for 4 hours between last meal and sleep (as underlined in CT7), go to my 2nd job, then am home around 10:45pm for a CT soak. I'm afraid to eat anything before the bath when it's that late at night and I don't have time to take one before going to my 2nd job. I am so frustrated. And THAT's probably increasing cortisol and not helping one damn bit. Ugh.

Ok, so I'm so sorry to say this, because I don't have a solution - I am a mom and work full time - so I get how hard it is to do all this!! I'm fortunate in I work from home when not with clients so I somehow squeeze in all the cooking and icing. Sounds like you are not doing that, AND that you are not sleeping a full amount each night. No sleep, no loss. CT-7 really brought this one home on how sleep is more important that CT - you simply can't get into the stages you need on low sleep.

If you are going to bed after 11p - I bet you are getting up before 8 a.m., and after sunlight is streaming in the room. I think people are sleeping 8-9 hrs when "detoxing" and adapting. The more fat you need to burn, the more you need.

Perhaps dial the CT back to make the lifestyle work a little better - so face dunk and spot icing while driving to/from work? Do baths when you have time. . but don't sacrifice sleep. If you are already doing tubs, then you can probably shove ice bags in your clothes while doing other things - and while driving.

I know this is easier said than done. As I said, I'm fortunate to have the juggling option on it all so it's easy to sit here calmly and say "do this do this".

As several have said before. . it sounds like it is working for you somewhat. You've got some symptoms - but Jack said it in an early post this a.m., no sleep, no weight loss.

And lets face it, all the other positives are great - but our brains are laser focused on weight being the marker for getting it done.

My completely unsolicited prescription for you is to give yourself a mental break. Make sleep your priority, and ice when you can and just chill. You've got to get to a place that this is just life now if you are going to reap the optimal benefits.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-23T16:32:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-4976>

>

>

> My completely unsolicited prescription for you is to give yourself a mental break. Make sleep your priority, and ice when you can and just chill. You've got to get to a place that this is just life now if you are going to reap the optimal benefits.

I agree with much of what you've said here. I'm up by 5:45 every morning while it's still dark outside. Often times I will go out to my car during my lunch break at the first job and nap, or grab a 30 min nap in between job #1 and job #2 (and these naps are the only time I'm sitting/laying down to spot-ice). I've tried spot-icing in the car, but my commute to both jobs is 10 min, max, so it seems sort of pointless since I have nowhere to store ice at either. I suppose I could spot ice on the 'to' journey for a combined 20 min. Something is better than nothing.

Last night I was soooo incredibly tired that I skipped the bath and just fell asleep on flat ice pack thing I have. This is the first morning this week I've felt better than the day before - not great still - but not worse than the previous. I was going downhill every single day. I take that as progress. I've taken to using my CPAP machine again (I was Dx with sleep apnea years ago for structural reasons, not weight-related). After sinus surgery a year ago I stopped using it because the symptoms went away and I felt no different when using and when not.

Appreciate your kindness in this. I am not purposely throwing up obstacles to be difficult. This is my life and as suboptimal as it is, it's where I am at the present. A person has to start where they are. I've been seriously considering quitting the 2nd job for some time, but I'm a single mother who's about to lose the last bit of child support for her daughter who's graduating this year, so it's not like my budget is going to improve any time soon - quite the opposite. But the harder I work, the fatter and more unhappy I get.

I believe my adrenals are shot which drove my hypothryoid and perimenopausal symptoms starting several years back. I'd like to focus on them, but can't afford to go to one of these BHRT clinics that I watch advertised on tv every morning, and my doctor is hopelessly useless. It's interesting to me how so many of the BHRT clinics in my town seem to be located in the more affluent areas and none of them take insurance. Hmmm. It leaves the natural approach with herbs and supplements (can you say $$?), and the skeptic in me questions all of these things because it seems like everyone on the internet wants to sell something. This forum and marksdailyapple have been my only real source of valid info on the topic and I've got so much more to learn.

I've digressed. Thank you again for your input. It is encouraging.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T01:10:55+0300

### Melinda — 2012-03-28T01:35:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7423>

> **PaleoNana;7446 wrote:** Would small bruises on my legs be caused by low K2? I see them now after full CTing and icing on a daily basis. I typically never bruise.

If you are taking fish or krill oil, eating lots of fish, you might need to add K2 to keep from bruising. So it might not be low K2 directly just that you are thinning your blood with all the O3 and need to balance that with extra K2.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-28T02:01:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7435>

After reading everyone's posts on this I think it's safe to say that bruising, stiffness, rashes, dizziness, and numbness, are fairly common, yet relatively benign side effects of CT.

If someone has a lot of these symptoms, they probably have to slow down a bit. In my mind, CT is a marathon anyway, not a sprint. Pace yourselves, amigos.

Ain't if fun playing on the bleeding edge of science?

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T04:47:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7535>

Alrighty, I've got one that I haven't seen mentioned. I apologize if I've missed it. My husband and I, after a couple of weeks of baths, decided to start using our pool. The pool temp has been in the mid 50's, though the outside air-temp has taken a nose dive. Anyhow, both yesterday and today My wrists and forearms got really achey in the water. When I moved my wrists in the water or even for a while after I got out of the water I would get a very strange and unpleasant sensation. The best I can describe is that it was like a current shooting up my forearm. Very weird, not like anything I've felt before. Any ideas?

### KetoWarrior — 2012-03-28T08:41:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7623>

### Inger — 2012-03-28T11:09:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7642>

I come to think about one symptom now, as it seem to have disappeared..

It happened about 2 weeks in to CT and it was in my right ear. It come suddenly and hold on for some minutes on and off. It felt like my ear was kind of blocked for a while (which I have had before especially at the end of a fitnessstudio-session or at work) but this time additionally a "knockin" sound...!!! That knocked about 5-10 times maybe.. and then made a short break and then knocked again. Very strange. As someone was knocking on my eardrum! Now I have not had it in a few days..

Strange huh.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-28T18:27:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7755>

Here's a symptom I don't think I've seen many, if any, folks state they have: increasing inches.

Apparently, not only am I _not losing inches through my 45+ min baths, 3x + a week in 57-60 degree water in addition to spot-icing 30+ min on non-soak days, but I'm gaining_ inches in some places. Most definitely my upper arms, as a shirt that was wearable is now too snug in the arms to fit into. Lovely.

Apparently CT is working brilliantly for many of you, but not at all for me, and may even be making me fatter than I was when I started. I've tried a lot of ridiculous things in my 44 years to lose weight, some have worked and some have been a waste of time. A primal diet, the leptin reset, and more recently, CT are a few that I've really felt held promise, not because of results, but because of the science and logic behind them. It is saddening to realize that once again, principles that apply to the majority do not apply to me.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-28T20:07:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7814>

> **differentstory;7832 wrote:** Clabbergirl, what if you posted a food/health diary in a new thread? It looks like lots of us want to help and give you suggestions, but we are not seeing the full picture?

I'm sorry, be forewarned, I am beside myself with grumpiness today. I will consider posting a food diary, and to what end? So I can be told that I'm fat because I eat dairy? Or I'm fat because I'm eating cured bacon (because I can't find/afford the other). Or maybe I'm fat because I have a leaky gut. Well surely. I'm about to slip into the pointlessness phase and I know, I know, I have no one to blame but myself. I get that. This is my pity party, please, have a seat or move along.

I don't miss wheat and grains and junk food at all; I miss feeling like there's hope. I'm spending more $ on food, more $ on supplements, more $ on coconut oil than I ever have in my life, yet getting none-to-worse results. At my current income, I need to justify every cent I spend and yet with great trepidation I've bought and ingested substance after substance with the sincere hope that this one_, this one, this _combination, will be what makes my body realize it doesn't need to carry around the extra weight equivalent of an 11 year old! My labs say I'm estrogen dominant, so I try to hack that myself with progesterone and pregnenolone, and 3 weeks into it I'm looking and becoming _fatter than before_. Get more labs, get more labs. I don't have the $ for more labs. Get BHRT, get BHRT. I don't have the $ for BHRT. Maybe it's my thyroid - yes it probably is but I have a moron for an M.D. and so tried doing it myself with Armour to the point that all I was left with was 6 grains a day and zero weight loss plus the same symptoms. I was afraid to go any higher and wasn't getting results anyway.

I've been eating so much healthier in the past 3 months and I've gained at least 5.5 pounds? High protein, quality fats, low carb/ketogenic, and gained_ 5 lbs? I've been keeping quiet about the CT because already I am a horrible spokesperson for primal eating, and for all obvious purposes, it's doing nothing beneficial for me. Obvious is all I have. (If we should listen to our bodies, my body is telling me it is in hell and doesn't like what I'm doing, as it gets more inflamed/fatter.) Dr. Kruse says stay with it, the more weight you have to lose, the more you have to go through. Ok, I can deal with that. Then I read today that he also said if you have a lot of weight to lose you should be spot-icing instead of soaking, and earlier rather than later - all the exact opposite of what I've been doing for the past month. I have so little time as it is, it was the icing on the gluten-free cake to think I've been wasting it for naught. What was this 2 weeks business about CT adaptation?? When does CT make DHEA rise and stay that way? When does it override the thyroid? When does it reset the female hormones? WHEN does CT create it's magic?_ When I've gained another 20 lbs?

I have read and reread many of Dr. Kruse's posts, and he is a brilliant man. I respect him and am not knocking him in any way. I'm just frustrated to the point of exhaustion and withdrawal. I'm deeply saddened that what is working for so many in little and big ways is working in the opposite way for me. I don't know what I am going to do because I can't go back to exercising for hours at a time either. I feel I am at the end, and if this doesn't work, afraid I will be fat forever. I thought I had made peace with that until I ran across the Paleo/Primal way of eating and Dr. Kruse, and fell victim to Hope again.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T20:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7820>

For the first two weeks, I had shooting almost burning pains from elbows to fingers, not anymore. That was while I was in the water (45-60 min)

Now I am experiencing same odd pain in my breasts, a heavy, hot ache. Not the tenderness that comes with Aunt Flo, it's deep inside and hurts like crazy for about 15 minutes. Then vanishes. it has happened twice this week, 30 min after a soak, Sunday and today.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T20:20:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7828>

Clabbergirl, I'm pretty sure I have put on muscle since I started CT ~3 weeks ago, I didn't want to take measurements too soon, I may do so on Saturday.

Perhaps this is the same for you? That is not a bad thing (although it probably isn't what you intended) but again it is not a bad thing, it will affect what you see happening on the scale.

I hope you find what you are searching for.
