So I can be told that I'm fat because I eat dairy? Or I'm fat because I'm eating cured bacon (because I can't find/afford the other). Or maybe I'm fat because I have a leaky gut. Well surely. I'm about to slip into the pointlessness phase and I know, I know, I have no one to blame but myself. I get that. This is my pity party, please, have a seat or move along.
>
>
>
> I don't miss wheat and grains and junk food at all; I miss feeling like there's hope. I'm spending more $ on food, more $ on supplements, more $ on coconut oil than I ever have in my life, yet getting none-to-worse results. At my current income, I need to justify every cent I spend and yet with great trepidation I've bought and ingested substance after substance with the sincere hope that this one_, this one, this _combination, will be what makes my body realize it doesn't need to carry around the extra weight equivalent of an 11 year old! My labs say I'm estrogen dominant, so I try to hack that myself with progesterone and pregnenolone, and 3 weeks into it I'm looking and becoming fatter than before_. Get more labs, get more labs. I don't have the $ for more labs. Get BHRT, get BHRT. I don't have the $ for BHRT. Maybe it's my thyroid - yes it probably is but I have a moron for an M.D. and so tried doing it myself with Armour to the point that all I was left with was 6 grains a day and zero weight loss plus the same symptoms. I was afraid to go any higher and wasn't getting results anyway.
>
>
>
> I've been eating so much healthier in the past 3 months and I've gained 5 pounds? High protein, quality fats, low carb/ketogenic, and gained_ 5 lbs? I've been keeping quiet about the CT because already I am a horrible spokesperson for primal eating, and frankly, it's doing nothing beneficial for me. Dr. Kruse says stay with it, the more weight you have to lose, the more you have to go through. Ok, I can deal with that. Then I read today that he also said if you have a lot of weight to lose you should be spot-icing instead of soaking, and earlier rather than later - all the exact opposite of what I've been doing for the past month. I have so little time as it is, it was the icing on the gluten-free cake to think I've been wasting it for naught. What was this 2 weeks business about CT adaptation?? When does CT make DHEA rise and stay that way? When does it override the thyroid? When does it setting the female hormones? WHEN does CT create it's magic? When I've gained another 20 lbs?
>
>
>
> I have read and reread many of Dr. Kruse's posts, and he is a brilliant man. I respect him and am not knocking him in any way. I'm just frustrated to the point of exhaustion and withdrawal. I don't know what I am going to do because I can't go back to exercising for hours at a time either. I am at the end and if this doesn't work, I will be fat forever. I thought I had made peace with that until I ran across the Paleo/Primal way of eating and fell to Hope again.

Hi, Clabbergirl. I hear you. I know you are having a hard time. The negative attitude that you display - been there. Last week was one of the worst week's of my life. I could barely function. I couldn't even work (at my job). This week I am feeling a million times better. That is why I am reaching out and want to help. I believe there is someone on this forum just like you that has had success. It will come.

I also was really struggling with the weight. I started at 203, lost 4 pounds in a week - I was so stoked. I was doing a slow carb diet. Felt bad and had lots of cravings so I started Leptin Rx. First week, I felt great, cravings gone, but I gained every pound back! ARGH! I cried everyday the scale went up. I dug into the research though and started adding a ridiculously high amount of fat to my diet and guess what? I'm down 6.6 pounds in the past two weeks (mostly past week).

I don't know what the answer is for you, but between MDA, this forum, and the few other Paleo ones. . .there is an answer. Post your diet and supplements and ask for help. And remember, your mood is coming from your wacky hormones. Try not to let it interfere. In your moments of clarity, make goals for yourself. Write them down and when you are feeling like sh\*t, just go by you wrote down. Tell yourself you will journal everyday (I journal my meals, BP, temp, energy, mood and measurements - takes just a few minutes a day). Show others your journal and ask for help. You will find a way if you don't give up.

And if all else fails, read Quelson's journal. He is an amazing inspiration in not giving up. It is on this forum.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-28T20:25:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7834>

Thank you, and I consoled myself with this also, until this morning, that is, when I got dressed. Can't wear one of my short-sleeved shirt now because it digs painfully into my arms when before it was merely snug. I had been thinking for the past week how it seems like I look fatter than I have lately - arms, double chin. I've been telling myself I'm imagining it. Guess not. I really am getting fatter. I'm not one of those daily weighers - the number isn't a big deal to me and I can gain/lose 5 lbs depending on what I ate that day. No big deal. But when my clothes start getting tight and inches start increasing, it's for real.

I believe, and this is just my opinion, that if you are feeling like sh\*t now and have a poor attitude, what you are doing is not working for you. Change something today. Change something everyday until you start to see an improvement (in mood, energy, weight, whatever). That's what I do. I personally don't believe in waiting 2 weeks if I see no changes (unless maybe I weighed 140lbs.). If I blindly wait 2 weeks, then I get to the 2-week mark, see no improvement and sink into a deeeeeeep depression.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T20:29:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7836>

Hi all I posted this in the Meet and Greet area as well....I wanted to say a hello to everyone, and pick your brain.

I have been on leptin reset for 3 months and CT for 7 weeks on saturday.

Having some issues, just wondering if anyone had some input for me...

I am late on my TOTM, by 2 weeks. I am never ever late unless I am pregnant, I know the minute I am, and I know I am not. I mean I know 28 days down to a few hours when it will arrive.

As well in the past week I am getting(dark) facial hair, I mean what? I am so strict about LRx and I know my hormones should go wacky, they did week 2/3 of CT, pmssy and acne and greasy and all. But this is awful.

I am in Canada, and my Dr will not run tests, just had them and they were 'fine' and I just cant afford a naturopath and hope that they may run them. I am freaking out, more so about the hair!

Sorry this isnt really a positive post, but I am thinking this is a deal breaker for me I just dont know what to do...

Thanks

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-28T20:31:16+0300

I've been keeping quiet about the CT because already I am a horrible spokesperson for primal eating, and for all obvious purposes, it's doing nothing beneficial for me. Obvious is all I have. (If we should listen to our bodies, my body is telling me it is in hell and doesn't like what I'm doing, as it gets more inflamed/fatter.) Dr. Kruse says stay with it, the more weight you have to lose, the more you have to go through. Ok, I can deal with that. Then I read today that he also said if you have a lot of weight to lose you should be spot-icing instead of soaking, and earlier rather than later - all the exact opposite of what I've been doing for the past month. I have so little time as it is, it was the icing on the gluten-free cake to think I've been wasting it for naught. What was this 2 weeks business about CT adaptation?? When does CT make DHEA rise and stay that way? When does it override the thyroid? When does it reset the female hormones? WHEN does CT create it's magic?_ When I've gained another 20 lbs?
>
>
>
> I have read and reread many of Dr. Kruse's posts, and he is a brilliant man. I respect him and am not knocking him in any way. I'm just frustrated to the point of exhaustion and withdrawal. I'm deeply saddened that what is working for so many in little and big ways is working in the opposite way for me. I don't know what I am going to do because I can't go back to exercising for hours at a time either. I feel I am at the end, and if this doesn't work, afraid I will be fat forever. I thought I had made peace with that until I ran across the Paleo/Primal way of eating and Dr. Kruse, and fell victim to Hope again.

For starters, I don't think one can expect to know how to dose oneself with bioidentical hormones. If you are estrogen dominant and it sounds like you are, when you use progesterone sometimes your body ups the estrogen until it falls into it's balance. Sometimes not, sometimes it just keeps increasing estrogen. What does estrogen do - it makes you hold on to water and be snappy at people. You have to have a good doc to help you dose progesterone. There is a ratio that has to be met. This sounds like the reason you are holding on to water ( don't believe you are gaining weight although Dr. K says if you gain weight you have hormone issues). Dr. Uzzi Reiss has some really good hormone books out there. The latest and my fav. is The Natural Superwomen. His first book is also good. It is easy to read and explains a lot. It is also cheap on Amazon if you buy it used.

I would also go to Drlam.com and read up on OAT. OAT = Ovarian, Adrenal and Thyroid axis imbalance. If you have one of these going on (and it sounds like you have more than one going on) the other 2 will be out of balance. Read Dr. K's blog on What Casey Anthony and OJ have in common. It is about the adrenals. More than likely you have an adrenal issue and no amount of thyroid med is going to fix that. You need to fix the adrenals first and then go after the thyroid. Actually, the thyroid should fix itself if you fix the adrenals providing you have secondary hypothyroidism. If you can't go to a good doc I would start with a salivary cortisol test to see where you are at. Post it out here and we will help you interpret it. You cannot fix what you don't know is broke so I believe you have to test, test and test if you really want to be healthy.

I have been doing the reset since last July. I am not giving up. I found I have issues to work on and that is why I am not having success yet although I do have some small wins even though. This has taken me 3 docs to figure out. Sometimes I have had to wait 3 months to get into them. Talk about frustrating. You eventually will have to make the choice to find a good doc and have the appropriate labs done or just give up like you are on the verge of doing (please don't give up). This is your life and only you can control it and make it happen. Change your mindset to change your DNA. You have to mentally turn things around and look at the picture differently. Yes, it is ok to rant but all it does is raise your cortisol and we all know what that does to us. Perhaps take a step back and approach this differently. Ask yourself, what can I do to help myself that isn't going to hurt myself. I beleive dosing on your own is really setting the stage for disaster and that's where you are at right now.

You mention ingesting substances - do you even know if you are absorbing those substances? I have been peeing them down the toilet. I recently found out via testing that I am not absorbing my food. I would read some articles on betaine HCL and see if you might need it. This would be a first approach to getting you on the right track. It has made a world of difference for me along with enzymes.

Also, I can only relate to those things that I have issues with so here me out on one more. I have Candida (hopefully soon not to) but you will not get anywhere with Candida. Candida is an intestional yeast overgrowth sort of like when women get yeast infections but it is in the gut. There will be no losing weight ever or anything positive. If you have eaten a lot of sugar, been on a lot of anit-biotics, had a lot of stress, etc. you probably have Candida. There are a lot of good websites on it.

I wish you many better days. Back off the progesterone until you can have someone help you figure it out. Read about betaine, then about Candida. Start small with these things and save your supps until you know they are benefitting you. Eat real food for now. You did not get this way overnight, just remember that. Please, keep on a CT'ing. The worse thing that will happen is you will lose a little imflamation in your body.

Whew.

Clabbergirl, I'm giving you a hug! And I know how you feel too. I'm close to my goal weight but not there and I have my days were I feel hopeless. And yes, hormones have a lot to do with it. So does thinking. This forum can be overwhelming because there is SO MUCH info...like you said - one day it's my thyroid that I think if I fix this, then everything will fall into place. Then it's my adrenals, next my in the toilet estrogen level! LOL I get myself very overwhelmed. And when I am having a day with bad low blood sugar, I get really down. But deep down, I know I'm on the right path and that everything is being undone like a movie in reverse.

I do want to say though if you are taking progesterone and don't need it, you will gain. SO if I were you, I would either stop everything and add one thing at a time and see how my body responds for at least three days before marking it safe. OR I'd cut one thing out at a time and see if I get any benefit. I read this quote today "When we most feel like giving up is when we most need to be keeping on. Please." I really find this true of me...in the past I have given up and maybe I was almost there. Sorry, don't mean to be preachy! Just hoping you feel better!

### Vicster — 2012-03-28T21:16:35+0300

Clabbergirl, A {{{HUG}}} for you! Hang in there, it will get better. Vkiernan has posted some sage advice above, think about implementing some of what she has said. Your gut could very well be an issue and you might want to assess what foods you are eating as you may be having an adverse reaction to something. Candida can and will throw everything off, believe me I know! Stay with the CT, it's cheap and I know for sure it will make things better. Sometimes worse before better, but definitely better.

vkiernan, you are such a help and a joy to have on this forum!

Opted for blood test earlier this month (and posted them previously); don't have the $175 for saliva test now. Already eating very low carb and no grain since Jan.

>
>
> Please, keep on a CT'ing. The worse thing that will happen is you will lose a little imflamation in your body.

This is a good point to consider. I have to weigh the balance between an hour of sleep and an hour of CT bath every single time I do it though, and it's getting to where sleep wins. After reading about spot icing and timing, I'm beginning to think sleep is going to win anyway. I can't take a bath at 6pm!

Thank you for your help in this. I am crabby and hateful and I don't know what to do but I do know I don't want to go on like this. If this is what CT is bringing up and I live through it, I will be a success story, indeed. I just don't know how much of it I can take. Trying to make some big decisions in my life right now too and that's not helping.

### Larry — 2012-03-28T21:34:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7874>

> **KetoWarrior;7820 wrote:** I had a bilateral inguinal hernia repair (open type - not laprascopic) 7 years ago and both surgical sites have started aching and at times tingling since starting CT. I'm hoping it's some sort of tissue remodeling going on. They haven't bothered me at all since the surgery till now and I've not done anything to aggravate them recently.

We are on the same page. Pretty darn fascinating if that is what is actually occurring!

### differentstory — 2012-03-28T22:04:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-7890>

> **vkiernan;7898 wrote:** For starters, I don't think one can expect to know how to dose oneself with bioidentical hormones. If you are estrogen dominant and it sounds like you are, when you use progesterone sometimes your body ups the estrogen until it falls into it's balance. Sometimes not, sometimes it just keeps increasing estrogen. What does estrogen do - it makes you hold on to water and be snappy at people. You have to have a good doc to help you dose progesterone. There is a ratio that has to be met. This sounds like the reason you are holding on to water ( don't believe you are gaining weight although Dr. K says if you gain weight you have hormone issues). Dr. Uzzi Reiss has some really good hormone books out there. The latest and my fav. is The Natural Superwomen. His first book is also good. It is easy to read and explains a lot. It is also cheap on Amazon if you buy it used.
>
>
>
> I would also go to Drlam.com and read up on OAT. OAT = Ovarian, Adrenal and Thyroid axis imbalance. If you have one of these going on (and it sounds like you have more than one going on) the other 2 will be out of balance. Read Dr. K's blog on What Casey Anthony and OJ have in common. It is about the adrenals. More than likely you have an adrenal issue and no amount of thyroid med is going to fix that. You need to fix the adrenals first and then go after the thyroid. Actually, the thyroid should fix itself if you fix the adrenals providing you have secondary hypothyroidism. If you can't go to a good doc I would start with a salivary cortisol test to see where you are at. Post it out here and we will help you interpret it. You cannot fix what you don't know is broke so I believe you have to test, test and test if you really want to be healthy.
>
>
>
> I have been doing the reset since last July. I am not giving up. I found I have issues to work on and that is why I am not having success yet although I do have some small wins even though. This has taken me 3 docs to figure out. Sometimes I have had to wait 3 months to get into them. Talk about frustrating. You eventually will have to make the choice to find a good doc and have the appropriate labs done or just give up like you are on the verge of doing (please don't give up). This is your life and only you can control it and make it happen. Change your mindset to change your DNA. You have to mentally turn things around and look at the picture differently. Yes, it is ok to rant but all it does is raise your cortisol and we all know what that does to us. Perhaps take a step back and approach this differently. Ask yourself, what can I do to help myself that isn't going to hurt myself. I beleive dosing on your own is really setting the stage for disaster and that's where you are at right now.
>
>
>
> You mention ingesting substances - do you even know if you are absorbing those substances? I have been peeing them down the toilet. I recently found out via testing that I am not absorbing my food. I would read some articles on betaine HCL and see if you might need it. This would be a first approach to getting you on the right track. It has made a world of difference for me along with enzymes.
>
>
>
> Also, I can only relate to those things that I have issues with so here me out on one more. I have Candida (hopefully soon not to) but you will not get anywhere with Candida. Candida is an intestional yeast overgrowth sort of like when women get yeast infections but it is in the gut. There will be no losing weight ever or anything positive. If you have eaten a lot of sugar, been on a lot of anit-biotics, had a lot of stress, etc. you probably have Candida. There are a lot of good websites on it.
>
>
>
> I wish you many better days. Back off the progesterone until you can have someone help you figure it out. Read about betaine, then about Candida. Start small with these things and save your supps until you know they are benefitting you. Eat real food for now. You did not get this way overnight, just remember that. Please, keep on a CT'ing. The worse thing that will happen is you will lose a little imflamation in your body.

vkiernan, what a GREAT post!!!!!

> Personally, I think this will go away for you. This is a common PCOS/thyroid thing. I have struggled with it for years. But in the past month of switching to the "Kruse plan", I have noticed that my facial hair is getting a lot better. It is definitely a hormone thing and I think in the long run it will go away.
>
>
>
> Hate to admit it, but I usually have my ENTIRE face waxed once a month (chin, sideburns, upper lip, eyebrows, and even under my chin all the way back to my neck). Yuck! I hate facial hair. But that seems to keep it at bay for 3+ weeks.
>
>
>
> The place I go too is all Asian ladies and you get a private room (thank god). The girl that does it doesn't speak very good English, but she is always saying "So hairy!!!! Look! Look!" and shows me the wax strips.

### vkiernan — 2012-03-29T03:52:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-8055>

> **Clabbergirl;7914 wrote:** This is where I get confused, because if CT is causing fat cells to die and estrogen to be released into my system, I should be even _more_ estrogen dominant, right? Therefore needing more progesterone to counterbalance it? I am shooting in the dark here, but it's what I've been operating under. Thank you for the suggestion on the book. Going to check if my library has a copy I can borrow.Are you using something like DIM or an aromatizer to help get it out of your body? Progesterone can help with the ratio but remove it from your body I don't believe. I have no idea how much to take. I was severely estrogen dominant - probably 3 times over the limit. DIM has greatly reduced that. I was on 120 mg a day of progesterone cream. This never overcame my est. dom. I will be working with my doc on that when she is ready to go down that road. I'm hoping soon cuz I have read so many benefits of prog.
>
>
>
>
> > Have read Dr. Lam's OAT theory and I'd say I have all 3 issues. I now suspect my adrenals were the first to fall about 10 years ago and have been limping along ever since. Read Dr. K's blog on that, fits me, and I'm following about 70% of his suggestions (have not reordered rhodiola or licorice root but taking the others). I'm still on caffeine but honestly I cannot function without it. I know this is a problem but short of sleeping for a month solid, unsure how to fix. Opted for blood test earlier this month (and posted them previously); don't have the $175 for saliva test now. Helpful folks already suggested I am super low on DHEA-S, Progesterone, and Testosterone. Might even be low on estrogen depending on when in my cycle I was. Never did find out how to figure prog/estrogen ratio though. I really want to be healthy, but if testing is the only way, then I just don't have the $ to be healthy.
>
> You and I are sisters strolling down the same damn dark dreary path. Damn I hate the night (sorry Dr. K.) Depending how bad your adrenals are you might need HC or something like Isocort (plant derived cortisol) to start delivering cortisol into your system. This is the hard part cuz without testing you are flying blind and can really hurt yourself. I probably wouldn't start on any cortisol until Doc recommended. Btw, Dr. K. told me that CT baths will definitely help and to not necessarily use HC and he knows where my levels are. I was only doing 30 min. and he said I need to up the time. I am working towards 60 min. Keep in mind I have true adrenal fatigue and have surpassed all the stages in between. I am below the line at all levels and my DHEA is a 1 and the range is 3-10. If your DHEA is that low as is mine too you could consider taking DHEA but start small and progress every week or so paying close attention to oily skin, hair loss, facial hair, etc. Anything men get as they age. This will tell you that you need to back off. Dr. Reiss tells you just how to do it. I am on 25 mg. a day which he says is a lot but it depends on the status of the adrenals and your current DHEA level. I am not taking 7-keto DHEA. For you and me with low Test., we want conversion from reg. DHEA. Perhaps later we could switch over once we raise our T high enough for a female. I am not saying to not supplement that too now but perhaps start with the real thing and see how you fare.
>
>
>
> I'm not sure I am absorbing anything, but I've been taking 3 -4 Super Enzymes tablets (w/200mg betaine HCI each) with at least 2 of the 3 daily meals for 2+ months. I've noticed it seems to curb hunger if I take them as opposed to when I don't

.

Mamagrok taught me so much about betaine. I have also read many websites to really get a good feel for what the word is on it. I take 7 -600 mg. with my BAB and I also take 4 of my enzymes at that time too. I have yet to feel the burn that says you have exceeded the betaine limit. I also take 3-4 more betaine plus 4 enzymes with lunch and also at suppertime. Also, my doc has me taking these same enzymes (Zypan) 4 in the mid morning, 4 in the mid afternoon and 4 after dinner before bed. That's appr. 15 betaine and 24 enzymes everyday. I have not had a bad reaction from all of this yet. Just tells me I am fricken broke (yeh, that's externally and internally) and need help.

> Never Dx with candida, but yes on antibiotics and stress. Finished 1 bottle of Now Foods Candida Clear in January, taking in 1-2 tbsp coconut oil by spoon and always in my cooking daily since October 2011, did a series of grapefruit seed extract over a month ago and felt exhausted like die-off symptoms. Already eating very low carb and no grain since Jan.Ok, sounds good. I wouldn't stop there. I have read where they will look for sugar and bury deeper or adapt to current conditions. I am no expert on this and I am praying I am getting rid of it. Here's what my doc did. She has me on Caprystatin (caplyic acid), 3 in the morning and 3 in the afternoon away from food. I have added to that Candida Clear and was taking 3 with breakfast and 3 with lunch. Then after so much time passed, I added kyolic garlic (recommeded by Glam). I am taking 4 with breakfast and 4 with lunch. Maybe overkill but I'll be damned if this stuff is going to get the better of me. I waited on the Candida Clear for maybe a month or so after I started the Caprystatin. I had die-off with the Caprystatin and with the Candida Clear. Both times I had headaches and nausea for about 2 weeks. My reasoning for waiting to introduce a new treatment is due to what I read about changing the program around to make sure they haven't adjusted to what you are feeding them kinda like cockroaches and pesticides adaptation.
>
>
> > This is a good point to consider. I have to weigh the balance between an hour of sleep and an hour of CT bath every single time I do it though, and it's getting to where sleep wins. After reading about spot icing and timing, I'm beginning to think sleep is going to win anyway. I can't take a bath at 6pm!
>
> What if you just split the .CT'ing and sleep up. Are you really going to miss 30 minutes? I know I wouldn't just cuz my sleep is pretty crap anyway. At least I feel like I am using my time wisely when I am actually accomplishing something. I was doing 30 minutes in the am right after BAB. This might mean you have to get up 1/2 hour earlier though. But, if night time really doesn't work then it is something. Heck, go for 15 and spot at night if you can. Anything is better than nothing. Run your air in your car. Drink a lot of cold beverages. Wear less clothing.
>
>
>
> Thank you for your help in this. I am crabby and hateful and I don't know what to do but I do know I don't want to go on like this. If this is what CT is bringing up and I live through it, I will be a success story, indeed. I just don't know how much of it I can take. Trying to make some big decisions in my life right now too and that's not helping.

Oh honey, trust me you can go on. You are woman! I am crabby and hateful a lot too. Just ask my DH (no, wait, maybe don't ask him) that is the estrogen doing it's thing. Big decisions along with all of this is really a lot. I am not going to tell you to stop any of it though. You are progressing whether you realize it or not. Anything is better than a state of no change. That means you will always get what you always got! Got it!

### Grottenolm — 2012-03-29T04:14:14+0300

Any other chicks out there packing on the muscle since starting CT? My arm muscles are getting bigger and overall my measurements are are all slightly up, but I am looking HEAPS better - less jiggle and more firm. It seems like a body recomp is happening fast, but I thought that it takes years for females to put on any muscle? Me LIKEY....

I have only been doing face dunks and ice packs to the belly so far, for about 1 month now. But the muscle changes are happening everywhere. I do bodyweight stuff about twice a week and Callanetics once a week. I walk a couple of kilometers most days. Minimal activity but big changes. Clabbergirl, could this be happening to you has well?

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-29T17:38:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-8284>

Thanks to everyone for taking the time and energy to talk me back from the edge. Yesterday was a particularly awful day and I'm going to do what I can to make today better. Vkiernan, you weren't too hard on me at all - I have been hard on myself.

I _have_ taken some of your advice and have changed some things, realizing that none of this is scientific, and therefore, keeping my expectations appropriate to that understanding. Same goes for any logic I try to apply.

-   Starting a diary on this forum of supplements, CT exposure, and meals (although that may be more sporadic than the previous). As of this am, I've added in DHEA. Blood test showed low testosterone and I have many symptoms so I figure, what the heck, and will be on the lookout for any male-like signs.
-   Researching progesterone more and taking a break from my 2x day dosing until I understand more about it. I'd like to find a forum of women using BHRT to review, but honestly I am barely keeping up with the reading at this blog, forum and MarksDailyApple. I have found decent info at [Hertoghe's](http://http://www.hertoghe.eu/patients/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=136&Itemid=170&lang=en) site and took the online questionnaires, fwiw.
    Reduction in CT baths while focusing energy on spot-icing. Seems many are seeing a reduction in inches at the sites they frequently spot-ice. Considering my schedule and the possible detox symptoms I've been dealing with, going slower might be better. I don't know, except that something's gotta give.

I wasn't having any physical hormone issues until I started CT, truthfully. But knowledge is power, and can be dangerous. Reading about how people here and at MDA have had miracle mood shifts when supplementing with Progesterone, etc., finding the [body fat map](http://http://healthhabits.files.wordpress.com/2009/08/body-fat-map-female-woman.jpg), it only fueled my interest to know more, which ultimately got me to have blood drawn and start trying to 'fix' myself. The fact that I've been eating this way for 3 months and seen no progress is also a big motivator.

Thanks to everyone for the encouragement. I sorely needed it. I hope I can be as supportive one day.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-03-29T21:33:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-8423>

Hi Cavemam,

You might find the information you are looking for in **Levee 8**. Dr Kruse says: _"ALEs are short for advanced lipoxidation end-products. One clinical measure we use of ALE toxicity in the body is presence of skin tags in folds of the skin." ... "This finding seems to correlate with the omega 6/3 ratio in plasma. Generally we want a diet that approaches a 1 to 1 balance of omega 6 (PUFA) to omega 3 fats but in the standard American diet (SAD) that ratio can be up to 40 to 1 with heavy intake of processed foods."_

After dealing with diet you can remove you own skin tags. An old bee keeper told me you can get rid of moles and skin tags by using propolis topically. I have tried it and it works. Tape a small amount of local propolis onto the skin tag. The tag will come off in about a week. It sometimes takes longer with big skin tags. I have never tried moles.

I just got this book yesterday and it is incredibly useful. Detailed descriptions of various hormones, including symptoms of deficiency, dosing, when to take, side effects, paradoxical effects, all written in understandable language. This is the best book on hormones I have (and I have acquired several recently). I'm learning far more through this than I got from my doctor.

> **Lyndra;5061 wrote:** Ine one of his blog postings, Dr. K mentioned the functionalmedicine.org site as a way to find a functional medicine practitioner. It lists some MDs in Boulder. Apparently the functionalmedicine.org site needs to fix it's search function, I put in 250mile for how far I would go and it gave me Singapore

I think I'm done trying to find a doctor, I'm going to stick to the reset, just keep doing CT, I will get the tests done that I can get done without a doctor's order, work on the leaky gut and find someone who works with bio-identical hormones if the testing shows my hormones are screwed up. I feel alot better since I started the reset and the CT, my body is reshaping itself, I think I just need to give it time.

I'm going to put the scale away, along with the measuring tape, and rely totally on how I feel, do my clothes fit better etc. Because this trying to find a doctor who doesn't think what I'm doing is nuts is getting really old. And it's got me feeling cranky and just the thought of talking to another doctor makes my stomach hurt.

We have an appt with someone about our mold exposure but we're moving out of this place in a couple of months and I'm sure once we're not exposed to the mold anymore we'll be on the mend.

Meanwhile I'm going to enjoy the paleo way of eating and not worry about the weight, it will come off that I'm sure of. Everything I've read on Dr K's blogs proves that and it's time I just let it work it's thing.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-02T19:37:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-10383>

> **Clabbergirl;8330 wrote:** Thanks to everyone for taking the time and energy to talk me back from the edge. Yesterday was a particularly awful day and I'm going to do what I can to make today better. Vkiernan, you weren't too hard on me at all - I have been hard on myself.
>
>
>
> I _have_ taken some of your advice and have changed some things, realizing that none of this is scientific, and therefore, keeping my expectations appropriate to that understanding. Same goes for any logic I try to apply.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> -   Starting a diary on this forum of supplements, CT exposure, and meals (although that may be more sporadic than the previous). As of this am, I've added in DHEA. Blood test showed low testosterone and I have many symptoms so I figure, what the heck, and will be on the lookout for any male-like signs.
> -   Researching progesterone more and taking a break from my 2x day dosing until I understand more about it. I'd like to find a forum of women using BHRT to review, but honestly I am barely keeping up with the reading at this blog, forum and MarksDailyApple. I have found decent info at [Hertoghe's](http://http://www.hertoghe.eu/patients/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=136&Itemid=170&lang=en) site and took the online questionnaires, fwiw.
>     Reduction in CT baths while focusing energy on spot-icing. Seems many are seeing a reduction in inches at the sites they frequently spot-ice. Considering my schedule and the possible detox symptoms I've been dealing with, going slower might be better. I don't know, except that something's gotta give.
>
>
>
> I wasn't having any physical hormone issues until I started CT, truthfully. But knowledge is power, and can be dangerous. Reading about how people here and at MDA have had miracle mood shifts when supplementing with Progesterone, etc., finding the [body fat map](http://http://healthhabits.files.wordpress.com/2009/08/body-fat-map-female-woman.jpg), it only fueled my interest to know more, which ultimately got me to have blood drawn and start trying to 'fix' myself. The fact that I've been eating this way for 3 months and seen no progress is also a big motivator.
>
>
>
> Thanks to everyone for the encouragement. I sorely needed it. I hope I can be as supportive one day.

I am 65yo and had a total hyst about 11-12 years ago. I was having MAJOR hormonal issues for the 20 years prior to that ... how do I know this? Because when they yanked my parts the doc told me I had a KILO'S worth of fibroid tumors. Two fist sized ones and dozens of thumb sized ones both inside the uterine wall and in the uterus itself.

After the surgery I spent an entire years LITERALLY only sleeping 2 hours a night. I had had major sleep issues for 10-15 years preceding the hyst but this was beyond anything I'd ever experienced. I am not exaggerating about the 2 hours/night.

Call it what you like, but I think the universe, or someone, sent someone to me to get me to read Dr. John R. Lee's book "What your doctor may not tell you about menopause." I didn't hear her the first time she told me (this was someone I "met" on the internet) so she told me again. I heard her, read the book and that was the last I heard FROM her. Weird, eh?

that book started me towards sanity. I began using OTC progesterone cream daily and fixed a number of issues, not the least of which was my sleep. Then I quit using it. AGAIN the universe or whomever sent someone to me to kick me in the butt. I began using the cream again and didn't quit until last year when, through a series of strange circumstances I would up reading TS Wiley's "Sex, lies and menopause."

It was like the light FINALLY dawned. I went to her website ([www.thewileyprotocol.com](http://http://www.thewileyprotocol.com)) and read more. I asked my doc about BHRT and although he is an enthusiastic supporter of it wouldn't prescribe because he didn't feel qualified. So I took matters in hand and found a certified Wiley provider in Colorado. I drove SIX HOURS to make the appointment (we have relatives there so that was a help) and he wrote me a prescription on the spot. That was last July.

When I tell you they can have my BHRT over my cold dead body I am not kidding. The brain fog is GONE. I"m sleeping better and better (some of that is thanks to Jack's Rx as well).

So what is the Wiley protocol? You supplement your hormones so you are at the levels you had in your TWENTIES. It's a lot.  You cycle just like your body did and in harmony with the phases of the moon. Progesterone is ONLY used two weeks out of the month just like when your body produced it.

It is WONderful. Do yourself a huge favor and read her book. Then, if you're so moved find a provider and go talk to them.

As for my local doc?  He is studying up and will soon begin dispensing BHRT. I just need to get him over onto the Wiley protocol because natural rhythms of hormonal secretion/application is the ONLY way to fly.

### Tizawee — 2012-04-03T04:45:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-10664>

I've been getting cramps in my toes. Immediately after face-dunking I feel twinges on the bottoms of my feet. Later I get woken up out a dead sleep by mutinous toes that act as if they're each trying to go their own way... incredibly painfully. The cramps eventually move to the ball then arch of my foot.

This happened on days 3 & 4 of CT. I decided to stop until I could figure out what would cause toe cramps like that. I had them a few years ago & could always "cure" them by taking a multi-vitamin & drinking a liter of water. These days I take vitamins daily & am plenty hydrated (drink a cold liter of water right before CT).

Started face-dunking again tonight after 3 days off. No cramps yet. I now have tonic water (for the quinine). My aunt tells me it's an old home remedy that works for her.

Anyone have any ideas?

Elin

### LinD — 2012-04-03T05:29:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-10671>

Elin.

Maybe you need more magnesium and/or potassium.

[http://jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-two/](http://http://jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-two/)

Ok, might be TMI but here goes. What is the chance that CT can heal a scarred uterus from thermal ablation. I have never had a bad TOM since I had it done 10 or so years ago. ### nonchalant — 2012-04-05T00:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-ive-not-seen-on-here-before-post-unusual-ct-symptoms-benefits.335/post-11514>

A couple weeks ago, my second toe on my right foot started hurting. Tender. Eventually, I had to be careful how I stepped with the foot. I couldn't figure it out, because I hadn't stubbed it or anything. Then I remembered that when I was a pre-teen, I broke that toe doing a crazy gymnastics stunt in the living room.

Not sure why it started hurting, but now it seems to have calmed down, finally. Strange.

I have tried it and it works. Tape a small amount of local propolis onto the skin tag. The tag will come off in about a week. It sometimes takes longer with big skin tags. I have never tried moles.

Interesting, will try that, I just went to the dermatologist to have a bunch removed, would rather do it myself at home without the pain  btw I lived in northern BC for years, beautiful area, have a sister in kelowna.

### agatha — 2012-04-05T13:51:21+0300

The European nature cure people talk about this. They call it retracing - as you detox your body revisits and sorts out remnants of old illnesses/injuries. They say this happens from the top of the body down, from the inside out (ie internal organs first and then skin) and in reverse order.

Sure hoping it is finishing the healing and will go away long term.

# Thread: Phoenix Suns basketball team uses Cryosauna...Newspaper Article
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix-suns-basketball-team-uses-cryosauna-newspaper-article.336/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2012-03-21T01:56:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix-suns-basketball-team-uses-cryosauna-newspaper-article.336/post-3639>

Several of my softball playing friends who know I freeze my butt off every evening ran up to me today at the field and told me about this article in the local Phoenix paper.

They use the Cryo for healing of the body of the wear and tear of professional basketball games.

Just think in two years we all can be playing at the NBA level if you will CT with gusto every day! LOL

[http://www.azcentral.com/sports/suns/articles/2012/03/16/20120316phoenix-suns-love-big-chill.html](http://http://www.azcentral.com/sports/suns/articles/2012/03/16/20120316phoenix-suns-love-big-chill.html)

### AKMan — 2012-03-21T02:10:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix-suns-basketball-team-uses-cryosauna-newspaper-article.336/post-3658>

Good catch, makes us realize we aren't (totally) crazy! These guys are doing 3 minutes at -257 degrees! This blast-freezes the very outermost layer of skin, activating the cold receptors, resulting in blood flow changes to the skin and extremities. when they warm back up, the blood flow returns quickly. I'd be willing to bet they don't shiver during or after this treatment.

Interestingly the article says they have been doing 15 minute, 50 degree baths for years and the people find the 3 minute treatment much more bearable.

Great article, thanks!

### Mikey7 — 2012-03-21T02:12:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix-suns-basketball-team-uses-cryosauna-newspaper-article.336/post-3662>

So that is how Grant Hill is still playing and Steve Nash is still a elite pg. Nice

### copychick — 2012-03-21T02:34:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix-suns-basketball-team-uses-cryosauna-newspaper-article.336/post-3678>

I just posted a separate thread on cryogenic treatments. There is a place in town that sells treatments in a cryosauna and since I don't have a tub and honestly can't imagine sitting in a cold bath for 30-50 minutes, I was wondering if a series of these treatments would be an effective alternative.

### hazyjane — 2012-03-21T02:41:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix-suns-basketball-team-uses-cryosauna-newspaper-article.336/post-3685>

Check out this article:

"_After the three sessions, my skin has a rosy glow and I feel noticeably energised. I also sleep deeply for the first time in years, and find myself doing strange things, like rising at 7.30am for water gymnastics and forging ahead on a morning of Alpine walking. I also feel permanently warm, even up mountains, like the Ready Brek kid. It’s as if my own inner central heating system has been kicked into action.

_"

[http://www.healthycold.com/eng~big\_chill](http://http://www.healthycold.com/eng~big_chill)

### Phoenix — 2012-03-21T02:56:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix-suns-basketball-team-uses-cryosauna-newspaper-article.336/post-3701>

I'm still not completely convinced that CT will really work the way Dr. JK says it will. I believe it helped him to achieve his fitness goals, but I've been so disappointed with other "fad" treatments for my issues, that I have very low expectations for CT. But I'm giving it a shot anyway because it couldn't hurt.

That being said, if it DOES work for the majority of the people on this site, I can see these cryogenic machines taking off, coming down in price, and being sold for home use. Can you imagine kicking your treadmill or elliptical to the curb and installing a cryogenic chamber in your home? They'd only have to drop about $45,500 in price for me to think about it. LOL!

I also noticed in the article that "The Suns have missed the fewest games to injury (four) of any NBA team." Hmmm ... I wonder if there's a correlation there?

(Keeping my fingers crossed that CT is for real)

# Thread: Turning up the heat, starting tonight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-up-the-heat-starting-tonight.337/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Phoenix — 2012-03-21T03:06:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-up-the-heat-starting-tonight.337/post-3705>

I've been pushing the CT for the past two weeks. Each night soaking longer and colder. The last time it was so cold and I was so uncomfortable and I slept so poorly afterward, that today I convinced myself to change my strategy.

I remember Dr. Kruse telling someone on his blog that a higher temperature (60-65 degrees) is not a waste of time. It just means that becoming optimal will take more time. And I've got the time.

So starting tonight I'm upping my water temperature about 5-10 degrees before I take the plunge. I've decided to be patient, and go easier for awhile. I know I'll get back to 55 degree baths eventually, but for now I'm going with 60-65 degrees. Once I become comfortably acclimated to that I'll inch my way back down to 55.

Again, I'm in no hurry to do this. As long as I know I'm moving in the right direction I'm OK with it.

### Birdy — 2012-03-21T03:15:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-up-the-heat-starting-tonight.337/post-3712>

>
>
> I remember Dr. Kruse telling someone on his blog that a higher temperature (60-65 degrees) is not a waste of time. It just means that becoming optimal will take more time. And I've got the time.
>
>

> So starting tonight I'm upping my water temperature about 5-10 degrees before I take the plunge. I've decided to be patient, and go easier for awhile. I know I'll get back to 55 degree baths eventually, but for now I'm going with 60-65 degrees. Once I become comfortably acclimated to that I'll inch my way back down to 55.
>
>
>
> Again, I'm in no hurry to do this. As long as I know I'm moving in the right direction I'm OK with it.
>
>
>

I am taking it slow, too. We'll get there.

### L8F — 2012-03-21T03:21:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-up-the-heat-starting-tonight.337/post-3718>

I did this too, it helped me stay in the bath, and got rid of some frank dread I was feeling. Once you are in the bath, you can always add ice if you want. Best not to burn out.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T03:39:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-up-the-heat-starting-tonight.337/post-3731>

I agree, you've got to have time to adapt at your pace. If it's sheer dread I think you SHOULD step back. On the other hand, perhaps your just detoxing all kinds of cranky, depressed, futile feeling hormones - and you need to back off while you work through them. In a few days you could be through, feeling better, and ready to go cold!

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-21T17:42:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-up-the-heat-starting-tonight.337/post-3910>

I'm totally with you all! I shiver for hours afterward @ 60, amazing that I'm cold but not shivering while CTing though. I'm enjoying the process and just the past two days have noticed changes... YIPEE! Little over one week into submersed CT @ 60-65 degrees and the last two days I've lost 2.5 lbs.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-21T19:28:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-up-the-heat-starting-tonight.337/post-3949>

Sounds like a good plan to me. I have backed off a bit myself - no more immersing my arms in the water. I would just shake for an hour after the bath. I was dreading putting my arms in which made me dread the bath entirely. I tend to try to rush through things to "win" and I've decided this time I'll take my time and get there whenever my body is ready. I get a lot of encouragement reading what others are experiencing and knowing that I'm not doing this alone.

### Phoenix — 2012-03-21T21:54:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-up-the-heat-starting-tonight.337/post-4023>

> **Lyndra;3974 wrote:** Sounds like a good plan to me. I have backed off a bit myself - no more immersing my arms in the water. I would just shake for an hour after the bath. I was dreading putting my arms in which made me dread the bath entirely. I tend to try to rush through things to "win" and I've decided this time I'll take my time and get there whenever my body is ready. I get a lot of encouragement reading what others are experiencing and knowing that I'm not doing this alone.

I'm very competitive too. So I kind of felt like a loser backing off. But it really worked well for me. I slept well last night, for the first time since I started CT. I'm actually looking forward to tonight's dunk. And I'll eventually get back to 55 degrees, but will definitely take my time doing it.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Anyone else get hives (cold urticaria) with CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Claudia — 2012-03-21T04:44:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3768>

I had a hives a week or so ago and they were pretty bad, only on the areas where I used ice while in the cold bath (on my abdomen, chest, and neck and chin area). I stopped CTing and waited until the hives were gone about 5 days. I started over with face dunks and showers and then, baths again and I am up to 25 minutes in a cold bath, with water about 53 degrees, and able to tolerate it ok (not easy but ok). Scared to use ice again while in there so no ice. But I did buy some of those bubble wrap ice packs thingies and used one very briefly on my tummy yesterday just to try it out, I had 2 shirts between me and the pack. My skin seemed fine yesterday (and I did not do my cold bath yesterday) until today where I noticed it was very sore and thought it had to do with some core exercises I had done a few days ago. Then, I looked, and it looks like the hives are back on my tummy, very red and sore. Very weird stuff. From only about 5 minutes on my tummy

Back to just cold baths for me. Curious if I'm the only one who has had this in this forum. I've been paleo for 2 years, before that I had a pretty decent diet for about 7 years (WAP-style).

### dinglebutt — 2012-03-21T06:18:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3794>

According to Kruse: [http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)

" If you develop **[cold urticaria](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cold_urticaria)** at this time, this is a sign you have high levels of tissue and serum omega six content. Stop the experiment and adjust your diet until you have a blood omega six to three ratio that is below 10 to 1 using a ketogenic paleolithic diet. "

### JC2K — 2012-03-21T07:05:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3800>

No problems here as of yet.

### Claudia — 2012-03-21T15:05:20+0200

Stop the experiment and adjust your diet until you have a blood omega six to three ratio that is below 10 to 1 using a ketogenic paleolithic diet. "

Thanks, I read that and I will try to get it tested if I doctor will go for it, but I've been paleo/ketogenic for 2 years...very strict low carb all of the time, no sugar. I have been using krill oil way before it became popular, for years and years, and fish oil and cod liver oil. I eat salmon every week, also for years. It just seems very unlikely that this could be the problem. I don't eat anything high in omega 6. I haven't touched processed foods in years, don't use vegetable oils (only coconut oil, butter). I know something is probably off with me. I could have some omega 6 from previous years stored in the fat I need to lose, but I only need to lose 8 lbs. or so. I've added even more fish and extra krill oil in the last couple of weeks.

### Claudia — 2012-03-21T15:15:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3858>

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-21T15:21:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3859>

I had hives after trying my first cold shower. That was about 5 days ago. Since then I've been doing cold baths ( 52 or 53 degrees). I haven't had the hives again. Do you have a history of getting hives? I had horrible allergies for years where I would get hives often, and I would get them very often in cold weather. (at its worst, I couldn't stay outside in the cold, even while bundled, even for a couple min.) I always thought the cold weather hives were related to the allergies I had. I'm so thankful that I'm able to do the cold baths, I could have never imagined doing them just a couple years ago!

The hives I had after the cold shower were gone in the same day (not sure exactly how long it took, but they were gone pretty fast). But I wonder if that's because my body has so much 'experience' with hives in the past!

I'm glad that you have found something that seems to work for you .... Maybe you just need to take it slow?

### Claudia — 2012-03-21T15:34:16+0200

(at its worst, I couldn't stay outside in the cold, even while bundled, even for a couple min.) I always thought the cold weather hives were related to the allergies I had. I'm so thankful that I'm able to do the cold baths, I could have never imagined doing them just a couple years ago!
>
> The hives I had after the cold shower were gone in the same day (not sure exactly how long it took, but they were gone pretty fast). But I wonder if that's because my body has so much 'experience' with hives in the past!
>

Thanks. I'm sorry you have had that problem. No history of hives, never had them my whole life. So weird to have them now. I have it bad on my tummy again, sore this morning still, and that's from just few minutes with a gel back on my tummy and clothes in between, not a compression shirt either. Are you adding ice to get the water to that temp? Does your skin get to that temp also? The cold baths seem to be ok for me, but the tap warming is starting to get warmer here in NY. It was around 52 and now it's closer to 55. I didn't take the bath last night because of the hives on my tummy again. This is such a bummer. I guess I do have to take it slow, which is fine, but will I still cold adapt without ice?

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-21T16:42:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3893>

> **Claudia;3890 wrote:** Thanks. I'm sorry you have had that problem. No history of hives, never had them my whole life. So weird to have them now. I have it bad on my tummy again, sore this morning still, and that's from just few minutes with a gel back on my tummy and clothes in between, not a compression shirt either. Are you adding ice to get the water to that temp? Does your skin get to that temp also? The cold baths seem to be ok for me, but the tap warming is starting to get warmer here in NY. It was around 52 and now it's closer to 55. I didn't take the bath last night because of the hives on my tummy again. This is such a bummer. I guess I do have to take it slow, which is fine, but will I still cold adapt without ice?

I just started to add ice packs (just the freezer packs that I put in lunches) to keep the bath water a bit colder. Out of the tap it is about 52 degrees and with the ice packs after 30 minutes it is 53 degrees. I'm going to stock up on some more ice packs to progressively cool the water. It seems cheap and easier than doing ice.

I haven't been able to check my skin temp's, since I use my digital meat themometer to test the bath water and it wont work on skin (I tried  )

Dr. Kruse said that cold adaptation is 'cumulative'....so any time you expose yourself to any kind of cold, you are doing a good thing! Even if it feels like 'something small', you are headed in the right direction. I try to look at it like any little bit helps. Try to look at the big picture, of where you are headed, and try not to fret about how quick (or slow) you will get there. Just do what you can each day, and try to keep perspective. I think one thing that really helped me in CT is that I already had the signs of LS. Are you Leptin Sensitive now, or have signs of it? Dr. Kruse says that the more Leptin Sensitive you are, the easier you'll cold adapt. I started with the Leptin Reset at the end of December, had the signs of LS in early February, and just last week started CT. I have had set-backs since starting the Reset (discovering a pork sensitivity, which caused me to feel like garbage for weeks before I realized it.....and then gaining 5 lbs and I'm still trying to figure out exactly what that was....and now I'm trying to heal my gut too....still am up 3 lbs, hoping to lose that and another couple lbs) but I try to think of the big picture: I have embraced this as a lifestyle, and I really have no plans of going back to what Conventional Wisdom says is 'healthy'. So I had to tell myself that if look me longer than I hoped, that in the long run it's ok! Since every day that we are making the truly healthy choices we are doing a great thing! And I'm thankful to have started it when I did, as opposed to waiting another 5 years.

You are doing a good job. Try not to be stressed or discouraged. You are headed in the right direction, just take it slow and remind yourself that every little bit helps!

### Marsha — 2012-03-27T00:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-6638>

Just a warning so you don't make the same mistake I did!!!

While watching Frozen Planet last night, I decided to ice my belly with a gel pad and compression belt. Really enjoyed the show and when it was over, removed my 'getup' - belly was very numb - then noticed some stinging high up on my belly. The gel pad had pressed directly against my skin, while I thought I was protected by the tights I had pulled up high - they must have inched down during the show and because I was numb in that area, I didn't realize it.

[](<http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/6674=52-Ice burn .jpg>)

It burns like crazy  even today - even after applying CO and an aloe leaf!

I'll certainly _be more careful from now on_!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T17:45:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-3911>

Hi all, I'll be starting HCG soonish, probably next month giving shipping times, etc. So I'd love suggestions for a paleo-style P2. I've already decided to nix the "toast" and fruit, and make up the calories with more protein. Any menu recommendations or other ideas are more than welcome!

Oh also, I'm tentatively planning on breakfast w/in 30 mins. of waking, then dinner probably around 5pm, so incorporating a bit of the Leptin Rx along with it.

Thanks!

\-- Jennifer

### colleencoble — 2012-03-21T19:07:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-3945>

There are several on the big forum doing that, jeepifer. I'd try to load as much of the protein for breakfast as you can so you can go through until dinner. Eager to hear how you do!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T19:17:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-3947>

Thanks, I found a P3 group but no one seems to be in P2. Although I'm sure I'll end up coinciding with some people eventually.

Oh, does the tomato count as a fruit? I know in P&I, it's listed as a veggie. I was thinking ground beef and some tomato (aka P2 "chili") but now I'm second guessing. I don't have any allergen issues w/ eggs or nightshades as far as I know.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-21T19:52:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-3959>

It counts as a veggie.  Check this link, Jeepifer. There are several threads relating to it: [http://hcgdietinfo.com/hcgdietforums/f42/](http://http://hcgdietinfo.com/hcgdietforums/f42/)

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-30T17:04:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-8775>

> **jeepifer;3936 wrote:** Hi all, I'll be starting HCG soonish, probably next month giving shipping times, etc. So I'd love suggestions for a paleo-style P2. I've already decided to nix the "toast" and fruit, and make up the calories with more protein. Any menu recommendations or other ideas are more than welcome!
>
>
>
> Oh also, I'm tentatively planning on breakfast w/in 30 mins. of waking, then dinner probably around 5pm, so incorporating a bit of the Leptin Rx along with it.
>
>
>
> Thanks!
>
> \-- Jennifer

im sorry i just noticed your post. but i tried this paleo/hcg and thought it didnt work. at least not for weight loss.. i had been doing the reset since nov 10. the end of jan i started an hcg round. eating mostly protein/some veggies. had no trouble with appetite. losses were painfully slow. but then they had been painfully slow the 3 rounds i tried last year that i did in a more conventional style. this was even slower. and by day 12 i had terrible protein cravings. i never had that before on hcg. but then my body may have been used to a lot more protein in recent months. i was down load weight and 5 more lbs. and i was due for dental surgery. so i thought best to eat all the protein i wanted. had the surgery. overate for a total of 8 days. it was a snap. gained some weight and rather than going back to hcg. i just called it quits as of day 12. turned to JUDDD to take off the weight. did it the first week. and of course i only had to diet every other day. so i am now sitting at almost 2 lbs below LIW. you know all that stuff we tell people on the hcg boards? that if you stop before 20 days you regain the weight and more? not so. i think it depends on what you do after. so i am now doing paleo/reset style eating on JUDDD and loving it. i think my hcg days are over.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-31T04:53:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-9090>

People who lose weight slowly on hcg tend to keep it off, thomasina. That probably helped you. There are always exceptions but it's very rare to keep the weight you've lost off if you stop before 23 days. Glad you did though. What is JUDD?

### Xena — 2012-03-31T05:22:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-9096>

I loaded protein in the a.m., sometimes with an allotted veggie, sometimes not. I lost 29.4 lbs in 30 days and ate grass-fed 90-10 hamburger nearly every day. I was thrilled at my losses. Didn't have dinner much because I ate all my food earlier but it worked great. I also took all of Dr. K's recommended supplements for HCG users. I am gearing up for another round in a few weeks and have been doing the reset since I came off HCG Feb. 9 (stabilized like a dream). I hope the next round works well but my losses will likely slow as I near where my body wants to be. I usually do about 30 day rounds--less is not that appealing and more makes me bored and likely to cheat.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-31T05:24:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-9097>

That's fabulous, Xena! What did you lose on the previous round?

### Dextery — 2012-03-21T20:07:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-doing-chronic-exercise-that-will-cause-you-fail.344/post-3968>

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2PdJFbjWHEU\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;2PdJFbjWHEU\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2PdJFbjWHEU\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;2PdJFbjWHEU)

Dr Doug McGuff, an ER doctor and owner of a gym in South Carolina talks about the reasons why people fail in their exercise and body composition goals. He also wrote the book _Body by Science_

He also talks about how one can do less work in the gym and make huge gains by resting 7 days between workouts.

For the med students and doctors on this board, there is a good section of this presentation regarding medical school biochemistry.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-doing-chronic-exercise-that-will-cause-you-fail.344/post-4523>

### Dextery — 2012-03-22T23:23:06+0200

The video was taped a couple years ago and CT was not being discussed. But Dr McGuff commented that he now has his gym at 60 degrees F and Dr Kruse replied to him that he could go even lower and increase the benefits.

If you use the markers that Dr McGuff uses as well as Drew Baye uses, [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8nIZ4Yih\_88&feature=relmfu\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;8nIZ4Yih\_88\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8nIZ4Yih\_88&feature=relmfu\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;8nIZ4Yih_88)

you can test yourself to see if you can decrease the time between workouts. There is a sweet spot for everyone...but the take away is

do not overtrain...as that results in frustration in not making any headway....the muscles must be allowed to recalibrate to handle higher and higher weight stress in the epigenetic pathway.

Many people are so locked into more lifting/exercising is better conventional wisdom that they set themselves up for failure.

As a side note, you are in Des Moines where Dr Terry Wahls does her work. She has licked her mutiple sclorosis and gives talks around your area. Here is her first on mitochondria [http://www.bing.com/search?q=dr+terry+wahls&form=HPNTDF&pc=HPNTDF&src=IE-SearchBox](http://http://www.bing.com/search?q=dr+terry+wahls&form=HPNTDF&pc=HPNTDF&src=IE-SearchBox) Minding your mitochondria.

# Thread: If I were to invest in one test ...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/>

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-21T20:44:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/post-3989>

What would you recommend?

DHEA? rT3? HS-CRP?

I can't afford a full panel of tests at this time, so what might you suggest will provide me with the most information for the least amount of $.

In case some background info is helpful: I'm on the reset and doing CT. I'm about 10-15 pounds of fat from ideal body comp. After 2 weeks on both protocols, I am seeing signs of LS, like improved energy, mood, libido, hair, skin, and body comp. Prior to starting the reset, I was LC Primal for nearly 2 years, and lost nearly 60 pounds that way, until hitting a major plateau this fall/winter.

Thanks!

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-21T20:46:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/post-3990>

My vote would be HS-CRP. It's non-specific, but predictive. It's a good sign-post for multiple issues. Get it when you feel well. It will go up with a cold, etc.

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-21T20:52:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/post-3993>

save your $$ if one test is all you can afford

follow the Leptin RX and merry it with any others you might need (leaky gut rx etc.) do CT

if you want to invest the money get a few good supplements... b vitamin complex, multi, d vitamin ... from the top ten supplement list

Or even better, get some high quality food grass-fed meat, wild fish...

### Garfield — 2012-03-21T21:07:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/post-4002>

Depends how much money you have available for your 1 test.

Personally I would recommend getting the March monthly special tests from [Direct Labs](http://https://www.directlabs.com/MonthlySpecials/tabid/2492/language/en-US/Default.aspx). For about $140 you get a gender specific health check and D3. That is a whole raft of tests and includes the hs-crp as well as a number of other good things. I just wish it had the reverse T3 and ferritin as well. (Adding those would cost about $110 from [I Need Labs](http://http://www.ineedlabs.com/) with their 15% discount.)

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-21T21:56:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/post-4025>

>
>
>
> follow the Leptin RX and merry it with any others you might need (leaky gut rx etc.) do CT
>
>
>
> if you want to invest the money get a few good supplements... b vitamin complex, multi, d vitamin ... from the top ten supplement list
>
>
>
> Or even better, get some high quality food grass-fed meat, wild fish...

THis is a good point. If you're really strapped, just spend money on the the very best food you can afford. Cold showers are pretty cheap, too, thankfully. One test isn't really going to give you a clear picture. I don't think you can go to far wrong with good food, good sleep, low stress, and some cold water.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-21T22:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/post-4037>

Thanks, everyone!

I'm already eating a high quality diet and taking most of the supplements that were recommended.

I might go for the direct labs special (Thanks for the link, Garfield!). That would give me HS-CRP, CWP, Urinalysis, Estradiol for $99. I already had my d levels checked last year (37 ng/mL), so I'm working on getting that up.

# Thread: Competition! Need Measurable Goals!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/competition-need-measurable-goals.347/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T20:58:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/competition-need-measurable-goals.347/post-3997>

Here's the deal. My husband joined me in LRx and CT a few weeks ago. The bum only has about 20ish pounds to lose and he's already lost at least 5 of those (he hasn't weighed in about a week). Me, I've lost 0. No surprise.

Anyhow, at his place of employment they're having a "Healthy Living Contest" Below are the rules:

We are encouraging all of our employees to team up and win with Healthy Living Teams. Each team of two to four employees will choose the measurable goal or goals that they wish to achieve by December of 2012. After a deliberation by outside professionals at the end of the year, the winning team will get a prize of their choice valued at up to $1000.

Criteria

Teams must register by Wednesday, March 28.

Teams must consist of two to four employees.

When registering, teams must specify what category their goal or goals fall into. All goals must have documented measurables at the close of competition which is on Friday, December 7, 2012.

Teams can determine what their prize will be, which cannot exceed a $1000 value total. Prizes will be reviewed by the exec team for feasibility.

Team results will be confidentially reviewed and judged by outside professionals.

There are a few more details, but that is the gist of it. The categories are medical, physical, and spiritual. He is currently trying to convince at least one more person who will join his team. He's had several people laugh at him and tell him he is crazy for doing CT. But our big question is... _**what should be the measurable goals??? Ideas???**_

Each team of two to four employees will choose the measurable goal or goals that they wish to achieve by December of 2012. After a deliberation by outside professionals at the end of the year, the winning team will get a prize of their choice valued at up to $1000.
>
>
>
> Criteria
>
> Teams must register by Wednesday, March 28.
>
> Teams must consist of two to four employees.
>
> When registering, teams must specify what category their goal or goals fall into. All goals must have documented measurables at the close of competition which is on Friday, December 7, 2012.
>
> Teams can determine what their prize will be, which cannot exceed a $1000 value total. Prizes will be reviewed by the exec team for feasibility.
>

# Thread: CT + keto -- concerns
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 117
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### anonymouse — 2012-03-21T21:17:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4010>

I've been digging through this website and others for about a week now to try and figure out why Dr. K recommends the ketogenic diet. I understand the concept -- all starch/sugar causes some level of damage and it's best to avoid them. But I'm hung up over the side effects that long-term ketosis can have....I posted this here because I wonder if CT is what fixes keto's problems, though I can't sift through the articles here :/

I've heard stories of long-term keto adherents ending up with very high fasting blood glucose levels, something about the body conserving glucose...does this cause harm? Also, temporary insulin resistance from long-term keto?

Also been reading about poor T4 -> T3 conversion leading to low metabolism on keto -- while this may not be a problem, it _does_ lead to crazy cholesterol numbers, apparently.

I need someone more knowledgeable than me to look at this and help alleviate my fears... I'm 2 weeks into CT and 3 days into

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T21:37:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4014>

Long story short with CT, keto is needed for the "ancient pathway" to work. SO if you are not ketogenic, the pathway has a roadblock.

This is a therapy to reverse things wrong. Once "fixed" you can then choose to live by natural cycles such as more carbs in summer, more keto in winter.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T21:49:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4016>

> **Cavemam;4040 wrote:** This is a therapy to reverse things wrong. Once "fixed" you can then choose to live by natural cycles such as more carbs in summer, more keto in winter.

And Dr. K thinks you should check telomere length ($290 blood test) to know how quickly your cells have aged relative to your chronological age. CT while keto will grow you telomeres, which he think almost everyone needs. Check for yourself...

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-21T21:57:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4026>

It's not long-term. It's temporary. It may be a few months for some, 2 years for others, but I've been VLC for close to 6 months and fasting blood glucose has only gone down. My LDL has gone up, but as you mentioned, it's a symptom of a thyroid problem that was already there. Thyroid stuff is getting better with the addition of dietary thyroid, and I expect it will continue to improve (and reverse the LDL trend) as my adrenals turn around.

BTW, my HDL has gone up, triglycerides have gone down, and percentage of LDL that is large & fluffy has increased. My cholesterol situation is undoubtedly improving, even if the LDL number is temporarily on the rise.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-21T22:00:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4027>

Keto is the only way to do CT because there were/are no carbs growing in cold climates or during winter. So, if you eat carbs and do CT, you are causing a mismatch, leading to inflammation. You confuse the body that thinks it is winter by feeding it food that is only available in the summer.

Keto and CT work in harmony, since our bodies expect and know how to respond to a high fat, moderate protein diet in winter/cold environments. For the biochemistry, you'll have to dig into the CT series directly on the blog. I'm a "non-geek," so I can't help with that.

### anonymouse — 2012-03-21T22:41:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4050>

Thanks for the replies...

MamaGrok, it's reassuring that your cholesterol is doing good. I'm going to keep reading into this, particularly on the whole thyroid thing, but I'll keep at it.

### hellojtm — 2012-03-22T03:17:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4200>

He covered this today pretty well in CT7

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-22T15:20:54+0200
