I don\`t see keto effects unless I am 75% and up, greatest effects are seen at 80 and up

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-22T15:35:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4358>

I have to get down to pretty much just meat, fat, & greens (all I want of them), and then the ketostix show purple. Just keep in mind that if they show purple, then stop, that doesn't necessarily mean you've gone out of ketosis, if diet is the same. It just could mean you're using all the ketones you're producing, instead of wasting them in your urine.

### primat — 2012-03-22T16:45:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4422>

I have to stay below 15-20 to get some pink on the stick. But it's unlikely you're not making ketones with such low carb intake - you just don't make enough to make it spill out into the urine. I guess that's what an efficient fat burner would do; make as little ketones as possible while utilizing free fatty acids directly.

Can someone advise me on a good ketone test product?

### Snowyky — 2012-03-22T23:04:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4647>

Not sure where to stick this but barely above trace on a ketostix has me wondering what the heck. I'm feeling pretty down about all this, why can't I lose weight? I'm over 300 lbs, I did lose when I first started but nothing since. 2 1/2 months on the leptin reset and over a month doing CT with ice packs, face dunks and cold baths and am now having ice baths for 60mins with the temp being 50\* - 55\* everyday now for 2 weeks. I take all the supplements Dr K mentions, I've been using the yellow glasses at night and am usually in bed by 9:30 with lights out by 10, I still wake up quite a bit at night and am getting hungrier by the day  I did better it seemed when I first started, I thought it was because I was going so very very low carb with no veggies, just fat and meat, so I added back a salad for lunch, add mushrooms to my BAB omelet. I've tried cutting out CO and it didn't make any difference. I notice fat disappearing in some areas ie: weird spots in my arms and wrists, husband says my legs are getting smaller but when I check my other measurements, they haven't moved and neither is the scale. When I'm in the tub I don't shiver and I'm warming up quicker once I get out and I'm starving when I get out  and can't eat because I do the bath after my last meal at night. I drink water which is probably what's making me get up during the night.

I've been looking at what labs I should get done as I read that Dr K told someone with the same kind of problems that something else is wrong if CT isn't stopping the hunger, but I'm lost when it comes to what labs, I have gone through menopause, but don't have any of the typical symptoms ie: hot flashes and dryness below, if you know what I mean. I read somewhere that Dr K suggested 3 labs if we can't afford all, and they were Alpha MSH, Vit D and HS CRP. Since I don't have much money left to spend as I've been spending well over $200 a month on supplements, does anyone have any suggestions, I had the typical labs done at my doctor's but she wouldn't order the rest as she didnt' think I needed them, and they included all the hormone ones which I thought was kind of strange, but she says I have no symptoms so why worry. Here are my labs for what I did get done,

Tryglycerides 106 mg/dl Range or =46

LDL Cholesteral 109 Rangeg weight, I've tried lowering the calories for lunch and dinner to less then 500, but no difference except more hungry. The BAB is usually around 51gm of protein, fitday says a typical day is Fat 75%, Protein 23%, Carbs 2%. avg 1100 to 1900 calories depending what I have. Exercise, I try to walk every day or use my gravity glider or tubes, my job is very sedentary so I try to move around at least once an hour via the gravity glider or walk outside for a bit.

If anyone has any ideas for labs or other tips that would get me on the weight loss road I would really appreciate it, I will stick to the reset and CT because i feel better when I'm doing it, my blood sugar is lower via CT so that's a definite plus. Oh yes the ketostix show very little even though I eat very little carbs, just slightly above a trace. Help, ~Eleanor

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-22T23:28:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4655>

Have you added the CO back in? I know I get very, very hungry when I don't have enough fat.

How were you eating before the LR? It looks like your low HDL & high Trigs, Insulin, Blood Glucose, are likely relics from your former way of eating? Are you having \*any\* wheat at all? How about dairy?

I would personally increase the protein at breakfast, make sure I had plenty of fat for breakfast & dinner, and \*not\* reduce dinner calories until I had a natural reduction in hunger (it will likely take a long while for this). Remember the LR range for BAB protein is 50-75gm ... some of usneed more to trigger the healing that leads to hunger reduction & craving reduction ... OTOH, stomach acid could also be a problem. Have you supplemented hcl at any point? Lacking the HCl or digestive enzymes neeeded to digest food can lead to hunger, since you're not getting the nutrients you need, even when eating a lot..

Remember, weight is a symptom of underlying problems - your weight will likely not budge until some other things turn around.

sorry about odd typos; my arms are still shaking from my cold bath!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-22T23:53:21+0200

Snowyky...have you read the Oprah blog? I re-read it today along with comments and it helped. Sorry, I don't know anything about labs but I am wondering why you are exercising. If you are still Leptin Resistant, exercise will not help, and may only make things worse. If you want, list out the foods you are eating...could be a food sensitivity or something.

Nice tip on the Hcl, MamaGrok - I think I need some.

Hang in Eleanor!

### Snowyky — 2012-03-23T00:45:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4690>

Thanks for your input MamaGrok,

Actually I had the same labs done a couple of years ago before I started the paleo way of eating and things got a bit higher on some things but i figured that would happen from what I read happened to other people,

2009

Trigs 126 now at 106

Cholesteral, Total 123 now 181

HDL Chol 45 now at 51

LDL Chol 53 now at 109

Glucose was 185 now at 100

HA1c was 7.9 now is 5.7

After I found out I was diabetic I followed weight watchers and eventually found Dr Berstein who is mainly low carb, then switched to Dr K's protocol when the weight wasn't moving.

I'm having absolutely no wheat, I make my own dressing using walnut oil and red wine vinegar when have salad, I've always eaten alot of eggs, we do eat out quite a bit but I have salad and meat or seafood, I can taste right away if there is any sugar in any of the meals and stop if I find any.

I will check out the HCL and keep on trucking, will also add extra protein, funny thing was I used be able to go all day until dinner with just a BAB, not quite sure when I noticed this changed, I think when I started the CT, which should've made it better eh?

Yeah I think they is something underlying, but what is the question.

Jennifer, I will go and read it again, thanks, also the exercise isn't really exercise like I was doing, it's only something to get my blood working since sitting at the computer for my job tends to make me feel sore and it's like my muscles are atrophying.

Here is what I eat on a typical day,

BAB

3 omega 3 eggs, 4 slices bacon, 4 links of sausage, sometimes 1/4 cup of mushrooms.

chocolate bark made with 1T coconut oil, 1t 100% Cacao, 5 drops of liquid splenda. Sometimes will have cheese but have stopped that along with other dairy.

Lunch

2 cups raw salad mix, mainly lettuce

2 T walnut oil with 2 T red wine vinegar

3 oz hard salami (less then 1 carb per oz)

chocolate bark

Dinner

shrimp scampi (5 oz shrimp, 3 T butter, garlic powder)

asparagus spears wrapped in bacon, 2 bundles of 5 with 1 bacon slice

chocolate bark

only change is for dinner I will have 4 - 5 oz of chicken or omega 3 beef (steak) tried 2 oz when i was watching calories, veggies sometimes will be 1 cup cabbage, cauliflower or broccoli with butter/coconut oil

I was having 1/2 sugar free jello with 1/4 cup homemade whipped cream using liquid splenda or divinci's syrups,

I sometimes have pork rinds for a snack along with a meal, usually with tuna salad at lunch.

I never go above 20 carbs usually stick below 10 until I have veggies with both meals.'

Been trying to eat more seafood, if we have fish I usually raise the size to 6oz, tilapia, cod or salmon.

thanks for you help ~Eleanor

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-23T00:55:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4697>

I also thought I'd mention that it's possible to get ketostix that are duds! I got a whole big package on them that were duds, I guess that's why they were so cheap. I've been showing the darkest level of ketones on my current ketostix for several weeks now but haven't lost even a pound! ARGH.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-23T01:07:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4710>

> **Snowyky;4718 wrote:**
>
> only change is for dinner I will have 4 - 5 oz of chicken or omega 3 beef (steak) tried 2 oz when i was watching calories, veggies sometimes will be 1 cup cabbage, cauliflower or broccoli with butter/coconut oil
>

> I was having 1/2 sugar free jello with 1/4 cup homemade whipped cream using liquid splenda or divinci's syrups,

>
> Been trying to eat more seafood, if we have fish I usually raise the size to 6oz, tilapia, cod or salmon.
>
> thanks for you help ~Eleanor

My hack for you is eat more. That's really not that much for someone at your weight. I think those that get down to 2 meals, and no hunger are less fat to be blunt. I can say that! I'm a woman and I have been that fat!. Perhaps the sheer number of fat cells are still sending out there little "i'm hungry!" signals. What realllllly helped me change my tastes was dropping diet drinks and any and all sweet tasting things. One of his posts allude to the taste receptors doing signalling, and I think for some of us, even the taste of sweet might have an impact. It was a good 6 months after cutting all sweets until I stopped being hungry for them - then BOOM, one day I realized my tastes had changed.

Also, I know we are not supposed to exercise until reset - but I found daily leisure walks (along with jumping and screaming during CT) to help bust a plateau. Plus it gets you out, gets the real vit d, and generally makes you happier.

Good Luck!

Keep us posted!

I'd ditch the Splenda, use stevia if you need sweetness. Grassfed beef is a better option to chicken. What's in your salami and sausages? Upping your seafood is a good idea. Increasing grassfed beef would be good too. Better to spend money in good food than lots of Supps. Homemade bone broths and fermented vegetables are healthy and cheap. Organs from grassfed animals are also really nutritious. I'd also increase your fats. Protein + fats is what every meal should consist of, then if you want some veg add some leafy greens or fermented veg. I drink green tea with coconut oil to add extra fat. All the best.

### Snowyky — 2012-03-23T04:00:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4797>

I'll get rid of the liquid splenda and just have coconut oil and the 100%cacao powder, hmmm I just remembered another thing I'm having that probably has carbs in it, lemon juice, I was drinking 'True lemon' with splenda and ended up buying a box of just the 'true lemon' crystallized lemon and have been using that in my water, it says there are 0carbs but it has evaporated cane juice and soy in the ingredients. Probably have about 9 of them a day, still only 9 carbs if we go with at least 1 per packet. But I'll get rid of it, and try to stick with as pure food as I can get, the hard salami is from walmart, the package says carbs 0 gm per 28gm, but I count 1 carb per oz, sausage had no fillers but has 1 carb per 2 links.

I only eat grass fed beef and have for months now, the chicken is no hormone, no antibiotic from kroger, more expensive too, but really I don't eat much of that anymore, sticking with the seafood and grassfed beef. Can't eat organ meats, sorry, just can't make myself eat it, the smell and the taste makes me gag.

I'll add more fat and eat more protein, I guess it's the SAD heritage that is making me think .. eat less and exercise more even though I know what is best is the opposite. It's a hard habit to get rid of when you are working hard at eating right and the fat is just sitting there hanging on for dear life :|

Fitday says I had 103gm of protein today with 201 grms of fat, so I could up it a bit and see how I feel, will start to add the coconut oil to some tea as well.

Y'all are a great help and I thank you, while I was in the tub tonight I read some of the "Leptin Reset Experiment - the optimized living edition" and was inspired again, sounds like I'm not the only one getting hungry from CT but it looks like things will eventually even itself out, I'll just keep working the plan.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-23T04:32:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4819>

Snowyky, just wanted to second what others have said about the importance of a clean diet. Use more CO to up your fats and cut out the substandard stuff like jello and pork rinds. Even bits of sugar slipping in have the effect of making me go bananas (or, well, chocolate!) and make me feel that awful craving feeling all day long. CT does definitely increase hunger, at least after you warm up. Increasing the fat in the meal prior is helpful. And, like others have said, as soon as I start feeling hungry and deprived I end up with other problems.

As far as organ meats go, get some bruschweier (that's probably not spelled correctly) liverwurst. It's easy to get down (and I was a vegetarian for 25 years so that's a big statement!).

Only other thought that comes to mind based on my similar experiences... work on the gut! If your gut is leaky all those supplements are lost. Make yourself some sauerkraut (EASY to do and very cheap - all you need is a head of organic cabbage, sea salt and a container) and maybe think about other probiotics, digestive enzymes and anti-parasite things. It helped me a lot. I re-read the leaky gut Rx posts often. My budget for supplements is almost non-existent, so I've tried to focus on the gut and the liver and deal to them most directly via a very clean paleo keto diet and CT.

Good luck!! And hang in there.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-23T04:41:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4824>

I'd definitely swap the splenda for stevia (or nothing), and chuck the pork rinds \*if\* they're storebought (usually fried in some junky oil).

Lauren, how do you prepare the bruschwhatever. I have two different packagess in my freezer, one uncooked from US Wellness meats, and another uncooked from a local farm.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-23T05:07:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4837>

MamaGrok, I just slice and pan fry (usually in CO). It's very mild (at least to me) and doesn't taste at all of liver in any other form that I've tried it. Sometimes, if I'm having it in the morning, I'll pan fry and cut those slices into bite-sized bits and add eggs.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-24T17:31:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-5436>

I have to second the idea of spending the $ on food before supps. Read some of the paleo books/info on bad oils. It makes you realize how bad the oils are that are not "clean".

So you mission, should you choose to accept it - eat more real food. Stop putting everything in Fit day! The only thing to worry about on the reset is enough protien at BAB. 8oz of a clean protein source should do it. Don't worry about only two meals till the weight starts to move and you don't want them.

I bet this helps!

### Birdy — 2012-03-24T18:52:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-5469>

OP, I think you have been spending too much time reading stuff at the banana websites. Please go back and read Dr. K's earlier posts on leptin and other hormones. For information about low carbs and insulin resistance, read up on Hyperlipid. Kresser believes that low carb diets cause high rT3, but if the problem was rampant, it would have been noted by the other low carb people.

### Cody — 2012-03-24T19:03:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-5474>

> **Birdy;5503 wrote:** OP, I think you have been spending too much time reading stuff at the banana websites. Please go back and read Dr. K's earlier posts on leptin and other hormones. For information about low carbs and insulin resistance, read up on Hyperlipid. Kresser believes that low carb diets cause high rT3, but if the problem was rampant, it would have been noted by the other low carb people.

+1,000,000. This is a result of the FUD spread by Evelyn "Carbsane" and other low fat freaks on the PaleoHacks site/blogosphere. It's crap.

Ketosis gives one a clear performance improvement in most areas of life. The heart runs more efficiently on ketones, the brain is more alert and quicker (look up Seth Robert's Butter testing on brain function), and things just work better in general.

Athletes who take the time to get fully fat adapted perform better and longer than sugar burners and their inflammation levels are waaaaay lower.

There are essential fats and essential proteins, but no essential sugars. Wanna guess why???

> 3 oz hard salami (less then 1 carb per oz)
>
> chocolate bark
>
>
>
> Dinner
>
>
>
> shrimp scampi (5 oz shrimp, 3 T butter, garlic powder)
>
> asparagus spears wrapped in bacon, 2 bundles of 5 with 1 bacon slice
>
> chocolate bark
>
>
>
> only change is for dinner I will have 4 - 5 oz of chicken or omega 3 beef (steak) tried 2 oz when i was watching calories, veggies sometimes will be 1 cup cabbage, cauliflower or broccoli with butter/coconut oil
>
>
>
> I was having 1/2 sugar free jello with 1/4 cup homemade whipped cream using liquid splenda or divinci's syrups,
>
>
>
> I sometimes have pork rinds for a snack along with a meal, usually with tuna salad at lunch.
>
> I never go above 20 carbs usually stick below 10 until I have veggies with both meals.'
>
> Been trying to eat more seafood, if we have fish I usually raise the size to 6oz, tilapia, cod or salmon.
>
> thanks for you help ~Eleanor

I'm no expert, but I noticed that no one has mentioned the amount of processed meat you're eating. I know us paleos love our bacon, but I still have yet to find bacon at a store, even whole foods, that doesn't have some sort of evaporated cane juice or corn syrup or some other sweetener. Not to mention the stuff they use to cure it that is linked to cancer (can't remember off the top of my head). I'm sure wellness meats has some awesome grassfed "no bad stuff in it" bacon, but you mentioned walmart so I don't think yours is from there. Also, the amount of sodium that you are getting from all of that processed meat (bacon, sausage, & salami). I don't usually worry about salt, but if you're not losing weight then you might be seriously retaining water from all of the sodium you're getting.

I would eat cleaner meat. Cut out all the processed at least until you lose the weight. Eat steak, grnd beef, fish, ect. Eat fattier meat as well.. I'd skip chicken all together. On the lists I've seen of best for your meat/protein chicken is always at the bottom. Also, I'd cut some of the supplements and get grassfed meat and organ meats instead. I'd build the healthiest nutrient-wise food plan for the month and then fill in with supplements where you are still lacking. Which shouldn't be too many really.

Hope you figure it out. My guess, your food is the issue nothing else. With needing to lose so much still it really shouldn't be as hard as it seems it has been for you. Also, I wouldn't worry about calories too much. If you're hungry then eat more.. maybe at BAB, but dinner too if the CT is making you hungry.

### Snowyky — 2012-03-26T04:19:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-6195>

Wow never really realized how much 'processed' food I was still eating via bacon and salami. I'm going to order some bacon and sausage from the US Wellness site. The bacon is definitely not pastured pork, and you're right if I'm buying it at Walmart, it's not going to be pastured meat. I've also been looking at a problem I developed when we moved in to this duplex, I got real sick, couldn't breath, spent mega $'s on testing for sleep apnea, asthma, hormones, but all done with CW wisdom. So they were never dealt with but masked as usual and this is something we all fight against. I did the online screening test for Biotoxins and it was positive, came back with both eyes failed the test, I'm going to make an appointment with an Immunologist and see if he will order the tests Dr Shoemaker suggests, or at least order tests that will show the Biotoxin problem I'm sure I have. My husband also is noticing he's having breathing problems, he was over the road trucking but in the last 3 months he was moved to a dedicated run that keeps him home now. When he was only home on the weekends he had some breathing problems but they would go away when he was in the truck all week. We will be moving back into the apartment we lived in when we first came here to Gtown, so we won't really be able to do much about the mold exposure until we get out of here. Within 3 months of living in this place I developed debilitating asthma, put on a cpap machine that helped alot, we are now able to afford to move again and once this lease is up we'll be out of here.

To make a long story short, the people who own the complex we are in admitted there was a leak in the wall of our bedroom that was fixed but they apparently closed it up and never treated it for mold, the rug in our bedroom was also flooded and not replaced. Tried to contact the health department about the problem and didn't get very far, not sure what else to do.

I know it's all connected and it clicked when I read Dr K's last blog entry where he mentions Biotoxins, will work on cleaning up my food intake, deal with the Biotoxins and if things still aren't moving along then I guess it's time to find a doctor who will do the hormone testing. I think all of this is beyond my family doctor, she tries but won't order tests that I ask for because she doesn't think I'm sick. lol yeah tell that to a 300 lb woman with a straight face eh?

Thanks all, I appreciate all your help and input, ~Eleanor

### Birdy — 2012-03-26T05:42:11+0300

> **Cody;5508 wrote:** +1,000,000. This is a result of the FUD spread by Evelyn "Carbsane" and other low fat freaks on the PaleoHacks site/blogosphere. It's crap.
>
>
>
> Ketosis gives one a clear performance improvement in most areas of life. The heart runs more efficiently on ketones, the brain is more alert and quicker (look up Seth Robert's Butter testing on brain function), and things just work better in general.
>
>
>
> Athletes who take the time to get fully fat adapted perform better and longer than sugar burners and their inflammation levels are waaaaay lower.
>
>
>
> There are essential fats and essential proteins, but no essential sugars. Wanna guess why???

Oh, I don't think it is crap, just thinking the incidence is low. Many of the people yakking about this aren't really fat adapted. They probably went from burning sugar from carbs to burning sugar from protein. They would also have a problem if doing low carb with low fat. That's how people start out if they aren't reading some of the good books first. However, I really think there is something to the carb re-feed, but not every few days or once a week or anything like that.

### Cody — 2012-03-26T05:52:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-6229>

> **Birdy;6263 wrote:** Oh, I don't think it is crap, just thinking the incidence is low. Many of the people yakking about this aren't really fat adapted. They probably went from burning sugar from carbs to burning sugar from protein. They would also have a problem if doing low carb with low fat. That's how people start out if they aren't reading some of the good books first. However, I really think there is something to the carb re-feed, but not every few days or once a week or anything like that.

What I think is actually happening are two things.

1\. They are addicted to carbs. Carbs help drive tryptophan into the brain where it is converted into seratonin (along with Magnesium and B6). They may be severely magnesium depleted, as the underlying cause of this addiction, amongst many other possible reasons. This can cause sleep loss (experienced this myself very badly, to the point of hallucinating, started taking 5htp, Magnesium, and B6, problem solved).

2\. Rapid water loss causes minerals to be lost along with the water. People who don't use enough salt feel horrible when they go into keto style eating. They also may not be drinking enough water. I drink over a gallon per day and use a mix of regular salt and potassium salt.

There is no valid reason people cannot adapt to ketosis, if they look at the underlying science and understand how the human body works and what causes theses symptoms that people are such wusses about. Dr. Kruse would say that they are choosing not to be optimal, and that would be correct.

All of this information is WELL KNOWN in low carb circles, and all people have to do is read Atkins or Eades among many other low-carb authors. Heck, even a google search will help them figure this stuff out. I think they are just lazy, scared, or both.

Carb refeeds are not neccesary and will only hinder long term fat adaptation. Don't take my word for it. Ask Dr. Kruse...

Great discussion, though.

### David — 2012-03-26T15:33:38+0300

>

I believe that Dr Kruse has said that one shouldn't pay any attention to the results shown by a ketostix self-test (or use scales, either, for that matter) and I have also read that one's body could be effectively using all of the ketones produced and none are left to be excreted in urine, so nothing could show on the ketostix. It also may be that your body is adapting slowly even though you are pursuing the correct course, it's just that all outward signs (weight-loss, etc) are not yet manifesting themselves.

### Cody — 2012-03-26T15:44:07+0300

I agree with David. Barely showing a trace on the ketostix is normal if you are properly hydrated. You can manipulate it. Workout really hard and then pee on them. Or try drinking half as much water as normal. You'll see darker on them.

But it really doesn't matter. Keep doing the proper thing and the results will follow.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-26T17:06:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-6374>

Some people do experience a rise in fasting BS with dropping carbs. I don't, but don't know why some do and some don't.

> **quelsen;6387 wrote:** how long have you been Keto?
>
>
>
>
>
> It took Phoenix over 6 months to stop being a sugar burner. She finally has no blood sugar drops when hungry. she started the same time i did but she was not as anal about it.

I haven't had a blood sugar drop between meals in several years. 1.5 years ago we went camping and didn't eat at all until 2pm and no problems. In the past, I couldn't go 3hrs without eating. I read all over the internet about people getting clarity, energy, and losing cravings when they enter ketosis. That has never happened for me.

Every time I dropped carbs (until about two months ago), I got the orthostatic hypotension (wooziness on standing, pulse rising, from my low bp) a day or two later. That doesn't happen anymore, but I've only taken carbs above 50g once or twice int he last several months.

I don't do anything calculated (can't drink coconut milk, anyway, b/c it gives me carb wooziness in the head instantly with that much, and cravings, too), just eat mostly meat and fat with some non-starchy veggies added for breakfast.

I have turned ketostix purple, ha dthe "classic" low electrolyte morning wooziness after going keto, and still, never ever regained even the normal mental clarity I used to have before everything went downhill 5-6 years ago.

### Lahrie — 2012-03-26T17:35:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-6389>

**Snowyky;4675 wrote:** Not sure where to stick this but barely above trace on a ketostix has me wondering what the heck. I'm feeling pretty down about all this, why can't I lose weight? I'm over 300 lbs, I did lose when I first started but nothing since. 2 1/2 months on the leptin reset and over a month doing CT with ice packs, face dunks and cold baths and am now having ice baths for 60mins with the temp being 50\* - 55\* everyday now for 2 weeks. I take all the supplements Dr K mentions, I've been using the yellow glasses at night and am usually in bed by 9:30 with lights out by 10, I still wake up quite a bit at night and am getting hungrier by the day  I did better it seemed when I first started, I thought it was because I was going so very very low carb with no veggies, just fat and meat, so I added back a salad for lunch, add mushrooms to my BAB omelet. I've tried cutting out CO and it didn't make any difference. I notice fat disappearing in some areas ie: weird spots in my arms and wrists, husband says my legs are getting smaller but when I check my other measurements, they haven't moved and neither is the scale. When I'm in the tub I don't shiver and I'm warming up quicker once I get out and I'm starving when I get out  and can't eat because I do the bath after my last meal at night. I drink water which is probably what's making me get up during the night.

I've been looking at what labs I should get done as I read that Dr K told someone with the same kind of problems that something else is wrong if CT isn't stopping the hunger, but I'm lost when it comes to what labs, I have gone through menopause, but don't have any of the typical symptoms ie: hot flashes and dryness below, if you know what I mean. I read somewhere that Dr K suggested 3 labs if we can't afford all, and they were Alpha MSH, Vit D and HS CRP. Since I don't have much money left to spend as I've been spending well over $200 a month on supplements, does anyone have any suggestions, I had the typical labs done at my doctor's but she wouldn't order the rest as she didnt' think I needed them, and they included all the hormone ones which I thought was kind of strange, but she says I have no symptoms so why worry. Here are my labs for what I did get done,

Tryglycerides 106 mg/dl Range or =46

LDL Cholesteral 109 Range get this inflammed, but shouldn't I be losing weight, I've tried lowering the calories for lunch and dinner to less then 500, but no difference except more hungry. The BAB is usually around 51gm of protein, fitday says a typical day is Fat 75%, Protein 23%, Carbs 2%. avg 1100 to 1900 calories depending what I have. Exercise, I try to walk every day or use my gravity glider or tubes, my job is very sedentary so I try to move around at least once an hour via the gravity glider or walk outside for a bit.

If anyone has any ideas for labs or other tips that would get me on the weight loss road I would really appreciate it, I will stick to the reset and CT because i feel better when I'm doing it, my blood sugar is lower via CT so that's a definite plus. Oh yes the ketostix show very little even though I eat very little carbs, just slightly above a trace. Help, ~Eleanor

Snowyky -

I am not a doctor, but based on _optimal_ ranges listed on Dr. Kaslow's website, and what you've written, you may be dealing with Insulin Resistance and clogged/fatty liver with some adrenal/cortisol issues that may be affecting your efforts. I mention these because they are the easiest to test for/treat yourself. Here's a link to Dr. Kaslow's site [http://www.drkaslow.com/index.html](http://http://www.drkaslow.com/index.html) . There are many "optimal" lab ranges listed here and possible causes for low/high test results. Don't panic when you read these, some are worst case scenario.  I went here and saw definite patterns in my own test results that led me to narrow down the supplements and focus the follow-up labs to what I wanted to address first, second, etc.

I also agree with the other advice you've received here re: clean eating and increasing your CO to help with hunger. This website is not for diagnosis, but is another tool that was incredibly helpful to me in narrowing my focus. All of the information can become overwhelming at times....  It may be just what you need to add clean eating to some specific testing and treatment/supplements. You are making progress! Taking the steps to change your life and not accepting what CW/your MD says is normal or healthy is intimidating but you are doing it day after day. Keep taking steps and you will get there!

Lahrie

### Cody — 2012-03-26T18:16:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-6407>

> **Shijin13;6421 wrote:** Ok. So I'm probably in keto. The question am I keto adapted?

Unless you've been strictly keto eating for a year or more, probably not. Once you are fully keto adapted, you will be able to "cheat" and get back into ketosis the next day. It's a pretty awesome place to be...

> **MamaGrok;6452 wrote:** How do you know that you stay in it, Cody? How are you gauging this?

Based on what I eat. Usually less than 10 grams of carbs per day. I don't bother tracking. I have eggs and meat for breakfast cooked in coconut oil, and meat and a cup of veggies (broccoli, cauliflower, spinach, etc. etc.) for supper. I don't really need a lot of variety any more. It's just food to me now. Use to be a big deal.

### Cody — 2012-03-26T20:46:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-6487>

> What I'm asking is, how do you know that you've returned to keto the day after a cheat, and that before you were keto-adapted, you didn't?
>
>
> Cheaters never win and winners never cheat.
>
>
>
> I haven't been strict keto for that long. I allowed cheat days on Saturdays and it would usually take me four days (I'm a big guy with lots of muscle and a big liver) to get all my glycogen depleted and get back into regular ketosis.
>
>
>
> I can always tell by scale weight. Not in ketosis == 10 pounds heavier, in ketosis == 10 pounds lighter. It may be harder for you ladies, depending on your cycle.
>
>
>
> This time around though, I am not cheating. My goal is to go at least 6 months with my carbs under 20 every day.
>
>
>
> That's how I am now too, getting tired of cooking though, would love to drink my food, get it over with and go about my day, afraid that's a product of our generation eh, just too easy to drive down the road and grab a meal cooked and served to you, problem is it's what got me sick to begin with. I'm going to try some of the crock pot meat and see if it will work for me for breakfast, easy prep and hopefully good tasting.

I have a cast iron skillet that lives on top of my stove. First thing in the AM, on goes the stove. Then I make my Upgraded coffee. Then I throw some coconut oil into the skillet and throw in a pound of whatever meat I'm having for breakfast. Then in my nonstick ceramic greenpan goes more coconut oil and four free range eggs. Mixture of regular/potassium salt and fresh ground pepper over everything, and I'm good to go. Easy peasy.

### Snowyky — 2012-03-27T01:10:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-6649>

> **MamaGrok;6657 wrote:** The taste does a lot more than just taste sweet.

Very true Mama, since I was diagnosed diabetic I cut off all sweets except the liquid splenda :| which Dr K said was ok, but I guess that's just for people who aren't stalled like myself. The sugar jumps right out at me now if it's in something I'm served, had a Caesar salad awhile back and I tasted something real sweet and the server said they didn't add sugar to the salad, lol gawd anyway turns out they usually add a honey glazed croissant to the dish and the server took it off when he realized I asked for no bread.

Well today I've stuck with absolutely no goodies ie: splenda in my lemon water, no lemon even, just plain iced water, just meat fried in coconut oil for breakfast, no eggs since they seem to be a no no if I have leaky gut. Shrimp scampi for lunch and leftover shrimp for dinner, with just irish butter and garlic powder. Tested my blood sugar after lunch and it was better, down to 104, I'm going to have an ice bath shortly and I'm sure my sugar will be down even further.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-27T15:55:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-6963>

I also wanted to mention that the issue with sweeteners is what it does to your blood sugar-IMO. I read Dr K say that he is even off Stevia now because even though it is all natural, etc, if your body says "yum, sweet" it still can react with too much insulin. I can tell if something sets my insulin off because it makes me feel like I could go into a feeding frenzy - one taste is never enough! I recently found some 99% chocolate and it does not trigger this response in me. I haven't mixed it with anything yet. You do have to watch the carbs with it though. I guess the key is to know your own body and if you can handle it or not.

The low blood sugar is something I have been dealing with since August. It started when I was doing Hcg and I have not been able to regulate. A glucometer is a good idea. I have always had what I call "touchy blood sugar." A bowl of ceral with milk would make me feel like I was starving. Pancakes with syrup would have me on the floor in a sugar crash. I haven't been able to eat sugar (if I wanted to) without feeling super light headed since Aug. My symptoms are - being REALLY irritable, like I want to kill people, super short temper, everything annoys me and I get dizzy (can't do my yoga poses without falling over) and I feel dehydrated. I am constantly trying to balance the mag and K. Either I feel good but am constipated (and then feel like crap that way) or I have the runs and feel dizzy. I don't always have all symptoms. And I do have good days. Today I am super crabby. My doc recently put me on Estradiol for low estrogen and I'm doing leaky gut Rx, Adrenal Rx and AI protocol. I really thought I ate enough protein this morning but I feel so bad and am STARVING. What do you eat for your BAB the keeps you until dinner?? This weekend I did ok. Only ate BAB and dinner. But I don't do as well at work -- sitting around and stuff. I'm 24 days into Leptin Reset and 20 on CT.

Thanks MamaGrok!

### lisaeh3 — 2012-03-27T17:38:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-7031>

What about supplementing with progesterone. I take Progest. I have had a total hysterectomy and can't live without it. Prior to my surgery I was a total mess and found that it would calm me down and allow me to least be civil to others. Hormones suck! They get worse after you have kids. Good luck to you!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-27T18:14:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-7060>

> **Jennifer;7061 wrote:** I really thought I ate enough protein this morning but I feel so bad and am STARVING. What do you eat for your BAB the keeps you until dinner?? This weekend I did ok. Only ate BAB and dinner. But I don't do as well at work -- sitting around and stuff. I'm 24 days into Leptin Reset and 20 on CT.
>
> Thanks MamaGrok!

It's a process. When I started 50g BAB, it kept me till lunch (although I was no longer hungry, I could still have kept eating for a lot longer after the 50g bAB!), then I ate a big lunch and that kept me till dinner,, then I ate a big dinner and that kept me till bedtime.

After 4 months, I got an interior nudging to go up to 65g BAB. Even at that level, I still felt like I could eat the BAB all over again as soon as I was finished. After a month, I started to get full at breakfast, then I started to get full enough that I didn't really care about lunch after two months at 65g. Only then was the BAB "enough" to keep me through to dinner - after almost 6 months.

So it wasn't a matter of being _enough food; it was a matter of my _body_ being nourished enough_ not to need more ... and that took a long time for me, even after having been primal for over a year and "traditional foods" for several more. I think it's gonna vary a lot, individual to individual.

>
> OH, I see. That helps to hear. Because I think I should be "getting somewhere" - LOL - where I have no idea! It's so hard to figure because I am all over the place. One day, I'll eat a BAB, and still be hungry but 3 hours later, I am stuffed and can't eat lunch. Then there are days that I feel full and hungry at the same time. Like today. I feel like I am starving and can't wait for lunch but the thought of actually putting food in my stomach - I think, I'm too full. Like I didn't already feel all bipolar inside with the hormones! I guess I need to stop "thinking" and reading into everything and just respond. If I am hungry, eat. If not, don't. Should be easy right?
>

### shilohman — 2012-03-27T19:04:27+0300

I don't have Raynaud's, but I do have years of excess omega 6 in my body and the improvements are gradual, but sure. Keep-on-Trucking.

### anonymouse — 2012-03-28T05:14:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-7558>

Just wondering, what do you (and Dr. Kruse) all consider a ketogenic diet? Before I even knew about CT'ing I was Primal and eating ~80-90 carbs a day, lots of fat and meat, and felt glorious. Been in ketosis (obvious urine odor) for a week now and feel basically awful. Carb intake is down under 30 for sure though I have no way of accurately measuring (have lots of raw greens, still). Strength is somehow the same...and this is saying a lot for a lifter who's been stalled out forever; I think CT is balancing out the (temporary) negatives of keto.

What carb range is acceptable?

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T15:43:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-7686>

Thanks Destiny. I wish I had met you sooner! I was taking progesterone tablets a couple years ago and was paying out the whazoo for them! I'm just not sure if it's ok to supplement with progesterone when your estrogen is low?? Looking at my labs, both values are low. But I am keeping this in the back of my mind for future reference.

Prolactin 7.3 (non-pregnant range 3-30; Postmenopausal 2-10),

Progesterone 10 mg (Follicular phase < or = 185; Luteal phase < or = 285; Postmenopausal < or = 45);

Testosterone 11 (2-45); Testosterone Free 1.2 (0.1-6.4).

My DHEA is 141 (range 102-1185).

My Estradiol is < 15 (as in way low).

My FSH is normal.

### Snowyky — 2012-04-05T17:30:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-11743>

> **Lovelife;5649 wrote:** I'm no expert, but I noticed that no one has mentioned the amount of processed meat you're eating.

Thought I'd drop in and let y'all know that things are moving south  I looked back at what I was eating and took your suggestions and they have helped alot. I was eating way to much processed food, I dropped the bacon rinds, when I was eating carbs my favorite snack was barbeque chips, guess the rinds were a leftover of those days, so I got rid of them. I also quit drinking lemon with splenda in my water, plain ice water now. I stopped taking most of the supplements I was taking (because of the possible leaky gut and it was all going down the drain anywho), although I'm thinking of adding them back because the CT is not as pleasant as it was before, I kept the bitter melon which is what Dr K suggested to help with the CT. I did add Betaine HCL for the low acid problem with my stomach and it seems to be helping, the diarrhea has slowed down and I don't notice as much un-digested food passing through that I used. Been eating as clean as I can and trying not to stress about it all, I've relaxed when it comes to 'am I doing this all perfectly' which I think was my biggest problem.

I'm down 3 lbs which is pretty good for a week, it's broken my plateau and I'm starting to sleep like a log which is one of the markers for being on the right track to wellness

Also noticing the weight is shifting so things are happening, I haven't bothered with the scale except the one time when I had a doc appt, my clothes fit better and I'm feeling good, it eventually works if we stick with it.

### Snowyky — 2012-04-05T17:34:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-11746>

I should say, I've noticed the fat is shifting, such as my chin is more pronounced, I also am seeing more of a shadow on my cheeks so my cheekbones are becoming more pronounced as well, my stomach fat is getting more spongy and it like it's dividing up in the center, hard to explain but something is happening with it lol

### Cody — 2012-04-05T19:13:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-11793>

Rock on!!!! Woo hoo! Way to go!

I noticed facial changes pretty quickly, too, and I think this is attributed to better hormones. Our hormonal health is reflected very heavily in our faces. Proper hormones make women "prettier" and men "more handsome" from what scientists have seen when asking people to look at photos of people's faces.

# Thread: carbohydrates content in vegetables
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbohydrates-content-in-vegetables.349/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 9

### graham — 2012-03-21T22:13:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbohydrates-content-in-vegetables.349/post-4033>

Can anybody let me know what is the carb content of vegetables, as I'm having difficulty in finding this information

thanks

### differentstory — 2012-03-21T23:11:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbohydrates-content-in-vegetables.349/post-4067>

> **graham;4059 wrote:** Can anybody let me know what is the carb content of vegetables, as I'm having difficulty in finding this information
>
> thanks

I search for "broccoli nutrition" and it comes up? I usually wind up on a page like this:

[http://nutritiondata.self.com/facts/vegetables-and-vegetable-products/2356/2](http://http://nutritiondata.self.com/facts/vegetables-and-vegetable-products/2356/2)

And it tells you the carbs.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-22T00:40:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbohydrates-content-in-vegetables.349/post-4106>

If you google the "induction phase of the Adkins diet," you'll find a few pages with this info.

### diane — 2012-03-22T01:07:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbohydrates-content-in-vegetables.349/post-4113>

I use [Calorie King](http://http://www.calorieking.com/). It gives protein, carbs, and has a pretty good database. Plus you can change serving sizes. Should give you the carbs in veggies pretty easily!

### Lyndra — 2012-03-22T18:06:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbohydrates-content-in-vegetables.349/post-4474>

This is probably a very basic question - BUT - when you are trying to be in ketosis and keeping your carb intake down, do you count the **total carb count of veggies or just the net** after subtracting out fiber? I've seen suggestions to do it both ways.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-22T19:05:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbohydrates-content-in-vegetables.349/post-4514>

> **Lyndra;4502 wrote: This is probably a very basic question - BUT - when you are trying to be in ketosis and keeping your carb intake down, do you count the **total** carb count of veggies or just the net** after subtracting out fiber? I've seen suggestions to do it both ways.

I believe it's net carbs.

### Croak — 2012-03-22T19:23:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbohydrates-content-in-vegetables.349/post-4530>

This isn't Atkins.  It's total, not net.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-22T21:34:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbohydrates-content-in-vegetables.349/post-4614>

> **Croak;4558 wrote:** This isn't Atkins.  It's total, not net.

I just remember when doing Atkins induction it was net carbs, and I was definitely in ketosis. Thanks for clarifying, though, Croak.

### Petra — 2012-03-29T11:59:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbohydrates-content-in-vegetables.349/post-8177>

Just for your idea

100 grams of spinach has 3.6 grams of carbs

100 grams of sweet pepper has 4.6 grams of carbs, so I think a salad will do me good and still stay in ketosis. Just as long as I do not eat more then 1 kg of it.  Right?

Take care,

Petra

# Thread: When food becomes a pain in the shoulder
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-food-becomes-a-pain-in-the-shoulder.350/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-22T02:42:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-food-becomes-a-pain-in-the-shoulder.350/post-4169>

Okay gang, not looking for answers, but would love to know what questions we aren't asking on this one.

Almost exactly 2 years ago, my dh started having pain in his right shoulder when he eats. The pain is at the base of the shoulder blade, between the spine and the shoulder blade. It doesn't usually happen with breakfast, would be stronger with lunch and debilitating with dinner. The pain start as a dull ache and within minutes progress to a sharp stabbing pain. It would subside after the meal within 1-4 hours.

This continued for about 6 months during which he saw a cardiologist and had a complete stress test. He saw a gastro doc who did an EGD and found nothing. Bloodwork done didn't show any abnormalities.

It just faded away that October and all we could ever attribute to it was that he did start taking a baby aspirin since had turned the magic 50. That was the only thing we could find that was different. So no aspirin and no pain

Until now it is back. What the heck is this? Norovirus has run through our town. Both of us have had colds - mine with a fever - his without. Mine with a sinus infection bad enough to request dentures, his without a sinus head ache. Both of us tired.

Does anyone know what this is?

### colleencoble — 2012-03-22T02:45:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-food-becomes-a-pain-in-the-shoulder.350/post-4173>

Sounds like it might be gallbladder. If he had an ultrasound, that doesn't show everything. If he's been checked for gallbladder with no results, have them run a gallbladder function test. I suffered with this for 3 years until the dr ran one of those. The ultrasounds were fine but when they finally did the function test, it was only operating at 8%.

But don't rush to get it out. Work on getting it working instead.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-22T03:43:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-food-becomes-a-pain-in-the-shoulder.350/post-4206>

Yay! Aha! Yippee! I asked the dh and sure enough he had an ultrasound but no function test. Bless you for giving some direction! That's why Dr. K sings your praises!

### colleencoble — 2012-03-22T04:35:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-food-becomes-a-pain-in-the-shoulder.350/post-4231>

It's just I've had most ailments known to man. LOL That's experience talking.

### L8F — 2012-03-22T14:32:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-food-becomes-a-pain-in-the-shoulder.350/post-4319>

> **colleencoble;4257 wrote:** It's just I've had most ailments known to man. LOL That's experience talking.

And making the most of it by helping others!! Thank goodness for you out there! You an JanSz should get together for a hormone Fx panel and charge admission.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-23T19:26:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-food-becomes-a-pain-in-the-shoulder.350/post-5061>

Turns out the EGD did include a function test, that was normal. But as I told my dh, that was in Sept 2010 when the symptoms were subsiding, so maybe it was back within normal limits. But I also told him - that was then, this is now. And he needs a current test!

My quick search says coffee and chocolates are no-no's. Guess who is a 2 cup a day guy with that dreadful fake creamer crap in it?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-23T19:48:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-food-becomes-a-pain-in-the-shoulder.350/post-5075>

> **colleencoble;4199 wrote:** Sounds like it might be gallbladder. If he had an ultrasound, that doesn't show everything. If he's been checked for gallbladder with no results, have them run a gallbladder function test. I suffered with this for 3 years until the dr ran one of those. The ultrasounds were fine but when they finally did the function test, it was only operating at 8%.
>
>
>
> But don't rush to get it out. Work on getting it working instead.

!00% concur w/this... when I'm out of alignment at my atlas - I'll get pain their - and its totally gallbladder

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-23T19:49:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-food-becomes-a-pain-in-the-shoulder.350/post-5076>

> **colleencoble;4257 wrote:** It's just I've had most ailments known to man. LOL That's experience talking.

Collen

I'm following in your footsteps... it seems...

# Thread: brain freeze
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-freeze.351/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-03-22T02:59:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-freeze.351/post-4187>

So when I've done the face dunking I get the dreaded ice cream headache. I've had the same experience when I turn my shower nozzle onto my face to simulate face dunking during a cold shower.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brain\_freeze](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brain_freeze)

"The most effective way to prevent it is to consume the cold food or liquid at a slower rate. Keeping it in one's mouth long enough for the palate to become used to the temperature is also an effective preventative."

In about an hour I'll be face dunking again. I'm going to try holding cold water in my mouth several times for a few minutes building up to it and see if the brain freeze is avoided.

jesse

### Powder — 2012-03-22T03:03:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-freeze.351/post-4191>

I have the same experience in the shower, when the water is as cold as possible, although it is not as painful nor does it last as long as the actual Ice Cream headaches I can remember (been a while). I don't have the exact quote, but I remember Dr Kruse saying in oneof the blogs/comments that they are a sign that you are not cold-adapted.

Hopefully, when they no longer happen, it's a good sign that we are.

### hellojtm — 2012-03-22T03:16:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-freeze.351/post-4199>

Yes and he also mentioned that it could be a sign of improper cell membrane fluidity and a sign that your tissue omega-6 levels are high.

I'm personally ruling out the second one because my diet composition is ~3:1 - n3:n6, and has been that way over a year, so the thought that my n6 could be too high is just too baffling for me. I have absolutely no way of validating the first one so I'm going to go with the brain freeze

Green tea is very beneficial for the persons who want to lose the weight.

It prevents from the heart diseases and keeps the skin fresh.

### Cody — 2012-03-22T18:46:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.358/post-4502>

### MartiD — 2012-03-23T20:42:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.358/post-5096>

### CheshireCat57 — 2012-03-23T20:48:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.358/post-5100>

First post after lerking for a week or more...

I drink a lot of green tea when I am working from home as I did today. I think that I have made tea three times - the teapot I use is approx 2 pints so 6 pints over the course of the working day. I read somewhere that there might be a problem linking Green tea with reduced Thyroid activity (which I do have - and take meds for) but I have only come across this argument once. Most writers regard the use of green tea as beneficial. I would be interested in the views of others.

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-23T21:17:44+0200

6 green tea bags

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-23T21:25:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.358/post-5113>

Green tea makes it hard for me to sleep. Morning only for me. Does it have this effect on anyone else? Sleep is are key "hack" for me, so I go easy. Sleep is more important to me.

### Sherry — 2012-03-23T22:20:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.358/post-5141>

Green tea wires me. I can drink the decaffeinated and I have to trust that the label's "naturally decaffeinated" is true because I certainly wouldn't want to insert the chemically produced decaf into my body especially while detoxing with CT.

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-23T22:58:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.358/post-5155>

Steeping tea in cold water \*greatly\* reduces the level of caffeine. ;-)

### tweety — 2012-03-23T23:51:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.358/post-5170>

### Cody — 2012-03-24T01:22:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.358/post-5222>

theanine is relaxing, not a stimulant. Google it.

### tweety — 2012-03-24T01:41:59+0200

it wasn't about relaxing or stimulating... it was muscle testing. I love green tea though, so I am having a hard time accepting it.

# Thread: Nail Biting?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nail-biting.359/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HeyChelle — 2012-03-22T16:32:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nail-biting.359/post-4409>

Wasn't sure where to ask... Anyone conquer this nervous habit?

I chewed my nails as a kid, then kicked the habit in highschool. Had nice nails until we found out about my sons food allergies. He was still exclusively breasted, I had eliminated dairy and reduced egg in my diet, had him tests via skin and rast at 9 months, went into a bit of a shock when I got the lab results and realized all the foods he was allergic to and I would have to remove from my diet, promptly chewed off all my nails while in a state of shock on the drive home from the doctor. That was over 6 years ago and I have chewed my nails badly ever since.

Except - for about one month a couple years ago. I had started metformin for pcos and insulin resistance (pre-paleo: I unknowingly screwed my body while I was on the elimination diet for my son.). The first month on metformin I dropped weight, felt very at peace, and stopped biting my nails without even trying. After week 6, though the Meds started making me very sick and I stopped taking it.

At this point my health issues are melting away along with body fat, but I'm still biting my nails. I'm determined to kick this habit, but I wondered if there is something else I can do to kick the underlying issue that creates this nervous habit.

Thank you in advance. I'm so ready to kick this habit the same way I destroyed the insulin resistance, overrating, and excess body fat.

### tweety — 2012-03-22T20:41:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nail-biting.359/post-4591>

I have noticed I bite mine when they are weak and brittle, but when they are growing strong I leave them alone. I wondered if it was related to a vitamin deficiency?

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-23T04:36:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nail-biting.359/post-4821>

I think this is completely not a "habit" so much as an expression of something interior, like you said. Whether it's cortisol or neurological imbalance or gut imbalance or a combination (or something else), I don't know. I do think you will kick it in this process.

### HeyChelle — 2012-03-24T06:05:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nail-biting.359/post-5334>

Thanks for the ideas. I have my labs coming up soon. Cortisol is on the list.

### Petra — 2012-03-29T14:06:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nail-biting.359/post-8191>

> **HeyChelle;5367 wrote:** Thanks for the ideas. I have my labs coming up soon. Cortisol is on the list.

that is always good. I thought that nail referred to an deficit in magnesium.

### mumone — 2012-03-30T08:44:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nail-biting.359/post-8663>

I dont know the answer but my nails are strong, and grow like crazy....and no urge to bite.

its really incredible cause i was a serious nail biter!

and I fell off the wagon the other weekend with my sugar intake and hey presto bitten to the quick by the end of the weekend.

so its gotta be something i am doing on the Leptin Rx + CT combo.

# Thread: 20 min bath or hour long spot CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-min-bath-or-hour-long-spot-ct.360/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-03-22T17:14:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-min-bath-or-hour-long-spot-ct.360/post-4430>

If those were your choices, which would you do to get the biggest bang? So far I'm only managing about 20-22 minutes in the tub at 55 degrees water temp. I just can't stand more and most evenings I don't have the time to do more either, which is going to be the case tonight. I figure I should be able to get an hour of spot CT'ing in though since I can do it while making dinner, doing the dishes, etc.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-22T17:39:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-min-bath-or-hour-long-spot-ct.360/post-4443>

I do both - bath mid-afternoon, then walk the dogs to warm up, and spot CT during the evening. That way I don't feel guilty if, for any reason, I miss a bath. I also make sure that my shower is cold in the morning as well.

### Ane — 2012-03-22T17:44:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-min-bath-or-hour-long-spot-ct.360/post-4447>

I have only done the baths, but I have not been able to get them cold enough. I am going to do both this evening.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-22T17:46:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-min-bath-or-hour-long-spot-ct.360/post-4448>

I think the biggest bang will be the 20 minute cold bath. Maybe add a little ice to get it down to 50 F?

I've done both and the bath definitely gets my entire body down in temp. Imagine all those skin sensors sending signals at once.

However, the bath process takes some time for me - so I use icepacks for the convenience if I can't fit in a bath.

### sandy — 2012-03-22T18:17:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-min-bath-or-hour-long-spot-ct.360/post-4481>

The bath is much more uncomfortable to me(therefore more effective??) than the spot CT. But, since I am sitting here on my ass reading this forum anyhow, I have ice packs on. I will continue with the baths tho

### JC2K — 2012-03-22T21:50:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-min-bath-or-hour-long-spot-ct.360/post-4621>

For me, I just started using a cold vest for an hour and I've noticed that I have less tolerance for the showers/baths on the day that I do (more shivering, unpleasantness). So I've been alternating, vest day, bath day, etc. I'll try both again in a bit and see if I've adapted.

### Riversedge — 2012-03-23T05:19:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-min-bath-or-hour-long-spot-ct.360/post-4845>

I just did my first bath. I have been doing showers. Made it for ten minutes with the shower running on my chest and stomach. Wow what a difference the tap water is still at 50Ã‚Â° here I'll post any changes in sleep. Lately I can't sleep I'm hoping it helps.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-23T05:46:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-min-bath-or-hour-long-spot-ct.360/post-4857>

> **oliviascotland;4471 wrote: I do both - bath mid-afternoon, then walk the dogs to warm up, and spot CT during the evening. That way I don't feel guilty if, for any reason, I miss a bath. I also make sure that my shower is cold in the morning as well.
>
>
> I do this as well. Spot-CT, cold showers (can't tolerate warm showers anymore) and baths in the evening on the nights I'm not going out.
>
>
>
> villjamur\_stevenson;4476 wrote:** I think the biggest bang will be the 20 minute cold bath. Maybe add a little ice to get it down to 50 F?
>
>
>
> I've done both and the bath definitely gets my entire body down in temp. Imagine all those skin sensors sending signals at once.
>
> However, the bath process takes some time for me - so I use icepacks for the convenience if I can't fit in a bath.

I think baths are more efficient at you reaching your goals with CT. I know it sped up the process for me... for 3 weeks I only ever did spot-CT, but didn't really get blasted into great results (muscle gains, better sleep) until I included full submerges.

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-23T09:47:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-min-bath-or-hour-long-spot-ct.360/post-4885>

My vote is on immersion. I have been acclimating with my pool at 62 degrees. I have done 45 minutes. A local gym has a cold pool that I plan to use next. I believe it is 50 degrees.

I use cold showers too but also need hot showers in order to get clean since cold is not as effective in that arena. Have to open up those pores.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-23T14:19:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-min-bath-or-hour-long-spot-ct.360/post-4915>

I agree...I think I remember Dr K say you will cold adapt much faster in water. I do spot CT in the morning with ice packs and cold bath at night. But I have been wondering which would be more optimal - the 40 mins I do in 55 degree water or 20 mins in 50 degrees? It is all I can do to convince myself to get in the 55 degree water and I freeze my already flat tushy off

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-23T15:16:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-min-bath-or-hour-long-spot-ct.360/post-4933>

It seems like logic would dictate that immersion gives the greater benefit for reasons like more skin receptors feeling the temp change, quicker reduction in overall skin temp, etc. But doing something is better than nothing, surely. What can you manage in 20 min?

There's significantly less prep and clean-up time for me to spot-ice 20 min than there is to immerse in a cold bath. Of course, if you've got a jacuzzi you can moderate, a pool, lake, etc., then it would be just about as easy as a spot-ice session. The only clean-up being to dry off.

# Thread: Newbie food questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-food-questions.366/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TerrierMom — 2012-03-22T19:47:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-food-questions.366/post-4554>

I did Atkins-style low carb 10 years ago and lost 60 lbs in 6 mo, then decided to give up smoking and turned to my carby friends for support. I did it even though eating the atkins bars, shakes etc, having cured meats like bacon and deli meat, and dairy. Of course I ballooned back up and never quite got the guts to change again until now, when doc told me I'm borderline Type 2 Diabetic... NO PILLS FOR ME. I know I can heal myself if I'll get off my fat butt and CHANGE what I put in my mouth (and add light/dark and CT and good sleep to the mix!)

Fast forward to now. I get it that NO grains or legumes are good for me, and that if I want to reset my Leptin dairy is out too... and nightshades until my LDL gets to where it needs to be. I know how to eat VLC from before and lose weight.

BUT.... I want to be OPTIMAL not just lose weight. I've bought half a grass-fed cow in the past so I have another on order for my freezer (the BEST meat EVER!) .. and I found a pastured egg source (though reading on this thread not sure if I have liver/leaky gut issues so eggs may go out the window soon for a while)

The dumb questions are: lots of folks have Bacon in their BAB, but is it super-special bacon or just the kind you can get from any store? Is sugar-cured a problem? nitrites? Same question on other meats. I know, I know, these are PROCESSED meats so I am guessing it's gonna be a NO to them. If I can't get grassfed meet, is grocery store meat going to set me back? (Sisson says pick LEAN and trim the fat if not grassfed).

Also I saw in Sisson's book a NO to smoked meats. Well we have a smoker in the backyard and smoke our own meats. Is smoking meat BAD for this lifestyle? It is so much flavor! I went ON VACATION the second week of VLC and took little pouches of pulled pork (no sauce of course) for my lunch and weight continued to come off..

But, I want to get to OPTIMAL and start off as best as I can and avoid screwing up. I have been VLC for 4 weeks now and am down 19 lbs (only 100 to go!) but from all the reading I've been doing I still have questions as to what some no-no's are for Optimal.

### TerrierMom — 2012-03-22T21:46:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-food-questions.366/post-4619>

also.... is vinegar bad?? and if so, why. (or is it just bad for leaky gut folks).

what do you put on a salad? I was going to go Olive Oil/Balsamic but I would want to know if vinegar is ok first.

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-22T22:46:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-food-questions.366/post-4635>

> **Krusing\_tptimal\_in\_CA;4582 wrote:**
>
> The dumb questions are: lots of folks have Bacon in their BAB, but is it super-special bacon or just the kind you can get from any store? Is sugar-cured a problem? nitrites? Same question on other meats. I know, I know, these are PROCESSED meats so I am guessing it's gonna be a NO to them. If I can't get grassfed meet, is grocery store meat going to set me back? (Sisson says pick LEAN and trim the fat if not grassfed).
>
>
>
> Also I saw in Sisson's book a NO to smoked meats. Well we have a smoker in the backyard and smoke our own meats. Is smoking meat BAD for this lifestyle? It is so much flavor! I went ON VACATION the second week of VLC and took little pouches of pulled pork (no sauce of course) for my lunch and weight continued to come off..
>
>
>
> But, I want to get to OPTIMAL and start off as best as I can and avoid screwing up. I have been VLC for 4 weeks now and am down 19 lbs (only 100 to go!) but from all the reading I've been doing I still have questions as to what some no-no's are for Optimal.

• Bacon...check the label (often sugar is left off the nutrition facts but is in the ingredient list). I buy Wrights "hickory smoked" at Publix

• Not sure if it is legal, but I eat smoked meats & pepperoni all the time.

• Salad dressing - I've read Dr. Kruse mention vinegar is "not optimal," but I doubt it's a deal breaker. I use lemon juice instead.

• Legal oils - Coconut oil, Palm oil, Macadamia nut oil (best substitute for Olive oil IMO), Rendered bacon fat.

• Staples at the deli counter (all Boars Head brand): Ovenroast gold turkey, Sweet slice ham, London Broil, Top round roast beef, pepperoni, liverwurst, corned beef brisket,Bi'anco D'oro salami, pepperoni.

### sandy — 2012-03-23T01:33:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-food-questions.366/post-4730>

I am curious about the vinegar, because i thought he recommended eating pickles? whats wrong with vinegar?

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-23T01:49:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-food-questions.366/post-4737>

Come to think of it...I eat pickles too. I've only read Dr. Kruse mention vinegar being "not optimal" one time in an early blog post, (or possibly a comment on another paleo forum). I guess I'd put it in the "not too harmful" category.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-23T02:25:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-food-questions.366/post-4755>

> **Krusing\_tptimal\_in\_CA;4582 wrote:
>
> The dumb questions are: lots of folks have Bacon in their BAB, but is it super-special bacon or just the kind you can get from any store? Is sugar-cured a problem? nitrites? Same question on other meats. I know, I know, these are PROCESSED meats so I am guessing it's gonna be a NO to them. If I can't get grassfed meet, is grocery store meat going to set me back? (Sisson says pick LEAN and trim the fat if not grassfed).
>
>
>
> Also I saw in Sisson's book a NO to smoked meats. Well we have a smoker in the backyard and smoke our own meats. Is smoking meat BAD for this lifestyle? It is so much flavor! I went ON VACATION the second week of VLC and took little pouches of pulled pork (no sauce of course) for my lunch and weight continued to come off..
>
>
>
> But, I want to get to OPTIMAL and start off as best as I can and avoid screwing up. I have been VLC for 4 weeks now and am down 19 lbs (only 100 to go!) but from all the reading I've been doing I still have questions as to what some no-no's are for Optimal.
>
>
> With your blood sugar history, I'd avoid sugar in meats, at least to begin with. My **bacon comes from the pastured pig I buy from a local farmer. YURM!!!!! Anyway, back to the discussion ....
>
>
>
> I personally doubt **nitrates/nitrites are a big problem. They're traditional. Are there too much in store meats? IDK, maybe. I wouldn't hesitate to cure my own meats with them, though. All "uncured" meats use something like celery seed & lactic acid to form their own sodium nitrate; I don't see the difference.
>
>
>
> I agree that **if you can't get grassfed meats, get lean ones, and see if you can't order lard/tallow/duck fat from a local or online farm, plus coconut oil, etc., to make up for the missing fat.
>
>
>
> NO PROBLEM WHATSOEVER with **smoking your own meat!!!! It's the storebought smoked stuff that's questionable.
>
>
>
>
> > **Krusing\_tptimal\_in\_CA;4647 wrote:** also.... is vinegar bad?? and if so, why. (or is it just bad for leaky gut folks).
> >
> >
> >
> > what do you put on a salad? I was going to go Olive Oil/Balsamic but I would want to know if vinegar is ok first.
>
>
>
> sandy;4758 wrote:** I am curious about the vinegar, because i thought he recommended eating pickles? whats wrong with vinegar?********

********

I haven't seen his position on vinegar**, but I assume it's not far off from mine. Avoid white vinegar (for eating; I clean with it, lol); it's usually made from corn or some such. Avoid most balsamic vinegars; "caramel coloring" = sugar. I buy TJ's that has none of that nonsense in it. I can't see any way that raw apple cider vinegar (I take 1T in a glass of water) or authentic balsamic vinegar (a teaspoon in my salad) could be harmful. (I'm a leaky gut folk.)

Avoid olive oil** if you're on the leptin reset. Afterwards, a little bit on salad is fine; don't cook in it (it's too unstable) or go overboard (too many O6's for most of us). Make sure it's real olive oil (there's a serious problem with fake stuff out there); it should get solid in the fridge over a few days/weeks.

Pickles** - you want REAL pickles. That means fermented. So here's the short version of the story - nearly every culture on earth ferments vegetables**. That means you put them in a jar with water & salt and leave them at room temperature for days or weeks, which turns the sugars in them into lactic acid, which makes them (and the liquid) tart, and preserves the veggie. These were called pickled vegetables.

Enter the Industrial Revolution. Factories need a consistent product (fermentation results depend on which bacteria strain happens to be on your veggie), and they need it tart, preserved, and cheap. Vinegar does the job. So they just pour vinegar in, but of course, if you don't boil it, you kill people, so they boil it. Now housewives are used to the idea of vinegar pickles (they still use the word pickle) and they do it. Problem is, vinegar pickling takes a lot more time & effort, kills many nutrients and all the beneficial bacteria, and doesn't taste as wonderfully complex. Fermentation is easy, \*increases\* nutrients (seriously), and makes a probiotic product.

So don't buy Vlassics, okay? Buy Bubbies, or google "fermented pickles" and make your own. It's just 2T unrefined sea salt per quart of veggies, with (filtered) water poured over them and a lid, for 3 days to 3 weeks (your preference). Couldn't be simpler!

### TerrierMom — 2012-03-24T17:47:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-food-questions.366/post-5445>

Thanks MamaGrok... Went and got Bubbies yesterday sauerkraut and dills and relish too... Will avoid OO but mine is locally sourced great stuff as is the balsamic, will avoid for now.

Thx for the answers, I knew a veteran would know. Now to find some pastured bacon... Already have a 1/4 beef on the way!
