# Thread: Deoderant?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 47
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoSue — 2012-03-24T02:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5246>

Hi, everyone. I was wondering what you all use for deoderant? Are you concerned about the aluminum in the standard stuff?

### L8F — 2012-03-24T02:43:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5257>

I think about it, but then just use it anyway. I love my Fa deodorant. I have tried natural ones, but most have given me a rash for some reason, and don't work either. Also the crystals at our store all have aluminum in them as well. I am not that paleo...I also wear heels, and use many cosmetics that are not quite chemical free. I might not be the best to answer this now that I have just written all that.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-24T02:52:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5262>

I use deodorant, not anti-perspirant. I also stink a LOT less now that I got the trace amount of wheat out of my diet ... hmmm, interesting. I have heard many times stories of people whose farmer grandfathers never stank, despite constant outside work and no deodorant.

I've used

\- a random natural brand (didn't work)

\- my husband's Old Spice (deodorant only, worked, but permanently stained my clothes with their stupid blue color!)

\- Burt's Bees men's deodorant (worked)

\- Tom's (worked)

\- vinegar (worked for about a year, don't know why it didn't work as well toward the end, had to re-apply after or just before heavy work)

\- alcohol (only when I have nothing else, works)

\- combination of 50:50 baking soda & coconut oil (works great)

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-24T02:54:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5263>

I am absolutely concerned about the mainstream stuff and would stay away. There are many crystal deodorants that are very safe, as well as other alternatives like a bit of coconut oil and some baking soda. What you put on your skin you also put into your body. As someone once said to me, if you wouldn't eat it, don't wear it. But that's just me. (Btw, I refuse to 'stink' and am very very conscious of body odor. Between safe deodorants, perfume oils etc and a very clean diet, I smell good! My hubby will vouch for me.  )

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-24T03:36:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5277>

I was using TOMs and found it wasn\`t as good as I would have liked for smell. switched to crystal and pretty happy with this one so far

### Dextery — 2012-03-24T03:54:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5284>

For about 9 months I have been using baking soda for my washing soap and for my deodorant. The baking soda works as a scrubbing agent on a wash clothe as opposed to a soap agent that encapsulates dirt particles and carries them away. I like it because there are not unpronouncible words on the label I also use it for cleaning hair.

Some people have said the baking soda feels like rocks under their arms but I am not that sensitive. I use arm and hammer that I buy at Costco about every 18 months 13 pounds for about $6.00.

Having said that, many people just use plain water and a wash cloth and no deodorant. I have noticed that I do not really need the baking soda as I don't seem to stink at all any more on a ketogenic diet. But at first, keto breathe was a real pain but it has gone away after a couple years keto.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-24T04:10:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5297>

I don't use anything but then again I hardly sweat and I used to sweat a lot. My BF has been using baking soda and magnesium oil for about a year. The baking soda helps the smell and the magnesium helps the sweat. The magnesium actually keeps me from sweating at all (OK, that was back in the sweaty days). BF has switched to baking soda and lavender oil because he wants to expel the toxins in sweat.

### chewingthefat — 2012-03-24T04:38:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5311>

I use coconut oil, let it absorb a bit, then, the crystal deodorant. I've asked DH and kids...they haven't mentioned a problem since I started 5-6 months ago. Also, confided to my co-worker, asked her to let me know if and when she notices a change in aroma and she has not...yet. Hope it works in the summer! Never use any moisturizer than coconut oil. It's anti-bacterial properties might be helpful here.

### L8F — 2012-03-24T04:44:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5317>

all great ideas!! thanks for the tips. i might give it a whirl.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-24T20:16:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5511>

I made a homemade deodorant in the past that worked really well. I got lazy when it ran out and just use a mix of coconut oil and baking soda now w/ a little lavender EO. Super quick and easy. I actually use something very similar for toothpaste too. I try to make all our personal care products these days . . . yep, total hippie here!

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-24T23:13:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5559>

I make a paste of CO and Baking Soda too. It is amazing!

### hazyjane — 2012-03-25T01:19:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5602>

I usually use fresh lemon juice (it does sting if I've just shaved!)

I also like Dr. Mist, which is a Dead Sea salt spray. It works better than anything else I've ever used, but I get lazy about ordering it online and it's a little pricey. I still smell good with it on day 2 though!

### healthnut — 2012-03-25T01:47:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5613>

Old fashioned calamine lotion. Zinc oxide. French white clay powder.

### L8F — 2012-03-25T02:01:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5622>

OMG, so many options! I had no idea. I thought I was stuck with nasty, ineffective jason brand at the health food store that gives me a rash. Thank you all for these excellent ideas. Sounds like lots of stuff will work. Going to try mag oil first since I have a gallon.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-25T05:40:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5680>

I use a rock crystal thing. Works well. I don't smell since going primal though.

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-25T07:33:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5718>

I have been using [this](http://http://www.vitacost.com/alvera-all-natural-roll-on-deodorant-aloe-unscented) for over a year. I works great for odors but does not help with sweat. I may be changing to a more natural alternative in the future.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-25T16:43:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5779>

I've tried Tom's, which didn't work well enough for my liking. The crystal stuff gave me a rash as well, and honestly, contain aliminum themselves. Ive also used the coconut oil/baking soda concoction, and it did work well. I didn't like the slight residue it left, but perhaps I was over applying. I'm back to the hypoallergenic Dry Idea gel, as I have sensitive skin and and it's just easier.

I may give the coconut oil another try now that the weather is warmer.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-25T16:44:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-5782>

I kept a small container premixed in the bathroom. Just apply with the fingertips - however, it does harden/soften with temp changes.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-26T01:41:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-6096>

I'm curious: How much oil to baking soda?

I'm currently using the Crystal, and have been pleasantly surprised that I think I actually smell better than when I was using standard deodorant/antiperspirant. But there is still a little bit of smell.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-26T02:21:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-6126>

The recipe I've used is 1/4 cup corn starch, 1/4 cup baking soda, and 5 tablespoons coconut oi.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-26T21:21:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-6510>

> **hazyjane;5636 wrote:** I usually use fresh lemon juice (it does sting if I've just shaved!)
>
> I also like Dr. Mist, which is a Dead Sea salt spray. It works better than anything else I've ever used, but I get lazy about ordering it online and it's a little pricey. I still smell good with it on day 2 though!

This is easy to make at home - and mucho cheap! I also use it on wet hair to bring out the natural curls and tame frizzies.

### TerrierMom — 2012-10-20T05:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59284>

This sounds great.... Thx for all the ideas, Especially shampoo... Haven't gone there yet but may!

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-20T07:06:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59294>

After a few years being cleanly keto paleo, I only use baking soda as deodorant. As long as my diet is clean (no sugar, etc), the only smell I produce is a normal human musk that baking soda easily takes care of for times when the natural human smell isn't socially appropriate. And no, I don't mean "stinky" by that, I mean a slight, "clean" but animal-like smell. If I do eat any sugar or wheat type foodstuff, then my sweat gets a burnt/sour smell like it did back in my pre-paleo days.

I've also completely ditched all shampoo,conditioner, and soap. After a brief transition period using baking sofa and apple cider vinegar, my skin and hair are self-maintenancing with only water.

Toothpaste got chucked for baking soda and the occasional hydrogen peroxide. I will also occasionally do oil-pulling with coconut oil.

Baking soda is wonderful, versatile thing.

### Endless — 2012-10-20T09:34:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59299>

This stuff works amazingly well but gives me a rash.... I have sensitive skin though.

### Dali Dula — 2012-10-20T15:00:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59318>

I have been using mag oil that i make from nigari crystals. I rub down entire body including pits after CT. Mag makes you feel good and is an effective deodorant.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-21T01:28:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59405>

> **Dali Dula;60861 wrote:** I have been using mag oil that i make from nigari crystals. I rub down entire body including pits after CT. Mag makes you feel good and is an effective deodorant.

Can you say more about how to make the oil, Dali Dula? I've always wanted to try the mag oil but it's super expensive. Thanks!

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-21T06:38:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59435>

I've been using crystal deodorant for twenty years now - its so effective. I think its really important not to put chemicals under the arms, but also i was very conscious when i was breastfeeding my babies not to have strong smells there as their little noses where so close

### Dali Dula — 2012-10-22T19:46:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59669>

> **KiwiLauren;60952 wrote:** Can you say more about how to make the oil, Dali Dula? I've always wanted to try the mag oil but it's super expensive. Thanks!

I buy nigari at the local co-op. It is available online. Very inexpensive- 3$ for a month's supply and I use it liberally. Nigari is a white crystal that is the byproduct of desalination of sea water. This is what is left after they extract sodium chloride from sea water. It is magnesium chloride with trace minerals. Basically all the good ocean minerals without sodium. It is used as a coagulant in the making of tofu. So buy a few dollars worth and supersaturate it.Put the crystals in a jar fill water to top of crystals, shake, let sit overnight, it should turn completely clear. If there are undissolved crystals in the bottom, shake and let sit again.This result is strong mag oil. I use it full strength. If you have sensitive skin you can water it down. It burns in a cut or on chafed skin. I rub it all over my body. I also add a teaspoon or so to a glass of water twice a day. If this loosens the stool cut back on the ingested amount till your good. Inside and out it makes you feel good.

### nonchalant — 2012-10-22T20:35:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59680>

> **Cavemam;6546 wrote:** This is easy to make at home - and mucho cheap! I also use it on wet hair to bring out the natural curls and tame frizzies.

Cavemam, were you saying the sea salt spray is easy to make? Can you elaborate on how you use it? I'm interested in trying it.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-22T21:00:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59686>

> **Nonchalant;61248 wrote:** Cavemam, were you saying the sea salt spray is easy to make? Can you elaborate on how you use it? I'm interested in trying it.

Super easy! Just dissolve at about a 6/1 ratio of sea salt to water then put in a spray bottle. Spray and scrunch! Beautiful curls!

The method that works best is leave your hair kinda wet from the shower then spray liberally, comb with wide comb and just let it air dry. . .think "beach hair", like how nice the curls look when you come out of the ocean and it just dries from wet with no help from you.

What I don't know is if it dries out the hair (salty mineral laden hair) so I don't use it very often. It's gotta be tons better than the chemical factories. I use it when I need to look good an curly.

On ratio's, I've done anywhere from a 4/1 - 10/1

I think letting the hair dry itself without you fussing with it is the best trick.

### MamaGrok — 2012-10-22T21:30:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59691>

> **CuiviÃ«;60836 wrote: After a few years being cleanly keto paleo, I only use baking soda as deodorant. As long as my diet is clean (no sugar, etc), the only smell I produce is a normal human musk that baking soda easily takes care of for times when the natural human smell isn't socially appropriate. And no, I don't mean "stinky" by that, I mean a slight, "clean" but animal-like smell. If I do eat any sugar or wheat type foodstuff, then my sweat gets a burnt/sour smell like it did back in my pre-paleo days.
>
>
> Absolutely ditto here.

>
> I also stopped washing my hair 5 years ago. No shampoo since. Among the wonders (for instance, after a night in a smoky bar, my hair smells completely clean by morning - same after tons of hairspray for a wedding - it cleans itself), my board-straight hair developed a natural wave, like the ocean curl cavemam described above. It does get waxy sometimes and then I wash with ACV & BS.
>
>
>
> I love camping even more now. Just bring CO & Bkg Sda & I'm good for a week or more! Skin, pits, hair, teeth, all good.
>
>
>
> Dali Dula;61237 wrote:** I buy nigari at the local co-op. It is available online. Very inexpensive- 3$ for a month's supply and I use it liberally. Nigari is a white crystal that is the byproduct of desalination of sea water. This is what is left after they extract sodium chloride from sea water. It is magnesium chloride with trace minerals. Basically all the good ocean minerals without sodium. It is used as a coagulant in the making of tofu. So buy a few dollars worth and supersaturate it.Put the crystals in a jar fill water to top of crystals, shake, let sit overnight, it should turn completely clear. If there are undissolved crystals in the bottom, shake and let sit again.This result is strong mag oil. I use it full strength. If you have sensitive skin you can water it down. It burns in a cut or on chafed skin. I rub it all over my body. I also add a teaspoon or so to a glass of water twice a day. If this loosens the stool cut back on the ingested amount till your good. Inside and out it makes you feel good.

Great description, thanks!

### ealachan — 2012-10-22T21:43:37+0300

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-23T00:24:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59724>

> **Dali Dula;61237 wrote:** I buy nigari at the local co-op. It is available online. Very inexpensive- 3$ for a month's supply and I use it liberally. Nigari is a white crystal that is the byproduct of desalination of sea water. This is what is left after they extract sodium chloride from sea water. It is magnesium chloride with trace minerals. Basically all the good ocean minerals without sodium. It is used as a coagulant in the making of tofu. So buy a few dollars worth and supersaturate it.Put the crystals in a jar fill water to top of crystals, shake, let sit overnight, it should turn completely clear. If there are undissolved crystals in the bottom, shake and let sit again.This result is strong mag oil. I use it full strength. If you have sensitive skin you can water it down. It burns in a cut or on chafed skin. I rub it all over my body. I also add a teaspoon or so to a glass of water twice a day. If this loosens the stool cut back on the ingested amount till your good. Inside and out it makes you feel good.

Wow, thank you! Brilliant.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-23T00:41:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59733>

> **ealachan;61261 wrote:** I think I missed it somewhere along the way - what are the benefits of using magnesium oil, again? Is it just an additional way to supplement, or are there skin benefits as well?

+1 on learning more on this. I have some leftover from hubby's surgery and I need motivation to slather it on. I think it has a wierd feeling so I've not been doing it.

### Dali Dula — 2012-10-23T15:19:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deoderant.388/post-59804>

> **ealachan;61261 wrote:** I think I missed it somewhere along the way - what are the benefits of using magnesium oil, again? Is it just an additional way to supplement, or are there skin benefits as well?

I am T2D so i need all the mag I can get. I take 800mg malate,800 mg threonate, 2 teaspoons mag oil orally and I apply mag oil on skin for transdermal benefit. Mag oil is not oil, but has a viscous feel on the skin. It is completely water soluble. If the concentration is high it might leave a chalky residue, if so thin it out. I don't know that it's good for skin, but it really makes you feel good soon after application.

We make the oil from crystals and make it very concentrated to maximize the effectiveness.

It tastes terrible but DH has been swishing it in his mouth at night for around 5 min and he has absolutely no plaque buildup on his lower bottom teeth. I'm trying to do it regularly but it really tastes BAD.

# Thread: Rice....opinions?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rice-opinions.389/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PrimalPam — 2012-03-24T04:13:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rice-opinions.389/post-5298>

Is rice optimal or not? I read differing opinions. Why is white rice different than brown rice vis a vis Paleo?

I ate sushi today (a lot), and I felt so sluggish this afternoon I actually dozed off for the first time in a long time since I started LR. I used to doze off in the afternoon almost every day; today felt like that again.

### LinD — 2012-03-24T04:58:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rice-opinions.389/post-5320>

I wonder if that answers your question about rice? Did you eat it with the sushi?

Sent from my Sprint Samsung Galaxy S II Epic.

### TerrierMom — 2012-03-24T16:28:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rice-opinions.389/post-5410>

No grains no legumes, right?

While white rice likely has less of the "antinutrients" without its outer bran, we're still not designed to digest it.

Stick to sashimi. I go to sushi every Fri night for years now, just now I eat a sashimi salad and sashimi. Now and then I'll have a white cucumber roll which uses cukes as the wrapper with tuna and avocado in the middle (skip the fake crab, LOADED with sugar!).

Also my place makes a Redeye roll which is wrapped with tuna with avo n other fish inside and some veggies.

Careful with the sauces they squirt in too, eel sauce is really sweet.

I'm guessing soy sauce isn't optimal either, not only is it a legume to begin with but really processed!

### LinD — 2012-03-24T17:33:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rice-opinions.389/post-5438>

Soy sauce likely includes gluten...

Sent from my Sprint Samsung Galaxy S II Epic.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-24T17:41:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rice-opinions.389/post-5441>

> **PrimalPam;5331 wrote:** Is rice optimal or not? I read differing opinions. Why is white rice different than brown rice vis a vis Paleo?
>
>
>
> I ate sushi today (a lot), and I felt so sluggish this afternoon I actually dozed off for the first time in a long time since I started LR. I used to doze off in the afternoon almost every day; today felt like that again.

The whole CT thing is about Ketogenic. Rice (even if it ends up being safe, which Dr. K disagrees with) would most assuredly not gel with CT. It's working on a pathway that is either on or off. Carbs turn it off - no safe starches for this method.

# Thread: Optimal Pool
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-pool.390/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2012-03-24T04:28:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-pool.390/post-5303>

So I've been reading on a site called troublefreepool.com and they hate Ecosmarte because they say it doesn't sanitize well enough. I was considering getting a system for my pool at some point but not sure...I like the fact you don't have to use chlorine to kill algae. Is sanitation really a huge issue in a residential pool that will at most have 5 people in it.

I'm thinking that isn't a huge issue. I have heard you have to monitor ph and some stuff more because of stain issues. Also if anyone has an opinion on pool filter types or pumps I'd love to hear about it. I know of DE, Sand, Sand Filter with GlassPack Media, and Cartridge. I've heard the filter type you want is determined by what kind of things will get in your pool. I live in Lewisville, Texas if that helps any with the what filter to buy. My brother was going to get a 3.0 horsepower variable speed pump for faster cleaning and probably run it slower for general operation.

Supposedly in a properly maintained pool chlorine doesn't have any negatives...I don't think I buy that, but don't know enough since I'm new to pools.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-25T19:10:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-pool.390/post-5873>

What type of pool do you own? Gunnite? Vinyl liner? In or above ground? How many gallons?

How do you currently sanitize?

We have an inground gunnite pool, and troublefreepool.com is a good resource. I personally do feel that sanitation is a big issue, and I'm not convinced that the chlorine free alternatives do a great job - in addition, you're generally advised to use an algaecide...which is basically a liquid herbicide that you add to the water. I'm more concerned with that.

Pool chemistry is a delicate dance with a few parameters, including pH, alkalinity, stabilizer, and chlorine.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-03-26T07:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-pool.390/post-6261>

Its an in ground Gunnite pool with 22-23,000 gallons and its sanitized currently with Chlorine. Well with the Ecosmarte system killing algae is done with the copper if I remember right. From what I read the only issue with a properly maintained Ecosmarte system is it isn't super fast at killing organisms so it isn't great on the sanitation issue. I don't see how its a major issue unless you have sick people constantly swimming in it...for a commercial pool or even maybe a residential pool with lots of guests I could see this as an issue.

My family doesn't get sick all that often though and I just don't see how it could be that big a deal unless someone who was going to swim was immunocompromised. I guess if we start swimming a ton maybe the bacteria and such will build up but if the body is working properly ...I'm not sure it can't deal with things fine. I guess maybe I just want it not to matter...because I like the idea of Chlorine free so much.

That being said even if sanitation isn't that big a deal whether we can properly maintain an Ecosmarte system or not is not clear at this point. At the moment we don't have the money for the 4-5k system anyway so we'll be using Chlorine for the time being.

# Thread: Prevastatin side affects - need diet help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prevastatin-side-affects-need-diet-help.391/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Justfourme — 2012-03-24T04:32:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prevastatin-side-affects-need-diet-help.391/post-5306>

I have 2 siblings who have had major side affects from prevastatin that include severe muscle weakness. One stopped the drug after 30 days but is still suffering. One took for a couple months and has more severe complications. Both were put on prednisone. One is now entirely off that. One has transitioned from prednisone to methotrexate. I have both on a paleo type diet, supplementing with B vitamins, Qco10, potassium. One now seems to be also super sensitive to sodium - was drinking Gatorade to boost potassium and electrolytes which I had her stop as soon as I found out about that. Stopped using salt entirely.

What other diet changes or supplements can I recommend?

### Greg rx — 2012-03-24T15:49:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prevastatin-side-affects-need-diet-help.391/post-5394>

check out [www.spacedoc.net](http://http://www.spacedoc.net) It is a huge site dedicated to statin damage. It seems that some of us will be permanently damaged. Pray that the damage does not include the heart and lung muscles. A couple quick suggestions, change Coq10 to the reduced form Ubiquinol and L carnitine.

Pharmacist whose life was ruined by lipitor.

# Thread: Optimizing for CrossFit
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-for-crossfit.392/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SnuffySmith — 2012-03-24T05:50:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-for-crossfit.392/post-5331>

OK, I follow OPT and am pretty pumped up about Dr. Kruse. Someone on the OPT blog posted a link a couple days ago and I can't get enough of the content on here.

This post is all about trying to understand how to optimize the Kruse prescription for CrossFit performance. I have great coaching from OPT ... actually starting personal coaching with one of his trainers soon. I follow a paleo diet, but I eat starchy carbs and have a post workout recovery shake with variable amounts of carbs depending on workout intensity.

Piecing the prescriptions on here together, I should do the following:

Eliminate the post workout shake. Eliminate all starchy carbs. Increase fat intake. Decrease number of meals to 2 or 3. Do a 30 minute ice bath before bed every night.

Is this accurate? Is there more I could do to improve performance?

What about working out in the morning? OPT frequently has more than one workout a day ... if there's more than one, I'll do one in the AM and the other in the PM. Does that really matter?

What results should I expect? From what I have read: the diet changes may help a little (because of similar current diet), and the ice bath can take 24-36 months to show results. Will it really take this long to experience a difference on the ice baths? Any other suggestions?

Another question about the cold adaptation stuff ... when talking about Phelps and Armstrong, it sounds like they were exposed during training, not before or after. Does the bath after training have the same effect? Can someone talk to this ... I mean, do I need to step up the A/C to get the results?

Thanks,

Snuffy

### Dextery — 2012-03-25T07:30:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-for-crossfit.392/post-5716>

> **SnuffySmith;5364 wrote:** This post is all about trying to understand how to optimize the Kruse prescription for CrossFit performance. I have great coaching from OPT ... actually starting personal coaching with one of his trainers soon. I follow a paleo diet, but I eat starchy carbs and have a post workout recovery shake with variable amounts of carbs depending on workout intensity.
>
>
> Dr Kruse is not a big fan of crossfit because it beats the body up and does not allow the body to recover adequately. And it shortens one's telomeres by inducing inflammation. And short telomeres means short lives.
>
>
>
>
> > Piecing the prescriptions on here together, I should do the following:
> >
> > Eliminate the post workout shake. Eliminate all starchy carbs. Increase fat intake. Decrease number of meals to 2 or 3. Do a 30 minute ice bath before bed every night.
> >
> >
> >
> > Is this accurate? Is there more I could do to improve performance?
> >
> >
> > Eliminate the wheat and sugar and starchy carbs forever in your diet and turn yourself into a fat burning machine from what you have been burning..ie a carb/sugar burning machine. And people who do enough cold thermogenesis are seeing tremendous strength increases without working out...but one day a week for less than 20 min.
> >
> > See this thread [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?356-Are-you-doing-chronic-exercise-that-will-cause-you-fail](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?356-Are-you-doing-chronic-exercise-that-will-cause-you-fail)
> >
> > There are two videos...one by Dr McGuff and one by Drew Baye. If you really want to get strong, follow their routine and ditch the crossfit madness conventional wisdom thinking that "more is better".
> >
> >
> >
> >
> > > What about working out in the morning? OPT frequently has more than one workout a day ... if there's more than one, I'll do one in the AM and the other in the PM. Does that really matter?
> >
> >
> >
> > When doing a workout, optimal time is 3-6pm according to Dr. K.
> >
> >
> >
> > What results should I expect? From what I have read: the diet changes may help a little (because of similar current diet), and the ice bath can take 24-36 months to show results. Will it really take this long to experience a difference on the ice baths? Any other suggestions?
>
> People are showing strength results in 4 weeks but since CT is additive...the more you do, the stronger you get.
>
>
>
> Another question about the cold adaptation stuff ... when talking about Phelps and Armstrong, it sounds like they were exposed during training, not before or after. Does the bath after training have the same effect? Can someone talk to this ... I mean, do I need to step up the A/C to get the results?

Dr K has a cold room that has no lights in it except for a red reading light that has no blue wavelength. He drives with AC in his car on full blast winter and summer and he drinks lots of cold water as there is a receptor in our mouths for cold.

The whole premise is that cold adapted people operate more efficiently with greater clarity of mind when the CT is combined with the Leptin Prescription and the Leaky Gut Prescription.

Can you do crossfit and be successful in the short term. Sure. You will look really good on the outside...but you will damage your long term survivability by shortening your telomeres. You can do crossfit if you want...but should you is the question...especially when Dr McGuff's Body by Science routines yield better results with 20 min a week work to failure as compared to the WODs 3x per week. It is hard to believe...but it does work. Fredrick Hahn in NYC also imploys this method with great success.

Good luck on whatever path you take...CW or less is more.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-27T18:22:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-for-crossfit.392/post-7067>

I think OPT would be the first one to say that if you are truly dialed in to the entire nutrition, lifestyle realm that you will realize there are choices to be made. One is longevity vs near term performance. You can certainly work with crossfit and many of the optimal principals here, BUT it depends on how you are programming the exercise... more of a traditional crossfit approach with separate strength and metcon days is going to work better than two a days. I so sympathize with you because Crossfit is so amazing, it is hard to see how to dose it to maximize performance. I have had the "benefit" of reaching the point where my recovery sucked and my peformance was decreasing. That forced me to step back and find the balance that is right for me. The Games are NOT in my cross hairs - never have been, never will be. If they are for you... go for it. I found that I as Leptin resistant - and pretty much screwing myself with intense workouts (find Dr. K's post on why Oprah is still fat).

I am now in the post reset phase where you start lifting heavy again and start to add back HIIT. I'm finding that hard to do in a box right now with so much focus on the Open, peformance, and the "sport of fitness". So, I would say start back at square one and set your goals and then develop the programming that meets the goal. Making a serious run at the Games maybe pretty much like running that marathon... everyone should do it once or twice... and then settle down into fine tuning the body for living a long, strong, playful life. And that isn't sissy play either... look at Erwan LeCorre's videos.

Love crossfit, love my box and peeps. Loving the optimal approach. It is hard to make it all work together unless the coaches at your box embrace it.

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-24T13:22:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-while-traveling.396/post-5362>

I have a business trip next week to Vegas of all places and I'm wondering what you all eat for BAB on the road. I've been avoiding eggs and would like to continue, but I might just have to go back to that traditional protein for breakfast unless I can come up with an idea of something else. I'll not have a car, so it'll be whatever the hotel offers, what is within walking distance, or what I can pack in my suitcase. I'd love to hear your thoughts and suggestions!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-24T15:08:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-while-traveling.396/post-5380>

Great question...I have to travel in a week, so I am thinking about this too. I will be staying with relatives, which in some ways makes it easier but in other ways harder. My grandmother is so opinionated! I am afraid lunch and dinner may be more difficult (timing wise). I'm not doing eggs either, and for complicated reasons, I may not have access to the kitchen whenever I want to start cooking bacon...LOL. But I was thinking I'll just pack a lot of canned salmon. Maybe sausage that I could microwave in a pinch. I purchased canned herring but cannot bring myself to try it yet. I also am going to pack my crockpot. That will probably help. The upside is I am going to Maine - lots of seafood!

But for you, I bet you can get steak and poached salmon for breakfast. A lot of places offer those items. And Vegas - must have all night buffets?

### TerrierMom — 2012-03-24T16:20:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-while-traveling.396/post-5404>

> [JeffieJoe said:](/goto/post?id=0)
>
> I have a business trip next week to Vegas of all places and I'm wondering what you all eat for BAB on the road. I've been avoiding eggs and would like to continue, but I might just have to go back to that traditional protein for breakfast unless I can come up with an idea of something else. "
>
>
>
> Ever see steak and eggs on a breakfast menu? I always thought that was weird but not anymore! Get the steak, hold the eggs.
>
>
>
> I know you don't eat eggs but for others reading this thread, back 10 yrs ago on my VLC journey I'd get 2 sausage mcmuffins w egg and use the sausage as a 'bun' with the eggs in the middle. 42g protein. It works in a pinch anyway!
>
>
>
> Or a side of breakfast meats at a coffee shop. Even a burger sans bun....
>
>
>
> Also if you are traveling.. At home we fry up 2 lb of bacon on the weekend and put it in a giant ziplock bag and eat from it all week. Did that on a weeklong trip this month already too, saves time in the morning.
>
>
>
> Hope something there helped!

### Pamela — 2012-03-24T16:21:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-while-traveling.396/post-5405>

I'm traveling this week to Vegas also for a trade show and have been thinking about how I'll do the BAB and Paleo while I'm there. Here is what I've thought of so far. For Breakfast I will eat in the hotel restaurant and have steak and eggs. Since I'm not eating eggs right now I will give the eggs to my mother coming with me. If the steak isn't enough, which is probably isn't, then I'll add a side of sausage or bacon even though I know its not nitrate free. For lunches (since the trade show food is the most yucko ever) I'm bringing some things: grass fed jerkey in small packages, canned salmon with pop off lids that I found at Trader Joe's, as well as a can of Tuna from WF with pop of lid. I also have some grass fed beef sticks from US Wellness that I'm going to stick in my suitcase with an ice bar thingy inside a thermal bag I have and keep those in the fridge of the hotel room.

For dinner, this should be easy since LV has a huge amount of restaurants and awesome buffets with all the beef, seafood, and veggies you could want.

I'm going to bring my own green tea bags and stevia packets.

Hope this helps.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-24T16:30:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-while-traveling.396/post-5411>

I travel at least once a month. Since I don't eat eggs, I precook some 10 oz hamburgers (combo of beef, bacon, and liver) and pack them in my carry-on. I keep them in the fridge at the hotel for my BAB. I usually don't have a problem with dinners. I've done this 3x now and it has worked out great!

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-25T16:37:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-while-traveling.396/post-5773>

Thanks for all the great ideas!

# Thread: Yes!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yes.397/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-24T14:53:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yes.397/post-5376>

You can find my back story scattered around here - mostly in the meet/greet - but I will post more to follow. But today I absolutely must take a moment to note that I'm at 158 lbs this am. So what, I'm sure you may say since I am 47 @ 5'8 and my optimal weight is probably somewhere in the high 140s. But this is the first time in a DECADE that I have been below 160.

Props to my CF box - the home I've had for 4 years where I've heard the good word about nutrition and learned to love lifting really heavy things

Props to Mark Sisson - who has helped me change my mental outlook to one of living in my body vice beating it into submission and for having that breadth of understanding that not every body is the same body. What works for one, will need tweaking for the next

Props to Robb Wolf - for making Paleo fun science and mentioning the Leptin Reset in a podcast.

and the biggest cheer to Dr. K who has put the circadian structure on my life, validated my hatred for hot weather, and really started to make me well. After yo-yo'ing in the 165 - 173 range for 3 years, I'm shedding it, sleeping, saying goodbye to PMDD, nixing the cognitive fog, and getting motivated to live another 50 years.

On 19 May, my oldest daughter is going to be commissioned an officer in the USMC, just like her mom was 25 years ago. My dream is to stand next to her on that day within USMC ht/wt standards - that is just 2 lbs away and so very much within reach. Dr K... you get a very loud and thunderous OOORAAAAH!

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-24T15:09:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yes.397/post-5381>

HoooAhhh! Awesome job! Congrats on dd following in mummy's combat boots!!! I would be lucky if eithere of mine choose to follow my footsteps and go to USAFA. Of course I'd be happy if either decided to go into the military. I'd glady salute them! Are you doing the actual commissioning & pinning on???? I remember when my uncle did that for me.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-24T15:53:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yes.397/post-5396>

> **Shijin13;5415 wrote:** HoooAhhh! Awesome job! Congrats on dd following in mummy's combat boots!!! I would be lucky if eithere of mine choose to follow my footsteps and go to USAFA. Of course I'd be happy if either decided to go into the military. I'd glady salute them! Are you doing the actual commissioning & pinning on???? I remember when my uncle did that for me.

Shijin - you can only guess how closely she's followed. NROTC scholarship to Notre Dame - my alma mater. Plan is to run a 5k with her around the twin lakes early in the am before commissioning. Her dad - who went to Nebraska - actually stayed in long enough to retire, so I think deferring to him to do the swearing in is appropriate. That said, I was an adjutant by MOS and have read the warrant at many a ceremony and I may ask to do the same here. The pinning is definitely one a piece to Mom and Dad. The first salute will come from her brother who is also a midshipman. Still investigating to see if I can swing it for a set of real gold butter bars. I didn't have them, and was dumb and didn't preserve the set I wore that day.

### Birdy — 2012-03-24T19:50:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yes.397/post-5496>

congrats on all of your accomplishments

The three years I was stationed at Camp Pendleton were really hard. This girl from upstate NY needs her seasons. And now I know why!

# Thread: CT newwbie confused
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newwbie-confused.399/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-24T17:43:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newwbie-confused.399/post-5442>

Hi Everyone,

first some background:

I am day 5 into Leptin Rx, I have been on pretty strict Low carb Paleo: meats and veggies for 13 months. down form 225lbs-184. stalled at 184 for 2 months, and now moving down again (182) I'm 5'9 medium build male.

I have sleep issues: wake up at 4:30 am and can't go back to sleep averaging 5-6 hours a night. I suspect my cortisol its all screwed up. may get it tested, but in either case, although I do not have much in the way of body fat to lose, I do want to get optimized. I hope that CT will improve my sleep. and shred the rest of the body fat. I do 1-2 days of heavy weights 20-30 min per day. may introduce sprints every two weeks,when my hip tendon heals.

I have some detail related questions on CT.

began today with face dunks in cold tap water in my sink. I did 5, 30 second dunks with adding cold water to the sink as I think it gets warmed up by my body temp.

what does the progression using face dunks look like? (what time increments?) do I add ice? and when?. I plan on using a thermometer for the water. it should be 50F right? I also got hold of some skin thermometers. what should they read?

when do I graduate to body icing? how many weeks or what goal reached.

this is very new to me and a bit confusing, hope that some CT veterans will weigh in! Thanks in advance!

Alex

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-24T17:56:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newwbie-confused.399/post-5448>

> **PaleoDentist;5476 wrote:** Hi Everyone,
>
> first some background:
>
> I am day 5 into Leptin Rx, I have been on pretty strict Low carb Paleo: meats and veggies for 13 months. down form 225lbs-184. stalled at 184 for 2 months, and now moving down again (182) I'm 5'9 medium build male.
>
>
>
> I have sleep issues: wake up at 4:30 am and can't go back to sleep averaging 5-6 hours a night. I suspect my cortisol its all screwed up. may get it tested, but in either case, although I do not have much in the way of body fat to lose, I do want to get optimized. I hope that CT will improve my sleep. and shred the rest of the body fat. I do 1-2 days of heavy weights 20-30 min per day. may introduce sprints every two weeks,when my hip tendon heals.
>
>
>
> I have some detail related questions on CT.
>
>
>
> began today with face dunks in cold tap water in my sink. I did 5, 30 second dunks with adding cold water to the sink as I think it gets warmed up by my body temp.
>
>
>
> what does the progression using face dunks look like? (what time increments?) do I add ice? and when?. I plan on using a thermometer for the water. it should be 50F right? I also got hold of some skin thermometers. what should they read?
>
>
>
> when do I graduate to body icing? how many weeks or what goal reached.
>
>
>
> this is very new to me and a bit confusing, hope that some CT veterans will weigh in! Thanks in advance!
>
>
>
> Alex

I'm totally addicted to my face dunks, and here is what I did.

I started the first few days just holding breath. Then I got a snorkel and just stayed under, increasing each day by 2 mins a time till I was up to two weeks/twenty mins. First icy dunk and 10-15 seconds was max. 2nd day I could do a min, 3rd day 4-6 mins no prob. Then I just added 2 mins a day from there.

I found a bowl chilled down with ice gets water to about 32-35, and my face from 47-55. . .right in the target zone. I used an infrared therm to measure.

There has been some discussion on the usefulness of the snorkel - as you can achieve the same with coming up for breath. Your skin probably won't rise in temp so fast that it gets out of zone. The debate is if the "dive reflex" is relevant, and that requires breath holding.

Since I was enjoying the face stuff so much, I just kept doing with snorkel. I've moved on to spot icing, but kept the face dunking as I enjoyed it - and I swear my face looks younger!

Also, I think it helped me with the mental barrier and will hopefully get me through the first baths. Since once the initial "dunk" was over, I just didn't feel cold on my face! It was super feeling. I would go longer except I get tired of standing over a bowl!

It also helps with the "how the F%^&$ am I going to set my ass down in a tub of cold water" instinct. It puts a little light at the end of the CT tunnel.

### Sherry — 2012-03-24T18:09:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newwbie-confused.399/post-5454>

I too started with the face dunks and since my hormones are so out of whack I really wanted to move on to the baths as I initially thought that they would move things along faster. I then moved to the ice bags and finally to the baths. And, I still use ice bags most nights in addition to the baths. That process took me about 8 days, maybe less. Although, I would say that this is all an individual process. Each of us are unique and therefore will have unique progression, unique detox symptoms, etc. I have been doing the baths for maybe three weeks now. I can't remember when Dr. K first blogged about the protocol but I started the process that day. There are days when I get in that tub and have no problem and there are days when I shiver my teeth loose. I meditate while in there and it makes it so much easier. I've tried reading, etc. It doesn't work for me but do whatever works for you and dive in.

And, Cavemam I like the snorkel idea. I didn't think of it until I had moved on to the ice packs. But, since I've noticed skin differences I think I should start adding the face dunks again. Thanks for the momentum.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-24T18:19:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newwbie-confused.399/post-5456>

Thanks! I like the snorkel idea as well. I actually have one already! So I need to start with ice water? I guess plain cold tap water is too warm. will try that ice tomorrow. do we need to warm up afterward? like drying off with a towel to taking a warmer shower?

Sherry, do you take cold baths every day? with ice? how long is each session? is there a max time?

### LinD — 2012-03-24T18:44:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newwbie-confused.399/post-5464>

Uh-oh.... Did I miss something crucial to the CT process by not starting with face dunks and going straight into the cold bath? Or is that simply a step to help one move into the cold bath?

### Sherry — 2012-03-24T18:44:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newwbie-confused.399/post-5465>

> **PaleoDentist;5490 wrote:** Thanks! I like the snorkel idea as well. I actually have one already! So I need to start with ice water? I guess plain cold tap water is too warm. will try that ice tomorrow. do we need to warm up afterward? like drying off with a towel to taking a warmer shower?
>
>
>
> Sherry, do you take cold baths every day? with ice? how long is each session? is there a max time?

You can start with warmer water and then work your way down in temperature. It's all an individual thing. I have to use ice because I live in the Florida Keys and our water is piped in from the mainland above ground. It's probably near 80 out of the tap this time of year. I've got ice going in all different directions and I buy the 20# bags.

I am not doing baths every day. I probably get in about 4-5 per week. But, I do use ice bags in the evening when reading or watching a movie - every night - even after I've done a bath. I do my baths around mid-day because they energize me and then I can't sleep. But, many, many people are reporting excellent sleep afterwards so it is all individual. I stay in the bath at least 35 minutes. Sometimes when I'm shivering that is about all I can take. Other times when I'm not shivering (that varies) I'll stay in for 50-55 minutes. Dr. Kruse has been in for hours so I don't think there is a max. Good luck.

### LinD — 2012-03-24T19:26:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newwbie-confused.399/post-5484>

What gets me out sooner than later, Sherry, is the need to use the bano... (lol). Guess I could get back in the bath? But the other factor is time....

### Sherry — 2012-03-24T21:34:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newwbie-confused.399/post-5532>

> **LinD;5518 wrote:** What gets me out sooner than later, Sherry, is the need to use the bano... (lol). Guess I could get back in the bath? But the other factor is time....

I haven't had that problem and it's probably a good release of toxins so that is good. Time is always an issue. Dr. K said that the effects are cumulative so any time you can give it is always good.

### DarleenMB — 2012-03-25T00:11:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newwbie-confused.399/post-5581>

[http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)

> The safest way to adapt to it is to submerge your face first into water in a sink where you add ice to with a cooking thermometer. Wait until the water is between 50-55 degrees and enter face first and see how long you can tolerate it using a time piece. Record the time. For the next two weeks work your way up until you need to take a breath for O2. The rate of adaptation to this will vary for people. When you finish this proceed to number 2.
>
>
>
> 2\. Buy a compression shirt that is quite tight and begin to place 20 or 40 lbs of ice on your torso. Double bag the ice to stop leakage on clothing or furniture.
>
>
>
> snip
>
>
>
> The length of time you expose yourself should be matched to your BMI. The fatter you are the longer your exposure should be. You want your skin to always remain pink to cherry red when you are doing this. If it gets white you need to stop the test and take a warm shower. Do these things indoors initially where you can control the air temperature during adaptation. Do not start this outside until you cold adapt for at least a month. When you can tolerate the skin being covered for one hour with pink to cherry red skin you’re now ready for the Cold Tub step.

### Dextery — 2012-03-25T06:11:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newwbie-confused.399/post-5695>

John,

Just draw non heated water into the tub and stand in the tub for five minutes. Then crouch down and barely allow your butt into the water for a couple of minutes. Then close your eyes and just sit down and then scream for 30 seconds until you have no more air to scream with...and by that time you will find that you say to yourself "this really isn't so bad". Have a book to read and stay as long as you can. The book helps the time pass or watch a youtube video. Dr Lustig's Sugar, The Bitter Truth is a good one or Dr Doug McGuff on the paleo diet is another good one. I have a tub/shower and I set a chair beside the opening to the tub and can watch videos on my laptop.

Then repeat the next evening before bed and for most people, CT induces sleep...it does for me...but be aware for some it is so invigorating that sleep following CT is difficult.

After a week, I was able to do 45 min easily and now I do 1.25 hours easily in 60 degree water. You can do the same. Your neolithic mind is your only obstacle. You will change your DNA by how you think about cold thermogenesis. You do want your telomeres longer....don't you?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Hack my freezing butt
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-freezing-butt.401/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Birdy — 2012-03-24T18:37:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-freezing-butt.401/post-5460>

I put away the winter comforter, which I had been throwing off pretty easily lately. I replaced it with a thin fleece blanket and thin cotton quilt bedspread. We turn off all heat at night, but I don't know how low it gets until someone gets up in the morning and turns it back on, but it was only at 60 by the early morning, which is relatively high. I was fine for awhile, and then got cold. When it got too uncomfortable to sleep, I got up, put on some pj bottoms and threw on a fleece baby blanket. I just could not keep warm! All night really, every time I woke up, I noticed I was freezing my butt off. Finally, right before I was to get up, I got hot and threw all the covers off. I thought I would be better adapted by now. Is this typical, or is it an indication of me doing something wrong with the CT?

### Adria — 2012-03-24T18:50:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-freezing-butt.401/post-5468>

Birdy, I find the colder I let myself get in the day affects how I tolerate the cold at night. I used to sleep with long sleeve pjs and thick socks along with several blankets. And kept the temps at 70plus. Since CT I've been wearing less clothing in the day then at night I find just a little warmth puts me to sleep. We've been turning off the furnace and it stays around 60 degrees at night. I'm down to my summer pjs and blankets. Progress slowly and be sure to keep yourself warm enough to sleep.. the most important thing is your sleep.

# Thread: Fish.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish.405/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kathy — 2012-03-24T20:36:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish.405/post-5517>

Not a big fish eater & never have been. I'd like to change that but don't have any idea where to start.

Although I live by the ocean & my stores have lots fresh fish available, I'm confused about farmed vs wild, sustainability, mercury and last week got an email about danger of eating any pacific ocean fish because of Fukushima reactor mess (all west coast food supply actually - but i'm in denial on that one for now).

I used to eat canned tuna/salmon or fried (grains) smothered in mayo or tartar sauce, but not so hot on the canned idea and mayo/fried are no longer options.

The couple of times I've tried frying in butter, my kitchen stunk for days & I didn't like that either.

Any help?

### Lyndra — 2012-03-24T21:34:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish.405/post-5533>

I find wild caught salmon is fairly mild tasting but perhaps I'm used to it. My preferred cooking technique is en papillote - I put a few herbs on the fillets, wrap them in buttered aluminum foil, and bake at 450 for 15 minutes. Comes out great every time and no fish smell. I live in land-locked Colorado so it's hard to find a good variety of fish at a reasonable price. I love ono when I can get it and sea bass (probably not the most sustainable choice) tastes buttery rich and is my fav though very pricy.

### Kathy — 2012-03-26T19:54:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish.405/post-6451>

Thanks Lyndra. I'm going to try the foil today. Seems like they should be but don't think prices here are much better.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-26T20:02:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish.405/post-6457>

As a general rule you want wild fish. There are some types like Scottish Salmon that is sustainable raised in the cold water, in the ocean, that is almost like wild. Tastes good too. Don't ever buy atlantic salmon. That is bordering on Frankenfish in my mind. Alaskan Halibut is another good cold water fish that is really mild. I also like red snapper as a mild fish.

Not sure about the Fukashima radiation affecting fish but I have read a bit on the radiation and know it is definitely around us.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-27T22:17:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish.405/post-7326>

I'm struggling with learning to cook fish, too. I wrecked two wild-caught tuna fillets this week. $20 down the drain. But hopefully I will learn.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-27T22:51:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish.405/post-7348>

> **PaleoSue;7364 wrote:** I'm struggling with learning to cook fish, too. I wrecked two wild-caught tuna fillets this week. $20 down the drain. But hopefully I will learn.

As long as you didn't burn (turn them black) them you could chop/shred the tuna up and make tuna burgers with the fish. Even if the taste is off sometimes just making them into little fish patties they taste so much better!

Could it be that the mercury stuck to your insulin receptors acts like a barometer?

I'm looking to see if I can find that... there are lots of links to wars and sunspots and mental health and sunspots

someone please share if they know.

[http://communities.washingtontimes.com/neighborhood/tango-mind-and-emotion/2012/mar/8/solar-flare-sun-touches-our-psyche/](http://http://communities.washingtontimes.com/neighborhood/tango-mind-and-emotion/2012/mar/8/solar-flare-sun-touches-our-psyche/)

### chocolate — 2012-05-12T01:28:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-happen-to-your-senses-before-a-storm-to-make-the-water-feel-warm.409/post-23287>

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Statocyst](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Statocyst)

Statocyst

The statocyst is a balance sensory receptor present in some aquatic invertebrates, including bivalves,\[1\] cnidarians,\[2\] echinoderms,\[3\] cephalopods,\[4\] and crustaceans.\[5\] A similar structure is also found in Xenoturbella.\[6\] **The statocyst consists of a sac-like structure containing a _mineralised mass (statolith)_ and numerous innervated sensory hairs (setae). The statolith's inertia causes it to push against the setae when the animal accelerates. Deflection of setae by the statolith in response to gravity activates neurons, providing feedback to the animal on change in orientation and allowing balance to be maintained.

Statocysts in echinoderms

Because many echinoderms of this group have only simple nervous systems without a controlling "brain", they are limited in their actions and responses to stimuli. The statocyst is therefore useful for telling the animal whether it is upside down or not.** An upside-down echinoderm is in danger since its belly is not protected by its spiny skin.

### chocolate — 2012-05-17T16:38:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-happen-to-your-senses-before-a-storm-to-make-the-water-feel-warm.409/post-24876>

[http://brainz.org/15-pets-who-returned-home-remarkable-distances/](http://http://brainz.org/15-pets-who-returned-home-remarkable-distances/)

Animals that sense their way home

[http://www.eternalanimals.com/Ripley%27s%20Believe%20It%20Or%20Not%20Animal%20Stories.htm](http://http://www.eternalanimals.com/Ripley%27s%20Believe%20It%20Or%20Not%20Animal%20Stories.htm)

Did You Miss Me? Popcorn, a cat owned by Nancy Beecham, disappeared when her family moved from Oahu, Hawaii, to La Mesa, California. The cat was found seven weeks later in a cat carrier, elated to see her owners after having gone 49 days without food.

# Thread: HmmÃ¢â‚¬Â¦I seem to be losing muscle and fat. Help!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 11

### codys182 — 2012-03-25T02:34:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5635>

So I started CT about 2 weeks ago. I did the face dunks, ice packs, and just did my first bath yesterday. The weight is falling off. However, while working out today I felt and looked much weaker, and my performance had decreased. I am pretty strict keto and following the leptin reset protocol. I know my calories had dropped quite a bit during the past few weeks as I have lost most of my hunger. They are not too low, thoughÃ¢â‚¬Â¦and probably close to 2,000 cals a day.

My workouts are pretty low intensity - lift heavy for about 30 minutes, using mostly compound lifts. I have only been doing this 2-3 days a week. I am about 5'10, 185 lbs, and about 15% body fat.

Does anyone have any recommendations or run into this issue? Should I possibly add in some carbs? Or just make an effort to eat more?

### Glamorama — 2012-03-25T02:37:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5639>

I would focus on upping protein. I lost strength after a week of CT but it returned massively with increases in week 3! I haven't lost hunger though, I eat well... Lots of fat and protein. Try that and see how you go this week.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-25T02:38:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5641>

Ps! Fat loss releases estrogen into your system which often leads to lack of strength and libido in men. It's part of detox. Get B12 and B6.

### codys182 — 2012-03-25T02:44:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5646>

### Cody — 2012-03-25T05:15:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5670>

I've only had muscle gain with CT and the Leptin Reset. Gained 8 freaking pounds! But my waist is getting smaller.

Lift heavier, that tells your body to hold onto your muscle. 5 reps, last one should be FREAKING HARD!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-25T17:01:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5787>

I know that many lifters do as well. Dr. K says that you will get weaker at first and then have massive gains, but that it can take 3 months - 2 yrs! This is why most paleo lifters seem to prefer more carbs. But, I believe Dr. K that it isn't the best plan and will shorten telemeres, but I don't care if I have weakness for even a couple years as I don't compete or anything like that. My experience though has been added strength since doing CT, but I still can tell a difference in endurance. My muscles literally feel weaker and I can't do even half of what I normally can in endurance when I'm keto. But, it has been getting better with the CT for me.

How do you know if you need zinc? I know if it's really bad then you get a metallic taste in your mouth, which I don't have. Do you know if it's OK to take a zinc or just leave it?

### Glamorama — 2012-04-11T05:27:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-13627>

> **Gladina;13670 wrote:** How do you know if you need zinc? I know if it's really bad then you get a metallic taste in your mouth, which I don't have. Do you know if it's OK to take a zinc or just leave it?

I did a taste-test with my naturopath. I was very deficient, the solution tasted like water to me. I supplemented daily for 14 weeks, and byt that point I started feeling sick after taking zinc, so the doc told me I probably had my storage full, and could stop taking it altogether. Now I take it every 2nd night again, without any side-effects.

# Thread: Set the standard and they will follow!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/set-the-standard-and-they-will-follow.413/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-03-25T02:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/set-the-standard-and-they-will-follow.413/post-5642>

After thinking mommy was quite crazy these last few weeks, my children did their own versions of CT tonight! My 7-year old son sat on an ice block for a good 30 minutes while my 11-year old daughter decided on her own to wet and freeze a towel and then lay on it. I'm thrilled and hoping they continue! They also both asked me to buy them a pair of the blue light blocking glasses. I jumped and ordered some on Amazon right away!

They already do fairly well with diet. I would say they are 90% paleo/primal with just the occasional slip when my daughter is at a friend's house or my mom gives my son gluten free pizza - still trying to work on some of those issues. My DD was at a friend's house last night and had regular pizza. She told me today when she got home that she'd rather not "eat that stuff," but she doesn't want to be rude, so we had a talk about how to handle those situations. I love that she's thinking about things on her own and CHOOSING to do what she feels is best for her. My son even said he knows he feels better when he eats clean - that's HUGE for a 7 year old! I know they would have never had the same reaction if I forced all of this on them.

Just wanted to post to encourage all of you to continue being the example and shining the light for your children. They are watching and absorbing every little thing they see. My kids knew I had been very sick for awhile to the point of not even being able to take walks with them or play at times. The last week or so I've finally started to get some energy back and they're noticing.

Now if only I could get my parents and some others in my life on board . . .

# Thread: CT and ear ringing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2012-03-25T04:47:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-5664>

Has anyone had any experience with CT and improving ringing in the ears?

I've been doing CT for about a week - started with face dunking (and didn't mind the cold, I'm Canadian LOL, but didn't like holding my breath), have been doing spot icing with ice packs around my middle (and melting them within about 40 minutes), and one cold bath so far, as well as cold showers in the morning. I've been feeling like I have a bit more energy, and have noticed that I'm a bit smaller around the middle so far.

I've had ringing in my ears for over 6 years. It's constant ringing, sometimes louder than others. I also have constant congestion in my head and runny eyes, many food allergies (probably more like intolerances) and think that the ringing is related. Have also been tested and have adrenal fatigue and leaky gut.

I'm hoping that this will help, because I haven't been able to even reduce it yet!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-25T04:49:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-5665>

Yes, my tinnitus has improved. some days it\`s quite dull and I forget I have it but if I am back under stress it returns. I had a fever this week and noticed today the tinnitus is back. I\`m back on CT Baths tomorrow and hope it will calm down again

### Melinda — 2012-03-25T05:21:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-5672>

Just the opposite for me: my tinnitus had improved with singing and choir practice and "listening hard" to try and retrain the damaged hearing loss areas that are ghost adding the ring. But since I started the full body cold soaks a few days ago, it has increased again intermittently. I'm thinking it might just be a symptom of detoxing and will hush again in a few weeks. I'm calm about it b/c I have been able to shush it before. I remember Its the woo said it is louder with high glucose/insulin. Some ppl report elevated glucose numbers as they CT, maybe that's the tie in.

### Mikey7 — 2012-03-25T06:05:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-5689>

I have mild case of tinnitus, and I haven't notice anything. I know it still there but it doesn't bother me at all.

### Tita — 2012-04-11T21:27:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-13860>

Ha, funny... I thought you were writing about ringing in ears after CT.

I noticed after my swims in cold ocean, I got ringing in my ears and wondered why....(othervise I have no problems with sinuses&...)

### nonchalant — 2012-04-11T23:04:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-13884>

I had improvement in tinnitus when I increased my dose of magnesium. I rarely notice it any more. I also don't react as badly to sudden loud noises, like a slamming screen door. I seem to be more tolerant, for some reason. And I **think** my hearing might be a little better.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-04-11T23:42:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-13920>

I had tinnitus that came on after I started CT, it lasted for about 2.5 days and then I finally got the drainage going and it went away. My subsequent soaks didn't cause this, but I still get lots of sinus drainage because of all the garbage I'm detoxing. So I go slow and only get in when I am feeling good... which will NOT be for the next couple of weeks when the pollen count is out of this world here!

### Kirstin — 2012-04-12T04:06:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-14050>

I noticed that my tinnitus improved dramatically by cutting out the grais, sugars, etc and eating strict paleo. Haven't been too consistent with CT, so haven't noticed too many changes yet.
