# Thread: Question regarding sleep...I would appreciate your input
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennyha — 2012-03-25T05:24:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5673>

I had a quick question I was hoping someone could help me with regarding my sleep. I have not been able to sleep well for the past 5 years or so and have been taking some kind of over the counter sleeping aid or melatonin tabs 3 mg every single night. I literally could not sleep without them. I decided to stop taking them about 2 months ago and now just sleep in pitch black every night without any aids. I lay down around 10 pm, fall asleep around 11 pm or so, and wake up every morning around 4:30. I cannot get back to sleep no matter what I do. So I go make and eat breakfast and lay back down around 5:30 a.m. and within a half hour I get very sleepy immediately and I fall back asleep from 6:00 to 9:00 a.m. before getting up for the day. A few questions...

1\. What is causing this? I find myself VERY sleepy after my breakfast, lunch, and dinner each day as well.

2\. Is this a problem and/or unhealthy for me?

3\. Should I stop laying back down after my breakfast and just remain up for the day from 4:30 a.m. on even though I am still very tired and my body is obviously still seeking the sleep?

Thanks for any light anyone could shed on this topic for me. I greatly appreciate it

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-25T05:38:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5678>

Not sure about on the nighttime issue, but sleepy after meals says insulin resistance (or diabetes) to me. Do you get sleepy if you don't eat any carbs at mealtime? I would recommend buying a cheap blood glucose meter at walmart & testing yourself. It should read less than 100 when you wake up in the morning; 101 to 130 is considered "pre-diabetic/insulin resistant"; over 130 is diabetic.

PS testing yourself in the morning is considered a "Fasting Blood Glucose" test; ie, don't eat anything 12 hours before testing.

### janagram — 2012-03-25T08:42:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5731>

Five and a half hours- you're almost there! six hours is good....I know 7 is better, but who gets 7?

Maybe don't eat brkfst, just read or something until you get sleepy again. (easy to say...I know)

I have a similar problem....so keep reporting....

### bennyha — 2012-03-25T15:59:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5755>

> **Chrisgraff;5712 wrote:** Not sure about on the nighttime issue, but sleepy after meals says insulin resistance (or diabetes) to me. Do you get sleepy if you don't eat any carbs at mealtime? I would recommend buying a cheap blood glucose meter at walmart & testing yourself. It should read less than 100 when you wake up in the morning; 101 to 130 is considered "pre-diabetic/insulin resistant"; over 130 is diabetic.
>
>
>
> PS testing yourself in the morning is considered a "Fasting Blood Glucose" test; ie, don't eat anything 12 hours before testing.

I haven't really eaten many carbs for the past few years. I use to carb cycle for a few years on workout days. So on Mon, Wed, Fri I would lift heavy weights then eat around 150 grams of carbs (usually 2 big sweet potatos with cinnamon post-workout). Then the other 4 days of the week I would only eat green veggies and carbs were around 30 grams or so. But I've been eating very low carb the past 3 or 4 weeks trying to get into ketosis along with cold therapy. I had a horrible binge eating problem for 2 years despite being very slim at 5'10 155 lbs and single digit bodyfat. I could literally eat 15,000 calories in 3 or 4 hours and not even befull. But I finally got rid of that after doing the leptin reset protocol. Back to regular hunger and have lost about 10 lbs I didn't want or need to lose. Maybe it's just taking my body some time to adjust? I'm sure the 2 years of binging on nuts and ice cream did some damage to me

### bennyha — 2012-03-25T16:03:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5756>

Also, I did notice the last time I ate some sweet potatoes a month or so ago, I did feel even more tired and experience much more brain fog than when I ate just fat and protein

### Adria — 2012-03-25T16:28:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5770>

The components to good sleep are diet, light, cold. Stick with the keto diet and be sure not to allow lights when the sun is setting, including computers etc. You might want to get some blue blocking glasses to use at night. Can you give a menu of what you eat? Seems strange you are sleepy after eating keto-paleo, post a typical menu. Are you Cting, if so give more detail.

### bennyha — 2012-03-25T18:12:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5836>

> **Adria;5804 wrote:** The components to good sleep are diet, light, cold. Stick with the keto diet and be sure not to allow lights when the sun is setting, including computers etc. You might want to get some blue blocking glasses to use at night. Can you give a menu of what you eat? Seems strange you are sleepy after eating keto-paleo, post a typical menu. Are you Cting, if so give more detail.

Thanks for your reply. A typical breakfast is eggs and bacon cooked in ghee and some salmon and a little spinach or broccoli. Lunch is usually mackeral or salmon with some ghee and a little spinach or broccoli. Dinner is usually beef or some more fish. Also supplement with high dose EPA/DHA fish oil at every meal. Maybe I'm eating too much protein? I'm 150 lbs and eat around 200 grams of protein per day, 50+ grams at each meal. Carbs are really low as you can tell and fat is moderate to high.

As for CT, I sit in a tub of 50 degree water everyday for 35-40 minutes. It's a jacuzzi-style tub, so I am able to lay down fully with the water coming up to my chin the entire time, so my entire body is covered. At first I would be miserably cold after getting out of the tub for up to 2 hours. Now I find that I'm only uncomfortable for a half hour or so after getting out.

It's not falling asleep that's bothering me, it's the extreme brain fog/fatigue I feel after each meal

### L8F — 2012-03-26T03:02:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-6167>

Wondering about cortisol levels, have you done them? How about absorption issues with the intense prior cravings? Using Betaine HCl or enzymes? (Just listened to a Robb Wolff podcast on this digestion stuff..a lot can happen in the gut as we all know)

### bennyha — 2012-03-26T04:42:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-6204>

> **L8F;6202 wrote:** Wondering about cortisol levels, have you done them? How about absorption issues with the intense prior cravings? Using Betaine HCl or enzymes? (Just listened to a Robb Wolff podcast on this digestion stuff..a lot can happen in the gut as we all know)

I actually started taking digestive enzymes about a month ago and noticed significant improvement in the reduction of bloating after a meal. After I would eat a big meal for the past year or so it would just sit in my stomach and look like I was pregnant for like 3 hours. I also would have a bunch of bumps after I ate for an hour or two. All that has gone away since I started taking the digestive enzymes before meals. I highly recommend them to anyone who thinks they may have any digestion issues as they helped me significantly

### Adria — 2012-03-26T15:09:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-6294>

With the brain fog and bloating you describe, I think the key for you is diet. You could be allergic or sensitive to the foods you're eating. I would experiment with it. Cut out all vegs and go with no ghee, basically zero carb. Just try it for a week and see how you feel and if your sleep and brain fog improves. If not try removing the seafood. Test everything and take your time, let your body adjust to each change (several days).

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-25T17:38:43+0300

Ok trying to up load my food porn from dinner on Friday: garlic & tomatoes mussels, shrimp & cod

I modified this recipe from MDA: [http://www.marksdailyapple.com/tomato-garlic-mussels/#axzz1q8snfAce](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/tomato-garlic-mussels/#axzz1q8snfAce)

Attached files [](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/5845=41-ImageUploadedByTapatalk1332686238.169348.jpg)

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-25T17:42:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-porn-post-your-pics.427/post-5814>

Grassfed/finished beef short ribs

Prepare like a regular pot roast using the following ingredients:

2 Tbs ghee/butter/coconut oil - your choicd

2 large onios

garlic

1 can of fire roasted tomatoes - or 2 large tomatoes

paprika

bayleaf

1-2 c white wine

1-2 c homemade bone broth

thyime

cinnamon

2lbs boneless short ribs (ours had bones - so we saved them and made more bone broth!)

in dutch oven cook onions and garlic til translucent - add tomatoes, and seasoning - then add beef short ribs

throw all together in a 300deg oven

You don't need to sear the ribs before hand - if you leave the top off of the dutch oven

# Thread: Lab question for the geeks
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-25T16:34:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-5771>

I want to check my Leptin sensitivity.

which main tests do I need and what ranges equal Leptin sensitivity or which numbers equal leptin resistance? I want the biggest bang for the buck without ordering every test under the sun?

I am sure it has been posted already, I just don't have time to comb the threads. Thanks!

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-25T17:30:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-5805>

VAP or NMR lipid profile - checks your Leptin Sensitivity at the Liver

Free T3, Reverse T3, TSH, Free T4, TSH, Thyroid Antibodies: TPO & AB - checks Leptin Sensitivity at the Brain (HPA - Thyroid Axis)

Salivary cortisol - verifies - your Leptin sensitivity at the Brain -(what is the HPA -Thyroid axis doing)

Sex hormones (DHEA-S, Pregnenolone, Prolactin, Testosterone, Estrogen, Progesterone, Alderstrone) - identifies if you have pregnenolone steal - If any of the above are out of alignment - you'll have problems w/your sex hormones

VO2Max - will test your leptin sensitivity at the muscles - most ppl wont get this test... but there are ways out there to determine it w/intense exercise - and blood spot tests after each intense repetition - I remember when training at the national level for swimming we'd test VO2Max - by doing 3x200m all out swims w/1min rest btwn each 200m. we'd do blood spot tests at the completion of each 200m - some measurement was done - and by the end we could see where our VO2Max was...

Quelsen has a good list of labs + the labcorp codes...

### Owl — 2012-03-25T18:17:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-5841>

Dr. K said this about determining if you're LR, "you can get a Leptin level or a T3 and rev T3 and then use the conversion calculator at the STTM.com to see where your ratio is. We like it above 20. Other signs......high LDL levels because of a low T3 conversion to pregnenolone. high ferritin, haptoglobin, HS CRP, low Vit D and low DHEA levels.......all things that walk hand and hand with LR."

And from his Leptin FAQ blog:

"How do I determine if I am leptin resistant? Remember, you can be LR (leptin resistant) if youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re fat or skinny. If youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re overweight by more than 30lbs, it is a lock you have some degree of LR. If youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re underweight by 20 lbs, you are likely LR, too. If you had an eating disorder, youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re likely suffering from a serious leptin issue.

The easiest test is to look in the mirror. The mirror does not lie and it is really cheap. For those people who still canÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t be sure after peeking in the mirror, you can order some blood tests. My favorite is the HS CRP (highly sensitive C-Reactive protein) and the reverse T3 tests (but there are others). They are accurate in over 90 % of cases.

My next favorite test is the salivary cortisol test, along with at least two of the following: HDL level, serum ferritin, homocysteine, fasting triglycerides, fasting insulin, or post-prandial glucose readings done every 15 minutes for one hour after eating. (Read my Hormone 101 blog for details.)"

### Owl — 2012-03-25T18:44:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-5860>

As far as ranges, he has said he wants hs-CRP under .5, and preferably way lower than that. STTM site he mentions with the calculator is: [http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/rt3-ratio/](http://http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/rt3-ratio/)

You want your FreeT3/RT3 ratio over 20, or your total T3/RT3 ratio over 10.

The adrenal saliva test should show all readings in the normal range.

I think he wants insulin under 2. Fast BG should be in the low 80's.

I'm not sure on the other levels.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-25T19:31:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-5891>

> **Owl;5875 wrote:** Dr. K said this about determining if you're LR, "you can get a Leptin level or a T3 and rev T3 and then use the conversion calculator at the STTM.com to see where your ratio is. We like it above 20. Other signs......high LDL levels because of a low T3 conversion to pregnenolone. high ferritin, haptoglobin, HS CRP, low Vit D and low DHEA levels.......all things that walk hand and hand with LR."
>
>
>
> And from his Leptin FAQ blog:
>
>
>
> "How do I determine if I am leptin resistant? Remember, you can be LR (leptin resistant) if youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re fat or skinny. If youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re overweight by more than 30lbs, it is a lock you have some degree of LR. If youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re underweight by 20 lbs, you are likely LR, too. If you had an eating disorder, youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re likely suffering from a serious leptin issue.
>
>
>
> The easiest test is to look in the mirror. The mirror does not lie and it is really cheap. For those people who still canÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t be sure after peeking in the mirror, you can order some blood tests. My favorite is the HS CRP (highly sensitive C-Reactive protein) and the reverse T3 tests (but there are others). They are accurate in over 90 % of cases.
>
>
>
> My next favorite test is the salivary cortisol test, along with at least two of the following: HDL level, serum ferritin, homocysteine, fasting triglycerides, fasting insulin, or post-prandial glucose readings done every 15 minutes for one hour after eating. (Read my Hormone 101 blog for details.)"

Ok, well, here's a question... Are we talking CM LDL levels or is there an alternative level I should know about. I got my LDL back and it was high (figured, not measured). Out of curiosity, I used that "Iranian Formula" that I had seen on another site that claims it is a better estimation of LDL for low carbers with low trigs (which mine were, with a very high HDL) and it lowered my LDL to within acceptable range.......

### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-25T20:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-5926>

Thanks owl, I'll refer back to here when do my blood work!

### Owl — 2012-03-26T01:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-6100>

> **cjco;5925 wrote:** Ok, well, here's a question... Are we talking CM LDL levels or is there an alternative level I should know about. I got my LDL back and it was high (figured, not measured). Out of curiosity, I used that "Iranian Formula" that I had seen on another site that claims it is a better estimation of LDL for low carbers with low trigs (which mine were, with a very high HDL) and it lowered my LDL to within acceptable range.......

I am not very knowledgeable about lipids, but he did say, "high LDL levels because of a low T3 conversion to pregnenolone." So you could test your pregnenolone. If it's low, you may have that problem.

# Thread: will  sweat like crazy when its hot
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-sweat-like-crazy-when-its-hot.432/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cowboy — 2012-03-25T17:35:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-sweat-like-crazy-when-its-hot.432/post-5808>

Been doing the CT since Dr. K blogged it a few weeks back. Face dunkings graduated to one or two gel packs once a day to five gel packs three times a day, and luke warm showers graduating to up to three minutes of 50 degree water. Next is a large stock tank for the back yard for the full immersions.

I assume that my internal flame willing I will achieve cold adaptation.

My BIG QUESTION/WORRY is that one this occurs and the summer temps hit 95 degrees I will be sweating like a pig.

I've lost 60 pounds with vlc/paloe eating over the last year and a half. Started the LR in November and to be honest haven't had great results. Loosing 60 pounds is like taking off a warm jacket. Heat doesn't bother me nearly as much, but I am concerned about cold adaptation and then always feeling warm and always seating.

Is anyone far enough along to know what happens with cold adaptation and hot weather.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-25T18:11:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-sweat-like-crazy-when-its-hot.432/post-5834>

There have been a few who have asked about this and the response is that once your body is cold adapted it will also function better in warmer temps and you will not have to worry about overheating.

the biggest problem with looking for guidance regarding summer temps is that few people outside of Dr K and his patients have gone through the process over a full year. the rest of us are just catching up

### Glamorama — 2012-03-26T06:33:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-sweat-like-crazy-when-its-hot.432/post-6242>

In summer, you should be better at regulating your body-temp thanks to CT. So you should be feeling comfortable rather than insanely hot.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-26T22:23:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-sweat-like-crazy-when-its-hot.432/post-6556>

I think I have most of the signals for LS with the exception of not sweating. How does your sweating change when you become LS? All my life I have never sweat much. Of course, I never was a big water drinker either, now I drink at least two quarts a day of iced water along with my morning coffee and afternoon mint tea and water to take my supplements with.

### Vicster — 2012-03-26T23:08:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-sweat-like-crazy-when-its-hot.432/post-6577>

> **Glamorama;6277 wrote:** In summer, you should be better at regulating your body-temp thanks to CT. So you should be feeling comfortable rather than insanely hot.

I do hope this is true! I'm in South Texas and I usually sweat profusely in the heat and humidity of our summers.

# Thread: CT while having a cold?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-while-having-a-cold.433/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColdBren — 2012-03-25T18:19:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-while-having-a-cold.433/post-5843>

Is it okay to take cold baths with a cold?

### Adria — 2012-03-25T18:27:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-while-having-a-cold.433/post-5848>

For a mild cold ok but don't overdo it. If you're have fever, coughing, etc I would skip for a few days, maybe just do ice packs instead. I seemed to get a slight cold a few wks into ct and just kept going, the cold went away and didn't get any worse.

### agatha — 2012-03-26T18:47:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-while-having-a-cold.433/post-6427>

I got a streaming cold in the second week and I backed off a little - only did the baths if i felt like it. I wasn't sure if it was a real infection or just a detox but either way I figured my body was struggling to cope and I should probably listen to my instincts. I think it was probably detox as I felt well otherwise.

# Thread: Some people must already be adapted...

### BenG — 2012-03-25T19:54:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-people-must-already-be-adapted.435/post-5905>

Funny story, I’m in the tub on Friday doing a CT session. My wife (after giving me dumb look about CT for over a week), says,"I’ll get in, too" (no real adaptation/training). I tried to warn her she wasn’t ready;--she should gradually adapt.. she didn’t care. So she plops in the tub and gives me a dumb look — this is easy. I kept asking her, if she was cold, etc as the tub was filling up…"no. it’s fine"…45 minutes later...she acted as if she did this forever. Of course she decides she needs bubbles so she fills the 50-55 degree tub with soap and bubble bath.

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-25T20:01:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-people-must-already-be-adapted.435/post-5908>

### BeingVenus — 2012-04-22T22:00:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-people-must-already-be-adapted.435/post-17863>

Been plateuaued ever since doing LR and CT as of a week ago to see if I can push through it. I always took cold showers and slept with it really cold/no covers though. Especially when I was pregnant last year. Hmmm...

# Thread: CT for Athletes - Results / Experiences
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SnuffySmith — 2012-03-25T20:45:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-5931>

Is there anyone out there who started CT while they were a competitive / very active athlete? I'm curious to find out what you did, what improvements you realized, and how long it took to experience the improvements. I'm contemplating the idea of buying a rubbermaid stock tank, but would like see some more experiential evidence, or studies (yes, I've searched google scholar, etc.).

For most everyone else out there ... I think it's great that you're making progress with the protocols. I've read where working out can be bad and I accept this. I'm all for the non athlete to be eating primal and CT their way to lots of weight loss and an occasional pullup. For perspective, I'm interested in responses regarding people who can do things like deadlift over 400# and can run a 5K in less than 24 minutes.

Thanks,

Snuffy

### anonymouse — 2012-03-26T16:55:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-6366>

I'm not quite where you want me to be, but the 5k is easy (~22 minutes) and here are my stats:

BW: 165

DL: 325x6

Sq: 315x2

B: 230x1

OHP: 155x1

I've been paleo for about 3 months and been CT'ing for about 2 weeks. Haven't seen anything significant from CT yet, but it probably hasn't kicked in. Low carb has brought me down from 176lb while maintaining strength (added on OHP and DL, lost a bit on squat and bench).

I'll keep you posted. Will probably be a month before you see me on here again.

### SnuffySmith — 2012-03-27T00:50:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-6640>

anon...,

Thanks for the reply ... I started the nightly ice bath yesterday ... will see what happens!

### Bill Strahan — 2012-03-27T19:12:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-7134>

44 year old male. 5'10" 195 pounds. 435 deadlift. My last 5k run was 24:17 so I guess I don't qualify, but I'm awful at running. I can row it on a Concept II in 20:10. Does that count?  90 pound weighted pullups, bench 275, strict press 185, squat 345.

In any case, I used to do ice baths after workouts. Fell out of the practice until about 10 days ago, and I read CT-6. Hmmm. Got me thinking to previous experiences. So I got a 100 gallon rubbermaid livestock trough, and have worked up to a non-stop 45 minutes submerged from ankles to my neck. I put a block under one end of the trough to hold it an angle, so my feet are just out of the water on the end that is propped up but the water comes all the way up to my neck on the other side. Usually keep my arms in, and sometimes my hands. Finish with the last two minutes holding my breath totally underwater.

I haven't been doing single rep max lifts recently, but I've tried to get dead-hang muscleups for a long time. Suddenly yesterday I noticed I could pull myself several inches higher on the rings. Was THIS (imagine my fingers held apart about 1/4") close to getting on top of the rings without a kip.

That is a sudden and recent change. I'll keep doing this and see what happens. I've been doing CrossFit since 2008, and the PRs are harder to come by. I track everything pretty detailed, so I'll notice if I suddenly start being capable of things I previously wasn't. FWIW in late 2007 I was over 240 pounds, couldn't to 1 pullup, could get about 3 pushups, and I was a tub of lard. Started bodyweight work, then found CrossFit, then started Zone diet, then switched to Paleo...that took about 1 year to progress through all that. I've gotten stronger and faster more or less continuously (barring a weird non-CF injury) for the last 4 years.

### SnuffySmith — 2012-03-27T22:51:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-7349>

Bill,

That's good stuff! Thanks for sharing. I was looking at the rubbermaid troughs and wasn't sure if I'd need a 100 or 150 gallon tub! Guess I'll go with the 100 when I get to it!

Can't wait to hear more about your results.

BTW, what's your skin temp at the end? The lowest I've been so far is 68.

Thanks!

Snuffy

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T02:24:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-7449>

I am going to look into the 100 gal trough for home and my gym. I have been doing daily cold baths in the evenings, abt 3 weeks now. About 45-60 min. I powerlift, and before I started CT my squat was 220, paused bench was 130, deadlift was 260. I am now up to 230 squat, 140 bench, 285 DL. That's pretty damn good! Considering it took about 6 weeks to go from 215 to 220 on the squat. I am 40 yo female.

I have a meet in 3 weeks, so now I am just soaking in the cold. After the meet, I will start doing some spot icing during training.

Actually, this past Friday, I did a little experimenting. I pulled 2 singles 245, 265, this was after heavy squat singles. My upper back felt weary, I know this feeling, and I had 1 more lift, 285. I absolutely know that if I didn't do what I did next, I wouldn't have been able to make that 285. So my upper back felt weary, and hot. I took 2 icy cold water bottles out of the little gym fridge, put them on the floor side by side and place my upper back on them so that they were on either side of my spine, in the mid thoracic area, where I tend to round when fatigued. I stayed on them for 4-5 minutes, the area was numb. I got up, chalked up, approached the bar and boom, flat back easy pull. Amazing. Deadlift has always been my "iffy" lift, so this was pretty exciting.

### Bill Strahan — 2012-03-28T03:10:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-7466>

No idea on skin temp. I think you'll waste a lot of ice if you get the 150 gallon tub. I got the 100, and tilt it as I described to minimize the total volume of water I have to get down to 45-50 degrees.

I'm contemplating making a wood wedge from plywood, partially filling it with sand (so it wont' float), sealing it, and putting it on the end my legs are on to displace water that isn't doing any good. I'm probably using 60 of the 100 gallon capacity, but most of that 60 gallons isn't touching my skin. Anything I could do to get the tub shape closer to the shape of my body when immersed would reduce the amount of ice I have to make.

I got some weird looks at the tractor supply laying down inside the tubs to see which to get.

I bought some plastic containers that are a little bigger than shoe boxes at Target. Each one will hold about 1.25 gallons with room to expand when it turns into ice, so I use the freezer in my hanger to freeze four of those at a time. that gets me about 40 pounds of ice. If I think I'm benefitting from this stuff, I'll look at making an refrigerated tub. Make a custom tub from foam and fiberglass, and run a coil of tubing into a small refrigerator to circulate the water...we'll see

# Thread: Question about optimal ranges?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-optimal-ranges.442/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-26T02:57:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-optimal-ranges.442/post-6160>

So, I'm wondering about my labs...

I'm trying to drill down to what precisely is going on so I have a leg to stand on when I go see my naturopath. For my salivary cortisol and DHEAs (salivary) ranges, is it OK if I'm within range or should I be aiming at a certain part of the range? Also, can someone explain to me why I have high nighttime cortisol (everything else is "within range" although noon and evening are at the lower end of "normal"--see question above, LOL) and I have never ever ever had the tiniest problem falling asleep or staying asleep. In fact, often I fall asleep on the couch-sometimes with half a mug of coffee in hand!

Really curious here and I want to make sure that the lab result is not in error. Its hard when the lab says your nighttime cortisol is way out of range but you have no symptoms that would back that up.

I don't know if he has DHEA, but you want to be at the top of the range on that.

Estradiol (saliva) 0.6 pg/ml 1.3-3.3 Premenopausal (Luteal)

Progesterone (saliva) 8 pg/ml 75-270 Premenopausal (Luteal)

cjco;3263 wrote:** Hormone Test 03/05/2012 Units Range

Estradiol (saliva) 0.6 pg/ml 1.3-3.3 Premenopausal (Luteal)

Progesterone (saliva) 8 pg/ml 75-270 Premenopausal (Luteal)

Ratio: Pg/E2 (saliva) 13 Optimal: 100-500 when E2 1.3-3.3 pg/ml

Testosterone (saliva) 31 pg/ml 16-55 (Age Dependent)

DHEAS (saliva) 6.8 ng/ml 2-23 (Age Dependent)

Cortisol Morning (saliva) 6.1 ng/ml 3.7-9.5

Cortisol Noon (saliva) 1.8 ng/ml 1.2-3.0

Cortisol Evening (saliva) 0.8 ng/ml 0.6-1.9

Cortisol Night (saliva) 2.4 ng/ml 0.4-1.0

Free T4 (blood spot) 1.6 ng/dL 0.7-2.5

Free T3 (blood spot) 2.5 pg/ml 2.5-6.5

TSH (blood spot) 0.6 uU/ml 0.5-3.0

TPO (blood spot) \* 15 IU/ml 0-150 (70-150 borderline)

Vitamin D, 25-OH, D2 < 4.0 ng/ml

# Thread: Cold Thermogenesis for dummies -
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-for-dummies.443/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### aug19 — 2012-03-26T03:18:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-for-dummies.443/post-6171>

Can anyone give me the break down of CT?

I want to add this to my ongoing LeptinRX/LeakyGut/AIPaleo protocol.

Plan on using my very deep garden tub.

I need to get a digital meat thermometer for temperature.

What temp water? (50's)

What temp skin?

Why wear Champion shirt and running pants?

I need to dunk my face in some too?

Any dangers?

Anything I need to watch out for?

Okay to do this daily?

I sit for 2-4 hours for work daily - I need to lose most in my butt so should I be sitting on ice packs?

Thanks for the advice.

Love this forum! I could read and read all day long. It's all I want to talk about with others but most aren't interested in learning. Want so bad to be successful to 1. be healthy and 2. encourage others to do some research

### marthabob2u — 2012-03-26T04:31:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-for-dummies.443/post-6199>

Start by face dunkng in cold water for as long as you can, then don't wait for the digital meat thermometer, just do it!

By all means if you are outside in a garden tub wear light clothing or the buzzards may circle!

What I have been doing is adding ice blocks to my 'cool' Mexican water and just soaking for as long as possible. I add ice packs to my lardy tummy and you could sit on a couple for sure. It is OK to get red skin but don't go beyond that. I often start with a washcloth between me and the ice block, then do skin contact for a while, keeping an eye on skin colour.

Do it as often as you can- I think every day is preferable. At first I didn't see much progress and then suddenly my waist dropped a couple of inches. It WORKS!

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-27T05:14:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vegan-men-better-in-bed.444/post-6777>

Here's her rebuttal. Don't read it if stuff like this gets your cortisol up, lol.

> They are obviously not very informed on hormones in their diets if they are worried about the phytoestrogens in soy and not all the excess hormones, including those produced by the lactating cows, in the animal flesh and bodily secretions they are consuming.
>
>
>
> According to wikipedia: Males
>
> A 2010 meta-analysis of fifteen placebo-controlled studies said that "neither soy foods nor isoflavone supplements alter measures of bioavailable testosterone concentrations in men."\[28\] Furthermore, Isoflavones supplementation has no effect on sperm concentration, count or motility, and show no changes in testicular or ejaculate volume.\[29\]\[30\]
>
>
>
> While this article from the Harvard archives talks about the dangerous levels of ANIMAL estrogens in dairy:
>
> [http://news.harvard.edu/gazette/2006/12.07/11-dairy.html](http://http://news.harvard.edu/gazette/2006/12.07/11-dairy.html)
>
>
>
> I just did a check on what, exactly, the paleo diet is. Seems like it omits dairy, which is great. Dairy is poison. That being said, when you consume animal flesh you are still consuming the hormones they produce, including estrogen. So if these paleo men think their diets are free from estrogens, they are sadly mistaken.
>
>
>
> In addition, not all vegans eat a lot of tofu. I eat almost none!
>
>
>
> Also, what I have found that affects my libido the most is the amount of vitamin D I am taking in. I'm not talking through food supplementation, but through actually being out in the sun. I have noticed a direct correlation between spending time in the sun without sunscreen below my face and my sex drive.
>
>
>
> As an aside, vegan gas isn't anywhere close to being rank like that of a meat eater!! =)
>
>
>
> They should check out the movie 'Forks Over Knives.' It explains the benefits of a plant based diet better than I ever could.
>
>
>
> Oh. Just to remind you. My point about vegans sexuality wasn't about libido, it was about impotence and how vegans, having healthier hearts and circulation due to the lack of cholesterol in our diets, don't have the issues with impotence that men who eat animal products do.
>
>
>
> Leave it to propaganda put out by the dairy industry to muddy the waters, though!!!

### GathererGatherer — 2012-04-02T21:14:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vegan-men-better-in-bed.444/post-10432>

Hi Souldanzer,

As an athletic vegan man for over half my life (I'm 31) with a high sex drive and bountiful libio, (and who often places first during burpees) I have to agree that science is on the side of your roommate and all the peanut-gallery here sound like a bunch of sexist bullies. Fructarians are vegan by default, as would be someone who eats air, but do not think that they represent any significant portion of those who consider themselves vegan. I am not at all spiritual and don't associate with Buddhists or anyone who aims to "transcend" this plane of existence (sex and all), and I believe that critics of vegan diets often associate and mistake what is popularly considered feminine (compassion, caring) with weakness. It is pretty offensive and sexist to say that empathy and compassion are weak. Protectors, defenders and heroes for those that are weaker than ourselves requires incredible strength both physical and that of character. Eating meat is simply a performance of power against the powerless.

It has been my experience, which of course is anecdotal, that most Paleo guys I've met are homophobic bullies who subscribe to romanticized stone-age gender roles - and it makes sense that vilifying anything involving estrogen is popular. How does one attempt to discredit veganism? By saying it causes men to have breasts and become women, which is a load of chauvinist BS. The mythology that if you eat muscle you become muscle is as silly as believe that eating tiger penis increases virility or eating brain increases intelligence. These are mythologies that flatter male identity but are not scientifically accurate, and there are many cultural rewards for reinforcing traditional male-dominance so these claims are often supported by groups who socially benefit from their percieved truthfulness.

As opportunistic omnivores, we can choose where we derive our nutrition. Of course you can be a healthy meat eater, but that doesn't mean that's the only way to be healthy. We are not carnivores.

The scientific truth is that "Dairy accounts for 60 percent to 80 percent of estrogens consumed"(1) and A 2010 meta-analysis of 15 placebo controlled studies showed that neither soy foods nor isoflavone supplements alter measures of bioavailable testosterone or estrogen concentrations in men.(2)

Check these vegans out:

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

1- [http://news.harvard.edu/gazette/2006/12.07/11-dairy.html](http://http://news.harvard.edu/gazette/2006/12.07/11-dairy.html)

2- Hamilton-Reeves, Jill M.; Vazquez, Gabriela; Duval, Sue J.; Phipps, William R.; Kurzer, Mindy S.; Messina, Mark J. (2010). "Clinical studies show no effects of soy protein or isoflavones on reproductive hormones in men: Results of a meta-analysis". Fertility and Sterility 94 (3): 997â€“1007._

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-03T01:13:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vegan-men-better-in-bed.444/post-10547>

> **GathererGatherer;10501 wrote:** Fructarians are vegan by default, as would be someone who eats air, but do not think that they represent any significant portion of those who consider themselves vegan.
>
>
>
> Eating meat is simply a performance of power against the powerless.

GathererGatherer, I think it's great you've weighed in with an alternate opinion. I was wondering if you've listened to what Matt LaLonde has to say about vegetarian/vegan living. He is not anti-vegetarian, but is specific about what constitutes optimal diets for humans. For example, this interview: [http://paleodietnews.com/4470/killing-some-sacred-cows-of-the-paleo-diet/](http://http://paleodietnews.com/4470/killing-some-sacred-cows-of-the-paleo-diet/)

For me (a woman and former vegetarian/vegan (for over 20 years)), these are not issues to be taken lightly. Nor do I think that derision of anyone is helpful.

# Thread: My lips were blue!!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lips-were-blue.447/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-26T18:06:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lips-were-blue.447/post-6404>

Is that bad? It certainly was a little off-putting, that's for sure! I've been doing CT for a month or so. coldest water was just below 50 degrees. That was to my belly button. The past two days the water has been slightly warmer (low 50's) but I've submerged to my neck. This morning when i got out after about twenty minutes, I looked in the mirror and my lips were blue!

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-26T19:16:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lips-were-blue.447/post-6437>

> **cjco;6440 wrote:** Is that bad? It certainly was a little off-putting, that's for sure! I've been doing CT for a month or so. coldest water was just below 50 degrees. That was to my belly button. The past two days the water has been slightly warmer (low 50's) but I've submerged to my neck. This morning when i got out after about twenty minutes, I looked in the mirror and my lips were blue!

I've never experienced this. I would say take it slower. Perhaps others with more experience can jump in.

### JC2K — 2012-03-26T19:58:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lips-were-blue.447/post-6455>

Blue lips = moderate hypothermia. You'll want to take it slower. Less time if you're submerging the whole body.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-26T20:01:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lips-were-blue.447/post-6456>

i've gotten blue lips from swimming in 80F water before. Not sure what the significance is.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-26T20:05:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lips-were-blue.447/post-6459>

I got blue lips when I was doing my scuba certification. The instructor wouldn't let anyone else go into the ocean (under 40 degree water) until my lips got some color in them. We waited over two hours. I was not the most popular gal that day!!! Funny thing is that I haven't seen them since that day about 20 years ago. My sis used to get them all the time going in any water.

Now sometimes doing CT some of my fingernails turn a little blue. Weird thing is that I don't even put my hands in the water!!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-26T20:15:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lips-were-blue.447/post-6464>

> **JC2K;6491 wrote:** Blue lips = moderate hypothermia. You'll want to take it slower. Less time if you're submerging the whole body.

Thanks. like I said, I've been doing this for a month or so. Colder water, even. But previously I had only submerged up to just under my breasts. This was my second time going up to my neck. I'm back to shivering, too, which Ia haven't had in a while. Also, my skin is much less red than it used to get on my legs. Now only my abdomen gets red. Even with the full submersion, I don't think my top half got red .....

### Lyndra — 2012-03-26T23:24:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lips-were-blue.447/post-6586>

Back in my windsurfing/waterskiing days, I often got blue lips when I got really cold (lakes in Colorado don't get very warm in the summer). I'd have to sit in the car with the heater running full blast for 20 minutes to warm up. I never got blue lips skiing, even on frigid days, so I think it's the water immersion that so effectively reduces core body temperature. During CT, I would avoid staying in so long that your lips turn blue.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-27T01:56:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lips-were-blue.447/post-6674>

cjco,

I've experienced this a few times myself. I asked Dr. Kruse about this awhile back, and he said it happens to him sometimes, too, and that it is not harmful if you are otherwise well. Check your core temperature, though...if you have blue lips AND are dropping your core temp, then back off for sure. Personally, it doesn't take much for me to get blue lips...I check my temp, and if all is well, then I know I'm good. Also, be sure you are checking in on yourself regularly...check your skin color, feeling, etc...you may just be prone to having blue lips.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-27T02:01:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lips-were-blue.447/post-6680>

> **lsutiger143;6710 wrote:** cjco,
>
>
>
> I've experienced this a few times myself. I asked Dr. Kruse about this awhile back, and he said it happens to him sometimes, too, and that it is not harmful if you are otherwise well. Check your core temperature, though...if you have blue lips AND are dropping your core temp, then back off for sure. Personally, it doesn't take much for me to get blue lips...I check my temp, and if all is well, then I know I'm good. Also, be sure you are checking in on yourself regularly...check your skin color, feeling, etc...you may just be prone to having blue lips.

Thanks. I felt fine, other than being cold and shivering (which stopped but started again once I've started full submersion). Is an oral temp OK?

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-27T02:15:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lips-were-blue.447/post-6688>

> **cjco;6716 wrote:** Thanks. I felt fine, other than being cold and shivering (which stopped but started again once I've started full submersion). Is an oral temp OK?

Yeah, oral temp is all I do. Believe me, I was freaked out the first time I walked in my bathroom from my pool and saw the blue lips/ghost face!!! But, I know from the past that even if I would eat ice cream or alot of ice, my lips would lose some color. If you are still worried, you can always have someone hang around while you CT. You feeling fine otherwise?

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-27T02:15:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lips-were-blue.447/post-6689>

Oh, wait, you said you did feel fine. I should learn to read =)

# Thread: Hack my lack of desire for cold tub?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-26T22:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-6558>

Day 3 of baths.

Day one, two ten min baths.

Day two, two twenty min baths

Today - do not want.

After last nights, i was cold feeling through half the night. Am still feeling chilly now. I walked for an hour this am in 50 degree weather.

I have no instinct on what to do. I have the feeling i used to get before a hard planned exercise. Dread.

I know i can power through it and do it. But should i give body some recovery time? Just take a step back?

### hazyjane — 2012-03-26T23:10:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-6579>

Go slow with it! There were times I sensed that my body didn't want to do a cold bath, so I just iced instead or skipped CT that day. When I didn't listen to my body, I always had a hard time warming up. I figured out that if I hadn't slept well, it was not a good time to CT.

If I was feeling chilled already, I'd do an alternating hot/cold shower ending on cold, but I'd keep the cold parts short (this always warmed me up).

After a few weeks, there were days I started to crave the cold water!

### Sherry — 2012-03-26T23:13:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-6582>

I've been there with the lack of desire for jumping into the tub. Honestly, I just had to talk myself into it with the promises of all the benefits down the road because it sure isn't instant gratification for me. And if that doesn't work for me I don't do it. I've been trying to do 4-5 baths per week and if I don't have the desire some days I tell myself that the icing at night just has to suffice.

And, that lack of desire can very well be that the body needs some space. So, take that step back Cavemam and give yourself a hug for doing this in the first place. Think about how many people think we're nuts and have 1001 excuses of why not to do it. At least you're doing it no matter the times when you take a break or how hard it is to think about being in that cold water. We all deserve a hug for being here.

### Marsha — 2012-03-27T00:25:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-6627>

Go back to your face dunks...that relaxed you, right?

The other night I used the snorkel for awhile and tried to breathe very slowly - was quite nice! Then I decided I had to try the 'mammilian dive reflex' and held my breath for several dunks. Each time I went back in the water seemed warmer- but was still about 50 degrees.

I just read on the FACTOR X thread that cold water on the face/head makes everything else in the tub feel warmer. Don't know if I can make myself do it, but at least the seed is planted!

Last night got an ice burn while watching Frozen Planet, but will post about that at a more appropriate place.

Good luck, Cavemam - I've been feeling cold today, too, so you're not the only one!

### bonita — 2012-03-27T01:03:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-6646>

i agree with the others, go slow. i'm not really set up to do much with the ice packs, but i'm flush with cold water so i started with what i thought were cold showers (thermometer eventually told me otherwise). eventually i just sat down during a "cold" shower one day and put the plug in the tub. i started at 80F and originally started lying down--but that's actually much harder. shivered constantly, so i sat up and started lowering the temperature every time. (i also put towels under my butt and feet so they're not directly on the metal and started wearing my bra--it doesn't keep me warmer, it's just feels... i dunno, less vulnerable somehow.) after 2 weeks i found myself sitting in 55 degree water for 15 minutes.

now i start around 48-49 degrees (so it will still be under 55 when i'm done) and sit for 35-40 minutes and lie back for another 15. if you can only do a couple minutes lying back then just do that. interestingly in the link jack provided,

[http://www.me-cvs.nl/index.php?pageid=3076](http://http://www.me-cvs.nl/index.php?pageid=3076)

the doc there explains his protocol for water therapy (20 years ago) and he's basically suggesting the same thing i've done.

sometimes i have to push myself a bit to get to the tub, but it's not what i'd call a struggle. and the funny things is, i only have about 10 seconds of discomfort in the whole time i'm in the tub. when i first turn the water on and when it reaches my hoohaw and then when i lie back... the takes a few birthing breathes i must say. i am a chilled when i get out so i just pile on clothes and take the dog for a walk and come back toasty.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-27T02:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-6687>

> **bonita;6682 wrote:** i agree with the others, go slow. i'm not really set up to do much with the ice packs, but i'm flush with cold water so i started with what i thought were cold showers (thermometer eventually told me otherwise). eventually i just sat down during a "cold" shower one day and put the plug in the tub. i started at 80F and originally started lying down--but that's actually much harder. shivered constantly, so i sat up and started lowering the temperature every time. (i also put towels under my butt and feet so they're not directly on the metal and started wearing my bra--it doesn't keep me warmer, it's just feels... i dunno, less vulnerable somehow.) after 2 weeks i found myself sitting in 55 degree water for 15 minutes.
>
>
>
> now i start around 48-49 degrees (so it will still be under 55 when i'm done) and sit for 35-40 minutes and lie back for another 15. if you can only do a couple minutes lying back then just do that. interestingly in the link jack provided,
>
>
>
> [http://www.me-cvs.nl/index.php?pageid=3076](http://http://www.me-cvs.nl/index.php?pageid=3076)
>
>
>
> the doc there explains his protocol for water therapy (20 years ago) and he's basically suggesting the same thing i've done.
>
>
>
> sometimes i have to push myself a bit to get to the tub, but it's not what i'd call a struggle. and the funny things is, i only have about 10 seconds of discomfort in the whole time i'm in the tub. when i first turn the water on and when it reaches my hoohaw and then when i lie back... the takes a few birthing breathes i must say. i am a chilled when i get out so i just pile on clothes and take the dog for a walk and come back toasty.

Ok everyone, Thanks! I very much appreciate, and I think it's sage advice. I did end up sitting down in the cold tub naked this time, instead of donning the compression. Speaking of whoo-ha, YOOOUUUUCH. So I started shivering right away - and only spent 5 mins. I think I am going to back up to face dunks and icing until I want to try bath again. . .of course I'm shivery and cold right now. I bet once I start to feel warm again, I will be ready for the bath again. Hopeful. I also noticed the girls did NOT appreciate the water. Nip-objection at full blast.

### bonita — 2012-03-27T02:26:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-6694>

> **Cavemam;6723 wrote:** I also noticed the girls did NOT appreciate the water. Nip-objection at full blast.

no kidding. i wonder if that's where the expression "it's getting a bit nippy out" comes from? although interesting, only problematic for me on the right side, left is always fine, though not exactly relaxed.

just remember, any temperature below your body temperature creates a gradient that will lead to cold adaptation... just depends on how much time you wanna spend getting there. oh, and the girls should always be deferred to.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-27T19:15:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-7139>

I think you need to walk the line between pushing yourself and listening to your body. It's kind of like any sort of physical training - without some pressure to adapt, you won't, but if you overdo it, you risk going backwards either physically or mentally. Ask yourself what about the cold tub do you dread - is it the temperature, duration, or possibly both? Consider raising the temperature; Dr. K said any temperature differential would have a positive effect so it doesn't have to be right-out-of-the-tap cold. Or cut back the time - if 10 minutes sounds bearable, then do ten minutes. Wait until something inside you tells you to go for a lower temperature or longer duration and then go forward slowly. Maybe there's a boredom factor at work. Are you reading, listening to a podcast, or doing something that might help make the time go by faster? I'm all in favor of doing whatever works for you.

I find that the hardest point is right before getting in the tub, but once I do it, it's OK. I also give myself permission to get out early if that's what I feel I need that day. I also stay mindful of my motivation for doing this - the long-term improvements I am striving to make. That helps me get over the negative self-talk that tells me to skip it for today. I am also keeping a log of what I'm doing and how I feel. It's good to go back a few days and see how much better I'm feeling and how much easier it is to do.

### differentstory — 2012-03-28T18:25:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-7752>

I have a suggestion for you.

Don't fill the tub all the way up. I filled it all the way up in the beginning with 80 degree water thinking I would just keep stepping down the temp but I quickly started hating CT baths. So, instead, I did the coldest temperature I could and just did 3-4 inches of bath water and only did my butt and legs (at first crossed legged so my thighs didn't get in the water and then I started straightening them out). Once I felt COMPLETELY fine with that and could stay in and be warm for at least 30 minutes, I started pouring in more water and found that I transitioned from 4 inches to full emersion in a matter of 2 days! I was so stoked! The first time I had the water up to my chest and it was freezing cold (not me but the water per my husband), I started giggling uncontrollably. HA! I was thinking, "HOLY AMAZEBALLS! I am totally DOING THIS!!"

My husband gapes at me in awe.  Love it! It really should be only 30 seconds of pain and the rest is easy. So if it's not, do not put yourself through the misery.

My new thing is that I am having a hard time warming up. I find that it takes me 6+ hours to warm up (I'm not in very good shape). So, my new deal is that I only give myself an hour of natural warm-up and then I bring in electronic heat (stove, blanket, hot shower, whatever). Having what turns into an 8 hour CT session with how cold I feel afterwards was also making me want to quit, so now I'm giving myself a bit of a break. I absolutely know I'll get there. Maybe it will just take me 6 months.

I often picture myself jumping into a frozen pond while at Thanksgiving with the family and watching everyone gasp in shock and amazement. So fun!! That daydream alone keeps me going. HA!

Oh, also wanted to say, that I have found when you are cold-adapted that stepping the temp down 3-5 degrees is not a big deal in the least. So, I think the first few days I started with 60 degree water. Now, the water fluctuates anywhere from 55 to 60 and I really don't even notice. So don't feel like you need to do 50 right off the bat. Wait until you are cold-adapted. (With our water here I can't seem to get it down to 50 yet - I'm already at 1 milk jug of frozen water. I think I'm going to have to do 3. Thank goodness we have a big tub!)

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-28T19:16:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-7780>

Here's my approach: I get in the tub, _then_ turn the water on. It's much less bracing. The water just slowly migrates up over me. The only place I sort of "jump on in" is in the mountain stream I frequent on the weekends. That's much harder, but I don't stay in as long. I'm am, however, sort of getting addicted to a certain sensation: running down a mountain trail after a 20 minute soak in "snow run-off" cold water...pure euphoria. I might just start carrying a spear...

### differentstory — 2012-03-28T19:25:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-7786>

> **Dan in Utah;7822 wrote:** Here's my approach: I get in the tub, _then_ turn the water on. It's much less bracing. The water just slowly migrates up over me. The only place I sort of "jump on in" is in the mountain stream I frequent on the weekends. That's much harder, but I don't stay in as long. I'm am, however, sort of getting addicted to a certain sensation: running down a mountain trail after a 20 minute soak in "snow run-off" cold water...pure euphoria. I might just start carrying a spear...

hysterical! Don't trip and fall on it though.

### agatha — 2012-03-28T23:13:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-7939>

I don't do my bath if I'm feeling cold and tired - I feel like my body is telling me to slow down. Sometimes I have been cold and gone for a walk and felt warm enough to do my bath when I returned. I'm also not doing a bath tonight because I have a detox headache - I figure let the body clear this bunch of toxins before releasing more.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T23:40:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-7956>

I've enjoyed recovery from a morning bath much more than from evening bath. Morning - being active and follow-up child out of mind. Night - cold trying not to shiver while I try to relax.

### caroline — 2012-03-28T23:49:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-7962>

> **Dan in Utah;7822 wrote:** Here's my approach: I get in the tub, _then_ turn the water on. It's much less bracing. The water just slowly migrates up over me. The only place I sort of "jump on in" is in the mountain stream I frequent on the weekends. That's much harder, but I don't stay in as long. I'm am, however, sort of getting addicted to a certain sensation: running down a mountain trail after a 20 minute soak in "snow run-off" cold water...pure euphoria. I might just start carrying a spear...

What an amazing picture that would be! Clothed or not?

### nonchalant — 2012-03-29T04:59:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-8096>

Cavemam, can you up the temperature? It took me weeks to get the temp down to 55F, and that was after a couple weeks of face-dunking at 50-55F. I look forward to my baths, and only the first one was shocking, at 65F. And I only up the time by 5 minutes, and that is only if I feel the last time was too easy.

### SimonM — 2012-04-01T01:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-9479>

> **Dan in Utah;7822 wrote:** Here's my approach: I get in the tub, _then_ turn the water on. It's much less bracing. The water just slowly migrates up over me.

Me too. I am a total whimp about being wet AND cold.

Isn't bitter melon extract supposed to make this easier?

### BJK77 — 2012-04-01T02:30:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-lack-of-desire-for-cold-tub.450/post-9577>

> **agatha;7981 wrote:** I don't do my bath if I'm feeling cold and tired - I feel like my body is telling me to slow down. Sometimes I have been cold and gone for a walk and felt warm enough to do my bath when I returned. I'm also not doing a bath tonight because I have a detox headache - I figure let the body clear this bunch of toxins before releasing more.

I'm the same. I have some days where I literally crave the cold bath - still don't enjoy it! - but do crave it and feel like I need it. Other days, like today when it's damp out and I spent 2 hours outside freezing this morning, my body has just had ENOUGH and I figure it needs a break to recover from what it has already endured. My toes are still trying to warm up from earlier and I've now been inside for 6 hours although we've been keeping the heater off so it's only 59 degrees in here!

I just think it's so important to listen to your body. I've been averaging 5 baths per week and I'm happy with that. The CT protocol suggests 2-5 times depending on your weight and health issues. I don't really have any weight to lose, just focusing on reversing my hypothyroidism and improving hormone levels. I'm hopeful that 5 days a week will get me where I need to be.

# Thread: opposite problem to Sandy....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opposite-problem-to-sandy.451/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### janagram — 2012-03-27T00:08:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opposite-problem-to-sandy.451/post-6615>

....known as constipation from VLC....what do you all do?

### Lyndra — 2012-03-27T00:15:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opposite-problem-to-sandy.451/post-6622>

Magnesium. I use magnesium citrate because it's cheap & easy to find. I take it first thing in the morning and then before bed, both times on an empty stomach to let it digest. It took quite a while for me to find the right dose and periodically I have to adjust it. I was a big user of "healthy whole grains" and needed a lot of magnesium when I transitioned to Paleo because of the fiber differences.

Dr. K has a lot of info about magnesium at [http://jackkruse.com/gnolls-com-opens-the-door-to-obesity-fight/](http://http://jackkruse.com/gnolls-com-opens-the-door-to-obesity-fight/)

### janagram — 2012-03-27T00:54:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opposite-problem-to-sandy.451/post-6644>

Lyndra...thanks. will get some mag citrate in case that works better than the malate I take.

### Guest — 2012-03-27T14:37:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opposite-problem-to-sandy.451/post-6923>

We will recover any problem so we will get a lot of satisfaction as well as perfect health and

remarkable goal so be conscious and happy for well growth.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-04-11T04:08:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opposite-problem-to-sandy.451/post-13586>

What say people about Potassium with the Magnesium. I know some ppl think you need K with Mg together...?

# Thread: Things to brush up on,  and perhaps come prepared with,  when trying a new doc
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/things-to-brush-up-on-and-perhaps-come-prepared-with-when-trying-a-new-doc.454/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-27T02:40:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/things-to-brush-up-on-and-perhaps-come-prepared-with-when-trying-a-new-doc.454/post-6705>

Ok,

So I really want to get the labs done, and so I've made an appointment with a new doc. I'm extremely nervous. I hate going into all of this. I'm a writer not a talker (classic introvert). What information/printouts etc would you think to be helpful when talking to a doc?

I've got the great list of Labs to request, but I'm pretty sure I can't just sit down and go "would you please just run these labs and not stress me out with your CW? Thanks"

Docs on here? what would you respond to? Are docs all worn out from patients bringing them websites?

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-27T03:41:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/things-to-brush-up-on-and-perhaps-come-prepared-with-when-trying-a-new-doc.454/post-6731>

I am pretty jaded when it comes to a regular doc. I personally would try to find a paleo doc or a D.O. who at least has heard of paleo. But I even had a D.O. who is supposed to be into alternative types of medicine tell me to stop eating fat because I'm overweight. \*sigh\*

On the plus side, you are able to order some lab tests for yourself. The downside is insurance won't pay for them.

### nonchalant — 2012-03-28T00:17:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/things-to-brush-up-on-and-perhaps-come-prepared-with-when-trying-a-new-doc.454/post-7394>

You could make a notation about why you want some of the tests..

Also, if you have copies of previous tests results that might be helpful.

The doctor has to be able to justify the tests for your insurance company. Also, he/she will want to run some basic stuff, just because you're new. You might have to wait for the second visit.

Jimmy Moore and Robb Wolfe have lists of doctors who understand the real science.
