# Thread: Neoprene-Do you use it?  Feet in or out?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vwaggs — 2012-03-27T03:59:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-6742>

I used to keep my feet out of the bath but it was a pain so I bought the neoprene 5mm socks and it helps so much keeping my feet warm. However, I seem to be having more shivering and want to get out faster. I do have more detox symptoms now though too (4 weeks in) so that could also be part of it. I know this isn't a linear process but I thought the cold would be easier to take by now. I had ONE great bath that didn't make me shiver and my furnace kicked on right after and I was warm in no time. What happened?? It thought I was over the difficult part. I read Dr. K say on his blog to not use modern items (neoprene) to help cope. Use only what Mother Nature gave us. My feet hurt so bad in the cold though. Do I just leave them out of the water or tough it out? Seems like the cold would be harder to take with feet in. Who puts feet in? Arms? I guess I was under the impression to keep the extremeties out. What do you all do?

### DarleenMB — 2012-03-27T05:21:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-6782>

I'm not at the bath stage yet but I WILL be leaving my feet and hands OUT of the water. When my feet get chilled I cannot get warm. I say do what is comfortable for you at this point. If it's too much to keep your feet in the water, take 'em out.

### Vicster — 2012-03-27T05:25:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-6785>

I use the neoprene booties, water past my breasts, hands out. I have bouts of easy days and hard days in the last month, sometimes I shiver other times not. Detox is a funny thing. Dr. K says that even he still shivers form time to time. Be sure to eat a high fat/protein meal prior to CT and drink a couple of big glasses of ice water.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-27T05:47:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-6795>

FYI. Dr. Kruse's take on Neoprene booties:

Kami Says:

March 23rd, 2012 at 6:49 pm

HI Dr. K I have been doing the CT for 34 days on only missed 3 days. The first 3 weeks went well and I was wearing socks in the water. I was seeing improvements in mood and sleep. In the 4th week I added neoprene booties and now I am shivering in the water and not craving the water anymore. Could the booties have something to do with it?

Jack Says:

March 24th, 2012 at 8:43 am

@Kami……stop using modern things to offset the program…..use what mother nature gave you.

PS-I just keep my feet out for now because of Reyanuds. Sometimes my toes turn white EVEN outside the water.

### vwaggs — 2012-03-27T15:53:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-6959>

Patty Cakes that was the exact post I was referring to! My feet would get numb for hours even out of the tub with slipper socks on. The Neoprene helped this issue a lot.

Guess I will try putting in my feet tonight to see what happens. They hurt just thinking about it LOL! I wonder how many others put in their feet?? Did you do it right away or work up to it? Am I missing out by not putting in my feet or will I adapt just fine keeping them out?

### agatha — 2012-03-27T16:50:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-6992>

I use neoprene gloves in the bath - they really help. But I do cold water dunking of my hands at other times to help the adaptation. I will try without the gloves soon. I don't have socks but I take my feet out of the water after i first get in and then gradually lower them in towards the end of the time - by this time it feels quite pleasant to do this.

### sk1nny_f4t — 2012-03-27T17:16:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7018>

> **vwaggs;6996 wrote:** I wonder how many others put in their feet?? Did you do it right away or work up to it? Am I missing out by not putting in my feet or will I adapt just fine keeping them out?

My experience has been opposite of yours, I just dove right in, 3 soaks down wearing only a knit hat in a 50degree trash can filled with water and ice. I crouch so that the water is just below my chest and each of the times I have done 30 min soaks it takes at least an hour, if not more, for my feet to come back to normal. If you have ever been reading on the toilet for so long that your feet fall asleep, that's how mine feel so I am ordering a pair of the booties today.

### freshveggies — 2012-03-27T17:44:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7035>

HI Vwaggs, I forgot to reply back to you about the neoprene on the blog. I have used very light weight wool socks in the tub, but am considering not using anything based on what Dr. K said. My 06/03 ratio is good so I shouldn't have a problem with the water on my feet. I think it will help me adapt better if I dont' cover my feet.

### freshveggies — 2012-03-27T17:48:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7037>

I get in the tub then turn the cold water on and let it fill. Since I quite the neoprene, I don't shiver in the water like I was after I started the neoprene. I just thought it was a short cut and would work. No cold feet---how nice. It back fired.

### ealachan — 2012-03-27T18:14:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7061>

This is not particularly helpful to the point of the thread at all, I know, but I just have to say that the mental images I conjure up of y'all trying to keep hands and feet out of the bath while soaking amuse me to no end. I wish I was a better artist - this begs for cartooning...

### Susan M. — 2012-03-27T18:52:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7102>

> **Vicster;6821 wrote:** I use the neoprene booties, water past my breasts, hands out. I have bouts of easy days and hard days in the last month, sometimes I shiver other times not. Detox is a funny thing. Dr. K says that even he still shivers form time to time. Be sure to eat a high fat/protein meal prior to CT and drink a couple of big glasses of ice water.

Is shivering while you are IN the water a sign of detoxing? I notice that only the parts of me that are in the water are shivering. My legs, while in the water, shiver to the point of almost being painful.

As for the feet, I used the neoprene booties at first to keep the spray from the faucet off of my feet while the tub was filling up. Now, I can submerge my feet with the booties on. I take them off after the first ten minutes or so, but I wind up putting them back on because they still feel better on in the water. I will be glad when I don't need them any more.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-27T19:05:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7125>

I've been doing cold baths for 3 weeks now and up until a couple of days ago, was using neoprene booties. After reading that exchange on the blog, I switched to wool socks. My toes get quite cold and my feet feel "tingly" afterwards, but in an odd way, that tingly sensation feels good. I was afraid I'd get that awful pins & needles feeling you get when your feet are really cold, but not at all.

I haven't noticed a difference in how much I shiver while in the tub. It seems that if I put my upper arms in the water, I shiver off-and-on throughout, but if I keep them out, I don't shiver until the last few minutes. I'm working up to more arm immersion, but slowly. Much as I usually like to rush through things, I've decided on this one to listen to my body and go at a reasonable pace.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-27T19:08:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7131>

I put my feet in. The first week of baths it made my feet really ache. No problems with it now though. In fact, yesterday my husband and I spent almost an hour in our in-ground MICHIGAN pool. Pool thermometer said it was 56 degrees. The outside temp was 42 degrees. If any of the neighbors saw us, they probably think we've gone mad!

### Vicster — 2012-03-27T21:58:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7312>

> **Susan M.;7139 wrote:** Is shivering while you are IN the water a sign of detoxing? I notice that only the parts of me that are in the water are shivering. My legs, while in the water, shiver to the point of almost being painful.
>
>
>
> As for the feet, I used the neoprene booties at first to keep the spray from the faucet off of my feet while the tub was filling up. Now, I can submerge my feet with the booties on. I take them off after the first ten minutes or so, but I wind up putting them back on because they still feel better on in the water. I will be glad when I don't need them any more.

I don't shiver while in the water. It takes about 4 minutes to fill the tub while I sit in it and that to me is the hardest part. After the tub is full I am comfortable. It's when I get out and towel off that I sometimes shiver, sometimes not. Since I am feeling comfortable in the water I will try it without the booties and see how my feet feel. Before I used them I could hardly walk after CT, but that's been a few weeks ago so it might be better now.

### Ane — 2012-03-27T23:19:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7368>

Wow...I never thought about booties. I just get in and my feet go numb within a couple of minutes.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-27T23:30:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7376>

I've been using wool socks. Works for me. No issues during or after the bath, so I'm gonna stick with this, until I build up the courage to go barefoot in the tub. For now, having compression shorts and top, with the socks and a hat help me psychologically, even though I know I don't \*need\* them. I'm going to work towards weaning myself of them and going nekkid.

### differentstory — 2012-03-28T18:48:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7766>

> **Vicster;7350 wrote:** I don't shiver while in the water. It takes about 4 minutes to fill the tub while I sit in it and that to me is the hardest part. After the tub is full I am comfortable. It's when I get out and towel off that I sometimes shiver, sometimes not. Since I am feeling comfortable in the water I will try it without the booties and see how my feet feel. Before I used them I could hardly walk after CT, but that's been a few weeks ago so it might be better now.

I had this problem too, Vicster. I found that the noise of the water pouring in and the cold water splashes aggrevated me. So now, instead, last night I filled the tub up and then got in when it was quiet. A MILLION TIMES BETTER! It was definitely hard to sit in the tub at first but I just sat down, counted to 43 seconds and I was fine. I will be doing that now on.

Also, I use the booties as well. Maybe one day I won't, but for now this is hard enough. I think we should take it slow and I personally think shivering and booties have nothing to do with each other. I have found that eating and shivering do though. If I haven't eaten as much I find my body seems to lack the energy to keep me warm and I start to shiver. Just my theory (and I have a lot of those! HA!).

My feet get so cold that I keep glancing at them thinking they must be blue. That was why I started the booties. And I remember thinking, "if my feet didn't hurt so damned much, this wouldn't even be hard!!" So that's why I got the booties.

### youthfuliz — 2012-03-28T18:55:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7770>

I've got neoprene booties after reading about them here.  I use them in cold bath/shower and keep my feet in water. I take them off for the last 10 minutes though since by then I feel like I want my feet to be at the same temperature as the rest of my body.  That's also why I tend to wash my hair and let cold water run down the back of my head toward the end of the cold shower.

### JC2K — 2012-03-28T19:02:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7773>

Personally, I'm planning on using the booties/gloves until I cold adapt more, then take them off. So it's a short term thing rather than a permanent thing.

### youthfuliz — 2012-03-28T19:03:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7774>

And for those of you who have cold feet for hours after the cold bath/shower, I found a way to fix it: use your feet for 10 minutes after the shower/bath, do a little running/walking/jumping/leg lifts with ankle weights, etc, get blood running in your legs/feet, they will become warm within minutes.  It works for me!

### JC2K — 2012-03-28T19:12:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7779>

> **Susan M.;7139 wrote:** Is shivering while you are IN the water a sign of detoxing?

Yes and no. The shivering is not a symptom of detoxing. The shivering is your body trying to warm itself up, causing your body to draw on those fat cells for energy (at some point, depending on your food).The detoxing comes from the emptying of the fat cells for energy. But I have no idea how much at one time. It's not like you use a pound of fat in one session. So the toxins come out because of shivering, you don't shiver because of toxins. Clearer?

### Susan M. — 2012-03-28T19:50:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7801>

> **JC2K;7821 wrote:** Yes and no. The shivering is not a symptom of detoxing. The shivering is your body trying to warm itself up, causing your body to draw on those fat cells for energy (at some point, depending on your food).The detoxing comes from the emptying of the fat cells for energy. But I have no idea how much at one time. It's not like you use a pound of fat in one session. So the toxins come out because of shivering, you don't shiver because of toxins. Clearer?

Thank you JC2K for the explanation. I was thinking about it last night, and I guess I should have said that I have major goosebump on my legs while I am in the water. They really look unnatural. I don't have any goosebumps on my top half while I am in the water, and most of my noticeable shivering is done after I have been out of the tub for about five to ten minutes and continues sporadically for about 30 minutes.

Thanks again for the response. A little more clear, yes. It takes me a long time to figure out things. I took a statin drug for only about a year, but it really messed up my ability to focus, and I am still recovering I suspect. I know that is a poor excuse, but it is what it is, but eventually I get there. I haven't noticed much in the way of detoxing, I guess. Just starting to get some loose squishy fat on my stomach. I notice that the next day, if I haven't done my soak yet, that it has firmed up just a bit. I really don't know what that is all about. I guess I should hope for some more out-of-the tub shivering?

This is all so facinating!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T19:57:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7803>

I use the booties I find if I don't my feet cramp badly and I can't stay in the water. even with the booties I can get cramping but my water temp is about 47F

### Jim — 2012-03-28T20:42:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-7846>

No booties for me. I get the water running then add some Epsom Salts. I step in barefooted and help the salts dissolve with my feet. Then I sit down while the tub "fills". The water only comes up to about my waist so my upper body is out of the water. I have ice packs on my thighs, the tops of which are exposed, and on my tummy. I move them around a bit to make sure I'm not getting frostbite. When I take my cold shower I also start with my legs and move up body. Of course with the shower I am getting everything wet. The back goes last but by then I am fairly acclimated. My feet are now fine using this process. When I first started they would be a bit stiff. Give it time and things will get better.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-13T06:46:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neoprene-do-you-use-it-feet-in-or-out.456/post-14599>

I haven't used the neoprene booties for two weeks now. I find it's sufficient to keep my feet out from time to time. I alternate between sitting up with water up to my stomach, feet in, and full body immersion, up to my neck, and keep my feet out. I'll keep the booties to use in the future when I walk in a lake - in case the sand is not smooth or there're shells...

# Thread: DELICIOUS desert...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/delicious-desert.461/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### gemstar — 2012-03-27T08:54:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/delicious-desert.461/post-6882>

My sweetener of choice is date syrup. Since I live in Israel this is readily available and unlike honey, even the strictest paleo person will accept it as a sweetener. I created these cookie balls last week, and they are fantastic.

Eyeballed recipe...btw. But I will try to make it something that I can pass on to others.

About 6 cups maybe 7 cups of dry stuff. Since this is a 'ball' cookie, you can play with this until you get it right. My dry ingredients were made up of what I have around.

1 cup hazelnuts, almost ground to a flour

1 cup almonds " " "

1 cup freshly ground flax seeds

1 cup freshly ground whole seseme seeds

1 cup coarsely ground pumpkin seeds

1 cup coarsely ground sunflower seeds

1/2 - 1 cup 85% chocolate (optional but heaven)

1 cup whole grain tahini sauce

1/2 cup date syrup.

Mix wet and dry ingredients well until it forms a dough that will stick together. If too dry add some more tehini sauce. If too wet, add some more of the ground to flour nuts or flax. Form into small balls the size of walnuts and roll in white seseme seeds. Refrigerate on large tray then when hard transfer to a container. Keep refrigerated.

I deliberately did not use coconut in this, just to work on a non coconut recipe. But I make the same thing with coconut too. I also then add some coconut oil along with the tehini. I also usually add dried fruit to the coconut balls, usually organic dried cranberries. As you can probably tell, this is the kind of thing that begs for experimentation. Since this is not a baked item you basically cannot go wrong, and you can constantly taste it to know what to add or cut back on.

ENJOY!!!

I hope it's ok to post a recipe in this section.

### Guest — 2012-03-27T14:32:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/delicious-desert.461/post-6920>

In any place of earth we will get a lot of ideas and always feel a perfect observation as well as freshness

and breeze so we will be happy and enjoy atmospheric n any stage.

### gemstar — 2012-03-28T08:16:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/delicious-desert.461/post-7618>

Thanks Jolly John.....this is soooo true!

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-28T09:52:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/delicious-desert.461/post-7633>

That sounds delicious! Sending to wife . . .

### Snowyky — 2012-04-05T21:14:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/delicious-desert.461/post-11837>

Why.. so she can print it out for you to make later?

### Marsha — 2012-03-28T04:11:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-on-top-of-the-covers-naked-anyone.463/post-7510>

My husband and I both sleep in the nude.

For winter, we each have our own 'dyne' - Norwegian for duvet or eiderdown comforter...otherwise you get too hot with two bodies under one!

Since starting this whole new way of living (Leptin reset and CT) with windows open, I'm shoving the whole thing off of me by 3AM and reaching for it after I cool off. Then maybe just my feet out till I'm convinced it's getting light out (been wearing a sleep mask and ear plugs) and get up to start my day.

Soon it will be so hot in Colorado, that I'll only need a sheet and/or AC, but that won't last long if I keep up with the CT!!!

# Thread: CT is Fasted State Training
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-is-fasted-state-training.464/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2012-03-27T14:30:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-is-fasted-state-training.464/post-6918>

To continue my "Fasted State" training adaptations thread, there is something else which I have noticed that correlates with CT.

Doing ultra long distance, fasted state and minimal fueling exercise puts the body in a significant calorie deficit. As mentioned I have been doing this for years, yet my body weight has remained relatively stable, my LMM has increased and my BF % has reduced. Again, non-controlled, N=1, so no hard data to go on.

To put things into perspective, here's an example of one of my days

Wake: 7am

Breakfast : 2 x freshly ground coffee's

8am: pre race warm up run (1hr), easy jog over mountainous terrain

9.30 - 2pm: 52Km mountain ultra race, tough race over mountainous terrain, ran harder (i.e. at a higher threshold) than normal longer races

Result: 4th

Intake During: approx 500kcal

Post Race Intake: approx 2500kcal

Conservative Estimate of a 5.5hr mountain run EE = 3000kcal

Add BMR/PRAL of say 2000kcal and you have a total EE of ~5000kcal... and I still think thats conservative.

So intake for the day was roughly 3000kcal, expenditure was approx 2000kcal greater.... do the math !!

There has got to be some serious adaptation mechanisms possibly related to T3/T4 andd leptin going on ?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Pacing Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-27T15:42:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-6948>

How do you know you are ready to add on another 5-10 minutes to the cold immersion bath?

I want to keep advancing, but I also don't want to push it too far.

### vwaggs — 2012-03-27T15:56:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-6964>

I just forced it. Mind over matter in my case. I play podcasts to keep my mind busy so I don't keep complaining in my head LOL. Makes the time fly by now! How long are you going now? How cold?

### indigogirl — 2012-03-27T16:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-6986>

Are you both gradually cold adapting, or did you just take plunge?

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-27T18:42:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-7084>

I'm not sure if this is gradual or not. But I did the face dunking, then the icepack to 60 minutes. Then started with 10 minute 50F baths.

Now I am up to 35 minutes at 50 F. But I'm just not sure when to push it to 40 minutes and beyond.

### shilohman — 2012-03-27T18:49:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-7096>

> **villjamur\_stevenson;7121 wrote:** I'm not sure if this is gradual or not. But I did the face dunking, then the icepack to 60 minutes. Then started with 10 minute 50F baths.
>
> Now I am up to 35 minutes at 50 F. But I'm just not sure when to push it to 40 minutes and beyond.

Just let your body tell you. If you are shivering really bad after the 35 minute bath, then stick to the 35 minutes and allow your body to adapt. The shivering won't go away, but it should subside as you adapt. If I eat right before the bath, then I find that I shiver the least or very little. Make sure you are supplementing or eating bitter melon.

### indigogirl — 2012-03-27T18:57:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-7111>

How long to face dunk before doing CT in a bath?

I assume I can do ice packs on specific body parts at any time during the adaptation?

Do I really need to measure temp of water and my skin?

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-27T18:58:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-7113>

Initially, I shivered the first immersion bath I did.

But since then, nada.

I don't shiver at all. But after 35 minutes, I am darn cold!!! And it takes a long time to warm up - couple of hours.

# Thread: Starting Wk4 of LRx first CT bath yesterday.  Fibro flares..big time.  Any input????
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-wk4-of-lrx-first-ct-bath-yesterday-fibro-flares-big-time-any-input.467/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2012-03-27T17:04:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-wk4-of-lrx-first-ct-bath-yesterday-fibro-flares-big-time-any-input.467/post-7006>

Hi all long time reader 1st time poster.

I have a long history of chronic illness that came to head in 2009 with a lyme, babesia, bartonella diagnosis. 18+ months of antibiotics/anti malarials (gut killers) and countless supplements later I am like a new person. All cognitive functions are back, joint pain gone, migraines gone, fevers gone, symptoms of LUPUS gone, CFS gone, no more shingles flare ups. All labs show massive improvement all around.

That said, my fibromyalgia was gone as well until now. Any reason why after 3 full weeks of Leptin Rx and first CT bath my fibro would flare? Its not debilitating just this searing burn in the back and neck I remember all to well. While in the cold bath (59 degrees...my tap doesnt get any colder) my back really seized up on me. I lasted 20 mins but the back pain got me out...not the cold.

I am still waging war against systemic candida, low progesterone, low estrogen, hypothyroid.

Trying to figure out what spiked the back pain and how I can get control of that?

Thanks for any input

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-27T19:07:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-wk4-of-lrx-first-ct-bath-yesterday-fibro-flares-big-time-any-input.467/post-7129>

I'd take the CT slower & strictly follow the protocol as outlined in CT4:

1\. Face dunks

2\. ice with compression shirt

3\. ice directly on skin

4\. baths (wear a hat at first)

5\. baths with ice on torso

There's a temptation to heal ourselves more quickly than our bodies will allow. Considering your history I would proceed _very_ slowly & back off if you have adverse effects (as you mentioned).

Dr. K has also mentioned two supplements to aid the transition of cold adaptation:

1\. 500mg of Krill oil 3x daily

2\. bitter melon extract 2hrs prior to CT

Good luck!

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-27T19:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-wk4-of-lrx-first-ct-bath-yesterday-fibro-flares-big-time-any-input.467/post-7137>

I stumbled on this today.

The short version: Ovarian/Adrenal/Thyroid problems are very much connected. Fix the Adrenal and the others will fall in line.

I was hypo (not hashi) and was able to fix it with adrenal supplements alone. I might suggest seeing a naturopath and having a cortisol salivary test. I do also think it is common for some symptoms as the toxins. I do hope you can get some relief!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-30T21:59:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-wk4-of-lrx-first-ct-bath-yesterday-fibro-flares-big-time-any-input.467/post-8928>

> **cantweight;7043 wrote:** Any reason why after 3 full weeks of Leptin Rx and first CT bath my fibro would flare?

Can't say that I know the reason, but I have been having a similar experience. I have RA and I have been having all sorts of weird flare ups since starting CT baths. I'm low on all steroids except Estrogen. I think I have some level of adrenal fatigue. My backgound is chronic ENT infections as a kid that had to be treated with antibiotics ... 20+ years of that ended in abcessed tonsils that were removed as an adult. Don't think the gut ever recovered from all those treatments of antibiotics. It is certainly in a dismal state right now. I believe this was the first stepping stone that lead to my joint issues.

FWIW - I had to back off the CT baths (because my core temp was dropping post bath) and just do the icing with a compression shirt. I just got back into the bath this last week. Currently I do the bath while icing the tum. Core temp is not dropping any more. My tap water ranges between 48 - 50 degrees right now. I started at 20 minutes and added 5 minutes each time. I'm now shooting for 45 minutes 1x day. I plan on giving it 6 weeks before making a judgement. My poor hubby is freaking out on my flare ups - latest conversation was about how maybe I should go to the Mayo Clinic because they have more experts there. Sigh. Yes, they have more experts who will want me to take toxic drugs and will tell me the rest of my issues are just "to be expected ... you are getting older ...". I tried to explain to him that they don't truely care because they dont expect people with RA (or fibro or MS, etc) to have a good quality of life and they certainly don't expect us to have a long life.

Also - have you tried an autoimmune paleo diet (no eggs, dairy, grains, legumes, nightshades, nuts, seeds)? You might find that could help minimize your flare ups. It can be tough but it is worth it. Ohh and (if you are not already) double down on food based probiotics.

If you keep up with the CT baths, will you let me know how it goes for you?

### cantweight — 2012-04-04T18:28:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-wk4-of-lrx-first-ct-bath-yesterday-fibro-flares-big-time-any-input.467/post-11322>

Thanks so much for all the input. I attempted baths again and really focused on keeping my upper body relaxed. I was so tense with my arms out of the tub for 20 mins I think that was my problem. Handling much better now.

I have been on adrenal support supplements for a few years now. I work very hard to keep stress under control....but right now I am selling my house, in college, and have 3 kids so stress is at an all time high....possibly the cause of flare? lol

Wk 5 of leptinrx....awesome. Wondering now if any of the fat loss could be the cause of newer autoimmune symptoms flaring.

This stuff is wild!

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-27T17:13:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-evening-cortisol-levels.468/post-7015>

What ideas do you all have if someone has low morning, and high afternoon/evening cortisol levels?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-27T17:26:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-evening-cortisol-levels.468/post-7022>

Circadian mismatch.

did they ever work shift work? are they "night owls"

I did several years of swings/graves in the military ~5yrs worth of either working 4-midnight or midnight -8am. result - even 15yrs later was a backwards circadian rhythm...low in the am (when I'd be getting ready to go to sleep after shift work) and High at night (When I'd be getting ready to go to work)

I focused on adpatocrines, and getting light on my eyes first thing in the am, and turning off all lights etc at night...

# Thread: Being LS and Recovery Time/Sweating?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/being-ls-and-recovery-time-sweating.469/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-27T17:50:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/being-ls-and-recovery-time-sweating.469/post-7039>

Hey guys. So, I'm trying to decide if I can move onto the post leptin diet part and one of the things to look at is recovery time.

Here's my question. What do you guys think constitutes as "better recovery time" from exercise?

Here's my before.. If I'm out of shape and haven't exercised in months or years and I do heavy lifting or a hard bodyrock workout (basically overtrain) I will be sore 3-4 days. If I have been working out even like once a wk and I do the same then I would be sore for 1-2 days. I think that being sore for at least a day is normal as your muscles are recovering and rebuilding.

So, any of you who are LS what is your experience like in recovery time? What do you think "proves" you are LS?

Also, the part about sweating. Dr. Kruse lists " You will notice a change in your sweating pattern."

What kind of change? What is good sweating vs bad sweating?

Thanks for your help guys. Btw I'm a 28 yr old female if that matters in all of this.

# Thread: Cold adaptation protocol using air per Dr. K - how do i do it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-protocol-using-air-per-dr-k-how-do-i-do-it.470/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tulipwood — 2012-03-27T18:11:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-protocol-using-air-per-dr-k-how-do-i-do-it.470/post-7057>

Hi

per Dr. K's faq on Cold Thermogenesis

"The physics of heat loss following cold water immersion dictate a more rapid drop in surface and core temperature than from exposure to a cold air environment.Ã‚ So most people will choose to use water because it works a lot faster"

I am curious how does one go about doing cold adaptation using air?

also would it be advantageous to dress lightly and be a bit chilly in my office, movie theaters, etc this summer where they run the a/c super cool vs wearing a sweater?

tx

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-27T19:40:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-protocol-using-air-per-dr-k-how-do-i-do-it.470/post-7170>

Seems like the easiest way to do it would be while you sleep. Dr. K says he keeps the thermostat @ 60ÂºF at night.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-27T20:35:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-protocol-using-air-per-dr-k-how-do-i-do-it.470/post-7253>

A lot depends on where you live. If you live in the North and have cold winter temps - you can just spend time outdoors and start wearing less and less layers. Eventually walking outside when it is freezing cold in short and a T - shirt.

If you live wear the temps are warmer, then you gotta us AC or work in a cooler.

That is why ice and water are easier - better access and they conduct more energy than air as well.

### kalibex — 2012-03-28T15:44:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-protocol-using-air-per-dr-k-how-do-i-do-it.470/post-7689>

I've been supplementing the spot icing and cold baths by wearing fewer layers when outside, especially on the random cold mornings/evenings here in New England. I'll sometimes take a jacket or sweater along in my bag as a security blanket, but ultimately there's this feeling of security in knowing that even if it's chilling and uncomfortable, that as long as it's not too close to actually being freezing outside, it's okay; your body can handle it. Also been leaving my heat off and my window cracked open, including at night. (Though felt like a wimp the other night when I had to close it during an over-night hard freeze...  )

# Thread: How are you getting your Selenium?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-your-selenium.471/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-27T19:01:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-your-selenium.471/post-7117>

Dr. Kruse says:

> Selenium is needed for the proper functioning of the immune system cells. It is a cofactor in the activation of neutrophils, macrophages, and Natural killer cells and T lymphocytes and many other immunoregulatory functions as laid out in FerencikÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s paper in 2003. Selenium seems to act by two modes to protect us from cancer. One is that is catalyzes a many immune reactions to increase our immune function in the GALT. Secondly, Se seems to hinder oncogenesis by protecting the p53 gene in the gut and does not allow oncogenesis to occur. In fact, it appears to favor low level inflammation seen in autoimmune conditions and favor apoptosis of defective cells instead. It appears that low Selenium levels reduce the production of plasma glutathione peroxidase. Patients with low selenium levels are known to have higher rates of polyps and autoimmunity. (Fernandez-Banares et al., 2002) Naturally occurring Selenium is found in plants like garlic and broccoli as a compound called Se-methylselenocysteine (Se-MSC). Se-MSc produces 33% reduction in cancerous lesions (colon) than selenite (synthetic). It also produces a 50% decrease in new tumor development locally in the gut. It also induces cells to undergo apoptosis and not oncogenesis. It has been shown to reduce angiogenesis to make the cellular terroir less hospitable to cancer. Se- MSC also directly down regulates vascular endothelial growth factor that is commonly found in most GI cancers. So the story seems to show that selenium is pretty critical to gut health and disease prevention.

How are you getting yours?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-27T19:09:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-your-selenium.471/post-7132>

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-27T19:24:30+0300

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-27T23:19:02+0300

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis

### ChimpChick — 2012-03-27T22:05:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7320>

If CT is only effective while one is ketogenic, and doing CT in a non keto state causes a mismatch (inflamation, weight gain, bad things) Does this mean that if one were to get the Zeltiq procedure done in the summer while eating seasonal paleo-approved carbs it would be ineffective? Or worse could cause the opposite of the intent?

Thanks!

### Owl — 2012-03-27T22:11:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7324>

It doesn't sound like Wim Hof is ketogenic and he's doing ok. Maybe not absolutely optimal, but he's certainly cold adapted and healthier for it. Zeltiq is being used by the public eating the SAD. If it didn't work during a certain time of year, that would have come out by now.

### ChimpChick — 2012-03-27T22:35:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7343>

Thanks Owl! Good point.. The seasons are throwing me for a loop.. I have an unheated pool that will be open soon and will still be quite chilly until August, so I want to make the most of it.. But I would also like to eat a blueberry now and then during the short period of time they are allowed. So I guess the answer is to plan my fruit around my swimming and never mix the two..

### caroline — 2012-03-28T01:59:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7432>

> **ChimpChick;7381 wrote:** Thanks Owl! Good point.. The seasons are throwing me for a loop.. But I would also like to eat a blueberry now and then during the short period of time they are allowed. So I guess the answer is to plan my fruit around my swimming and never mix the two..

I asked about zeltiq before. When I read their web site it says results take 2 to 4 months. I was wondering if you would detox after the procedure like we are doing?

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-28T16:27:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7702>

I didn't realize that Zeltiq took that long. That is good to know. I was getting a little worried when my weight loss plateaued a bit.

And if Zeltiqu takes 2-4 months, then I would expect a slower gradual process, like the CT here, would take even longer.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-28T17:03:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7723>

CT works for \*anyone\* at any time to increase metabolism and increase BAT (= spot fat burning). It works for far more (adrenal, thyroid, etc., etc.) if you pair it with the ketogenic diet, and far more quickly if you use water (especially immersion).

### hazyjane — 2012-03-28T21:34:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7873>

> **ChimpChick;7358 wrote:** If CT is only effective while one is ketogenic, and doing CT in a non keto state causes a mismatch (inflamation, weight gain, bad things) Does this mean that if one were to get the Zeltiq procedure done in the summer while eating seasonal paleo-approved carbs it would be ineffective? Or worse could cause the opposite of the intent?
>
>
>
> Thanks!

I know a lady who was part of a Zeltiq clinical trial and she didn't get results from it. I have a feeling that diet affects the results of Zeltiq. Some people get great results and others, none. Next time I see her, I'll ask her what she was eating at that time.

### JC2K — 2012-03-28T21:56:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7886>

I would expect some detox from Zeltiq, but over a longer amount of time, since the cells are dieing off at a slow rate.

### LinD — 2012-03-28T22:21:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7906>

> **villjamur\_stevenson;7743 wrote:** I didn't realize that Zeltiq took that long. That is good to know. I was getting a little worried when my weight loss plateaued a bit.
>
> And if Zeltiqu takes 2-4 months, then I would expect a slower gradual process, like the CT here, would take even longer.

That IS interesting.

Makes me love and appreciate CT even more!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T22:27:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7908>

> **MamaGrok;7764 wrote:** CT works for \*anyone\* at any time to increase metabolism and increase BAT (= spot fat burning). It works for far more (adrenal, thyroid, etc., etc.) if you pair it with the ketogenic diet, and far more quickly if you use water (especially immersion).

I agree with this. Also, think of it like in the wild. Animals/humans went into water plenty.. whether the ocean, lake, or river.. the water would be cold or at least not heated much of the time. Have you ever been in the Pacific Ocean? That bitch is cold even in 100 degree weather! LOL. The pool you're going into isn't heated which is exactly what natural sources of water would be like. This is what makes sense to me on "CT" in the summer. It's more like just natural behavior to take a cool/cold shower as water isn't heated in the wild.

### ShuffleUp — 2012-03-28T23:26:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7944>

If you read some about Zeltiq they mention that you should tell your doc if you have any liver problems. I read something that said they anticipated some detox issues but have not largely seen problems with it. I have also read that it is not for the obese, and should be used generally for the last few pounds and is fda approved for the love handle area. They say it kills about 20% of the fat in the treated area. I was also suprised it does take 2-4 months to see the full effects which to me seems to indicate that those looking for results in our CT experimentation (which for most of us hasn't been longer than 6 weeks) are probably expecting tangible weight loss results too soon. Although I'm unclear as to whether or not Zeltiq was more of an anaology than the exact function that CT is having on us. i.e. Zeltiq probably does not exhibit much BAT activity increase which is a big part of what I believe CT, as laid out by Kruse, is meant to do.

[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/femail/article-1299505/Instead-melting-away-flab-laser-hottest-craze-America-freeze-death.html](http://http://www.dailymail.co.uk/femail/article-1299505/Instead-melting-away-flab-laser-hottest-craze-America-freeze-death.html)

### caroline — 2012-03-29T00:46:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7980>

I think you are right shuffleup. We are expecting too much too soon. I just want this blubber gone!

# Thread: cold therapy and the nervous system
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-therapy-and-the-nervous-system.477/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-03-27T23:52:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-therapy-and-the-nervous-system.477/post-7381>

I'm partway through an old book called Dexterity and It's Development. It's fascinating and a must read for anyone interested in evolutionary biology.

The point is that part of the reason we are so dextrous is that we are warm blooded. Our nerves require a certain baseline temperature to transmit signals properly. This is why you lose coordination when you are cold. Think of your hands when you are skiing/snowboarding trying to do something on your coat or bindings that requires coordination.

Anyway, it is interesting for me to note that during my cold adaptation period I am finding myself fairly uncoordinated for long periods after the cold exposure. I just spent an hour outside, lightly dressed, in ~50f weather with windchill unaccounted for. I've been inside now ~45 minutes and i can still feel a little difficulty typing this.

My assumption is that this is a transitionary phase and that after adaptation my body temp will be within the optimal range for nervous system communication regardless of ambient temperatures.

So at least that can serve as a marker for me. There is no way I'm cold adapted as long as my dexterity is compromised.

jesse

### Melinda — 2012-03-28T01:21:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-therapy-and-the-nervous-system.477/post-7413>

I find my mental dexterity is hampered as well. I'm doing 30 min in my salt water pool, swimming and playing today-no more being a statue, and toward the end of the secession and a while afterwards I can't find the words I'm looking for with the usual speed. It's like my mind is so busy processing other stuff that it doesn't have the bandwidth to help me be eloquent.

### hellojtm — 2012-03-29T19:12:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-therapy-and-the-nervous-system.477/post-8350>

Interesting feedback Melinda. Thanks. What temperature is your pool?

# Thread: Pork Spare ribs in a Crock pot - let me have your best Recipes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2012-03-28T01:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-7415>

Ok, this entire week in my house hold has been about breaking out of the \*rut\* we've been stuck in. so for tomorrow I'm planning on making some pork spare ribs in the crock pot... but I need some Ideas...

I've found two so far - but I'm kinda meh about them...

[http://kaitsprimalkitchen.blogspot.com/2011/08/paleo-bbq-marinated-crock-pot-spare.html](http://http://kaitsprimalkitchen.blogspot.com/2011/08/paleo-bbq-marinated-crock-pot-spare.html)

and

[http://www.theclothesmakethegirl.com/2012/02/23/5-spice-slow-cooker-pork-ribs/](http://http://www.theclothesmakethegirl.com/2012/02/23/5-spice-slow-cooker-pork-ribs/)

so ladies and gents....give me your best recipes!!! I want something that's both OPTIMAL And RockStar!

### tweety — 2012-03-28T22:41:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-7921>

I'll be watching. I had an AMAZING recipe that I used last year. It cooked all day in this beautiful Italian tomato sauce. The meat just fell off the bones. And now, I can't remember what cookbook it was in! I had a phase where I tried a whole bunch of cookbooks from the library and I can't remember which one it was. My husband said it was exactly like what his little Italian Grandma used to cook when he was a kid. Off to cearch again... fingers crossed!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T22:55:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-7930>

We make lots of meats in the crock. 2-3 times a week. I had to give up on cooking them overnight for morning consumption though, the yummyness kept waking me up.

Here is my best advice.

1\. Consider cooking in a big enameled iron pot. You can brown it to develop fond, then put it in over on 175-200 degrees for hrs just like crock.

I would be happy to offer more info on that. . .

but since you asked about crock pot, I'm going to assume you don't want the hassle of browning in one pan, moving it etc. Who has time for that.

1\. Lay ribs on bed of onion.

2\. Salt all ribs excessively.

3\. handful of cumin seeds

4\. a little spoon of finely ground coffee

5\. a heap of chocolate power (valrohna is my fav, make sure it's nothing but chocolate)

5\. a cup or so of broth (I've used whatever is on hand, chicken, beef, veal)

6\. a cup or so of malbec

7\. excessive amounts of garlic.

8\. Can of chopped tomatoes.

Once done, strain all solids and put juice in a sauce pan. If more liquid is needed add either broth or wine or both. Might need to salt again. Take off heat, add several little pieces of ice cold butter and wisk it in. This will make a glistening and to-die-for sauce.

### tweety — 2012-03-29T00:23:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-7972>

Yum, Cavemam. That sounds awesome!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-29T01:26:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-7992>

I know you are looking for a crock pot recipe but this is my favorite all time recipe for ribs:

[http://www.oprah.com/food/Quincy-Jones-Thriller-Ribs](http://http://www.oprah.com/food/Quincy-Jones-Thriller-Ribs)

There are two steps involved which may be too much work cuz I know you have a busy life. They have always been the most tender most flavorful ribs! YUM!!

Cavemam that recipe sounds fabulous! Thanks.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-29T02:19:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-8015>

I just took some paleo BBQ sauce, mustard, balsamic vinegar, liquid smoke & hickory seasoning. Tossed it all together and put it on low. Fraking amazing. Dd turned down asparagus for a 2nd helping of ribs!!! I thought we'd have enough for left overs tomorrow but I doubt it!!!

### Slone07 — 2012-03-29T02:33:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-8019>

I like mine cooked under sauerkraut that's been sautÃ©ed with bacon, onions, and garlic. And creole seasoning...my usual seasoning.

Yours sound fantastic, though!

### skupe — 2012-03-29T03:53:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-8056>

In case you are unaware, Accent is MSG and very unhealthy. I would find something else to use in its place like some organic smoked salt.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-29T16:15:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-8242>

Shinjin, I just had a big grok party with spare ribs. I just salt and peppered them, put them on low (250F) in the oven for 3-4 hours and finished them off on the grill for the last 10-15 minutes. Even the SAD people in attendance didn't add bbq sauce. The ratio of pork to fat makes spare ribs the perfect cut without too much fuss. So tasty!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-29T16:16:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-8243>

For sweet, I would second cavemam's suggestion for cocoa, but also cinnamon and sea salt and CO is amazing with pork! YUM!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-30T01:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-8505>

Ok, made beef roast with my standard method mentioned before. I encourage everyone to brown before crockpotting on beef roast. Pork it's not as necessary (due to the way the proteins loosen, slow low for whole thing is good).

For beef, I don't add the cocoa and cinnamon, but use Onion, red wine, cumin seeds and an obscene amount of salt. I brown the beef in CO. Kitchen fills with smoke.

[](<http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/8553=68-photo \(2\).JPG>)

[](<http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/8553=69-photo \(3\).JPG>)

Again, I caution about leaving overnight (although this is an awesome breakfast!) because you can't sleep with all the yummy smells!

### mafixit — 2012-09-22T03:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-53348>

I take a pork shoulder, soak it overnight in liquid smoke, then the next morning add worstershire sauce and garlic if desired and cook on low for 24 hours.

Hubby likes to add BBQ sauce to his. I like it just like it comes out of the cooker.

# Thread: Some weightlifting while on the Leptin Reset?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-weightlifting-while-on-the-leptin-reset.481/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Xena — 2012-03-28T02:15:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-weightlifting-while-on-the-leptin-reset.481/post-7442>

So, I know a cool trainer at a CrossFit place in Denver and he tells me that there are some studies show doing some weightlifting while one's adrenals are fatigued, help the adrenals get better. Has anyone else heard of this? I know Dr. K. doesn't say to do this until LS signs are coming in.

My stats are: I have badly fatigued adrenals and have for several years. My numbers are slowly getting better. Doc is great and said no exercise except light walking which I do a few times a day. Next cortisol test is in a few months.

I'm female, age 49, 6'1" tall and weigh 218. Sounds like a lot but I'm down to a size 14 tall which blows my mind. Still, I'd like to weigh less-trying to let that go. I've been on the reset for 7 weeks, haven't lost any weight (but lost 29.4 lbs just prior to going on the reset), but continue to lose inches. I'm doing the reset dairy free except a tiny bit of grass-fed butter in my coffee, mostly nut free (a few macadamias once a week) and nightshade free, although I don't have auto-immune disease. I eat organ meat a few times a week, stick to grass-fed, pastured everything and generally feel amazing every day. Optimal is coming, I can feel it.

I'm on week 3 or so of CT and spot icing 1-2x per day in addition to face dunking.

Appreciate any thoughts/hacking.

Xena

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T02:23:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-weightlifting-while-on-the-leptin-reset.481/post-7448>

I would make sure the trainer/coach knows your goals, and that you are able to do strength training only: oly or powerlifting. Not metcons. And by lifting I mean 5 x 5s etc, not metcons with a barbell. They are still metcons LOL

Good luck, it is possible to do strength only programs at a crossfit gym. I do.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-30T18:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-weightlifting-while-on-the-leptin-reset.481/post-8818>

I concur with Teenie Leek - if you can get your coach on board to customize the programming a bit for you to do this in a box - go for it. If not, figure out how to do the lifting on your own. Make sure though that you are adhering to the protocol. Most people take 6-8 weeks to get LS and you really shouldn't start lifting before that. That said, if you have the signs in your skin and your appetite that means you are heading the right direction. Since adrenals are very much part of the sleep equation, I wouldn't start weights until I had seen some improvement in that sign as well. And absolutely positively, if you do lift - don't do it more than 2-3x a week, see the value in bodyweight exercises as strength training as much as the barbells, and drop it like a 300# DL if you see any negative impact to doing it. Negatives would be rotten recovery - DOMS that is still there when you hit the second lift of the week, fatigue, or a down turn in your sleep.

### Xena — 2012-03-31T05:26:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-weightlifting-while-on-the-leptin-reset.481/post-9099>

Thanks, my sleep keeps improving and now I'm waking up right before my husband's alarm goes off at 5:15, sometimes an hour earlier. I'm not dragging around either. But I REALLY want optimal and am feeling more cautious about this today. I'll find out from my contact about the perimeters of doing this--I am only on week 8 of the reset and in some ways feeling like I'm still a beginner.

# Thread: What breaks or slows cold adaptation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-breaks-or-slows-cold-adaptation.487/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-03-28T04:15:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-breaks-or-slows-cold-adaptation.487/post-7515>

I'm looking for an official answer, if one exists. Here is the kind of info I'm looking for.

I'm at stage X and I have food Y, this will set me back Z amount.

e.g.

1\. I'm at the cold tub stage for 20 minutes every day, I am eating a ketogenic diet with no starch/fruit and I had 10oz of ice cream over the weekend at a wedding. This will set me back 3 days while my body forgets that I ate something sweet.

2\. I'm at the compression shirt stage, up to 40 minutes, I am eating a ketogenic diet but due to work I had to look at the computer for 3-4 hours after sunset 3 days in a row. This will set me back 3 days while my body forgets about the SCN and focuses on the cold receptor...

That above examples are phony but point out the issue, in my opinion.

thanks,

jesse

### hellojtm — 2012-03-28T07:01:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-breaks-or-slows-cold-adaptation.487/post-7602>

Scenario time again

3\. I'm at the face dunking stage, skin temps of 49f and can stay under 25s at a time for a total of 5 minutes underwater. I am eating a ketogenic diet with no starch/fruit. I wear only shorts and a t-shirt independent of weather, a/c, etc... I took a ten minute hot shower 2 days ago. This will set me back 1 day.

(?? I have no clue how much these things impact the adaptation period or the maintenance of adaptation).

Another question, i know that there is the sweet sensor on the tongue that is important for the ancient pathway. Put a banana in that mouth and your brain wires for warm adaptation. But is it just the sweet taste? It seems that the diet is ketogenic too, the recommended one. Is that just an artifact of removing all the sweet things? I don't think so, you could be ketogenic while eating minimal sweets as long as your total glucose consumption is below your bodies threshold. So then, coming back around, what if I ate a non-ketogenic diet, but had nothing sweet to the taste, if I ate 150g of bland carbohydrates a day in the form of veggies and tubers. No sweet, will I remain adapted? Will I slow my adaptation?

I'm hoping there is an official answer.

thanks,

jesse

### Lyndra — 2012-03-28T20:24:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-breaks-or-slows-cold-adaptation.487/post-7832>

I'm pondering what cold adaptation would look like based on normal seasonal patterns. In the fall, the temperatures fall, fruits & veggies start to disappear, but tubers are still available if you know where to dig. As the season progresses, tubers disappear or the ground freezes and they are unavailable, and the cold intensifies. It makes sense to me to ease into CT in terms of both cold and diet. You'd naturally cut back carbs gradually in the fall as the temperatures drop. So, as you start doing CT, you can use warmer water and still eat some carbs to mimic the natural progress of fall, but as you adapt, you need to lower the water temperature and cut back on carbs so you are living in a true winter environment.

Furthermore, the cutback in carbs would be non-linear. You might be no-carb for a while and then come upon a stash of tubers and eat them and leave ketosis temporarily. Same with cold - the temps go down, you start to adapt, then you have an Indian summer period where it is warm again before you get more cold weather. I think as long as you trend toward lower carbs and ketosis, you should be good as you are starting CT.

But as usual we all have our goodies....so I can brag and say that we live in rural area and have our own olive orchards for oil, and I am pretty good at pickling olives!!!

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-28T08:49:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cultured-coconut-milk.488/post-7624>

Gemstar, it's really hard to go wrong with kraut if you have a crock with a moat. I have a 7.5Lt Harsh, but there are other brands.

[http://www.amazon.com/Harsch-Gairtopf-Fermenting-Crock-Pot/dp/B000H6PB0Q](http://http://www.amazon.com/Harsch-Gairtopf-Fermenting-Crock-Pot/dp/B000H6PB0Q)

[http://www.amazon.com/Boleslawiec-Polish-Fermenting-Crock-Pot/dp/B006WC0NWK/ref=pd\_sim\_sbs\_k\_1](http://http://www.amazon.com/Boleslawiec-Polish-Fermenting-Crock-Pot/dp/B006WC0NWK/ref=pd_sim_sbs_k_1)

I shred a large cabbage, put it in a plastic tub with 5 tablespoons of salt, wait for it to start sweating (with a bit of 'massage'), then pack it in the crock. Once the lid is on, and the moat is filled with water, there's nothing left to do but wait. Anywhere from 3 to 6 weeks later you have delicious sauerkraut.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-28T19:57:26+0300

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-30T00:41:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cultured-coconut-milk.488/post-8493>

> **donkjellberg;7670 wrote:** I have kefir grains ready to go. Any tips on making coconut milk kefir from scratch?

I use the Thai coconut milk but also make coconut 'milk' from scratch that I kefired. It is more economical and works in a pinch if I haven't got any canned available.

Take 2 cups of shredded organic non sweetened coconut, add 3 cups of very warm (but not boiling) water, stir and let set for an hour. Then I put the hydrated coconut through a fine sieve or cheesecloth. Sqeeze out as much of the liquid as possible.

Take the squeezed coconut fibers and add 1 more cup of vw water and repeat above.

Put in fridge overnight and then take off the chunk of 'oil' that hardens on the surface. SAVE this because it is the same thing you buy by the jar when you get Nutiva coconut oil. Eat that when ever--num-num!

Put the milk grains in the coconut milk. (I have never used water grains...think it would kill them). Set on counter and check in 12 to 24 hours depending on warmth of kitchen. When thick refrigerate and enjoy. Make sure to catch it so it doesn't separate alot (over kefired).

I like to add a little vanilla bean in the milk.

Sometimes I add the Thai organic canned milk to it if it isn't as thick and creamy as I like. I do store the grains in between ferments in whole milk. Just rinse them quick in tepid filtered water before adding them to coconut milk.

I re-dehydrate the left over coconut then in an oven with light on overnight to dry. Spread them out thinly on a cookie sheet. Then put in my bullet to grind into coconut flour. That then gets put in freezer for when I make any goodies that call for coconut flour. No waste!

### JoeBranca — 2012-03-30T01:42:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cultured-coconut-milk.488/post-8508>

I make it at home using milk kefir grains and Native Forest coconut milk or the Aroy-D brand in the box. Love it. The baby gets some every morning mixed with a couple of raw pastured egg yolks. On a lot of mornings in addition to bacon or meat I'll throw together a little bowl of the kefir and mix in: ceylon cinnamon, cacao powder, cacao nibs for crunch, either a few frozen cranberries or blueberries or rasberries, a couple of raw egg yolks, ginger powder, turmeric, and a dash of vanilla extract. It's completely awesome, I love the loud tartness of it.

### Tizmal — 2013-09-04T17:10:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cultured-coconut-milk.488/post-94801>

I don't do dairy for about a year and I also make coconut milk kefir and drink coconut milk, its veery useful - here's the article [about the nutritional value of coconut milk](http://juicycoco.com/nutritional-value-of-coconut-milk-vitamins-and-minerals/) and its benefits. I've tried to make my own one because I'm sure its healthier than store-bought commercial milk with preservatives and additives, but unfortunately i like the taste of canned milk more.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-09-04T18:27:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cultured-coconut-milk.488/post-94810>

A long time ago I grew milk kefir grains the size of an egg, and I also had water kefir grains (tibi grains). I know that the kefiran gel produced by the milk grains is supposed to be beneficial, though I don't believe the commercial milks have any kefiran in them. Kefir grains can be used to culture coconut and soymilk, for example, but the grains will suffer for it over time. You may need to get new ones or switch them back to real milk from time to time.

If you eat dairy and want to try a "fun" culture, get some villi culture. It forms a thick gelatinous culture overnight at room temperature on the kitchen counter. No heating required,and all it takes is 1 tablespoon of the previous culture stirred into a new bowl of milk and left overnight covered with a cloth. Villi is not sour. Another fun, no-heat type is caspian sea yogurt.

### kathylu — 2013-09-06T19:18:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cultured-coconut-milk.488/post-95048>

> [GlenPDQ said:](/goto/post?id=94810)
>
> A long time ago I grew milk kefir grains the size of an egg, and I also had water kefir grains (tibi grains). I know that the kefiran gel produced by the milk grains is supposed to be beneficial, though I don't believe the commercial milks have any kefiran in them. Kefir grains can be used to culture coconut and soymilk, for example, but the grains will suffer for it over time. You may need to get new ones or switch them back to real milk from time to time.
>
> If you eat dairy and want to try a "fun" culture, get some villi culture. It forms a thick gelatinous culture overnight at room temperature on the kitchen counter. No heating required,and all it takes is 1 tablespoon of the previous culture stirred into a new bowl of milk and left overnight covered with a cloth. Villi is not sour. Another fun, no-heat type is caspian sea yogurt.

I keep kefir grains in milk. Of course, I don't drink dairy anymore, so my novel use for them is: rinse grains, add them to a quart of RO water, let sit overnight. Then you will have a quart of thick "kefiran" gel, filled with probiotics. I drink a bit of it and use the rest to take a bath in. You heard me right, a bath! Our skin can use a probiotic lift now and then too! Eliminates minor skin problems (itchiness, dryness) and this is subjective, but I think my allergies have improved a bit...less inflammation. Store kefir grains in milk until the next use.

### endless — 2013-09-06T20:20:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cultured-coconut-milk.488/post-95062>

I make coconut milk kefir....I just dump the grains in a jar of coconut milk and leave it on the counter for a couple of days

### GlenPDQ — 2013-09-07T07:29:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cultured-coconut-milk.488/post-95116>

You can go whole hog and swallow any extra kefir grains. It's kinda like chewing sour lemon flavored cauliflower shaped raw octopus.

### caroline — 2013-09-07T08:47:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cultured-coconut-milk.488/post-95118>

> [GlenPDQ said:](/goto/post?id=95116)
>
> You can go whole hog and swallow any extra kefir grains. It's kinda like chewing sour lemon flavored cauliflower shaped raw octopus.

That is a great visual! Where do you normally get keffir grains? I know you can get them on line...I did get some and then wasn't really sure what to do with them.............

Months later - I think they are still in the fridge somewhere..........

# Thread: Poll:  What is your core temp?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-core-temp.489/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Birdy — 2012-03-28T06:15:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-core-temp.489/post-7586>

First of all, how is it even measured? I have only an old-fashioned thermometer. What time of the day do you measure?

### Owl — 2012-03-28T07:11:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-core-temp.489/post-7608>

Core temp is only taken in your esophagus, or anally. Neither is very convenient!

Your basal temp is good enough, taken under your tongue. It'll be a little lower than your core temp.

### Inger — 2012-03-28T08:09:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-core-temp.489/post-7615>

> **Birdy;7624 wrote:** First of all, how is it even measured? I have only an old-fashioned thermometer. What time of the day do you measure?

My core temp is in between 98,6 - 99,5 F. Mostly 99,1 F.

It always was so high as long as I remember and I hate it cause I guess it makes me age faster.

I want it to get lower.. I do hope CT will do it eventually.

I am 35 and do have some wrinkles already. I am healthy though and normal weight so for that I am thankful.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T10:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-core-temp.489/post-7639>

My basal temperature has always hovered around the 36.5C/97.7F. If I take my temperature in the mornings before I get out of bed, then it will be around the 35.4C/95.7F mark.

# Thread: talking to teachers about fitness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/talking-to-teachers-about-fitness.490/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-28T06:48:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/talking-to-teachers-about-fitness.490/post-7598>

Does anyone have specific ideas or experience about talking with teachers (my children are 11 and 7) about fitness? Both my kids are being told that 'cross country' training is beginning (their version of daily PE right now), with my 11 year old having to do long runs daily, building up to a several mile course in a couple of months. I am aware of the information about chronic cardio increasing cortisol and decreasing telomere length, but I've not seen anything specific to children. New Zealand has this huge anti-obesity thing going right now... worried we're going to end up like the US!... and keeping kids 'active' is huge. My kids play sports, walk/scooter to school, walk the dog, etc. But I'm concerned about the move toward 'cross country' running. Thoughts?

### MJ* — 2012-04-18T15:28:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/talking-to-teachers-about-fitness.490/post-16188>

> **KiwiLauren;7636 wrote:** Does anyone have specific ideas or experience about talking with teachers (my children are 11 and 7) about fitness? Both my kids are being told that 'cross country' training is beginning (their version of daily PE right now), with my 11 year old having to do long runs daily, building up to a several mile course in a couple of months. I am aware of the information about chronic cardio increasing cortisol and decreasing telomere length, but I've not seen anything specific to children. New Zealand has this huge anti-obesity thing going right now... worried we're going to end up like the US!... and keeping kids 'active' is huge. My kids play sports, walk/scooter to school, walk the dog, etc. But I'm concerned about the move toward 'cross country' running. Thoughts?

Just saw this...I feel your pain, my kids are very active. I also feel that talking to your children's teachers about this is a good idea, so long as you are prepared to do a ton of educating. In my experience most people do not know what Paleo is let alone telomeres...you may not change their minds all at once so you have to be prepared for that. If you really don't want your kids participating in it, you could try getting your doctor on board and then just have him/her not sign off on this activity for them (maybe discuss with your children's doc first). I do though think that education is key and most teachers are willing to at least listen to what you have to say. They are your children, and it's your right to question what's being asked of them...

# Thread: Which JK-blog post changed your life?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-jk-blog-post-changed-your-life.491/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamorama — 2012-03-28T07:18:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-jk-blog-post-changed-your-life.491/post-7610>

The first blog that got me really thinking, and in turn made me do the Leptin-Rx was [http://jackkruse.com/why-is-oprah-still-obese-leptin-part-3/](http://http://jackkruse.com/why-is-oprah-still-obese-leptin-part-3/)

The 2nd blog-post that really made me up my game was [http://jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-6-the-ancient-pathway/](http://http://jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-6-the-ancient-pathway/)

I loved this post because it made me realize how many neolithic thoughts and "truths" I was still hanging onto to!

What are your favourite posts by Jack Kruse, and why?

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-28T07:49:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-jk-blog-post-changed-your-life.491/post-7613>

I stumbled across Jack when he was in the middle of his Osteoporosis series. My mum has it; spine like an s-bend, two hip replacements, waiting for knee replacements. I knew I had to do something different to what she had done, but didn't know what. I'm at further risk because I don't have a thyroid; Jack got my attention with that one!

What's Leptin?? I had to go back and read his Leptin series, and started the Leptin Rx immediately. It felt as though the more I read of Jack, the more I had to read just to make it all make sense. And it always made sense in the end!

I don't have a favourite blog. What changed my life was the fact that Jack was willing to put it all out there, and I found it.

### Petra — 2012-03-28T12:47:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-jk-blog-post-changed-your-life.491/post-7651>

I'm intrigued by the Leptin series. For some reason I get back there and find new information.

Also the leaky gut rx explained so much for me. My overall health is okay, but I started to feel those little tiny aches that just don't belong in my body. Most of it is gone. It just takes time to adjust yourself.

Warm, or cool regards,

Petra

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-28T14:05:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-jk-blog-post-changed-your-life.491/post-7658>

I was always searching for bits on peripheral neuropathy and tendonitis, and of course got sucked in to many other topics. (The neuropathy is completely gone, and the tendonitis is 90% gone.)

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T14:45:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-jk-blog-post-changed-your-life.491/post-7667>

Hormones 101 and Leaky gut

both are more on the practical side but I find new stuff each time I read them
