> Could you have fat malabsorption, prohibiting the D from rising? Or perhaps the form of D you're taking isn't working for you?

That's a great site, and one that I've spend a good amount of time reading through. I actually came to the same conclusion as you did, narrowing things down to the pituitary. So I went to see a pituitary endocrinologist. Big mistake. After an hour of discussing my symptoms and a brief exam, he said there is absolutely nothing wrong with me. I even have a hard, peanut-like bump in my neck in the thyroid region. He couldn't find it so he just dismissed it as a lymph node! He didn't even feel it! RAR. Anyway, I'm hoping my hormone labs will reveal something that I can work on.

Never thought about fat malabsorption because I have difficulty losing weight... How can I test for fat malabsorption? Should I test for other fat soluble vitamins A and E?

Thanks Mama!

PS - I didn't realize there ia a whole forum for labs, so I re-posted over there. In case this doesn't really belong here.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-12T03:40:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-14044>

> **Shijin13;8474 wrote: If you stick w/the plan you get results. I remember when I was tracking and doing the LR - BAB came in at about 1100 cals, Lunch and dinner would put me anywhere btwn 1800-2800 depending on what I ate.
>
> once I stopped I went by if I was still hungry or if I was full - Once I stopped caring things started happening... its timing, light, and sleep
>
>
>
> if you have adrenal problems... 3meals/day is key - you have to do it... even if lunch/dinner are much smaller to fit them in...
>
>
>
> today I'm eating lunch/dinner right now...b/c I have class tonight & don't want to eat after 7pm... so I'm having asparagus, mushrooms, roast beef and some salami.. this will hold me until HAB tomorrow morning!
> I SO needed to read this...I can freak out over how many calories my BAB is and how much I eat in a day. Seems like a lot some day (like today)! And I sometimes stress myself over "geez, I still NEED lunch." But I am working on rehabbing my adrenals, so I feel better knowing it's common to need lunch. Thanks!
>
>
> > **cgk;9016 wrote:**
> >
> > So key points
> >
> > Follow the protocol - yes customize to your situation, but don't use that to justify something that is anathema to the protocol!
> >
> > This is natural healing - it takes time.
> >
> > Weight is symptom of other issues... address them not the weight.
> >
> > And if all you can say is "I need to lose weight", well then it is time for testing.
>
> good points,thanks!
>
> KiwiLauren;9047 wrote:** I've been thinking about the themes in this thread for a couple of days now and realised that what really hits home for me is the need to try and 'do everything right.' I feel so much pressure to understand it all, to get it right, to not fall prey to my neolithic thoughts, to succeed, to solve the problems, to figure it all out, etc etc. While I believe in this framework and that sustains the effort it takes, at the same time this is an 'old' habit of mine, trying to be 'perfect' (and failing!). I don't know if anyone else can relate to this, but this thread made me realise that I feel constantly under pressure. I try to relax and liberate myself, but I think that feeling that as hard as I'm trying I can never quite understand all the information can put me in a bad place. So it's no surprise to me that if my weight doesn't shift or I start feeling yuck or test results come up with less-than-optimal results, that I (we?) can feel pretty discouraged.
>
>
>
> For example, I realised today that my husband (who is following along with keto paleo and CT, despite having read almost nothing on the site) is not only losing weight at high speed but is feeling little hunger and is totally cold adapted already. So I am cold all the time AND eating more than he does (I am 5'3", he is 6'1") and I had a bit of a freak out. I feel like I should be on the remedial bus to the School of JK.

I am the same way - either I am playing the role of the "A" student or I feeling like I'm flunking out! Why do we put so much pressure on ourselves?! I keep acting like it's a big deal if I pick the wrong supplement for gosh sake. Lately I tell myself that you have to use trial and error to see what works and what doesn't!

Thanks this whole thread was super helpful!

### somewhatchubby — 2012-08-18T10:24:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-45507>

Thank goodness I found this thread, I am fairly typical, worrying about the amount of food I am eating (hard to lose CW on that )and really frustrated that I am gaining rather than losing. Of course, the reason I am doing this is to repair my system. Thanks so much for pointing the blindingly obvious out to me !!

I am so looking forward to losing some weight though.

### Destiny — 2012-08-18T15:54:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-45525>

> **somewhatchubby;45880 wrote:** Thank goodness I found this thread, I am fairly typical, worrying about the amount of food I am eating (hard to lose CW on that )and really frustrated that I am gaining rather than losing. Of course, the reason I am doing this is to repair my system. Thanks so much for pointing the blindingly obvious out to me !!
>
> I am so looking forward to losing some weight though.

Be ready that it might take some time. I have been doing this for 10 months and lost only 7 lbs., my husband lost 43 lbs. during the same period of time. So it is individual depending on many other things.

In my case, my hormones and thyroid are a mess so my journey is not straightforward but I am moving forward!

### somewhatchubby — 2012-08-27T00:26:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-47361>

hi destiny, my DH today complained that he had only lost 3lbs in two weeks. I am 2lbs heavier.However I know that I will keep up the effort longer than he will, he is already focussing on what he cant have rather than what he can.

I had a couple of sleep blips, not sure why, first night I just couldnt switch off and didnt get to sleep until midnight, then the next night I was so stuffed ,despite not eating that much that at bedtime I was still stuffed and had some indegestion which is very unusual for me. I took a smaller brekky today and was careful to eat modestly tonight, so I hope for a good night's sleep.My appetite is definitely getting smaller.

# Thread: Is there a supplement to heal a twitch?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bontask — 2012-03-29T02:51:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-8027>

Since beginning LR about 4 weeks ago, my fibromyalgia (which only rarely flared up in past few years) has returned big time. Also suffering from an aggravating twitch in an eyelid. I am considering using CORVALEN again ([http://www.bioenergy.com/store/Main.aspx?html=ds&topic=b](http://http://www.bioenergy.com/store/Main.aspx?html=ds&topic=b) but I'm not sure if that is a good move or not. Years ago, the corvalen was an effective tool for me to end the fibromyalgia symptoms.

I barely have a grasp on the science of LR (I am studying!!!) and would sincerely appreciate any insight, suggestions about why the onset of the fibromyalgia again, is the twich related? and is corvalen a mistake???

Many thanks in advance.

Barbara

### L8F — 2012-03-29T03:58:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-8059>

Often twitches are mag deficiency--which is really really common.

### ealachan — 2012-03-29T04:34:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-8080>

I second the magnesium suggestion. It has REALLY helped my restless leg issues. It also helps me drop off to sleep more easily. I take 500mg in the evening about an hour before bed.

### shilohman — 2012-03-29T04:39:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-8084>

> **bontask;8070 wrote:** Since beginning LR about 4 weeks ago, my fibromyalgia (which only rarely flared up in past few years) has returned big time. Also suffering from an aggravating twitch in an eyelid. I am considering using CORVALEN again ([http://www.bioenergy.com/store/Main.aspx?html=ds&topic=b](http://http://www.bioenergy.com/store/Main.aspx?html=ds&topic=b) but I'm not sure if that is a good move or not. Years ago, the corvalen was an effective tool for me to end the fibromyalgia symptoms.
>
>
>
> I barely have a grasp on the science of LR (I am studying!!!) and would sincerely appreciate any insight, suggestions about why the onset of the fibromyalgia again, is the twich related? and is corvalen a mistake???
>
>
>
> Many thanks in advance.
>
>
>
> Barbara

Have you been tested for celiac disease or leaky gut? Do you eat any diary?

### bontask — 2012-03-29T19:17:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-8352>

> **shilohman;8127 wrote:** Have you been tested for celiac disease or leaky gut? Do you eat any diary?

I'm on Armour Thyroid, I've been tested for celiac, it was negative but except for an occasional lapse don't eat grains at all. I don't know about the leaky gut and I do eat dairy - goat milk yogurt every once in a while and cream - I try to avoid milk. I have a healthy diet - prepare meals from scratch. Trying to get my head around the science of this so that I can properly select the right supplements - it would be lovely to have a collected list somewhere - I'm parsing through posts to try to do a better job with that!

It sounds like corvalen would be a good idea then (Each serving contains 5 grams ribose, 40mg elemental magnesium (in the form of 800mg magnesium gluconate) and 240mg malate).

Thank you all.

Barbara

### REN2BRAINS — 2012-03-29T19:33:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-8361>

I had eyelid twitches when my thyroid was out of whack with Hashimoto's. Started on Armour and have never had them again.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-01T18:09:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-9800>

> **bontask;8070 wrote:** Since beginning LR about 4 weeks ago, my fibromyalgia (which only rarely flared up in past few years) has returned big time. Also suffering from an aggravating twitch in an eyelid. I am considering using CORVALEN again ([http://www.bioenergy.com/store/Main.aspx?html=ds&topic=b](http://http://www.bioenergy.com/store/Main.aspx?html=ds&topic=b) but I'm not sure if that is a good move or not. Years ago, the corvalen was an effective tool for me to end the fibromyalgia symptoms.
>
>
>
> I barely have a grasp on the science of LR (I am studying!!!) and would sincerely appreciate any insight, suggestions about why the onset of the fibromyalgia again, is the twich related? and is corvalen a mistake???
>
>
>
> Many thanks in advance.
>
>
>
> Barbara

there is one other thing to consider for nerve involvement. that is lecithin. you can get granules flavored with pineapple/coconut by kirkman labs. doesnt taste bad. you can mix it in liquids or just eat it off the spoon. the covrerings of the nerves are 90% lecithin. so not unreasonable to try to add this to your supplement intake.

### Optigal — 2012-04-05T15:16:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-11687>

I would also suggest you check out your b vitamins especially 6 & 12 & folate, hope it improves for you

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-13T14:56:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-14658>

Magnesium helped me. I'm on Erfa for hashimoto's and hashimoto's patients often have chronic magnesium loss and celiac (or gluten intolerance) which depletes the body of minerals ... And loose stools will also rid the body of magnesium. (My toe-cramps come back each time I go through that.)

Just check which magnesium you try if you decide to try. The bioavailability is very different between the types.

# Thread: Love-story J. Kruse-style
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-story-j-kruse-style.508/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamorama — 2012-03-29T03:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-story-j-kruse-style.508/post-8043>

When I first met my husband, 7 years ago, I was 25 and quite fit. I wore these tight skinny jeans on the night we first met, and because they were so expensive, I kept those jeans over the years even though they didn't fit me very well as I kept putting on weight (sounds familiar, anyone?).

I have a photo taken of me on my 25th birthday, wearing those jeans, and I always use to marvel at how tiny my bum looked in that photo. I had no muffin-top, no spillage...! I use to think "_Those were the days, I'll never wear them again, but I have the memories". Anyway, I put them on today, and they fit perfectly without_ a muffin-top. I can only assume that it means my bum look tiny again. And I can marvel at the fact that these are the jeans I wore when I first met The One. Thats my love-story Jack Kruse-style! You got me back into those wicked jeans! My husband thinks its brilliant- told you I got a good bum in those jeans, haha! LOL

The moral of the story is: I've lost so much weight but my brain hasn't caught up. I don't look in the mirror and see and slender person. But I KNOW that those jeans only fit when I was fit and slim. So the fact that they fit again now pulls my brain into the process: "Yes, you have lost a lot of weight!!! By eating fat!"(my favourite part).

# Thread: Exercise and Hcg and CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-hcg-and-ct.510/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Claudia — 2012-03-29T04:47:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-hcg-and-ct.510/post-8090>

I will be starting a round early April. I've been CTing for a few weeks, going slow, had hives.

I want to do this round continuing my normal exercising, walking/running once or twice per week, yoga/pilates, some strength training body exercises like pushups, planks, lunges, and squats using body weight.

I know, I know...people say not to, but I don't want to lose muscle and I'm not doing something new just what I"ve been doing. Last round I did my body became worse proportioned and I never did lose the inches with the pounds everyone was talking about. I'd like to exercise when I feel up to it and I won't if I'm too tired or don't feel good.

Any thoughts? I've actually had to exercise less when I started CT because I felt tired and weak from the CT so I took it easy a lot of days. But I'm feeling ok this week and exercising normally again.

### indigogirl — 2012-03-29T05:06:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-hcg-and-ct.510/post-8100>

Hi Claudia,

When I did my first round of HCG last year, through an MD, I was told to continue whatever exercise I was already doing.

Who says not to exercise?

I know on the LR you are not supposed to exercise until you are LS.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-31T04:56:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-hcg-and-ct.510/post-9091>

you can do whatever you're used to. There's no ban on exercise.  What form of hcg did you use and dose?

### Claudia — 2012-04-03T04:22:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-hcg-and-ct.510/post-10652>

I have done Rx sublingual and take .5 ml twice per day. I'm still waiting for my hcg to arrive. I tried JUDDD for 5 weeks but lost nothing and decided to order the hcg. I've been doing CT for several weeks, too. I should have written down when I started but I didn't so I just know it's been several weeks, maybe 4-5 weeks. I took a CT break because I got a bad cold and I'm back to CT today hoping it will help with the cold. I just couldn't bear to get into the cold bath feeling so crummy. I feel better so it wasn't as hard to do it today and I did it mid-day instead of at night, easier to do I think.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-03T04:29:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-hcg-and-ct.510/post-10656>

Just make sure the exercise you do falls in with Dr. Kruse's guidelines too. That's the only thing.

# Thread: Super Slow is working Super Awesome !!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoMom — 2012-03-29T04:48:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8091>

Unlike the many hard core CT's on this forum, I'm a wuss when it come to cold water. Funny since I keep the house cooler than most, my bedroom is about 50 and I don't overdress outside, but water? I'm a wuss.

Soooo, I've started a super slow approach that is working quite nicely. I did face dunks and tons of ice packs with no problem, but trying the baths and showers was brutal. I decided that in only 1 month I could lower the water temp by a single degree daily and reach my goal. Day 1- 80 degrees, day 2- 79 deg. etc..... Today was 72 deg. (1 hour baths)

What I've noticed. First day I was shivering like mad in 80 deg water (sad, I know) but by even the second day I couldn't believe how much more comfortable it was. Each day I go down a degree and yet it always feels warmer than the day before! Amazing!

I'm often doing more spot CT in bed after the bath too for another hour. I'm feeling warmer during the day, burning up at night and often sleeping with no sheet or just a sheet at night even in our cold bedroom.

Just thought I'd share to help out anyone else like me who wants to do this but just can't jump right into 55 or lower degree water. I'm still getting the signs of getting cold adapted, and like I said earlier, it will only take a month to get painlessly down to 50 deg.

The only negative I've noticed is daytime fatigue, needing more sleep and not feeling rested on waking (all things that were awesome for me before CT)

### Adria — 2012-03-29T16:41:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8251>

Great to hear, Paleomom! I'm also going my own slow route with CT. I'm up to 20min in the cold bath 55 degrees at CT wk#5. But seeing benefits already !!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-29T17:00:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8260>

How are you decreasing and measuring the water? My amazon.com thermogun seems to be measuring the tub, not the water, so I can only go with the measurement after sitting in the water a long while, when the tub has cooled down to match the water.

### differentstory — 2012-03-29T18:40:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8327>

> **PaleoMom;8134 wrote:** Unlike the many hard core CT's on this forum, I'm a wuss when it come to cold water. Funny since I keep the house cooler than most, my bedroom is about 50 and I don't overdress outside, but water? I'm a wuss.
>
>
>
> Soooo, I've started a super slow approach that is working quite nicely. I did face dunks and tons of ice packs with no problem, but trying the baths and showers was brutal. I decided that in only 1 month I could lower the water temp by a single degree daily and reach my goal. Day 1- 80 degrees, day 2- 79 deg. etc..... Today was 72 deg. (1 hour baths)
>
>
>
> What I've noticed. First day I was shivering like mad in 80 deg water (sad, I know) but by even the second day I couldn't believe how much more comfortable it was. Each day I go down a degree and yet it always feels warmer than the day before! Amazing!
>
>
>
> I'm often doing more spot CT in bed after the bath too for another hour. I'm feeling warmer during the day, burning up at night and often sleeping with no sheet or just a sheet at night even in our cold bedroom.
>
>
>
> Just thought I'd share to help out anyone else like me who wants to do this but just can't jump right into 55 or lower degree water. I'm still getting the signs of getting cold adapted, and like I said earlier, it will only take a month to get painlessly down to 50 deg.
>
>
>
> The only negative I've noticed is daytime fatigue, needing more sleep and not feeling rested on waking (all things that were awesome for me before CT)

This WAS going to be my plan too, but my opinion is that it was so much easier to just go whole hog and drop the temp down. I started at 80 and it was brutal. Did that a few times. Went to 60 and it was brutal. 3 days of that and I was fine. Now the water fluctuates anywhere between 50 and 60 and I don't really notice. Just my opinion.

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-29T19:51:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8370>

Keep it up PaleoMom; whatever works, works. I am transitioning too and it gets easier. You can get there but I am finding it takes patience and persistence. I listen to my body more these days and that helps with more successes than losses.

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-29T20:42:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8392>

> **MamaGrok;8306 wrote:** How are you decreasing and measuring the water? My amazon.com thermogun seems to be measuring the tub, not the water, so I can only go with the measurement after sitting in the water a long while, when the tub has cooled down to match the water.

I'm using a thermometer made for putting in the fridge and freezer. I wasn't sure it was even water safe, but seems to be doing fine. It is slow to adjust though, so that can be challenging. It works fine though for me and it won't be long before I just go full cold and don't think about it.

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-29T20:44:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8394>

I'm just such a comfort person and non adrenaline junkie! It's great to find an easy way to do this without feeling like I'm torturing myself. I have patience and I've got plenty of time to cold adapt before the local lake is ready for swimming (they raise the water level in May)

### primat — 2012-03-29T20:52:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8399>

# Thread: I'm getting stronger!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kiera2 — 2012-03-29T05:55:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8125>

I am just blown away with how quickly I'm making progress. Only a few weeks ago, I couldn't lift anything due to degenerative disc disease. I couldn't hold 2Kg at arms length. Then, after 2 weeks of CT, I could hold out 4Kg, with no pain, and no following migraine. First time since 2003!

Now, I can raise 5Kg _in each hand_ at arms length! Both out in front, and at each side. Yep, 10kg! Unbelievable!

It's a little shakey, and I couldn't lift even a few grams more; and I'm so pleased!

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-29T06:20:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8132>

That's great coriander. I'm getting stronger too. One thing I've noticed is that I don't get sore after a workout anymore. It used to be, "no pain, no gain" but I'm liking the switch to: "no pain, ... good!"

I'm working on trying to do a pull up someday,

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-29T06:27:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8135>

Congrats Coriander! I love to hear these stories of such success. I look forward to great leaps in strength for me too!

### Glamorama — 2012-03-29T07:09:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8150>

Yay! Thats wodnerful!!

### Petra — 2012-03-29T12:02:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8178>

It confirms what I see in my body.

Thank you for posting this!

Glamorama,I'm sure you also made progress in the heavy lifting division, right?

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-29T13:40:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8187>

Awesome! That is an amazing story.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-30T07:48:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8652>

> **Petra;8222 wrote:** It confirms what I see in my body.
>
> Thank you for posting this!
>
>
>
> Glamorama,I'm sure you also made progress in the heavy lifting division, right?

Yes. Insane increases, but I didn't start out like Coriander, who had disc-issues. When I started, my squat was 15 kilos. 5 weeks later its 40 kilos.

### Grottenolm — 2012-03-30T08:10:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8659>

Amazing, inspiring progress ladies! Keep getting stronger

### agatha — 2012-03-30T12:40:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8685>

Awesome news! Have you showed your doctor yet?

# Thread: Pork Belly Suggestions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-03-29T14:26:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-8195>

Just picked up a pork belly at my local farm because it just sounded good . . . now I have no idea what to do with it! Suggestions? Recipes? Thanks!

### Martin — 2012-03-29T15:02:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-8208>

Hmmm, pork belly.

Such a grose sounding food item...... until one has a bite. Simply one of the best things I've ever eaten. But it was at a fancy restaurant so I'm afraid I can't offer much other then drool.

Martin

### David — 2012-03-29T16:05:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-8238>

Try this, it looks really good!

[http://momofukufor2.com/2010/01/roasted-pork-belly/](http://http://momofukufor2.com/2010/01/roasted-pork-belly/)

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-29T16:13:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-8240>

This is my FAVE cut of pork besides ribs! I slice it and treat it just like bacon. My favorite thing to do is bake it with sea salt and cinnamon. Yum!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-01T02:58:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-9608>

Hi BJK77,

I have just the thing for you. I used to make my own smoked bacon but I got to the point where it became too time consuming. (I would rather be spending my time making sausages!) Here is a simple recipe for salted pork belly. It is a lot like bacon without the nasty chemicals:

[http://www.eatkamloops.org/salted-pork-belly/](http://http://www.eatkamloops.org/salted-pork-belly/)

If you like that recipe, I have just completed an index of **eatkamloops.org** recipes. Here is over three years of recipes. Most of the recipes will be good for someone on the modified paleo diet:

[http://www.eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-scd-recipes/](http://http://www.eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-scd-recipes/)

### BJK77 — 2012-04-01T04:06:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-9637>

Thanks so much for all the suggestions. DH and I are finally, finally going to get a date night next weekend so I think I'll pick one of these recipes for a special meal!

### Baba — 2012-04-01T18:17:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-9803>

The secret for a crispy skin is a good soak. So cook it in the oven skin down in liquid (beer, wine, broth, water...) for about two hours. Low heat. Then take out, cool and dry the skin. Score the skin with a craft knife and sprinke with salt. Bake in a hot oven skin side up and watch the crackling almost explode. I'd say half an hour or so. Drool. Eat. Get someone else to clean the oven.

### Birdy — 2012-04-01T19:37:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-9856>

wow that looks good....

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-02T05:13:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-10143>

Coincidentally, this very moment, I just pulled out of the oven my third time making it using this recipe. Best luck finding your fave recipe!
[https://deadspin.com/how-to-cook-pork-belly-which-thoroughly-kicks-bacons-a-1619788169](https://deadspin.com/how-to-cook-pork-belly-which-thoroughly-kicks-bacons-a-1619788169)

PS drezy -- I lower my oven down to 300degF the last 20 minutes -- because I like the dark brown color at that point. Then, baked it to the end of the full recipe time. Forever in your debt!

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-16T02:34:41+0200

Thanks much Sheddie!

### Earth Monkey — 2018-03-17T07:57:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237351>

These instructions rom my local gourmet butcher (one of the top 3 in my city) produce excellent results: score the skin of the pork belly with a sharp knife in a criss-cross pattern, sprinkle liberally with salt and rub in. Roast in an open tray in the oven for 1hr @ 160deg celcius, then turn up to 220C for 20mins

### John Saf — 2018-03-17T18:23:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237367>

I make my pork belly low and slow: rub with salt (and black pepper if it is okay)
Owen for 72'C for 8-12 hours. In water bath half way up. If you have local onions and herbs now it gives a great taste if you put it it in the water.
Roasting it at high temps is not optimal even if the taste is great. Low/slow makes it melt in the mouth, hope it is aprowed method.

### drezy — 2018-03-17T19:15:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237370>

> [John Saf said:](/goto/post?id=237367)
>
> I make my pork belly low and slow: rub with salt (and black pepper if it is okay)
> Owen for 72'C for 8-12 hours. In water bath half way up. If you have local onions and herbs now it gives a great taste if you put it it in the water.
> Roasting it at high temps is not optimal even if the taste is great. Low/slow makes it melt in the mouth, hope it is aprowed method.

I'll have to give that one a shot. I found a good, but less slow recipe and got comments from others here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/)

That is only 2.5 hours so I bet you end up with fall apart pork at the end. I like your slower method more in principal already. I'll chime back in once I test drive it. Living where I do I'll have to drop some hot peppers into the water as well to infuse spiciness.

Just to share some of my kitchen thinking, if you add black pepper just go ahead and add turmeric and vice versa. They work together in more than just taste:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...-help-quantum-diseases-linked-to-light.18468/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/why-turmeric-curcumin-help-quantum-diseases-linked-to-light.18468/)

### John Saf — 2018-03-18T16:11:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237412>

Jack kruse:
the nitrate thing is a bunch of shit......what is not is cooking temps of your meat.......that is a big deal

### drezy — 2018-03-18T18:57:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237425>

> [John Saf said:](/goto/post?id=237412)
>
> Jack kruse:
> the nitrate thing is a bunch of shit......what is not is cooking temps of your meat.......that is a big deal

The whole conversation here: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/bacon.5315/#post-69293](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/bacon.5315/#post-69293)

### Sheddie — 2018-03-18T21:29:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237452>

> [John Saf said:](/goto/post?id=237367)
>
> I make my pork belly low and slow: rub with salt (and black pepper if it is okay)
> Owen for 72'C for 8-12 hours. In water bath half way up. If you have local onions and herbs now it gives a great taste if you put it it in the water.
> Roasting it at high temps is not optimal even if the taste is great. Low/slow makes it melt in the mouth, hope it is aprowed method.

Is that a method with a 'sous vide' appliance? Sounds delicious.

### drezy — 2018-03-18T23:43:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237462>

I have a vacuum sealer and a cast iron pot that I call my redneck sous vide setup for now. I think a crockpot could work well too.

### John Saf — 2018-03-19T00:00:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237468>

> [Sheddie said:](/goto/post?id=237452)
>
> Is that a method with a 'sous vide' appliance? Sounds delicious.

No sous vide. I cook in a heatresistant glass pan or stainless caseroll without lid. I dont want to do plastics/(heat)/food in my house. slowcocked fatty pork cuts in the owen with water kicks sousvides ass

### freesia — 2018-03-19T01:41:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237475>

John - so no water in with the pork....just in the bath around the pan the pork is sitting in?
I have been stockpiling pork belly strips in the freezer trying to figure out how I'm going to cook them well without too much heat. Might try your method but in my slowcooker.

Dr Seignalet, a French immunologist, wrote quite a lot about avoiding cooking (proteins, fats and carbohydrates) at temps above 110 degrees c. He warned about acrylamide - which as far as I can tell is why coffee (roasted) has fairly recently been classified carcinogenic.

### freesia — 2018-03-21T06:10:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237640>

Ha well I did it.
Laid pork belly strips in a (lidded) ovenproof glass pan, rubbed in salt, pepper and a bit of cayenne, ginger juice and lime zest. Propped the pan up an inch in the slow cooker so water would surround it from underneath. Filled with hot water to halfway up the pork pan and cooked on "low" setting for 5 hours...water was barely at a simmer. So easy to eat!

I opted for a lidded pan because the nature of cooking in a slow cooker is that condensation collects on the inside of the lid and I didn't know whether I wanted water dripping into my pork. It did cross my mind though - that water \*might\* be DDW...hence upgrade my pork further

### Sheddie — 2019-04-02T02:37:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-267075>

# Thread: random rashes - cold urticaria or... ?... and what do do
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-29T15:01:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-8207>

So after following Dr. K's CT protocol and getting to the icy tub stage a few times, I developed itchy/scabby areas on my head which I thought were contact dermatitis from my neoprene cpap headgear. So... I switched to another mask/headgear type, eased off the CT, and after 2 weeks of worsening, finally resorted to a weak cortisone lotion. That seemed to help a lot, so earlier this week, I started up with a couple of tubbings, alternating days with icepacks and....the scalp issues are BACK  I'm upping my fish oil and krill oil and started using the cortisone (.1%) lotion again. Should I quit the CT for awhile?? I was fine during the pre-tubbing coldpack stage, no problems at all. If it's cold urticaria, why my HEAD?? And if it's not, what is it and should I "stay the course" or...?

\*sigh\* So glad for this forum and all you folks!!

### shilohman — 2012-03-29T18:18:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-8323>

> **meyoolia;8252 wrote:** So after following Dr. K's CT protocol and getting to the icy tub stage a few times, I developed itchy/scabby areas on my head which I thought were contact dermatitis from my neoprene cpap headgear. So... I switched to another mask/headgear type, eased off the CT, and after 2 weeks of worsening, finally resorted to a weak cortisone lotion. That seemed to help a lot, so earlier this week, I started up with a couple of tubbings, alternating days with icepacks and....the scalp issues are BACK  I'm upping my fish oil and krill oil and started using the cortisone (.1%) lotion again. Should I quit the CT for awhile?? I was fine during the pre-tubbing coldpack stage, no problems at all. If it's cold urticaria, why my HEAD?? And if it's not, what is it and should I "stay the course" or...?
>
>
>
> \*sigh\* So glad for this forum and all you folks!!

Get your omega6/3 tested along with HS-CRP. Sounds like you have a lot of inflammation in your body. You might try rubbing some CO or olive oil on your head once the rash is gone. I would stop CT until the rash is gone or go back to the ice packs until the rash is gone.

### shilohman — 2012-03-29T18:45:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-8330>

Oops, forgot, you can't rub any oil on your head if you have hair. I don't , so ignore that part.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-29T20:14:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-8380>

I did just that a few weeks ago. 1.1 mg/L, with under 1.0 being "low risk" (for heart attack I guess), and 1-3 "average". yeeshk, i do hate the thought of having to reacclimate, but I'll back off some, back to the coldpack stage and up my krill oil til i can get my 6/3's tested.

And re the CO, heh heh... sure I can.... it just wouldn't be pretty! I might just give it a try, messier but less pharmaceutical than cortisone  And with this whole "mom's gone nuts" paleo/CT thing with the family these days, i think i could grease my head up good and stick twigs in and they'd simply shake their heads. rofl....

\*still smirking\* Good ideas, thanks

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-30T22:42:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-8952>

Ok, so I've picked up some homeopathic apis...gonna try that for a day or so, then it's on to your suggestion, Shilohman, and the coconut oil shmear (I've also picked up a fabric "bonnet" that should keep the worst of the oil off my pillow) - somehow with coconut oil being such a grand thing for intestinal nasties, it seems to ring right that it'd be good for skin nasties, too  ....then comes, maybe, aloe..... and somewhere in there - \*sigh\* - stopping even the icepacking awhile, if nothing else works.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-30T23:42:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-8988>

> **meyoolia;9004 wrote:** the coconut oil shmear

The ladies from India that I have worked with swear by combing CO through the hair in the evening and then washing it out in the morning. "Best conditioner ever" is what I've been told. So it's not just a paleo crazy thing.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-31T21:41:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-9338>

> **WeAreDevo;9040 wrote:** The ladies from India that I have worked with swear by combing CO through the hair in the evening and then washing it out in the morning. "Best conditioner ever" is what I've been told. So it's not just a paleo crazy thing.

Upside: They're right! my hair's great this morning after a night "soaking" in it.

Downside: Rash is worse  doubt it's due to the CO tho.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-31T22:30:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-9358>

When I started LC three years ago, my persistent scabby scalp cleared up. Then, when I began the reset with my husband at the beginning of February, it started popping up again. This was before I even began the CT protocol. I have been doing the CT for awhile now, and it seems to be easing up. So, I am not so sure that it is the CT causing the scabby scalp at all. I am trying to see it, perhaps, it might be one of the supplements that I am now taking. Since I don't think that it is the food that I have taken away, and I really haven't added any new foods, I am checking out the ingredients on the supplements.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-02T04:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-10109>

A possible lead.... "Localized cold reflex urticaria leads to symptoms of hives and swelling away from the area of direct exposure to cold. An ice-cube test on the arm may cause hives to form a few inches away from the site of ice-cube application, for example".

Has anybody experienced this?? Benadryl is helping a bit, but not much

# Thread: Fitness Gains?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evo Mama — 2012-03-29T22:33:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/post-8461>

Just wondering what you all are experiencing regarding fitness gains on the CT protocol.

I've been doing the Leptin Rx and CT for over 3 weeks now. My only workouts have been walking (as per the Leptin Rx protocol). Out of curiosity, I observed the following:

Push ups: Before LRx & CT, I could do 15 girl push ups, and 3 normal push ups. Last weekend, I tested and could do 8 normal push ups.

Chin ups: I've NEVER been able to do a chin up, EVER. Early this week, using my iron gym, I did a chin up with just a tiny bit of assistance (my feet against the door frame), and could hold myself for several minutes at the top.

I'm astounded that I've seen such progress without really working out at all.

What have ya'll been experiencing?

PS - I should mention, this wasn't even right after a CT session, which I do at night. I did both of these tests around 4pm.

### Dave — 2012-03-30T00:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/post-8483>

That's awesome progress - congrats and keep up the good work!

I myself have been training forever and have been significantly stronger in the past (although a lot heavier w/ more fat) so it's tough to say for sure, but during the 2nd week of CT I did get a lifetime PR in the overhead press for my current body-weight. Lately I've hit a slump in both lifting and CT (only the 3rd week) but I believe that's b/c there's some detox going on or at the least it exposed a few potential problems for me to look into (thanks to Dr. K's advice).

Probably in a month or so I'll be wishing I've done this stuff my whole life despite the current slump.

I started LRx at the same time and I believe that's made a huge difference (~20%) in my blood glucose levels (I have T2DM).

> **Evo Mama;8509 wrote:** Just wondering what you all are experiencing regarding fitness gains on the CT protocol.
>
>
>
> I've been doing the Leptin Rx and CT for over 3 weeks now. My only workouts have been walking (as per the Leptin Rx protocol). Out of curiosity, I observed the following:
>
>
>
> Push ups: Before LRx & CT, I could do 15 girl push ups, and 3 normal push ups. Last weekend, I tested and could do 8 normal push ups.
>
>
>
> Chin ups: I've NEVER been able to do a chin up, EVER. Early this week, using my iron gym, I did a chin up with just a tiny bit of assistance (my feet against the door frame), and could hold myself for several minutes at the top.
>
>
>
> I'm astounded that I've seen such progress without really working out at all.
>
>
>
> What have ya'll been experiencing?
>
>
>
> PS - I should mention, this wasn't even right after a CT session, which I do at night. I did both of these tests around 4pm.

### Cody — 2012-03-30T04:05:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/post-8571>

I posted about my gains in the CT forum.

But I'm able to do 10 good form full pushups now, a month ago, I couldn't do any (in good form or even go all the way down).

My arms are also much bigger and more defined. Less fat, more muscle. Good sign.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-30T04:10:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/post-8578>

I could do 8 proper push-ups before CT. a month later, with daily CT, I could do 14. I did not do a single push-up in that month, so didn't practice for it.

Did my first pull-up and chin-up, unassisted, after 3 weeks of CT. Never really practiced for that either, but was completely unable to do it prior to CT.

I double my squat in 3 sessions (I do legs once weekly at the gym).

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-31T02:19:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/post-9034>

Thanks for sharing guys!

I'm gonna try to wait a few more days before testing again, but I'm super impressed thus far.

After I finish the reset, I might have to join a gym for some decent heavy lifting.

### Hidan — 2012-04-03T10:34:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/post-10704>

Hello everybody,...

I read out all the posts and all the posts very informational about the fitness gain.But i think HCG diet is

the best solution for gaining fitness and weight.Gym an weight lifting more effective for good fitness.

### hellojtm — 2012-04-03T20:21:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/post-10863>

I haven't had any fitness gains that I'm aware of since starting CT. I'm probably not cold adapted, but maybe getting close based on some personal observations.

If anyone is claiming fitness gains it should be noted that you'd have to be tracking your program pretty closely for a long time, like maybe 1-2 years, to be really sure, unless it is some enormous undeniable gain. And then to attribute it to CT that'd have to be the only variable, for example I know that CT is part of an ongoing effort to correct circadian mismatches, and proper light exposure is another. If you have recently started sleeping properly for the first time, whether this was made possible by CT or not, then that is a major confounder.

jesse

### Jim — 2012-04-14T20:44:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/post-15108>

> **hellojtm;10932 wrote:** I haven't had any fitness gains that I'm aware of since starting CT. I'm probably not cold adapted, but maybe getting close based on some personal observations.
>
>
>
> If anyone is claiming fitness gains it should be noted that you'd have to be tracking your program pretty closely for a long time, like maybe 1-2 years, to be really sure, unless it is some enormous undeniable gain. And then to attribute it to CT that'd have to be the only variable, for example I know that CT is part of an ongoing effort to correct circadian mismatches, and proper light exposure is another. If you have recently started sleeping properly for the first time, whether this was made possible by CT or not, then that is a major confounder.
>
>
>
> jesse

I think I meet the criteria Jesse.

I have been on the reset for a while (Nov 2011) but started CT in Feb. My workout is basically unchanged and I have been doing it for years. I do 5 minutes of warmup on a recumbent bike then do my lifting routine. Since starting CT I noticed that my heart rate (HR) on the bike's HR monitor doesn't go as high as it used to. Typically I would break 100 BPM towards the end of my 5 minute warmup (at the same difficulty level). Now after CT it barely gets to 90. I attribute this fitness gain to CT.

Jim

### hellojtm — 2012-04-15T08:01:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/post-15252>

Thanks Jim. I'm still sucking wind over here. Given that I believe there are performance gains to be had my current thinking is that I've been overtraining by combining daily CT with 2-3 workouts a week. I would never do intense exercise daily so why do intense CT (note, intense is relative)

Thanks for the response.

### FF_BC — 2012-04-15T10:27:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/post-15264>

In BW exercises I've been experiencing some gains, though I'm not sure if they're from loosing weight or actual gain in strenght. In non BW exercises I've also had some gains, but I've been experiencing gains since I started lifting weights 3 months ago

I believe I will measure my gains in how _fast_ I reach 2xBW in Deadlifts and Squats compared to other non low carb, non fasting, non CT:ing people. I'm currently at 1,3xBW in deads and 1,2xBW in squats...

### puddles — 2012-08-21T14:06:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/post-46021>

wow great work man. keep up the spirit!

### Stephen — 2012-08-28T05:41:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitness-gains.533/post-47705>

> **FF\_BC;15333 wrote:** In BW exercises I've been experiencing some gains, though I'm not sure if they're from loosing weight or actual gain in strenght. In non BW exercises I've also had some gains, but I've been experiencing gains since I started lifting weights 3 months ago
>
>
>
> I believe I will measure my gains in how _fast_ I reach 2xBW in Deadlifts and Squats compared to other non low carb, non fasting, non CT:ing people. I'm currently at 1,3xBW in deads and 1,2xBW in squats...

That's probably a good way to measure your progress because 3 months of lifting is all beginner gains which should come easy and quick for a healthy injury free person with any sort of reasonable effort.

About 3 years ago as a non-CT'ing person I hit a 2 x BW deadlift after about 6 months of focusing almost entirely on getting stronger in the deadlift. Within 10 months I pulled 182.5kg @ 80kg BW. My all time 1RM PR is 200kg at about 85kg BW. The deadlift goes up quick if you focus on it.

Right now I'm CT'ing and working on my back squat with the goal of a 2 x BW 1RM at or below parallel and 1.5 x BW x 20reps. In theory CT'ing should allow me to recover better/quicker and thus squat more often a la John Broz.

# Thread: Can sciatica be nutrition related?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-sciatica-be-nutrition-related.535/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthnut — 2012-03-30T03:31:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-sciatica-be-nutrition-related.535/post-8540>

The past two weeks I've been experiencing sciatica.... sleep is impossible. I am doing all the stretches and exercises, but it has not gone away yet.

I was wondering if there might be specific depleted nutients in those who experience this.....or is it more of a mechanical problem?

Maybe there any supplements that might target nerve pain? The CT seems to help somewhat, but the pain is most pronounced at night....runs into the glutes, legs and the back of my hips....torture!

I had read a while back that sciatica may present as a type of neuropathy that is seen in diabetics? I take large doses of ALA & benfotiamine for peripheral neuropahty and that has kept me symptom free. Which leads me to believe the sciatica is more of a structural issue.

### tweety — 2012-03-31T01:53:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-sciatica-be-nutrition-related.535/post-9024>

Interesting. I got this a few weeks ago. I was trying to decide if I was healing old injuries (retracing) or if I had actually injured it again while walking in bad shoes. Well, it went away on its own. It started when I really cleaned up my diet and did CT, so I am leaning towards it being a healing reaction.

With everyone really cleaning up their diets here, I would guess there is a lot of healing going on.

### healthnut — 2012-03-31T03:12:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-sciatica-be-nutrition-related.535/post-9052>

I started CT a few weeks ago....maybe that's the deal. I didn't make the connection, thanks! The retracing theory is interesting. I bulged some discs about 5 years ago, so maybe the formerly injured areas are getting some new healing and nerve stimulation.

### shilohman — 2012-03-31T03:30:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-sciatica-be-nutrition-related.535/post-9061>

according to Dr K it most definitely is. Can't remember which blog where he states that. Just search for sciatica, you should find it.

### Melinda — 2012-03-31T03:31:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-sciatica-be-nutrition-related.535/post-9062>

Me too! I've had sciatica off and on over the years and two days after my first tub soak, wham, I bent over the dishwasher and all the muscles in my lower back pulled at once. The sciatica developed over a few days and I'm still holding myself carefully. I feel great when I'm in the swimming pool and I think I'm healing quickly. I wondered what got it started?

# Thread: HELP: Total Cholesterol Help (and fatty liver/thyroid?)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-total-cholesterol-help-and-fatty-liver-thyroid.540/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lennyaus — 2012-03-30T11:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-total-cholesterol-help-and-fatty-liver-thyroid.540/post-8676>

I'll give some background:

Overweight, trying leptin rest, cold baths on an almost daily basis for 20 minutes however I am in a rut with life. Work and play etc. Fiancee not liking this suggested anti-depressants (She is on them). I say no, but reached a compromise that I would see the GP. Well, we know how this is going to turn out right?

I spoke to my GP. He is very friendly and a great communicator. He doesn't feel that I need medication for my moods (yet) and likes my suggestion of a blood test as maybe something is out of whack. So I get a fasted one taken.

I get it back and my total cholesterol is high. According to the sheet I asked for it is : 6.8 mmol/L. GP says that it is close for prescribing medication for. Look, I am never going to go on that nonsense. However, I have a fiancÃ©e who is now concerned about my health and I can't change her ways because I am still overweight and depressed.

**Questions:**

-   Does anyone know if Total Cholesterol lowers with adherence to a ketogenic diet?
-   Do I stand my ground?
    Do I perhaps limit fat/meat intake a bit to keep the peace? For example, replace meat with more fish?

I looked at the low-cholesterol diet that I was given and want to spew! It sucks. I got my blood pressure measured and it was 120/70. So that's cool.

Also with the test two other things came up. Liver and Thyroid. Apparently my thyroid is on the over-active side? Well slightly. Liver reading is not good but that was expected. Perhaps Liver is why it is hard for me to lose weight? I did abuse alcohol for a period of years.

Here is the list of "chemistry bloods" from the blood test I got:

NAME UNITS RANGE:

S SODIUM:141 mmol/L (135-145)

S POTASSIUM: 4.6 mmol/L (3.5-5.5)

S CHLORIDE:103 mmol/L (95-110)

S BICARB: 25 mmol/L (20-32)

S UREA: 5.9 mmol/L (3.0-7.5)

S CREAT: 92 umol/L (60-110)

S eGFR: 84

ANION GAP: 18 mmol/L (10-20)

T-BILLI: 29 H (I assume H means high?) umol/L (4-20)

S ALP: 81 U/L (35-110)

S GGT: 24 U/L (5-50)

S ALT: 80 H (This is liver I think) U/L (5-40)

S AST: 24 U/L (10-40)

T PROTEIN: 74 g/l (66-83)

S ALBUMIN: 49 g/l (39-50)

S GLOBULIN: 25 g/l (23-39)

S CHOLESTEROL: 6.8 H mmol/L (3.5-5.5)

S TRIG: 1.5 mmol/L (0.5-2.0)

S HDL-CHOL: 1.12 mmol/L (>1.00)

S LDL-CHOL: 5.0 H mmol/L (>3.5)

S LDLC/HDLC: 4.5 H (>4.0)

S Chol/HDLC: 6.1 H (>4.5)

S IRON: 14 umol/L (5-30)

S TRF: 2.7 g/l (2.0-3.2)

S TRF.SAT.: 21 % (10-45)

S FERRITIN: 241 ng/ml (30-500)

GLU (FAST): 5.1 mmol/L (3.6-6.0)

I thought the doc asked for testosterone too, but I don't see it on this list.

Basically if anyone who has any understanding of the readings above could provide me some advice as to what is good/bad/ugly and how to combat it, it would be greatly appreciated.

I do not eat 100% optimal, I will say that now. That is defined by Dr.Kruses blogs. I am weak and have sugar free energy drinks in the morning. The most ridiculous thing about this is I know it is bad. I only drink them at work though. Coping habit? (I know this sounds pathetic, but no avoiding the facts). I eat a heavy meat diet with occasional white rice. Actually if someone who sounds like they know what they are talking about tells me sugar free energy drinks are the nutritional hybrid of Hitler/Kony it would be welcome.

Anyway sorry, this became a bit of a ramble/rant. I basically got home from the doctors, google'd some of the stuff listed above to try and decipher and started writing this post. Any input would be greatly appreciated. I've told my fiancee I'll cut down on the meat a bit. I don't want too, but maybe my quantities are too much. Thanks for reading.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-30T11:46:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-total-cholesterol-help-and-fatty-liver-thyroid.540/post-8680>

My total cholestrol was 7.2 last month. Thats after 9 months of Paleo eating. Doc wasn't worried, neither am I. My ratio between LDL and HDL is very good, indicating that the bad cholestrol is low why the good is high. You can never win if your doc only looks at the total, a Paleo diet will always give a high total reading, but that number is not relevant. Whats relevant is the ratio and if your LDLs are fluffy or dense.

I had liver-issues at my last blood-test. Suspected fatty liver. Def a reason behind me not burning off fat as fast as I should be. I'm seeing a naturopath an taking some herbal supplements to deal with that, my liver-reading was far better a month in that initially. I suggest you read some of the paleo bibles like Robb Wolff's books (he covers cholestrol in depth) and start taking what the doc says with a massive grain of salt. A naturopath might be more helpful for you.

And quit the energy drinks. They sure as hell are not helping your liver.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-30T19:49:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-total-cholesterol-help-and-fatty-liver-thyroid.540/post-8856>

I'd suggest reading this post by Griff on MDA re: cholesterol. An awesome read and super informative. This helped me tremendously when I freaked about my high cholesterol numbers. [http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread4723.html](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread4723.html)

Like Glam said above, it's about the ratio - not the individual #'s.

Oh - and definitely ditch the energy drinks. There's no room for that junk on a path to optimal.

# Thread: Keto paleo and hypothyroid/adrenal issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pardus — 2012-03-30T20:31:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-8877>

Hi all,

I know there is a lot of dogma going around the paleosphere about keto diets causing hypothyroid and adrenal fatigue issues (cold hands and feet, bloating, constipation, brain fog, etc) to which the solution is always to add more "safe starches".

Unfortunately, my hands are still cold, only warming up for about an hour after I eat a meal. Been doing CT cold showers and cold walks for over a month now.

What is your view on the situation of vlc/keto causing hypothyroid and adrenal fatigue?

Did anyone with cold hands/hypothyroid, leaky gut, adrenal fatigue, etc notice any improvement with CT? If so, what was your experience and how long did it take?

### CheshireCat57 — 2012-03-31T16:53:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-9220>

Hi Pardus, I qualify with the problems but have not been doing things long enough to become Cold Adapted. I don't have cold hands normally though so cannot offer anything for you on that. I do know that jack says all over his blog and in this forum that you should not be eating carbs in winter. I don't know when the cut over is but in the Northern hemisphere we are not in the season for harvesting (March 2012) many vegetables so I would think that carbs are still inappropriate.

One thread I read in the forum talked about not covering up when you stop the CT as the covers (shirts, Pj's etc) seem to trap the cold in. I did this last night and it worked for me...and the wife enjoyed it too  Jack speaks about keeping hands and feet warm during CT...

Hope this helps.

### Inger — 2012-03-31T16:58:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-9221>

Do you get organs and fishheads to eat? Plain meat only will not do it longterm.

I believe fishheads espesially are great for healing. Try fishbroth or eat whole small fish with head and all. I occasionally make a smoothie with mackerelheads and water and drink it. Tastes awful but makes me feel great... I know it sounds crazy. Fishbroth with heads from wild fish are also real good.

### Baba — 2012-03-31T17:05:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-9225>

My adrenals have recovered on a low-carb diet. I took Hydrocortisone for two years (to give them a break) and have recently weaned. The fact that I don't need antihistamines for hayfever (yet) this season is a sign that my adrenals are doing their job again. Although I weaned off HC before starting CT, I believe it helps a lot.

My thyroid doesn't show any signs of recovering, but I have Hashimoto's which may be a special situation.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-03-31T20:46:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-9317>

Hi Friends,

I was really worried about this too. I have been doing the SCD/GAPS/modified paleo diet for over six years. I found I did better on a low carbohydrate version of the diet though I have never tried to be ketogenic. I have also played around with seasonal eating. I have cured myself of asthma, allergies, chronic sinus infections, yeast infections, osteoarthritis and epilepsy.

BUT I do have a low body temperature. I measure my morning temperature before rising and have done so for over 35 cycles. My charts would say I am estrogen dominant and I have a standard infertility pattern. (I am 46.) My charts indicate that I have an adrenal problem not a thyroid problem due to my extreme daily fluctuations in my morning temperatures.

There are two main views out there about low body temperature. One says its a sign of endocrine dysfunction. This appears to be the dominant view in the paleo community at this time with all the talk about safe starches. The other view would say, I am in calorie restriction, and that lower body temperature is great because it will increase my longevity.

I have been trying to figure out which of these two opinions might be happening for me. I have been experimenting with over-eating, safe starches, adrenal extracts, herbals, etc from about 2009. None of these experiments have increased my body temperature for any length of time. I was starting to think that maybe there was nothing wrong with a low body temperature since I appear healthy in other ways.

It was late last year that I learned about Dr Krase's work but I really didn't understand his earlier posts. It has been just recently that I have come back to his website to find the cold thermogenesis massives. Wow, what can I say. His material blew my mind. If he is right, and he has done six years of clinic work, it doesn't matter if my adrenals are burnt-out or my thyroid is under-functioning. Cold thermogenesis, a seasonal ketogenic diet and balancing circadian cycles will open a pathway in the brain that will overrun any glandular endocrine dysfunction.

By the way, I asked Dr Kruse about his opinion on low body temperature. He thinks it's better but he didn't give a reason. Maybe he will expand on the reason why here.

I have gone moderately ketogenic. I am turning off the lights at sunset and I am in the first stages of cold thermogensis. I will keep you posted as to how it goes and what it does to my system. I like the term, biohack!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-03-31T20:54:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-9322>

> **Inger;9283 wrote:** Do you get organs and fishheads to eat? Plain meat only will not do it longterm.
>
> I believe fishheads espesially are great for healing. Try fishbroth or eat whole small fish with head and all. I occasionally make a smoothie with mackerelheads and water and drink it. Tastes awful but makes me feel great... I know it sounds crazy. Fishbroth with heads from wild fish are also real good.

Hi Inger,

Fish broths are very healing. The adrenal gland in fish is in the head, so you are eating fish adrenals. Eating the area behind the eyes is extremely high in vitamin C. Fish eggs are a superfood. Eating raw fish is very nourishing. I am impressed that you can eat the whole fish in a smoothie. You are a real trooper!

### hazyjane — 2012-04-02T06:20:11+0300

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-03T15:35:27+0300

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-03T15:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-10746>

back in august I had a backward circadian cycle - Cortisol was HIGH At night, and LOW in the morning - influenced by years of shift work, and stress.

I continued w/the LR - eating BAB w/in first 30mins of waking, lunch 4-5hrs later, dinner 4-5hrs later, b/w3hr prior to bed. I took the following: Maca, Ltheanine, Rodihola, high dose VitC, high dose B-complex, and Apex Energetics Adaptocrine. I also stopped wearing sunglasses during the day, and made sure I got plenty of sun on my eyes throughout the day, kept lights low or off at night... saw a reversal to normal 6mths later.

When I started CT - I discovered quite quickly night CTing was not for me... I usually CT in the AM... but now 8wks into CTing I can CT in the afternoon and not impact sleep...

CTing w/the LR, following the circadian cycles, and seasonal eating will right the adrenals - and turn back on the HPA axis - as well as the Pituary -Thyroid axis... but you have to be patient...

# Thread: persistent numb toes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-03-30T20:36:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8882>

OK. For the second time after CT i've had numb toes. I saw some other folks mention it and I searched the blogs. Dr. K. says something about the n3/n6 balance again and cell signaling for the nervous system. I believe it is a real phenomenon that we should start to iron out, i..e everyone share their experience with it so we can get a common thread and then let's understand the mechanism if possible. I think it will help us all, especially people that join in late and experience it, if we put our heads together and build our collective knowledge.

### hellojtm — 2012-03-30T20:43:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8887>

specifically my 2nd and 3rd toes on my right foot (if the big toe is the first toe). It'd be fun to get an acupuncturist or reflexologist to pontificate on it... I'm now going on 1.5 hours after getting out of the bath and they are starting to wake up. definitely a nerve thing as they feel asleep, possibly not getting any blood?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-30T21:03:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8895>

Are the toes white? Maybe it's Reynauds syndrome (an autoimmune disease).

Yesterday I had 3 toes on each foot turn white AFTER I got out of the bath. I don't even put my feet in the water and I've been CTing for six weeks! I took the dog for a walk yesterday right after the bath and it took 35 min for the numbness to go away.

In my case it's Reynauds. I know Dr. K had mentioned in the comments of one of the CT blogs that it is caused by too high 06s. I just had mine tested and I'm at 2.96:1. So I'm not really sure what is going on. I hope someone has some insights and look forward to the responses. I don't want to just say "Oh, I have this infliction". I want to not have this (Reynauds) at all.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-30T21:19:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8905>

After pushing the immersions from 20 minutes to 35 minutes, i get numbness intermittently in either my right small toe or my left small toe, and/or my small pinky finger. It isn't always my right or left side - however, if the toe and the finger go numb, it is always on the same side. example, my left pink and my left toe. my right pinky, my right toe. Sometimes it is just the toe too.

It usually goes away in 30 minutes, and they aren't turning white. I'm assuming it is just a side-effect of the vasoconstriction process.

### hellojtm — 2012-03-30T21:37:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8915>

Just to capture some more variables... getting my torso into my tub ends up being like holding a yoga pose for 30 minutes. I can't rule out some nerve impingement. My hips get sore and are complaining big time but there is not much room to shift around.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-30T22:25:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8944>

I have this happen as well - sometimes toes on both feet, but usually more isolated to my left side. At its worst, the numbness has lasted for over an hour. I also keep my feet out of the water and it doesn't happen until 20 minutes or so after I get out of the tub. I have yet to test my O6/O3 so not sure if that's the culprit in my case or not. I did develop a bit of Raynaud's this past winter for the first time, so I assume that's what it is.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-30T22:31:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8947>

ditto here, too, re numbness and not having feet in the water. Mostly on the "inside" half (if you cut it in half vertically, it's on the half that'd touch the other big toe  ) of my left big toe, and more recently similarly on the right... with a little tingliness on the 2nd left toe as well. It would take so long to clear I didn't even notice how long it took. I haven't had my o6/o3's checked either, but didn't develop contact cold urticaria when icepacking or tubbing. Weirdness!

### hellojtm — 2012-03-30T22:37:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8950>

Could be Raynaud's for me too. Interesting, just reading the mayo clinic web page about it.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-31T01:26:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-9019>

Today was my 15th 40 minute soak, and I am able to keep my feet in the water now for the entire time (at least today I could), and as usual, I have two of the smaller toes on my right foot go numb after I have been out of the tub for about ten minutes. I do wear neoprene booties. I try to keep my socks and shoes off until they are mostly back to normal. My heels are always much colder than my toes, but they don't seem to go numb. The biggest challenge I have with the toes being numb, is that it messes with my balance for awhile. Otherwise, things get back to normal fairly soon. I wonder if my toes are going to go numb every time regardless of how often, or who cold, I go?

### janagram — 2012-03-31T03:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-9071>

How common is reynaud's?

theres 3 of you on this thread who think you have it.....you're scaring me...

### MonteD — 2012-03-31T07:16:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-9120>

> **janagram;9123 wrote:** How common is reynaud's?
>
> theres 3 of you on this thread who think you have it.....you're scaring me...

Ditto.....

### KetoWarrior — 2012-03-31T18:29:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-9269>

From [Wikipedia](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raynaud's_phenomenon):

> People in their thirties are most likely to develop Raynaud's, and the phenomenon is more common in women than in men, with women three times more likely to be diagnosed than men. The Framingham Study showed that Raynaud phenomenon affects 5% of the population.

I got it a lot in my hands last winter. None this winter. I got in two toes during my first CT bath but not since and have experienced an overall improvement in my circulation to my hands and feet with CT just using skin color as an indicator.

### angieonthegogo — 2012-04-02T01:19:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-10018>

I had toe numbness for the 1st time last night but it didn't start until after I was out of the bath for 15 mins or so. I CTd for an hour but I also was contorting myself for quite some time in order to stay as submerged as possible. I'm wondering if that was it or I just pushed it in the water too cold for too long and since the last time I ate.

### Gonzo — 2012-04-02T02:35:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-10052>

I have Raynaud's. I soak in a tub at 50 degrees, but only put hands and feet in for about 15 minutes currently, during a 40-45 minute body soak. Fingers and toes usually do blanch white and go numb after I get out of the tub, just like typical Raynaud's episodes. My omega-6mega-3 just came back at 1.9:1, so I am not sure why the Raynaud's is still an issue. I plan to increase omega-6 foods just a bit to try to get to 4:1 or so, but don't know if it will worsen the Raynaud's. Of note, as I increase the time my hands and feet are in the water, it is less uncomfortable each time, and now I am comfortable within just 2-3 minutes. It is after the soak that I get numbness. I am hoping it will gradually improve with the CT.

### vkiernan — 2012-04-02T03:08:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-10074>

My heels and toes go a little numb once out of the tub and I chaulk it up to the body trying to warm everything up and the feet are last on the list. I have not been concerned at all with my feet getting this way. I leave them like this for a bit and then put them in luke warm water before leaving for work if they are still pretty bad where it might affect my walk.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-04-02T16:30:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-10284>

If I take long cold showers, I get nothing. But if I do an immersion, I tend to have a random toe, or finger go numb. The affected digit does not turn white - but starts getting numb about 15-30 minutes after I get out of the tub and am completely dry. Also, I do not submerge my hands or feet when soaking - i'm chalking this up to vasoconstriction that hopefully goes away----numb fingers crossed.
