>
> I hope we both find the answer! hugs

Thank you so much for your reply. It makes me angry sometimes that I am a woman.  My husband is overweight (although getting better) and it totally emotionally stable!!! ARGH.

I did start taking 5-HPT about 6 weeks ago and that has helped some. Enough where I quit taking my AD. But then I thought it was not doing much (the 5-HPT) so 3 weeks later I stopped taking it, and then things whent REALLY downhill. So I added it back and I'm definitely better. Still not near 100% though. I would say I am more like 50%.

I feel like I'm going to get fired from my job if I don't do something.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-03T00:58:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-out-here-i-am-a-miserable-mess-today.614/post-10541>

One thing I know about depression is that it is an inflammatory condition. Years ago I went to a talk by a biochemist who began by asking, "what do prozac and St John's Wort have in common?" They are both anti-inflammatories. If you are doing CT and making changes, you are likely in the process of major detox. I know I am - it began from the start, but after about a month of CT it is really ramping up. It is both emotional and physical detoxing. I would up your anti-inflammatory protocol (I take at least a teaspoon of tumeric every day, eat 100% cocoa for the resveratrol, for example), eat lots of fish, take a krill oil supplement (I believe Dr K recommends 500g 3x per day), get some B6/B12 on board to clear toxins and maybe some DIM too.

Also, realise that you are healing/reversing a long-standing pattern of disease. This means (at least for my n of 1) that the overall pattern will move toward health while the day-to-day can be VERY up and down. For example, during a big detox day I'll feel like shit and have very low energy. I remember Dr K commenting before that he took lots of naps early on. Nurture yourself. You may have to 'fake it' at work with colleagues but you don't have to fake it with yourself.

Also, make sure you are eating enough fat. Lots of CO and ghee and animal fat. Fat seems to be the most nourishing thing for our adrenals (stress) and helps a lot with mood and hunger too.

Hang in there. (You also have permission to throw away your scale. I did - great move!)

Are you supplementing with vitamin D3 at all? I won't bore you with my whole life story, but suffice it to say that I'm about your age, and I spent five years on and off Wellbutrin, Effexor, Lexapro, plus some Valium thrown in for good measure when I was having panic attacks. I finally got weaned off of everything, including the Wellbutrin, and started taking a high dose of Omega-3's along with vitamin D3 (I take 8000mg a day), and within about 6 weeks I started to feel like a whole new person. It hasn't fixed every mood swing or PMS meltdown, by any means...but it's made a HUGE difference.

Good luck!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-05T20:00:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-out-here-i-am-a-miserable-mess-today.614/post-11807>

>

I have symptoms of hyperthyroidism and marched into my dr. wanting to be tested - I just KNEW that was my problem. All of the tests came back "fine" though (she did do the T3 and T4). I don't have the numbers with me at the moment.**

**

Hi differentstory,

My heart goes out to you. I can feel your suffering. I tend to by a very happy person but after the birth of my second child I learned about postpartum depression. I found a few things helped me:

1\. Everyday, I would go for very long, slow walks with my daughter in a backpack.

2\. I found a support group and talked about my struggles.

3\. I read a little red book called Awakening Lovingkindness** by Pera Chodron. Every time I started to feel down I would read some of it:

[http://www.amazon.com/Awakening-Loving-Kindness-Shambhala-Pocket-Classics/dp/1570622590/ref=sr\_1\_15?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1333644015&sr=1-15](http://http://www.amazon.com/Awakening-Loving-Kindness-Shambhala-Pocket-Classics/dp/1570622590/ref=sr_1_15?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1333644015&sr=1-15)

4\. On my long walks I would wish "lovingkindness" to all the people I passed on my walk. I found this practice changed my world.

If you are already eating a strict paleo diet I would suggest you try to watch how much sweet foods you are eating. This may be the root to your problem. Eat a lot more saturated fats. You may be feeding "bad" gut bacteria that is producing endotoxins that are being reabsorbed by your bowel and adding to your neurological problems:

[http://www.eatkamloops.org/what-is-a-healthy-gut/](http://http://www.eatkamloops.org/what-is-a-healthy-gut/)

My husband used GABA when he came off his anti-depression medication a number of years ago. Now when my husband had a bout of blues/anxiety, he takes large doses of fermented cod liver oil and his mood improves within hours. You could look at the book **The Mood Cure** by Julia Ross. That is where I learned about GABA which helped get my husband off depression medication:

[http://www.amazon.com/The-Mood-Cure-Program-Emotions--Today/dp/0142003646/ref=sr\_1\_1?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1333644677&sr=1-1](http://http://www.amazon.com/The-Mood-Cure-Program-Emotions--Today/dp/0142003646/ref=sr_1_1?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1333644677&sr=1-1)

I hope that helps some. Do find someone to talk with. Find a support group if you are concerned about wearying family members.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Make ahead dinner ideas!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-04-03T00:30:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-10524>

Those of you with young children know that sometimes the days just get crazy, and at least for me sometimes I end up having to scramble around to figure out dinner. I used to do make ahead freezer dinners, but I've found that hard to do having my whole family on a low carb diet! Gone are the pasta dishes and casseroles. So... any ideas for make ahead dinners? Something easy to pull out of a freezer when you don't have time to mess with anything? Or maybe your fave crock pot dishes that you can throw in first thing in the morning and not think about it till dinner? Let's share recipes!

### Casey — 2012-04-03T02:24:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-10579>

Great thread, I need ideas too! Quiche/egg pies etc can be frozen and put straight in the oven when needed. Doesn't help me though because we can't eat eggs! Soup is pretty much the only meal I freeze ahead of time. I make bolar roast in my slow cooker/crockpot, no recipe but usually put in broth, splash of wine and herbs.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-03T21:54:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-10898>

I think this looks like a great recipe! Make a huge pot and freeze some for another meal!

[http://www.mommypotamus.com/ultimate-beef-liver-chili/](http://http://www.mommypotamus.com/ultimate-beef-liver-chili/)

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-04T00:27:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-10995>

Sooo much can be done with a CrockPot. You can generally prepare the pot the night before (but be careful about mixing veggies with raw meat ahead of time), and then just turn it on in the morning.

Or you could cook just meat in a broth in the CrockPot, and prepare veggies at night.

### thyme — 2012-04-04T14:14:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-11212>

I double or triple every recipe and freeze it. I've got 4-person servings of cooked beef rendang, oxtail stew, halibut curry, and beef heart kebabs in the freezer now...no microwave, but no young children, either, so I can usually remember to stick the frozen dinners in the fridge the night before and then stick them in the oven come night to eat.

### vlynnb — 2012-04-05T05:00:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-11598>

thyme, Are those your own recipes or out of Dr. K's book? I haven't purchased the book yet as I am waiting for a new computer and this one is tempermental if I add anything new. But, if they are your recipes can you share?

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-04-05T06:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-11633>

Here's some make ahead items that I do for the kids (and for hubby's lunches)

1\. quiche with bacon crust - I line mini quiche pan with either bacon or proscuitto and then add the quiche mix and bake. These can be frozen until you want to reheat

2\. Some cooked ribs (I pressure cook) and then just season and throw in the oven to reheat and broil to crisp up. I've had luck sticking the frozen (but pre-pressure cooked) in the oven. We love all types of "meat on a stick" around here... well I take that back ... no corndogs.

3\. One of my favorite foods is rouladen... it's a german food my grandma taught me. It's a slice of beef with bacon, onions, black pepper, grainy mustard on top and then rolled up and secured with tooth picks. I brown it in rendered pork fat and then pressure cook. Some people put pickle inside but I prefer to have a home fermented pickle on the side so as to not kill all the cultures. Kids think it's cool because its a roll. They're good reheated or the rolls can be made ahead of time.

4\. Roast beef slices..... the kids love rolling stuff in them and dipping them, etc.

5\. homemade sausage is a big hit around here - easy to have cooked and then reheat. ditto with meatballs. And these are great ways to hide the liver and such

6\. daughter goes wild over pulled pork.... i buy smoked pork picnic shoulder and throw it in the crockpot until it falls apart. it's great reheated too.

### Mud Flinger — 2012-04-11T05:59:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-13636>

I've made some fun meat balls with hard boiled eggs in the middle. Basically mix any ground meat with spices, etc that you like, make a patty and wrap it around a peeled hard boiled egg and bake. I called them dinosaur eggs - my 4 yr old son called them dragon eggs.

### thyme — 2012-04-11T19:08:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-13796>

vlynnb, they're my recipes and I'm happy to share--let me type them up over the weekend.

### thyme — 2012-04-11T19:22:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-13806>

[Beef heart kebabs, or Anticuchos](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?852-Anticuchos-peruvian-beef-heart-kebabs-%28-meat-on-a-stick-for-kids%29-has-nightshades&p=13873#post13873). I usually just cook the whole heart and freeze the already-cooked second dinner worth and the sauce.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-11T21:19:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-13854>

would love your recipes too!

### gemstar — 2012-04-12T06:41:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-14100>

You can freeze just about anything, especially proteins and reheat. I often reheat on the stove burner, adding a drop of boiling water, keeping the gas flame very low. I usually try to make leftovers to freeze, so that I always have something ready to eat. I do not use the microwave. I also always freeze in portions. BTW, BBQ'd meat is also great frozen and reheated. People do not know the difference from fresh grilled meats!!!

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-12T17:05:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/make-ahead-dinner-ideas.615/post-14223>

the crock pot saves us on the nights I have class, or we'll make a big roast - and eat it on those nights. Easter Sunday dinner of a 6lb leg of lamb - covered dinner for 3 nights this week... and there may be enough for my HAB tomorrow morning...

# Thread: Labs Please Help ... Hack Away!

**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 5

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-03T00:52:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-please-help-hack-away.617/post-10536>

Just got the BFs labs back and need a little help. Can anyone confirm if this is too much testosterone? All the others sex steroid hormones look low ..... so where is that high testosterone coming from?

Anyone can hack away on anything on these labs. All comments and contributions are welcome. Quelson, JanSz I need help as I don't understand the "man thing" as well as you guys do.

46 yr old male

No meds

6'1"

160#

SAD diet most of his life. Completely organic for last 10 yrs. Grass-fed meat, gluten free for a year. Grain free since Jan 1. Leptin Rx for 2 months due to high rT3 (low ratio).

Cholesterol 260 (100-199)

Triglycerides 41 (0-149)

HDL 90 (>39)

VLDL Calc 8 (5-40)

LDL Calc 162 (0-99)

These ranges are from LabCorp

Omega 06:03 ratio is 2.1:1

Pregnenolone 113 (180-200)

DHEAs 230 (500-640)

Progesterone apparently wasn't measured

Testosterone 848 (650-827)

Testosterone, Free 14 (6.8-21.5)

Estradiol, sensitive 15 (20) \*really bad

Prior to these numbers his thyroid borders on hyper. Graves disease runs in his family. Son has Graves.

CRPhs .33 (.0-3.0)

IGF-1 157 (94-252)

PSA .9 (3-70)

Iron, serum 106 (40-155)

Iron Saturation 46% (15-55)

TBIC 229 (250-450)

Ferritin 283 (30-400)

HA1c 5.4 (4.8-5.6)

Fasting Insulin 6.2 (2.6-24.9)

2 Hour postprandial Insulin 5.8 (2.6-24.9)

LH 7.2 (1.7-8.6)

FSH 5.8 (1.5-12.4)

GGT 14 (0-65)

Thank you!!!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-03T00:53:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-please-help-hack-away.617/post-10537>

I had this up over the weekend and got only a couple of responses so wanted to try again in the new lab sub-forum. I need some male lab experts to weight in.

### Enkidu — 2012-04-03T02:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-please-help-hack-away.617/post-10592>

Wow, that's an awesome Testosterone level. I'm not exactly an expert, but my understanding is that 250-850 is healthy range, but I defer to pretty much anyone else who chimes in.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-03T17:43:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-please-help-hack-away.617/post-10788>

Yeah, I thought that was a good testosterone but can't figure out where it is coming from since all the other hormones are low. Maybe he is just an efficient converter?!?!?

### mambo — 2012-04-29T23:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-please-help-hack-away.617/post-19936>

# Thread: Cold adapted people and gut bacteria...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapted-people-and-gut-bacteria.619/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### freesia — 2012-04-03T01:14:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapted-people-and-gut-bacteria.619/post-10548>

Is there any sense from you guys that becoming cold adapted is shifting the make-up of your gut bacteria without other intervention (probiotics)? Are you getting better absorption? Immunomodulatory effects?

I can't help feeling out gut bacteria must have been more optimal along with our good selves way back when....  Better at making B vitamins and goodness knows what other V. important things...

Thanks for being here!

Freesia.

\*I'm only three days into Leptin Reset (loving it) and CT is still but a twinkle in my eye (lots of reading to do yet) but I can't waut to get started.

### KA24 — 2012-04-25T21:53:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapted-people-and-gut-bacteria.619/post-18816>

I can't give you a direct answer, but anecdotal evidence from my CT has made me consider this same thought.

Prior to CT, I felt great about my gut. Digestion was good, I've dialed into pretty much anything that gives me issues. I'm in good shape, and only started CT to really see what impact it might have since everything else was dialed in.

Since starting, I've noticed even better bowel movements and what I can feel, improved digestion. I was using digestive enzymes before and have tapered off substantially since CT.

For about a 3-4 day period about 1-2 weeks in, I had an inconsistent, but nagging stomach-ache during the day. It subsided after only a few days, but this is very uncommon and hasn't ever happened before. The only thing I can think of was the CT had something to do with it as it was the only thing I really changed.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-04-25T22:10:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapted-people-and-gut-bacteria.619/post-18825>

I can now drink coffee without any after effects. That never happened before.

# Thread: Straddling both worlds
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/straddling-both-worlds.620/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Birdy — 2012-04-03T01:17:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/straddling-both-worlds.620/post-10549>

This morning I was freezing and shivering outside, but by the afternoon, I was in a cute pool skirt getting my vitamin D. Will I crack from the temperature change? Should I be eating keto in the morning and carbs in the afternoon? How and when do I transition?

### Kiera2 — 2012-04-03T01:45:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/straddling-both-worlds.620/post-10562>

> **Birdy;10618 wrote:** This morning I was freezing and shivering outside, but by the afternoon, I was in a cute pool skirt getting my vitamin D. Will I crack from the temperature change? Should I be eating keto in the morning and carbs in the afternoon? How and when do I transition?

Eating is seasonal, not circadian. Any transition would be through spring and autumn, if you're healthy. Otherwise, stay keto.

# Thread: I can stop the shivering
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-04-03T02:37:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10584>

So last nite when asked by my SO if I could stop shivering I realized that I could... I simply relaxed and I stopped shivering and would feel comfortable and warm. I know exactly what it feels like to do "it" and can do it now while typing this. However I can't maintain it for more than 10 seconds or so... maybe a limitation of attention span? Perhaps it needs some conditioning? This is preliminary stuff for me, things may change, but for now it is associated with a soft breath hold. When I breathe again I will start shivering, inhale or exhale...

I will keep playing with it. Along the lines of another thread I glimpsed there may be value in inducing non-shivering thermogenesis, but for all we know shivering triggers a faster adaptation.

### Birdy — 2012-04-03T02:57:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10590>

I find that I shiver at the start of CT, but then it usually settles down.Hmmmm, maybe I am just settling down.

### hellojtm — 2012-04-03T03:00:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10593>

For me the shivering begins after 5-10 minutes.

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-03T03:55:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10632>

It takes me 45 min - 1 hr (depending on water temp). The shivering = not cold adapted yet. Hang in there it gets easier. Each minute you go longer = lengthing of telomere & disease reversal

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-03T03:59:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10635>

I find exactly what you do, hellojtm, in terms of being able to temporarily stop shivering with breathing. Re: shivering meaning not being cold adapted, I remember Dr K saying to someone that he still shivers sometimes.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-03T04:01:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10640>

> **Shijin13;10701 wrote:** It takes me 45 min - 1 hr (depending on water temp). The shivering = not cold adapted yet. Hang in there it gets easier. Each minute you go longer = lengthing of telomere & disease reversal

I thought Dr. Kruse had said somewhere that he still shivers at times and I know he's cold adapted!

I don't start shivering until after I get OUT of the water.

### texasmom4 — 2012-04-03T04:04:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10641>

> **Trisha;10709 wrote:** I thought Dr. Kruse had said somewhere that he still shivers at times and I know he's cold adapted!
>
>
>
> I don't start shivering until after I get OUT of the water.

Me, too. No shivering in the tub. Shivering starts maybe 5-10 minutes after I'm out. What does that say about a person's cold adaptation status?

### hellojtm — 2012-04-03T04:19:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10651>

Durations, body part, and energy reserves may come into play here, along with adaptation. For what it's worth I start shivering lightly at between 5-10 minutes if my torso is in, and over 10 minutes if I'm doing legs. I skipped the ice pack on the body phase entirely due to logistics and went from face dunks and cold showers into baths. I am fairly lean ~11% body fat and have been eating a VLC high omega-3 medium calorie plenty of intermittent fasting diet. About once a week I do a set of exercises that depletes muscle glycogen. I've lost 3lbs this month due to these changes (lower carb and CT). Everything else was the same. I could try getting out when I start shivering and getting back in, but I'm afraid the water will warm up, it's been pushing 53f the last couple times and the air temp is working against me now.

jesse

### ste colombe — 2012-04-03T04:34:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10659>

> **texasmom4;10710 wrote:** Me, too. No shivering in the tub. Shivering starts maybe 5-10 minutes after I'm out. What does that say about a person's cold adaptation status?

This is me too. I then just lie naked on my bed and concentrate, relax and breath and it gets better, but you're right hellojtm it is so much mind over matter. I dont shiver in the bath, I "internally" shiver if that makes any sense....metabolism cranking up...i hope...

### shilohman — 2012-04-03T05:42:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10673>

Shivering is related to how much BAT we have. The more BAT the less we shiver. Since the vast majority of our fat is WAT, we will always shiver. Eating bitter melon is what helps form BAT. I can't remember if WAT coverts to BAT or if New BAT is formed. I need to go back and reread about BAT an Irisin.

### LinD — 2012-04-03T05:47:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10675>

> **texasmom4;10710 wrote:** Me, too. No shivering in the tub. Shivering starts maybe 5-10 minutes after I'm out. What does that say about a person's cold adaptation status?

Same here, but not always, meaning I don't always shiver after the bath. We'll see how it goes this week. Unfortunately, it's getting harder to get the water temp down as the water is getting warmer here in Texas.

### shilohman — 2012-04-03T06:07:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10682>

> **LinD;10744 wrote:** Same here, but not always, meaning I don't always shiver after the bath. We'll see how it goes this week. Unfortunately, it's getting harder to get the water temp down as the water is getting warmer here in Texas.

it is that the more BAT you have then the colder the water can be before you start to shiver.

# Thread: Do you feel hungry for breakfast?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### agatha — 2012-04-03T18:56:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/post-10819>

This morning I woke up and was HUNGRY!!! This has never happened to me before, even as a child. Sluggish, cold and mildly nauseous best describes my usual morning feelings. Does this mean my cortisol rhythm is beginning to normalise? Does everyone else feel hungry when they wake?

### Birdy — 2012-04-03T19:01:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/post-10825>

For me, it usually depends on what I ate the night before. I think that since I have added CT after the leptin reset, I am not as hungry in the morning.

### fitness@home — 2012-04-03T19:54:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/post-10844>

If I wake up at my normal time of 4 AM (weekdays), I am not hungry but do make sure I eat a BAB within 30 minutes per the Leptin Reset protocol.

Oddly enough, if I am sleeping poorly and wake up between 1-3 AM my appetite is off the charts. No problem getting a good breakfast in then

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-04-03T20:00:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/post-10852>

It comes and it goes. Some mornings I am ravenous - other mornings not so much.

I'm chalking it up to detox from estrogen stored in the adipose tissue.

Prior to doing CT, I had been IF for 3+ years - and was never hungry in the morning.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-03T22:28:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/post-10916>

Some mornings, yes, most mornings, no.

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-04T00:24:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/post-10993>

Most mornings, yes. I'm also routinely skipping dinner because I get home most nights after 7 and I'm generally not hungry.

But last night I ended up quite hungry and ate a large dinner. In that case, this morning I was not hungry at ALL. I munched a bit, but just couldn't get down my normal BAB.

### .lina — 2012-04-03T19:23:19+0300

### admin — 2012-04-03T20:39:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-pictures.630/post-10871>

Inside the text editor you'll see a little icon that looks like a frame with a tree inside. Click on that. The popup will default to select an image from your computer or from a URL. Please note here if you have any issues. Thanks!

### quelsen — 2012-04-03T22:04:20+0300

### .lina — 2012-04-03T22:28:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-pictures.630/post-10915>

ah you are _attaching_ an image, whereas when you post pics that show up immediately,

you take the image's URL and put and \[/IMG\*\] tags around them (without the\*), like this:

\[img\]http://forum.jackkruse.com/attachment.php?attachmentid=87&d=1333476384\[/IMG\*\]

\[img\]http://forum.jackkruse.com/attachment.php?attachmentid=87&d=1333476384

this can only be done if it's already uploaded somewhere though  i guess you can first attach it,

copy the URL and then edit the post to insert it in IMG tags, a little more work though ;D

# Thread: CT w/ chlorine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-w-chlorine.637/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-04-04T01:06:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-w-chlorine.637/post-11015>

I usually do my CT at home where I have well water, but this week I'm staying with a friend to help her while she recovers from surgery . . . trying to get her on board with CT too! Anyway, she has town water and I can actually smell the chlorine in it though DH calls me a "hound dog" as I am super sensitive to smells, so take that for what it's worth. Is it a good idea to CT with the chlorine? I try so hard to stay away from all chemicals. I just don't want to do anything to overload my system while I'm already detoxing.

I did do a bath last night because I already filled the tub before I smelled the chlorine and I woke up this morning with a sore throat . . . coincidence?? So, should I continue or hold off until I'm back home on Friday?

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-04T01:58:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-w-chlorine.637/post-11030>

I hope it's alright. I've been doing CT in my chlorinated pool. I don't know why it wouldn't be alright considering it gets me cold!

### Adria — 2012-04-04T15:17:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-w-chlorine.637/post-11230>

I'd wait until you're back home, better to avoid chemicals whenever possible.

### chocolate — 2012-04-04T17:39:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-w-chlorine.637/post-11286>

I think the doctor's pool looks chlorinated.

### shilohman — 2012-04-04T20:49:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-w-chlorine.637/post-11398>

I remember reading that your body will absorb through the skin only those minerals that it can use. So, go for it.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T11:51:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-w-chlorine.637/post-11656>

I wouldn't like it. Chlorine displaces iodine in our bodies.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-05T19:09:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-w-chlorine.637/post-11792>

All that is needed is a good filter. I have horrible water and have a whole house filter right now. In my old house I just had a good shower filter. I personally don't like chlorine or fluoride in my water. Both are bad for thyroids.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-05T20:25:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-w-chlorine.637/post-11815>

Patty Cakes, are you able to remove fluoride from your water?

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T20:54:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-w-chlorine.637/post-11828>

My research shows that fluoride can only be effectively removed with distillation or a proper reverse osmosis setup. Chlorine with activated carbon filters and with the shower "KDF" filters (but not chloramine which many utilities use now (or a combination of both)).

Bottled water has it all removed if you don't want to invest in a solid block actived carbon filter plus reverse osmosis system that removes all useful minerals...

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-05T21:08:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-w-chlorine.637/post-11835>

FWIW, I can't take a good hot bath anymore b/c the chlorine smell makes me lightheaded. And I can't swim at the Y (mod temp pool) for the same reason - I can hardly walk in the building! Even swimming laps in my neighborhood pool for longer than 15m will leave me with a headache growing from about 15m to about 1.5hrs after stopping. (heavy exercise in lake or ocean do not do this)

But I can easily take long cold baths in my same chlorinated water at home, no problems with chlorine head.

# Thread: Paddling Pool CTers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paddling-pool-cters.639/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Janet NZ — 2012-04-04T02:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paddling-pool-cters.639/post-11035>

OK, I've set up a child's paddling pool in our garage - filled the thing with water and put salt in it in an effort to keep it from going manky - because it takes an awful lot of water to get it waist-deep... and it takes FOREVER to empty and refill it.

But, after 2 weeks... murky things are starting to fuzz the bottom.... (the pool's bottom NOT mine!)

I know I'm not the only one using such a pool, so how are you keeping yours from growing manky murky gunk?

Thanks

### Janet NZ — 2012-04-04T04:01:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paddling-pool-cters.639/post-11088>

Haha! Funny, but I really do need help here please

### Cowboy — 2012-04-04T04:09:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paddling-pool-cters.639/post-11091>

there are small pool filters available for under $100.00. Also consider some type of bromine or chlorine tablets for the water.

### Cowboy — 2012-04-04T04:10:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paddling-pool-cters.639/post-11092>

Oh I see you are in NZ. They are available stateside on EBAY.

### Janet NZ — 2012-04-04T04:16:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paddling-pool-cters.639/post-11095>

Thanks Cowboy - I'll check and see what I can find here XO

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-04T07:51:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paddling-pool-cters.639/post-11177>

Janet, my guess is the paddling pool is just going to need regular clean-outs. But as it gets colder the drop in temp should make it so that the bacteria growth stops. Maybe next time scrub it with some white vinegar before you fill it. Btw, no bathtubs in Palmy?

### Janet NZ — 2012-04-04T08:23:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paddling-pool-cters.639/post-11185>

Not in this house Lauren - we had them removed because I have always hated soaking in hot water... now I'm banging my head against the wall!!! Lol! I really thought adding the salt would make the water bug-free...

# Thread: Swollen, painful toes WITHOUT CT??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-04-04T03:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-11067>

Since I've been increasing my CT lately and doing about 5-6 baths a week I'm finding my toes are swollen and painful all the time. Strange thing is that I don't even put my toes in the water at all. I keep them out and even have them covered with a warm pair of socks. It's happening to toes on both feet and they are super swollen to the point that wearing shoes is very uncomfortable as is walking and putting any pressure on them.

Any ideas what this is or what would be causing it?

### shilohman — 2012-04-04T03:48:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-11084>

> **BJK77;11136 wrote:** Since I've been increasing my CT lately and doing about 5-6 baths a week I'm finding my toes are swollen and painful all the time. Strange thing is that I don't even put my toes in the water at all. I keep them out and even have them covered with a warm pair of socks. It's happening to toes on both feet and they are super swollen to the point that wearing shoes is very uncomfortable as is walking and putting any pressure on them.
>
>
>
> Any ideas what this is or what would be causing it?

BJK77, Don't have a clue, but it could be results from detox from the CT. Swelling indicates inflammation which points to excess omega 6. Have you had your omega 6/3 tested? If not get tested. Do you take krill oil?

### Adriana — 2012-04-05T17:21:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-11737>

Is there a chance you might have gout?

### Aili — 2012-04-05T17:27:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-11741>

> **Adriana;11806 wrote:** Is there a chance you might have gout?

This seems a definite possibility.

### BJK77 — 2012-04-11T03:06:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-13540>

I never really considered gout. I don't know! I just read an article linking gout to high consumption of red meat and shellfish though, so it has me wondering since my diet is pretty much 100% red meant and shellfish! I wonder if Dr. K would have any insight on this.

### Adriana — 2012-04-11T03:40:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-13567>

> **BJK77;13609 wrote:** I never really considered gout. I don't know! I just read an article linking gout to high consumption of red meat and shellfish though, so it has me wondering since my diet is pretty much 100% red meant and shellfish! I wonder if Dr. K would have any insight on this.

You need to go to a doctor and have lab work done to get a definitive diagnosis. Not something a doctor can do over the internet.

### shilohman — 2012-04-11T03:49:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-13575>

I use to have gout in my big toe, very painful. You need to get your omega 6/3 test done or like Adriana said, see a doctor.

### jenn — 2012-04-13T21:58:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-14831>

I've been experiencing a similar type of thing -- my small toes get red, swollen, and shiny on top, and the bottoms feel almost like they have blisters. Definitely not gout, which tends to be in a joint and is excruciating (DH has had it in the past). I've been mostly keeping my feet out of the water too, and encased in socks. I also take ~ 1500mg krill oil and eat fish daily, so I don't think I have excess Omega 6 (though I've got quite a macadamia habit, so who knows?) It could be some weird transitional stage, as my feet and hands will typically get whitish in a Raynaud-type way after my daily baths, and are almost never immersed in the water. If I figure it out, I'll let you know!

### BJK77 — 2012-04-14T03:19:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-14968>

> **Jenn;14900 wrote:** I've been experiencing a similar type of thing -- my small toes get red, swollen, and shiny on top, and the bottoms feel almost like they have blisters. Definitely not gout, which tends to be in a joint and is excruciating (DH has had it in the past). I've been mostly keeping my feet out of the water too, and encased in socks. I also take ~ 1500mg krill oil and eat fish daily, so I don't think I have excess Omega 6 (though I've got quite a macadamia habit, so who knows?) It could be some weird transitional stage, as my feet and hands will typically get whitish in a Raynaud-type way after my daily baths, and are almost never immersed in the water. If I figure it out, I'll let you know!

Yes, definitely keep me posted on anything you find out. From what I've read, gout is excruciating as you said. This can be painful, but not unbearable so I assume it's not gout. I just ordered another omega test so it will be interesting to see what it shows. I do eat seafood at least once a day and have just started krill oil too, but I may still be paying for past transgressions! I have read others have had similar symptoms though and they have low O6:O3, so I'm not sure what to think.

### jenn — 2012-04-14T03:20:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-14970>

I've done some Googling of the symptoms I just described and have come to the reluctant conclusion that I have chilblains! My symptoms match the descriptions and photos I've seen pretty exactly, although they're a bit milder in presentation. Not sure what to do now, as all the recommended treatments I've read say to avoid cold and bundle up...pretty much the opposite of what I've been doing....

Check out chilblains online and see if it matches what you're experiencing.

### shilohman — 2012-04-14T03:24:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-14973>

I forgot to mention this in my previous post. When I started drinking 70-80 oz of water my gout never came back. How hydrated are you?

### Greg rx — 2012-05-25T07:17:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-27278>

Sounds like "the gout" except it is not usually bilateral. I have isolated my trigger to processed meats but I have had bacon almost every day since starting the reset 3 months ago. Usually I get little warnings so I just increase fluid intake and have had great success, but not this week. Went boating on Saturday, had great ct session in Lake St.Claire, lots of water to drink, way too much sun. Tuesday I thought I had stubbed my toe and Wednesday I could barely walk.

Maybe it was the"on sale" bacon or unusual amount of poultry, the point is that gout is a mystery even to most doctors. The good news is that just a short time of ice SEEMS to be clearing it up.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-04T04:32:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-11101>

It's gotten harder.  You can but it at [www.reliablerxpharmacy.com](http://http://www.reliablerxpharmacy.com) and [www.escrorefills.com](http://http://www.escrorefills.com). With escrow you need a code and you can use 150095. Any of the hcg brands are fine. You would need 2 5000 iu vials for a long round or 4 2000 iu vials (which will allow you to mix more to keep it fresher) and you'll need insulin needles, mixing vials, a mixing needle and bacteriostatic water. You can get the bac water at Amazon and the other things at [www.gpzservices.com](http://http://www.gpzservices.com).

### colleencoble — 2012-04-12T19:10:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-14283>

### colleencoble — 2012-04-12T19:44:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-14297>

I've seen hucog and Corion. Hucog is the smallest of the two but will still hold a total of 4cc--just barely. Corion is a bit larger. I haven't seen the other brands though. Definitely needs to be the 2000 iu though. Mixing only 4 cc of fluid with 5000 iu would be super concentrated so I wouldn't do it that way. I don't like the concentrated doses and neither did Dr. Simeon.  I actually usually mix mine with 8 cc in 2000 iu but most do the lesser water. I do 8 cc because that's the way Simeon mixed it.

### colleencoble — 2012-06-05T15:46:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-29526>

Is the vacation just with your family? If so, it's totally doable to take your food along and do it. The good thing about pushing through anyway is that it teaches you how to resist the pressure to eat things you shouldn't. The temptations are always going to be there--peer pressure, parties, good times that seem to always be associated with food--and you have to learn that food is fuel only. This is as good a time as any to learn that.  Without learning that, ultimate success doesn't come. It's too easy to fall back into emotional eating until you break the back of that monster.

Okay, lecture over.  This is just something I see over and over again. We get in social situations and don't know how to deal with it. I was proud of one gal over on the hcg forum who went to a bridal shower over the weekend and stayed strong. Doing things like that really develops your character. LOL

### colleencoble — 2012-06-05T15:47:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-29528>

Oh and if you skip a day a week, you should have enough hcg for about 2 40 days rounds. 3 vials will go 36 days plus skipping a day a week gives you another 4 days so 40 days.

No kidding, This last year has been a training round for this type of thing. I have to watch out for when I'm alone more than with others. Interestingly enough, the social stuff doesn't bother me nearly as much at the other people who can't stand it when I'm not participating. I've always been a nibbler in public and drive through in private so that part doesn't bug me. It's the practicality of finding the no-fat protien while on the road that is my worry.

So I think I'll just start when I get it, and let the chips fall where they may. Plan Plan Plan. The nice thing is I won't be spending NEARLY as much money on fancy restaurants so I can get a steak and salad/veg and not eating the rest. Upon reflection, it will probably pay for itself on that alone.

In reality, it's not eating much so how hard can it be? I'm thinking just get the veggies I can eat raw and bring grilled meats that I froze.

I can tell I'm not quite there on fixing the brain - as part of my excitement is having the free range "load days".

Next question for you guys  From what I can tell it's good to go into the RX + CT for P3? Is this what you are seeing?

You guys rock!!!

### Lyndra — 2013-01-01T22:29:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-68951>

Bank of America will let you create another credit card number to use for online shopping. You specify the maximum charge and the expiration date of the card. They call it ShopSafe. Discover has something similar. In my experience, it's good peace of mind but not a guarantee you won't have issues.

# Thread: interested Docs need bullet points I think
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interested-docs-need-bullet-points-i-think.646/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2012-04-04T10:51:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interested-docs-need-bullet-points-i-think.646/post-11193>

I have been thinking about this a lot lately. Those of us who have a hard time with all the science like to have bullet points so I think a busy Doc might appreciate their own bullet points prepared by Dr. Kruse. then they could go back for more info when ready. Over the last number of years I have been lucky enough to have friendships with some amazing, caring Docs - but they are so swamped. They adore their families and love their patients and contribute to their community but time is so limited. Just my 2 cents worth.

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-04T15:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interested-docs-need-bullet-points-i-think.646/post-11239>

Having lugged Dr K's work w/me to dr appointments - here's what I did... I picked the 1 blog I thought would be most valuable. when I went to the VA med ctr last august - that was the VAP; b/c I new my cholesterol would be high.

When you're working w/CW docs - you need to talk about things that can directly impact how they work today.

so lets say you've got high cholesterol - look at the VAP, and the Hormones blogs. Also consider bringing in some of Dr. Davis's blogs from track your plaque - he's a cardiologist - so why not include those things.

also make sure they understand you want a partner in your search for optimal health- find the things they have interest to lead them tow where Dr K is. some will say - don't trust everything you read on the internet - then ask them about the medical journals you've read etc on line - and their thoughts on the new findings etc.

Let them know you're trying to educate your self, you understand the recommendations they're making, and why,a s well as how it will help/hurt your condition/. Docs will be more open when you talk w/them about everything. granted you will run into docs who will tell you to just accept the way you are and you can't change anything... they are hopeless - and you should fire them.

If you're lucky you'll get a doc who's open, who also teaches young docs, and you'll have the opportunity to change not only your healthcare, but how your doctor not only treats you, but impact that future doc as well...

medical care isn't just about fixing whats wrong, but listening (both doctor and patient) its an exchange of ideas.... If you have to buy a copy of fathead or the perfect health diet and share that with them... ask them to watch the videos - so you can discuss the pros/cons of them at your next appointment...

### Tizawee — 2012-04-06T14:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interested-docs-need-bullet-points-i-think.646/post-12086>

I would like to see a page called "MD References" or something similar. It could contain points from Dr. Kruse specifically addressing the nuances of treating or operating on a cold-adapted human. I agree that we need to let them know we're looking for a partner, but it would be far easier to say "Go to jackkruse.com and look in the MD References section. If you still have questions after looking through the information from one doctor to another, let me know what they are, and I'll do some research."

I got this idea from [www.johnleemd.com](http://http://www.johnleemd.com). Dr. John Lee was the father of BHRT. He used progesterone cream in his clinical practice for 30 years, when everyone else thought he was nuts. He began writing books and one of his first was specifically directed at clinicians. So if I want to go to a doc who is unfamiliar with physiologic doses of progesterone to alleviate estrogen dominance symptoms, all I have to do is order the book or tell the doc to order it from the web site.

Just my two cents...

Elin

# Thread: Seasonal Recipes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-recipes.648/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2012-04-04T15:47:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-recipes.648/post-11237>

Summer is just around the corner. A thread with some of our favorite recipes that utilize seasonal produce would be great. This is my very favorite way to eat fresh zucchini from the garden:

**Zucchini Pancakes (Savory)**

1 Â½ C grated zucchini (drain between two paper towels)

Â¼ C coconut flour

Â¼ C Parmesan cheese

2 TBL chopped onion

2 TBL Mayo (real mayo, not Miracle Whip)

2 pastured eggs

Â¼ tsp oregano

Salt & Pepper to taste

Pastured butter or ghee for cooking (could also use coconut oil...will have to experiment!)

Combine ingredients. Let stand together in fridge a couple of hours or overnight. Cook in melted butter/ghee until brown. _Use no more than Â¼ C of mixture per pancake (experience has taught me that)._ These â€œpancakesâ€￾ smell and taste wonderful. Great side dish with meat & fish, but I also love them as part of a breakfast.

Enjoy!

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-11T21:31:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-recipes.648/post-13861>

sounds good. I'll omit the cheese though.

# Thread: I have a friend that had breast cancer, was on tamoxifin,
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-a-friend-that-had-breast-cancer-was-on-tamoxifin.649/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-04-04T17:01:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-a-friend-that-had-breast-cancer-was-on-tamoxifin.649/post-11265>

and was told it wasn't stopping the hormones. Now she needs a hysterectomy with no hormone replacement. I think she is misunderstanding or didn't have something explained to her. She has chronicled her entire journey on facebook. The tamoxifen made her gain tons and she wasn't small in the first place. I am so concerned that she hasn't been told that the liver is what controls SGHB, and that she has estrogen in all of that fat and will make it in her intestines and that cancer needs fructose for cell division. I sent her a note about the liver and SBHG, and that we were trying to prevent a repetition of under thirty breast cancer death in our gene pool and wished her the best. I feel like I'm watching a train wreck of uninformation. Its the worst.

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2007/11/071109171610.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2007/11/071109171610.htm)

I was looking at that blog with comments that was saying how chaotic this blog is, and honestly while it is chaotic, everything overlaps or goes with everything....the body is not such a straight line with all the mismatches. It seems like this article should have been shouted from the rooftop and passed out with pink ribbons.

### chocolate — 2012-04-08T17:00:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-a-friend-that-had-breast-cancer-was-on-tamoxifin.649/post-12662>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/pmc2756324/](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/pmc2756324/)

Metformin selectively targets breast cancer stem cells

[http://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=1&ved=0CDUQFjAA&url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.freerepublic.com%2Ffocus%2Ff-news%2F2354967%2Fposts&ei=OpaBT839GeGQiQKcpsG1Aw&usg=AFQjCNGMssvf1l88KJO1SfExXYPyp1S8UQ&sig2=ZDoyO4GXjFLhCXUVG9xaOg](http://http://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=1&ved=0CDUQFjAA&url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.freerepublic.com%2Ffocus%2Ff-news%2F2354967%2Fposts&ei=OpaBT839GeGQiQKcpsG1Aw&usg=AFQjCNGMssvf1l88KJO1SfExXYPyp1S8UQ&sig2=ZDoyO4GXjFLhCXUVG9xaOg) There is article mentioning that diabetic women on metformin get less breast cancer than those not. I would think that would carry over to pcos or any others with metabolic problems. I hope the off-label use isn't "off-label". If it prevents the manufacture of glucose and that is what feed the stems cells...help me out here....and fructose is what makes the cancer cells divide..... and grain/sugar/fructose/dairy is what stops shbg from being produced.....

I guess we need to figure out when the body makes the stuff on its own, and what the specific targets would be. We can stop eating the stuff. If any you are Dr Pepper/Snapple drinkers west of the Mississippi.... if it matter where they get their water, ask me. (Waste water plant adjacent is the hint.)

# Thread: HCG diet and trying to get pregnant?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### justducky — 2012-04-04T17:20:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-11274>

Hello all. I am new here. I have been reading (a little) about the HCG diet/protocol.

During my last pregnancy, I lost lots of weight. It was almost magical. I now wonder whether this was caused by the higher levels of HCG during the pregnancy.

To anyone's knowledge, does this diet affect one's ability to become pregnant?

Thank you,

Ducky

### colleencoble — 2012-04-04T17:41:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-11289>

Over the years we have seen women who had infertility issues become pregnant while on hcg or shortly after.

### justducky — 2012-04-04T18:43:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-11334>

Thank you. That almost seems counter-intuitive. I would think that elevated hcg levels would signal to the body that a pregnancy was already underway. Any idea how or why it works?

### colleencoble — 2012-04-04T19:04:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-11341>

I personally think some underlying hormonal issues are fixed and it allows the woman to finally get pregnant. We know for sure thyroid levels are elevated and normalized. And I always have to cut back on my progesterone and estrogen while on the protocol.

### Claudia — 2012-04-19T05:26:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-16481>

> **colleencoble;11410 wrote:** I personally think some underlying hormonal issues are fixed and it allows the woman to finally get pregnant. We know for sure thyroid levels are elevated and normalized. And I always have to cut back on my progesterone and estrogen while on the protocol.

Do you then add the progesterone and estrogen when you finish protocol and are in maintenance? Do you test first to see where you are? I need to test sex hormones but don't want to do it right after a round..not sure when it would be a good time after doing a round to test. I have been estrogen dominant in the past and used progesterone cream so was thinking I might do this again. I think hcg affected me and maybe made me more estrogen dominant than before hcg. Need to test though.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-19T16:50:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-16584>

No, I add them back in. I have no ovaries so I will always need to have them. And yes, it can make you more estrogen dominant for a short time, as can any diet when fat is released. Your fat cells store estrogen.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-19T17:33:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-16595>

August, that's what hcg DOES though. It protects the baby when the mom is too nauseated to eat by releasing the calories in that stored fat. So if you conceive on hcg, even MORE hcg will be coursing through your body and the baby is fine.  Now I wouldn't continue on the diet once you knew you were pregnant, but you're not harming the little one.

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-04-21T19:20:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-17470>

> **colleencoble;16654 wrote:** No, I add them back in. I have no ovaries so I will always need to have them. And yes, it can make you more estrogen dominant for a short time, as can any diet when fat is released. Your fat cells store estrogen.

interesting colleen! maybe that is why i'm experiencing a bit more bloating water retention since starting hcg? i'm peeing all the time -also on progesterone so that may be way - wondering if this might just be a transitory sympton as things balance out.

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-04-21T19:21:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-17471>

> **August;16660 wrote:** I have the impression that the HCG Protocol might increase the odds of conceiving . . I have nagging thoughts in the back of my mind though that if you are on a a VLCD or perhaps even soon after, it might not be the optimal time to conceive (epigenetics-wise - like for the baby) . . . I'm a worrier . .

VLCD are not bad for babies in fact you could argue that the avoiding grains sugars starches are more beneficial bc you are avoiding an insulin spiked roller coaster, inflammation of the gut (which releases allergens in the bloodstream) etc. i would not shy away from VLCD and pregnancy

edited to say: BUT i'm nowhere near ready for a baby yet! so my opinion is pretty much worthless in this arena, hehe

### colleencoble — 2012-04-21T19:25:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-17476>

Some of the weight lost the first week is water weight for most, but those are usually people who used to eat a lot of carbs. Progesterone can help you dump excess water though. But the bloating isn't usual. What have you been eating?

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-04-21T23:02:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-17538>

pretty much grilled chicken and fish with veggies to equal 500 calories (mostly spinach, but had some fermented saurekraut mixed in- only once though )...seasoned with S&P very simple...I remember you said simple is best. BUT i have heard some people react to progesterone differently....however I'm on a super small dose. my body tends to be sensitive to things though. i hope its transitory. i did lower the dose to .2 bc the .4 was making me super sleepy. what is the reasoning behind the morning dose suggestion of progesterone? would it still work for me if i took it at night (Thinking the sleepiness then would be a bonus!)

i updated dzugan so hoping they get back to me next week with some suggestions

### colleencoble — 2012-04-22T00:22:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-17560>

You're actually best to take progesterone 2x a day. The dose is pretty much gone after 12 hours.

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-04-22T18:34:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-diet-and-trying-to-get-pregnant.650/post-17772>

> **colleencoble;17631 wrote:** You're actually best to take progesterone 2x a day. The dose is pretty much gone after 12 hours.

hmmm interesting...perhaps i should do .2 in the am and .2 in the pm to equal the .4 recommendation..i'll be curious as to what they suggest. i think i'd rather have the topical gel so am anxious for it to get to me. next week...

### colleencoble — 2012-04-22T19:38:18+0300

# Thread: Daily torpor is associated with telomere length change over winter in Djungarian hams
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daily-torpor-is-associated-with-telomere-length-change-over-winter-in-djungarian-hams.651/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-04-04T18:29:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daily-torpor-is-associated-with-telomere-length-change-over-winter-in-djungarian-hams.651/post-11324>

[http://rsbl.royalsocietypublishing.org/content/8/2/304.full](http://http://rsbl.royalsocietypublishing.org/content/8/2/304.full)

### Dan in Utah — 2012-04-04T19:17:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daily-torpor-is-associated-with-telomere-length-change-over-winter-in-djungarian-hams.651/post-11354>

So, is daily torpor in the cold the new WOD?

### hellojtm — 2012-04-04T20:35:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daily-torpor-is-associated-with-telomere-length-change-over-winter-in-djungarian-hams.651/post-11390>

Some interesting notes: I added bold. and put my comments in _italics

RTL = relative telomere length

torpor = lethargy/inactivity (i had to look it up )

\- Twenty-five mature virgin female hamsters (aged: three to eight months) were switched from a long-day (16 L : 8 D) to a short-day photoperiod (8 L : 16 D, lights on: 07.00–15.00 h) and exposed to either a warm (20.0Â°C Â± 0.86Â°C s.d.) or cold **(9.2Â°C Â± 3.7Â°C) air temperature over 180 days. Food and water were provided ad libitum. (_not very cold air, I get this about 100 days out of the year in San Francisco, so dressing lightly is going to be significant)

\- **Initial RTL did not differ between the groups (RTL on day 0: 1.21 Â± 0.14; t = 0.34, p = 0.73). Over 180 days on a short-day photoperiod, the change in RTL differed significantly between the cold and warm groups (t = 3.17, p = 0.005). **Eleven out of 12 hamsters kept in the cold experienced an increase in their RTL (average change in RTL: 0.25 Â± 0.19), whereas RTL decreased or increased approximately equally among hamsters in the warm group** (average change: 0.03 Â± 0.14)

\- An effect of torpor was not associated with a reduction in metabolic energy expenditure: food intake was approximately 50 per cent greater in cold-exposed hamsters, regardless of torpor use**, yet neither group nor food intake was important in explaining the change in RTL _(death to cron theory?)_ (death to MFTHR and IGF-1 mTOR pathway theory?)_

\- Inferring from this set of models, the difference in body mass and initial RTL were the most important parameters (relative importance: 0.89 and 0.85, respectively) explaining the difference in RTL from day 0 to 180, whereas food intake was rather unimportant (relative importance value of 0.35). (I wasn't able to tell if it was starting, ending or changing BMI/TL**_**)

\- In this experiment, the use of daily torpor was associated with an increase in the length of telomeres in hamsters exposed to a short-day photoperiod. Our results support the hypothesis that, in addition to reducing energy expenditure, the use of torpor might also serve to retard somatic ageing during periods of unfavourable environmental conditions**.

jesse

### Jim — 2012-04-04T20:44:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daily-torpor-is-associated-with-telomere-length-change-over-winter-in-djungarian-hams.651/post-11395>

So Jesse, to run this test in humans I suppose we would need 25 mature virgin females too right?

### hellojtm — 2012-04-04T22:07:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daily-torpor-is-associated-with-telomere-length-change-over-winter-in-djungarian-hams.651/post-11432>

> **Jim;11464 wrote:** So Jesse, to run this test in humans I suppose we would need 25 mature virgin females too right?

whoops, did I forget to put bold on that part

# Thread: Leptin in the news
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-in-the-news.656/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Adria — 2012-04-04T20:34:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-in-the-news.656/post-11389>

On the news last night they spoke of obesity and how to measure it more accruately using leptin blood test:

[http://www.latimes.com/health/la-he-obesity-20120403,0,3807756.story](http://http://www.latimes.com/health/la-he-obesity-20120403,0,3807756.story)

**We may be fatter than we think, researchers report

A new study finds that the widely used body mass index, or BMI, may be understating obesity in many people.

The study also found that a measure of leptin, an appetite-boosting hormone secreted by the body's fat deposits, generally tracks with an individual's level of fatness as measured by DXA. Testing leptin levels may be a simpler, cheaper way to reliably distinguish people with healthy levels of body fat from those carrying too much, the researchers wrote.**

# Thread: Wearing socks
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pamela — 2012-04-05T04:37:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-11586>

So do you wear socks while in the water doing CT? If so what kind of socks and how does it help you?

Thanks.

### Destiny — 2012-04-05T13:43:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-11669>

> **Pamela;11655 wrote:** So do you wear socks while in the water doing CT? If so what kind of socks and how does it help you?
>
> Thanks.

I actually wear a hat. Strangely, it helps me with tolerating the cold on the rest of my body. I also try to keep my feet in there but not for the whole CT duration.

### agatha — 2012-04-05T13:47:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-11672>

Yes, I wear a very warm hat and neoprene gloves - they really help though I shall abandon them when i am better adapted. I take my feet out of the water as soon as I sit down and gently slide them in after the rest of the body has adjusted to the cold - it feels very nice to put them in the water by this stage.

### Evo Mama — 2012-04-05T14:25:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-11678>

Yes. I wear smart wool socks, and they help big time (though I've never tried a bath without them). I've never had any issues with my feet at all.

I also wear a hat and sometimes gloves, which also help.

\---

I am here: [http://tapatalk.com/map.php?jjgdic](http://http://tapatalk.com/map.php?jjgdic)

### TerrierMom — 2012-04-05T14:58:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-11682>

I think the idea behind socks and hat etc is what Dr K describes as vasoconstriction.. The natural reaction of your body to cold by shunting blood away from hands and feet and into the core, in response to cold. But we want all that blood near our skin. So we wear these things at first until our bodies are more adapted... Then we won't need them as our body will be used to cold and not do this vasoconstriction anymore in response to the temp we expose it to. (vaso=vascular=veins/circulatory system, and you know what constriction is)

He refers to this in one of the CT blog posts. I've tried 64\* F pool without, and I'm bringing my dive booties and gloves next time!!

### texasmom4 — 2012-04-05T15:58:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-11709>

> **Pamela;11655 wrote:** So do you wear socks while in the water doing CT? If so what kind of socks and how does it help you?
>
> Thanks.

When I first started CTing, my pool water was in the 50-55 range. I read a post by MamaGrok (I think?) that said she wore wool socks in the water and they seemed to trap heat in, even though they're wet. I tried it and it worked - no more painful feet in that cold water! Now, pool water is just under 80 so I'm in the tub every day. I keep my feet out of the water, and I wear a knit wool hat.

### Kathy — 2012-04-05T22:21:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-11868>

knit cap, wool socks, keep hands out of water

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-05T22:38:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-11880>

I wear 2 pairs of smart wool crew/ankle socks

### Lyndra — 2012-04-06T05:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-12053>

I started with neoprene booties and then after about 2 weeks, switched to wool socks. The first couple of times in socks, my toes got very cold, but now I am adapting and they seem to be fine. For some reason, though, I am totally not able to immerse my arms in the water - every time I try, I just shake like mad. Still more adapting to be done no doubt.

### Destiny — 2012-04-06T19:15:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-12186>

> **Lyndra;12122 wrote:** I started with neoprene booties and then after about 2 weeks, switched to wool socks. The first couple of times in socks, my toes got very cold, but now I am adapting and they seem to be fine. For some reason, though, I am totally not able to immerse my arms in the water - every time I try, I just shake like mad. Still more adapting to be done no doubt.

I immerse only my arms after I do face dunks in the sink in the morning (utilizing ice and water). Then, I do baths in the evening. Maybe try it if that works for you too.

### Pamela — 2012-04-06T21:09:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-12223>

I found once I lean back my feet kind of float up anyway so my toes stick out of the water and by doing this my feet were not frozen after getting out of the tub.

### cantweight — 2012-04-06T21:37:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-12234>

I am donning a shirt tied up around my chest daisy duke style..lol

No prob getting the feet in. Its my arms that are practically in the air for a half hour!

### Martha Ray — 2022-10-21T01:45:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-socks.667/post-316812>

> [Lyndra said:](/goto/post?id=12053)
>
> I started with neoprene booties and then after about 2 weeks, switched to wool socks. The first couple of times in socks, my toes got very cold, but now I am adapting and they seem to be fine. For some reason, though, I am totally not able to immerse my arms in the water - every time I try, I just shake like mad. Still more adapting to be done no doubt.

I do not use socks or a hat but think I will try both of those. My arms are cold but I purposely shake them in the water the movement makes them even colder and them when I stop the movement and keep them still they seem to be happy. If they get to feeling too cold I move them again and again keep them still and they are content for a while longer. I have very thin skin on my forearms but it is the upper arms that are the worst.

# Thread: Share your success of today
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoSue — 2012-04-05T05:06:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/post-11600>

We know that major change is really a series of small steps that add up, so this thread is a place to highlight your success \*today\*. Did you resist temptation? Did you cook a new recipe or try a new food? Have you made other progress? Tell us about it!

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-05T05:09:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/post-11601>

My success of the day is that I cooked broccoli and free-range bone-in split chicken breast (from a local farm), wrapped in bacon, to my 2 and 3 year old niece and nephew. I have rarely to never witnessed my nephew eating real meat. He chowed it down, not only the bacon but also the chicken. My sis-in-law said, "I have never seen them eat meat like that."

This from the kids who won't eat anything other than chicken nuggets or mac and cheese. I say, feed 'em bacon. And real chicken. ha ha.

### Glamorama — 2012-04-05T07:35:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/post-11638>

I went to the gym this morning, and once again I was able to load on more weights to my bars and dumbbells!

### donkjellberg — 2012-04-05T08:20:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/post-11648>

Bacon Pizza!!!

My kids are loving it. They keep saying more pizza please. LoL, a 4 and 6 year old both getting into the groove. I don't think there will be any left-overs for breakfast but that's okay

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-05T16:22:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/post-11712>

@donkjellberg, what is bacon pizza? sounds yummy.

My victory of today is that I am wearing pants to work that I haven't been able to fit into in over a year or possibly longer. And they are even a bit loose.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-04-06T02:32:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/post-12006>

My victory today is I ordered a pair of jeans online in a size down from what I usually order, and they came in the mail today and fit like a glove. When does that EVER happen?

Also, my day included uncured side pork (my version of bacon), and any day with bacon is a success story in my book. They say bacon is the gateway meat for vegetarians...I like it!

Cheers to all of you on your small victories!

### Susan M. — 2012-04-06T05:58:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/post-12059>

My husband and I just came back from Florence, on the Oregon Coast. The weather was mostly horrid, but I wasn't freezing like I normally do when I go to the wet, humid coast. I never warm up when I am on the NW coast, even when the sun is shining, but this time it was just awesome! At the hotel that we stayed at my husband was chilly at night with only a thin blanket, but I was roasting. Normally, he's roasting, and I'm freezing.

### agatha — 2012-04-09T21:26:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/post-13099>

Today I had to sit in our living room wearing just a bra and jeans because I was hot. DH was wearing long sleeve shirt, jumper and jeans.

### Brice — 2013-03-13T17:11:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/post-80363>

To day I took my first Yoga class.
I have heard a lot about the health benefits for the yoga workout.
Finally today I joined a yoga gym and took first training class.

### caroline — 2013-04-02T03:50:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/post-82559>

I bought a corded phone yesterday and am waiting for broadband to become live!

### BJK77 — 2013-04-03T00:27:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/post-82648>

My victory of the day was finding a new doctor who willingly prescribed my 300 mg of compounded Progesterone!! My last doctor fought me tooth and nail on increasing it from 50 mg to 100 mg even though my labs were still in the tank. I was doubling and tripling my dose the last couple of weeks to help my sleep and was terrified I would run out before I found someone to prescribe . . . no worries now

### cinnamon — 2013-04-03T00:55:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/post-82655>

This is from a couple of days ago, but here it is:

I ate oysters for the very first time!

This was after vacillating back and forth thinking they might be icky, LOL. A local fish store had a special "buck a shuck" event. I gathered my courage in both hands and went in to explain. The folks there were very friendly and explained how to go about it. The first one they recommended I try straight up - no sauces or condiments. Because how else was I gonna know if I liked it? I timidly sipped the liquor and immediately thought YUM! Then I tipped the shell and slurped the whole thing, gave it a couple of bites and swallowed. I loved it! I had 4 more! hehehe

I felt fantastic! I'm sure part of it was the magic of oysters, but also, it's always exhilarating to conquer your fears.
Maybe I need to ask for an oyster shucking knife for my birthday?
