Thank you OWL. I will report on the salivary cortisol. That should be interesting. It's the chronic stresses in life that you allude to that are biting me in the hind-quarters I'm sure. Maybe I just need a few weeks in Cancun...no wait, I meant Alaska.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-04-07T20:10:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-a-thryroid-problem-hack-my-thryoid-panel.706/post-12500>

BTW, there's a lot of gold in Dr. K's hormone blog post [http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/](http://http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/) That post in particular change my approach to a lot of things.

A few nuggets from there:

> Leptin controls all energy production in the body. Inflammation stops T4 to T3 conversion in the liver and abruptly turns off your thyroid’s ability to function properly despite normal thyroid labs. As an analogy, your car engine no longer has a gas pedal to use. Simultaneously Vitamin D production caves as well because TNF takes that out too. Immunity fails and bad things commence for the cellular terroir. (Levee 1)
>
> In Summary, once Leptin resistance occurs centrally in the brain, the liver soon follows and then the peripheral tissues become resistant too. This effects fecundity, bone metabolism, cardiac metabolism, the thyroid, and the immune system in that order.
>
>
>
> > Leptin resistance occurs first. Then insulin resistance happens next. This eventually leads to adrenal resistance.Ã¢€Â¨Cortisol is the stress hormone that allows for fight or flight syndrome (life or death). This is the hormone that allows you to run away from a hungry lion fast and live. Evolution always makes sure cortisol production stays ready for action at the expense of the other hormones that also are made from the same precursors. That precursor is pregnenolone. Pregnenolone is made from cholesterol, and cholesterol is made from LDL. So anytime the body is stressed or inflamed, it up-regulates cholesterol production to make more lifesaving hormones.
>
>
>
> So anytime cellular stress is high (high HS CRP), it also forces all the hormone backbone substrate called pregnenolone to be shunted to cortisol production. This is called pregnenolone steal syndrome. What exactly does this mean, Doc? DHEA, Androstenedione, Vitamin D, testosterone, estrogen, and aldosterone production all fall dramatically. These are all of the hormones that are made from a common precursor. Remember that chronic Leptin resistance leads to huge hypercortisolism all the time! NEVER FORGET THIS BIOLOGIC FACT!

### Owl — 2012-04-07T21:22:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-a-thryroid-problem-hack-my-thryoid-panel.706/post-12507>

Yep. Dr. K talked about high cortisol and pregnenolone steal a lot. I had that problem. But the vast majority of people who started on the MDA thread doing his leptin reset last summer found out that they have _low_ cortisol. It probably was high, and adrenal fatigue caused levels to drift down until they were all suppressed.

This is why he also doesn't get upset at high cholesterol, because it's needed to make hormones.

# Thread: Hack my supps: Fish oil pills vs. CLO?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-supps-fish-oil-pills-vs-clo.707/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ealachan — 2012-04-06T23:56:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-supps-fish-oil-pills-vs-clo.707/post-12282>

Anybody have any wisdom about what's better, fish oil capsules or fermented cod liver oil? I've been taking fish oil caps for years, but someone was recently telling me about CLO and I'm curious if there's that big of a difference between the two.

For the record, I currently take daily:

1x 1200mg fish oil cap

3x 2000mg D3 cap

1x 2000mg D3 + 45mcg K2 cap

1x "antioxidant blend" cap (which contains: 10000IU Vit A, 250mg Vit C, 200IU vit E, 7.5mg zinc, 15mcg selenium, 1mg copper, and 1.5mg manganese)

1x cheap-o multivitamin (don't have the contents handy)

2x 250mg magnesium (before bedtime - I take everything else in the morning)

I also take 25mg of hydrochlorothiazide (diuretic) daily for blood pressure control, if it matters any.

Would a daily dose of CLO give me what I need without having to take a handful of pills every morning, or was I over-sold on its awesomeness?

Thanks for the help, all!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-07T01:17:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-supps-fish-oil-pills-vs-clo.707/post-12312>

I'd say there is a big difference between fish oil capsules and fermented cod liver oil (vs cod liver oil, which I wouldn't recommend - I've read some concerning things). With fish oil caps you can't be sure of quality, for one thing. Dexter recommended food-grade salmon oil marketed for pets (I'll see if I can find the link for the US customers - it's in the MDA thread as well as in one of the threads here on O3's) which you take off the spoon - it's much cheaper and you can assure quality/freshness. MamaGrok knows heaps about the Green Pastures FCLO which is what I am giving my children. You can read about it on the Green Pastures website.

### ealachan — 2012-04-07T12:23:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-supps-fish-oil-pills-vs-clo.707/post-12421>

So, you give your kids FCLO, but you don't take it yourself? Or did I misunderstand that?

I've read about Dexter and others doing the bulk salmon oil thing, yes. I have to admit that I'm somewhat of a pansy when it comes to fishy-tasting stuff, and I'd prefer to take something like a flavored FCLO rather than straight salmon oil if I switch. I'm just curious if the FCLO would be any better for me than the fish oil caps I currently take.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-13T01:10:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-supps-fish-oil-pills-vs-clo.707/post-14467>

If I could afford it, I'd take fCLO too. But I can't, so my kids get it and I get bulk salmon oil off the spoon.

### shilohman — 2012-04-13T04:41:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-supps-fish-oil-pills-vs-clo.707/post-14547>

> **ealachan;12351 wrote:** Anybody have any wisdom about what's better, fish oil capsules or fermented cod liver oil? I've been taking fish oil caps for years, but someone was recently telling me about CLO and I'm curious if there's that big of a difference between the two.
>
>
>
> For the record, I currently take daily:
>
>
>
> 1x 1200mg fish oil cap
>
> 3x 2000mg D3 cap
>
> 1x 2000mg D3 + 45mcg K2 cap
>
> 1x "antioxidant blend" cap (which contains: 10000IU Vit A, 250mg Vit C, 200IU vit E, 7.5mg zinc, 15mcg selenium, 1mg copper, and 1.5mg manganese)
>
> 1x cheap-o multivitamin (don't have the contents handy)
>
> 2x 250mg magnesium (before bedtime - I take everything else in the morning)
>
>
>
> I also take 25mg of hydrochlorothiazide (diuretic) daily for blood pressure control, if it matters any.
>
>
>
> Would a daily dose of CLO give me what I need without having to take a handful of pills every morning, or was I over-sold on its awesomeness?
>
>
>
> Thanks for the help, all!

Dr K says krill oil is far superior than fish oil and I would assume CLO. Krill oil is a phospholipid where

fish oil is a triglyceride. Krill oil is better assimilated by your body because of this fact. Also Krill oil has more benefits to the body that fish oil doesn't. Don't ask me what they are because I have slept since I researched that and I don't remember

### ealachan — 2012-04-13T04:49:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-supps-fish-oil-pills-vs-clo.707/post-14550>

### Greg rx — 2012-04-18T06:09:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-supps-fish-oil-pills-vs-clo.707/post-16138>

You can not beat cod liver oil, period. I know Dr Mercola makes a big deal about krill instead but he sells krill and his arguments against CLO have to do with the ratio of vit A to vit d. I can tell you with out a doubt that my night vision is vastly improved since starting CLO and I bet the 400 units of vit D is equal to many times that amount of other d3 sources. Do not get the brands that remove most of the vit d. what's up with that? I think other kinds of fish oil are very suspect as to purity and mercury content. I have read that the Norwegians take great pride in the quality and purity of their CLO but they do keep the best, taken from the top of the barrel for themselves. I also do not buy the toxicity of vit A. Did you know that just 2 years ago every article about vit D carried the warning about the toxic effects of vit D? Then there was the theory that somehow taking vit A and Vit D together, protected against the toxicities of either one alone. Sounds like CW dogma to me.

Great tasting and cheap cod liver oil [http://www.iherb.com/Nature-s-Answer-Liquid-Norwegian-Cod-Liver-Oil-Natural-Lemon-Lime-Flavor-16-fl-oz-480-ml/7907](http://http://www.iherb.com/Nature-s-Answer-Liquid-Norwegian-Cod-Liver-Oil-Natural-Lemon-Lime-Flavor-16-fl-oz-480-ml/7907).

I would try Krill oil if I was not deathly allergic to shell fish. Anyone know anything about this?

Pharmacist whose life was ruined by lipitor (ct is helping)

**Posts:** 6

### vwaggs — 2012-04-07T15:27:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-5-weeks-i-burned-myself.715/post-12427>

I have been spot icing and ice bathing for 5 weeks now and yesterday when I took off the ice, I had a huge welt/burn from the ice brick. The SAME ice brick I always use and have actually kept on longer before. My other leg is completely fine. I thought I was past having something like this happen after 5 weeks. What gives?

### LinD — 2012-04-07T16:52:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-5-weeks-i-burned-myself.715/post-12449>

That IS odd vwaggs. I am curious to see the answer.

### vwaggs — 2012-04-08T01:35:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-5-weeks-i-burned-myself.715/post-12548>

No suggestions?? I guess I am the odd one then! Hurts really bad today with a bruised feeling and is hot to the touch.

### PaleoMom — 2012-04-08T05:38:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-5-weeks-i-burned-myself.715/post-12584>

No, you're not the only one. I'm 4 weeks in and just did this to myself too! My stomach feels really bruised and sensitive. The ice pack that did it was two days ago, but the tenderness didn't show up until today. Just like you, it is the same pack I always use and wasn't for a longer time. I only had this happen once before when I first started, funny how it showed up again!

### vwaggs — 2012-04-08T16:36:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-5-weeks-i-burned-myself.715/post-12656>

### LinD — 2012-04-08T16:43:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-5-weeks-i-burned-myself.715/post-12658>

Feeling confident you will get better and better.

# Thread: Boredom
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ginastarke — 2012-04-07T21:46:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-12513>

Hi all,

I'm trying to be as close to zero carb as I can, with a few carbs from vegetables, unsweetened chocolate, eggs, and coconut manna included for sanity's sake.

I'm also on a very tight budget so that unfortunately means cafo meat, with as much frozen wild caught fish and local grass-fed "variety" cuts as I can afford.

With nightshades banned, much of the spices I cook with are also out - paprika, pepper, and the varieties of Tabasco sauce that I thought I couldn't live without. So almost everything- beef, chicken, pork, fish all winds up with garlic salt on it - any other ideas for seasonings? I read a lot hear about turmeric- what meats is it good on?

I understand Guynet's views on reward food - that if the food had too much flavor, we'll eat more than we need to, but I think the boredom is causing me to eat less than I should, leading to a frustrating stall.I'm pretty sure I'm in ketosis because even when I get home hungry, I eat much less than I plan on, especially since I'm only eating my BAB and dinner with nothing in between. - any ideas?

### ealachan — 2012-04-07T21:50:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-12516>

Turmeric can kind of be an acquired taste, I think. If you've never cooked with it before, you might want to taste it first.

I mainly use turmeric in my curries because I'm not fond of the taste of it by itself, but my dad loves turmeric mixed into sauteed veggie mixtures (his favorite is kale, onion, carrot, broccoli, garlic and turmeric).

### Croak — 2012-04-07T21:58:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-12517>

I use tumeric on just about everything these days, eggs, meat, and vegetables. It's more of a "savory enhancer" than an outright flavor, it's like powdered soy sauce in that regard, and it's very good for you. The only problem I have with it is that it's bad for "finger food", because of the stains on my fingers that make it look like I've just smoked a carton of Lucky unfiltered.

You can also try coconut aminos, or Braggs Liquid Aminos (lot of hate for that in some circles, based on what it's made from and how it is made), liquid smoke, butter/coconut oil basting, bacon grease, and just about anything else from the spice section that doesn't have sugar, wheat, or nightshades in it. Experiment!

And Gina, nightshades _might need to be avoided, it is not set in stone that everybody_ has to avoid them. And finally, and I'll make this easy to read:

BLACK PEPPER IS NOT A NIGHTSHADE!

### ginastarke — 2012-04-07T22:06:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-12519>

> **Croak;12586 wrote:** I use tumeric on just about everything these days, eggs, meat, and vegetables. It's more of a "savory enhancer" than an outright flavor, it's like powdered soy sauce in that regard, and it's very good for you. The only problem I have with it is that it's bad for "finger food", because of the stains on my fingers that make it look like I've just smoked a carton of Lucky unfiltered.
>
>
>
> And Gina, nightshades _might need to be avoided, it is not set in stone that everybody_ has to avoid them. And finally, and I'll make this easy to read:
>
>
>
> BLACK PEPPER IS NOT A NIGHTSHADE!

Thanks! I'm avoiding the night shade vegetables until I get a new cholesterol test because I can't remember what my ldl was last time around. I had a feeling that pepper and capers were ok , but I wanted to be sure.

### agatha — 2012-04-07T22:41:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-12526>

Hi Gina

Yes, I find meat and fish a tad boring too. Mint, lemon juice, cumin, thyme, oregano, basil, rosemary, sage, thai fish sauce, ground coriander, cloves, cinnamon, ginger, mace, galangal, fresh coriander, chives, black pepper, sechuan pepper, dill, tarragon - these are what I use to make things interesting. yum.

### ginastarke — 2012-04-07T22:56:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-12530>

lemon juice is OK? I though all fruit was out, It would sure be nice to have lemon or lime for my fish!

Well I've had a long ride on my bicycle to think it out. Sorry about the whining, the nightshades are are a part of the cuisine I grew up with. I'm going to do some research into cooking before they became commonplace, or into cuisines like French/German that don't use as them much. Hopefully I'll be posting some recipes soon!

### Guest — 2012-04-08T13:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-12619>

@ Croak --I stay away from black pepper because of the mold. I even think that the peppercorns I grind are moldy. I miss it but it still makes me sneeze in either form so I will continue not to use it.

### David — 2012-04-08T13:22:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-12623>

Many moons ago I had occasion to be learning proper laboratory techniques in my third-year microbiology class that required the generation of Petri dish streak plates on growth media. The sources were left to the student, so I chose various sources from my daily experience, among which were such things as a swab from my dog’s mouth, after one of his self-satisfying butt-licks (his butt, not mine) and black pepper from a student union cafeteria shaker. To my surprise, after incubation, the plate with the pepper streak had generated many times the biotic growth than any other sample. Quite revealing. But I still use a lot of black pepper.

### agatha — 2012-04-08T13:56:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-12627>

I'm not exactly sure about lemon juice - but since it is only a squeeze and isn't sweet I figured it would be OK. Does anyone else know about this?

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-08T14:56:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-12629>

You could try lemongrass instead? I must confess to using a little squeeze of lemon juice in my mortar and pestle, with chopped lemongrass which I then pound into a paste, which is heavenly on fish or chicken - sometimes a squeeze of lime juice is better, and lemon or lime zest are tasty, too - but I don't know if they're OK? One of my favourite marinades is to mix a squeeze of lime juice with chopped lemongrass, a crushed clove of garlic or garlic salt, some fresh coriander/cilantro and a little bit of sea salt, it's also great thinned with a little stock/bone broth if you need more liquid. It can be used on loads of different meats and fish and even some vegetables, too.

### hazyjane — 2012-04-11T21:51:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-13873>

Lemon juice is fine since it's the carbs you want to avoid and lemon juice is low in carbs.

Other fun spices/herbs: Chinese Five Spice, Garam Masala, leeks, fennel bulb (raw or sauteed), and you can make coconut chutney for fish.

### ealachan — 2012-04-11T21:59:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-13874>

> **hazyjane;13942 wrote:** Lemon juice is fine since it's the carbs you want to avoid and lemon juice is low in carbs.
>
>
>
> Other fun spices/herbs: Chinese Five Spice, Garam Masala, leeks, fennel bulb (raw or sauteed), and you can make coconut chutney for fish.

cauliflower roasted with garam masala is super nummy!

Also, last night I made a [Thai Red Curry fish stew](http://http://onegirlcooks.blogspot.com/2012/04/thai-red-curry-fish-stew.html) (with shrimp and cod, but you could use any white fish), if anyone is interested. It was super easy and very yummy. My blog post doesn't give an actual recipe as such, but I can write one up easily enough for anyone who wants it - just PM me.

### freshveggies — 2012-04-11T22:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-13881>

> **ealachan;13943 wrote:** cauliflower roasted with garam masala is super nummy!
>
>
>
> Also, last night I made a [Thai Red Curry fish stew](http://http://onegirlcooks.blogspot.com/2012/04/thai-red-curry-fish-stew.html) (with shrimp and cod, but you could use any white fish), if anyone is interested. It was super easy and very yummy. My blog post doesn't give an actual recipe as such, but I can write one up easily enough for anyone who wants it - just PM me.

Your soup looks so good. I am not a cook, but want to add more variety in my food. When you make this stew, is it made with whole coconut milk from a can? Is the red curry a seasoning? Thank you

### ealachan — 2012-04-11T23:15:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-13893>

> **freshveggies;13950 wrote:** Your soup looks so good. I am not a cook, but want to add more variety in my food. When you make this stew, is it made with whole coconut milk from a can? Is the red curry a seasoning? Thank you

Thanks! I'm not a professional cook either...just a food nerd.

I used whole canned coconut milk, yes...and the red curry is a paste you can get at most grocery stores that have an "international foods" section. The brand I used is Thai Kitchen. It's gluten-free, dairy-free, and sugar-free, and it's pretty widely available. It DOES have a nightshade element to it as it contains chili peppers. Also, it's not super spicey, but you can add chile peppers to the curry to spice it up if you like hot stuff.

### freshveggies — 2012-04-11T23:20:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boredom.721/post-13897>

> **ealachan;13962 wrote:** Thanks! I'm not a professional cook either...just a food nerd.
>
>
>
> I used whole canned coconut milk, yes...and the red curry is a paste you can get at most grocery stores that have an "international foods" section. The brand I used is Thai Kitchen. It's gluten-free, dairy-free, and sugar-free, and it's pretty widely available. It DOES have a nightshade element to it as it contains chili peppers. Also, it's not super spicey, but you can add chile peppers to the curry to spice it up if you like hot stuff.

thanks for the information.

# Thread: Detoxing Emotions?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-emotions.722/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColdBren — 2012-04-07T21:50:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-emotions.722/post-12515>

I may be an emotional person, but this has been a roller coaster. Does a detox dump cause emotional upheaval? Is it possible to even have negative emotions stored in the cells that are now being released...hopefully permanently?

### debbie98055 — 2012-04-08T01:56:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-emotions.722/post-12551>

I felt like my detoxing was estrogen, and I was VERY grouchy! Like an amped up version of pms, and I was more annoyed at males than I was at females : ).

### hazyjane — 2012-04-08T02:49:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-emotions.722/post-12559>

Yes! In Chinese medicine, the liver stores anger and frustration and it's common for these emotions to surface when your liver is being detoxed or is having a hard time keeping up with a toxin dump. PMS is a classic liver "stagnation"/overload syndrome.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-04-08T22:06:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-emotions.722/post-12772>

I think \*hopefully\* I'm done the estrogen dumping and/or liver toxin dump b/c my last cycle which was two weeks late (on an already long cycle ~30-33 day) and I was very emotional. However my hormones were getting very out of whack leading up to that with the two prior months actually having breast swelling and pain. I was NEVER a person to experience that kind of symptom EVER. I also was more pms-y. I also had a weight gain with each cycle starting in November. It was very frustrating. lol.

So the last cycle did not have shitty soreness and it was normal. Then after that I had a massive dump of fat. So I'm lean now, and hopefully next cycle will be a) no pain/cramp b) not emotional wreck c) no weight gain (although I didn't have that the recent cycle) d) no cravings.

So yes, I really think that once hormones and liver (and I guess ultimately leptin) is/are fixed, then yes, shitty emotions subside! Instead are replaced with being able to cope/manage stress. :-D

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-04-08T22:08:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-emotions.722/post-12774>

p.s. It's implied that I was doing CT since end of Feb/early March.

### vkiernan — 2012-04-09T01:26:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-emotions.722/post-12816>

I have been an emotional wreck as well. This CT stuff really does crazy things to one's hormones. At least I believe that is the reason. I was pms'ing for a full week and then the TOM was really bad and I was still a wreck for the whole week of. I am finally feeling more normal and I have been doing this 6 or 7 weeks now. Hoping we are on the path to better health after all of this.

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-04-09T01:35:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-emotions.722/post-12820>

I have had a similar experience....my poor husband and kids. It was a few weeks with what seemed like constant PMS. Little patience, and more annoyed with things, and just more irritated. I am almost 4 weeks in and I'm starting to see much more improvement in this,. However I have noticed that some detox symptoms will lessen, and then come back days later. Honestly it makes me kind of happy, since I know that it is doing something!! And I think it will definitely be worth it in the end. I want all that junk OUT of my body!

Hang in there.

### Pamela — 2012-04-09T04:10:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-emotions.722/post-12874>

I had a similar thing with really feeling grumped out and kind of pissed at everything. Couldn't wait to get in the cold bath today and afterwards I did feel much better with no return of the major grumps.

### ColdBren — 2012-04-09T04:54:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-emotions.722/post-12886>

It would be awesome if this was really clearing out all of that junk.....I can't wait to get to the other side. Sorry you guys are going through it too, but happy to hear it seems to be getting better....and maybe for good!

### Glamorama — 2012-04-09T05:10:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-emotions.722/post-12896>

I had a lot of negative emotions during my first month of detox! Mostly linked to the excess estrogen being released, but I was often feeling suicidal and super-depressed (this is not me at all!!!!). It cleared though, now I'm back to my mostly happy selfs.

# Thread: HDL and leaky gut
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### optimus prime — 2012-04-07T22:53:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/post-12529>

Hi! Wondering if you have to have low HDL to have leaky gut. Dr. Kruse says "...and if your gut is leaky as shown by a low HDL level." ([http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/](http://http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/))

I have a 107 HDL, 173 LDL, and I am not responding to my Vitamin D supplements. I assumed that I had a leaky gut issue...but now I don't know!

Thanks!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-07T22:59:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/post-12531>

You can have a nice HDL number and still have leaky gut. Ask MamaGrok!

I suspect there is other stuff going on that may be revealed in other labs. I had to start using an emulsified formula which is a common problem for autoimmune conditions. I'm getting testing next Tuesday so I will know for sure quantitatively. I could tell an increase in energy when I started it. I use Biotics D Mulsion and have for about 6 months now. Maybe something to look at...

### LinD — 2012-04-07T23:08:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/post-12533>

That's interesting. While I know it can be higher, October bloodwork has me at 89 HDL... And I have MS. No grains or legumes since November 2011 (if not before--I forget).

I tried to get my neuro to run some of the suggested labs, but it was a no go. :-( Guess I need to work on WHY I need these or find another doc in United Healthcare health insurance plan who will. I looked up John (Jack) Kruse (TN) and he's on there!

Dr. K... Please?

### Owl — 2012-04-08T03:39:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/post-12567>

Optimus, are you taking Vit **D3**? And most of us took 10,000iu/day for 3 to 6 months to get our numbers up. Some people took way more than that, because they didn't absorb it, or their bodies used up a huge amount. It's fat soluble, so you need to take it with a meal with fat.

### optimus prime — 2012-04-08T18:44:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/post-12721>

> **Owl;12636 wrote: Optimus, are you taking Vit D3**? And most of us took 10,000iu/day for 3 to 6 months to get our numbers up. Some people took way more than that, because they didn't absorb it, or their bodies used up a huge amount. It's fat soluble, so you need to take it with a meal with fat.

I've been taking 5,000 a day for 2 months. My D has been in the 30s and 40s for 3 years now. No idea what it was before that.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-09T04:25:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/post-12878>

> **optimus prime;12790 wrote:** I've been taking 5,000 a day for 2 months. My D has been in the 30s and 40s for 3 years now. No idea what it was before that.

Make sure you are taking an oil-based D3. You will not absorb it without the oil. If you don't have an oil-based D3 you can take it with olive oil. My level was 32 prior to starting D3. I was on 10,000 IU for over two years. The last time I tested my level was 76. I also read on Dr. Davis' blog that the longer you take D3 the less you have to take. I went down to 5,000 IU recently and will test again soon to see how I'm doing.

\--Steph

### shilohman — 2012-04-09T05:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/post-12890>

Also do not take D3 the same time you take magnesium because they compete for absorption.

### optimus prime — 2012-04-09T17:00:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/post-12976>

> **stephsxty9;12947 wrote:** Make sure you are taking an oil-based D3. You will not absorb it without the oil. If you don't have an oil-based D3 you can take it with olive oil. My level was 32 prior to starting D3. I was on 10,000 IU for over two years. The last time I tested my level was 76. I also read on Dr. Davis' blog that the longer you take D3 the less you have to take. I went down to 5,000 IU recently and will test again soon to see how I'm doing.--Steph

Wow, nice progress! Curious if you've noticed your body feeling different with higher D. Do you have more energy? Are you losing weight more easily (if weight is an issue).

My supplement is olive oil based, plus I take it right after breakfast.

### optimus prime — 2012-04-09T17:01:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/post-12978>

> **shilohman;12959 wrote:** Also do not take D3 the same time you take magnesium because they compete for absorption.

Thank you, I didn't know that! Good thing I take my magnesium at night.

### chasee33 — 2012-04-10T21:16:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/post-13439>

Blue Bonnet has a nice vitamin D3 supplement mixed with Medium Chain Triglycerides and oils like lemon that works nicely. I found it at Whole Foods.

### BenG — 2012-04-10T21:25:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/post-13442>

You typically get 1 ng/dl for every 100iu you take. I'd double yours to 10,000 IU per day. Even the Vitamin D council says that amount is safe. I was taking 20,000 IU a day for 3 months and got mine up fron 7ng/dl to 83 ng/dl, but if you are heavy, you need double to bring up levels. Dr. Kruse likes it between 70-100 ng/dl

> **optimus prime;12790 wrote:** I've been taking 5,000 a day for 2 months. My D has been in the 30s and 40s for 3 years now. No idea what it was before that.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-12T02:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hdl-and-leaky-gut.723/post-14016>

I didn't feel noticeably better when my D levels went up, but I had sever adrenal fatigue, which I've been trying to heal over the last two years. Also no weight loss with improved D levels, but that's because I was leptin resistant and wasn't able to exercise because of the AF. I'm also hypothyroid. Anyway, I'm sure that getting my D level up is helping with all the issues, but I definitely didn't have any noticeable improvement. It did possibly help with mood, but that's hard to judge since I've also done a lot to improve hormones.

I have the sports compression garments, but I'm not thinking they are doing much compression. . .and buying sizes too small have issues with not being long enough, or go up close enough to the lady parts. I took temps last night in tub while wearing compression - and did notice at least on surface the temp did drop lower than the readings from naked skin.

I agree!! It takes me ages to get into my compression shirt - although I seem to be getting better at it slowly! At first I was worried I was going to get stuck half in or half out! hard with an arthritic shoulder

# Thread: CT and intuition
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-intuition.732/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Birdy — 2012-04-08T17:58:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-intuition.732/post-12701>

This idea has popped up on several threads, and of course while I was doing CT. What has been your experience? It would be really neat to do a study, comparing going into a mini-spa mode, with either regular heat or cold, and see if there are different results, I know if my own case, I have become more creative and intuitive. I used to be that way, but along with my slim figure, I seem to have lost a lot of that after menopause was over.

I am finding that any CT, even soaking or cooling just a part of me, I do go into more of a creative mode. Even if I never lose another pound on CT, or gain another nanometer of telemere, I am hooked on CT for the creative burst is affords. (and gosh, I just hate auto-correct......)

# Thread: Come CT with me for 30 days. Start Monday April 9th.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 125
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamazon — 2012-04-08T21:22:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-12758>

This will be my first time starting tomorrow with face dunks for 2 weeks.

I aim to go slow and stick with the protocol.

I am not an athlete although I had started cross fitting and loved heavy lifting. I gave up my membership so I can participate in Dr. K's elite program. I have way more than 30 pounds hanging on me. More like a 100. Have some high BP that is managed by Lisinopril, some sciatica, generalized aches, probably PCOs (estrogen dominance by appearance and other symptoms), low vit D and chronic anemia problems. I may have a severed vagus nerve due to some surgery I had in 2001.

Don't have any hormone labs done but I had general labs done in January. My HDL/LDL are way below Kruse optimum. Waiting on my Telomere test to be delivered. Had it drawn one week after I started the Leptin Reset on 2/21/12.

I have lost almost 15 pounds since them. I am 54 and haven't had my TOM in two years but never had any menopausal symptoms other than maybe low energy.

Who wants to do it with me? This can be your first time or maybe you have fallen off the ice wagon and like to start anew.

All are welcome. We can encourage and support each other for the thirty days.

### Glamazon — 2012-04-08T21:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-12762>

This is the protocol we will follow. Thank you Chrisgraff for summarizing CT for dummies for us. [http://www.fixyourleptin.com/cold-thermogenesis/](http://http://www.fixyourleptin.com/cold-thermogenesis/)

Cold Thermogenesis

â€¢ best used with the diet & sleep protocols.

â€¢ should be added gradually.

â€¢ only for those with Omega6/Omega3 ratio of less than 10:1.

1\. Face dunks

â€¢ Remove facial products and/or makeup.

â€¢ 50-55ÂºF ice water in big pot or sink. (Use thermometer).

â€¢ Eat a meal high in fat/protein shortly before.

â€¢ Drink 16-32oz ice-cold water immediately before.

â€¢ Dunk/hold face in water as long as you can hold your breath.

â€¢ Never allow skin to fall below 50ÂºF â€“ Use Thermometer!

â€¢ Once daily for two weeks.

Supplements to ease transition

â€¢ Fish oil + Krill oil

â€¢ Bitter melon, (two hours prior)

â€¢ Matcha green tea powder stirred in cold water prior to CT.

2\. Wear compression shirt (ice, no water)

â€¢ tight compression shirt squeezes capillaries. MENS | WOMENS

â€¢ Same preparation/precautions as face-dunking.

â€¢ Hold 20-40 lb bag of ice on torso (w/shirt).

â€¢ Increase 5 min each session (1 hour max).

â€¢ If skin temp/color gets too cold, take warm shower immediately.

3\. Ice directly on skin (no water)

â€¢ Same preparations/precautions as #2.

â€¢ Hold 20-40 lb bag of ice on torso (no shirt).

â€¢ Monitor skin temperature â€“ never lower than 50ÂºF.

â€¢ If you develop cold urticaria, stop CT.

4\. Cold showers

â€¢ Optional transition to cold baths.

5\. Cold bath

â€¢ Same preparations/precautions.

â€¢ Wear knitted hat, gloves, socks (for first week).

â€¢ Start with 68ÂºF to 72ÂºF tap-water (use unbagged ice if necessary).

â€¢ Gradually increase duration per session, (max 45 min then decrease temp).

â€¢ Gradually decrease temperature per session.

â€¢ Water never colder than 50ÂºF (thermometer).

â€¢ See â€œEssential Readingâ€￾ (sidebar) for more information.

### agatha — 2012-04-08T23:37:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-12791>

Good for you Glamazon. I am 5 weeks into Leptin reset plus CT but I will be following your progress and experiences with interest and cheering you on. You are right to take it slowly - I went too fast wtih CT and got some unpleasant detox. I have also noticed some lovely benefits in wellbeing and energy and a little weight loss - the rigours of CT are well worth it.

### ealachan — 2012-04-09T02:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-12833>

Glamazon, I'm with you! I transitioned from face dunks to spot icing a few weeks ago, but need to suck it up and start submerging. I took the first baby-steps yesterday and today by standing in cold water about halfway up my calves - 5 min yesterday and 10 min today.

### Glamazon — 2012-04-10T05:56:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13250>

I just finished my first face dunking. I did 3 dunks for 10 seconds each. I was surprised I couldn't hold my breath for very long because I am a person who can swim underwater for a long time.

I was hesitant at first to do this because I am having such a bout with the spring allergies right now for the last couple of days. My nose is totally clogged up and running. My right eye itches. I am not taking anything because I don't want to impair the face dunking results.

The tap water comes out at 48 degrees according to my fancy temp laser reader from Amazon (15 bucks). My tap water is probably that cold in the summer too.

My forehead skin temp was 95 degrees prior the first 10 second dunk. It was 80 degrees after coming out.

2nd dunk my skin temp was 73 after coming out.

3rd dunk it was 73 again.

My face was red. The first dunk wasn't painful. The 2nd and 3rd ones were but bearable. I have a snorkel but I am going to see if my breath retention improves without the snorkel. I have been able to hold my breath for about 3 minutes in past times.

I did this at 815pm. I forgot to drink my ice water prior but I am a cold water drinking fan. It is my beverage of choice. But, I had a high fat, moderate protein meal prior. My meals usually are high fat, moderate protein.

The comical thing was I thought, What the heck am I gonna do with these orange glasses? I hated taking them off because my eyes don't like the blueness anymore. It bugs them.

So I ended up doing in semi darkness in the bathroom.

### freesia — 2012-04-10T06:18:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13255>

I'm with you!

Two weeks school holidays just started here so there will no immersion baths/naked recovery sessions until I have the house to myself again

It's Tues afternoon here so I'm a day out from your schedule, but I'm starting after my evening meal tonight. Woot!

Good to read how it went for you, and thanks for the shot in the arm I needed to commit to an actual start date.....No time like the present!

Will update later today

freesia (Day 10 LR, and loving it.)

### Endless — 2012-04-10T08:21:32+0300

### Kiera2 — 2012-04-10T09:00:01+0300

### freesia — 2012-04-10T11:13:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13287>

I got in three dunks of about 12 - 15 secs each too. I went in for a fourth but I could feel the water had warmed just slightly so I didn't count that one. I need to use a bigger bowl next time.

First dunk woke me up, and the second two hurt a bit.

My face and head felt invigorated after, and my skin felt great once it was dry.

I had a warm shower after and I noticed my sinuses felt a bit swollen after I got out. They still feel a little aggravated half an hour later.

Thanks Chrisgraff for the excellent summary!

### agatha — 2012-04-10T13:18:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13293>

I know what you mean about taking off the orange sunglasses Glamazon. I do my cold tubs by candlelight. I have to sneak upstairs and do it without my dogs noticing - one of them becomes very panicky when I light a candle.

### .lina — 2012-04-10T14:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13297>

aaah i missed this thread! i fell off the ice wagon over easter with my folks visiting and such, i will start facedunking again tonight! bookmarking this thread fo shizzle!  will be fun to compare results!

### Jane — 2012-04-10T23:58:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13493>

Hi Everyone! Count me in too!

I started face dunk yesterday also, but didn't have my thermometer until today. I used a big bowl and tap water (from a well) 60 degrees. I dunked 20 times for 10 seconds each. My face temp was 68 degrees after I finished. I plan on dunking again tonight, using ice water from the fridge. I'm also taking lukewarm showers, wearing a tee-shirt and shorts with all of the windows open.

I'm taking this slow because I have leaky gut issues (IBS-D dominant) and have been experiencing bouts of detoxing. Though I haven't done any testing, I'm sure I have hormonal issues. I'm 58 and post-menopausal for the last six years. I'm on meds for A-fib and high BP and also have 2 herniated discs, one in the lumbar region and one in my neck. I went off metformin about two years ago and have controlled T2D through diet and exercise since then. I've got sleep apnea and have dragged myself through the days with only 4 hours of sleep a night for eons.

The good news is that I went gluten free about six months ago and started the Leptin RX in the middle of this February. So far I've lost 20 lbs on the LRX, but have been stuck there for the last few weeks (about 90 more lbs to go). Unless I'm in the throes of detox, my energy is way up!

I'm looking forward to sharing the journey with everyone on this thread!

Jane

### Glamazon — 2012-04-11T07:46:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13664>

Yeah, it's late, I know. Did them at 1015pm.

I had an acupuncture treatment, came home and had my dinner. Then fell asleep for about 4 hours and woke up. I thought, "nooooo....should I still do it? What if I will never get to sleep?"

My eyes were so itchy allergy bothersome so I did it to get relief.

So, by candlelight, which is much better btw.

Only did twice this time.

1st time for 15 seconds dunk! woo hoo. water was 50 F. My skin temp before was 95 on my forehead. After, the temp was 75. It was more painful this time than the first initial dunk yesterday.

2nd time for 10 seconds. Couldn't hold my breath as long and it was painful. Skin temp after was 72.

My eyes are not so itchy but still some itchiness and puffy.

Since yesterday's dunk I do notice some differences. The runny, clogged up nose is less fierce. It seems to be clearing up. I felt better at work without having to use so many tissues to blow my nose all the time. I also don't feel so tired from the allergies. Still not taking any antihistamines for it.

I am going to get an ice gel pack out of the freezer, wrap it up and put it on my eyes for awhile when I go lay down.

I should have planned around the acupuncture. It always relaxes me so deeply that I usually end up falling asleep early on that day. One of the reasons I am getting the acupuncture is to help with the detox.

going to bed

### freesia — 2012-04-11T11:26:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13673>

Day 2 done and dusted here too!

I did 4 dunks tonight, around 7.45pm. Approx 9 - 10 seconds per dunk - nothing flash!

This morning I woke with a slight headache, persistant sinus drainage, and a watery left eye for a short while. The headache was worse by this evening and it was putting me off wanting to do CT, but by the time I'd drunk two big glasses of iced water in prep for the dunking my headache had vanished.

Hope others are doing well

### Glamazon — 2012-04-11T14:38:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13695>

So I did not sleep well at all last night. I have a problem sleeping well at night before I started the reset. It's been a life long problem since I became a night shift worker in my 20s as a nurse for about 20 years. I work a regular day job for the last 10 years. The reset is helping some and the glasses are helping me to relax earlier. But...this allergy stuff just makes it difficult to get good sleep. I can't breathe well. I am tired.

No, didn't hurt my eyes with the ice pack because I had to hold it in place with my hands. My eyes are still puffy and itchy. Just not as bad. My skin feels softer but that may be just because I am being more diligent about putting coconut oil on it every day.

So far so good. Thanks for the encouraging remarks.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-11T16:35:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13722>

After I was doing face dunks for a week, I started also dunking my arms. I was using the bathroom sink, and there is just enough room to get most of my arm in, minus the hands. I figured after dunking my face a couple times, I had all this ice water, why not use it some more?

I ended up getting a cup (with a handle because the water was so cold on my hands) to pour water on my shoulders also. I was really self-conscious about my flabby arms, so I took this extra step. And yes, it did help a bit with the flab.

One day I would dunk my right arm first, then the next day I'd dunk the left arm first (because the water would warm up during this process.)

### Jane — 2012-04-11T20:48:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13842>

**Day 3**

I used ice water from the fridge at 54 F this am. At first it was painful, especially eyes when I took my face out of the water to take a breath. After a few dunks it didn't hurt anymore, though that may have been due to the water warming up eventually to 58 F. I guess I'll need to add some ice next time. I did a total of 35-10 second dunks for a total of 5.8 minutes. My face temp was 62 F at the end. My sinuses ache slightly, but they've been draining ever since I started the Leptin RX. Other than that, I feel invigorated, even though I didn't sleep much last night.

### freesia — 2012-04-12T01:41:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13983>

Jane, good work! 35 dunks! That's commitment!

I wondered about adding a couple of ice cubes after each dunk to counteract the warming effect? Although as skin temp lowers there will obviously be less of a warming effect.

My husband is so impressed with benefits I'm getting from the the Leptin RX. In his words "it's like you're getting two extra hours of quality time in your day" - referring to the time before lunch and dinner I don't spend in a semi-crashed state. Anyway, after being initially a bit reserved about CT, which I started talking about pretty soon after beginning the Leptin RX, he's now _fully_ supportive of my plan to cold adapt. Yay!

I have only a minor background headache, and no sinus issues today, and I'm planning to CT with a few more dunks (inspired by Jane!) after dinner tonight.

### ealachan — 2012-04-12T03:00:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14017>

I got in a cold tub for the first time tonight. HOLY MOLEY, people. The water was 70 degrees, and it actually wasn't hard for me to get in at all, but I started shivering about 2 minutes in. I was shocked, as spot-icing never makes me shiver, walking outside in the cold (like 30 degrees) with no coat doesn't make me shiver, walking around naked in a 60 degree house doesn't make me shiver!

I spent 10 minutes in the water, then got out. My skin where it had been under water was reading at 71 degrees when I got out. I've still got kind of a sub-shiver (just feels like slight muscle tenseness without any actual shaking) thing going on 10 minutes later. I have just a hint of that lovely leaden-legged cold-tiredness I used to get as a kid when I'd spend literally hours every day in the pool or lake.

My biggest problem is that we have a really weird bathtub and there's nothing to help haul my fat butt out of it once I get down in it. I basically have to rely on my pretty measly upper body strength to propel myself up out of the tub when I'm done and that doesn't work so well. Sooo...I think it's back to icing for me, at least until I can start getting into the lake or my mom opens her pool up.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-12T03:13:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14022>

Hi Ealachan, congrats! Yes, the tub is a lot different from icing. 70 degrees and 10 minutes for the first time sounds perfect. If you wait a week, you will find that you shiver much less the next time. Until the pool opens, you could continue icing, with maybe some face dunks, and maybe soaking your feet in ice water? Some people take cool showers, that also might help.

You'll find that several weeks of CT helps with strength.

### ealachan — 2012-04-12T03:18:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14025>

If you wait a week, you will find that you shiver much less the next time. Until the pool opens, you could continue icing, with maybe some face dunks, and maybe soaking your feet in ice water? Some people take cool showers, that also might help.
>
>
>
> You'll find that several weeks of CT helps with strength.

I've been icing several times a week for about a month. I did start out with face dunks before starting with the icing. My showers are always cool, though I've only just gotten a thermometer today so I'm not sure how cool yet - I'll check in the morning!

Do you think cold (my tap water comes out at around 50 degrees at the coldest) foot soaks would have any benefit? I can easily do those...I'd just sit on the edge of the tub. I did it a couple times over the weekend, in fact.

### Jane — 2012-04-12T06:18:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14092>

Tonight I filled the bowl with ice water from the fridge and the temp was 54 F again. Before I dunked, I filled a pitcher with crushed ice and checked the temp of the water at 15 dunks. It had gone up to 56 F so I dumped in the ice and it went down to 51 F. I managed to get my skin temp down to 56 F.

It's great that your husband is so supportive. Mine loves how the Leptin RX is working for me, except when I'm detoxing and is supportive of how I'm eating (VLC), but he is skeptical of the CT. I didn't tell him I had started with the face dunks and he caught me at it this evening when he walked in from a meeting. I wasn't trying to hide it from him, because I'm doing it no matter what, but I wasn't sure what his reaction would be. I just want to prove to him that this works.

### Jane — 2012-04-12T06:25:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14094>

But after a few months of Leptin RX and no exercise, I'm able to pull myself out. I'm sure CT will make us superwomen!

### Jane — 2012-04-12T06:45:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14105>

**Day 3 pm**

Windy and cold all day, but I didn't turn on the heat, went barefoot outside and didn't put on a sweater all day. I got funny comments from DH, but oh well....

Tonight I filled the bowl with ice water from the fridge and filled a pitcher with crushed ice. I started dunking at 54 F and added ice when the temp went up. It brought the temp down to 51 F. Dunked 65 times for 10 seconds each. A total of 10.8 minutes. My skin temp was 56 F when I finished. I'm getting there! My whole face hurt at first, but after I reached 25 dunks, it stopped hurting. At 45 dunks, the water started to feel almost warm on my face so I tested it and the temp was only 54 F. It's strange but it feels relaxing, almost like meditation or a Zen sort of thing or a peaceful place. No sinus pain. My facial skin feels tight. It's interesting that a friend came to the door to drop something off and he said, "You look really great! I don't know why, but you do." Guess what, I'd just finished my CT.

### Glamazon — 2012-04-12T07:47:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14119>

So miserable today with these damn allergies or is it detox?

People were commenting at work. "your eye is red and swollen". My eyes itch so much. i am almost finished with the box of tissues I only bought two days ago. Damn...you would think I would lose ten pounds just from all the fluid that is coming out of my nose. I don't want to take any antihistamines because I don't want that crap drying up inside of me.

Instead my lips are getting chapped. I bought some homeopathic eye drops which are helping my eyes feel more comfortable.

Face dunk only twice tonight. Water 50 degrees F out of the tap. 10 seconds. Candlelight. I am just too wiped out and tired from this allergy stuff to put in more effort. 2nd dunk was painful.

I know the protocol says only twice for as long as I can hold my breath. I would like to push further. Just to review: (this from the Getting Started part that was just added to Dr. K's blog)

_Face Dunking:

What you need: skin thermometer, a timer, ice, and a bathroom sink or larger bowl.

Remove make-up or facial products

Get the water between 50-55 degrees

Dunk your face into the ice water and hold as long as you can

Record time and dunk again

Continue for the next two weeks and work your way to **using less breaths**

DO NOT let your skin temperature fall below 50-55 degrees_

The less breaths is confusing. Wouldn't it be for longer breaths?

### freesia — 2012-04-12T08:29:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14132>

@ Glamazon Less breaths/dunks to get to the ideal, or towards ideal, temperature maybe? But it would take more than 2 in the beginning for most of us I think?? That instruction is a little fuzzy around the edges!

65 Jane!! My face aches just thinking about it...

 Two hours yet until my Day 3. Looking forward to it.

### .lina — 2012-04-12T11:39:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14154>

i interpret less breaths as holding them for longer

i started facedunking last night after a small workout session, and it was extra hard this time because my cat Keso kept trying to drink the water from the bowl while i was in it, so i had him slurping next to my ear and i kept bursting out in laughter underwater! which is... interesting XD ... but i did a good session and found that it was easier to hold my breath after some time - i usually just get about 10 seconds max.

i need to get a thermometer though... i keep just checking my colours. but myes, proper tools.

afterwards i took a shower and i had the funniest sensation - i started out with warm water and as i increased the coldness of the water, i did shiver and gasp and such, but then as soon as i turned the water off my body and the air around me felt warm, as if i radiated warmth. it felt great, as up til this point i had only felt cold to the core from the showers. i went to bed with just a thin sheet over me and slept awesomely

and yes, i had planned only facedunking and hold off on the cold showers until after 2 weeks, but they are quite addictive so i couldn't help myself

### freesia — 2012-04-12T11:58:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14155>

Day 3. 6 dunks.

I realised I probably made a mistake the first day by having a warm shower right after my CT. I didn't shower my face...but anyway...

Tonight I decided to have my warm shower first, then I gradually lowered the temperature to cool and spent 3 or 4 mins quite comfortably cool. Got out, towelled off, downed another glass of iced water, wrestled on my sheepskin slippers and went straight into dunking. Water was 40, not sure on skin temp.

### Jane — 2012-04-12T19:56:54+0300

That would mean holding my breath for about 7 minutes to reach the ideal temp of 55 F. The water temp was between 51-54 the whole time. After about the 20th dunk my face doesn't hurt anymore.

Yesterday when I was out in the wind and rain barefoot without a sweater or coat, my DH asked me if I was cold. The funny thing is that I felt the cold on my skin, but I wasn't cold. Does that make any sense?

I can commiserate on your allergy symptoms! Before Leptin RX, I woke every morning with clogged sinuses and blew gunk from my nose all morning. Now I don't have that problem, but my sinuses drain all day (I take that as a good thing).

Any misery I'm feeling now, I pretty much chalk up to detox. How is your D3 level? Per Dex's comments I up mine from 10,000 to 20,000 for a week when I'm really miserable. Momagrok has said she uses vitamen C to fight infections starting at 1000mg and increasing until loose bowels occur. I haven't tried this due to chronic loose bowels from IBS. She also suggested soaking in warm detox baths: 2 cups of Epsom Salts or Fermented Apple Cider Vinegar. This really eases my symptoms though a warm bath contraindicated for CT. I think it works for me because my liver is overloaded from leaky gut and detoxing through the skin helps. It's also the only way I can get my magnesium because of the IBS.

Re: breaths, I know what I'm doing right now is not according to protocol, but I can't hold my breath very long because of asthma. Also, I can't read a clock without my glasses so I count the seconds in my head. Right now I'm shooting for more time and reaching 55 F skin temp until I can. It's a n=1 thing so I don't expect anyone else to do what I'm doing.

sorry to hear of your suffering - the itching is worse than anything. I rub coconut oil on my nose, lips and around my eyes. doesn't sotp the itch but stops the skin becoming dry and sore from all the streaming and wiping.

### freesia — 2012-04-13T00:08:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14428>

> **Jane;14375 wrote:** The funny thing is that I felt the cold on my skin, but I wasn't cold. Does that make any sense?

Yes that does make sense.

I was out early this morning looking at the sunrise in a very brisk temperature - it's mid Autumn here - and I registered the chill on my face as a pleasant sensation. I breathed it in and savoured it. I've _always_ liked cool air on my face, but this was icy cold. I feel like I'm teaching my body an old forgotten language, one it has a keen desire to re-learn.

Next week I may even venture outside without the slippers.....

Happy CTing everyone.

### Jane — 2012-04-13T05:57:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14590>

> I feel like I'm teaching my body an old forgotten language, one it has a keen desire to re-learn.

Freesia, well put. Love this!

### Jane — 2012-04-13T06:11:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14592>

a.m.:

I did 65 dunks at 10 seconds each except for 3 times when I managed 20 seconds each for a total of 11.3 minutes. With tap water and ice I started at 48 F. My skin temp was 55 F after 10 dunks. I had to add ice after 20 dunks even though the water temp was 50 F at that point, just to keep my skin temp at 55. Ended with skin temp at 54 F and water at 47. I had no ill effects after the initial pain. My face and throat (which wasn't in the water) were cherry red and the skin felt tight all day. I had a lot of energy though I didn't sleep well at all last night.

p.m.:

Since I didn't sleep well last night, I thought the 65 p.m. dunks might have been too invigorating, so I decided to try for fewer dunks and seeing how long I could hold my breath. I dunked five times with variable times: 10,15 and 20 seconds.

How did everyone else do?

### .lina — 2012-04-13T10:09:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14633>

i tried dunking in the morning but i couldn't cover the redness afterwards with makeup

and people kept asking me if i had a tan or was blushing :O so evenings it is... hehe.

but hey, what is best - cold shower before or after dunk? or is that up to prefs maybe.

i showered after dunk last night (cat was more eager than me to fill up the bowl, lol)

and i had the lights off all the time, just some weak light from a neighbouring room.

then on with my face-mask and into bed.

btw, do any of you moisturize after dunking? i am unsure what to do, i really hate that

tight dry feeling i get after a while, but i also feel like it might counteract the cold if i use

a moisturizer?

### agatha — 2012-04-13T12:38:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14642>

### freesia — 2012-04-13T12:42:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14643>

Hi .lina,

I don't moisturize personally, but I do know what you mean by the tight dry feeling. It doesn't bother me too much so far. It's hard to imagine how moisturizing would interfere too much but maybe it should be no warmer than your skin when you apply it? Hope someone will correct me if they think that's the wrong advice!

@Jane, you are doing well getting some dunks up to 20 seconds! What type of skin thermometer are you using?

My day 4:

I noticed extra sweating since last night. So in the .am I took a shower and got it fractionally cooler than yesterday for the final few minutes. I'm taking things slow....no shock horror cold blasts for me!

In the evening I did six dunks, I forgot to prepare quite enough ice to counteract what turned out to be slightly warmer tap water temperature today, and my water was 45. I managed slightly longer dunks too one up to 14 seconds. I felt the water warm slightly and I was out of ice so I stopped at six when I'd have been happy to do more. Tomorrow maybe?

I find I'm looking forward to my CT each day!

Best to All.

I was startled to see that I looked better without my make-up! I must have looked more pasty before. The dunking gave me some color and a tanned face is expected here in California (though I'm not getting much sunshine at this time of year).

Even in my fifties I can't use moisturizer, never have because it makes my face break out and I like the tight feeling of my skin after the dunks. It doesn't feel drying to my face, but I have combo oily skin. I would think that after the dunk would work the best since the protocol says to cleanse the face first, but I'm not an expert.

That is so funny about the cat. I don't think mine would get anywhere near a bowl of cold water. They prefer drinking out of our backyard fishpond.

Don't know about the showers. I would think whatever works best for you would be fine.

You are doing great! At this point are you finding that the dunks and showers are helping, hindering or neutral in affecting your sleep?

Wow! 14 seconds in 45 temp...brrr! I put too much ice in my bowl last night and couldn't take the 48 degrees. I had to add some warm water which I had handy because I was melting another tub of coconut manna. You are doing a fantastic job!

It's great that you are looking forward to the dunks. I find myself dreading it at first, but once I start, I enjoy it.

### NickieG — 2012-04-14T03:57:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14985>

Hi

I started face dunking a week ago. Then, didn't time my eating correctly and missed dinner. I was surprised at not being hungry during the evening. I was more surprised at missing face dunking. I find it relaxing after dinner.

I have noticed acne around my mouth since face dunking and slight headaches in the evening prior to face dunking. I rarely have acne or headaches so I am wondering if this is a detox symptom. After face dunking my headaches are gone.

I am on my 5th week of the Leptin Reset.

Nickie

Welcome to the thread Nickie! Acne and headaches can definitely be detox symptoms. I broke out in 2 huge pimples almost like cysts (which I've never had before) when I started the Leptin FX. The skin is one of the body's major detoxification organs. Reed Davis in a podcast at Underground Wellness said it is the body's "last resort" when the other detox organs (liver, gut, lymph etc.) are overloaded. so the CT may be stepping up your detox process already started by the Leptin reset you've been doing. It's interesting that the CT is helping the headaches...great news!

a.m.:

Starting water temp 50 F.

After the 20th dunk skin temp 54 F.

Ending at skin temp 57 F.

p.m.:

Happy dunking everyone!

### freesia — 2012-04-15T23:39:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-15392>

> Body temp was 97.5 going in, 98.8 coming out! WOO!

Love this!

Yep I'm going for non event, mostly. LOL. Good to be able to measure temps all the time so you know you're moving forwards. Are you getting any noticible detox from the CT ealachan?

I didn't update with my day 6 yesterday because I was running late and needed sleep!

So, **DAY 6**: About 9am I had a cool shower, turning to cooler for several minutes. Then I did 8 dunks in 45 degree water. I drank a couple of 8oz glasses of iced water during the shower and before the dunks. I had a headache free day

8.30pm I did another 8 dunks in 45 degrees. I had 3 glasses of iced water including one I finished off in bed after dunking. I had a bonus CT experience I hadn't planned on tonight! To get more of my face comfortably into the bowl I reach right under it and support it with my forearms up on a slight angle towards me. I thought I'd make my last dunk a good one but I pushed the time out a bit far and came up for air with a jerk...tipping freezing water down my front and a couple of icecubes into my clevage....LOL.

Hoping to get a thermometer so I can measure skin temp this week. I'm also planning to get those cool showers cooler yet. Gradual works best for me...accidents aside.

Glamazon hope you're doing OK. You'll be looking foward to allergy season being over!

### Jane — 2012-04-16T06:00:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-15509>

a.m.:

45 dunks for 10 seconds each

Water temp 51 F

At 40 dunks skin temp 54 F

Ending skin temp 56 F

Ending water temp 51 F

After a lukewarm shower, I blasted cold water (64 F) on my front, back, both sides and each inner thigh for 30 seconds each. I had goosebumps but didn't shiver. It felt so refreshing.

p.m.:

5 dunks 3 for 25 seconds each and 2 at 20 seconds each

Water temp at 50 F

Ending skin temp 63 F

Instant hot flash immediately after.

Yesterday I had a sharp shooting pain in my left breast and felt bloated all day (possible estrogen dump into my system?). This morning the scale had gone down 3 pounds. After a month's stall in weight loss I'm down 4 pounds this week, for a total of 24 pounds since Leptin RX starting 2 months ago!

With weight loss and age ahem...I've noticed a bit of loose puckery skin under my chin. When I dunk, I make sure to get that area into the water too. Perhaps it's just wishful thinking, but that area seemed a bit tighter. I may just keep dunking my face even after I start the regular CT to see if it is really helping. Who needs to go under the knife for a facelift?

### freesia — 2012-04-17T11:47:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-15840>

Good going Jane with those nice long dunks! I notice my eyesight sharpens up a bit after CT. Possibly other senses too?

Yesterday **Day 7, I showered in the evening for 10 minutes close to the cold setting then followed with dunking (8 times, 15 seconds the longest) in 45 degree water.

Today Day 8**, I showered 20 minutes inching right down until it was full on cold (checked after and it was 60 degrees). I guess the last 5 minutes were on the absolute coldest setting.

Turning the water down gradually works for me. I have check my self talk and make sure I'm registering the increasing cold as a positive thing. My warm adapted instinct is to cringe at it but I'm coaching myself to embrace it more. I didn't dunk tonight because I had neglected to make ice! When I opened the freezer just now to put iceblocks in for the morning the cold air hit me and I definitely felt it as a welcome thing.  Self talk's working?

...It's school holidays here and I've been out early with the kids the past 2 days so that's why I haven't managed two CT sessions. The rest of the week I hope to manage am and pm. Hope to stay on top of the ice situation too!

Plus, with my head in water for 20 mins, nobody can talk to me!

### AKMan — 2012-04-20T18:58:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-17128>

You folks doing the face-dunking: You are SO ready for a cold bath! I resisted it for a long time, thinking it would be miserable. The face dunking is really just getting your body ready for what's to come, if you just jump into cold water, unaclimated, you'd hyperventillate, heart would race, and vessel contraction would cause problems. Since your body now knows what cold is, it will let you 'chill-lax' on your cold dips.

If I could start over, here's what I'd do...face dunks for a week or so. Then, fill a tub as full as you can with water as cold as you can get it--32 degrees, if possible. Use lots of ice, make it so it's forming a skim of ice on top and ice cubes don't melt. Get it at least calf-deep. Step in very fast and experience the pain, shock, gasping, weird noises you'll make, all that. Stand there as long as you can, but at least a couple minutes. Feel how the pain goes away and is replaced by numbness. Drop down to you knees if you can. Do this naked. If you are male, watch in awe as your "meat and two veg" shrink up and disappear (but nothing compared to waist deep in freezing water, trust me).

On this first dip, don't go even think about full immersion, just the feet and/or legs.

Next night...fill tub with 55 or 60 degree water. Stand in it and notice the difference from 32 degree water--night&day, right? Drop to knees, then sit in it as deep as you can go. Shoot for 10 minutes, but you'll be able to do more. As you step in and settle into the water, pay close attention to your breathing. Breath normally, deeply, don't let your refelexes take over.

Do 55 or 60 degrees for a couple days, but my bet is you'll be down to 45 degrees in a couple days. And the thought of a dip in 32 degree water will eat at you until you do a full body in 32 degrees. I can happily say, 'been there, done that'!

I will be watching you guys progress, can't wait to see how you do.

Good luck!

AKMan

### freesia — 2012-04-21T08:16:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-17337>

I missed a day of CT, yesterday. Low blood pressure problems\* have worsened steadily the past 5 - 6 days and yesterday it got so I couldn't stand without dizziness. I had to be rescued from the city and brought safely home!...Bit embarrasing...but nevermind...moving on...

I'm not sure if I'm low on electrolytes. I took some mineral salt and potassium in water after dinner last night, and again before breakfast today, and I am feelng much better. I'd put the strange feelings, incl low blood pressure, down as a consequence of detox. I have high arsenic and lead reading from a recent toxic minerals hair test so perhaps LR and CT is mobilising some real nasties.

I took chelators last thing yesterday too. Wore sexy blue-blockers from sundown and slept really well. I was well enough to hit the farmer's market this morning so all is good.

This afternoon (**Day 11**) I had a cold shower and kept it on a cold blast for a solid 15 minutes. After I got out I did 16 dunks in iced water (45 degrees). I drank a litre of deliciously freezing iced water over the hour before the shower.

AK Man I like the method you suggested! Hoping to start baths when the kids are back at school next Monday.

Like Jane I'm quite enjoying the showers.

\*I have lowish blood pressure at the best of times.

### ealachan — 2012-04-21T18:30:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-17440>

15 min @ 60 degrees, legs only. Water felt warm after about 3 minutes...weird! Didn't actually want to finish when the timer went off, but given what happened after CT last weekend (whether it was the CT that caused it or not), I figured I'd go slow for a while.

Skin temp was 80 before I got in, 62 when I got out. Body temp (oral) went up 2.5 degrees 10 minutes after CT.

### Glamazon — 2012-04-21T22:09:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-17514>

### Jane — 2012-04-22T00:28:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-17562>

> **AKMan;17198 wrote:** You folks doing the face-dunking: You are SO ready for a cold bath! I resisted it for a long time, thinking it would be miserable. The face dunking is really just getting your body ready for what's to come, if you just jump into cold water, unaclimated, you'd hyperventillate, heart would race, and vessel contraction would cause problems. Since your body now knows what cold is, it will let you 'chill-lax' on your cold dips.
>
>
>
> If I could start over, here's what I'd do...face dunks for a week or so. Then, fill a tub as full as you can with water as cold as you can get it--32 degrees, if possible. Use lots of ice, make it so it's forming a skim of ice on top and ice cubes don't melt. Get it at least calf-deep. Step in very fast and experience the pain, shock, gasping, weird noises you'll make, all that. Stand there as long as you can, but at least a couple minutes. Feel how the pain goes away and is replaced by numbness. Drop down to you knees if you can. Do this naked. If you are male, watch in awe as your "meat and two veg" shrink up and disappear (but nothing compared to waist deep in freezing water, trust me).
>
>
>
> On this first dip, don't go even think about full immersion, just the feet and/or legs.
>
>
>
> Next night...fill tub with 55 or 60 degree water. Stand in it and notice the difference from 32 degree water--night&day, right? Drop to knees, then sit in it as deep as you can go. Shoot for 10 minutes, but you'll be able to do more. As you step in and settle into the water, pay close attention to your breathing. Breath normally, deeply, don't let your refelexes take over.
>
>
>
> Do 55 or 60 degrees for a couple days, but my bet is you'll be down to 45 degrees in a couple days. And the thought of a dip in 32 degree water will eat at you until you do a full body in 32 degrees. I can happily say, 'been there, done that'!
>
>
>
> I will be watching you guys progress, can't wait to see how you do.
>
>
>
> Good luck!
>
> AKMan

Hi AkMan, thanks for dropping by and for your advice. It sounds really good. Personally, I'm not dreading the baths, in fact I'm looking forward to them. My concern has to do with detox. I'm already dealing with a lot of symptoms (still trying to heal my leaky gut), including a few cherry angiomas. That's why I'm taking the CT a bit slower.

Hi Lanie, nice to have you along

**Day 12**, Had a busy morning so I just did evening CT. ### [email redacted] — 2012-04-23T20:24:01+0300
