32 degrees?!?! Wowsers, that's cold! How did you work up to that? Why not the 50-55 degrees that Dr. Kruse recommends on the blog? I would also agree about the showers. I can spend and hour in my 50 degree pool, but 50 degree showers are BRUTAL!

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-23T22:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18166>

> **Trisha;18192 wrote:** 32 degrees?!?! Wowsers, that's cold! How did you work up to that? Why not the 50-55 degrees that Dr. Kruse recommends on the blog? I would also agree about the showers. I can spend and hour in my 50 degree pool, but 50 degree showers are BRUTAL!

I just decided to do it! I was motivated by trying to steepen my personal curve to help with sub-q fat. I only intended 10 mins - but once in, I thought it was easier to do than some warmer temps. So even though I thought I was not adapting very fast, it was a total mental boost because if I can sit down in freezing water for 30 mins then I must be adapting! (hands, feet, head out).

I think those of you in pools and swimming, temp needs to be approached more carefully as you are getting the whole body freezing.

I also am having some major detox post bath and today. I'm going to take the day off CT to try and process through it - so I don't know if I gained that much other than the smug satisfaction of knowing I can do it!

### freesia — 2012-04-24T07:37:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18302>

Current detox symptoms....PIMPLES. Hmmm.

The past 2 days I've had showers starting at the M on WARM (pretty cool really) gradually decreasing to COLD over 4 - 5 minutes then giving myself a full 20 minutes on COLD. From about 8 minutes in to my shower I'm loving the cold. What I like about my shower is luxury of being able to adjust the temp down like that. My soft attitude might change when I'm more adapted?!  Another thing I like is that the daily temperature is coming down as we head into Winter which means COLD in the shower should be coming down fractionally each day too. Yes my shower set-up is like CT for Dummies. I like it. I also like the interesting poses I arrange myself into to get maximum surface coverage. I'll miss that in the bath!

My shower is over the bath. So I plan to keep the plug in tomorrow and shower myself right down into that bath... I'm not promising ice!

Hope everyone elses is doing well.

### freesia — 2012-04-24T07:40:35+0300

### Amazonmagic — 2012-04-24T16:58:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18353>

> **freesia;18377 wrote:** Oh I envy you your lake!! Nothing like making CT actual recreation to make the time speed by. (Good on the kids!)

I actually live on a peninsula, so I have many glacier fed lakes and the Puget Sound right outside my door. So I did it people, i CT'd in the Puget Sound (inlet off the Pacific Ocean), it was wonderful, I only lasted about 30 minutes though, not because of the cold, but because the theme song of Jaws began going through my head and my flight or fight instincts kicked in, I flew! So I have been getting some detox signs, not much, a few deep pimples on my face, bacne (dang it), and my paitence is thinner, but I just tell myself detox and breathe through it. That was until I did 2 days of open water CTing. I injured my wrist in 2008 trying to lift something heavy( I was a steel carpenter at the time, I could life heavy things). I sprang it pretty bad and it was extrememly weak for a couple of months, it healed and every once in a blue moon my wrist tweeks out on me again for about a week, especially when I tried to jump in to full pushups. Well during open water CT's that wrist has been aching, like I sprang it again, the ache stops after about 15 minutes in the water, but for about 30 minutes after I CT it's as weak as when I sprang it but without the pain. Anyone have any clue what this could be? I just think its so cool! Jusk like Jack said I am curing stuff I didn't even know was a problem from way back when! Also, I must have had a toxic dump, because for the first time I COULD NOT SLEEP, and I woke up swollen like I had gorged on a salt feast (dinner was backed chicken and 3 peices of broccoli), and my blook sugar was 118 and I had maybe 10 carbs, oh well, just tell the liver to heal heal heal!

but he bailed out on me when it came to the cold shower... that pussy!

i've only tried cold bath once but it was definitely less pleasant than the cold shower,

i like hopping around, it feels energizing, while the bath was very solemn and still.

### ealachan — 2012-04-25T03:06:40+0300

### Glamazon — 2012-04-25T07:00:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18625>

I had my first private Skype session with Dr. Kruse today. Highly recommend it if you can. It really helps you pinpoint or drill down to what you need personally especially if you have effed up physiology like mine. Going to go straight to icing starting tomorrow after supper. If you do schedule a session with him come prepared. Send him a history of what is going on with you, what has gone on with you. Add what has worked and what hasn't. let him know about your parents' health. Include past and current labs, surgeries, etc.Tell him what you want to accomplish. For me it boils down to being optimal.

One of the takeaways for me is to get more labs done, like my genome, and schedule a sleep study. For people who are really screwed up physiologically you can not beat this one on one to help you get clarity in what direction may be best for you. This process is all about fine tuning and refining for me and he is the best coach for this.

Even one session will save you a lot of time that might be wasted.

### Jane — 2012-04-25T22:17:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18827>

Hi Folks,

I've been too busy to get to my computer for the last few days, but I've kept up my CT except Monday when I took a day off. Basically, I did my face dunks same as before (a.m.s 50 times). I've moved on to cold showers (65 F) for about 10 to 15 minutes. I've also iced my belly and back for 20-25 minutes e/o day. I think I'm ready for the CT baths.

I've been doing the iodine protocol and am still VLC on the Leptin Reset. Detox is a constant, but I'm determined to get through this. My sleep is crappy, pretty much awake all night after wicked nightmares (probably the Melatonin). I think my main problem trying to sleep is my DH's snoring. It constantly wakes me up and keeps my adrenaline pumping. I'm going to try earplugs. Wish me luck!

The good news is that I was absentmindedly running my fingers over my belly the other night in bed and suddenly noticed that the stretch marks weren't as noticeable! I lost another pound this last week. That makes 25 pounds since the middle of February, a quarter of my weight-loss goal.

Glad to see everyone is doing well. Hello to the newcomers, glad you can join us on this journey to optimal health.

Jane

I sprang it pretty bad and it was extrememly weak for a couple of months, it healed and every once in a blue moon my wrist tweeks out on me again for about a week, especially when I tried to jump in to full pushups. ### Glamazon — 2012-04-26T13:39:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18991>

Started with a cold shower today. The water comes out of the tap at 50 degrees. I started warm and turned it down to not all the way cold but cold enough compared to my usual temp. I kept it mostly on my my upper back and neck which is one of the brown fat sites. I stayed for 2 minutes. It is a shock but the gradual cooling made it easier. When I say gradual I mean less than a minute to switch over. I going away for a long weekend today and there won't be ice there so I will be doing cold showers as I can. It will be ice time when I come back.

The session itself does wake me up. I feel very alert.

I told Dr. K that I hope soon to go up into the mountains and start soaking in snow melt in one of the creeks. He said, "as often as you can." This new way of living requires major lifestyle adjustments!!!

### Amazonmagic — 2012-04-26T17:32:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19031>

SO CT has been normal. On Wednesday I didn't want to! I couldn't do it outside but forced myself to do it in the tub, I was shivering at 64 degrees, crazy. The next day I was down and depressed, just let it flow, and it went away as soon as I did my CT last night, which I did a little differently. I usually lay on my back and my feet, legs, back, arms and shoulders are in the water with an ice pack on my belly, but yesterday I turned over and dunked from the other side. More was covered in water (only butt cheeks and upper back were out and wow was it easy. I did face dunks while soaking in 53 degree water right out of the tap. I'm getting there.

### ealachan — 2012-04-27T02:53:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19202>

20 min, 50 degree water (it was coming out of the tap at 47), legs only.

Skin was 85 degrees going in, 53 coming out. 10 minutes later, skin still feels very cold to the touch but I have no sensation of my legs being cold, if that makes sense.

Oral temp before I went in: 98.6

Oral temp after 12 minutes in water: 99.1

Oral temp at end of 20 min: 99.1

HOLY MOTHER OF SNOWBALLS! Coldest I've done so far. It felt like a million pins and needles stabbing my skin, especially my feet, the whole time. My skin turned lobster-red within 5 minutes. About 10 minutes post-soak, skin is mostly back to normal color even though it's still very cold to the touch.

Really tired as I was getting out of the tub - felt like I'd climbed a mountain and done a bazillion sit-ups.

### Birdy — 2012-04-27T03:36:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19208>

I was in the soak tub outside for about 20 minutes. After a few minutes, I didn't even really feel it, though parts above water were shivering a little bit at the beginning. I thought a storm was going to pick up, and I didn't want that only because it would wreck my book. Afterwards, I dried off and warmed up by emptying out my tub and watering all my plants. I am still not completely warmed up, but I haven't put all that many clothes back on yet.

### freesia — 2012-04-27T05:10:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19246>

I've been doing shower --> baths the last three mornings.

It takes a full 10 minutes in the coldest shower setting before I feel my body start to strengthen against the cold. I LOVE it when that kicks in. I sip iced water and rub iceblocks on my face while I'm in the shower and I pretend I'm at some flash spa taking the most expensive treatment on the menu.  I'd love to try this outdoors sometime.

The first time I tried a bath I underestimated how much ice I'd need, so it was a bit disappointing. Yesterday and today I'd made big ice bricks by filling 6 500ml yogurt containers with water and sitting them in the freezer overnight. I used a big bag of regular iceblocks as well. I sat the yogurt containers in the bath when I started and the ice popped right out of them by the time I was ready to sit down. This morning I was rubbing one of those huge iceblocks all over any skin that wasn't underwater (upper arms, chest and face) and it didn't even feel cold there because I'd already been well conditioned in the shower which was running at 57F. I stayed a further 15 minutes and it wasn't uncomfortable anywhere but my feet and hands. My feet are still cold four hours later. I haven't shivered yet, so I guess I'm taking it relatively gently?

The overnight temperature is forecast to go down to 50F here over the next week so my water (from an artesian aquifier) temperature will go down too. I'll just stick with what I'm doing and let nature take me down to the next level.

Detox. Yep. Pimples...pushing through concrete type fatigue, broken sleep, general achiness and some strange digestive upsets....nothing major, just unfamiliar.

### freesia — 2012-04-27T05:15:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19249>

> **Birdy;19281 wrote:** I was in the soak tub outside for about 20 minutes. After a few minutes, I didn't even really feel it, though parts above water were shivering a little bit at the beginning. I thought a storm was going to pick up, and I didn't want that only because it would wreck my book. Afterwards, I dried off and warmed up by emptying out my tub and watering all my plants. I am still not completely warmed up, but I haven't put all that many clothes back on yet.

I'd love to do this outside! I'd have thought of the book too  ...If this really takes off there might be a good market for weather proof books!

### freesia — 2012-04-27T05:18:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19250>

ealachan you're braver than me getting straight into 50 degrees!

Some people have said 32 is easier than 50. I mentioned 32 to my husband this morning and he said "you are not"....

Hehe.

### freesia — 2012-04-27T07:00:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19266>

Jane,

A big congratulations on reaching the 25% mark of your weightloss goal....since February...which seems like just yesterday! Yay!

I had to deal with the snoring a couple of nights ago. A 2am wake up call! Aaarrghh.

I hope the earplugs works well for you.

### ealachan — 2012-04-27T16:54:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19325>

> **freesia;19323 wrote:** ealachan you're braver than me getting straight into 50 degrees!
>
>
>
> Some people have said 32 is easier than 50. I mentioned 32 to my husband this morning and he said "you are not"....
>
>
>
> Hehe.

Lest anyone get the wrong idea, I didn't go straight into 50 degrees. I've been working my way down from 65 degrees since I started doing soaks, and I did a few weeks of spot icing before that. I would never advocate someone going super cold without having worked up to it. Bad idea.

### freesia — 2012-04-29T01:59:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19688>

I've forgotten what day it is  Week three sometime LOL.

I skipped a day yesterday due to time constraints. Today I had a 20 minute shower, cool --> COLD, getting down for the final 10 minutes to 53%. I shivered for the first time. It was pretty cold. Then 15 minutes in the bath at 50. More shivering but no real discomfort. I added more ice after 5 minutes to keep it at 50.

I got out nice and pink and I've been shivering (gently) the past 30 minutes.

I don't have weight to lose (maybe a few pounds?) but I do need to improve my body composition, plus hopefully reap a few other rewards from CT. Today I weighed to check and I'm down 4 pounds. I stayed completely stable the first 3 weeks of LRx. I can spare another 4 pounds maybe but I might need to look at eating more.

Still shivering, and typing is tricky so I'll sign off wishing everyone a great weekend!

### ealachan — 2012-04-29T03:17:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19703>

Holy suffering SeaMonkeys!! Fat girl not only got her fat butt INTO the tub tonight, but she GOT IT OUT. Awwww yeah. Victory points for me! For those who don't recall, the last time I tried sitting in the tub for CT, I had a really hard time getting out because, frankly, I'm too damn heavy to hoist myself back up without grab bars, pulleys, blocks and tackles, a team of draft horses and a suspension of the gravitational force itself. But tonight? TONIGHT...I just gave it the ol' heave-ho at the end of my 20 minutes in the water, and had WAY less struggle than last time. It's not like I weigh significantly less, so I'mma chalk that up to a strength gain and be overjoyed about it.

Anyway...the pertinents from tonight:

Water temp: 52 deg F Duration: 20 min, though it took me a while to actually ease down into the water, so I'll call it 15 minutes of actually sitting in the water fully.

Before CT: oral temp 97.7, skin temp 82

After CT: oral temp 99.0, skin temp 60 on lower legs, 70 on butt where I was touching the sides of the tub / didn't have good water circulation

Notes: Think my O6:O3 is still out of whack because my toe went white again. Just one toe, doesn't hurt, just turns waxy white. It looks pretty zombie-ish still, 15 min later. I did try to keep my toes out of the water once I got settled, but it didn't seem to matter. Tons of goosebumps while I was in the water, but no actual shivering while I was in. Slight amount of shivering started about 10 min post-CT, but not actually noticeable shaking...just muscles feeling tense. Felt like I'd climbed a mountain again (all in my legs) when I got out of the bath.

20 minutes post-bath, oral temp is 98.7...WHEEEEEEEEE!

### freesia — 2012-05-02T02:21:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-20563>

Week three update.

The cold water went down to 55, then 50, then today...46!

Yesterday I skipped CT as I'd had a sinus headache since the day before and it wasn't getting any better. Usually I treat sinus problems with high dose vitamin C, but since I'm doing the Leptin Rx and down to 2 meals, I limited my C intake to mealtimes...and that was pretty ineffective on the sinus front.

I've gone shower --> bath two days, and shower only the other days. The last bath, on Sunday, was 48. I stayed in 15 minutes, after a 20 minute "conditioning shower" and I took 3 hours to warm up.

Today's 46 degree shower was pretty brutal.  The bathroom temperature was only 54, though that felt cosy when I exited the shower! I shivered through most my shower and ended up with blue hands, which returned to normal colour 10 minutes after. Skin was nice and pink and I'm still shivering 1 1/2 hours later.

### freesia — 2012-05-03T00:59:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-20849>

The cold water's at 46 again and I'm putting off getting into my shower.

Yesterday I shivered for SIX hours. The water and air temperature had taken a dive from the previous day and I usually suffer when that happens anyway (no temperature control in the house which is usual here)...but yesterday was a bit extreme.

So anyway....what did I do at 4.30pm? I ran a warm/hot bath, put in mg flakes and soaked for 1/2 hour and warmed up! Felt like a truck had hit me when I got out but I was more functional than before. If I hadn't either have done that or put lots of heat on in the house (which we usually avoid doing) there would have been no dinner on the table last night! I couldn't use my hands properly b/c of the cold.

I'm going to try the same today and hope that my recovery time is shorter.

If not, what can I do to warm up (after several hours) that's more optimal than a hot bath? Excercise? Or is a warm/hot bath at the other end of the day OK?

Thanks if anyone can offer advice

### freesia — 2012-05-06T07:34:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-21741>

I skipped another day, then I've done two in a row.

Both yesterday and today the tap water read at 57. So I showered 15 minutes. then I added ice to the water that had collected in the bathtub and sat there another 15 minutes. Yesterday the bath read at 58 degrees (not enough ice) and today I got it to 54.

I don't know if anyone else has found similar, but a shower at 57 is much, **much** more skin numbing than a bath at 54. I turn around in the shower and make sure I concentrate the blast at my (emerging?) BAT area and my back goes completely numb even at 57.

Tonight I'm making twice the ice because 54 baths aren't really doing it for me. Not after the shower.

Goal for this coming week is to get baths to 50, and to rig up a spot icing garment.

Hope other Newbie CTers are doing well!!

### Glamazon — 2012-05-07T01:28:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-21842>

I just want to update y'all on what's happening with me. I had a private consult with Dr. K two weeks ago and I am doing a re-assess of my plans. My physiology is really jacked up which needs some pointed health coaching by Dr. K. I can go deeper now to help myself. I am holding off until I get my labs all drawn so I can have a baseline before I go full bore on this. I sent off my 23andme for DNA/SNP profile. I am awaiting for my salivary endocrine profile test to arrive so I can do that and send it back. (My insurance doesn't cover bio identical type treatments and labs.) I am also having some more labs done through my regular provider and a sleep study done. I am exploring cryotherapy locally.

[http://belowzerocolorado.com/](http://http://belowzerocolorado.com/)

I want to just accelerate what I can do as quick as possible and then look at what I can do at home.

You all sound like you are doing well. I hope to re-join you again soon. Still a newb that's for sure.

### Jane — 2012-05-10T08:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-22778>

Fell off the wagon for awhile. You don't even want to know why.  I started with icing my belly wearing my bathing suit again today for a half hour. Even my 80 degree pool feels cold getting in now. I could kick myself.

### Jane — 2012-05-11T06:44:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-23106>

Yesterday I was lying on the lounge out by the pool, my belly stacked with ice and my daughter and 5 year old grandsons walked in. The "buddy boys" thought grannie looked weird. I explained what I was doing. My daughter said, "why bother with that. I'd just jump in a bathtub full of ice." Bingo!

### Amazonmagic — 2012-05-11T17:46:03+0300

Just a check in. I've been continuing with my CT, but it as become a permanent outdoor thing. I either go in the lake or Puget Sound. **Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lactic-acidosis-and-metformin-anaerobic-energy-source-lactic-acid-inhibits-melanin.735/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 4

### chocolate — 2012-04-08T21:24:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lactic-acidosis-and-metformin-anaerobic-energy-source-lactic-acid-inhibits-melanin.735/post-12759>

[http://www.livestrong.com/article/373575-lactic-acid-pumping-up-a-muscle/](http://http://www.livestrong.com/article/373575-lactic-acid-pumping-up-a-muscle/)

[http://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=4&ved=0CEoQFjAD&url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.skincaretalk.com%2Ft%2F24927%2Fwill-lactic-acid-peel-lighten-past-my-natural-complexion&ei=tdeBT5ziG5HRiALR34n0Ag&usg=AFQjCNH25TlAq1mN-dEs3ZTDhtT9vDHDfQ&sig2=lowHGjAa8N\_JEUixrEf7KA](http://http://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=4&ved=0CEoQFjAD&url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.skincaretalk.com%2Ft%2F24927%2Fwill-lactic-acid-peel-lighten-past-my-natural-complexion&ei=tdeBT5ziG5HRiALR34n0Ag&usg=AFQjCNH25TlAq1mN-dEs3ZTDhtT9vDHDfQ&sig2=lowHGjAa8N_JEUixrEf7KA)

[http://www.metforminguide.com/](http://http://www.metforminguide.com/)

Rare But Serious Metformin Side Effects

There is a very rare, but very serious side effect that can occur while taking metformin. The condition is called Lactic Acidosis. If you are having any of these symptoms: increased sleepiness or unexplained lethargy, weakness, decreased or slowed heart rate, cold sensations, stomach pains, or if you are feeling light headed or have fainted either before or after taking metformin you could be showing some of the symptoms of lactic acidosis. This is a dangerous syndrome and you should seek emergency medical help right away.

Chest pain has been reported and other metformin side effects such as flushing, red face, etc. You should also be concerned if you are allergic to metformin or have allergic reactions after taking it. Allergic reactions can be marked by rashes, hives, itching, difficulties with breathing, or unexplained swelling.

[http://www.merckvetmanual.com/mvm/index.jsp?cfile=htm/bc/21703.htm](http://http://www.merckvetmanual.com/mvm/index.jsp?cfile=htm/bc/21703.htm) lactic acidosis with grain overload

that glucose was insufficient as an energy carrier during intense synaptic activity and finally, that lactate can be an efficient energy substrate capable of sustaining and enhancing brain aerobic energy metabolism in vitro.\[21\] The paper was positively commented by Kasischke: "The study by Ivanov et al. (2011) also provides novel data on biphasic NAD(P)H fluorescence transients, an important physiological response to neural activation that has been reproduced in many studies and that is believed to originate predominately from activity-induced concentration changes to the cellular NADH pools."\[22\]

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lactic\_acid](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lactic_acid)

Lactic acid is developing brain energy, I guess it would be repairing brain energy also. A lot of thinking has changed about lactic acid in the past couple years. I know that there are a couple of ridiculously muscular women I've seen around that have this all over golden glow and I am sure its the lactic acid causing it. It looks breathtaking.

# Thread: I was doing great until....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### janagram — 2012-04-09T09:01:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-12925>

I was doing great until we went on vacation....and now we're back, there's always some reason to veer off the reset rules....once you go off its so hard to tighten up the reins...it's the hardest thing! Today's easter... there's a reason to eat. I cooked breakfast for my son and granddaughter....so had to have muffins (glutenfree, but...!) Then to dinner with friends....wine, dessert, help me.......helllp!

I know almost all of you have been here at one time or another. You've been on a diet or a "plan" and then fallen off, way off, and going back is almost impossible.... I am going to have to find out what makes it possible to go back on plan. Just venting but if anyone has any insights, tips, or encouragement, please chime in.

### Petra — 2012-04-09T11:55:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-12928>

Just relax, take a deep breath.

As soon as you can, sit all by yourself for at least 30 minutes. No interruptions and think, think WHY you want this to work.

There has to be something deep down inside of you that want this to work.

Find it.

Love and care,

Petra

### Glamorama — 2012-04-09T12:46:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-12931>

Hi! It's normal... I had some Lindt milk chocolate yesterday and felt horrible after. The pure physical discomfort I was in was enough to get me back on track. Just wake up, start your brand new day with a really big BAB and up the fat if you have to... The cue is to get as full as possible on healthy foods, and cravings for carbs will go soon enough.

### caroline — 2012-04-09T13:38:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-12934>

don't beat yourself up- life happens. tomorrow will be a new day

### LisaAPB — 2012-04-09T16:28:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-12961>

Start by going back to a BAB. Eat a big protein/fat breakfast. Salad for dinner.

Get up the next day, eat a big protein/fat breakfast and salad for dinner.

Day three, repeat. Usually by this time, things start to sort themselves out. I do not know why, but 72 hours seems to be the magic point for me.

### janagram — 2012-04-09T21:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-13088>

I can't thank you guys enough! Each of you has said something that adds support to my aim. Oops, there I've said the word aim! Now I've gotta do it. If the wrd aim hadn't leapt out of my fingers I could still waffle, but "aim" is serious! Thank you all! Maybe I'll use this thread to follow up.

### TerrierMom — 2012-04-14T20:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-15097>

Janagram maybe this will help.

I am a carb sugar pastry cake cookie LOVER. Hard to think of breakfast without toast. If, 7 weeks ago, pre-leptin reset me would have looked into my food log that I've eaten the last few weeks, I'd not have wanted to go on the program! Go to a ball game without peanuts and hot dogs and soda and ice cream? Never! Not enjoy food to soothe my blue moods? Unthinkable! Have a bday without a cake ...unheard of!

But see, that is focusing on what you CANNOT have. Doing that will keep you from getting on the wagon.

Someone else posted, look inside and think what you DO want to have. For me it was just not feeling crappy,, like I was stuffed into a sausage casing, not winded at the top of a flight of stairs. The weight loss to me is just a by product of healing. So I focus on what I want to have - and I focus on what I CAN EAT. Bacon! Eggs! Steaks smothered in onions and mushrooms cooked in pastured butter!

At first I just focused on the BAB. And I found that it, with heaping helpings of CO (I melt it in a shot glass and drink it like a shot!), I started to crave, yes crave, meat and veggies. And was supremely happy eating what I was eating. And felt a sense of empowerment going to lunch with friends, because I can find something I can eat EVERYWHERE... Even if it is a burger without a bun. My friends think they know what I'm doing - but over time they learned no dairy, no nuts, no legumes, no grains... Not just low carb? You're crazy! (go ahead and think it but it's working!). They are 'proud' of how no matter if we go to a Mexican place, I get a salad with chicken and salsa and guacamole and don't touch the basket of chips. You couldn't pay me to eat them though...

I guess what I'm saying is eating that BAB will check your cravings and get you on track, and if you act as though you are determined to find a way to eat paleo even in any circumstance, you will be able to.

Whatever you are looking for, you will find it. I went to a baby shower. I made sure to have my BAB before (which usually gets me through til dinner!) and I was mentally prepared to have nothing but ice water the whole time. If I'd been looking for a reason why I could not eat paleo, I'd have found one. But I wasnt. They had tea there.. Fine. They had little cute bundt individual cakes that looked delicious..... They had a spinach crust less quiche. It had some Parmesan cheese in it but all else was ok. So I had a square of that, some water, some tea. I left there feeling awesome about my choices (and excited for my friends baby, of course!)

The moral of the story is - when faced with a menu look for what you CAN have and be determined to find it, even if you have to ask the server for a side salad with oil and vinegar instead of rice or potatoes....if you are determined to stay on, you will find a way! If you're looking for a reason to slide, you'll find that too.

As a good friend of mie always says -if you argue for your limitations, you get to keep them! So I argue for what I want, not for why I can't do it.

Hope that helps... You gotta wanna, you gotta believe. I have read just about every one of jacks blog posts and all the comments, some more than once. I find a forum like this, immersing myself in the science, helps my belief in 1) that it WILL WORK -and 2) that I can do it, with a little boost from the cheer squad now and then... And last but not least, try to find new recipes and fix 1-3 new things every week to put into your meal rotation to keep things interesting.

I swear if I read pastry recipes all day I'd stumble. I read recipes for things I can eat... And get inspired. jacks cookbook is great for that!

### chipmeister — 2012-08-21T01:27:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-45940>

Wow...what a great thread. I agree whole heartedly with all that has been said prior but one thing for me which keeps me on track is this: You must read and understand how eating the "bad" stuff is actually destroying you...it isn't just bad for your diet, it is doing nothing but providing you a small level of satisfaction for a short period of time while destroying your body from the inside.

That is what helps to motivate me when making my decisions. I want to live a long and healthy life. I don't want to be one of those guys wondering if I brought enough meds on my vacation, or trying to remember to bring my morning meds to breakfast and evening meds to dinner. I don't want to have to worry about any pills other than vitamins.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-21T01:50:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-45944>

One though I had when re-reading this is. . .part of this is a "fake it till you make it thing". By just DOING IT, over and over again, even when you falter (get back up and BAB damnit!) that what you WANT actually changes! Very few never fail. . .expect to fail. But I bet what happens, even without planning or desire is each time it's a little longer between fails. . .and each time that wheat-yummy tastes a little less awesome. . .and pretty soon you go weeks without even thinking about those things that used to bring you pleasure.

I'm not one for "tricking" myself or playing behavior games. . . and believe me I had a love affair with delish foods - but if you keep getting back up and starting over when you fail - YOU change.

### bigknitwit — 2012-08-21T01:57:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-45948>

I do what Jack said somewhere. In short it was something like, "embrace the discomfort." Really feel your level of discomfort and experience it. Remind yourself of previous discomforts that you got past and beyond (I think of my first several weeks on this diet), and in particular remember the discomfort you felt then. I find that when I do this, I find I can move forward in a better state of acceptance, rather than constantly fighting with it.

### popcornsally — 2012-08-23T05:14:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-46482>

+1 on the old me thinking, "No way can/will I do that," giving up pastries and other junk food. I have also been a serious cheese lover, but now since my chronic sinus headaches are gone, I am not even tempted by dairy. Not worth it!

Enough time goes by and you brain starts working again. Then it's not that hard to say no to food that messes you up for days.

### Rosekelly — 2012-08-23T18:35:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-46545>

I attended a discussion group last night where they always have snacks laid out. This time someone brought a beautiful cake. I didn't feel tempted by the cake, but I realized that I missed the social bonding over food. That's really more important than the actual food. Eating is communal, and refusing to eat when you've been offered a delectable treat can feel as unsociable as refusing to laugh at a really good joke. And we all want to fit in with our friends.

But my friends understand. I'm just the one that doesn't eat the cake, or the chocolates, or the crackers, etc. And last night we actually got talking about this, and I opened up more about my reasons, and damned if they didn't all have some of the same doubts about all this sugar. One has really been wanting to cut back but feels addicted. She told me afterwards she didn't even like the cake.

So maybe we quietly help our friends and families by not eating the cake with them. Just food for thought.

### Lyndra — 2012-08-23T19:33:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-46555>

Starfish. You're having a positive influence. So good your friends understand and are interested in hearing new ideas.

### caroline — 2012-08-24T00:49:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-46614>

> **Rosekelly;46949 wrote:** I attended a discussion group last night where they always have snacks laid out. This time someone brought a beautiful cake. I didn't feel tempted by the cake, but I realized that I missed the social bonding over food. That's really more important than the actual food. Eating is communal, and refusing to eat when you've been offered a delectable treat can feel as unsociable as refusing to laugh at a really good joke. And we all want to fit in with our friends.
>
>
>
> But my friends understand. I'm just the one that doesn't eat the cake, or the chocolates, or the crackers, etc. And last night we actually got talking about this, and I opened up more about my reasons, and damned if they didn't all have some of the same doubts about all this sugar. One has really been wanting to cut back but feels addicted. She told me afterwards she didn't even like the cake.
>
>
>
> So maybe we quietly help our friends and families by not eating the cake with them. Just food for thought.

I relate to what you are saying so much - I too am the friend that doesn't eat the cake or crackers or chocolates. That is nice to think that maybe I am having an impact in a subtle way. Thanks

### vlynnb — 2012-08-24T06:17:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-46668>

Rosekelly and Caroline, I will think of you at my monthly book club when they bring out the goodies. And Jana, I really do relate and hope you are still trying. Most of my problems have come in the last few months frequent visitors, then an illness and then death of a long time friend, more visitors coming to the funeral, having to care for lots of grandchildren for one reason or another ... always something. Actually I haven't been too non-compliant, I am just a little more than a little disorganized and live far out in the country so sometimes if someone eats my planned protein its a long way to the store and I have a hard time getting my BAB and that is when I have trouble, That's when I succumb to just a tiny little bite of something one of my gourmet chef children have come up with. But I agree with others that the discomfort and sinus problems afterward are making it easier and easier to say no. And my tastes and cravings for their creations have changed.

My children are harder to deal with than my friends at book club. The children think I am a little nuts, if the friends do, they are too polite to say so. Well, most are. And after several months of turning the friends down two of them have joined me at least in trying to get rid of yeast in their systems. The others in the group are now trying to make sure there is at least something we can eat.

In fact the book club has kept me on target for another reason. I heard one lady who is not always gracious whisper to another that I would never succeed at keeping the weight off after the Hcg diet (she and her husband didn't), So, I have to succeed! Besides, I really like her husband and his health is starting to deteriorate so I want to be a good influence for him.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-08-25T20:38:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-47079>

For me, it goes in cycles on my thoughts regarding this whole situation.

Sometimes no matter what I feel so good and I am so confident, I don't care what people try to offer me. To state, I NEVER actually have any 'cravings' at all. However, the social stigma is REALLY hard to overcome. To be the 'weird' one...or sometimes can't help but think about the people that do EVERYTHING wrong yet still look/feel (better) than you do...what the hell?

So I guess I always just hope for the days I feel confident and strong...b/c while I can resist, the emotional grief I feel for those cyclical times is damned hard.

Thanks.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-26T03:54:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-47174>

> **vlynnb;47077 wrote:**
>
>
>
> In fact the book club has kept me on target for another reason. I heard one lady who is not always gracious whisper to another that I would never succeed at keeping the weight off after the Hcg diet (she and her husband didn't), So, I have to succeed! Besides, I really like her husband and his health is starting to deteriorate so I want to be a good influence for him.

hehe I'm the same way. . .someone says "you can't do this" and by god I'm going to Fing doing it!

### caroline — 2012-08-26T04:30:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-47181>

It is my book club that is a big problem too. It seems to be an epic food fest of very rich food and a lot of desserts and wine. It is disturbing to see these mostly quite unhealthy and overweight women stuffing themselves. I am now at a really decent weight for me \[5'5" and 59K\] - but someone is always saying - are you sure you aren't getting too thin??? I am definitely not too thin but it seems to disturb people for some reason.

### janagram — 2012-08-28T20:16:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-doing-great-until.738/post-47823>

vlynn, and all...I am still trying, thank you for the wish!

I am doing better in small ways that add up. My sleep is much better...

Oh, yeah, this is about social stuff. I just got back from a week on retreat with a meditation group...living with 4 other women friends...we cooked together, etc. We went out to dinner a lot too. It seemed to work for me...as they would all dig into bread with meals out I would just sip my wine...I ate a couple very small desserts of fruit with "coconut bliss" ice cream before I said I can't really do sugar at all, and I stuck to it. There were some sugarfree chocolate bars in the cabin which I ate bits of. I ate lots of fish. Approaching optimal.....Oh and check out my CT experience on the CT thread. (I'm lousy at it)

# Thread: Night Sweats??? Anybody else or is it just me...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vkiernan — 2012-04-09T14:55:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-12942>

Ok, I'm confused, what is the chance CT is causing night sweats? I have had them for the past week & 1/2 during a really bad TOM and now even after Tom almost every night. What is up with this? The only other changes since starting CT over 7 weeks ago is that I am taking supps for adrenal fatigue: DHEA, pregnenolone and Isocort (now switched to HC but that was only mid last week). It is either menapause starting really fast, the new supps or CT. I'm thinking a call in to my doc is warranted. Something has changed for sure.

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-09T15:46:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-12951>

I haven't had night sweats - but I have had periods where I'm so freaking HOT I CANT STAND IT.. I get all flushed, I'm walking around wearing tank tops, shorts, dresses to work. b/c I've been getting too hot...

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-04-09T16:05:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-12955>

I had them the first couple of nights when starting the ice packs. It went away after 3 days.

### vkiernan — 2012-04-09T20:22:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13056>

> **Shijin13;13020 wrote:** I haven't had night sweats - but I have had periods where I'm so freaking HOT I CANT STAND IT.. I get all flushed, I'm walking around wearing tank tops, shorts, dresses to work. b/c I've been getting too hot...

It sounds like you are either LS or becoming cold-adapted or maybe both. Woohoo! Has dr. K confirmed you are LS at this point or do you know for sure?

I would be excited to be hot all the time. Although, I am definitely warmer than I have been. When I crawl into bed at night I have noticed that I warm up really quick. I only sleep in a light tank and undies and when my legs hit the cold sheets it is usually miserable; but lately they warm right up and I don't worry about climbing into a cold bed anymore. I also get a little warm feeling in my legs in the CT bath. That has got to be a little bit of cold adaptation wouldn't you think????

I also followed you with drinking cold things all the time. That was good advice. Rarely do I drink hot things and now if I do, they are too hot! Bring on the cold baby!

### vkiernan — 2012-04-09T20:24:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13060>

> **villjamur\_stevenson;13024 wrote:** I had them the first couple of nights when starting the ice packs. It went away after 3 days.

Only 3 days? Hmmm, maybe I just have more detoxing (loads of excess estrogen) to do and that is why they have been happening the last 1.5 weeks. Oh well, chalk it up to in the name of OPTIMAL! That's my story and I'm sticking to it!

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-09T20:30:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13066>

> **vkiernan;13125 wrote:** It sounds like you are either LS or becoming cold-adapted or maybe both. Woohoo! Has dr. K confirmed you are LS at this point or do you know for sure?
>
>
>
> I would be excited to be hot all the time. Although, I am definitely warmer than I have been. When I crawl into bed at night I have noticed that I warm up really quick. I only sleep in a light tank and undies and when my legs hit the cold sheets it is usually miserable; but lately they warm right up and I don't worry about climbing into a cold bed anymore. I also get a little warm feeling in my legs in the CT bath. That has got to be a little bit of cold adaptation wouldn't you think????
>
>
>
> I also followed you with drinking cold things all the time. That was good advice. Rarely do I drink hot things and now if I do, they are too hot! Bring on the cold baby!

my last set of labs showed I was pretty close but not quite there... needed help in the T3 department..but my Thyroid finally turned on! I think by my next set of labs my FT3:RT3 ratio will be above 20! as I was at 15.5

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-09T20:42:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13081>

I've had a couple of nights of horrible nightsweats, which were also combined with intense nausea. Don't know whether it's from the CT, though?

### vkiernan — 2012-04-09T21:06:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13086>

> **Shijin13;13135 wrote:** my last set of labs showed I was pretty close but not quite there... needed help in the T3 department..but my Thyroid finally turned on! I think by my next set of labs my FT3:RT3 ratio will be above 20! as I was at 15.5

Awesome. You have worked sooo hard. I'm really happy for you and your progress. Can I ask how you know it "turned" on? Did you have a great T3 lab result? That would be fantastic.

### vkiernan — 2012-04-09T21:07:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13087>

> **oliviascotland;13150 wrote:** I've had a couple of nights of horrible nightsweats, which were also combined with intense nausea. Don't know whether it's from the CT, though?

Sounds like it. We all sure have had our share of detoxing going on. I have to blame it all on CT cuz this isn't normal. But, I did ask my doc today about the hot flashes and she thinks I am taking too much HC in the afternoon. I am cutting back today by 1/3 and we'll see what tonight brings.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-09T21:42:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13106>

I would guess by taking the HC and DHEA and pregn that you are starting to "feel" some hormonal changes going on and manifesting as night sweats. Most docs say that you need estrogen but we know that is not the case. I think you have just stirred things up and with CT and the detoxing it's going to take a while for your body to find it's sweet spot.

I'm having a couple times during the night where I get warm but not even to take the one light blanket off. I think the hormones in my body are getting stirred up too but not quite as much as yours are. It might be the HC but I don't know. It's really the only thing I see that you are on that I am not on and our situations are eerily similar.

### Sherry — 2012-04-10T00:40:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13148>

I've been heating up for years. I'm ready to be done with it. Not even BHRT helped in the long run. Since starting CT things have been more intense and I can only hope that it is all the detoxing going on, the hormones readjusting themselves, etc. It's not linear either. The first few weeks were tough and then things leveled out in that area but now in the last few days they are intense again. I've even had hot flashes in the 48 degree tub!

Has anyone noticed if the hot flashes/night sweats are preceded by a fear or anxiety-type of feeling? One thing that has been happening to me is that I can lay down to sleep and initially fall asleep with no problem. Within minutes I am wide awake inside a 450 degree oven with a feeling of panic/slight unknown fear accompanying it. And then I lay there forever trying to get back to sleep.

Geez will I be glad when I'm detoxed and over the hump.

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-10T00:47:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13150>

My TSH went from 2.1 to 4.8 My free t3 came up and my RT3 value went down.

### N~Doro — 2012-04-10T16:57:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13333>

> **Sherry;13217 wrote:** I've been heating up for years. I'm ready to be done with it. Not even BHRT helped in the long run. Since starting CT things have been more intense and I can only hope that it is all the detoxing going on, the hormones readjusting themselves, etc. It's not linear either. The first few weeks were tough and then things leveled out in that area but now in the last few days they are intense again. I've even had hot flashes in the 48 degree tub!
>
>
>
> Has anyone noticed if the hot flashes/night sweats are preceded by a fear or anxiety-type of feeling? One thing that has been happening to me is that I can lay down to sleep and initially fall asleep with no problem. Within minutes I am wide awake inside a 450 degree oven with a feeling of panic/slight unknown fear accompanying it. And then I lay there forever trying to get back to sleep.
>
>
>
> Geez will I be glad when I'm detoxed and over the hump.

I've been having hot flashes since the beginning of the year so I can't blame CT detox.

From what I have read, hot flashes can have as much to do with blood sugar regulation and adrenal problems as estrogen. The anxiety you are feeling is mostly due to a big release of adrenalin when your blood sugar drops. I usually have pounding heartbeat and racing mind for about an hour and a half or two hours before I can get back to sleep.

My lovely husband brilliantly put together the concept of the relaxed feeling following the dive reflex with my nightly frustration trying to get back to sleep. When I wake up with the inevitable hot flash, I get up and splash cold water on my face or go for an all out face dunk. Sounds totally counter-intuitive, but it has been easier to get back to sleep.

FYI on the hormone or other treatments for hot flashes, it is my understanding that they only work about 50% of the time because unless you are constantly testing estrogen levels, it is hard to tell if you are on an up or down swing. It only works for one of those conditions ( I think it is when e is dropping). During Peri-menopause estrogen charts look pretty much like a major earthquake event on a seismograph, hence all the fun times.

### Sherry — 2012-04-10T20:38:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13421>

> **N~Doro;13402 wrote:** I've been having hot flashes since the beginning of the year so I can't blame CT detox.
>
>
>
> From what I have read, hot flashes can have as much to do with blood sugar regulation and adrenal problems as estrogen. The anxiety you are feeling is mostly due to a big release of adrenalin when your blood sugar drops. I usually have pounding heartbeat and racing mind for about an hour and a half or two hours before I can get back to sleep.
>
>
>
> My lovely husband brilliantly put together the concept of the relaxed feeling following the dive reflex with my nightly frustration trying to get back to sleep. When I wake up with the inevitable hot flash, I get up and splash cold water on my face or go for an all out face dunk. Sounds totally counter-intuitive, but it has been easier to get back to sleep.
>
>
>
> FYI on the hormone or other treatments for hot flashes, it is my understanding that they only work about 50% of the time because unless you are constantly testing estrogen levels, it is hard to tell if you are on an up or down swing. It only works for one of those conditions ( I think it is when e is dropping). During Peri-menopause estrogen charts look pretty much like a major earthquake event on a seismograph, hence all the fun times.

This is helpful. I do know that the adrenals are involved although I have been regularly testing my four salivary cortisol levels and finally, in the last year they are in the proper ranges. Of course, I do know that there are a bunch of other ranges to test that I haven't done. Right now I'm laying off the testing for a while since CT can throw them all over the place. For years and years I tested my estrogens every 6 weeks and you're right they were like a seismograph.

Blood sugar could be an issue. Do you think it is a good idea to test FBG daily? It seems counter-intuitive to me because I'm not going to stop doing this keto Paleo and CT and for me I find the stress of the tests to not be helpful. But, if someone gave me a good reason to do it I would think about it.

I like the face dunk idea. I'll take that one on board and see how it works. I only have this problem right after falling asleep - not in the middle of the night so it seems like it would be easier to talk myself into getting up and doing it then rather than if it were in the wee hours. Thanks.

### vkiernan — 2012-04-10T20:38:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13422>

> **Shijin13;13219 wrote:** My TSH went from 2.1 to 4.8 My free t3 came up and my RT3 value went down.

So glad to hear it. You are definitely going in the right direction. I was making the same sort of progress before the new doc took me off t3 only and put me on armour. Now, my t3 has actually come up a little and my rt3 is going bad again.

I am seriously thinking about going to Tenn. to see Dr. K. When Dr. K gives me little messages sometimes after I post things, he has made me realize I am dealing with major health issues that shouldn't be taken lightly. I am so grateful everytime he clues me in. I don't know of a doc who can treat the whole like he will. If my insurance would at least cover some of the labs it would be very doable. At some point in one's life you have take the leap to get you above just treating the symptoms. I want my health back and am very willing to pay for it one way or another. I will never understand why when one has insurance that it matters what dr. you see. You are paying for something very limited and that is very frustrating.

### Sherry — 2012-04-10T22:37:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-sweats-anybody-else-or-is-it-just-me.741/post-13468>

> **vkiernan;13491 wrote:**
>
> If my insurance would at least cover some of the labs it would be very doable. At some point in one's life you have take the leap to get you above just treating the symptoms. I want my health back and am very willing to pay for it one way or another. I will never understand why when one has insurance that it matters what dr. you see. You are paying for something very limited and that is very frustrating.

Insurance companies are a necessary evil. I've used alternative health care my entire adult life and once in a blue moon they might end up paying a small percentage of what something has cost. And then when you do use a bonafide doctor it turns out it isn't one they want to pay for. They must have spies out there to let them know that the doctor is doing something unconventional that just might work and that's the criteria for refusal. Good idea going to see Dr. K. He has made me understand so much in the five+ months I've been following his blog. Keep us posted.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

I have my first physical with a new doctor from Paleo Physicians Network in 2 weeks and plan to get all my labs done then.

I have been taking a variety of vitamins for months (prior to Dr Kâ€™s Leptin reset) that include: K2, Krill oil, Omega 3, astaxanthin, Magnesium, B12/6, D3, Chlorella, evening 5-htp/St. Johnâ€™s wort sometimes melatonin.

Ideally, once I am done w/Leptin reset I donâ€™t plan to take any vitamins except D3 in winter, Maybe Krill/Omega3 and Magnesium for occasional constipation. I track my micro nutrients and am conscious of getting optimal nutrients from the least amount of calories by choosing foods/herbs w/the greatest nutrient value and even combining synergistic foods for highest absorption.

My question would be do you think I should stop taking all vitamins for a few weeks before my appointment so I can see if I actually am deficient in certain nutrients?

I stopped taking everything a few days ago but donâ€™t want to stall my amazing progress. Thoughts on this?

# Thread: Foods/Supplements that increase UCP1 and BAT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foods-supplements-that-increase-ucp1-and-bat.746/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-04-09T17:02:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foods-supplements-that-increase-ucp1-and-bat.746/post-12980>

Besides Green Tea and Bitter Melon - are there other foods or supplements we can take to increase the CT effect/transition?

Bitter Melon has a noticeable effect on me - it really ramps up the heat production.

Looking though the literature, I found a few things that seemed suggestive:

Red peppers (and maybe isothiocyanates?)

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/3244046](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/3244046)

[http://www.jappl.org/content/95/6/2408.full](http://http://www.jappl.org/content/95/6/2408.full)

EV Olive Oil

[http://www.jnutbio.com/article/S0955-2863(07)00013-7/abstract](http://http://www.jnutbio.com/article/S0955-2863\(07\)00013-7/abstract)

Garlic

[http://jn.nutrition.org/content/129/2/336.short](http://http://jn.nutrition.org/content/129/2/336.short)

I realize that we don't want to mess with the Leptin Reset diet folks and that we want to eat high fat/protein - but what do you think? Would it help to add some hot sauce, garlic, etc?

Is there anything to this?

### Glamorama — 2012-04-10T01:11:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foods-supplements-that-increase-ucp1-and-bat.746/post-13160>

Yes to the garlic. I don't know what it is, but it seriously cranks up my inner heater! Cayenne pepper is also very good for increasing metabolism.

### AKMan — 2012-04-10T01:36:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foods-supplements-that-increase-ucp1-and-bat.746/post-13171>

I read an article with credible references a while back that said the thermogenic effect of peppers wasn't from the capsaicin, but another chemical that's not hot, so even peppers that lack heat are thermogenic. I couldn't find it, but did find this you may find interesting.

From Livestrong.com

Thermogenic foods may help increase metabolism and calorie burning by enhancing thermogenesis, a process in which the body burns calories to utilize the foods you have just eaten, converting those calories to heat. Your body mainly burns calories consumed through maintaining its regular metabolic functions and through physical activity, however a small percentage, about 10 percent, of calories are burned through diet-induced thermogenesis, according to the Mayo Clinic. The following is a list of foods that may enhance thermogenesis:

Spices

Consuming foods with spices such as red hot peppers or black pepper, increases thermogenesis and can potentially have a significant effect on feelings of satiety and fat oxidation, according to Dr. Margriet Westerterp-Plantenga and colleagues at the Department of Human Biology at Maastricht University in The Netherlands in a review published August 2006 in the journal Physiology & Behavior.

Capsaicin, the substance that gives hot peppers their pungent flavor, has been reported to increase thermogenesis, and in studies in Japanese men and women who consumed a meal containing capsaicin, the participants experienced an increase in energy expenditure immediately following their spicy meal, according to Dr. Westerterp-Plantenga.

Similarly, black pepper contains piperine, a substance that has been shown to influence thermogenesis through stimulating the nervous system, according to Dr. Westerterp-Plantenga and colleagues.

Green Tea

Green tea contains two substances--caffeine and polyphenols called catechins--that have been shown to boost thermogensis, and may enhance each other's effects. according to the August 2006 review by Dr. Westerterp-Plantenga and colleagues published in the journal Physiology & Behavior.

Catechins in green tea may increase thermogenesis through inhibiting a particular enzyme, and green tea contains high amounts of a certain catechin, called epigallocatechin gallate, that is "probably the most pharmacologically active," according to Dr. Westerterp-Plantenga and colleagues.

Coconut Oil

Coconut oil contains mainly medium-chain fatty acids, fats that, when consumed, have been shown to inhibit fat deposition through increased thermogenesis and fat burning, in studies conducted in animals and in humans, according to a review by Dr. Koji Nagao and colleagues at the Laboratory of Nutrition Biochemistry, Department of Applied Biochemistry and Food Science, at Saga University in Saga, Japan, that was published in March 2010 in the journal Pharmocological Research.

Protein

Diet-induced thermogenesis can also be affected by the proportion of macronutrients consumed--carbs, fats, and proteins--with high-protein diets being the most thermogenic compared to high-carb or high-fat diets, according to researcher Helen H. Hermsdorff at Universidad de Navarra, in Pamplona, Spain, and colleagues in a study published in the journal Archivos Latinoamericanos de Nutricion in March 2007. Sources of protein include lean cuts of red meat, poultry, eggs, and nuts.

Read more: [http://www.livestrong.com/article/198798-thermogenic-foods-list/#ixzz1raLhIMjC](http://http://www.livestrong.com/article/198798-thermogenic-foods-list/#ixzz1raLhIMjC)

### Jim — 2012-04-10T01:49:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foods-supplements-that-increase-ucp1-and-bat.746/post-13176>

> **Anna K.;13222 wrote:** HI, how much bitter melon do you take? thanks.

I take 500mg twice a day. Here is what Dr K does.

February 12th, 2012 at 1:48 pm

How much bitter melon? Extract or whole herb? When did you take it?

February 12th, 2012 at 2:49 pm

### freesia — 2012-04-10T02:25:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foods-supplements-that-increase-ucp1-and-bat.746/post-13189>

A good way to get black pepper, is to put a half to a whole Tablespoon of black peppercorns in with the bones when making bone broth. The long slow cooking seems to release something warming in more than a "spicy hot" way.

I'm enjoying garlic too.

Early in LR (10 days in), so won't try the others yet but thanks for the useful list-to-look-forward-to.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-04-10T15:07:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foods-supplements-that-increase-ucp1-and-bat.746/post-13308>

> **AKMan;13240 wrote:** I read an article with credible references a while back that said the thermogenic effect of peppers wasn't from the capsaicin, but another chemical that's not hot, so even peppers that lack heat are thermogenic. I couldn't find it, but did find this you may find interesting.

I believe capsaicin has the effect, but there are analogs that are not spicy that have the same effect.

They dubbed them nonpungent analogs aka capsinoids. I guess they are found in all peppers but aren't hot.

### chocolate — 2012-04-15T03:20:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foods-supplements-that-increase-ucp1-and-bat.746/post-15190>

Dietary capsaicin reduces obesity-induced insulin resistance and hepatic steatosis in obese mice fed a high-fat diet.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19798065](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19798065)

I thought I would look to see if other thermogenic foods reduced insulin resistance. Probiotics seem to be anti-inflammatories that do this.

### Dextery — 2012-04-15T04:01:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foods-supplements-that-increase-ucp1-and-bat.746/post-15200>

Just remember that all peppers are in the nightshade class of veggies and are a gut irritant. You can eat them. The question is: Should you?

### nonchalant — 2012-04-15T04:40:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foods-supplements-that-increase-ucp1-and-bat.746/post-15209>

# Thread: Lab results - I'm lost without you!

### optimus prime — 2012-04-09T17:25:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-results-im-lost-without-you.747/post-12995>

\*I posted this in a different thread before I saw this dedicated optimal labs forum. Sorry for the redundancy if you've read this already.

I just got some blood work done and I'd like some help deciphering the results. I have no idea what many of these values mean and I want to be well prepared when I go back for my follow up on Wednesday. I haven't had any hormones tested yet. I'll be doing that Wednesday as well. For now I just have the results of my "General Health Panel". Below are my thyroid, lipids and few other things. I posted the rest of the results to a google doc. It is set to accept edits, so you can type comments directly on the spreadsheet if you like. My questions are listed below the results on this post. Here is the link to my full lab report: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheet/ccc?key=0AhpZKlPqqhpFdHBtTWVnZXp1Y2Q5OFA5dWJBOWVBRXc#gid=0.

\-B12 - holy cow, I have never tested my B levels before, but I guess that's one thing that's responding to the supplements...Weird. I also was taking them every day leading up to the test. Maybe I should stop taking them and ask for a restest on Wednesday.

\*Questions

\-I know it's hard to determine much without cortisol and sex hormone levels, but I mainly just want to know about the cholesterol and inflammation relationship. Could this be an indication of something else going on?

\-Does anyone know what would cause chronically low TSH and vitamin D with glaring hypothyroid symptoms?

\-Could low D be related to low TSH? What else can I do to get my Vitamin D up? I've been supplementing for at least a year and the level hasn't really budged. Also, not much sun in Chicago, but I feel like there's something else causing this. When I came back from 3 months in Phoenix,AZ where I spend a lot of time outdoors, my vitamin D was still in the low 40s

\-Finally, I thought I might have absorption issues but the high vitamin B and improved Iron are making me second guess that. So, I'm left wondering what could be causing low D...

Reminder: I'm 30, 5'4", 160 lbs (still no weight loss, 6 weeks into leptin rx)

Thank you for any and all help!

### quelsen — 2012-04-09T18:23:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-results-im-lost-without-you.747/post-13017>

lets tell a story here

Your body isn't asking your brain to make thyroxine.\[TSH\]

and you dont have good levels of active thyroxine in your blood\[Free (T3)\]

and you dont have good levels of storage thyroxine in your blood to convert into active thyroxine \[T4,Free\]

but you dont seem to have any conversion blockers \[Thyroid Peroxidase (TPO), Antithyroglobulin Ab\]

so the issue is most likely an energy imbalance.

Your BMI is normal range \[HDL Cholesterol\] to possibly thinner than normal

but you have a lot of oxidative damage to be controlled \[LDL Chol. Calc\]

and you are a poor metabolizer of glucose \[Glucose, Serum\] but we already knew that from your thyroid status.

Your ferritin is low but your B is high so you must be suplementing.

and with your D low and your T3 low your cortisol is out of balance and i would suspect your sleep is poor.

that is the story your labs tell me given what i know so far

ETA i missed your height and weight. that tells me more information

your estorgens are high ( pcos? ) as your hdl is strong but your weight is up. also causes you to make less t3 as adipose tissue exerts a depressive metabolic effect to thyroxine

### mambo — 2012-04-12T06:26:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-results-im-lost-without-you.747/post-14095>

Those are good numbers. Your thyroid #s are very similar to mine. TSH is really in the sweet spot. Your Free T3 isn't really that low: just a tad off from 3.25. There aren't many conventional doctors who'll decide to treat you based on those #s and your negative antibodies. You may be considered "subclinical" but most will claim that it's "psychosomatic."

Excellent pH, excellent Ferritin, which you claim is too low. Really, on paper, you look good. FBG isn't really reliable based on just one number. LDL could be from ApoE status or something else; even if it is, you're mostly Type A. Vit D at 40 is low if you're supplementing 5,000. I'm clueless why it would be low.

She sent me home with a cortisol and neurotransmitter test as well. She seems fairly confident that my cortisol test will reveal the issue. She's a naturopath, so her response was similar to yours regarding T3. "It's not that low, but I'd like to see it higher." When we talked about the LDL, she agreed that it's too high and we're looking into possible inflammation.

Vitamin D is a mystery to me as well. I've been supplementing for years and it hasn't really budged. I did notice that my calcium has been at 10.0 or over since 2008...There has to be a connection there...

Regarding Ferritin, I said it's the best I've seen it in years! It was formerly in the low 30s. I'm not complaining at all.

Since we have really similar thyroid levels, I wonder what your weight situation is? Do you have difficulty maintaining healthy weight?

### herringsalad — 2012-04-16T17:46:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-results-im-lost-without-you.747/post-15586>

> **optimus prime;13064 wrote:** \*
>
> What else can I do to get my Vitamin D up? I've been supplementing for at least a year and the level hasn't really budged. !

What kind of Vitamin D supplements are you taking? You have to make sure that you take Vitamin D3 not Vitamin D2. And it's a good idea to take one that is in an olive oil capsule so that it's bioavailable. I take Healthy Origins - almost doubled my levels in less than six months.

### herringsalad — 2012-04-16T17:49:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-results-im-lost-without-you.747/post-15588>

Also if you want to build up your levels 5,000 IU is probably not high enough. According to the Vitamin D Council website, many adults use that much a day. So if you want to build up your levels shoot for 10,000 IU for three months, then test again. Once it's at the level you want then you can drop back down to 5,000 IU.

# Thread: Mixing for sublingual Rx Help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mixing-for-sublingual-rx-help.751/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 15

### Claudia — 2012-04-09T20:16:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mixing-for-sublingual-rx-help.751/post-13050>

Just received my order of hcg and need to mix it up for tomorrow's start. I did it last year but mixed with saline and vodka. I'm questioning everything I did last year as I got some strange advice in many ways. I have been pretty messed up hormonally since I ended my last round 9 months ago. I gain weight with every TOM that doesn't come off again and I'm up 14 lbs. I desperately want to do things right this time.

I did Leptin Reset for 5 weeks from Nov. to December. I've been doing CT for about a month and will continue with it through my round.

So, I have several 5000 iu vials and will mix with saline wound wash. I have one amber bottle, previously used. I have syringes for mixing. The instructions I have are mix with 20 mL. Last time, I used 18 mL of saline and 2 mL of vodka. Not sure I even need to use the vodka and maybe shouldn't. I have also been told to use b12 for mixing but I don't have that either. I will probably pick up a b12 supplement to take and looking for good recommendations for that as well.

Thanks so much for any mixing and other advice .

### Bernadette — 2012-04-10T02:02:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mixing-for-sublingual-rx-help.751/post-13179>

The Dr. Belluscio study showed that mixing with straight saline kept the potency of HCG longer than other diluents, I'd mix with straight saline.

Having done both Rx sublingual and Rx injections, I definitely noticed a huge difference doing the injections, so those are now my preferred method. The hunger suppression was significantly increased.

### Claudia — 2012-04-10T02:21:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mixing-for-sublingual-rx-help.751/post-13186>

Thank you Bernadette. I wasn't hungry at all and didn't have a problem with that when I did sublingual. So, you were more hungry on sublingual Rx, then? Is that the only difference? Because if it is, then I think I'll just do sublingual since I didn't have a problem with that at all.

Thank you Colleen. I decided to do shots and have just ordered what I need, more needles and bacteriostatic water. I have 5000 iu hcg vial. For 125 iu dose per day, I need to use 10mL of the bact. water, right? That will last me 30 days at least, right? And I will do subc injections into my tummy or thighs. I have done injections to my mom in the past so I'm not that freaked out about it  but it's a little weird to do them to myself.

### Lynn1 — 2012-04-10T21:37:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mixing-for-sublingual-rx-help.751/post-13446>

> **colleencoble;13304 wrote:** I have better reshaping on shots as compared to sublingual. Just FYI. I won't do anything but shots now.

Hi Colleen,

I am rxhcg, sublingual, no problems. However, most who try shots, do not go back to subl. Can you tell me what you mean by "better reshaping"? Thanks.

Also, I have been following discussion...did you add MCT or CO to your morning food?

### colleencoble — 2012-04-12T06:44:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mixing-for-sublingual-rx-help.751/post-14102>

By better reshaping, I mean I lost more inches where I carried my weight--in the hips. With the sublingual, I had more lost where I didn't need to lose it.

I have only done strict protocol rounds. No MCT or CO. And from what I'm seeing from those who are trying it with CT, the added fat doesn't seem to be a good idea. A few seem to be doing okay but I think I'm only going to add CT not fats.

### Claudia — 2012-04-15T19:06:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mixing-for-sublingual-rx-help.751/post-15327>

I decided to do injections and started...today is day 3, just did third injection and 11:30 am and still loading today but only healthy fats (CO and butter), dark chocolate, some peanut butter, usually proteins.

Do I need to keep injections at the exact same time every day? I used to do this with sublingual. My first injection was at 2pm on Friday because I got my supplies in the mail and then did it as soon as I could. Then, I tried to move the time earlier yesterday and did it at 11am. Today, I missed 11 am, forgot, and did 11:30. How important is it? I could try to move it earlier to a more convenient time. I work at home, morning rush isn't good because the kids are around so I could do it after dropping them at school, come home to work, do injection, around 9:30, but that doesn't work on Thursday because I go straight to yoga from drop off at school.

I am doing the same as you Colleen, no fats or oils. Although I do have a question about soap and shampoo/conditioner. What do you use for these, oil free versions? I have a bath and shower gel that I use to wash my hands and in the shower. It's an Alba Organics and I used on previous rounds. Now I am wondering since it does say very emollient. Perhaps I would do better using something else. I could use a good recommendation for shampoo/conditioner. My hair is dry and on the coarser side and long. Thanks so much.
